Memory Arc — Idea Substrate

Every idea extracted from the seven-project memory-architecture research arc (Apr–Jul 2026) plus AMBench, live session transcripts, and cross-project syntheses. Nothing ranked, nothing discarded — mechanisms, framings, experiments, metaphors, coinages, dead ends, and open questions all preserved as raw creativity substrate.

P1 · cognivault 238 ideas

Apr 2026 — origin seed: vector+graph memory for Claude Code (tyxter-memory) · P1-cognivault.md

A. The origin brainstorm (docs/idea.txt, 2026-04-13)

◈ The literal genesis: a Pydantic Memory model with two hooks

product

The whole project starts as a single narrow request: a Pydantic Memory model usable with semantic search to retrieve memories at a task-start hook and save memories at a task-end hook. Everything else (memory typing, consolidation, the graph) spirals out of this one sentence via a model dialogue.

docs/idea.txt (line 3)
"I need a pydantic Memory model so I can use with semantic search to retrieve memories at a task start hook, and to save memories with a task end hook"
the literal genesis: a pydantic memory model with two hooks the whole project starts as a single narrow request: a pydantic `memory` model usable with semantic search to *retrieve* memories at a task-start hook and *save* memories at a task-end hook. everything else (memory typing, consolidation, the graph) spirals out of this one sentence via a model dialogue. product docs/idea.txt (line 3) i need a pydantic memory model so i can use with semantic search to retrieve memories at a task start hook, and to save memories with a task end hook

◈ Modeling memory forces you to pick a lens

framing/ontology

There are three distinct framings for "what is a memory": human cognition (psychology/neuroscience), AI systems (context window vs external store), and software/database implementation. The design borrows the human distinctions (type, how-learned, when) but implements them as vector + rich structured metadata.

docs/idea.txt (lines 4-6, 92-93)
modeling memory forces you to pick a lens there are three distinct framings for what is a memory : human cognition (psychology/neuroscience), ai systems (context window vs external store), and software/database implementation. the design borrows the human distinctions (type, how-learned, when) but implements them as vector + rich structured metadata. framing/ontology docs/idea.txt (lines 4-6, 92-93)

◈ Atkinson-Shiffrin memory taxonomy imported into agent memory

framing/ontology

Human memory's multi-store model — sensory / short-term-working / long-term, with long-term split into explicit (episodic "what happened to me", semantic "what is true") and implicit (procedural "how to do"), plus prospective memory ("remember to do something in the future") — is adopted as the type system for agent memories. Episodic/semantic/procedural become the MemoryType enum.

docs/idea.txt (lines 6-16, 126-129)
atkinson-shiffrin memory taxonomy imported into agent memory human memory's multi-store model — sensory / short-term-working / long-term, with long-term split into explicit (episodic what happened to me , semantic what is true ) and implicit (procedural how to do ), plus prospective memory ( remember to do something in the future ) — is adopted as the type system for agent memories. episodic/semantic/procedural become the `memorytype` enum. framing/ontology docs/idea.txt (lines 6-16, 126-129)

◈ Learning-method taxonomy: how a memory was acquired matters

mechanism

Every memory records how it was formed: one_shot (single strong event), repeated_practice, reinforcement, associative (linked to existing knowledge), observational, or from_error (learned from a mistake). Acquisition method is treated as first-class metadata affecting durability and later extraction defaults.

docs/idea.txt (lines 20, 131-137); docs/PRD.md §6.1
learning-method taxonomy: how a memory was acquired matters every memory records *how it was formed*: `one_shot` (single strong event), `repeated_practice`, `reinforcement`, `associative` (linked to existing knowledge), `observational`, or `from_error` (learned from a mistake). acquisition method is treated as first-class metadata affecting durability and later extraction defaults. mechanism docs/idea.txt (lines 20, 131-137); docs/prd.md §6.1

◈ Encoding strength, emotional valence, and salience as memory metadata

mechanism

Borrowing from human encoding depth: each memory carries strength (0-1, influenced by repetition/emotion/surprise), importance (1-10), confidence, and even an optional emotion_valence ("positive", "frustrated", "neutral") "for human-like flavor."

docs/idea.txt (lines 23, 51, 151-171)
encoding strength, emotional valence, and salience as memory metadata borrowing from human encoding depth: each memory carries `strength` (0-1, influenced by repetition/emotion/surprise), `importance` (1-10), `confidence`, and even an optional `emotion_valence` ( positive , frustrated , neutral ) for human-like flavor. mechanism docs/idea.txt (lines 23, 51, 151-171)

◈ Retrieval is cue-dependent and reconstructive, not exact playback

framing/ontology

A human-memory principle adopted as a design premise: recall is triggered by cues and reconstructs, it does not replay verbatim records. This motivates semantic + graph recall over literal lookup, and later grows into the entire cue/trigger axis of the system.

docs/idea.txt (line 26)
"Retrieval is cue-dependent and reconstructive (not exact playback)."
retrieval is cue-dependent and reconstructive, not exact playback a human-memory principle adopted as a design premise: recall is triggered by cues and reconstructs, it does not replay verbatim records. this motivates semantic + graph recall over literal lookup, and later grows into the entire cue/trigger axis of the system. framing/ontology docs/idea.txt (line 26) retrieval is cue-dependent and reconstructive (not exact playback).

◈ Memory = vector + rich structured metadata

mechanism

The core implementation thesis: represent each memory as an embedding for semantic similarity plus a structured record (type, learning method, dates, tags, entities, scores, associations) so it is queryable by similarity, by filters, and by graph links simultaneously.

docs/idea.txt (lines 36-93)
memory = vector + rich structured metadata the core implementation thesis: represent each memory as an embedding for semantic similarity *plus* a structured record (type, learning method, dates, tags, entities, scores, associations) so it is queryable by similarity, by filters, and by graph links simultaneously. mechanism docs/idea.txt (lines 36-93)

◈ Hybrid retrieval trio: similarity + filters + graph hops

mechanism

From day one the retrieval design is a three-way hybrid: vector search for "find memories similar to this query," exact metadata filtering (type, date range, importance), and graph-link traversal for associations. No single retrieval mode is sufficient.

docs/idea.txt (lines 76-81, 301)
hybrid retrieval trio: similarity + filters + graph hops from day one the retrieval design is a three-way hybrid: vector search for find memories similar to this query, exact metadata filtering (type, date range, importance), and graph-link traversal for associations. no single retrieval mode is sufficient. mechanism docs/idea.txt (lines 76-81, 301)

◈ Tiered memory: fast short-term buffer + durable long-term vector store

mechanism

Scaling suggestion from the origin dialogue: keep short-term memory in a fast in-memory store (e.g. Redis) and long-term memory in a durable vector DB — mirroring the human short-term/long-term split at the infrastructure level.

docs/idea.txt (line 91)
tiered memory: fast short-term buffer + durable long-term vector store scaling suggestion from the origin dialogue: keep short-term memory in a fast in-memory store (e.g. redis) and long-term memory in a durable vector db — mirroring the human short-term/long-term split at the infrastructure level. mechanism docs/idea.txt (line 91)

◈ Forgetting as a designed feature: decay factors and eviction

mechanism

Memories carry a decay_factor and access statistics; a background process evicts low-strength, old, rarely-accessed items ("delete memories that haven't been accessed in 30 days and have low importance"). Forgetting is deliberate garbage collection, not a bug.

docs/idea.txt (lines 25, 74, 86, 302)
forgetting as a designed feature: decay factors and eviction memories carry a `decay_factor` and access statistics; a background process evicts low-strength, old, rarely-accessed items ( delete memories that haven't been accessed in 30 days and have low importance ). forgetting is deliberate garbage collection, not a bug. mechanism docs/idea.txt (lines 25, 74, 86, 302)

◈ mark_accessed(): retrieval itself reinforces

mechanism

Every retrieval bumps access_count and last_accessed_at via a mark_accessed() helper and writes the update back to the store — recall frequency becomes an input to future ranking and to pruning decisions. Use strengthens the trace.

docs/idea.txt (lines 218-221, 287-289)
mark_accessed(): retrieval itself reinforces every retrieval bumps `access_count` and `last_accessed_at` via a `mark_accessed()` helper and writes the update back to the store — recall frequency becomes an input to future ranking and to pruning decisions. use strengthens the trace. mechanism docs/idea.txt (lines 218-221, 287-289)

◈ LLMs shouldn't generate system fields — split the schemas

mechanism

Because LLMs are bad at (and shouldn't be trusted with) UUIDs, timestamps, and access counters, the design splits a lean CreateMemoryInput (what the LLM populates: content, type, learning_method, source, tags, importance) from the rich internal MemoryItem (system-managed IDs, dates, counters, embeddings).

docs/idea.txt (lines 222-236); docs/PRD.md §6.2
llms shouldn't generate system fields — split the schemas because llms are bad at (and shouldn't be trusted with) uuids, timestamps, and access counters, the design splits a lean `creatememoryinput` (what the llm populates: content, type, learning_method, source, tags, importance) from the rich internal `memoryitem` (system-managed ids, dates, counters, embeddings). mechanism docs/idea.txt (lines 222-236); docs/prd.md §6.2

◈ Pydantic field descriptions double as extraction-prompt instructions

mechanism

Field description strings on the Pydantic models are written for the LLM, not just humans — when structured-output extraction runs, the model reads those descriptions to know how to populate the data. The schema is part of the prompt.

docs/idea.txt (lines 112-113)
pydantic field descriptions double as extraction-prompt instructions field `description` strings on the pydantic models are written for the llm, not just humans — when structured-output extraction runs, the model reads those descriptions to know how to populate the data. the schema *is* part of the prompt. mechanism docs/idea.txt (lines 112-113)

◈ Enum-enforced typing prevents hallucinated memory categories

mechanism

Strict enums for MemoryType and LearningMethod mean Pydantic validation rejects invented categories like type="random_fact" — the model literally cannot write a memory of a type the system doesn't understand.

docs/idea.txt (lines 300)
enum-enforced typing prevents hallucinated memory categories strict enums for `memorytype` and `learningmethod` mean pydantic validation rejects invented categories like `type= random_fact ` — the model literally cannot write a memory of a type the system doesn't understand. mechanism docs/idea.txt (lines 300)

◈ "Toy" vs "cognitive" memory: the consolidation dividing line

framing/ontology

The claim that separates an infinite vector log from a learning knowledge graph is consolidation: at write time, the system must compare new information against existing memories and decide whether to strengthen a pattern, record an edge case, flag a contradiction, or insert something novel — rather than blind-inserting.

docs/idea.txt (lines 318-319)
"You have hit on exactly what separates a 'toy' AI memory system (an infinite vector log) from a 'cognitive' AI memory system (a learning knowledge graph)."
toy vs cognitive memory: the consolidation dividing line the claim that separates an infinite vector log from a learning knowledge graph is consolidation: at write time, the system must compare new information against existing memories and decide whether to strengthen a pattern, record an edge case, flag a contradiction, or insert something novel — rather than blind-inserting. framing/ontology docs/idea.txt (lines 318-319) you have hit on exactly what separates a 'toy' ai memory system (an infinite vector log) from a 'cognitive' ai memory system (a learning knowledge graph).

◈ Cognitive appraisal loop: Extract → Recall → Appraise → Commit

mechanism

The write path is a four-step loop: (1) extract candidate learnings from the task, (2) recall similar existing memories before saving, (3) LLM appraises the relationship between new and old (with a short-circuit to insert when nothing similar exists), (4) execute the resulting graph/database mutation.

docs/idea.txt (lines 374-404)
cognitive appraisal loop: extract → recall → appraise → commit the write path is a four-step loop: (1) extract candidate learnings from the task, (2) recall similar existing memories *before* saving, (3) llm appraises the relationship between new and old (with a short-circuit to insert when nothing similar exists), (4) execute the resulting graph/database mutation. mechanism docs/idea.txt (lines 374-404)

◈ ConsolidationAction: NOVEL / STRENGTHEN / EDGE_CASE / CONTRADICTION

mechanism

The original four-way classification of every new learning: completely new → insert; matches existing → reinforce/update; exception to a rule → insert-and-link; opposes existing → overwrite or flag. This enum is the seed of the eventual seven-action consolidation vocabulary.

docs/idea.txt (lines 332-336)
consolidationaction: novel / strengthen / edge_case / contradiction the original four-way classification of every new learning: completely new → insert; matches existing → reinforce/update; exception to a rule → insert-and-link; opposes existing → overwrite or flag. this enum is the seed of the eventual seven-action consolidation vocabulary. mechanism docs/idea.txt (lines 332-336)

◈ MemoryLinks: graph-lite edges on the memory record

mechanism

The first graph structure: each memory carries reinforces (list of IDs), nuance_of (single parent ID), and contradicts (list of IDs) — typed links making the store a graph rather than a list. (Later replaced by a dedicated edges table, but this is where edges-as-first-class starts.)

docs/idea.txt (lines 338-344)
memorylinks: graph-lite edges on the memory record the first graph structure: each memory carries `reinforces` (list of ids), `nuance_of` (single parent id), and `contradicts` (list of ids) — typed links making the store a graph rather than a list. (later replaced by a dedicated edges table, but this is where edges-as-first-class starts.) mechanism docs/idea.txt (lines 338-344)

◈ ConsolidationDecision: structured output for the appraisal verdict

mechanism

A dedicated Pydantic schema forces the appraising LLM into a machine-executable verdict: action, target_memory_id, merged_content (synthesized phrasing for STRENGTHEN), edge_case_context (the if/when condition), and confidence_delta (±0.5 adjustment to the target's strength).

docs/idea.txt (lines 348-373)
consolidationdecision: structured output for the appraisal verdict a dedicated pydantic schema forces the appraising llm into a machine-executable verdict: `action`, `target_memory_id`, `merged_content` (synthesized phrasing for strengthen), `edge_case_context` (the if/when condition), and `confidence_delta` (±0.5 adjustment to the target's strength). mechanism docs/idea.txt (lines 348-373)

◈ Recall-before-save: query the DB before every insert

mechanism

Baked-in rule of the consolidation hook: the top-k similarity search runs against each candidate learning before any write, so the appraisal always sees its nearest existing neighbors. Deduplication is a retrieval problem solved at write time.

docs/idea.txt (lines 385-391)
recall-before-save: query the db before every insert baked-in rule of the consolidation hook: the top-k similarity search runs against each candidate learning *before* any write, so the appraisal always sees its nearest existing neighbors. deduplication is a retrieval problem solved at write time. mechanism docs/idea.txt (lines 385-391)

◈ The appraisal prompt as referee ("the secret sauce")

mechanism

A prompt template casts the LLM as "an expert cognitive memory manager" whose job is to integrate a NEW LEARNING into EXISTING MEMORIES following four numbered rules (unrelated→NOVEL, same→STRENGTHEN, exception→EDGE_CASE, opposes→CONTRADICTION), outputting strictly to the JSON schema. The prompt is explicitly identified as the highest-leverage component.

docs/idea.txt (lines 446-465)
the appraisal prompt as referee ( the secret sauce ) a prompt template casts the llm as an expert cognitive memory manager whose job is to integrate a new learning into existing memories following four numbered rules (unrelated→novel, same→strengthen, exception→edge_case, opposes→contradiction), outputting strictly to the json schema. the prompt is explicitly identified as the highest-leverage component. mechanism docs/idea.txt (lines 446-465)

◈ STRENGTHEN kills infinite append: "Python is good" 50× → one clean vector

result/finding

The canonical worked example: learning the same fact 50 times should trigger STRENGTHEN 49 times — one vector row whose reinforcement_count climbs and strength saturates at 1.0 — not 50 duplicate rows. Sub-linear growth of the store becomes the visible proof that consolidation works.

docs/idea.txt (lines 466-467); docs/PRD.md §8
"If the agent learns 'Python is good' 50 times, it doesn't create 50 vectors. It triggers STRENGTHEN 49 times... The vector stays clean."
strengthen kills infinite append: python is good 50× → one clean vector the canonical worked example: learning the same fact 50 times should trigger strengthen 49 times — one vector row whose `reinforcement_count` climbs and `strength` saturates at 1.0 — not 50 duplicate rows. sub-linear growth of the store becomes the visible proof that consolidation works. result/finding docs/idea.txt (lines 466-467); docs/prd.md §8 if the agent learns 'python is good' 50 times, it doesn't create 50 vectors. it triggers strengthen 49 times... the vector stays clean.

◈ EDGE_CASE is "where expertise is born"

framing/ontology

Exceptions to general rules ("Python is good, except heavy multithreading because of the GIL") become new memories explicitly linked to the parent rule with content templated as "Usually [Parent Rule], BUT {condition}: {learning}". Accumulating linked edge-cases is the system's model of developing expertise.

docs/idea.txt (lines 432-445, 468)
"This is where expertise is born."
edge_case is where expertise is born exceptions to general rules ( python is good, except heavy multithreading because of the gil ) become new memories explicitly linked to the parent rule with content templated as usually [parent rule], but {condition}: {learning} . accumulating linked edge-cases is the system's model of developing expertise. framing/ontology docs/idea.txt (lines 432-445, 468) this is where expertise is born.

◈ Edge cases boost the parent's importance

mechanism

When an edge-case child is attached, the parent rule's importance gets subtly incremented — the rationale being that a rule foundational enough to accumulate exceptions is itself more important.

docs/idea.txt (lines 444-445)
edge cases boost the parent's importance when an edge-case child is attached, the parent rule's `importance` gets subtly incremented — the rationale being that a rule foundational enough to accumulate exceptions is itself more important. mechanism docs/idea.txt (lines 444-445)

◈ The nuance_of retrieval upgrade: rule + exceptions in one hop

mechanism

Because nuance_of stores the parent's UUID, the recall path can do a fast metadata query for all children when a core memory is retrieved — loading the general rule and all its expert edge-cases into context together. This becomes the one-hop graph expansion of the production recall path.

docs/idea.txt (line 469); ARCHITECTURE.md §4.2
---
the nuance_of retrieval upgrade: rule + exceptions in one hop because `nuance_of` stores the parent's uuid, the recall path can do a fast metadata query for all children when a core memory is retrieved — loading the general rule *and* all its expert edge-cases into context together. this becomes the one-hop graph expansion of the production recall path. mechanism docs/idea.txt (line 469); architecture.md §4.2 ---

B. The seed ruling (tyxter-memory/memory/project_cognivault.md, 2026-04-13)

◈ Books on a shelf vs associative recall — we want both

metaphor

The load-bearing metaphor for the whole project: Claude Code's native markdown memory (~/.claude/*.md, CLAUDE.md) is "books on a shelf" — explicit, human-curated reference material — while Cognivault is the associative recall layer above it. The markdown system is too primitive for cross-session learning but too useful to discard; Cognivault is additive, never a replacement, never migrates or overwrites the files.

tyxter-memory/memory/project_cognivault.md; docs/PRD.md §1, §3
"Books on the shelf (markdown) vs. associative recall (vector graph). We want both."
books on a shelf vs associative recall — we want both the load-bearing metaphor for the whole project: claude code's native markdown memory (`~/.claude/*.md`, `claude.md`) is books on a shelf — explicit, human-curated reference material — while cognivault is the associative recall layer above it. the markdown system is too primitive for cross-session learning but too useful to discard; cognivault is additive, never a replacement, never migrates or overwrites the files. metaphor tyxter-memory/memory/project_cognivault.md; docs/prd.md §1, §3 books on the shelf (markdown) vs. associative recall (vector graph). we want both.

◈ Claude Code / agent-CLI only — no framework adapters

framing/ontology

Deliberate scope decree: no LangGraph, CrewAI, or AutoGen adapters. Integration is only through standard agent-CLI hooks plus a local HTTP client; any CLI with hooks is a viable host. Keeps the integration surface tiny and standard.

tyxter-memory/memory/project_cognivault.md; docs/PRD.md §3
claude code / agent-cli only — no framework adapters deliberate scope decree: no langgraph, crewai, or autogen adapters. integration is only through standard agent-cli hooks plus a local http client; any cli with hooks is a viable host. keeps the integration surface tiny and standard. framing/ontology tyxter-memory/memory/project_cognivault.md; docs/prd.md §3

◈ pgvector only — one storage substrate by decree

framing/ontology

No Pinecone/Qdrant/Chroma/FAISS, no second datastore ever. Diego runs local Postgres + pgvector; everything (vectors, graph, evidence, logs, FTS) lives in that one database. Zero extra ops burden.

tyxter-memory/memory/project_cognivault.md; docs/PRD.md §3; CLAUDE.md
pgvector only — one storage substrate by decree no pinecone/qdrant/chroma/faiss, no second datastore ever. diego runs local postgres + pgvector; everything (vectors, graph, evidence, logs, fts) lives in that one database. zero extra ops burden. framing/ontology tyxter-memory/memory/project_cognivault.md; docs/prd.md §3; claude.md

◈ Infrastructure-only v1: ship as plumbing

product

v1 has no UI, no dashboards, no service to operate — "just hooks, a local REST daemon, and a pgvector-backed graph schema." The value must land as invisible infrastructure first; visualization is icing once the graph is trusted.

tyxter-memory/memory/project_cognivault.md; docs/PRD.md §2 goal 8, §10
---
infrastructure-only v1: ship as plumbing v1 has no ui, no dashboards, no service to operate — just hooks, a local rest daemon, and a pgvector-backed graph schema. the value must land as invisible infrastructure first; visualization is icing once the graph is trusted. product tyxter-memory/memory/project_cognivault.md; docs/prd.md §2 goal 8, §10 ---

C. The PRD (docs/PRD.md, Draft v0.2, 2026-04-15)

◈ The tyxter-memory hang: MCP stdio blocks the host agent

dead-end

Documented failure of the v0.1 shape (three cooperating subprocesses: recall hook, write hook, MCP stdio server). MCP stdio binds the server's liveness to the agent's process tree: any slow call inside the server — cold psycopg connect, a 5s embeddings timeout, a bad pgvector query — freezes the agent's turn because the agent has no independent timeout on tool-call reads. "In practice it hangs."

docs/PRD.md §0, §3a "Why REST-only"
"stdio-tool servers and in-process hook subprocesses are the wrong shape for anything that might take longer than an agent is willing to wait"
the tyxter-memory hang: mcp stdio blocks the host agent documented failure of the v0.1 shape (three cooperating subprocesses: recall hook, write hook, mcp stdio server). mcp stdio binds the server's liveness to the agent's process tree: any slow call inside the server — cold psycopg connect, a 5s embeddings timeout, a bad pgvector query — freezes the agent's turn because the agent has no independent timeout on tool-call reads. in practice it hangs. dead-end docs/prd.md §0, §3a why rest-only stdio-tool servers and in-process hook subprocesses are the wrong shape for anything that might take longer than an agent is willing to wait

◈ Hook cold-start is not free: Python import cost blows the recall budget

result/finding

Empirical finding from v0.1: every UserPromptSubmit invocation paid Python import, psycopg pool init, and OpenAI client construction; at p95 this routinely exceeded the 400ms recall budget, and Windows (Git Bash shim + Python launcher) made the cold-start floor worse.

docs/PRD.md §0
hook cold-start is not free: python import cost blows the recall budget empirical finding from v0.1: every `userpromptsubmit` invocation paid python import, psycopg pool init, and openai client construction; at p95 this routinely exceeded the 400ms recall budget, and windows (git bash shim + python launcher) made the cold-start floor worse. result/finding docs/prd.md §0

◈ Short-lived subprocesses leave nowhere to put retries or backpressure

result/finding

With work living inside per-hook subprocesses and an in-band MCP server, there is no place to buffer, retry, or degrade — a transient embedding outage propagates straight into the user's prompt latency. Resilience requires a long-lived process.

docs/PRD.md §0
short-lived subprocesses leave nowhere to put retries or backpressure with work living inside per-hook subprocesses and an in-band mcp server, there is no place to buffer, retry, or degrade — a transient embedding outage propagates straight into the user's prompt latency. resilience requires a long-lived process. result/finding docs/prd.md §0

◈ State lives in the wrong place without a long-lived owner

result/finding

Session context, stop-hook buffers, and the prompt-cache baseline were reloaded from disk on every hook invocation because no process owned them. A daemon that owns the connection pool, embedding client, and session cache fixes the whole class of problem.

docs/PRD.md §0
state lives in the wrong place without a long-lived owner session context, stop-hook buffers, and the prompt-cache baseline were reloaded from disk on every hook invocation because no process owned them. a daemon that owns the connection pool, embedding client, and session cache fixes the whole class of problem. result/finding docs/prd.md §0

◈ The v0.2 REST-daemon pivot: long-lived daemon + thin hard-deadline clients

mechanism

Cognivault's runtime moves into a long-lived local REST daemon (FastAPI + uvicorn on 127.0.0.1) owning the pool, embedding client, session cache, and consolidation runner; hooks become ~30-line HTTP clients with hard client-side timeouts that print nothing and exit 0 on any failure. The early MCP adapter is dropped; REST is the only transport.

docs/PRD.md §0, §3a, §5; CLAUDE.md
the v0.2 rest-daemon pivot: long-lived daemon + thin hard-deadline clients cognivault's runtime moves into a long-lived local rest daemon (fastapi + uvicorn on 127.0.0.1) owning the pool, embedding client, session cache, and consolidation runner; hooks become ~30-line http clients with hard client-side timeouts that print nothing and exit 0 on any failure. the early mcp adapter is dropped; rest is the only transport. mechanism docs/prd.md §0, §3a, §5; claude.md

◈ The five-point indictment of native markdown memory

framing/ontology

Why ~/.claude/*.md isn't "remembering": (1) flat, not associative — no notion that "use Poetry" is a nuance of "Python tooling preferences"; (2) all-or-nothing recall — the whole file loads regardless of relevance, burning context on noise; (3) no reinforcement, no decay — a once-given correction weighs the same as one repeated ten times, stale advice never fades; (4) no consolidation — new learnings are manually filed or lost; (5) no semantic retrieval.

docs/PRD.md §1
"primitive — closer to keeping a stack of reference books on the desk than to actually remembering"
the five-point indictment of native markdown memory why `~/.claude/*.md` isn't remembering : (1) flat, not associative — no notion that use poetry is a nuance of python tooling preferences ; (2) all-or-nothing recall — the whole file loads regardless of relevance, burning context on noise; (3) no reinforcement, no decay — a once-given correction weighs the same as one repeated ten times, stale advice never fades; (4) no consolidation — new learnings are manually filed or lost; (5) no semantic retrieval. framing/ontology docs/prd.md §1 primitive — closer to keeping a stack of reference books on the desk than to actually remembering

◈ Memory is not linear across time (constellations)

framing/ontology

First-class problem statement: durable insights form as constellations across time — an earlier, seemingly unrelated session primes a later realization; multiple sessions on different platforms independently derive the same conclusion; a present cue activates a sparse cross-time subgraph rather than the single most-similar note. The failure mode of a naive vector store is "topical recall without synthesis": it returns what looks similar but misses the dormant precursor, bridge, or independent derivation that makes the current moment intelligible.

docs/PRD.md §1a; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §1
memory is not linear across time (constellations) first-class problem statement: durable insights form as constellations across time — an earlier, seemingly unrelated session primes a later realization; multiple sessions on different platforms independently derive the same conclusion; a present cue activates a sparse cross-time subgraph rather than the single most-similar note. the failure mode of a naive vector store is topical recall without synthesis : it returns what looks similar but misses the dormant precursor, bridge, or independent derivation that makes the current moment intelligible. framing/ontology docs/prd.md §1a; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §1

◈ Memory is state-dependent: preserve the conditions of derivation

framing/ontology

The same prompt asked in a fresh session, a lightly-primed session, a heavily-conditioned session, or after an unrelated primer yields outputs that look similar while carrying very different evidentiary weight. So the system must model derivation conditions as provenance.

docs/PRD.md §1b
memory is state-dependent: preserve the conditions of derivation the same prompt asked in a fresh session, a lightly-primed session, a heavily-conditioned session, or after an unrelated primer yields outputs that *look* similar while carrying very different evidentiary weight. so the system must model derivation conditions as provenance. framing/ontology docs/prd.md §1b

◈ "Independent re-derivation" defined by conditioning, not by transcript count

framing/ontology

A definitional move: independent re-derivation means "arrived here without materially sharing the same conditioning context," not merely "appeared in another transcript." This prevents confusing genuinely convergent insight with restatement under priming or sycophantic context-following.

docs/PRD.md §1b
independent re-derivation defined by conditioning, not by transcript count a definitional move: independent re-derivation means arrived here without materially sharing the same conditioning context, not merely appeared in another transcript. this prevents confusing genuinely convergent insight with restatement under priming or sycophantic context-following. framing/ontology docs/prd.md §1b

◈ Delegation principle: the memory layer never runs an LLM

mechanism

Cognivault holds no LLM API key, ever. The host CLI agent does all extraction and appraisal because (a) it already has the full conversation warm in its head — re-reading a transcript with a fresh model is wasteful and lossy — and (b) the user is already paying for that agent's subscription; a second LLM bill is friction without value. Cognivault is pure plumbing: embeddings, pgvector queries, graph mutations.

docs/PRD.md §3a Principle 1; CLAUDE.md
"If a change implies Cognivault 'thinks,' it's wrong."
delegation principle: the memory layer never runs an llm cognivault holds no llm api key, ever. the host cli agent does all extraction and appraisal because (a) it already has the full conversation warm in its head — re-reading a transcript with a fresh model is wasteful and lossy — and (b) the user is already paying for that agent's subscription; a second llm bill is friction without value. cognivault is pure plumbing: embeddings, pgvector queries, graph mutations. mechanism docs/prd.md §3a principle 1; claude.md if a change implies cognivault 'thinks,' it's wrong.

◈ Delegation and daemonization compose

framing/ontology

The two load-bearing principles reinforce each other: because Cognivault doesn't run an LLM, the daemon's per-request work is bounded (one embedding + one SQL query), making strict per-endpoint timeouts tractable; because the daemon is long-lived, the thin hooks pay no cold-start cost and can enforce tight deadlines.

docs/PRD.md §3a
delegation and daemonization compose the two load-bearing principles reinforce each other: because cognivault doesn't run an llm, the daemon's per-request work is bounded (one embedding + one sql query), making strict per-endpoint timeouts tractable; because the daemon is long-lived, the thin hooks pay no cold-start cost and can enforce tight deadlines. framing/ontology docs/prd.md §3a

◈ Embeddings: the one allowed external call, isolated to one file

mechanism

pgvector can't function without an embedding model and no agent CLI exposes embedding generation as a tool, so OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (1536d) is the single sanctioned external API — isolated to embeddings.py so it can be swapped for Voyage, local sentence-transformers, or Ollama's nomic-embed-text by editing one file. Not in the LLM hot path; doesn't expose user data to a chat model.

docs/PRD.md §3a, §7.1; CLAUDE.md
embeddings: the one allowed external call, isolated to one file pgvector can't function without an embedding model and no agent cli exposes embedding generation as a tool, so openai `text-embedding-3-small` (1536d) is the single sanctioned external api — isolated to `embeddings.py` so it can be swapped for voyage, local sentence-transformers, or ollama's `nomic-embed-text` by editing one file. not in the llm hot path; doesn't expose user data to a chat model. mechanism docs/prd.md §3a, §7.1; claude.md

◈ The four-touchpoint integration surface

mechanism

The entire host-agent contract is four touchpoints: (1) UserPromptSubmit hook → POST /recall → "## Relevant memories" block; (2) Stop hook → local regex classification → hint buffer append; (3) explicit /extract-memory while warm → propose/commit REST calls; (4) explicit cognivault consolidate <sid> recovery re-invoking the host agent for one session.

docs/PRD.md §3a, §5
the four-touchpoint integration surface the entire host-agent contract is four touchpoints: (1) userpromptsubmit hook → post /recall → ## relevant memories block; (2) stop hook → local regex classification → hint buffer append; (3) explicit `/extract-memory` while warm → propose/commit rest calls; (4) explicit `cognivault consolidate <sid>` recovery re-invoking the host agent for one session. mechanism docs/prd.md §3a, §5

◈ Never block the host session — fail-open recall

mechanism

Absolute failure posture: if the daemon is down, slow, or erroring, hooks time out client-side, print nothing, and exit 0; the agent keeps moving with an empty block instead of a hang. Every client has a deadline strictly longer than its server endpoint's deadline; missing either yields empty output, never a wait.

docs/PRD.md §3a, §6.3, §6.6b, §7.1; ARCHITECTURE.md §9
never block the host session — fail-open recall absolute failure posture: if the daemon is down, slow, or erroring, hooks time out client-side, print nothing, and exit 0; the agent keeps moving with an empty block instead of a hang. every client has a deadline strictly longer than its server endpoint's deadline; missing either yields empty output, never a wait. mechanism docs/prd.md §3a, §6.3, §6.6b, §7.1; architecture.md §9

◈ The diary metaphor: why extraction never runs per-prompt

metaphor

Writing to long-term memory after every action "is like pausing to update a diary every time you take a step" — you record mostly noise (mid-conversation guesses, half-formed intents, things immediately corrected) and durable signal drowns. Extraction is a reflective activity performed at explicit or safe boundaries.

docs/PRD.md §5
"like pausing to update a diary every time you take a step"
the diary metaphor: why extraction never runs per-prompt writing to long-term memory after every action is like pausing to update a diary every time you take a step — you record mostly noise (mid-conversation guesses, half-formed intents, things immediately corrected) and durable signal drowns. extraction is a *reflective* activity performed at explicit or safe boundaries. metaphor docs/prd.md §5 like pausing to update a diary every time you take a step

◈ Stop-hook signal capture: an attention map, not a write path

mechanism

After each agent turn, a pure-stdlib regex classifier reads the latest (user, assistant) pair and appends hits ({turn_idx, signal_type, excerpt, at}) to ~/.cognivault/buffers/<session_id>.jsonl. No DB, no embeddings, no LLM, no network — it produces cheap "attention pointers" telling later extraction passes which turns to revisit.

docs/PRD.md §5; ARCHITECTURE.md §5.1; CLAUDE.md
stop-hook signal capture: an attention map, not a write path after each agent turn, a pure-stdlib regex classifier reads the latest (user, assistant) pair and appends hits ({turn_idx, signal_type, excerpt, at}) to `~/.cognivault/buffers/<session_id>.jsonl`. no db, no embeddings, no llm, no network — it produces cheap attention pointers telling later extraction passes which turns to revisit. mechanism docs/prd.md §5; architecture.md §5.1; claude.md

◈ Ingest native markdown as gap-filler and cold-start seed

mechanism

POST /ingest/markdown chunks and embeds ~/.claude/*.md / CLAUDE.md files into the vector store. Double duty: day-one cold start (point at existing CLAUDE.md, get a populated graph immediately) and mid-session gap-fill (Claude Code already writes its own "what I just learned" markdown mid-session; ingesting it gives coverage between explicit extraction passes without running an extraction loop per prompt). A sync-markdown watcher re-ingests on mtime change.

docs/PRD.md §5, §6.6a
ingest native markdown as gap-filler and cold-start seed `post /ingest/markdown` chunks and embeds `~/.claude/*.md` / `claude.md` files into the vector store. double duty: day-one cold start (point at existing claude.md, get a populated graph immediately) and mid-session gap-fill (claude code already writes its own what i just learned markdown mid-session; ingesting it gives coverage between explicit extraction passes without running an extraction loop per prompt). a `sync-markdown` watcher re-ingests on mtime change. mechanism docs/prd.md §5, §6.6a

◈ The cleanest division of labor across three memory layers

framing/ontology

Native markdown handles mid-session capture (cheap, already happening, agent-controlled); the stop-hook buffer plus explicit extraction handles reflective consolidation; ingest-markdown is the bridge pulling the markdown layer into the vector/graph layer without duplicating extraction work.

docs/PRD.md §6.6a
the cleanest division of labor across three memory layers native markdown handles mid-session capture (cheap, already happening, agent-controlled); the stop-hook buffer plus explicit extraction handles reflective consolidation; `ingest-markdown` is the bridge pulling the markdown layer into the vector/graph layer without duplicating extraction work. framing/ontology docs/prd.md §6.6a

◈ Five archetypal use cases for agent memory

product

(1) Preference recall — "Stop suggesting Pip — I told you last week we use Poetry" never happens again; (2) procedural reinforcement — commit-message style learned once, applied forever; (3) edge-case knowledge — "SQLAlchemy 2.0 style except in legacy/"; (4) contradiction flagging — a changed convention supersedes instead of competing; (5) research synthesis recall — resurface the prior primer, strongest fresh derivation, and one independent cross-model derivation, not the most topically similar old note.

docs/PRD.md §4
five archetypal use cases for agent memory (1) preference recall — stop suggesting pip — i told you last week we use poetry never happens again; (2) procedural reinforcement — commit-message style learned once, applied forever; (3) edge-case knowledge — sqlalchemy 2.0 style *except* in legacy/ ; (4) contradiction flagging — a changed convention supersedes instead of competing; (5) research synthesis recall — resurface the prior primer, strongest fresh derivation, and one independent cross-model derivation, not the most topically similar old note. product docs/prd.md §4

◈ The memories table: a maximal single-node schema

mechanism

One memories table carrying content + embedding + type + learning_method + source + strength + importance + confidence + tags[] + related_entities[] + superseded_by + three timestamps + access_count + reinforcement_count + scope + industry + domain + business_function + project_id + workspace_id + originating_session_id + source_excerpt. Every cognitive attribute from the origin brainstorm becomes a queryable column.

docs/PRD.md §6.1
the memories table: a maximal single-node schema one `memories` table carrying content + embedding + type + learning_method + source + strength + importance + confidence + tags[] + related_entities[] + superseded_by + three timestamps + access_count + reinforcement_count + scope + industry + domain + business_function + project_id + workspace_id + originating_session_id + source_excerpt. every cognitive attribute from the origin brainstorm becomes a queryable column. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1

◈ Controlled sector vocabularies: industry / domain / business_function

mechanism

Three orthogonal classification axes set by the LLM at extraction time from fixed vocabularies (industry: fintech…other; domain: frontend…other; business_function: engineering…other), NULL when agnostic. Explicitly disambiguated: domain is the tech layer, business_function is the org unit that owns the work.

docs/PRD.md §6.1
controlled sector vocabularies: industry / domain / business_function three orthogonal classification axes set by the llm at extraction time from fixed vocabularies (industry: fintech…other; domain: frontend…other; business_function: engineering…other), null when agnostic. explicitly disambiguated: `domain` is the *tech layer*, `business_function` is the *org unit that owns the work*. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1

◈ superseded_by: the one sanctioned denormalization

mechanism

All graph relationships live in the edges table except superseded_by, kept inline on memories so a partial index (WHERE superseded_by IS NULL) can exclude retired rows in a single hop without a join. Retired memories stay in the table for audit but are invisible to recall; recall transparently follows the pointer to the newer memory.

docs/PRD.md §6.1
superseded_by: the one sanctioned denormalization all graph relationships live in the edges table *except* `superseded_by`, kept inline on `memories` so a partial index (`where superseded_by is null`) can exclude retired rows in a single hop without a join. retired memories stay in the table for audit but are invisible to recall; recall transparently follows the pointer to the newer memory. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1

◈ Inline edge columns rejected: no two sources of truth

dead-end

An earlier draft carried reinforces / nuance_of / contradicts as columns on memories; this was dropped because it duplicates the edges table. Canonical graph representation is memory_edges only.

docs/PRD.md §6.1 "Canonical graph representation"
inline edge columns rejected: no two sources of truth an earlier draft carried `reinforces` / `nuance_of` / `contradicts` as columns on `memories`; this was dropped because it duplicates the edges table. canonical graph representation is `memory_edges` only. dead-end docs/prd.md §6.1 canonical graph representation

◈ memory_revisions: STRENGTHEN is never destructive

mechanism

Every STRENGTHEN snapshots the target's prior content into a memory_revisions table keyed by (memory_id, revision_at) before rewriting it with the merged phrasing — a full audit trail making every merge reversible (cognivault undo).

docs/PRD.md §6.1, §6.4, §9
memory_revisions: strengthen is never destructive every strengthen snapshots the target's prior `content` into a `memory_revisions` table keyed by (memory_id, revision_at) before rewriting it with the merged phrasing — a full audit trail making every merge reversible (`cognivault undo`). mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1, §6.4, §9

◈ Index design as recall semantics

mechanism

The index set encodes retrieval behavior: ivfflat/hnsw cosine on embedding, B-tree on (scope, project_id, type), B-tree on (industry, domain, business_function) for cross-project sector recall ("every backend fintech engineering learning") without full scans, partial embedding index excluding superseded rows, GIN on tags, edge-table indexes for spreading activation, evidence index for constellation breadcrumbs.

docs/PRD.md §6.1
index design as recall semantics the index set encodes retrieval behavior: ivfflat/hnsw cosine on embedding, b-tree on (scope, project_id, type), b-tree on (industry, domain, business_function) for cross-project sector recall ( every backend fintech engineering learning ) without full scans, partial embedding index excluding superseded rows, gin on tags, edge-table indexes for spreading activation, evidence index for constellation breadcrumbs. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1

◈ Cosine similarity is the wrong primary signal for associative recall

framing/ontology

Pure cosine reliably surfaces topically-adjacent rows — which in practice duplicate what's already loaded from CLAUDE.md, i.e. zero marginal information. Cognivault's job is the opposite: the unbidden flash of a sparse, rarely-encountered learning the agent wouldn't otherwise have in hand. So similarity is just one term in a composed score.

docs/PRD.md §6.1a
"Cognivault's job is the opposite: the unbidden flash of a sparse, rarely-encountered learning that the agent would not otherwise have in hand."
cosine similarity is the wrong primary signal for associative recall pure cosine reliably surfaces *topically-adjacent* rows — which in practice duplicate what's already loaded from claude.md, i.e. zero marginal information. cognivault's job is the opposite: the unbidden flash of a *sparse, rarely-encountered* learning the agent wouldn't otherwise have in hand. so similarity is just one term in a composed score. framing/ontology docs/prd.md §6.1a cognivault's job is the opposite: the unbidden flash of a sparse, rarely-encountered learning that the agent would not otherwise have in hand.

◈ The composed recall scorer

mechanism

final = (similarity + sector_boost + 0.15·(importance/10) + 0.10·strength) · confidence. Sector boost is +0.05 per matching axis (soft, never a hard filter); importance lets a weighty lesson clear the bar at low topical similarity — "mimicking how weighty lessons pop into human recall on weak cues"; strength is entrenchment from reinforcement; confidence multiplies the whole thing down for hedged memories.

docs/PRD.md §6.1a
the composed recall scorer `final = (similarity + sector_boost + 0.15·(importance/10) + 0.10·strength) · confidence`. sector boost is +0.05 per matching axis (soft, never a hard filter); importance lets a weighty lesson clear the bar at low topical similarity — mimicking how weighty lessons pop into human recall on weak cues ; strength is entrenchment from reinforcement; confidence multiplies the whole thing down for hedged memories. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1a

◈ Confidence as multiplicative demotion — felt certainty modeled

mechanism

A low-confidence memory can still surface but is proportionally deprioritized, deliberately "matching the felt difference between 'I'm sure about this' and 'I think I remember…'". Confidence is authored (by the agent at extraction), never reinforced by the decay job.

docs/PRD.md §6.1a
confidence as multiplicative demotion — felt certainty modeled a low-confidence memory can still surface but is proportionally deprioritized, deliberately matching the felt difference between 'i'm sure about this' and 'i think i remember…' . confidence is authored (by the agent at extraction), never reinforced by the decay job. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1a

◈ The relevance floor: drop, never pad

mechanism

Hits scoring below RELEVANCE_FLOOR (0.55) are dropped entirely rather than padded into top-k — "better to return three strong memories than eight diluted ones, better still to return zero than to pollute the context with noise." This restraint behavior is called out as the key difference from a raw vector DB.

docs/PRD.md §6.1a
the relevance floor: drop, never pad hits scoring below relevance_floor (0.55) are dropped entirely rather than padded into top-k — better to return three strong memories than eight diluted ones, better still to return zero than to pollute the context with noise. this restraint behavior is called out as *the* key difference from a raw vector db. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1a

◈ Edge-case children ride along at a 0.8 discount

mechanism

During recall, edge-case children inherit their parent's score multiplied by 0.8 — so exceptions accompany the rule only when the rule itself was genuinely relevant, never on their own weak merits.

docs/PRD.md §6.1a
edge-case children ride along at a 0.8 discount during recall, edge-case children inherit their parent's score multiplied by 0.8 — so exceptions accompany the rule only when the rule itself was genuinely relevant, never on their own weak merits. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1a

◈ Write-path salience mutation rules

mechanism

STRENGTHEN bumps strength and applies an agent-supplied confidence_delta clamped to ±0.5 (reinforcement can firm up or soften a claim); CONTRADICTION inserts a fresh row at agent-supplied confidence and hard-supersedes the target; NOVEL/EDGE_CASE accept confidence on insert (default 1.0). Decay only ever touches strength — never confidence or importance, which are authored not reinforced.

docs/PRD.md §6.1a
write-path salience mutation rules strengthen bumps strength and applies an agent-supplied `confidence_delta` clamped to ±0.5 (reinforcement can firm up *or soften* a claim); contradiction inserts a fresh row at agent-supplied confidence and hard-supersedes the target; novel/edge_case accept confidence on insert (default 1.0). decay only ever touches strength — never confidence or importance, which are authored not reinforced. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1a

◈ memory_evidence: one insight, many traces

mechanism

A memory row is only the canonical node; a separate memory_evidence table preserves every supporting trace with session_id, source_excerpt, transcript_path, platform (claude_code/grok/ai_studio), model_family, captured_at, evidence_type (direct / independent_derivation / primer / supporting_example), and independence_group (to avoid counting near-duplicates as independent). One semantic insight can be supported by many sessions on different platforms months apart — a single originating_session_id is too lossy.

docs/PRD.md §6.1b; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §3
memory_evidence: one insight, many traces a memory row is only the canonical node; a separate `memory_evidence` table preserves every supporting trace with session_id, source_excerpt, transcript_path, platform (claude_code/grok/ai_studio), model_family, captured_at, evidence_type (direct / independent_derivation / primer / supporting_example), and independence_group (to avoid counting near-duplicates as independent). one semantic insight can be supported by many sessions on different platforms months apart — a single originating_session_id is too lossy. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1b; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §3

◈ Evidence roles: a functional vocabulary for traces in a constellation

mechanism

Each evidence trace records its role in the constellation — primer / trigger / bridge / independent_derivation / support / counterexample / external_artifact — describing the trace's function, not just its type. Plus source_type (session/markdown/manual_note/url/image/other) for what kind of artifact produced it.

docs/PRD.md §6.1b
evidence roles: a functional vocabulary for traces in a constellation each evidence trace records its `role` in the constellation — primer / trigger / bridge / independent_derivation / support / counterexample / external_artifact — describing the trace's *function*, not just its type. plus `source_type` (session/markdown/manual_note/url/image/other) for what kind of artifact produced it. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1b

◈ context_regime: grading the conditioning state of every trace

mechanism

Each evidence trace records the conditioning state the host agent was in when the trace was produced: fresh / lightly_primed / heavily_primed / full_context. This becomes the freshness factor in independence weighting (fresh=1.0 down to full_context=0.1).

docs/PRD.md §6.1b, §6.1c
context_regime: grading the conditioning state of every trace each evidence trace records the conditioning state the host agent was in when the trace was produced: `fresh` / `lightly_primed` / `heavily_primed` / `full_context`. this becomes the freshness factor in independence weighting (fresh=1.0 down to full_context=0.1). mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1b, §6.1c

◈ conditioning_sources + cue_summary: computable overlap, human-readable trigger

mechanism

Each trace stores a jsonb list of the sessions/files/artifacts explicitly loaded into context when it was produced (used to compute Jaccard conditioning-overlap against other traces) and a free-text cue_summary of what activated it (e.g. "Sam Altman tweet", "unrelated photonic-computing walk", "fresh prompt, no prior context").

docs/PRD.md §6.1b
conditioning_sources + cue_summary: computable overlap, human-readable trigger each trace stores a jsonb list of the sessions/files/artifacts explicitly loaded into context when it was produced (used to compute jaccard conditioning-overlap against other traces) and a free-text `cue_summary` of what activated it (e.g. sam altman tweet , unrelated photonic-computing walk , fresh prompt, no prior context ). mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1b

◈ memory_edges: seven typed, weighted relationships

mechanism

The canonical graph table: reinforces, nuances, contradicts, abstracts, derived_from, primes, coactivates — each edge with a 0-1 weight, created_at, and originating_session_id provenance. primes (an earlier unrelated memory raised the probability of a later synthesis) and coactivates (memories that fire together) are the two edge types with no analogue in conventional RAG.

docs/PRD.md §6.1b
memory_edges: seven typed, weighted relationships the canonical graph table: `reinforces`, `nuances`, `contradicts`, `abstracts`, `derived_from`, `primes`, `coactivates` — each edge with a 0-1 weight, created_at, and originating_session_id provenance. `primes` (an earlier unrelated memory raised the probability of a later synthesis) and `coactivates` (memories that fire together) are the two edge types with no analogue in conventional rag. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1b

◈ graph_events: append-only timeline of graph life

mechanism

An append-only event log (memory_created, evidence_attached, memory_accessed, memory_reinforced, memory_revised, memory_superseded, edge_created, edge_weight_updated, with strength/weight deltas) so timeline UIs and audits can replay how node activity and connection weights evolved, instead of inferring history from snapshots. Canonical current state stays in memories/memory_edges.

docs/PRD.md §6.1b
graph_events: append-only timeline of graph life an append-only event log (memory_created, evidence_attached, memory_accessed, memory_reinforced, memory_revised, memory_superseded, edge_created, edge_weight_updated, with strength/weight deltas) so timeline uis and audits can *replay* how node activity and connection weights evolved, instead of inferring history from snapshots. canonical current state stays in memories/memory_edges. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1b

◈ abstraction_level: trace → synthesized → principle

mechanism

Memories carry an abstraction ladder: raw trace, synthesized (a canonical abstraction over multiple traces), and principle (highest tier, requiring manual review to reach). Promotion is evidence-gated, not enthusiasm-gated.

docs/PRD.md §6.1b, §6.4
abstraction_level: trace → synthesized → principle memories carry an abstraction ladder: raw `trace`, `synthesized` (a canonical abstraction over multiple traces), and `principle` (highest tier, requiring manual review to reach). promotion is evidence-gated, not enthusiasm-gated. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1b, §6.4

◈ independence_score replaces a raw derivation counter

mechanism

Deliberate design change: instead of counting independent derivations, store a weighted independence_score — because "not all independent derivations are equally independent; a fresh cross-platform derivation counts far more than a full-context restatement."

docs/PRD.md §6.1b
independence_score replaces a raw derivation counter deliberate design change: instead of counting independent derivations, store a weighted `independence_score` — because not all independent derivations are equally independent; a fresh cross-platform derivation counts far more than a full-context restatement. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1b

◈ latent flag: dormant but structurally load-bearing

mechanism

A boolean marking memories that are inactive by every access metric yet structurally important (evidence for a synthesis, sole bridge, primer) — excluded from pruning even when strength and access_count would qualify them for deletion.

docs/PRD.md §6.1b, §6.5
latent flag: dormant but structurally load-bearing a boolean marking memories that are inactive by every access metric yet structurally important (evidence for a synthesis, sole bridge, primer) — excluded from pruning even when strength and access_count would qualify them for deletion. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1b, §6.5

◈ epistemic_status: keeping brainstorm-grade material out of execution sessions

mechanism

Every memory is tagged preference / policy / procedure / fact / hypothesis / synthesis / open_question at extraction. This distinguishes stable coding conventions ("use Poetry") from speculative research threads ("time might be energy exhaust") so recall can avoid polluting execution sessions with brainstorm-grade material.

docs/PRD.md §6.1b
epistemic_status: keeping brainstorm-grade material out of execution sessions every memory is tagged preference / policy / procedure / fact / hypothesis / synthesis / open_question at extraction. this distinguishes stable coding conventions ( use poetry ) from speculative research threads ( time might be energy exhaust ) so recall can avoid polluting execution sessions with brainstorm-grade material. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1b

◈ task_mode on memories + mode-aware recall

mechanism

Memories record the mode of their originating session (execution / research / brainstorm / debugging / writing / strategy), and recall filters by detected current mode: execution/debugging prefers preference/policy/procedure/fact and demotes speculative categories unless their score is very high; research/brainstorm/strategy unlocks hypothesis/synthesis/open_question at full weight; writing weights synthesis+fact. Mode detection: cwd + recent transcript + optional explicit --mode flag.

docs/PRD.md §6.1b, §6.1c
task_mode on memories + mode-aware recall memories record the mode of their originating session (execution / research / brainstorm / debugging / writing / strategy), and recall filters by detected current mode: execution/debugging prefers preference/policy/procedure/fact and demotes speculative categories unless their score is very high; research/brainstorm/strategy unlocks hypothesis/synthesis/open_question at full weight; writing weights synthesis+fact. mode detection: cwd + recent transcript + optional explicit `--mode` flag. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1b, §6.1c

◈ The dormant-thread guarantee

product

The concrete promise mode-aware recall makes: a speculative "time-as-energy-exhaust" research thread stays dormant during daily coding and reappears exactly when the user is actually thinking about it again.

docs/PRD.md §6.1c "Mode-aware recall"
the dormant-thread guarantee the concrete promise mode-aware recall makes: a speculative time-as-energy-exhaust research thread stays dormant during daily coding and reappears exactly when the user is actually thinking about it again. product docs/prd.md §6.1c mode-aware recall

◈ Recall as spreading activation, not nearest-neighbor

mechanism

Two-stage recall: (1) seed retrieval by vector similarity (+ optional FTS) with scope filter and sector boost; (2) graph activation propagating across nuances/derived_from/abstracts/primes/coactivates edges with damping, promoting a synthesized memory when multiple weakly-activated supporting traces converge on it — even if none individually cleared the relevance floor — and returning the synthesis plus 1-3 supporting evidence traces so the agent sees both abstraction and lineage.

docs/PRD.md §6.1c; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §4
recall as spreading activation, not nearest-neighbor two-stage recall: (1) seed retrieval by vector similarity (+ optional fts) with scope filter and sector boost; (2) graph activation propagating across nuances/derived_from/abstracts/primes/coactivates edges *with damping*, promoting a synthesized memory when multiple weakly-activated supporting traces converge on it — even if none individually cleared the relevance floor — and returning the synthesis plus 1-3 supporting evidence traces so the agent sees both abstraction and lineage. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1c; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §4

◈ The extended five-term score

mechanism

final = base_similarity + sector_boost + salience_bonus + convergence_bonus + graph_activation_bonus — where convergence_bonus is driven by independence_score (not raw repetition, not raw trace count) and graph_activation_bonus comes from spreading activation through the edge graph.

docs/PRD.md §6.1c
the extended five-term score `final = base_similarity + sector_boost + salience_bonus + convergence_bonus + graph_activation_bonus` — where convergence_bonus is driven by independence_score (not raw repetition, not raw trace count) and graph_activation_bonus comes from spreading activation through the edge graph. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1c

◈ The independence-weight formula

mechanism

Per evidence trace: independence_weight = freshness_factor(context_regime) × (1 − conditioning_overlap) × platform_diversity_factor × model_diversity_factor × time_separation_factor (the last a saturating function of Δt); independence_score = Σ over distinct traces. Ten mentions in one session contribute one low-weight trace; two genuinely independent derivations from different platforms months apart with disjoint conditioning dominate; a full-context restatement gets near-zero weight but is retained as role=support.

docs/PRD.md §6.1c
the independence-weight formula per evidence trace: `independence_weight = freshness_factor(context_regime) × (1 − conditioning_overlap) × platform_diversity_factor × model_diversity_factor × time_separation_factor` (the last a saturating function of δt); `independence_score = σ` over distinct traces. ten mentions in one session contribute one low-weight trace; two genuinely independent derivations from different platforms months apart with disjoint conditioning dominate; a full-context restatement gets near-zero weight but is retained as role=support. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1c

◈ Constellation output: breadcrumbs instead of bare notes

mechanism

When recall surfaces a synthesized memory, the formatted block returns the synthesis plus a "why this surfaced" breadcrumb trail assembled from its top evidence traces ordered by role (primer → trigger → bridge → independent_derivation → support) — giving the user the path, not just the note, and making graph-activation recalls legible without a UI. Example block shows "Synthesis: Time-as-energy-exhaust thread / Why now: current prompt matched a dormant synthesis with prior cross-model convergence."

docs/PRD.md §6.1c "Constellation output"
constellation output: breadcrumbs instead of bare notes when recall surfaces a synthesized memory, the formatted block returns the synthesis plus a why this surfaced breadcrumb trail assembled from its top evidence traces ordered by role (primer → trigger → bridge → independent_derivation → support) — giving the user the *path*, not just the note, and making graph-activation recalls legible without a ui. example block shows synthesis: time-as-energy-exhaust thread / why now: current prompt matched a dormant synthesis with prior cross-model convergence. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.1c constellation output

◈ Extraction enums as literals: rejecting free-text drift

mechanism

CreateMemoryInput passes the full controlled vocabulary as enum literals so Pydantic rejects free-text drift like "FinTech", "back-end", or "People Ops" — validation as vocabulary police at the extraction boundary.

docs/PRD.md §6.2
extraction enums as literals: rejecting free-text drift `creatememoryinput` passes the full controlled vocabulary as enum literals so pydantic rejects free-text drift like fintech , back-end , or people ops — validation as vocabulary police at the extraction boundary. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.2

◈ Three-tier scoping solves cwd fragmentation

mechanism

Every memory is scoped global (applies everywhere, e.g. "user dislikes the word 'delve'"), workspace (a related set of repos, e.g. a monorepo's backend+frontend), or project (current cwd only), assigned by the LLM at extraction. Recall filters scope='global' OR (workspace AND workspace_id=?) OR (project AND project_id=?) — explicitly framed as the fix for the cwd-fragmentation problem of an earlier draft.

docs/PRD.md §6.3a "Scoping"
three-tier scoping solves cwd fragmentation every memory is scoped `global` (applies everywhere, e.g. user dislikes the word 'delve' ), `workspace` (a related set of repos, e.g. a monorepo's backend+frontend), or `project` (current cwd only), assigned by the llm at extraction. recall filters `scope='global' or (workspace and workspace_id=?) or (project and project_id=?)` — explicitly framed as the fix for the cwd-fragmentation problem of an earlier draft. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.3a scoping

◈ Sector recall makes procedural knowledge transfer across projects

mechanism

The current session's detected industry/domain/business_function (inferred from cwd + recent transcript, cached in session_context.json) soft-boosts matching memories above equally-similar ones from other sectors — even project-scoped memories from different projects. The promise: a payments-flow gotcha learned on client_a surfaces when you start client_b in the same industry; a "how PMs like PRDs structured" memory follows you across every product task regardless of stack.

docs/PRD.md §6.3a "Sector recall"
sector recall makes procedural knowledge transfer across projects the current session's detected industry/domain/business_function (inferred from cwd + recent transcript, cached in session_context.json) soft-boosts matching memories above equally-similar ones from other sectors — even project-scoped memories from *different* projects. the promise: a payments-flow gotcha learned on client_a surfaces when you start client_b in the same industry; a how pms like prds structured memory follows you across every product task regardless of stack. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.3a sector recall

◈ Hybrid retrieval v1.1: vector + Postgres FTS for exact-phrase cold-start

mechanism

Vector similarity cold-starts on exact-phrase facts embeddings represent poorly — literal API-key prefixes, error-message strings, git refs. Planned follow-up: merge pgvector hits with Postgres full-text-search hits at 0.7/0.3 weights (same DB, no new dependency), then sector-boost as usual. Pattern explicitly borrowed from Hermes Agent's dual-layer SQLite FTS5 model.

docs/PRD.md §6.3a "Hybrid retrieval (v1.1)"
hybrid retrieval v1.1: vector + postgres fts for exact-phrase cold-start vector similarity cold-starts on exact-phrase facts embeddings represent poorly — literal api-key prefixes, error-message strings, git refs. planned follow-up: merge pgvector hits with postgres full-text-search hits at 0.7/0.3 weights (same db, no new dependency), then sector-boost as usual. pattern explicitly borrowed from hermes agent's dual-layer sqlite fts5 model. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.3a hybrid retrieval (v1.1)

◈ Cache discipline: freeze the baseline recall block per session

mechanism

The recall block computed on the first user prompt is frozen as the session baseline (stored in session_context.json) and reused verbatim on subsequent prompts — preserving the host model's prompt-cache window so every later turn pays the cached-token rate. Borrowed from Hermes Agent's freeze-at-session-start pattern.

docs/PRD.md §6.3a "Cache discipline"; ARCHITECTURE.md §4.2
cache discipline: freeze the baseline recall block per session the recall block computed on the first user prompt is frozen as the session baseline (stored in session_context.json) and reused verbatim on subsequent prompts — preserving the host model's prompt-cache window so every later turn pays the cached-token rate. borrowed from hermes agent's freeze-at-session-start pattern. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.3a cache discipline ; architecture.md §4.2

◈ Delta recall: additive supplements on topic shift

mechanism

Mid-session, a small supplementary memory block is appended below the frozen baseline when (a) the semantic centroid of the last N user turns diverges from the baseline embedding beyond a threshold, (b) a new entity cluster appears in the transcript, or (c) the user explicitly pivots ("different question", "switching gears", /clear-adjacent language). Baseline stays frozen for cache reuse; deltas are additive — the practical compromise between prompt-cache cost and non-linear recall.

docs/PRD.md §6.3a "Cache discipline"; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §6; ARCHITECTURE.md §4.3
delta recall: additive supplements on topic shift mid-session, a small supplementary memory block is appended below the frozen baseline when (a) the semantic centroid of the last n user turns diverges from the baseline embedding beyond a threshold, (b) a new entity cluster appears in the transcript, or (c) the user explicitly pivots ( different question , switching gears , /clear-adjacent language). baseline stays frozen for cache reuse; deltas are additive — the practical compromise between prompt-cache cost and non-linear recall. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.3a cache discipline ; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §6; architecture.md §4.3

◈ The first-prompt assumption is wrong — and the review caught it

result/finding

The PRD review flags that caching the recall block from the first prompt "conceptually assumes the first prompt defines the session's relevant memory set," which contradicts the constellation evidence that later cues activate different memories. The fix (delta recall) keeps the cache but drops the assumption.

docs/20260415-prd_review.md §6
the first-prompt assumption is wrong — and the review caught it the prd review flags that caching the recall block from the first prompt conceptually assumes the first prompt defines the session's relevant memory set, which contradicts the constellation evidence that later cues activate different memories. the fix (delta recall) keeps the cache but drops the assumption. result/finding docs/20260415-prd_review.md §6

◈ Two-phase write: propose returns neighbors, commit executes the verdict

mechanism

REST consolidation flow: POST /memories/propose runs the security scan, embeds, runs the same scope+sector recall limited to top-5, and returns neighbors; the agent reads them, chooses a ConsolidationAction, and POST /memories/commit executes it. The LLM decides; the daemon mutates.

docs/PRD.md §6.4
two-phase write: propose returns neighbors, commit executes the verdict rest consolidation flow: `post /memories/propose` runs the security scan, embeds, runs the same scope+sector recall limited to top-5, and returns neighbors; the agent reads them, chooses a consolidationaction, and `post /memories/commit` executes it. the llm decides; the daemon mutates. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.4

◈ The seven-action consolidation vocabulary

mechanism

Commit accepts novel (insert), strengthen (snapshot revision, merge content, bump strength/reinforcement), edge_case (insert + nuances edge + "WHEN…" prefix + parent importance bump), contradiction (insert + contradicts edge + hard supersession), abstract (create/update a synthesized higher-order memory, wiring abstracts/derived_from edges and seeding its evidence from source traces), link_derivation (attach a new independent-derivation evidence trace with fresh independence_group without touching content), and prime (record a low-weight primes edge from an earlier unrelated memory to a later synthesis).

docs/PRD.md §6.4; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §5
the seven-action consolidation vocabulary commit accepts `novel` (insert), `strengthen` (snapshot revision, merge content, bump strength/reinforcement), `edge_case` (insert + nuances edge + when… prefix + parent importance bump), `contradiction` (insert + contradicts edge + hard supersession), `abstract` (create/update a synthesized higher-order memory, wiring abstracts/derived_from edges and seeding its evidence from source traces), `link_derivation` (attach a new independent-derivation evidence trace with fresh independence_group without touching content), and `prime` (record a low-weight primes edge from an earlier unrelated memory to a later synthesis). mechanism docs/prd.md §6.4; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §5

◈ The strengthen-vs-link_derivation rule: don't collapse lineage

mechanism

Explicit anti-pattern rule: strengthen is only for repeated/clarified versions within one coherent line of thought; independent cross-session re-derivations (different session, platform, or substantial time gap) must use link_derivation, optionally followed by abstract. Using strengthen as a catch-all "collapses lineage that convergence_bonus needs to score correctly."

docs/PRD.md §6.4; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §5
the strengthen-vs-link_derivation rule: don't collapse lineage explicit anti-pattern rule: `strengthen` is only for repeated/clarified versions within one coherent line of thought; independent cross-session re-derivations (different session, platform, or substantial time gap) must use `link_derivation`, optionally followed by `abstract`. using strengthen as a catch-all collapses lineage that convergence_bonus needs to score correctly. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.4; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §5

◈ Promotion gate: ≥2 independent evidence paths before "synthesized"

mechanism

A memory may only be promoted to abstraction_level=synthesized with at least two distinct independence_group values in its evidence — preventing "a premature canonical node on the strength of a single session's enthusiasm." Promotion to principle additionally requires manual review; undo can demote a synthesis back to its constituent traces.

docs/PRD.md §6.4, §9
promotion gate: ≥2 independent evidence paths before synthesized a memory may only be promoted to abstraction_level=synthesized with at least two distinct independence_group values in its evidence — preventing a premature canonical node on the strength of a single session's enthusiasm. promotion to `principle` additionally requires manual review; `undo` can demote a synthesis back to its constituent traces. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.4, §9

◈ consolidation_log: every memory traceable to the decision that made it

mechanism

Both write endpoints log every call — candidate text, neighbors considered, action taken, resulting row ID, originating session — so "any weird memory can be traced back to the exact session and decision that produced it." Observability of the write path is a first-class schema concern.

docs/PRD.md §6.4
consolidation_log: every memory traceable to the decision that made it both write endpoints log every call — candidate text, neighbors considered, action taken, resulting row id, originating session — so any weird memory can be traced back to the exact session and decision that produced it. observability of the write path is a first-class schema concern. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.4

◈ Per-session write caps bound the blast radius

mechanism

Hard daemon-enforced limits per originating session: 10 candidates extracted, 10 NOVEL inserts, 10 STRENGTHENs (mutating rows is riskier than inserting — capped independently), 5 CONTRADICTIONs ("never let one session retire more than a handful of memories"), 10 edge cases. Exists to bound runaway extraction or a compromised session; borrowed from tyxter-worker's firmware-suggestion rate limits.

docs/PRD.md §6.4 "Per-session caps"
per-session write caps bound the blast radius hard daemon-enforced limits per originating session: 10 candidates extracted, 10 novel inserts, 10 strengthens (mutating rows is riskier than inserting — capped independently), 5 contradictions ( never let one session retire more than a handful of memories ), 10 edge cases. exists to bound runaway extraction or a compromised session; borrowed from tyxter-worker's firmware-suggestion rate limits. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.4 per-session caps

◈ Ebbinghaus decay with structural immunity

mechanism
