A nightly job applies strength *= exp(-days_since_access / half_life) and hard-deletes rows with strength<0.05 AND importance≤3 AND access_count==0 — but never deletes a memory that: is a nuance_of a living parent, is evidence for a surviving synthesis, is the only bridge between two live clusters (removal would disconnect the subgraph), is a primer for a later high-importance memory, or contributes independent derivations to any surviving abstraction.

docs/PRD.md §6.5; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §7
ebbinghaus decay with structural immunity a nightly job applies `strength *= exp(-days_since_access / half_life)` and hard-deletes rows with strength<0.05 and importance≤3 and access_count==0 — but never deletes a memory that: is a nuance_of a living parent, is evidence for a surviving synthesis, is the only bridge between two live clusters (removal would disconnect the subgraph), is a primer for a later high-importance memory, or contributes independent derivations to any surviving abstraction. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.5; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §7

◈ "Dormant is not the same as useless"

framing/ontology

The decay-protection principle in one line: some memories matter because they complete a future path, not because they are frequently accessed in the present. Access-frequency-driven pruning is structurally wrong for constellation memory.

docs/PRD.md §6.5; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §7
"Dormant is not the same as useless. Some memories matter because they complete a future path, not because they are frequently accessed in the present."
dormant is not the same as useless the decay-protection principle in one line: some memories matter because they complete a *future* path, not because they are frequently accessed in the present. access-frequency-driven pruning is structurally wrong for constellation memory. framing/ontology docs/prd.md §6.5; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §7 dormant is not the same as useless. some memories matter because they complete a future path, not because they are frequently accessed in the present.

◈ The full CLI surface as product design

product

A deliberately small verb set: init, serve, daemon install/start/stop/status, recall (hook), on-stop (hook), consolidate <sid> [--transcript-path], decay [--offline], inspect [--project], inspect --recent-decisions / --rejections / --partial / --revisions, search "<query>" (with --industry/--domain/--function filters), forget <id>, undo <memory_id> (revert a STRENGTHEN from revisions), ingest-markdown <path>, sync-markdown. Everything except serve/init/daemon/decay-offline is a thin client.

docs/PRD.md §6.6; CLAUDE.md
the full cli surface as product design a deliberately small verb set: `init`, `serve`, `daemon install/start/stop/status`, `recall` (hook), `on-stop` (hook), `consolidate <sid> [--transcript-path]`, `decay [--offline]`, `inspect [--project]`, `inspect --recent-decisions / --rejections / --partial / --revisions`, `search <query> ` (with --industry/--domain/--function filters), `forget <id>`, `undo <memory_id>` (revert a strengthen from revisions), `ingest-markdown <path>`, `sync-markdown`. everything except serve/init/daemon/decay-offline is a thin client. product docs/prd.md §6.6; claude.md

◈ Loopback-only bind + shared-secret token file

mechanism

Daemon binds 127.0.0.1:8765 and rejects non-loopback binds unless --allow-remote; init generates ~/.cognivault/daemon.token with 0600 perms (startup refuses if perms loosen), sent as X-Cognivault-Token on every request — defense-in-depth so a hostile local process can't scrape memories just by knowing the port. Rotation via daemon rotate-token.

docs/PRD.md §6.6b, §9
loopback-only bind + shared-secret token file daemon binds 127.0.0.1:8765 and *rejects* non-loopback binds unless `--allow-remote`; `init` generates `~/.cognivault/daemon.token` with 0600 perms (startup refuses if perms loosen), sent as `x-cognivault-token` on every request — defense-in-depth so a hostile local process can't scrape memories just by knowing the port. rotation via `daemon rotate-token`. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.6b, §9

◈ Single-worker daemon by reasoning, not default

mechanism

uvicorn runs with exactly one worker because the daemon is I/O-bound and single-tenant — extra workers "give nothing and complicate the in-process session cache." Process-model minimalism as an explicit decision.

docs/PRD.md §6.6b
single-worker daemon by reasoning, not default uvicorn runs with exactly one worker because the daemon is i/o-bound and single-tenant — extra workers give nothing and complicate the in-process session cache. process-model minimalism as an explicit decision. mechanism docs/prd.md §6.6b

◈ Nested-deadline graceful degradation

mechanism

Every endpoint has a server-side deadline; every client has a deadline strictly longer than its server's. Server blowing its deadline returns 503; clients print nothing on 503 and exit 0. Latency budgets: /recall p95 <300ms server-side, 400ms client deadline (100ms left for process start + stdio plumbing); on-stop p95 <10ms; consolidate unbounded (off the hot path).

docs/PRD.md §6.3, §6.6b
nested-deadline graceful degradation every endpoint has a server-side deadline; every client has a deadline strictly longer than its server's. server blowing its deadline returns 503; clients print nothing on 503 and exit 0. latency budgets: /recall p95 <300ms server-side, 400ms client deadline (100ms left for process start + stdio plumbing); on-stop p95 <10ms; consolidate unbounded (off the hot path). mechanism docs/prd.md §6.3, §6.6b

◈ The memory store is a persistent cross-session prompt-injection surface

framing/ontology

Threat model: the memory store is "a write path the LLM controls" — an adversarial or compromised session can inject content that, once written, will be retrieved into every future session on matching topics. The (in-universe) OpenClaw 2025 supply-chain incident is cited as evidence memory layers are a real attack surface.

docs/PRD.md §7.2
the memory store is a persistent cross-session prompt-injection surface threat model: the memory store is a write path the llm controls — an adversarial or compromised session can inject content that, once written, will be retrieved into *every future session* on matching topics. the (in-universe) openclaw 2025 supply-chain incident is cited as evidence memory layers are a real attack surface. framing/ontology docs/prd.md §7.2

◈ Four-category pre-write content scanner

mechanism

Every candidate learning passes a pure-stdlib, microsecond scan_memory_value(content) before INSERT/STRENGTHEN, rejecting: (1) prompt injection ("ignore previous instructions", fake system: role markers, chat-template tags, persona overrides); (2) credential leakage (sk-/pk-/ak-/rk- keys, AWS AKIA*, GitHub PATs, sk-ant-, password assignments, PEM blocks) — which would otherwise be exfiltrated on recall into another session; (3) invisible Unicode (20 codepoints: zero-width chars, BOM, RTL/LTR overrides, variation selectors) as steganographic injection; (4) oversized base64 blobs (≥100 chars decoding to >4KiB) hiding binary payloads. Borrowed wholesale from tyxter-worker's memory_security module.

docs/PRD.md §7.2
four-category pre-write content scanner every candidate learning passes a pure-stdlib, microsecond `scan_memory_value(content)` before insert/strengthen, rejecting: (1) prompt injection ( ignore previous instructions , fake system: role markers, chat-template tags, persona overrides); (2) credential leakage (sk-/pk-/ak-/rk- keys, aws akia*, github pats, sk-ant-, password assignments, pem blocks) — which would otherwise be *exfiltrated on recall into another session*; (3) invisible unicode (20 codepoints: zero-width chars, bom, rtl/ltr overrides, variation selectors) as steganographic injection; (4) oversized base64 blobs (≥100 chars decoding to >4kib) hiding binary payloads. borrowed wholesale from tyxter-worker's memory_security module. mechanism docs/prd.md §7.2

◈ Fail-closed on write, fail-open on read — the one inversion

framing/ontology

Cognivault fails open everywhere (never block the session) except the security scan, which fails closed by dropping the candidate: "memory poisoning is silent and persistent; a missed STRENGTHEN is recoverable on the next session." Rejections are logged, other candidates continue, and inspect --rejections surfaces them. The scan is write-side one-way; a future audit-existing command can re-scan the whole table with new patterns and quarantine matches.

docs/PRD.md §7.2
fail-closed on write, fail-open on read — the one inversion cognivault fails open everywhere (never block the session) *except* the security scan, which fails closed by dropping the candidate: memory poisoning is silent and persistent; a missed strengthen is recoverable on the next session. rejections are logged, other candidates continue, and `inspect --rejections` surfaces them. the scan is write-side one-way; a future `audit-existing` command can re-scan the whole table with new patterns and quarantine matches. framing/ontology docs/prd.md §7.2

◈ Success metrics that measure "feels like learning"

experiment/benchmark

The metric set mixes hard and soft: agent stops re-suggesting ≥3 previously-given corrections after 2 weeks; memory-count growth sub-linear vs session count (STRENGTHEN > NOVEL as proof consolidation works); p95 recall <400ms; zero session-breaking failures; at least one surfaced memory whose evidence spans >30 days; independent derivations preserved as separate paths not flattened; at least one recall triggered by graph convergence rather than topical similarity alone; and the explicitly subjective "Diego says 'this thing actually feels like it's learning.'"

docs/PRD.md §8; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §8
success metrics that measure feels like learning the metric set mixes hard and soft: agent stops re-suggesting ≥3 previously-given corrections after 2 weeks; memory-count growth sub-linear vs session count (strengthen > novel as proof consolidation works); p95 recall <400ms; zero session-breaking failures; at least one surfaced memory whose evidence spans >30 days; independent derivations preserved as separate paths not flattened; at least one recall triggered by graph convergence rather than topical similarity alone; and the explicitly subjective diego says 'this thing actually feels like it's learning.' experiment/benchmark docs/prd.md §8; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §8

◈ The extraction prompt is the highest-leverage component

open-question

Named risk: "what counts as a learning?" — over-extraction produces noise, under-extraction misses signal, and the extraction prompt is identified as the single highest-leverage piece to iterate on.

docs/PRD.md §9
the extraction prompt is the highest-leverage component named risk: what counts as a learning? — over-extraction produces noise, under-extraction misses signal, and the extraction prompt is identified as the single highest-leverage piece to iterate on. open-question docs/prd.md §9

◈ Embedding model lock-in mitigated by per-row model names + lazy re-embedding

mechanism

Changing embedding models invalidates every stored vector; mitigation is to store the embedding model name per row and support lazy re-embedding rather than a big-bang migration.

docs/PRD.md §9
embedding model lock-in mitigated by per-row model names + lazy re-embedding changing embedding models invalidates every stored vector; mitigation is to store the embedding model name per row and support lazy re-embedding rather than a big-bang migration. mechanism docs/prd.md §9

◈ Priming vs coincidence: the noisiest inference in the system

open-question

Open question: detecting whether an earlier unrelated session genuinely primed a later insight is inherently noisy — co-occurrence within a recall activation is weak evidence. Mitigations: primes edges start at low default weight, a primes-only path can never clear the relevance floor alone, and candidate priming links surface via inspect --recent-decisions for manual confirmation before accumulating influence.

docs/PRD.md §9; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §8
priming vs coincidence: the noisiest inference in the system open question: detecting whether an earlier unrelated session *genuinely* primed a later insight is inherently noisy — co-occurrence within a recall activation is weak evidence. mitigations: primes edges start at low default weight, a primes-only path can never clear the relevance floor alone, and candidate priming links surface via `inspect --recent-decisions` for manual confirmation before accumulating influence. open-question docs/prd.md §9; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §8

◈ Over-abstraction: premature canonical nodes collapse distinct ideas

open-question

Open question: the system may promote synthesized memories too early, merging ideas that should stay distinct. Mitigations: the ≥2-independent-paths promotion gate, manual review for principle, and undo demotion back to constituent traces.

docs/PRD.md §9; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §8
over-abstraction: premature canonical nodes collapse distinct ideas open question: the system may promote synthesized memories too early, merging ideas that should stay distinct. mitigations: the ≥2-independent-paths promotion gate, manual review for `principle`, and `undo` demotion back to constituent traces. open-question docs/prd.md §9; docs/20260415-prd_review.md §8

◈ False causal linking: temporal adjacency isn't priming

open-question

Distinct from priming-vs-coincidence: the system may infer that an earlier trace "primed" a later synthesis when the relation is only temporal adjacency. Mitigation: primes edges cannot seed recall on their own and require either manual confirmation or repeated co-activation across multiple recall events before materially influencing ranking.

docs/PRD.md §9
false causal linking: temporal adjacency isn't priming distinct from priming-vs-coincidence: the system may infer that an earlier trace primed a later synthesis when the relation is only temporal adjacency. mitigation: primes edges cannot seed recall on their own and require either manual confirmation or *repeated co-activation across multiple recall events* before materially influencing ranking. open-question docs/prd.md §9

◈ Silent degradation: the fail-open trap and its stderr warning

mechanism

Because hooks fail open, a user could run for days with the daemon crashed and never see recall blocks — blaming "the memory thing not working." Mitigation: a one-line "⚠ cognivault daemon unreachable" warning printed to stderr (stdout stays clean so agent context is untouched) on the first failed hook of a session, rate-limited to once per session_id, plus daemon status and /health surfacing.

docs/PRD.md §9
silent degradation: the fail-open trap and its stderr warning because hooks fail open, a user could run for days with the daemon crashed and never see recall blocks — blaming the memory thing not working. mitigation: a one-line ⚠ cognivault daemon unreachable warning printed to *stderr* (stdout stays clean so agent context is untouched) on the first failed hook of a session, rate-limited to once per session_id, plus `daemon status` and `/health` surfacing. mechanism docs/prd.md §9

◈ Session state is cheap to rebuild — daemon restarts are safe mid-session

mechanism

Restarting/upgrading the daemon mid-session drops the in-process cache, but the next UserPromptSubmit repopulates it from the durable session_context.json file; an in-flight request during restart fails closed client-side and the next succeeds. Statelessness-by-rebuildability as an operational property.

docs/PRD.md §9
session state is cheap to rebuild — daemon restarts are safe mid-session restarting/upgrading the daemon mid-session drops the in-process cache, but the next userpromptsubmit repopulates it from the durable session_context.json file; an in-flight request during restart fails closed client-side and the next succeeds. statelessness-by-rebuildability as an operational property. mechanism docs/prd.md §9

◈ The Obsidian-style live memory graph (stretch)

product

Post-v1 stretch: a local web app rendering the memory graph force-directed à la Obsidian — click any node for content, strength, access history, and the consolidation decisions that shaped it; manually rename/merge/delete/re-link memories; and crucially a live highlight: when cognivault recall fires during a Claude Code session, the matched nodes pulse/glow in the open graph view — "turning memory from invisible plumbing into something you can watch the agent reason over."

docs/PRD.md §10; tyxter-memory/memory/project_cognivault.md
"a real-time 'the agent just remembered this' visualization"
the obsidian-style live memory graph (stretch) post-v1 stretch: a local web app rendering the memory graph force-directed à la obsidian — click any node for content, strength, access history, and the consolidation decisions that shaped it; manually rename/merge/delete/re-link memories; and crucially a **live highlight**: when `cognivault recall` fires during a claude code session, the matched nodes pulse/glow in the open graph view — turning memory from invisible plumbing into something you can watch the agent reason over. product docs/prd.md §10; tyxter-memory/memory/project_cognivault.md a real-time 'the agent just remembered this' visualization

◈ Windows integration gotchas as documented platform lore

result/finding

Two hard-won operational findings: hook command paths in settings.json must use forward slashes because Claude Code runs hooks through Git Bash, which silently mangles backslashed paths (\U, \D as escapes) into "command not found"; and DATABASE_URL must use 127.0.0.1 rather than localhost to avoid IPv6 lookup hangs on Windows.

docs/PRD.md Appendix A; CLAUDE.md "Platform gotchas"
windows integration gotchas as documented platform lore two hard-won operational findings: hook command paths in settings.json must use forward slashes because claude code runs hooks through git bash, which silently mangles backslashed paths (`\u`, `\d` as escapes) into command not found ; and database_url must use 127.0.0.1 rather than localhost to avoid ipv6 lookup hangs on windows. result/finding docs/prd.md appendix a; claude.md platform gotchas

◈ Stale MCP registration cleanup as migration debt

result/finding

Machines that installed pre-pivot prototypes still have a cognivault mcp entry in ~/.claude.json; the removal command is documented and init no longer prints any MCP-related instruction. Even abandoned transports leave install-base debt.

docs/PRD.md §6.4a, Appendix A
---
stale mcp registration cleanup as migration debt machines that installed pre-pivot prototypes still have a `cognivault mcp` entry in ~/.claude.json; the removal command is documented and `init` no longer prints any mcp-related instruction. even abandoned transports leave install-base debt. result/finding docs/prd.md §6.4a, appendix a ---

D. The PRD review (docs/20260415-prd_review.md) — reframings not already merged above

◈ "Treat memory as constellations, not rows"

framing/ontology

The review's condensed thesis and the conceptual pivot of the whole project: memory is not just "what was similar to the prompt" or "what got reinforced recently" — it is also "what separate, distant, even unrelated traces jointly make legible now," and that deserves explicit representation in the problem statement, schema, consolidation, retrieval, cache behavior, and decay rules.

docs/20260415-prd_review.md "The shortest summary"
"Treat memory as constellations, not rows."
treat memory as constellations, not rows the review's condensed thesis and the conceptual pivot of the whole project: memory is not just what was similar to the prompt or what got reinforced recently — it is also what separate, distant, even unrelated traces jointly make legible now, and that deserves explicit representation in the problem statement, schema, consolidation, retrieval, cache behavior, and decay rules. framing/ontology docs/20260415-prd_review.md the shortest summary treat memory as constellations, not rows.

◈ A README as a memory failure-mode specimen

experiment/benchmark

Methodological move: the me-on-a-random-day README is used as concrete design evidence — the failure mode to design against is not "the system forgot a preference" but "the system flattened a multi-path, cross-time synthesis into isolated notes." A lived ideation chain becomes the acceptance test for a memory architecture.

docs/20260415-prd_review.md §intro; docs/learning_from_memories.md
a readme as a memory failure-mode specimen methodological move: the me-on-a-random-day readme is used as *concrete design evidence* — the failure mode to design against is not the system forgot a preference but the system flattened a multi-path, cross-time synthesis into isolated notes. a lived ideation chain becomes the acceptance test for a memory architecture. experiment/benchmark docs/20260415-prd_review.md §intro; docs/learning_from_memories.md

◈ Repetition vs independence as a schema-level distinction

framing/ontology

The review's key goal addition: repeated mentions in one session must not count the same as independent rediscoveries across sessions, models, or time periods — reinforcement_count alone cannot represent "same conclusion reached by different paths," which is why evidence traces and convergence bookkeeping exist at all.

docs/20260415-prd_review.md §2, §5; docs/insights.md §5
repetition vs independence as a schema-level distinction the review's key goal addition: repeated mentions in one session must not count the same as independent rediscoveries across sessions, models, or time periods — `reinforcement_count` alone cannot represent same conclusion reached by different paths, which is why evidence traces and convergence bookkeeping exist at all. framing/ontology docs/20260415-prd_review.md §2, §5; docs/insights.md §5

◈ "Vector-backed, lineage-aware memory graph" (coinage)

coinage

The review upgrades the project's self-description from "vector-backed, graph-aware memory" to "vector-backed, lineage-aware memory graph" — a small wording change that makes derivation ancestry (not just connectivity) the defining property.

docs/20260415-prd_review.md §8
vector-backed, lineage-aware memory graph (coinage) the review upgrades the project's self-description from vector-backed, graph-aware memory to vector-backed, lineage-aware memory graph — a small wording change that makes derivation ancestry (not just connectivity) the defining property. coinage docs/20260415-prd_review.md §8

◈ The four-levers embryo

framing/ontology

Read together, the review's additions seed what the downstream cognivault-memory project later names its four levers: scope (scope filter + sector boost), co-activation (the coactivates edge), authority/convergence (evidence-independence gating promotion to synthesized abstractions), and antecedent/priming (primes edges + spreading activation). All four exist here in embryo, attached to schema and scoring rather than named as levers.

docs/20260415-prd_review.md §3-§5; docs/PRD.md §6.1b-c (connection noted in the recovered compilation)
---
the four-levers embryo read together, the review's additions seed what the downstream cognivault-memory project later names its four levers: **scope** (scope filter + sector boost), **co-activation** (the coactivates edge), **authority/convergence** (evidence-independence gating promotion to synthesized abstractions), and **antecedent/priming** (primes edges + spreading activation). all four exist here in embryo, attached to schema and scoring rather than named as levers. framing/ontology docs/20260415-prd_review.md §3-§5; docs/prd.md §6.1b-c (connection noted in the recovered compilation) ---

E. Extracted insights on human memory + ideation (docs/insights.md)

◈ The dimensionality of complex ideation is the dimensionality of connection

framing/ontology

Central claim: human ideation behaves like an associative graph, not a linear chain — the power of an idea comes not from one deep thread but from many unrelated threads converging. A final idea may depend on memories, moods, prompts, unrelated conversations, physical context, and prior failed/partial threads that don't look relevant until later.

docs/insights.md §0-1
the dimensionality of complex ideation is the dimensionality of connection central claim: human ideation behaves like an associative graph, not a linear chain — the power of an idea comes not from one deep thread but from many unrelated threads converging. a final idea may depend on memories, moods, prompts, unrelated conversations, physical context, and prior failed/partial threads that don't look relevant until later. framing/ontology docs/insights.md §0-1

◈ Earlier, apparently unrelated context can become load-bearing later

result/finding

The primer conversation (LLM limits + academia) had "zero content" overlap with the time/energy ontology, yet shaped the mental posture — skepticism toward existing frameworks, comfort in sparse conceptual territory, belief in the human creative jump — that made the later synthesis possible.

docs/insights.md §1
earlier, apparently unrelated context can become load-bearing later the primer conversation (llm limits + academia) had zero content overlap with the time/energy ontology, yet shaped the mental posture — skepticism toward existing frameworks, comfort in sparse conceptual territory, belief in the human creative jump — that made the later synthesis possible. result/finding docs/insights.md §1

◈ A memory can matter for the stance it creates, not the facts it contains

framing/ontology

The intellectual seed of the primes edge and the later "antecedent" lever: priming is cognitive state transfer, not content transfer. A previous conversation leaves behind a disposition (more skeptical, more ambitious, more abstract, more willing to challenge defaults) that changes what future prompts produce — and a topical-similarity-only retriever structurally misses it.

docs/insights.md §1-2
"Priming is not just content transfer. It is cognitive state transfer."
a memory can matter for the stance it creates, not the facts it contains the intellectual seed of the primes edge and the later antecedent lever: priming is *cognitive state transfer*, not content transfer. a previous conversation leaves behind a disposition (more skeptical, more ambitious, more abstract, more willing to challenge defaults) that changes what future prompts produce — and a topical-similarity-only retriever structurally misses it. framing/ontology docs/insights.md §1-2 priming is not just content transfer. it is cognitive state transfer.

◈ The walk as an ideation primitive: ideation starts before language

framing/ontology

The originating leap came from embodied, cross-context cognition during a walk — noticing black holes exist but "energy wells" don't. The first valuable object of an idea may not be a clean proposition; it may be "a vague pressure, asymmetry, image, contradiction, or 'this feels connected' moment," with formal language arriving later. Implication: a memory system's earliest traces of an idea may be pre-verbal cues.

docs/insights.md §3
the walk as an ideation primitive: ideation starts before language the originating leap came from embodied, cross-context cognition during a walk — noticing black holes exist but energy wells don't. the first valuable object of an idea may not be a clean proposition; it may be a vague pressure, asymmetry, image, contradiction, or 'this feels connected' moment, with formal language arriving later. implication: a memory system's earliest traces of an idea may be pre-verbal cues. framing/ontology docs/insights.md §3

◈ Ideation is selection pressure, not just generation

framing/ontology

Role split: the machine contributes formalization, structural mapping, independent derivation, and cross-framework comparison; the human contributes cross-contextual leaps, creative seeding into sparse territory, and selection pressure — noticing an anomaly, asking a weird question, preserving a fragile intuition, rejecting median answers, pushing the model into higher-dimensional search, recognizing convergence when it appears.

docs/insights.md §4
"The human does not need to generate every formal component. The human's key role is choosing which strange path is worth continuing."
ideation is selection pressure, not just generation role split: the machine contributes formalization, structural mapping, independent derivation, and cross-framework comparison; the human contributes cross-contextual leaps, creative seeding into sparse territory, and *selection pressure* — noticing an anomaly, asking a weird question, preserving a fragile intuition, rejecting median answers, pushing the model into higher-dimensional search, recognizing convergence when it appears. framing/ontology docs/insights.md §4 the human does not need to generate every formal component. the human's key role is choosing which strange path is worth continuing.

◈ An idea gets stronger when rediscovered through genuinely different paths

framing/ontology

Independent re-derivation carries more evidentiary weight than repetition because it suggests the idea isn't an artifact of one prompt, one model, or one biased context. A memory system should treat "said 3× in one conversation" and "emerged independently across sessions/prompts/models/contexts" as categorically different events.

docs/insights.md §5
an idea gets stronger when rediscovered through genuinely different paths independent re-derivation carries more evidentiary weight than repetition because it suggests the idea isn't an artifact of one prompt, one model, or one biased context. a memory system should treat said 3× in one conversation and emerged independently across sessions/prompts/models/contexts as categorically different events. framing/ontology docs/insights.md §5

◈ Context changes model intelligence: the conditioning loop

result/finding

Observed across sessions: a physics-biased context produced deeper answers; a fresh session gave safer "median human" answers; after critique the model escalated and produced a novel ontology. Model output quality is state-dependent on the user's framing, pressure, and prior context — so working with AI is not "ask → answer" but condition context → probe response → critique failure mode → force abstraction → select useful structure.

docs/insights.md §6
context changes model intelligence: the conditioning loop observed across sessions: a physics-biased context produced deeper answers; a fresh session gave safer median human answers; after critique the model escalated and produced a novel ontology. model output quality is state-dependent on the user's framing, pressure, and prior context — so working with ai is not ask → answer but `condition context → probe response → critique failure mode → force abstraction → select useful structure`. result/finding docs/insights.md §6

◈ For complex ideas, the derivation path is part of the idea

framing/ontology

The project's value comes partly from preserving the full path (primer, tweet, old walk, biased session, fresh session, Claude session, synthesis); the proof-of-work is the prompt/iteration history, not just the final theory. Ancestry reveals which parts were independent, primed, human-originated, model-formalized, and which convergences are meaningful.

docs/insights.md §7, §10
for complex ideas, the derivation path is part of the idea the project's value comes partly from preserving the full path (primer, tweet, old walk, biased session, fresh session, claude session, synthesis); the proof-of-work is the prompt/iteration history, not just the final theory. ancestry reveals which parts were independent, primed, human-originated, model-formalized, and which convergences are meaningful. framing/ontology docs/insights.md §7, §10

◈ Depth alone is insufficient — novel ideation requires dimensionality

framing/ontology

A person can go arbitrarily deep in one field and still miss the insight; the insight appears when separate domains become connectable (collapse theory supplies a stance, a tweet supplies the trigger, a walk supplies the intuition, photonics biases toward physics, sycophancy critique changes interaction style, topology supplies formal structure, the human recognizes it's one path).

docs/insights.md §8
depth alone is insufficient — novel ideation requires dimensionality a person can go arbitrarily deep in one field and still miss the insight; the insight appears when separate domains become connectable (collapse theory supplies a stance, a tweet supplies the trigger, a walk supplies the intuition, photonics biases toward physics, sycophancy critique changes interaction style, topology supplies formal structure, the human recognizes it's one path). framing/ontology docs/insights.md §8

◈ A seven-relation vocabulary for a graph of derivations

framing/ontology

Proposed relation types for notes in a memory system: primed_by (earlier context shaped a later idea without shared content), triggered_by (a cue activated dormant memory), independently_re-derived_as (another session/model reached the same structure via a different path), formalized_by (vague human intuition converted into explicit framework), converges_with (separate derivations map to the same insight), contrasts_with (different mechanisms explain the same target), selected_over (human judgment chose one path as worth preserving). The summarizing formula: memory should be "not a list of facts, but a graph of derivations."

docs/insights.md §9
"not a list of facts, but a graph of derivations"
a seven-relation vocabulary for a graph of derivations proposed relation types for notes in a memory system: **primed_by** (earlier context shaped a later idea without shared content), **triggered_by** (a cue activated dormant memory), **independently_re-derived_as** (another session/model reached the same structure via a different path), **formalized_by** (vague human intuition converted into explicit framework), **converges_with** (separate derivations map to the same insight), **contrasts_with** (different mechanisms explain the same target), **selected_over** (human judgment chose one path as worth preserving). the summarizing formula: memory should be not a list of facts, but a graph of derivations. framing/ontology docs/insights.md §9 not a list of facts, but a graph of derivations

◈ The condensed ideation thesis

framing/ontology

"Human memory is associative, state-dependent, and provenance-sensitive. Human ideation emerges when distant memories, unrelated contexts, and external triggers become connected under selection pressure. AI can expand, formalize, and compare candidate structures, but the human contribution is the cross-context jump: sensing that unrelated traces belong to the same hidden pattern."

docs/insights.md §10
---
the condensed ideation thesis human memory is associative, state-dependent, and provenance-sensitive. human ideation emerges when distant memories, unrelated contexts, and external triggers become connected under selection pressure. ai can expand, formalize, and compare candidate structures, but the human contribution is the cross-context jump: sensing that unrelated traces belong to the same hidden pattern. framing/ontology docs/insights.md §10 ---

F. The imported evidence base (docs/learning_from_memories.md — the me-on-a-random-day README, used here as source material)

◈ "How the shitshow starts": the multi-dimensional chain-of-thought exhibit

framing/ontology

The README's thesis, imported into cognivault as design evidence: the chain of thought to reach complex ideas is not 2- or even 3-dimensional — a tweet, a walk, photonic computing, model sycophancy, topological mathematics, and a different model's independent derivation all converge on one point, and "remove any one dimension and the path collapses."

docs/learning_from_memories.md
how the shitshow starts : the multi-dimensional chain-of-thought exhibit the readme's thesis, imported into cognivault as design evidence: the chain of thought to reach complex ideas is not 2- or even 3-dimensional — a tweet, a walk, photonic computing, model sycophancy, topological mathematics, and a different model's independent derivation all converge on one point, and remove any one dimension and the path collapses. framing/ontology docs/learning_from_memories.md

◈ The Sam Altman tweet as external trigger

result/finding

"What is the hardest question I could ask you that you might get right?" — an external cue (a tweet) functions as the trigger node of the whole constellation, later generalized into the trigger evidence role and TRIGGER cue type.

docs/learning_from_memories.md "The Trigger"
the sam altman tweet as external trigger what is the hardest question i could ask you that you might get right? — an external cue (a tweet) functions as the trigger node of the whole constellation, later generalized into the `trigger` evidence role and trigger cue type. result/finding docs/learning_from_memories.md the trigger

◈ The primer session: unrelated topic, hours before, seeds everything

result/finding

A Grok debate about LLM limits, synthetic data cannibalization ("Model Autophagy Disorder" — synthetic loops suffer irreversible information loss, gaps papered over with generic content), the human-in-the-loop escape hatch (LLM as massive parallel hypothesis generator, human as filter), and a proof-of-work academia platform with MMR scoring for "high-quality hallucinations" — completely unrelated to time/energy, yet it primed the entire chain that followed.

docs/learning_from_memories.md "The Primer"
the primer session: unrelated topic, hours before, seeds everything a grok debate about llm limits, synthetic data cannibalization ( model autophagy disorder — synthetic loops suffer irreversible information loss, gaps papered over with generic content), the human-in-the-loop escape hatch (llm as massive parallel hypothesis generator, human as filter), and a proof-of-work academia platform with mmr scoring for high-quality hallucinations — completely unrelated to time/energy, yet it primed the entire chain that followed. result/finding docs/learning_from_memories.md the primer

◈ Session 0: the walk-fueled time/energy brainstorm

result/finding

The 3-month-old raw material: time only exists because energy is being spent/decaying; energy drains (black holes) exist but no energy wells → finite system; all energy concentrating into a single point = timeless nothingness containing all potential; reality layered on a quantum background, simulation-like, with start and end.

docs/learning_from_memories.md "Session 0"
session 0: the walk-fueled time/energy brainstorm the 3-month-old raw material: time only exists because energy is being spent/decaying; energy drains (black holes) exist but no energy wells → finite system; all energy concentrating into a single point = timeless nothingness containing all potential; reality layered on a quantum background, simulation-like, with start and end. result/finding docs/learning_from_memories.md session 0

◈ Session 1: deliberately biasing the context (photonic computing)

experiment/benchmark

An experimental manipulation: seed a session with photonic-computing content (O(1) optical matrix multiplication, weight-stationary optical LLMs, optical RAG, space-based compute with delay-line memory storing data in laser beams between satellites) then ask the hardest question — producing physics-level interdisciplinary answers.

docs/learning_from_memories.md "Session 1"
session 1: deliberately biasing the context (photonic computing) an experimental manipulation: seed a session with photonic-computing content (o(1) optical matrix multiplication, weight-stationary optical llms, optical rag, space-based compute with delay-line memory storing data in laser beams between satellites) *then* ask the hardest question — producing physics-level interdisciplinary answers. experiment/benchmark docs/learning_from_memories.md session 1

◈ Session 2: the cold-start control and "you're only as smart as the user you're trying to please"

result/finding

The same question in a fresh session yields safer "median human" answers — the control condition demonstrating context-dependence — followed by the sycophancy realization (a model calibrates its ambition to its user) and the ETS ontology: time as topological friction of energy changing shape, with novel mathematical syntax, which the model then maps onto the 3-month-old Grok brainstorm.

docs/learning_from_memories.md "Session 2"
session 2: the cold-start control and you're only as smart as the user you're trying to please the same question in a fresh session yields safer median human answers — the control condition demonstrating context-dependence — followed by the sycophancy realization (a model calibrates its ambition to its user) and the ets ontology: time as topological friction of energy changing shape, with novel mathematical syntax, which the model then maps onto the 3-month-old grok brainstorm. result/finding docs/learning_from_memories.md session 2

◈ Session 3: CED and the Fertile Void — a third model's independent derivation

result/finding

Claude, given all prior sessions, produces a different ontology — Configurational Exclusion Dynamics: time as the logical consequence of a finite energy system unable to instantiate all its configurations simultaneously (exclusion pressure of unrealized configurations, vs ETS's geometric friction) — including the "Fertile Void" paradox (the singularity is simultaneously timeless and maximally temporal, formalizing "nothingness that contains everything"). Two independent ontologies from two models both map onto the same walk-fueled brainstorm; a sharpened question then demands a third independent path as the test that the insight isn't an artifact of any single framework.

docs/learning_from_memories.md "Session 3"
session 3: ced and the fertile void — a third model's independent derivation claude, given all prior sessions, produces a *different* ontology — configurational exclusion dynamics: time as the logical consequence of a finite energy system unable to instantiate all its configurations simultaneously (exclusion pressure of unrealized configurations, vs ets's geometric friction) — including the fertile void paradox (the singularity is simultaneously timeless and maximally temporal, formalizing nothingness that contains everything ). two independent ontologies from two models both map onto the same walk-fueled brainstorm; a sharpened question then demands a *third* independent path as the test that the insight isn't an artifact of any single framework. result/finding docs/learning_from_memories.md session 3

◈ The paper as proof-of-work artifact

product

All sessions synthesized into an academic-style document ("Time as Energy's Exhaust: Convergent Ontologies from Independent Derivations") explicitly framed as the first proof-of-work artifact for the meritocratic platform envisioned in the primer session — the constellation eating its own tail.

docs/learning_from_memories.md "The Paper"
---
the paper as proof-of-work artifact all sessions synthesized into an academic-style document ( time as energy's exhaust: convergent ontologies from independent derivations ) explicitly framed as the first proof-of-work artifact for the meritocratic platform envisioned in the primer session — the constellation eating its own tail. product docs/learning_from_memories.md the paper ---

G. The memory state model (docs/memory_state_model.md)

◈ Stop using the same words for three different things

framing/ontology

The motivating diagnosis: the codebase conflated durable memory state, prompt-local retrieval math, and event/activity summaries under shared names ("intensity", "activation"). The document freezes a state vocabulary before any scoring refactor, on the principle that naming discipline precedes math.

docs/memory_state_model.md "Status"/"Why This Exists"
stop using the same words for three different things the motivating diagnosis: the codebase conflated durable memory state, prompt-local retrieval math, and event/activity summaries under shared names ( intensity , activation ). the document freezes a state vocabulary before any scoring refactor, on the principle that naming discipline precedes math. framing/ontology docs/memory_state_model.md status / why this exists

◈ memory_state_cache: a persisted, rebuildable hot-path projection

mechanism

One row per live memory per tenant holding the memory's durable state, derived from canonical tables + retrieval sidecars + telemetry. Explicitly not a second source of truth: if dropped it must be rebuildable; versioned independently from embeddings (projection_version); safe to recompute incrementally; and explicit about input freshness via source watermarks (source_graph_event_at, source_activation_event_at, source_cue_event_at). Status in this repo: "accepted target for the next memory-state iteration" — the vocabulary was frozen before the scoring refactor, and until the projection exists retrieval keeps recomputing freshness/activation/convergence/independence ad hoc from mixed raw tables (a model and a rebuild endpoint exist by repo-freeze time).

docs/memory_state_model.md
memory_state_cache: a persisted, rebuildable hot-path projection one row per live memory per tenant holding the memory's durable state, derived from canonical tables + retrieval sidecars + telemetry. explicitly *not* a second source of truth: if dropped it must be rebuildable; versioned independently from embeddings (projection_version); safe to recompute incrementally; and explicit about input freshness via source watermarks (source_graph_event_at, source_activation_event_at, source_cue_event_at). status in this repo: accepted target for the next memory-state iteration — the vocabulary was frozen before the scoring refactor, and until the projection exists retrieval keeps recomputing freshness/activation/convergence/independence ad hoc from mixed raw tables (a model and a rebuild endpoint exist by repo-freeze time). mechanism docs/memory_state_model.md

◈ The four-layer contract: truth / sidecars / state / observability

mechanism

Layer ownership table: canonical truth (memories, memory_evidence, memory_edges, graph_events) owns content/lineage/structure/history; retrieval sidecars (memory_cues, memory_embeddings) own indexes and trigger representations; the hot-path state projection owns persisted state; observability (memory_activation_events, timeline/event APIs) owns activity traces. Each layer has explicit "must not own" clauses.

docs/memory_state_model.md "Layer Contract"
the four-layer contract: truth / sidecars / state / observability layer ownership table: canonical truth (memories, memory_evidence, memory_edges, graph_events) owns content/lineage/structure/history; retrieval sidecars (memory_cues, memory_embeddings) own indexes and trigger representations; the hot-path state projection owns persisted state; observability (memory_activation_events, timeline/event apis) owns activity traces. each layer has explicit must not own clauses. mechanism docs/memory_state_model.md layer contract

◈ The four state dimensions: time / context / trigger / intensity

framing/ontology

Memory state decomposes into four named dimensions, each with three-to-six normalized signals: time (freshness, dormancy, reactivation_rate), context (independence, convergence, conditioning_risk), trigger (cue_salience, cue_fire_history, cue_diversity), intensity (confidence, utility, stability, activation, entrenchment, conflict_pressure). This vocabulary later doubles as the four measurement axes of the graph-analysis tool and echoes the product vision's correlation axes.

docs/memory_state_model.md
the four state dimensions: time / context / trigger / intensity memory state decomposes into four named dimensions, each with three-to-six normalized signals: **time** (freshness, dormancy, reactivation_rate), **context** (independence, convergence, conditioning_risk), **trigger** (cue_salience, cue_fire_history, cue_diversity), **intensity** (confidence, utility, stability, activation, entrenchment, conflict_pressure). this vocabulary later doubles as the four measurement axes of the graph-analysis tool and echoes the product vision's correlation axes. framing/ontology docs/memory_state_model.md

◈ conditioning_risk: apparent support may be contamination

mechanism

A persisted per-memory signal computed from evidence context_regime and conditioning_sources: "high risk means apparent support is likely contaminated by shared prior context." The state cache carries a standing estimate of how much of a memory's support might be an echo chamber.

docs/memory_state_model.md "Context" table
conditioning_risk: apparent support may be contamination a persisted per-memory signal computed from evidence context_regime and conditioning_sources: high risk means apparent support is likely contaminated by shared prior context. the state cache carries a standing estimate of how much of a memory's support might be an echo chamber. mechanism docs/memory_state_model.md context table

◈ cue_diversity: one brittle trigger vs many independent routes

mechanism

A trigger-dimension signal distinguishing a memory reachable by a single brittle cue from one with many independent trigger routes (distinct cue values, types, and evidence coverage) — resilience of recallability as a first-class measured property.

docs/memory_state_model.md "Trigger" table
cue_diversity: one brittle trigger vs many independent routes a trigger-dimension signal distinguishing a memory reachable by a single brittle cue from one with many independent trigger routes (distinct cue values, types, and evidence coverage) — resilience of recallability as a first-class measured property. mechanism docs/memory_state_model.md trigger table

◈ Entrenchment: one concept absorbing two bonuses

mechanism

The persisted entrenchment signal (from strength, reinforcement_count, and reinforcing graph-event deltas) deliberately collapses the previously separate strength_bonus and reinforcement_bonus — a scoring simplification achieved by better state vocabulary.

docs/memory_state_model.md "Intensity" table
entrenchment: one concept absorbing two bonuses the persisted `entrenchment` signal (from strength, reinforcement_count, and reinforcing graph-event deltas) deliberately collapses the previously separate `strength_bonus` and `reinforcement_bonus` — a scoring simplification achieved by better state vocabulary. mechanism docs/memory_state_model.md intensity table

◈ Stability vs activation: durable stickiness vs readiness to fire

mechanism

Two intensity signals sharply separated: stability is the durable support/stickiness summary (derived from independence, convergence, entrenchment, conflict_pressure); activation is baseline readiness to fire (derived from freshness, reactivation_rate, cue salience/fire history) — and current-prompt graph spread is explicitly not part of it.

docs/memory_state_model.md "Intensity" table
stability vs activation: durable stickiness vs readiness to fire two intensity signals sharply separated: `stability` is the durable support/stickiness summary (derived from independence, convergence, entrenchment, conflict_pressure); `activation` is baseline readiness to fire (derived from freshness, reactivation_rate, cue salience/fire history) — and *current-prompt graph spread is explicitly not part of it*. mechanism docs/memory_state_model.md intensity table

◈ conflict_pressure: contradiction as a standing force on a node

mechanism

A persisted signal from contradicting/superseding edges and related events representing "tension against the memory remaining a stable live node" — contradiction isn't only an event, it's continuous pressure a memory lives under.

docs/memory_state_model.md "Intensity" table
conflict_pressure: contradiction as a standing force on a node a persisted signal from contradicting/superseding edges and related events representing tension against the memory remaining a stable live node — contradiction isn't only an event, it's continuous pressure a memory lives under. mechanism docs/memory_state_model.md intensity table

◈ The state-vs-query-time rule

framing/ontology

Bright line: "if a value depends on the current prompt, current seed set, or caller window, it is not memory state." Semantic/cue-space/context-space similarity, exact cue matches, seed_channels, matched_cues, sector_boost, mode-fit adjustments, graph_bonus, activation_sources, base/final scores — all stay query-time retrieval outputs.

docs/memory_state_model.md "What Must Stay Query-Time"
the state-vs-query-time rule bright line: if a value depends on the current prompt, current seed set, or caller window, it is not memory state. semantic/cue-space/context-space similarity, exact cue matches, seed_channels, matched_cues, sector_boost, mode-fit adjustments, graph_bonus, activation_sources, base/final scores — all stay query-time retrieval outputs. framing/ontology docs/memory_state_model.md what must stay query-time

◈ The observability rule and the mislabeled "intensity" object

mechanism

Second bright line: "if a number is a count, delta total, or caller-windowed event summary, it belongs to observability." The existing graph API's intensity object (total_events, access_events, strength_delta_total…) is diagnosed as event-centric and must be renamed activity/event_summary — with a full naming contract (state.time., state.context., state.trigger., state.intensity.) and a current-surface→target-surface migration table.

docs/memory_state_model.md "What Is Observability Only" / "Naming Contract"
the observability rule and the mislabeled intensity object second bright line: if a number is a count, delta total, or caller-windowed event summary, it belongs to observability. the existing graph api's `intensity` object (total_events, access_events, strength_delta_total…) is diagnosed as event-centric and must be renamed `activity`/`event_summary` — with a full naming contract (`state.time.*`, `state.context.*`, `state.trigger.*`, `state.intensity.*`) and a current-surface→target-surface migration table. mechanism docs/memory_state_model.md what is observability only / naming contract

◈ Multiple embedding representations per memory (sidecar reveal)

mechanism

The state model and tuning docs reveal that a memory is embedded in multiple representation spaces — main content, cue space, context space, abstraction space (memory_embeddings sidecar) — each a separate retrieval channel with its own similarity and bonus. A memory is not one vector.

docs/memory_state_model.md "What Must Stay Query-Time"; docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (channel bonuses)
multiple embedding representations per memory (sidecar reveal) the state model and tuning docs reveal that a memory is embedded in multiple representation spaces — main content, cue space, context space, abstraction space (memory_embeddings sidecar) — each a separate retrieval channel with its own similarity and bonus. a memory is not one vector. mechanism docs/memory_state_model.md what must stay query-time ; docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (channel bonuses)

◈ memory_cues: triggers as stored rows with firing history

mechanism

Cues are first-class rows (cue_type, normalized_value, display_value, weight, fire_count, last_fired_at) attached to memories — the system remembers not just content but what tends to activate it and how often each trigger has actually fired.

docs/memory_state_model.md; CLAUDE.md models list; backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/*.json
---
memory_cues: triggers as stored rows with firing history cues are first-class rows (cue_type, normalized_value, display_value, weight, fire_count, last_fired_at) attached to memories — the system remembers not just content but *what tends to activate it* and how often each trigger has actually fired. mechanism docs/memory_state_model.md; claude.md models list; backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/*.json ---

H. ADRs

◈ ADR 0001: no SessionEnd hook, no marker queue — reflection must be chosen

dead-end

Resolves two competing write-path stories in favor of: stop-hook hints + explicit warm /extract-memory (primary) + explicit session-scoped consolidate recovery. SessionEnd is rejected because it only fires on specific close commands (closer to a manual trigger than reliable capture), is no warmer than explicit extraction, and "discards the strongest signal source: the live agent's own warm context." Also rejected: any /sessions/{id}/end endpoint and parked pending-marker queues.

docs/adr/0001-memory-write-architecture.md; docs/PRD.md §6.3a
adr 0001: no sessionend hook, no marker queue — reflection must be chosen resolves two competing write-path stories in favor of: stop-hook hints + explicit warm `/extract-memory` (primary) + explicit session-scoped `consolidate` recovery. `sessionend` is rejected because it only fires on specific close commands (closer to a manual trigger than reliable capture), is no warmer than explicit extraction, and discards the strongest signal source: the live agent's own warm context. also rejected: any `/sessions/{id}/end` endpoint and parked pending-marker queues. dead-end docs/adr/0001-memory-write-architecture.md; docs/prd.md §6.3a

◈ ADR 0002: single-tenant runtime on a tenant-aware schema

mechanism

Keep tenant_id on every owned row and filter every read by resolved tenant, even though v1 is strictly single-tenant with one auto-resolved default tenant — because retrofitting ownership into live tables later is worse than carrying it now. Explicitly rejected middle ground: optional/hint tenancy, which "leaks ambiguity into read filters, uniqueness rules, and future migrations."

docs/adr/0002-single-tenant-runtime.md
---
adr 0002: single-tenant runtime on a tenant-aware schema keep `tenant_id` on every owned row and filter every read by resolved tenant, even though v1 is strictly single-tenant with one auto-resolved default tenant — because retrofitting ownership into live tables later is worse than carrying it now. explicitly rejected middle ground: optional/hint tenancy, which leaks ambiguity into read filters, uniqueness rules, and future migrations. mechanism docs/adr/0002-single-tenant-runtime.md ---

I. Architecture (ARCHITECTURE.md)

◈ The two-flows mental model

framing/ontology

"Cognivault is two flows, not a bag of hooks": Flow A retrieval (hot path, every turn, non-LLM, bounded, helps the current task) and Flow B recording (reflective, explicit, deep work off the hot path, improves future tasks). Two load-bearing rules fall out: if it runs every turn it does not write memory; if it writes memory it runs at an explicit or safe boundary.

ARCHITECTURE.md §1, §3
the two-flows mental model cognivault is two flows, not a bag of hooks : flow a retrieval (hot path, every turn, non-llm, bounded, helps the current task) and flow b recording (reflective, explicit, deep work off the hot path, improves future tasks). two load-bearing rules fall out: if it runs every turn it does not write memory; if it writes memory it runs at an explicit or safe boundary. framing/ontology architecture.md §1, §3

◈ Status tags keep the map honest: [wired] / [next] / [later] / [avoid]

mechanism

Every subsystem in the architecture doc is tagged with implementation status "so the map reflects reality, not ambition" — including an explicit [avoid] tag for things intentionally kept off the main path. Documentation-as-ground-truth as a working method.

ARCHITECTURE.md "Status legend"
status tags keep the map honest: [wired] / [next] / [later] / [avoid] every subsystem in the architecture doc is tagged with implementation status so the map reflects reality, not ambition — including an explicit `[avoid]` tag for things intentionally kept off the main path. documentation-as-ground-truth as a working method. mechanism architecture.md status legend

◈ Three extraction cases, one skill

mechanism

Case 1: always-on stop-hook regex signal capture (attention pointers, buffer survives session boundaries). Case 2: warm mid-session /extract-memory — "KV cache warm, judgment fresh. Best signal. This is the primary recording path." Case 3: explicit recovery/backfill — prefer manually resuming the saved session and re-running the skill so the agent regains its own context; consolidate <sid> exists for transcript replay. All three converge on the same extract-memory skill and REST write endpoints.

ARCHITECTURE.md §2, §5
three extraction cases, one skill case 1: always-on stop-hook regex signal capture (attention pointers, buffer survives session boundaries). case 2: warm mid-session `/extract-memory` — kv cache warm, judgment fresh. best signal. this is the primary recording path. case 3: explicit recovery/backfill — prefer manually resuming the saved session and re-running the skill so the agent regains its own context; `consolidate <sid>` exists for transcript replay. all three converge on the same extract-memory skill and rest write endpoints. mechanism architecture.md §2, §5

◈ Repo-local pending-extraction reminders (rate-limited nagging)

mechanism

The repo-local stop hook also persists per-session pending-extraction state and emits a rate-limited advisory reminder once a session has accumulated enough turns and signal hits without a successful extraction — the system nudges the user toward reflection instead of writing memory behind their back. Thresholds are a named tuning knob (too low → nagging; too high → stale buffers).

ARCHITECTURE.md §2, §5.1, §11
repo-local pending-extraction reminders (rate-limited nagging) the repo-local stop hook also persists per-session pending-extraction state and emits a rate-limited advisory reminder once a session has accumulated enough turns and signal hits without a successful extraction — the system nudges the user toward reflection instead of writing memory behind their back. thresholds are a named tuning knob (too low → nagging; too high → stale buffers). mechanism architecture.md §2, §5.1, §11

◈ Best-effort detached auto-extraction, demoted to convenience

mechanism

The repo-local Codex hook can opportunistically spawn a detached background extraction after new signals arrive and a cooldown allows — but this is explicitly "convenience only, not the architectural write contract": if it fails the session proceeds normally, and one of the listed "next changes" is precisely to demote it from architecture to convenience.

ARCHITECTURE.md §2, §5.1, §10
best-effort detached auto-extraction, demoted to convenience the repo-local codex hook can opportunistically spawn a detached background extraction after new signals arrive and a cooldown allows — but this is explicitly convenience only, not the architectural write contract : if it fails the session proceeds normally, and one of the listed next changes is precisely to demote it from architecture to convenience. mechanism architecture.md §2, §5.1, §10

◈ Session start gets a deterministic seed, not semantic retrieval

mechanism

At session start there is no prompt anchor yet, so semantic search is underspecified and noisy — a documented finding. The only acceptable session-start load is a tiny non-semantic project-scoped seed: pinned memories, very recent project memories, high-confidence local conventions/procedures, and a small slice of unresolved open-question memories, emitted as a "## Bootstrap memories" block answering "what should the agent know immediately just because it entered this project?" No LLM, ideally no embedding call.

ARCHITECTURE.md §4.1
session start gets a deterministic seed, not semantic retrieval at session start there is no prompt anchor yet, so semantic search is underspecified and noisy — a documented finding. the only acceptable session-start load is a tiny non-semantic project-scoped seed: pinned memories, very recent project memories, high-confidence local conventions/procedures, and a small slice of unresolved open-question memories, emitted as a ## bootstrap memories block answering what should the agent know immediately just because it entered this project? no llm, ideally no embedding call. mechanism architecture.md §4.1

◈ /clear as a retrieval-epoch reset

mechanism

/clear is semantically "we are done with this context; start a fresh one": optionally offer to record first (suggest, don't force), clear the cached baseline, and let the next prompt become the new semantic anchor. Explicit command-semantics rule: do not overload /clear to mean "write memory now" — clearing context and recording memory are different actions.

ARCHITECTURE.md §4.4, §6
/clear as a retrieval-epoch reset `/clear` is semantically we are done with this context; start a fresh one : optionally offer to record first (suggest, don't force), clear the cached baseline, and let the next prompt become the new semantic anchor. explicit command-semantics rule: do not overload /clear to mean write memory now — clearing context and recording memory are different actions. mechanism architecture.md §4.4, §6

◈ Retrieval and recording allowed/never contracts

mechanism

Retrieval may read/rank memories, inject a compact block, and write lightweight access telemetry; it may never call an LLM, replay a transcript, write long-term memory, block on heavy work, or exceed p95 400ms — "a context enhancement path, not a thinking path." Recording may capture cheap hints every stop, run deep extraction at explicit boundaries, queue deferred work, and write via propose/commit; it may never run deep extraction on every stop, block the task with transcript replay, blindly summarize every session, or commit directly from regex heuristics without appraisal.

ARCHITECTURE.md §4.5, §5.5
retrieval and recording allowed/never contracts retrieval may read/rank memories, inject a compact block, and write lightweight access telemetry; it may never call an llm, replay a transcript, write long-term memory, block on heavy work, or exceed p95 400ms — a context enhancement path, not a thinking path. recording may capture cheap hints every stop, run deep extraction at explicit boundaries, queue deferred work, and write via propose/commit; it may never run deep extraction on every stop, block the task with transcript replay, blindly summarize every session, or commit directly from regex heuristics without appraisal. mechanism architecture.md §4.5, §5.5

◈ The two failure modes the split prevents

framing/ontology

(1) "Memory harms the task" — slow/invasive/frequent retrieval or recording makes the agent pay a cognition tax every turn; (2) "memory captures noise instead of learning" — automatic write-per-response fills the store with unstable mid-task chatter. Cognivault should behave like "a silent support layer": retrieve when context is needed, record when explicitly asked or after the task, never confuse the two moments.

ARCHITECTURE.md §8
the two failure modes the split prevents (1) memory harms the task — slow/invasive/frequent retrieval or recording makes the agent pay a cognition tax every turn; (2) memory captures noise instead of learning — automatic write-per-response fills the store with unstable mid-task chatter. cognivault should behave like a silent support layer : retrieve when context is needed, record when explicitly asked or after the task, never confuse the two moments. framing/ontology architecture.md §8

◈ The four-question design test

mechanism

A proposed change belongs in Cognivault only if it passes all four: (1) helps the current task without noticeable friction; (2) if it writes memory, runs at an explicit or safe boundary; (3) couldn't be achieved with a cheaper signal-capture step now and deeper extraction later; (4) if daemon/DB is down, the host session still proceeds normally with empty output.

ARCHITECTURE.md §9
the four-question design test a proposed change belongs in cognivault only if it passes all four: (1) helps the current task without noticeable friction; (2) if it writes memory, runs at an explicit or safe boundary; (3) couldn't be achieved with a cheaper signal-capture step now and deeper extraction later; (4) if daemon/db is down, the host session still proceeds normally with empty output. mechanism architecture.md §9

◈ The tuning-knob inventory: every dial is empirically open

open-question

A named list of dials with no "correct" value yet, flagged so the next iteration knows where to instrument: recall top_k (block size vs prompt-cache blast radius), sector boost weights, nuance_of expansion depth ("1-hop is the current guess"), delta-recall divergence threshold, bootstrap seed size (too big → token cost; too small → no grounding), signal-classifier regex set (precision vs recall of attention pointers), pending-reminder thresholds, detached auto-extract cooldown, consolidate prompt template, embeddings model.

ARCHITECTURE.md §11
the tuning-knob inventory: every dial is empirically open a named list of dials with no correct value yet, flagged so the next iteration knows where to instrument: recall top_k (block size vs prompt-cache blast radius), sector boost weights, nuance_of expansion depth ( 1-hop is the current guess ), delta-recall divergence threshold, bootstrap seed size (too big → token cost; too small → no grounding), signal-classifier regex set (precision vs recall of attention pointers), pending-reminder thresholds, detached auto-extract cooldown, consolidate prompt template, embeddings model. open-question architecture.md §11

◈ The one-sentence architecture

coinage

The whole design compressed to a maxim.

ARCHITECTURE.md §11
"Retrieve automatically and cheaply; record deliberately and reflectively."
the one-sentence architecture the whole design compressed to a maxim. coinage architecture.md §11 retrieve automatically and cheaply; record deliberately and reflectively.

◈ Signal taxonomy for the stop-hook classifier

mechanism

The regex classifier's categories of "turns worth revisiting": user correction, user confirmation, agent concession, error recovery, and explicit boundary markers (/clear, "we're done", "extract memory"). These four-plus-boundary signal types are the entire always-on sensorium of the recording path.

ARCHITECTURE.md §5.1; skills/extract-memory/SKILL.md
---
signal taxonomy for the stop-hook classifier the regex classifier's categories of turns worth revisiting : user correction, user confirmation, agent concession, error recovery, and explicit boundary markers (/clear, we're done , extract memory ). these four-plus-boundary signal types are the entire always-on sensorium of the recording path. mechanism architecture.md §5.1; skills/extract-memory/skill.md ---

J. Skills (skills/extract-memory, skills/install-cognivault) and CLAUDE.md operational ideas

◈ Extraction is a focused pass, not task continuation

mechanism

The extract-memory skill opens by re-framing the agent's job: "look back at this conversation, identify what's worth remembering, write it to cognivault. Stop when you're done — do not continue whatever the session was doing." Memory writing is a distinct cognitive mode with its own termination condition.

skills/extract-memory/SKILL.md
extraction is a focused pass, not task continuation the extract-memory skill opens by re-framing the agent's job: look back at this conversation, identify what's worth remembering, write it to cognivault. stop when you're done — do not continue whatever the session was doing. memory writing is a distinct cognitive mode with its own termination condition. mechanism skills/extract-memory/skill.md

◈ Read the signal buffer first — hints direct attention

mechanism

The skill instructs the agent to read ~/.cognivault/buffers/<session_id>.jsonl before reviewing the conversation, because the pre-computed hints "point at the turns most likely to contain learnings" — the cheap always-on classifier steers the expensive reflective pass.

skills/extract-memory/SKILL.md
read the signal buffer first — hints direct attention the skill instructs the agent to read `~/.cognivault/buffers/<session_id>.jsonl` *before* reviewing the conversation, because the pre-computed hints point at the turns most likely to contain learnings — the cheap always-on classifier steers the expensive reflective pass. mechanism skills/extract-memory/skill.md

◈ Headless-safety: never ask interactively

mechanism

Because the skill may run under cognivault consolidate where a question would hang the subprocess, it must discover the session id from transcript path / CLAUDE_SESSION_ID / hook payload, and if none is available, read whatever buffers exist and note the ambiguity in the extraction rationale rather than prompting.

skills/extract-memory/SKILL.md
headless-safety: never ask interactively because the skill may run under `cognivault consolidate` where a question would hang the subprocess, it must discover the session id from transcript path / claude_session_id / hook payload, and if none is available, read whatever buffers exist and note the ambiguity in the extraction rationale rather than prompting. mechanism skills/extract-memory/skill.md

◈ Signal-type → memory-characteristics mapping table

mechanism

Each stop-hook signal type maps to default extraction settings: user_correction → from_error, importance 7-9; agent_concession → from_error 7-9 (often pairs with a correction on the same turn); user_confirmation → reinforcement 5-7 ("the judgment is confirmed"); error_recovery → from_error 6-8 ("root-cause + fix is the learning"). Cheap regex classes pre-price the memories.

skills/extract-memory/SKILL.md
signal-type → memory-characteristics mapping table each stop-hook signal type maps to default extraction settings: user_correction → from_error, importance 7-9; agent_concession → from_error 7-9 (often pairs with a correction on the same turn); user_confirmation → reinforcement 5-7 ( the judgment is confirmed ); error_recovery → from_error 6-8 ( root-cause + fix is the learning ). cheap regex classes pre-price the memories. mechanism skills/extract-memory/skill.md

◈ Conservative extraction doctrine

mechanism

Five rules: (1) quality over quantity — "five good memories beat twenty noisy ones," and approaching the session caps means you're over-extracting; (2) never memorize ephemeral work — "'I just implemented X for the user' is not a learning. 'The user requires X pattern in Y context' is"; (3) prefer novel over strengthen when unsure — a duplicate is recoverable, a wrong merge destroys original phrasing; (4) prefer edge_case over contradiction when unsure — supersession is irreversible from recall; (5) don't re-extract — the propose-neighbors path makes running the skill twice on the same session safe/idempotent.

skills/extract-memory/SKILL.md
conservative extraction doctrine five rules: (1) quality over quantity — five good memories beat twenty noisy ones, and approaching the session caps means you're over-extracting; (2) never memorize ephemeral work — 'i just implemented x for the user' is not a learning. 'the user requires x pattern in y context' is ; (3) prefer novel over strengthen when unsure — a duplicate is recoverable, a wrong merge destroys original phrasing; (4) prefer edge_case over contradiction when unsure — supersession is irreversible from recall; (5) don't re-extract — the propose-neighbors path makes running the skill twice on the same session safe/idempotent. mechanism skills/extract-memory/skill.md

◈ Scope humility: when in doubt, narrower

mechanism

Scope decision defaults: universal craft advice → global; about the current or a sibling repo → workspace; wrong anywhere else → project; and the tiebreak rule "when in doubt, narrower. A misplaced global pollutes every future recall."

skills/extract-memory/SKILL.md
scope humility: when in doubt, narrower scope decision defaults: universal craft advice → global; about the current or a sibling repo → workspace; wrong anywhere else → project; and the tiebreak rule when in doubt, narrower. a misplaced `global` pollutes every future recall. mechanism skills/extract-memory/skill.md

◈ Sector tags only when confident — a wrong soft boost is a demotion

mechanism

Because sector fields are soft recall boosts, a wrong tag actively demotes relevant memories for unrelated work; the skill instructs tagging industry/domain/business_function only under confidence, noting domain is the most commonly useful axis.

skills/extract-memory/SKILL.md
sector tags only when confident — a wrong soft boost is a demotion because sector fields are soft recall boosts, a wrong tag actively demotes relevant memories for unrelated work; the skill instructs tagging industry/domain/business_function only under confidence, noting domain is the most commonly useful axis. mechanism skills/extract-memory/skill.md

◈ Repo-local install pack: memory wiring as a scaffolded skill

product

Installation itself is a skill: a scaffold script writes the .codex/ hook pack (config.toml, hooks.json, common/session_start/user_prompt_submit/stop hook scripts) plus .cognivault/buffers and .cognivault/logs, gitignores the state dir, merges rather than overwrites existing config, and a verify script checks files + token + daemon health with an optional --smoke-prompt recall test.

skills/install-cognivault/SKILL.md
repo-local install pack: memory wiring as a scaffolded skill installation itself is a skill: a scaffold script writes the `.codex/` hook pack (config.toml, hooks.json, common/session_start/user_prompt_submit/stop hook scripts) plus `.cognivault/buffers` and `.cognivault/logs`, gitignores the state dir, merges rather than overwrites existing config, and a verify script checks files + token + daemon health with an optional `--smoke-prompt` recall test. product skills/install-cognivault/skill.md

◈ Retrieval belongs on UserPromptSubmit, never SessionStart

mechanism

Installation invariant: put retrieval on the prompt-submit hook (where a semantic anchor exists); SessionStart is only for health/init context. A second invariant: if a child codex exec is used for automatic extraction, hooks must be disabled in the child process to avoid recursion (the extractor triggering its own extraction).

skills/install-cognivault/SKILL.md
retrieval belongs on userpromptsubmit, never sessionstart installation invariant: put retrieval on the prompt-submit hook (where a semantic anchor exists); sessionstart is only for health/init context. a second invariant: if a child `codex exec` is used for automatic extraction, hooks must be disabled in the child process to avoid recursion (the extractor triggering its own extraction). mechanism skills/install-cognivault/skill.md

◈ Host paths, not container paths, in memories

mechanism

Docker rule: project-scoped memories must record the host repo path (not the container's), or prompt-time recall will never match the host cwd. When the daemon runs in Docker, host hooks and backend share the repo-local .cognivault/ directory via bind mount and COGNIVAULT_HOME_DIR.

skills/install-cognivault/SKILL.md; CLAUDE.md "Platform gotchas"
host paths, not container paths, in memories docker rule: project-scoped memories must record the *host* repo path (not the container's), or prompt-time recall will never match the host cwd. when the daemon runs in docker, host hooks and backend share the repo-local `.cognivault/` directory via bind mount and cognivault_home_dir. mechanism skills/install-cognivault/skill.md; claude.md platform gotchas

◈ Seed memories only on request, high-signal over bulk

mechanism
