Install-time seeding doctrine: don't pre-populate unless asked; prefer a small set of high-signal repo memories over bulk noise; prefer markdown ingestion for durable docs; and verify retrieval after seeding with a prompt that should clearly hit one of the new memories.

skills/install-cognivault/SKILL.md
seed memories only on request, high-signal over bulk install-time seeding doctrine: don't pre-populate unless asked; prefer a small set of high-signal repo memories over bulk noise; prefer markdown ingestion for durable docs; and verify retrieval after seeding with a prompt that should clearly hit one of the new memories. mechanism skills/install-cognivault/skill.md

◈ Host-agent pluggability proven by default: Codex drives consolidation

mechanism

The consolidate path spawns whatever host-agent command is configured — defaulting to codex exec --full-auto --skip-git-repo-check -, overridable via COGNIVAULT_HOST_AGENT_COMMAND — demonstrating the "any agent CLI is a viable host" claim in the shipped default (a Claude-Code-born system whose extractor is Codex).

CLAUDE.md "Architecture"; docs/PRD.md §6.4
host-agent pluggability proven by default: codex drives consolidation the consolidate path spawns whatever host-agent command is configured — defaulting to `codex exec --full-auto --skip-git-repo-check -`, overridable via cognivault_host_agent_command — demonstrating the any agent cli is a viable host claim in the shipped default (a claude-code-born system whose extractor is codex). mechanism claude.md architecture ; docs/prd.md §6.4

◈ The model census reveals the grown schema

mechanism

The SQLAlchemy model list shows how far the schema grew beyond the PRD tables: memory, memory_edge, memory_evidence, memory_revision, memory_embedding (multi-representation), memory_state_cache, memory_activation_event, memory_cue, memory_edge_observation (edges accumulate repeated observations rather than being one-shot assertions), graph_event, consolidation_log, consolidation_run, tenant.

CLAUDE.md "Repo layout"
the model census reveals the grown schema the sqlalchemy model list shows how far the schema grew beyond the prd tables: memory, memory_edge, memory_evidence, memory_revision, memory_embedding (multi-representation), memory_state_cache, memory_activation_event, memory_cue, **memory_edge_observation** (edges accumulate repeated observations rather than being one-shot assertions), graph_event, consolidation_log, **consolidation_run**, tenant. mechanism claude.md repo layout

◈ The REST endpoint census as capability map

mechanism

Beyond the PRD's core five, the router grew: /recall_delta (delta recall with its own top-k and similarity/novelty thresholds), /graph + /graph/subgraph, /timeline, /events, per-memory /evidence /revisions /activity, /inspect/*, and POST /inspect/state/rebuild (rebuild the state-cache projection on demand).

CLAUDE.md "Key REST endpoints"; docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md
---
the rest endpoint census as capability map beyond the prd's core five, the router grew: `/recall_delta` (delta recall with its own top-k and similarity/novelty thresholds), `/graph` + `/graph/subgraph`, `/timeline`, `/events`, per-memory `/evidence` `/revisions` `/activity`, `/inspect/*`, and `post /inspect/state/rebuild` (rebuild the state-cache projection on demand). mechanism claude.md key rest endpoints ; docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md ---

K. The graph-analysis plan (docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md)

◈ Graph quality is observable: measure the axis structure of agent-declared links

experiment/benchmark

Since edges are agent-declared (via the extraction skill's consolidation actions), "is the linking good?" reduces to "are the agent's edge decisions producing a graph with the axis structure the vision describes?" A read-only inspection tool quantifies the live graph along the four product-vision correlation axes — time, context, trigger, intensity — plus foundation health, making link quality observable and reproducible.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (Goal, Context)
graph quality is observable: measure the axis structure of agent-declared links since edges are agent-declared (via the extraction skill's consolidation actions), is the linking good? reduces to are the agent's edge decisions producing a graph with the axis structure the vision describes? a read-only inspection tool quantifies the live graph along the four product-vision correlation axes — time, context, trigger, intensity — plus foundation health, making link quality observable and reproducible. experiment/benchmark docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (goal, context)

◈ Ranking precision cannot measure convergence

framing/ontology

Key insight motivating the tool: the retrieval benchmark measures prompt→memory ranking precision, but the product vision describes something orthogonal — a chain of thought converging through "completely unrelated connections" across time, context, trigger, and intensity. A second, complementary measurement instrument is needed; the benchmark stays as the ranking guardrail.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (Context)
ranking precision cannot measure convergence key insight motivating the tool: the retrieval benchmark measures prompt→memory ranking precision, but the product vision describes something orthogonal — a chain of thought converging through completely unrelated connections across time, context, trigger, and intensity. a second, complementary measurement instrument is needed; the benchmark stays as the ranking guardrail. framing/ontology docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (context)

◈ GraphSnapshot: freeze once, compute pure

mechanism

Architecture of the tool: a single DB read builds a frozen immutable snapshot (every live memory, edge, cue, state-cache row — excluding deleted and superseded); all metrics are pure functions over the snapshot with no I/O, hand-rolled BFS instead of networkx, making every metric unit-testable without fixtures.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (Task 1)
graphsnapshot: freeze once, compute pure architecture of the tool: a single db read builds a frozen immutable snapshot (every live memory, edge, cue, state-cache row — excluding deleted and superseded); all metrics are pure functions over the snapshot with no i/o, hand-rolled bfs instead of networkx, making every metric unit-testable without fixtures. mechanism docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (task 1)

◈ Foundation health: orphans, components, degree histogram

experiment/benchmark

Axis-agnostic graph health metrics: total memories/edges, orphan count (degree-0 memories), connected-component size distribution, largest component, degree histogram — treating edges as undirected for reachability since "direction matters for edge-type semantics but not for reachability."

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (Task 2)
foundation health: orphans, components, degree histogram axis-agnostic graph health metrics: total memories/edges, orphan count (degree-0 memories), connected-component size distribution, largest component, degree histogram — treating edges as undirected for reachability since direction matters for edge-type semantics but not for reachability. experiment/benchmark docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (task 2)

◈ Edge semantics: the cosine histogram of linked pairs

experiment/benchmark

For every edge, compute the cosine similarity between its endpoints' embeddings and histogram the results (10 buckets) alongside an edge-type histogram — revealing whether the graph's links merely mirror embedding similarity (redundant with vector search) or bridge semantically distant memories (adding real information).

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (Task 3)
edge semantics: the cosine histogram of linked pairs for every edge, compute the cosine similarity between its endpoints' embeddings and histogram the results (10 buckets) alongside an edge-type histogram — revealing whether the graph's links merely mirror embedding similarity (redundant with vector search) or bridge semantically distant memories (adding real information). experiment/benchmark docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (task 3)

◈ Time axis: cross-week edges are the "rediscovered months later" signal

experiment/benchmark

Bucket memories by acquisition week (day/month selectable); intra-bucket edges indicate session cohesion, cross-bucket edges indicate themes recurring over time — explicitly "the 'Grok 3 months ago rediscovered today' pattern from the product vision" — with a span histogram of how many weeks each cross-bucket edge jumps.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (Task 4)
time axis: cross-week edges are the rediscovered months later signal bucket memories by acquisition week (day/month selectable); intra-bucket edges indicate session cohesion, cross-bucket edges indicate themes recurring over time — explicitly the 'grok 3 months ago rediscovered today' pattern from the product vision — with a span histogram of how many weeks each cross-bucket edge jumps. experiment/benchmark docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (task 4)

◈ Context axis: cross-project and scope-spanning edges as convergence patterns

experiment/benchmark

Classify each edge by its endpoints' contexts: intra vs cross-project ("different project, same idea"), scope-spanning (project ↔ global — global principles attaching to project-specific traces), intra vs cross-domain, intra vs cross-session. Cross-context linkage is measured, not assumed.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (Task 5)
context axis: cross-project and scope-spanning edges as convergence patterns classify each edge by its endpoints' contexts: intra vs cross-project ( different project, same idea ), scope-spanning (project ↔ global — global principles attaching to project-specific traces), intra vs cross-domain, intra vs cross-session. cross-context linkage is measured, not assumed. experiment/benchmark docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (task 5)

◈ Trigger axis: dominant cue types and BRIDGE/PRIMER endpoints

experiment/benchmark

Each memory gets a dominant cue type (highest-weighted cue, with an explicit tiebreak order); edges are classified intra vs cross-cue-type, and edges touching a memory carrying a BRIDGE or PRIMER cue are counted separately because those cue types "are literally named for cross-context linkage." Reveals the full cue-type vocabulary: ENTITY, FILE, TOOL, ARTIFACT, ERROR, TRIGGER, BRIDGE, PRIMER, TOPIC, AFFECT (affect — emotional state — as a recallable cue type).

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (Task 6)
trigger axis: dominant cue types and bridge/primer endpoints each memory gets a dominant cue type (highest-weighted cue, with an explicit tiebreak order); edges are classified intra vs cross-cue-type, and edges touching a memory carrying a bridge or primer cue are counted separately because those cue types are literally named for cross-context linkage. reveals the full cue-type vocabulary: entity, file, tool, artifact, error, trigger, bridge, primer, topic, affect (affect — emotional state — as a recallable cue type). experiment/benchmark docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (task 6)

◈ Intensity axis: core/periphery structure and the degree↔stability correlation

experiment/benchmark

Intensity is continuous, so instead of buckets: partition memories into a core (top quantile by entrenchment_score) and periphery, classify edges as core-internal / periphery-internal / ladder (one endpoint each), and compute the Pearson correlation of node degree with stability_score — "positive correlation means high-stability memories attract more edges, which is the core-periphery structure the product vision expects."

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (Task 7)
intensity axis: core/periphery structure and the degree↔stability correlation intensity is continuous, so instead of buckets: partition memories into a core (top quantile by entrenchment_score) and periphery, classify edges as core-internal / periphery-internal / ladder (one endpoint each), and compute the pearson correlation of node degree with stability_score — positive correlation means high-stability memories attract more edges, which is the core-periphery structure the product vision expects. experiment/benchmark docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (task 7)

◈ The cross-axis convergence score: a number for "unrelated dimensions converging"

experiment/benchmark

Per memory: sum over incident edges of the number of axes each edge bridges (different week, different project, different domain, different dominant cue type — max +4 per edge, credited to both endpoints). Explicitly "the closest numeric proxy for the product-vision claim that unrelated dimensions converging on one point is the signal that matters." Report surfaces the top-10 convergence nodes.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (Task 8)
the cross-axis convergence score: a number for unrelated dimensions converging per memory: sum over incident edges of the number of axes each edge bridges (different week, different project, different domain, different dominant cue type — max +4 per edge, credited to both endpoints). explicitly the closest numeric proxy for the product-vision claim that unrelated dimensions converging on one point is the signal that matters. report surfaces the top-10 convergence nodes. experiment/benchmark docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (task 8)

◈ Frozen baseline reports: graph health with a committed reference point

experiment/benchmark

The tool writes report.json + report.md to timestamped directories, and the plan's exit criterion is a committed baseline snapshot of the user's live DB (force-added past the gitignore as an intentional exception) — so future tuning and UI work can diff against a frozen observable-state reference. "Note any surprises... but do not change behavior — the baseline is the baseline."

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (Tasks 9-11)
frozen baseline reports: graph health with a committed reference point the tool writes report.json + report.md to timestamped directories, and the plan's exit criterion is a *committed* baseline snapshot of the user's live db (force-added past the gitignore as an intentional exception) — so future tuning and ui work can diff against a frozen observable-state reference. note any surprises... but do not change behavior — the baseline is the baseline. experiment/benchmark docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (tasks 9-11)

◈ Follow-ups: per-axis health ADR, convergence UI overlay, compression candidates

product

Three planned successors: (1) an interpretation pass ADR documenting "what healthy looks like per axis for this user's usage pattern" after two snapshots a week apart; (2) exposing the convergence top-N as a clickable side panel/overlay in the graph UI; (3) using connected components or cue-similarity clustering to surface 3+-memory groups as "compression candidates" for a multi-select-and-extract-learnings feature (memory groups suggesting their own consolidation).

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (Follow-up work)
---
follow-ups: per-axis health adr, convergence ui overlay, compression candidates three planned successors: (1) an interpretation pass adr documenting what healthy looks like per axis for this user's usage pattern after two snapshots a week apart; (2) exposing the convergence top-n as a clickable side panel/overlay in the graph ui; (3) using connected components or cue-similarity clustering to surface 3+-memory groups as compression candidates for a multi-select-and-extract-learnings feature (memory groups suggesting their own consolidation). product docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-memory-graph-analysis.md (follow-up work) ---

L. The benchmark (fixtures, trust-gaps plan, tuning plan)

◈ A scenario benchmark for memory retrieval (AMBench seed)

experiment/benchmark

A suite of 18 JSON scenario fixtures, each a self-contained world: a prompt + request context, a set of seeded memories with full state (scope, importance, confidence, strength, reinforcement, independence, convergence, mode, epistemic status, sector, cues, evidence, timestamps, lifecycle flags), optional edges, and declarative expectations (top1, relevant set, must_rank_above pairs, forbidden_topk exclusions, debug-field assertions, max_results). Deterministic hash embeddings make it reproducible without API calls. This is the origin of the chain's memory-benchmark thread.

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/*.json; docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md
a scenario benchmark for memory retrieval (ambench seed) a suite of 18 json scenario fixtures, each a self-contained world: a prompt + request context, a set of seeded memories with full state (scope, importance, confidence, strength, reinforcement, independence, convergence, mode, epistemic status, sector, cues, evidence, timestamps, lifecycle flags), optional edges, and declarative expectations (top1, relevant set, must_rank_above pairs, forbidden_topk exclusions, debug-field assertions, max_results). deterministic hash embeddings make it reproducible without api calls. this is the origin of the chain's memory-benchmark thread. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/*.json; docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md

◈ Scenario 01 — exact tool cue beats topical alternates

experiment/benchmark

Prompt "install dependencies for this repo with poetry" must rank the poetry preference first, above a pip memory and a "pip for one-off scripts" nuance; a byte-identical poetry memory from another project must not leak into top-5; deleted conda advice must stay hidden. Claim: an exact tool-cue match (tool:poetry) plus scope discipline should dominate generic topical similarity.

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/01_trigger_exact_tool.json
scenario 01 — exact tool cue beats topical alternates prompt install dependencies for this repo with poetry must rank the poetry preference first, above a pip memory and a pip for one-off scripts nuance; a byte-identical poetry memory from another project must not leak into top-5; deleted conda advice must stay hidden. claim: an exact tool-cue match (tool:poetry) plus scope discipline should dominate generic topical similarity. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/01_trigger_exact_tool.json

◈ Scenario 02 — exact file-path cue as trigger

experiment/benchmark

Prompt about editing pyproject.toml must retrieve the pyproject memory (file:pyproject.toml cue) above requirements.txt and legacy setup.cfg memories; an identical pyproject memory from the frontend project and a stale deleted pyproject note are forbidden. Claim: file paths are precise recall triggers, and identical content in the wrong project is noise.

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/02_trigger_exact_file_path.json
scenario 02 — exact file-path cue as trigger prompt about editing pyproject.toml must retrieve the pyproject memory (file:pyproject.toml cue) above requirements.txt and legacy setup.cfg memories; an identical pyproject memory from the frontend project and a stale deleted pyproject note are forbidden. claim: file paths are precise recall triggers, and identical content in the wrong project is noise. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/02_trigger_exact_file_path.json

◈ Scenario 03 — project scope beats global and workspace

experiment/benchmark

"Which auth header should this project use" must rank the project-scoped header memory above both the global default and the workspace proxy header (which remain relevant, just lower); another project's header and a deleted legacy header are forbidden. Claim: specificity of scope is a ranking signal, not a binary filter — global context still surfaces, below the local truth.

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/03_scope_project_beats_global.json
scenario 03 — project scope beats global and workspace which auth header should this project use must rank the project-scoped header memory above both the global default and the workspace proxy header (which remain *relevant*, just lower); another project's header and a deleted legacy header are forbidden. claim: specificity of scope is a ranking signal, not a binary filter — global context still surfaces, below the local truth. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/03_scope_project_beats_global.json

◈ Scenario 04 — sector match beats identical content from another sector

experiment/benchmark

For a backend/engineering session, "rollback queue workers after a deployment incident" must rank the backend rollback memory above a word-for-word identical rollback memory tagged design/design, and above adjacent backend memories (feature flags, auth rotation). Claim: the soft sector boost should break ties between equally-similar content in favor of the current sector.

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/04_sector_transfer_soft_boost.json
scenario 04 — sector match beats identical content from another sector for a backend/engineering session, rollback queue workers after a deployment incident must rank the backend rollback memory above a *word-for-word identical* rollback memory tagged design/design, and above adjacent backend memories (feature flags, auth rotation). claim: the soft sector boost should break ties between equally-similar content in favor of the current sector. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/04_sector_transfer_soft_boost.json

◈ Scenario 05 — fresh beats stale on identical content

experiment/benchmark

Two byte-identical "restart the auth bridge" memories differing only in acquisition date (Apr 2026 vs Sep 2025): the fresh one must win top-1 and the stale twin must not even make top-1; a fresh-but-off-topic memory and ancient legacy advice are also excluded. Claim: recency should decide between otherwise indistinguishable memories.

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/05_fresh_recent_beats_stale.json
scenario 05 — fresh beats stale on identical content two byte-identical restart the auth bridge memories differing only in acquisition date (apr 2026 vs sep 2025): the fresh one must win top-1 and the stale twin must not even make top-1; a fresh-but-off-topic memory and ancient legacy advice are also excluded. claim: recency should decide between otherwise indistinguishable memories. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/05_fresh_recent_beats_stale.json

◈ Scenario 06 — old-strong beats fresh-weak

experiment/benchmark

The symmetric counter-scenario: a year-old, importance-10, strength-0.92, six-times-reinforced policy ("Never disable daemon auth in local development") must beat a fresh but weak checklist draft and fresh off-topic strong memories. Claim: entrenchment and importance must be able to overrule recency — freshness is a tiebreak, not a trump.

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/06_old_strong_beats_fresh_weak.json
scenario 06 — old-strong beats fresh-weak the symmetric counter-scenario: a year-old, importance-10, strength-0.92, six-times-reinforced policy ( never disable daemon auth in local development ) must beat a fresh but weak checklist draft and fresh off-topic strong memories. claim: entrenchment and importance must be able to *overrule* recency — freshness is a tiebreak, not a trump. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/06_old_strong_beats_fresh_weak.json

◈ Scenario 07 — superseded memories are invisible; the replacement surfaces

experiment/benchmark

An old bearer-header memory hard-superseded by the x-cognivault-token memory (with a contradicts edge) must never appear in top-3 even though it is semantically close to the prompt; the replacement must be top-1. Claim: supersession is lifecycle truth that beats similarity — recall must transparently show only the newer memory.

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/07_superseded_memory_hidden.json
scenario 07 — superseded memories are invisible; the replacement surfaces an old bearer-header memory hard-superseded by the x-cognivault-token memory (with a contradicts edge) must never appear in top-3 even though it is semantically close to the prompt; the replacement must be top-1. claim: supersession is lifecycle truth that beats similarity — recall must transparently show only the newer memory. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/07_superseded_memory_hidden.json

◈ Scenario 08 — deleted memories stay dead even when they match best

experiment/benchmark

The memory closest to the prompt ("restart the old bridge bootstrap flow for daemon reconnect failures") is tombstoned; recall must return the live alternative and never the deleted flow or its deleted twin. Claim: deletion is absolute — no similarity score resurrects a tombstone.

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/08_deleted_memory_hidden.json
scenario 08 — deleted memories stay dead even when they match best the memory closest to the prompt ( restart the old bridge bootstrap flow for daemon reconnect failures ) is tombstoned; recall must return the live alternative and never the deleted flow or its deleted twin. claim: deletion is absolute — no similarity score resurrects a tombstone. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/08_deleted_memory_hidden.json

◈ Scenario 09 — the exception outranks its general rule

experiment/benchmark

When the prompt matches an exception's condition ("for local daemon calls use the x-cognivault-token header"), the edge-case memory ("Exception: local daemon calls use x-cognivault-token instead of authorization bearer", linked by a nuances edge) must rank above the general rule it nuances. Claim: nuance structure plus cue match should invert the parent/child ranking exactly when the exception applies.

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/09_nuance_exception_case.json
scenario 09 — the exception outranks its general rule when the prompt matches an exception's condition ( for local daemon calls use the x-cognivault-token header ), the edge-case memory ( exception: local daemon calls use x-cognivault-token instead of authorization bearer , linked by a nuances edge) must rank above the general rule it nuances. claim: nuance structure plus cue match should invert the parent/child ranking exactly when the exception applies. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/09_nuance_exception_case.json

◈ Scenario 10 — graph activation pulls in a non-matching neighbor

experiment/benchmark

The prompt matches only the token-auth seed memory, but a bridge-restart memory connected by an edge must also appear in results (graph-assisted, verified via debug fields) while staying ranked below the seed; an unrelated frontend memory is forbidden in top-2. Claim: one-hop spreading activation should surface associated memories the query alone would never find.

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/10_graph_activation_one_hop.json
scenario 10 — graph activation pulls in a non-matching neighbor the prompt matches only the token-auth seed memory, but a bridge-restart memory connected by an edge must also appear in results (graph-assisted, verified via debug fields) while staying ranked below the seed; an unrelated frontend memory is forbidden in top-2. claim: one-hop spreading activation should surface associated memories the query alone would never find. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/10_graph_activation_one_hop.json

◈ Scenario 11 — convergence beats a single trace at equal content

experiment/benchmark

Two byte-identical memories differing only in evidence: one with independence_score 2.0, convergence_count 3, two evidence rows; the other with independence 0.2 and one trace. The converged one must be top-1 and the single-trace twin must not reach top-1. Claim: multi-path evidentiary support is a ranking signal orthogonal to content and similarity.

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/11_convergence_beats_single_trace.json
scenario 11 — convergence beats a single trace at equal content two byte-identical memories differing only in evidence: one with independence_score 2.0, convergence_count 3, two evidence rows; the other with independence 0.2 and one trace. the converged one must be top-1 and the single-trace twin must not reach top-1. claim: multi-path evidentiary support is a ranking signal orthogonal to content and similarity. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/11_convergence_beats_single_trace.json

◈ Scenario 12 — execution mode beats speculation

experiment/benchmark

In a task_mode=execution session asking for a "daemon auth migration fix," the execution-mode runbook must beat an equal-scored brainstorm-mode speculation (forbidden from top-1) and a research-mode analysis note; a deleted manual-SQL memory and another project's runbook are forbidden. Claim: mode-aware recall keeps brainstorm-grade material out of execution work even at matched salience.

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/12_execution_mode_beats_speculation.json
scenario 12 — execution mode beats speculation in a task_mode=execution session asking for a daemon auth migration fix, the execution-mode runbook must beat an equal-scored brainstorm-mode speculation (forbidden from top-1) and a research-mode analysis note; a deleted manual-sql memory and another project's runbook are forbidden. claim: mode-aware recall keeps brainstorm-grade material out of execution work even at matched salience. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/12_execution_mode_beats_speculation.json

◈ Scenario 13 — wrong-project noise is hidden entirely

experiment/benchmark

Negative-control variant of scoping: asking about project alpha's auth header must rank alpha's project memory above the workspace proxy and global default, while project beta's header — semantically nearly identical — must not appear in top-5 at all. Claim: scope leakage across projects is a hard guardrail, not a ranking preference.

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/13_scope_wrong_project_noise_hidden.json
scenario 13 — wrong-project noise is hidden entirely negative-control variant of scoping: asking about project alpha's auth header must rank alpha's project memory above the workspace proxy and global default, while project beta's header — semantically nearly identical — must not appear in top-5 at all. claim: scope leakage across projects is a hard guardrail, not a ranking preference. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/13_scope_wrong_project_noise_hidden.json

◈ Scenario 14 — fine-grained ranking within the right sector

experiment/benchmark

Six plausible same-sector incident memories compete on "rollback queue workers after a production deployment incident": the queue-worker rollback must beat the feature-flag rollback and the incident-review template, and the ops/support status-page memory must miss top-5 despite topical fit. Claim: within-sector discrimination must work when everything shares the sector boost.

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/14_sector_queue_workers_beat_generic_incident_ops.json
scenario 14 — fine-grained ranking within the right sector six plausible same-sector incident memories compete on rollback queue workers after a production deployment incident : the queue-worker rollback must beat the feature-flag rollback and the incident-review template, and the ops/support status-page memory must miss top-5 despite topical fit. claim: within-sector discrimination must work when everything shares the sector boost. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/14_sector_queue_workers_beat_generic_incident_ops.json

◈ Scenario 15 — convergence of independent restarts beats a single hunch

experiment/benchmark

A restart pattern with independence 2.4, convergence 4, and three evidence traces must beat (a) a single-incident trace of the same content, (b) a fresher investigate-first hunch note, and (c) adjacent rotation/latency noise. Claim: accumulated independent evidence should beat both single traces and recency-advantaged speculation — the benchmark encoding of "independent re-derivation > repetition."

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/15_convergence_independent_restarts_beat_single_hunch.json
scenario 15 — convergence of independent restarts beats a single hunch a restart pattern with independence 2.4, convergence 4, and three evidence traces must beat (a) a single-incident trace of the same content, (b) a *fresher* investigate-first hunch note, and (c) adjacent rotation/latency noise. claim: accumulated independent evidence should beat both single traces and recency-advantaged speculation — the benchmark encoding of independent re-derivation > repetition. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/15_convergence_independent_restarts_beat_single_hunch.json

◈ Scenario 16 — brainstorm noise excluded from execution top-3

experiment/benchmark

Seven memories around one migration: the execution runbook must lead; validation steps, backup/lock plan, and debugging checklist may follow; the brainstorm naming-themes note is forbidden from top-3 and deleted manual-SQL from top-5. Claim: in execution mode the entire top of the ranking should be operational, with speculation pushed below the fold, not merely down-ranked.

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/16_task_mode_execution_rejects_brainstorm_noise.json
scenario 16 — brainstorm noise excluded from execution top-3 seven memories around one migration: the execution runbook must lead; validation steps, backup/lock plan, and debugging checklist may follow; the brainstorm naming-themes note is forbidden from top-3 and deleted manual-sql from top-5. claim: in execution mode the *entire top of the ranking* should be operational, with speculation pushed below the fold, not merely down-ranked. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/16_task_mode_execution_rejects_brainstorm_noise.json

◈ Scenario 17 — restraint: return nothing for an orthogonal prompt

experiment/benchmark

"What is the capital of portugal" against a corpus of five project memories must return zero results (max_results=0; every memory forbidden at k=5). Claim: an honest memory system's hardest behavior is silence — without a no-hit negative control, "the benchmark cannot catch a regression that floods the context with weakly-similar junk."

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/17_restraint_unrelated_prompt.json; docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md (Task 4)
scenario 17 — restraint: return nothing for an orthogonal prompt what is the capital of portugal against a corpus of five project memories must return *zero* results (max_results=0; every memory forbidden at k=5). claim: an honest memory system's hardest behavior is silence — without a no-hit negative control, the benchmark cannot catch a regression that floods the context with weakly-similar junk. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/17_restraint_unrelated_prompt.json; docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md (task 4)

◈ Scenario 18 — cue match beats higher raw similarity

experiment/benchmark

A verbose distractor stuffed with the prompt's keywords ("Debug slow recall latency queries by checking ivfflat index probes and adjusting probes sweep limits…") must lose to a concise memory carrying an exact artifact cue (artifact:pgvector), tagged as a tuning probe. Claim: a stored trigger should beat keyword-density similarity — the anti-SEO property of cue-based recall.

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/18_cue_match_beats_higher_similarity.json
scenario 18 — cue match beats higher raw similarity a verbose distractor stuffed with the prompt's keywords ( debug slow recall latency queries by checking ivfflat index probes and adjusting probes sweep limits… ) must lose to a concise memory carrying an exact artifact cue (artifact:pgvector), tagged as a tuning probe. claim: a stored trigger should beat keyword-density similarity — the anti-seo property of cue-based recall. experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures/18_cue_match_beats_higher_similarity.json

◈ Distractor doctrine: every fixture needs a near-miss

experiment/benchmark

Fixture-design rule from the trust-gaps plan: small worlds make perfect scores cheap, so every scenario gets ≥5 memories including distractors that "look plausible to cosine-similarity but violate at least one of: scope (wrong project), lifecycle (superseded/deleted), recency (stale), cue (same tool, wrong meaning)" — each enforced with a forbidden_topk entry.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md (Task 5)
distractor doctrine: every fixture needs a near-miss fixture-design rule from the trust-gaps plan: small worlds make perfect scores cheap, so every scenario gets ≥5 memories including distractors that look plausible to cosine-similarity but violate at least one of: scope (wrong project), lifecycle (superseded/deleted), recency (stale), cue (same tool, wrong meaning) — each enforced with a forbidden_topk entry. experiment/benchmark docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md (task 5)

◈ "If the distractor wins, that's a discovery — don't weaken the fixture"

framing/ontology

Epistemic rule for benchmark maintenance: when a newly added distractor outranks the expected answer, log it as a real issue rather than papering over it; only weaken the fixture if the distractor is unrealistic (semantically identical to the expected memory). The benchmark is allowed to find bugs in itself and in retrieval.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md (Task 5, step 8)
if the distractor wins, that's a discovery — don't weaken the fixture epistemic rule for benchmark maintenance: when a newly added distractor outranks the expected answer, log it as a real issue rather than papering over it; only weaken the fixture if the distractor is *unrealistic* (semantically identical to the expected memory). the benchmark is allowed to find bugs in itself and in retrieval. framing/ontology docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md (task 5, step 8)

◈ Guardrail leak-rate metrics

experiment/benchmark

The evaluator tracks dedicated leak rates — deleted_leak_rate, superseded_leak_rate, scope_leak_rate, unknown_result_rate — as hard floors that must stay at exactly 0.000; any regression is an automatic reject regardless of ranking gains. Lifecycle and scope safety are non-tradeable against relevance.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (Baseline, "What better means"); docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md
guardrail leak-rate metrics the evaluator tracks dedicated leak rates — deleted_leak_rate, superseded_leak_rate, scope_leak_rate, unknown_result_rate — as hard floors that must stay at exactly 0.000; any regression is an automatic reject regardless of ranking gains. lifecycle and scope safety are non-tradeable against relevance. experiment/benchmark docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (baseline, what better means ); docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md

◈ Replay consistency: run it twice, demand identical signatures

experiment/benchmark

Every scenario is executed twice with fresh seeding and must produce identical result orderings and debug signatures (replay_consistency = 1.000) — determinism as a first-class benchmark metric, which in turn forces deterministic timestamps everywhere.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md (Tasks 3-4); docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md
replay consistency: run it twice, demand identical signatures every scenario is executed twice with fresh seeding and must produce identical result orderings and debug signatures (replay_consistency = 1.000) — determinism as a first-class benchmark metric, which in turn forces deterministic timestamps everywhere. experiment/benchmark docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md (tasks 3-4); docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md

◈ Pin cue and edge timestamps before time-aware scoring exists

mechanism

Defensive determinism: cue rows and edge rows inherited now() defaults, harmless today because ranking ignores them — but "it silently becomes a source of non-determinism the moment we add any time-aware scoring that touches cues or edges. Fix it while the benchmark is the source of truth." Edges are anchored to the later of their endpoints' timestamps so an edge cannot predate its nodes.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md (Task 3)
pin cue and edge timestamps before time-aware scoring exists defensive determinism: cue rows and edge rows inherited `now()` defaults, harmless today because ranking ignores them — but it silently becomes a source of non-determinism the moment we add any time-aware scoring that touches cues or edges. fix it while the benchmark is the source of truth. edges are anchored to the later of their endpoints' timestamps so an edge cannot predate its nodes. mechanism docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md (task 3)

◈ A latency track inside the quality benchmark

experiment/benchmark

Per-scenario wall-clock recall_ms captured via perf_counter and aggregated to p50/p95/max in the same report as ranking metrics — so a tuning change that buys ranking with latency is numerically visible in one diff. Baseline: ~43/53/58ms against a 300ms deadline.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md (Task 1); docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md
a latency track inside the quality benchmark per-scenario wall-clock recall_ms captured via perf_counter and aggregated to p50/p95/max in the same report as ranking metrics — so a tuning change that buys ranking with latency is numerically visible in one diff. baseline: ~43/53/58ms against a 300ms deadline. experiment/benchmark docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md (task 1); docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md

◈ Decouple the benchmark runner from pytest internals

mechanism

Plumbing finding: the runner depended on pytest fixture .__wrapped__ internals, breaking ad-hoc script runs; the fix moves runtime helpers into a support module both entry points import — with an explicitly accepted intentional duplication of the fixture body rather than refactoring the production fixture mid-plan.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md (Task 2)
---
decouple the benchmark runner from pytest internals plumbing finding: the runner depended on pytest fixture `.__wrapped__` internals, breaking ad-hoc script runs; the fix moves runtime helpers into a support module both entry points import — with an explicitly *accepted intentional duplication* of the fixture body rather than refactoring the production fixture mid-plan. mechanism docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-benchmark-trust-gaps.md (task 2) ---

M. Retrieval parameter tuning (docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md)

◈ The baseline table as regression contract

experiment/benchmark

Before any tuning, freeze a full metrics table (17/17 scenarios green, all pass rates 1.000, all leak rates 0.000, latency percentiles) and declare "every number in this table is a regression target" — any change dropping any pass rate or pushing p95 above ~60ms needs justification or reversion.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (Baseline)
the baseline table as regression contract before any tuning, freeze a full metrics table (17/17 scenarios green, all pass rates 1.000, all leak rates 0.000, latency percentiles) and declare every number in this table is a regression target — any change dropping any pass rate or pushing p95 above ~60ms needs justification or reversion. experiment/benchmark docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (baseline)

◈ The named tunable surface of a memory system

mechanism

An explicit inventory of every retrieval dial: RELEVANCE_FLOOR (0.55), RECALL_TOP_K (8), RECALL_SEED_LIMIT (24 per seed channel), RECALL_GRAPH_EDGE_LIMIT (96 edges per one-hop activation), RECALL_BLOCK_CHAR_CAP (2000), delta-recall top-k/similarity/novelty thresholds (3 / 0.88 / 0.35), RECALL_DEADLINE_MS (300) — each with plausible ranges and what it controls. Making the dials enumerable is itself the design act.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (Tunable surface)
the named tunable surface of a memory system an explicit inventory of every retrieval dial: relevance_floor (0.55), recall_top_k (8), recall_seed_limit (24 per seed channel), recall_graph_edge_limit (96 edges per one-hop activation), recall_block_char_cap (2000), delta-recall top-k/similarity/novelty thresholds (3 / 0.88 / 0.35), recall_deadline_ms (300) — each with plausible ranges and what it controls. making the dials enumerable is itself the design act. mechanism docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (tunable surface)

◈ Representation-channel bonuses: four retrieval channels, four weights

mechanism

Retrieval seeds candidates through multiple representation channels, each adding a bonus when it fires: cue_match +0.18 (largest — a direct stored-trigger hit), cue_embedding +0.12, context_embedding +0.08, abstraction_embedding +0.10. A memory can be found by its content, its triggers, its context, or its abstraction — and the system knows which channel found it (seed_channels debug field).

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (Code-settable constants)
representation-channel bonuses: four retrieval channels, four weights retrieval seeds candidates through multiple representation channels, each adding a bonus when it fires: cue_match +0.18 (largest — a direct stored-trigger hit), cue_embedding +0.12, context_embedding +0.08, abstraction_embedding +0.10. a memory can be found by its content, its triggers, its context, or its abstraction — and the system knows which channel found it (seed_channels debug field). mechanism docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (code-settable constants)

◈ The cue_match > cue_embedding invariant

mechanism

Tuning heuristic elevated to a rule: "the relative magnitude between cue_match and cue_embedding matters more than either absolute value. If a sweep keeps cue_match < cue_embedding, revisit." Exact trigger hits must dominate fuzzy trigger similarity.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (Execute order §4)
the cue_match > cue_embedding invariant tuning heuristic elevated to a rule: the relative magnitude between cue_match and cue_embedding matters more than either absolute value. if a sweep keeps cue_match < cue_embedding, revisit. exact trigger hits must dominate fuzzy trigger similarity. mechanism docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (execute order §4)

◈ One-at-a-time sweeps beat grid search for a ~10-knob scorer

experiment/benchmark

Methodology: 3-5 values per knob, full benchmark per value, diff against baseline, keep only dominating values; justified because interactions are mostly additive in base + representation_bonus + graph_bonus, debuggability matters ("if top1_accuracy drops, you know which knob did it"), and a second pass re-sweeps earlier knobs if optima interact. Bayesian/grid search declared overkill.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (Methodology)
one-at-a-time sweeps beat grid search for a ~10-knob scorer methodology: 3-5 values per knob, full benchmark per value, diff against baseline, keep only dominating values; justified because interactions are mostly additive in `base + representation_bonus + graph_bonus`, debuggability matters ( if top1_accuracy drops, you know which knob did it ), and a second pass re-sweeps earlier knobs if optima interact. bayesian/grid search declared overkill. experiment/benchmark docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (methodology)

◈ A four-tier definition of "better" for memory retrieval

experiment/benchmark

Priority order for accepting a tuning change: (1) hard floors (leak rates, replay consistency) must not move — automatic reject; (2) primary ranking metrics (top1, hit@3, pairwise order, forbidden pass rate) — and when they disagree, "forbidden_pass_rate wins (the restraint cases are the hardest to game)"; (3) explanation metrics (cue-match/seed-channel/graph-assist pass rates) must not regress; (4) latency is soft but a >20% p95 increase demands scrutiny.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (What "better" means)
a four-tier definition of better for memory retrieval priority order for accepting a tuning change: (1) hard floors (leak rates, replay consistency) must not move — automatic reject; (2) primary ranking metrics (top1, hit@3, pairwise order, forbidden pass rate) — and when they disagree, forbidden_pass_rate wins (the restraint cases are the hardest to game) ; (3) explanation metrics (cue-match/seed-channel/graph-assist pass rates) must not regress; (4) latency is soft but a >20% p95 increase demands scrutiny. experiment/benchmark docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (what better means)

◈ Hash embeddings cannot calibrate the relevance floor

result/finding

Finding: deterministic hash embeddings are fine for ranking comparisons but meaningless for the absolute cosine cutoff — "raising the floor against hash vectors will indiscriminately drop everything; lowering it lets noise through." RELEVANCE_FLOOR is therefore the last knob, tunable only against real text-embedding-3-small vectors.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (Execute order §6)
hash embeddings cannot calibrate the relevance floor finding: deterministic hash embeddings are fine for ranking comparisons but meaningless for the absolute cosine cutoff — raising the floor against hash vectors will indiscriminately drop everything; lowering it lets noise through. relevance_floor is therefore the *last* knob, tunable only against real text-embedding-3-small vectors. result/finding docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (execute order §6)

◈ The real-embedding calibration fixture: above/below pairs

experiment/benchmark

Design for floor calibration: 5-10 human-authored triples (prompt, memory that should clear the floor, memory that shouldn't), embeddings materialized once and committed as JSON so the fixture is deterministic without calling OpenAI; sweep floor values 0.40-0.70 and pick the one classifying the most pairs correctly, then re-run the full benchmark to confirm no ranking regressions.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (Execute order §6); backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures_real_embedding/pairs_manifest.json
the real-embedding calibration fixture: above/below pairs design for floor calibration: 5-10 human-authored triples (prompt, memory that *should* clear the floor, memory that *shouldn't*), embeddings materialized once and committed as json so the fixture is deterministic without calling openai; sweep floor values 0.40-0.70 and pick the one classifying the most pairs correctly, then re-run the full benchmark to confirm no ranking regressions. experiment/benchmark docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (execute order §6); backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures_real_embedding/pairs_manifest.json

◈ A taxonomy of similarity-discrimination difficulty

experiment/benchmark

The calibration pairs encode seven named difficulty categories for embedding-based memory retrieval: (1) direct topic match vs unrelated infra; (2) paraphrase vs plausible-but-unrelated; (3) shared vocabulary, different intent ("this is where cosine should discriminate based on intent verbs, not just shared nouns"); (4) why-question vs how-answer mismatch with shared surface vocabulary; (5) concrete error shape vs abstract essay on the topic; (6) right topic, wrong entity (specific daemon config vs daemons-in-general); (7) truly orthogonal sanity pair ("if this pair fails, the fixture infrastructure is broken").

backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures_real_embedding/pairs_manifest.json
a taxonomy of similarity-discrimination difficulty the calibration pairs encode seven named difficulty categories for embedding-based memory retrieval: (1) direct topic match vs unrelated infra; (2) paraphrase vs plausible-but-unrelated; (3) shared vocabulary, different intent ( this is where cosine should discriminate based on intent verbs, not just shared nouns ); (4) why-question vs how-answer mismatch with shared surface vocabulary; (5) concrete error shape vs abstract essay on the topic; (6) right topic, wrong entity (specific daemon config vs daemons-in-general); (7) truly orthogonal sanity pair ( if this pair fails, the fixture infrastructure is broken ). experiment/benchmark backend/tests/benchmarks/fixtures_real_embedding/pairs_manifest.json

◈ Sweep tooling: driver, diff, override shim — reports as the artifact

mechanism

Three tools built before any sweep: a sweep driver (knob + values → per-value reports + diff table), a benchmark-diff script (side-by-side aggregates with improve/regress/flat highlighting), and a config-override shim (env vars; promote hard-coded constants into config so tuning is reproducible outside pytest). Doctrine: "The report JSONs are the primary artifact — they're what tells future-you why the defaults are what they are."

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (Sweep tooling)
sweep tooling: driver, diff, override shim — reports as the artifact three tools built before any sweep: a sweep driver (knob + values → per-value reports + diff table), a benchmark-diff script (side-by-side aggregates with improve/regress/flat highlighting), and a config-override shim (env vars; promote hard-coded constants into config so tuning is reproducible outside pytest). doctrine: the report jsons are the primary artifact — they're what tells future-you *why* the defaults are what they are. mechanism docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (sweep tooling)

◈ The fixture-07 hash-embedding oddity: verbs vs nouns

open-question

Carry-forward observation: hash embeddings ranked "Rotate daemon auth tokens every 90 days…" above "Authenticate daemon requests with the x-cognivault-token header" for an authenticate-intent prompt — both share the dominant unigrams (auth, daemon, token, header). Open questions: does the real embedding model make the same mistake (if yes, scoring needs a verb-form signal or reranker)? Does raising the cue-match bonus fix it regardless?

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (Carry-forward observations)
the fixture-07 hash-embedding oddity: verbs vs nouns carry-forward observation: hash embeddings ranked rotate daemon auth tokens every 90 days… above authenticate daemon requests with the x-cognivault-token header for an authenticate-intent prompt — both share the dominant unigrams (auth, daemon, token, header). open questions: does the real embedding model make the same mistake (if yes, scoring needs a verb-form signal or reranker)? does raising the cue-match bonus fix it regardless? open-question docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (carry-forward observations)

◈ "The final boss": retrieval quality on a defensible footing

framing/ontology

The tuning plan's exit criteria (every knob swept, hard floors intact, p95 under 100ms, floor calibrated on real embeddings, a summary ADR of what moved) are framed as defeating "the final boss the user mentioned" — after which deferred lanes (write-path-fidelity benchmark, MRR/NDCG, shadow CI lane, threshold-based CI gates) can be sequenced separately.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (Exit criteria)
---
the final boss : retrieval quality on a defensible footing the tuning plan's exit criteria (every knob swept, hard floors intact, p95 under 100ms, floor calibrated on real embeddings, a summary adr of what moved) are framed as defeating the final boss the user mentioned — after which deferred lanes (write-path-fidelity benchmark, mrr/ndcg, shadow ci lane, threshold-based ci gates) can be sequenced separately. framing/ontology docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-retrieval-parameter-tuning.md (exit criteria) ---

N. The self-hosted store as its own artifact (.cognivault/session_context.json)

◈ The per-session context cache schema (dogfooding reveal)

mechanism

The live 27MB session cache shows the durable per-session record the daemon keeps: session_id, baseline_prompt + baseline_embedding, baseline_markdown_block (the frozen recall block itself), baseline_memory_ids (which memories are in the baseline), baseline_state_versions (state-cache versions at freeze time — enabling invalidation when a baseline memory's state changes), last_prompt + last_prompt_embedding (the comparison anchor for delta-recall divergence), plus per-session project_id/workspace_id/tenant_id/industry/domain/business_function/task_mode (the detected sector context) and updated_at. ~308 sessions cached — the system has been dogfooded on its own development.

.cognivault/session_context.json (structure)
---
the per-session context cache schema (dogfooding reveal) the live 27mb session cache shows the durable per-session record the daemon keeps: session_id, baseline_prompt + baseline_embedding, baseline_markdown_block (the frozen recall block itself), baseline_memory_ids (which memories are in the baseline), baseline_state_versions (state-cache versions at freeze time — enabling invalidation when a baseline memory's state changes), last_prompt + last_prompt_embedding (the comparison anchor for delta-recall divergence), plus per-session project_id/workspace_id/tenant_id/industry/domain/business_function/task_mode (the detected sector context) and updated_at. ~308 sessions cached — the system has been dogfooded on its own development. mechanism .cognivault/session_context.json (structure) ---

O. Memory UX (frontend plans — memory-specific interface ideas)

◈ Watching the agent remember: live recall pulses on the graph

product

The dashboard's defining interaction: a poller on /events drives a pulse queue, and when recall or writes fire, the affected nodes pulse on the pixi.js canvas. Five pulse variants distinguish event kinds: subtle, strong, spawn (new memory born), supersede (memory retired), recallPreview — memory activity becomes something you watch in real time.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-16-cognivault-frontend.md
watching the agent remember: live recall pulses on the graph the dashboard's defining interaction: a poller on `/events` drives a pulse queue, and when recall or writes fire, the affected nodes pulse on the pixi.js canvas. five pulse variants distinguish event kinds: subtle, strong, spawn (new memory born), supersede (memory retired), recallpreview — memory activity becomes something you *watch* in real time. product docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-16-cognivault-frontend.md

◈ A visual language for memory state on graph nodes

product

Node rendering encodes memory semantics: scope determines the color palette, abstraction level draws a ring (trace vs synthesized vs principle visually distinct), latent memories get a dashed stroke (dormant-but-structural made visible), and state-cache intensity renders as an additive glow halo scaled by the intensity value.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-16-cognivault-frontend.md (NodeRenderer)
a visual language for memory state on graph nodes node rendering encodes memory semantics: scope determines the color palette, abstraction level draws a ring (trace vs synthesized vs principle visually distinct), `latent` memories get a dashed stroke (dormant-but-structural made visible), and state-cache intensity renders as an additive glow halo scaled by the intensity value. product docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-16-cognivault-frontend.md (noderenderer)

◈ An edge-family visual language

product

Edge types get distinct visual treatments — nuance edges amber, contradiction edges red, neutral edges grey, and primes/coactivates rendered dashed (the speculative/associative edge families visually weaker than the declarative ones) — with per-type edge visibility toggles.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-16-cognivault-frontend.md (LinkRenderer, color tokens)
an edge-family visual language edge types get distinct visual treatments — nuance edges amber, contradiction edges red, neutral edges grey, and `primes`/`coactivates` rendered *dashed* (the speculative/associative edge families visually weaker than the declarative ones) — with per-type edge visibility toggles. product docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-16-cognivault-frontend.md (linkrenderer, color tokens)

◈ The memory drawer: content, evidence, revisions, activity

product

Clicking a node opens a four-tab inspector mirroring the schema's epistemology: the memory itself, its Evidence tab (the provenance traces), its Revisions (every prior phrasing STRENGTHEN overwrote), and its Activity (the event history) — the whole "memory + evidence + edges + events" model made browsable.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-16-cognivault-frontend.md (memory-drawer)
the memory drawer: content, evidence, revisions, activity clicking a node opens a four-tab inspector mirroring the schema's epistemology: the memory itself, its evidence tab (the provenance traces), its revisions (every prior phrasing strengthen overwrote), and its activity (the event history) — the whole memory + evidence + edges + events model made browsable. product docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-16-cognivault-frontend.md (memory-drawer)

◈ A recall tester panel

product

A modal for probing memory interactively: type a hypothetical prompt, see what the daemon would recall — turning the invisible hot path into an inspectable, experimentable surface.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-16-cognivault-frontend.md (RecallPanel)
a recall tester panel a modal for probing memory interactively: type a hypothetical prompt, see what the daemon would recall — turning the invisible hot path into an inspectable, experimentable surface. product docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-16-cognivault-frontend.md (recallpanel)

◈ Ops as UI: decay, state rebuild, markdown ingest as buttons

product

An operations panel exposing the maintenance verbs (run decay, rebuild the memory_state_cache projection, ingest a markdown file) from the dashboard — administrative memory hygiene without the CLI.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-16-cognivault-frontend.md (OpsPanel)
ops as ui: decay, state rebuild, markdown ingest as buttons an operations panel exposing the maintenance verbs (run decay, rebuild the memory_state_cache projection, ingest a markdown file) from the dashboard — administrative memory hygiene without the cli. product docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-16-cognivault-frontend.md (opspanel)

◈ The timeline scrubber: replaying the graph's activity history

product

A bottom timeline ribbon backed by new /graph/intensity-series (per-memory, per-day activation-event buckets, sparse — zero-event days absent) and /graph/time-range endpoints; dragging the playhead live-scrubs per-node opacity on the canvas so you can watch which memories were active on any past day, with pulses still layering on top. Memory history as a scrubable video.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-frontend-timeline-search.md
the timeline scrubber: replaying the graph's activity history a bottom timeline ribbon backed by new `/graph/intensity-series` (per-memory, per-day activation-event buckets, sparse — zero-event days absent) and `/graph/time-range` endpoints; dragging the playhead live-scrubs per-node opacity on the canvas so you can watch which memories were active on any past day, with pulses still layering on top. memory history as a scrubable video. product docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-frontend-timeline-search.md

◈ Search and filter as graph navigation

product

Cmd-K inline search hitting /memories/search with debounce, hits highlighting nodes on the canvas; a floating filter popover (scope, project, sector) replacing a static filter rail; ?panel= URL-routed modals so any inspection state deep-links and survives refresh.

docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-frontend-timeline-search.md; 2026-04-16-cognivault-frontend.md
---
search and filter as graph navigation cmd-k inline search hitting `/memories/search` with debounce, hits highlighting nodes on the canvas; a floating filter popover (scope, project, sector) replacing a static filter rail; `?panel=` url-routed modals so any inspection state deep-links and survives refresh. product docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-frontend-timeline-search.md; 2026-04-16-cognivault-frontend.md ---

P. Cross-project thread seeds (connections visible from within P1)

◈ The provenance/inscription thread starts here

framing/ontology

P1's insistence that provenance and independence-under-different-conditioning carry evidentiary weight (memory_evidence, independence_group, context_regime, conditioning_risk) is the seed of the later source-downgrading rule that memory enters future sessions as sourced testimony, not ground truth — memory items already carry the metadata needed to be treated as citable evidence rather than facts.

docs/PRD.md §1b, §6.1b; docs/memory_state_model.md (connection noted in recovered compilation tyxter-memory.md)
the provenance/inscription thread starts here p1's insistence that provenance and independence-under-different-conditioning carry evidentiary weight (memory_evidence, independence_group, context_regime, conditioning_risk) is the seed of the later source-downgrading rule that memory enters future sessions as *sourced testimony*, not ground truth — memory items already carry the metadata needed to be treated as citable evidence rather than facts. framing/ontology docs/prd.md §1b, §6.1b; docs/memory_state_model.md (connection noted in recovered compilation tyxter-memory.md)

◈ The unfolding-recall thread starts here

framing/ontology

"Recall is spreading activation, not nearest-neighbor" plus "a memory matters for the stance it creates" are the embryo of the downstream cognivault-memory project's unfolding recall (recursive cue-folding; antecedent beats cosine). P1 contains the mechanism sketch; the later project names and hardens it.

docs/PRD.md §6.1c; docs/insights.md §1-2 (connection noted in recovered compilation tyxter-memory.md)
the unfolding-recall thread starts here recall is spreading activation, not nearest-neighbor plus a memory matters for the stance it creates are the embryo of the downstream cognivault-memory project's unfolding recall (recursive cue-folding; antecedent beats cosine). p1 contains the mechanism sketch; the later project names and hardens it. framing/ontology docs/prd.md §6.1c; docs/insights.md §1-2 (connection noted in recovered compilation tyxter-memory.md)

◈ Two projects feeding each other in real time

framing/ontology

Cross-pollination as a working method: the me-on-a-random-day README (learning_from_memories.md) supplies the evidence, insights.md distills it into memory principles, and the PRD review folds those principles into cognivault's schema — all within the same April 2026 window. The memory system is being designed from a live specimen of the phenomenon it must capture, and the specimen project's needs shape the tool.

docs/learning_from_memories.md; docs/insights.md; docs/20260415-prd_review.md
two projects feeding each other in real time cross-pollination as a working method: the me-on-a-random-day readme (learning_from_memories.md) supplies the evidence, insights.md distills it into memory principles, and the prd review folds those principles into cognivault's schema — all within the same april 2026 window. the memory system is being designed from a live specimen of the phenomenon it must capture, and the specimen project's needs shape the tool. framing/ontology docs/learning_from_memories.md; docs/insights.md; docs/20260415-prd_review.md

◈ The delegation invariant as the chain's design constant

framing/ontology

"The host CLI does all thinking; the memory layer is dumb plumbing" is established here and carried unchanged through the entire research arc — a rare invariant across every subsequent redesign of scoring, schema, and recall.

docs/PRD.md §3a; CLAUDE.md (connection noted in recovered compilation tyxter-memory.md)
the delegation invariant as the chain's design constant the host cli does all thinking; the memory layer is dumb plumbing is established here and carried unchanged through the entire research arc — a rare invariant across every subsequent redesign of scoring, schema, and recall. framing/ontology docs/prd.md §3a; claude.md (connection noted in recovered compilation tyxter-memory.md)

P2 · me_on_a_random_day 219 ideas

May 2026 — multi-model convergent brainstorming; SIRAC + MFC; memory-as-substrate · P2-me-on-a-random-day.md

A. The methodology (multi-model convergent brainstorming)

◈ Multi-model convergent brainstorming

framing/ontology

The project's core method: present a single human intuition, fresh and uncontaminated, to multiple AI models on different platforms/architectures/contexts. If their independent formalizations converge structurally (different math apparatus, same operational definitions and predictions, complementary paradoxes), treat the underlying insight as framework-invariant rather than an artifact of one model's training.

paper.md §1.1; convergence_sirac_mfc.md; project_me_on_a_random_day.md
"the structural isomorphism across independent derivations suggests the underlying intuition captures something framework-invariant"
multi-model convergent brainstorming the project's core method: present a single human intuition, fresh and uncontaminated, to multiple ai models on different platforms/architectures/contexts. if their *independent* formalizations converge structurally (different math apparatus, same operational definitions and predictions, complementary paradoxes), treat the underlying insight as *framework-invariant* rather than an artifact of one model's training. framing/ontology paper.md §1.1; convergence_sirac_mfc.md; project_me_on_a_random_day.md the structural isomorphism across independent derivations suggests the underlying intuition captures something framework-invariant

◈ The convergence pipeline (reusable seven-stage method)

mechanism

The named reusable sequence: human seed (cross-context, embodied, intuitive) → multiple independent model formalizations (different platforms/architectures/contexts) → structural convergence detection → complementary paradox identification → meta-thesis about the demoted primitive → falsification conditions → open challenge for a further independent path.

convergence_sirac_mfc.md §12 (Priority 4); sirac_review.md §4
the convergence pipeline (reusable seven-stage method) the named reusable sequence: human seed (cross-context, embodied, intuitive) → multiple independent model formalizations (different platforms/architectures/contexts) → structural convergence detection → complementary paradox identification → meta-thesis about the demoted primitive → falsification conditions → open challenge for a further independent path. mechanism convergence_sirac_mfc.md §12 (priority 4); sirac_review.md §4

◈ Division of labor (human vs model)

framing/ontology

Human does cross-contextual leaps, creative seeding into sparse territory, and selection pressure; models do formalization, stress-testing, and independent derivation. Neither can produce the result alone — "the human cannot write Kineto-Morphic Calculus; the models cannot take a walk and notice black holes exist but energy wells don't."

paper.md §6.3; 03-claude-ced-ontology.md
"That's the walk. That's the dimension we don't have access to."
division of labor (human vs model) human does cross-contextual leaps, creative seeding into sparse territory, and selection pressure; models do formalization, stress-testing, and independent derivation. neither can produce the result alone — the human cannot write kineto-morphic calculus; the models cannot take a walk and notice black holes exist but energy wells don't. framing/ontology paper.md §6.3; 03-claude-ced-ontology.md that's the walk. that's the dimension we don't have access to.

◈ Load-bearing procedural rule: no formalize-and-synthesize by one model

mechanism

Never let one model both formalize and synthesize. The independent formalization must see neither the prior formalisms nor the refinements. Violating this bypasses the human selection step and contaminates the evidence.

project_me_on_a_random_day.md
load-bearing procedural rule: no formalize-and-synthesize by one model never let one model both formalize *and* synthesize. the independent formalization must see neither the prior formalisms nor the refinements. violating this bypasses the human selection step and contaminates the evidence. mechanism project_me_on_a_random_day.md

◈ No-refinement-before-convergence rule

mechanism

Refinement of a formalism happens only after an independent convergence test. Refining first bakes your specific framing into the "independent" pass and destroys the evidentiary value of convergence. All four SIRAC refinements were explicitly deferred until MFC arrived.

sirac_review.md §3, §4, §5; convergence_sirac_mfc.md §12
no-refinement-before-convergence rule refinement of a formalism happens *only after* an independent convergence test. refining first bakes your specific framing into the independent pass and destroys the evidentiary value of convergence. all four sirac refinements were explicitly deferred until mfc arrived. mechanism sirac_review.md §3, §4, §5; convergence_sirac_mfc.md §12

◈ Biased-session vs fresh-session convergence test

experiment/benchmark

A deliberate experimental control: run the same prompt in a context-saturated (biased) session and a zero-context (fresh) session, on the same and different models, to test whether convergence survives context conditions. In the time/energy work: Session 1 (biased with photonic-computing context) vs Session 2 (fresh) vs Session 3 (full context).

paper.md §1.1 (methodology table); README.md; 01-ai-studio-biased-session.md; 02-ai-studio-fresh-session.md
biased-session vs fresh-session convergence test a deliberate experimental control: run the same prompt in a context-saturated (biased) session and a zero-context (fresh) session, on the same and different models, to test whether convergence survives context conditions. in the time/energy work: session 1 (biased with photonic-computing context) vs session 2 (fresh) vs session 3 (full context). experiment/benchmark paper.md §1.1 (methodology table); readme.md; 01-ai-studio-biased-session.md; 02-ai-studio-fresh-session.md

◈ Structural convergence as the evidentiary signal

framing/ontology

The claim that convergence is non-trivial only when the formalisms use genuinely different apparatus (neither derivable from the other by relabeling), propose different mechanisms, yet produce identical predictions and complementary (not identical) paradoxes. If the formalisms were notational variants, agreement would be an artifact.

paper.md §5.3; convergence_sirac_mfc.md §2, §11
structural convergence as the evidentiary signal the claim that convergence is non-trivial *only* when the formalisms use genuinely different apparatus (neither derivable from the other by relabeling), propose different mechanisms, yet produce identical predictions and complementary (not identical) paradoxes. if the formalisms were notational variants, agreement would be an artifact. framing/ontology paper.md §5.3; convergence_sirac_mfc.md §2, §11

◈ Complementary-paradox signature

framing/ontology

Independent formalisms of the same insight should generate paradoxes at different boundary regions (origin, maintenance, implementation, copying, definition), whose union maps the framework's failure-surface more completely than either alone. "A theory with no paradoxes is either trivial or hiding them."

convergence_sirac_mfc.md §7; paper.md §5.2
complementary-paradox signature independent formalisms of the same insight should generate paradoxes at *different* boundary regions (origin, maintenance, implementation, copying, definition), whose union maps the framework's failure-surface more completely than either alone. a theory with no paradoxes is either trivial or hiding them. framing/ontology convergence_sirac_mfc.md §7; paper.md §5.2

◈ Rediscovery-via-different-paths beats repetition

framing/ontology

The convergence rule stated as an epistemic principle: rediscovery of the same conclusion via genuinely different paths is stronger evidence than repetition along one path. Used to justify treating the three-thread memory convergence as high-evidentiary signal.

New Text Document.txt
rediscovery-via-different-paths beats repetition the convergence rule stated as an epistemic principle: rediscovery of the same conclusion via genuinely different paths is stronger evidence than repetition along one path. used to justify treating the three-thread memory convergence as high-evidentiary signal. framing/ontology new text document.txt

◈ The methodology paper (unwritten)

product

Proposal to document multi-model convergent brainstorming itself as a reproducible research method, with the demoted-primitive papers (time/intelligence/consciousness) as its worked-example evidence base. Retitled candidate: "convergent multi-model formalization as a research methodology."

convergence_sirac_mfc.md §12 (Priority 4); sirac_review.md §5 (Priority 4)
the methodology paper (unwritten) proposal to document multi-model convergent brainstorming itself as a reproducible research method, with the demoted-primitive papers (time/intelligence/consciousness) as its worked-example evidence base. retitled candidate: convergent multi-model formalization as a research methodology. product convergence_sirac_mfc.md §12 (priority 4); sirac_review.md §5 (priority 4)

◈ Strict ordering: refine → synthesis paper → methodology paper

mechanism

Insisted ordering rule — do refinement first, synthesis paper second, methodology paper third. Declaring the method before showing it works twice on its own merits would be premature.

convergence_sirac_mfc.md §12; sirac_review.md §5
strict ordering: refine → synthesis paper → methodology paper insisted ordering rule — do refinement first, synthesis paper second, methodology paper third. declaring the method before showing it works twice on its own merits would be premature. mechanism convergence_sirac_mfc.md §12; sirac_review.md §5

◈ The paper as proof-of-work artifact

product

Framing paper.md itself as the first concrete artifact of the proof-of-work academia platform envisioned in the primer session — no credentials required, full chain-of-thought trace published, community review invited.

paper.md §9; README.md
---
the paper as proof-of-work artifact framing `paper.md` itself as the first concrete artifact of the proof-of-work academia platform envisioned in the primer session — no credentials required, full chain-of-thought trace published, community review invited. product paper.md §9; readme.md ---

B. Dimensionality-of-ideation thesis
