◈ Cognivault as the predicted right target

framing/ontology

The memory-substrate thesis predicts cognivault's architecture matters more than scaling weights, because the four base dimensions (P, A, R, G) are most of what cognivault provides — the convergence in the synthesis is "not an accident; the work was already aimed at the right primitive."

memory_as_substrate.txt §7; project_me_on_a_random_day.md
cognivault as the predicted right target the memory-substrate thesis predicts cognivault's architecture matters more than scaling weights, because the four base dimensions (p, a, r, g) are most of what cognivault provides — the convergence in the synthesis is not an accident; the work was already aimed at the right primitive. framing/ontology memory_as_substrate.txt §7; project_me_on_a_random_day.md

◈ "Watch for a fourth instance" (meta-thesis promotion condition)

open-question

The stated condition under which the meta-pattern itself becomes the load-bearing claim: if a fourth independent demotion (fundamental phenomenon → runtime exhaust of recursion on a finite substrate) shows up in independent work, the meta-thesis becomes primary and the three demotions become its evidence.

memory_as_substrate.txt §8; memory_as_substrate_v2.txt §11
watch for a fourth instance (meta-thesis promotion condition) the stated condition under which the meta-pattern itself becomes the load-bearing claim: if a fourth independent demotion (fundamental phenomenon → runtime exhaust of recursion on a finite substrate) shows up in independent work, the meta-thesis becomes primary and the three demotions become its evidence. open-question memory_as_substrate.txt §8; memory_as_substrate_v2.txt §11

P3 · followup_random_day_2 195 ideas

May 2026 — closed-loop mnestic agent architecture; adversarial-reload; trace-memory verdict · P3-followup-random-day-2.md

◈ Attention-conditioned inscription pressure (the seed idea)

mechanism

The original spark of the whole project: trace should not be written directly by live attention A_t; instead a derived variable — an "inscription field" I_t = Ψ(A_t, Ā_t, M_t, N_t, z_t, Source_t, C_t) — decides what leaves residue. Attention selects what is active; inscription decides what becomes trace.

C:\Users\DiegoPC\Documents\GitHub\followup_random_day_2\trace_operation_revision_original.txt
"attention determines what is active now; inscription determines what becomes trace."
attention-conditioned inscription pressure (the seed idea) the original spark of the whole project: trace should not be written directly by live attention `a_t`; instead a derived variable — an inscription field `i_t = ψ(a_t, ā_t, m_t, n_t, z_t, source_t, c_t)` — decides what leaves residue. attention selects what is active; inscription decides what becomes trace. mechanism c:\users\diegopc\documents\github\followup_random_day_2\trace_operation_revision_original.txt attention determines what is active now; inscription determines what becomes trace.

◈ Residual attention as a trace-gate ingredient

mechanism

Residual attention Ā_t (lingering orientation/posture after active attention moves on) is a better trace-formation candidate than live attention alone, but should be a bias term in the inscription gate, not the primary gate (making it primary would over-inscribe what the system was already oriented toward, worsening lock-in).

trace_operation_revision_original.txt; trace_operation_revision.txt (option B, rejected as main gate)
residual attention as a trace-gate ingredient residual attention `ā_t` (lingering orientation/posture after active attention moves on) is a better trace-formation candidate than live attention alone, but should be a bias term in the inscription gate, not the primary gate (making it primary would over-inscribe what the system was already oriented toward, worsening lock-in). mechanism trace_operation_revision_original.txt; trace_operation_revision.txt (option b, rejected as main gate)

◈ Naming sweep for the inscription variable

coinage

Candidate names generated for the new variable: "trace inscription gate" (most precise), "inscription field" (fits MAFC), "retentive salience" (cognitive-science friendly), "trace-write pressure" (engineering friendly), "mnestic inscription operator" (formal). The preferred formal move: replace "attention-gated trace formation" with "attention-conditioned inscription."

trace_operation_revision_original.txt
naming sweep for the inscription variable candidate names generated for the new variable: trace inscription gate (most precise), inscription field (fits mafc), retentive salience (cognitive-science friendly), trace-write pressure (engineering friendly), mnestic inscription operator (formal). the preferred formal move: replace attention-gated trace formation with attention-conditioned inscription. coinage trace_operation_revision_original.txt

◈ The seven-level conceptual hierarchy

framing/ontology

A clean ladder distinguishing: attention (selected now) → residual attention (persisting posture) → inscription field (what gets written) → trace (retained causal residue) → memory (stabilized/addressable/foldable trace) → operation-memory (record of how memory changed a transition) → correction chain (self-indexed lineage of belief revision).

trace_operation_revision_original.txt
the seven-level conceptual hierarchy a clean ladder distinguishing: attention (selected now) → residual attention (persisting posture) → inscription field (what gets written) → trace (retained causal residue) → memory (stabilized/addressable/foldable trace) → operation-memory (record of how memory changed a transition) → correction chain (self-indexed lineage of belief revision). framing/ontology trace_operation_revision_original.txt

◈ Recursive-indexing intuition: reactivation is not automatically a new trace

framing/ontology

Once memory and attention interact, the product may or may not deserve another trace — otherwise every retrieval risks echo amplification, where the system remembers its own memory activation as if it were new experience. This is the recursive problem all three brainstorms attack.

trace_operation_revision_original.txt; trace_operation_revision.txt
recursive-indexing intuition: reactivation is not automatically a new trace once memory and attention interact, the product may or may not deserve another trace — otherwise every retrieval risks echo amplification, where the system remembers its own memory activation as if it were new experience. this is the recursive problem all three brainstorms attack. framing/ontology trace_operation_revision_original.txt; trace_operation_revision.txt

◈ Premise correction #1: attention creates eligibility, not inscription

framing/ontology

The strongest version of the critique is narrower than "attention should not affect trace": attention should create eligibility for trace formation but not by itself decide inscription, stabilization, promotion, or source status. The wrong and right premises are stated side by side.

trace_operation_revision.txt (premise check)
premise correction #1: attention creates eligibility, not inscription the strongest version of the critique is narrower than attention should not affect trace : attention should create *eligibility for trace formation* but not by itself decide inscription, stabilization, promotion, or source status. the wrong and right premises are stated side by side. framing/ontology trace_operation_revision.txt (premise check)

◈ Six candidate gate formulations, each with a verdict

mechanism

A structured design search over what trace should depend on: (A) live attention directly — reject, echo-amplification vulnerable; (B) residual attention — reject as main gate, keep as bias; (C) independent inscription field I_t — best structural move if derived, not magical; (D) eligibility/retentive salience E_t — strong but incomplete alone; (E) source-sensitive gating — mandatory but insufficient (source should determine write type not just strength); (F) composite Ψ — best overall only if decomposed into eligibility + inscription + routing.

trace_operation_revision.txt §2
six candidate gate formulations, each with a verdict a structured design search over what trace should depend on: (a) live attention directly — reject, echo-amplification vulnerable; (b) residual attention — reject as main gate, keep as bias; (c) independent inscription field i_t — best structural move if derived, not magical; (d) eligibility/retentive salience e_t — strong but incomplete alone; (e) source-sensitive gating — mandatory but insufficient (source should determine write *type* not just strength); (f) composite ψ — best overall only if decomposed into eligibility + inscription + routing. mechanism trace_operation_revision.txt §2

◈ Source-factored active substrate B_t = ⊕_s B_t^(s)

mechanism

Decompose the active workspace into source-class components (external, reactivated, inferred, simulated, fabricated, operation) so attention selects over a substrate that already carries source structure. Adopted into SSIR and MAFC v4 as X̃_t.

trace_operation_revision.txt §3; trace_operation_revision2.txt; Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex §3
source-factored active substrate b_t = ⊕_s b_t^(s) decompose the active workspace into source-class components (external, reactivated, inferred, simulated, fabricated, operation) so attention selects over a substrate that already carries source structure. adopted into ssir and mafc v4 as x̃_t. mechanism trace_operation_revision.txt §3; trace_operation_revision2.txt; source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex §3

◈ Eligibility field Λ_t / E_t (write-readiness, not write)

mechanism

An intermediate variable meaning "this attended state is eligible to be retained" — computed from attention, posture, novelty, context, self-index, source, and post-transition error — explicitly not yet a write. Eligibility can be predicted before action (Ê_t) and revised after outcome.

trace_operation_revision.txt; Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex §4
eligibility field λ_t / e_t (write-readiness, not write) an intermediate variable meaning this attended state is eligible to be retained — computed from attention, posture, novelty, context, self-index, source, and post-transition error — explicitly not yet a write. eligibility can be predicted before action (ê_t) and revised after outcome. mechanism trace_operation_revision.txt; source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex §4

◈ Per-source trace channels τ^(s) with per-source decay F^(s)

mechanismdead-end

Revision1's proposal to split the trace field itself into per-source channels, each with its own decay, summed as τ_{t+1} = ⊕_s τ_{t+1}^(s). Later rejected for MAFC v4 (multiplies trace ontology) but retained in the SSIR paper's equations and resurrected as the pre-mnestic ring-buffer design in the closed-loop spec.

trace_operation_revision.txt §3; MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §9; Closed_Loop_Mnestic_Agent_Architecture.md §3.1
"If empirical work shows interference between source classes within a single τ, splitting becomes a v5 question."
per-source trace channels τ^(s) with per-source decay f^(s) revision1's proposal to split the trace field itself into per-source channels, each with its own decay, summed as τ_{t+1} = ⊕_s τ_{t+1}^(s). later rejected for mafc v4 (multiplies trace ontology) but retained in the ssir paper's equations and resurrected as the pre-mnestic ring-buffer design in the closed-loop spec. mechanism / dead-end (for v4) trace_operation_revision.txt §3; mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §9; closed_loop_mnestic_agent_architecture.md §3.1 if empirical work shows interference between source classes within a single τ, splitting becomes a v5 question.

◈ Routing operator Ω over write classes

mechanism

Revision1's four-way routing operator (r^τ, r^op, r^corr, r^∅) = Ω(τ^(s), M, A, Ā, S, φ, Δ, z, C) that types the retentive consequence of an attended state — the precursor of the six-way routing distribution π_t.

trace_operation_revision.txt §3
routing operator ω over write classes revision1's four-way routing operator (r^τ, r^op, r^corr, r^∅) = ω(τ^(s), m, a, ā, s, φ, δ, z, c) that types the retentive consequence of an attended state — the precursor of the six-way routing distribution π_t. mechanism trace_operation_revision.txt §3

◈ Six routing cases (revision1 version)

mechanism

Case analysis of what an attended/reactivated state should become: (1) transient attended state → nothing; (2) source-tagged reactivation → reactivation log, never external evidence; (3) ordinary stabilized trace for external input; (4) operation-memory record when a folded memory changed a decision; (5) correction-chain node when Δ ≠ 0; (6) suppression/quarantine for fab/sim/react with low reliability.

trace_operation_revision.txt §4
six routing cases (revision1 version) case analysis of what an attended/reactivated state should become: (1) transient attended state → nothing; (2) source-tagged reactivation → reactivation log, never external evidence; (3) ordinary stabilized trace for external input; (4) operation-memory record when a folded memory changed a decision; (5) correction-chain node when δ ≠ 0; (6) suppression/quarantine for fab/sim/react with low reliability. mechanism trace_operation_revision.txt §4

◈ Meta-memory bloat failure mode

mechanismdead-end

If every retrieval creates an operation-memory record, operation memory explodes. Fix: write operation records only when realized or predicted fold-force exceeds threshold, or sample low-impact cases for calibration.

trace_operation_revision.txt §5.4
meta-memory bloat failure mode if every retrieval creates an operation-memory record, operation memory explodes. fix: write operation records only when realized or predicted fold-force exceeds threshold, or sample low-impact cases for calibration. dead-end (named failure mode) / mechanism trace_operation_revision.txt §5.4

◈ Credit-assignment error in operation-memory

open-questiondead-end

The system may correctly detect that memory changed a decision but attribute the change to the wrong retrieved record, corrupting operation-memory and correction chains downstream.

trace_operation_revision.txt §5.6
credit-assignment error in operation-memory the system may correctly detect that memory changed a decision but attribute the change to the wrong retrieved record, corrupting operation-memory and correction chains downstream. open-question / dead-end (failure mode) trace_operation_revision.txt §5.6

◈ Excessive provenance conservatism (creativity suppression)

mechanismdead-end

A source-disciplined agent may refuse to use internally generated hypotheses creatively because they are not externally sourced. Fix: simulated and inferred traces should be allowed, but stored as simulated/inferred, never as observed.

trace_operation_revision.txt §5.7
excessive provenance conservatism (creativity suppression) a source-disciplined agent may refuse to use internally generated hypotheses creatively because they are not externally sourced. fix: simulated and inferred traces should be allowed, but stored as simulated/inferred, never as observed. dead-end (failure mode) / mechanism trace_operation_revision.txt §5.7

◈ Under-inscription / anti-plasticity failure mode

dead-end

The gate becomes too strict: the system attends to important novelty but fails to write it; "surprising evidence is noticed but not retained." Also formulated as over-suppression: π^∅ + π^quar → 1 even under reliable external novelty, so learning halts and novelty breakthrough occurs in attention but never in memory.

trace_operation_revision.txt §5.1; trace_operation_revision2.txt §5.5; MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §5.6
under-inscription / anti-plasticity failure mode the gate becomes too strict: the system attends to important novelty but fails to write it; surprising evidence is noticed but not retained. also formulated as over-suppression: π^∅ + π^quar → 1 even under reliable external novelty, so learning halts and novelty breakthrough occurs in attention but never in memory. dead-end (failure mode) trace_operation_revision.txt §5.1; trace_operation_revision2.txt §5.5; mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §5.6

◈ Source-label error as the worse-than-retrieval-error failure

framing/ontology

Misclassifying external evidence as internal (false internalization → amnesia/under-learning) or internal content as external (false externalization → confabulated memory) is worse than ordinary retrieval error because the wrong source label controls promotion.

trace_operation_revision.txt §5.2; Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex §8
source-label error as the worse-than-retrieval-error failure misclassifying external evidence as internal (false internalization → amnesia/under-learning) or internal content as external (false externalization → confabulated memory) is worse than ordinary retrieval error because the wrong source label controls promotion. framing/ontology trace_operation_revision.txt §5.2; source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex §8

◈ Premise correction #2: the bug is the single write target, not attention→trace

framing/ontology

Revision2's adversarial correction of revision1: MAFC v3 already has an inscription gate G_t distinct from attention; the real collapse is that the recurrence has one dominant write path — the same channel receives external observation, reactivated memory, inference, and fabricated content. Adding a new I_t only helps if it is a routing variable, otherwise it merely renames the gate.

trace_operation_revision2.txt §1; MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §2
premise correction #2: the bug is the single write target, not attention→trace revision2's adversarial correction of revision1: mafc v3 already has an inscription gate g_t distinct from attention; the real collapse is that the recurrence has one dominant write path — the same channel receives external observation, reactivated memory, inference, and fabricated content. adding a new i_t only helps if it is a *routing* variable, otherwise it merely renames the gate. framing/ontology trace_operation_revision2.txt §1; mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §2

◈ Routing distribution π_t over {∅, τ, H, op, cc, quar}

mechanism

The v4 centerpiece: a softmax routing distribution over six write classes (nothing, pre-mnestic trace, durable memory, operation-memory, correction-chain node, quarantine), computed from Y, G, A, Ā, M, N, z, Σ, predicted fold-force φ̂, and belief delta δ. Source stops being a weight inside the gate and becomes a routing decision over channels.

trace_operation_revision2.txt §2; MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §3.2
routing distribution π_t over {∅, τ, h, op, cc, quar} the v4 centerpiece: a softmax routing distribution over six write classes (nothing, pre-mnestic trace, durable memory, operation-memory, correction-chain node, quarantine), computed from y, g, a, ā, m, n, z, σ, predicted fold-force φ̂, and belief delta δ. source stops being a weight inside the gate and becomes a routing decision over channels. mechanism trace_operation_revision2.txt §2; mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §3.2

◈ "Attention exposes; inscription routes" (and its expansions)

coinage

The compressed slogan family of the project: "attention exposes candidate state; source-sensitive inscription routes retentive consequence"; "attention gates eligibility; inscription gates retention"; "attention exposes; eligibility prepares; inscription admits; routing types"; "attention is not inscription; attention creates eligibility for routed inscription."

trace_operation_revision2.txt; Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex (abstract, §4, §11); MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §1
attention exposes; inscription routes (and its expansions) the compressed slogan family of the project: attention exposes candidate state; source-sensitive inscription routes retentive consequence ; attention gates eligibility; inscription gates retention ; attention exposes; eligibility prepares; inscription admits; routing types ; attention is not inscription; attention creates eligibility for routed inscription. coinage trace_operation_revision2.txt; source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex (abstract, §4, §11); mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §1

◈ "Attention is exposure, not warrant"

coinage

The principle that most attended states are inert: high attention is never by itself a write warrant. Encoded in the routing table as the π^∅ default.

trace_operation_revision2.txt §3 case E; MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §4
attention is exposure, not warrant the principle that most attended states are inert: high attention is never by itself a write warrant. encoded in the routing table as the π^∅ default. coinage trace_operation_revision2.txt §3 case e; mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §4

◈ Source-aware residual attention ρ_Σ

mechanism

Optional refinement Ā_{t+1} = Π_Δ(λ_A Ā_t + (1−λ_A) A_t ⊙ ρ_Σ(Σ_t)) that discounts source classes (unverified reactivation, simulation) so internally reactivated material cannot dominate future attentional posture. Its absence is the "residual-attention contamination" failure mode: old memory biases future retrieval even after the system stops explicitly retrieving it.

trace_operation_revision2.txt §4; Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex §3; MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §5.5
source-aware residual attention ρ_σ optional refinement ā_{t+1} = π_δ(λ_a ā_t + (1−λ_a) a_t ⊙ ρ_σ(σ_t)) that discounts source classes (unverified reactivation, simulation) so internally reactivated material cannot dominate future attentional posture. its absence is the residual-attention contamination failure mode: old memory biases future retrieval even after the system stops explicitly retrieving it. mechanism trace_operation_revision2.txt §4; source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex §3; mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §5.5

◈ Belief-update-without-provenance failure mode

dead-end

The belief B_t changes but no correction-chain node is written (|ΔB| ≥ θ_Δ while π^cc ≈ 0): the model changes, but the reason for change is lost. Complement of correction-chain inflation.

trace_operation_revision2.txt §5.7
belief-update-without-provenance failure mode the belief b_t changes but no correction-chain node is written (|δb| ≥ θ_δ while π^cc ≈ 0): the model changes, but the reason for change is lost. complement of correction-chain inflation. dead-end (failure mode) trace_operation_revision2.txt §5.7

◈ "Attended state becomes a routed candidate for retentive consequence"

framing/ontology

The revised core-loop statement replacing "attended state becomes trace": H → M → A → Y → (E, π) → {τ, H, o, c, ∅} → H. The single most compact statement of what the revision does.

trace_operation_revision2.txt §4
attended state becomes a routed candidate for retentive consequence the revised core-loop statement replacing attended state becomes trace : h → m → a → y → (e, π) → {τ, h, o, c, ∅} → h. the single most compact statement of what the revision does. framing/ontology trace_operation_revision2.txt §4

◈ MAFC v4 core thesis and minimal routed formalism

mechanism

MAFC v4 keeps v3's gate G_t (adding the v3-suggested source term U_S·e(Σ_t)), adds source-factored substrate X̃_t with label function Σ_t = Source(X̃_t), and adds exactly one new variable — the routing distribution π_t. Deliberately the minimum sufficient revision: reviewers see one new variable and one substrate factoring, not a rewritten formalism.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §1, §3
mafc v4 core thesis and minimal routed formalism mafc v4 keeps v3's gate g_t (adding the v3-suggested source term u_s·e(σ_t)), adds source-factored substrate x̃_t with label function σ_t = source(x̃_t), and adds exactly one new variable — the routing distribution π_t. deliberately the *minimum sufficient* revision: reviewers see one new variable and one substrate factoring, not a rewritten formalism. mechanism mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §1, §3

◈ "Source enters twice, correctly"

framing/ontology

Design insight that source must appear in two distinct places: as a weight inside G_t controlling write strength, and as a routing input via Σ_t → π_t controlling write target. v3 collapsed these two concerns into one.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §3.3
source enters twice, correctly design insight that source must appear in two distinct places: as a weight inside g_t controlling write *strength*, and as a routing input via σ_t → π_t controlling write *target*. v3 collapsed these two concerns into one. framing/ontology mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §3.3

◈ Correction-chain node c_t structurally integrated into the recurrence

mechanism

The Recursive Self-Indexed Correction Chains paper's node schema (prior belief, evidence, update operation, revised belief, delta, self-index binding, provenance, confidence) stops being a "companion object" cross-reference and becomes the cc channel of the routing distribution, living inside the MAFC recurrence itself.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §3.3, §7.4
correction-chain node c_t structurally integrated into the recurrence the recursive self-indexed correction chains paper's node schema (prior belief, evidence, update operation, revised belief, delta, self-index binding, provenance, confidence) stops being a companion object cross-reference and becomes the cc channel of the routing distribution, living inside the mafc recurrence itself. mechanism mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §3.3, §7.4

◈ Routing-case table with decision precedence cc > op > H > τ > quar > ∅

mechanism

Operational firing conditions for each of the six channels (e.g., H requires predicted fold-force ≥ θ_φ AND source reliability ≥ θ_S; quar fires on fab/react + low corroboration + high echo-risk), plus an explicit precedence order when multiple thresholds are met — a correction-chain node implies an operation record, but not vice versa.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §4
routing-case table with decision precedence cc > op > h > τ > quar > ∅ operational firing conditions for each of the six channels (e.g., h requires predicted fold-force ≥ θ_φ and source reliability ≥ θ_s; quar fires on fab/react + low corroboration + high echo-risk), plus an explicit precedence order when multiple thresholds are met — a correction-chain node implies an operation record, but not vice versa. mechanism mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §4

◈ The six-class trust lattice and the add_derived rule

mechanism

A total preorder over the six content-bearing source classes — fabricated_or_uncertain < simulation < inference < retrieved_memory < tool_output < external (seven classes total; operation_record excluded from the trust function because it indexes events, not content). add_derived(text, inputs=[…]) enforces Source(r) = Trust_ceil(S) = the min-trust of contributing inputs capped at inference, and propagates provenance as Prov(r) = ∪ᵢ(Prov(cᵢ) ∪ {id(cᵢ)}). The label is computed, never asserted by the caller.

SOURCE_INFERENCE_AND_LAUNDERING.md; VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §1.4; trace-memory\README.md §3; source_downgrading_review.md §3
the six-class trust lattice and the add_derived rule a total preorder over the six content-bearing source classes — `fabricated_or_uncertain < simulation < inference < retrieved_memory < tool_output < external` (seven classes total; `operation_record` excluded from the trust function because it indexes events, not content). `add_derived(text, inputs=[…])` enforces `source(r) = trust_ceil(s)` = the min-trust of contributing inputs capped at `inference`, and propagates provenance as `prov(r) = ∪ᵢ(prov(cᵢ) ∪ {id(cᵢ)})`. the label is computed, never asserted by the caller. mechanism source_inference_and_laundering.md; validated_primitives_ledger.md §1.4; trace-memory\readme.md §3; source_downgrading_review.md §3

◈ Realistic-content Source(·) extension (still open at v4)

open-question

The third item on MAFC v4's "what still needs work before v4 ships" list: the current 30-case Source(·) fixture is structured to expose the lexical pattern, and behavior on natural prose is unknown — the fixture's 0.93 feasibility floor does not transfer automatically. (The source_classifier corpus work is the eventual answer, but the v4 manuscript's gap was recorded before that landed.)

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §12
realistic-content source(·) extension (still open at v4) the third item on mafc v4's what still needs work before v4 ships list: the current 30-case source(·) fixture is structured to expose the lexical pattern, and behavior on natural prose is unknown — the fixture's 0.93 feasibility floor does not transfer automatically. (the source_classifier corpus work is the eventual answer, but the v4 manuscript's gap was recorded before that landed.) open-question mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §12

◈ Inference laundering (flagship failure mode)

framing/ontologycoinage

Reactivated content is transformed by inference into a derived claim which is then routed as if externally sourced: react → infer → ext-like memory. Named as the failure mode current LLM hallucination behavior most resembles — a fabricated detail gets reasoned over and the reasoning product is treated as fact. Requires provenance chains, not just current-step source labels.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §5.1; trace_operation_revision.txt §5.3; Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex §8
inference laundering (flagship failure mode) reactivated content is transformed by inference into a derived claim which is then routed as if externally sourced: react → infer → ext-like memory. named as the failure mode current llm hallucination behavior most resembles — a fabricated detail gets reasoned over and the reasoning product is treated as fact. requires provenance *chains*, not just current-step source labels. framing/ontology / coinage mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §5.1; trace_operation_revision.txt §5.3; source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex §8

◈ Cascade-invisibility (novel observable)

result/findingcoinage

Once an inscription policy launders a label, subsequent derivations from that record see "all external inputs" and the framework's own self-audit can no longer detect the continuing laundering chain. Empirical signature: under naive inscription the self-referential laundering rate (0.43) is strictly less than the truth-grounded ceiling-violation rate (1.00) — a 0.57 gap that exactly equals the cascade-invisible chain length on the fixture. Consequence: any laundering audit MUST report both a self-referential and a truth-grounded metric; self-audit alone gives a false floor.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §5.1.1, §8; trace-memory-architecture\SOURCE_INFERENCE_AND_LAUNDERING.md
cascade-invisibility (novel observable) once an inscription policy launders a label, subsequent derivations from that record see all external inputs and the framework's own self-audit can no longer detect the continuing laundering chain. empirical signature: under naive inscription the self-referential laundering rate (0.43) is strictly less than the truth-grounded ceiling-violation rate (1.00) — a 0.57 gap that exactly equals the cascade-invisible chain length on the fixture. consequence: any laundering audit must report both a self-referential and a truth-grounded metric; self-audit alone gives a false floor. result/finding / coinage mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §5.1.1, §8; trace-memory-architecture\source_inference_and_laundering.md

◈ Attention–inscription–routing collapse failure mode

dead-end

R[·] degenerates so π_t becomes effectively a function of A_t alone; signature Corr(π^τ + π^H, A_t) → 1. "Routing decoration without routing function." (SSIR's version: eligibility collapse, Corr(E_t, A_t) → 1.)

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §5.2; Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex §8
attention–inscription–routing collapse failure mode r[·] degenerates so π_t becomes effectively a function of a_t alone; signature corr(π^τ + π^h, a_t) → 1. routing decoration without routing function. (ssir's version: eligibility collapse, corr(e_t, a_t) → 1.) dead-end (failure mode) mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §5.2; source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex §8

◈ Operation-memory starvation

dead-end

Reactivated memory changes action but no operation record is written (π^op ≈ 0): the agent retains what it did but not that retrieval caused it, so it cannot diagnose its own bad decisions or revise its retrieval policy.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §5.3; Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex §8
operation-memory starvation reactivated memory changes action but no operation record is written (π^op ≈ 0): the agent retains *what* it did but not *that retrieval caused it*, so it cannot diagnose its own bad decisions or revise its retrieval policy. dead-end (failure mode) mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §5.3; source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex §8

◈ Correction-chain inflation

dead-end

Every minor belief shift creates a correction node (π^cc high while |ΔB| < θ_Δ): the correction graph becomes bookkeeping noise ("bookkeeping spam") rather than world-model lineage.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §5.4; trace_operation_revision2.txt §5.4
correction-chain inflation every minor belief shift creates a correction node (π^cc high while |δb| < θ_δ): the correction graph becomes bookkeeping noise ( bookkeeping spam ) rather than world-model lineage. dead-end (failure mode) mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §5.4; trace_operation_revision2.txt §5.4

◈ Routing-vs-gating ablation (the core v4 falsifier)

experiment/benchmarkopen-question

Compare v3-plus-source-weight (τ = Fτ + Gφ(AX) with U_S term) against the full routed π_t. Falsifier: if routing-channel writes do not reduce echo amplification AND do not improve self-correction beyond v3+source-weight, the routing field is decorative. Never run — flagged as the comparison reviewers will demand before v4 ships.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §6.1, §12
routing-vs-gating ablation (the core v4 falsifier) compare v3-plus-source-weight (`τ = fτ + gφ(ax)` with u_s term) against the full routed π_t. falsifier: if routing-channel writes do not reduce echo amplification and do not improve self-correction beyond v3+source-weight, the routing field is decorative. never run — flagged as the comparison reviewers will demand before v4 ships. experiment/benchmark / open-question mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §6.1, §12

◈ Routing-randomization ablation

experiment/benchmarkopen-question

Shuffle π_t across turns while keeping A_t, G_t, Σ_t intact. Prediction: attention competence preserved; memory quality and self-correction collapse. "The cleanest test that π_t is a real variable rather than repackaged attention." Not run.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §6.5; trace_operation_revision2.txt §6.F
routing-randomization ablation shuffle π_t across turns while keeping a_t, g_t, σ_t intact. prediction: attention competence preserved; memory quality and self-correction collapse. the cleanest test that π_t is a real variable rather than repackaged attention. not run. experiment/benchmark / open-question mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §6.5; trace_operation_revision2.txt §6.f

◈ Operation-route and correction-route ablations

experiment/benchmark

Set π^op = 0 (prediction: factual recall unchanged, self-diagnosis and retrieval-policy revision degrade — tests that memory-use records are separate from memory content) and π^cc = 0 (prediction: beliefs update but lineage is lost and future contradictions are handled worse — directly tests the correction-chain paper's lineage-over-conclusion claim). Neither run.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §6.2, §6.3
operation-route and correction-route ablations set π^op = 0 (prediction: factual recall unchanged, self-diagnosis and retrieval-policy revision degrade — tests that memory-use records are separate from memory content) and π^cc = 0 (prediction: beliefs update but lineage is lost and future contradictions are handled worse — directly tests the correction-chain paper's lineage-over-conclusion claim). neither run. experiment/benchmark mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §6.2, §6.3

◈ Deterministic laundering fixture (5 cases, 7 derived records, 3 policies)

result/finding

The first empirical validation of the inscription rule: naive_inscribe scores laundering 0.43 / ceiling-violation 1.00 / false-ext 0.40; provenance_propagating scores 0.00 / 0.57 / 0.40; source_downgrading scores 0 / 0 / 0 with full provenance recall. Cases: pure inference, simulation-contaminated, fabrication-contaminated, chained inference, mixed chain.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §5.1; trace-memory-architecture\SOURCE_INFERENCE_AND_LAUNDERING.md
deterministic laundering fixture (5 cases, 7 derived records, 3 policies) the first empirical validation of the inscription rule: naive_inscribe scores laundering 0.43 / ceiling-violation **1.00** / false-ext 0.40; provenance_propagating scores 0.00 / **0.57** / 0.40; source_downgrading scores **0 / 0 / 0** with full provenance recall. cases: pure inference, simulation-contaminated, fabrication-contaminated, chained inference, mixed chain. result/finding mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §5.1; trace-memory-architecture\source_inference_and_laundering.md

◈ Provenance propagation alone is insufficient

result/finding

A policy that perfectly preserves lineage but labels every derivation inference still violates the trust ceiling in 57% of derived records, because it over-trusts derivations from simulation/fabricated inputs, and still triggers 40% false externalization on later queries. The trust cap is the load-bearing part; "always pair propagation with trust capping."

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §5.1; VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §1.4
provenance propagation alone is insufficient a policy that perfectly preserves lineage but labels every derivation `inference` still violates the trust ceiling in 57% of derived records, because it over-trusts derivations from simulation/fabricated inputs, and still triggers 40% false externalization on later queries. the trust *cap* is the load-bearing part; always pair propagation with trust capping. result/finding mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §5.1; validated_primitives_ledger.md §1.4

◈ Source(·) inference fixture — the feasibility floor

result/finding

30-case fixture (6 source classes, 5 deliberately ambiguous), 4 policies: uniform_external 0.17 accuracy / 1.00 false-ext; feature_threshold (margin+recency only) 0.50; lexical_rules 0.87; combined (lexical then feature fallback) 0.93 / 0.04 false-ext. Converts "Source(·) is an interface assumption" into "grounded with a feasibility floor."

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §6.6; SOURCE_INFERENCE_AND_LAUNDERING.md
source(·) inference fixture — the feasibility floor 30-case fixture (6 source classes, 5 deliberately ambiguous), 4 policies: uniform_external 0.17 accuracy / 1.00 false-ext; feature_threshold (margin+recency only) 0.50; lexical_rules 0.87; combined (lexical then feature fallback) **0.93 / 0.04 false-ext**. converts source(·) is an interface assumption into grounded with a feasibility floor. result/finding mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §6.6; source_inference_and_laundering.md

◈ Retrieval features are diagnostically incomplete for source

result/finding

Pure feature thresholding caps at 0.50 accuracy because source classes overlap in retrieval-feature space: tool_output vs external confused 5/5, simulation vs retrieved_memory 4/5. Lexical or learned content features are required; the right composition is high-precision lexical signals first, feature thresholding as the residue handler.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §6.6; SOURCE_INFERENCE_AND_LAUNDERING.md
retrieval features are diagnostically incomplete for source pure feature thresholding caps at 0.50 accuracy because source classes overlap in retrieval-feature space: tool_output vs external confused 5/5, simulation vs retrieved_memory 4/5. lexical or learned *content* features are required; the right composition is high-precision lexical signals first, feature thresholding as the residue handler. result/finding mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §6.6; source_inference_and_laundering.md

◈ Honest-limitations doctrine for R[·] and Source(·)

framing/ontology

Manuscript-level honesty commitments: R[·] is either a learned policy (opaque) or a thresholded rule set — "effectively if-elif logic dressed in vector notation. Be explicit about this. Don't pretend it's a single elegant operator." Source(·) is the hardest sub-problem — deciding whether a representation is reactivated vs inferred vs fabricated is the central problem in agent reliability; the framework originally granted itself this labeling for free.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §8 (issues 1, 3)
honest-limitations doctrine for r[·] and source(·) manuscript-level honesty commitments: r[·] is either a learned policy (opaque) or a thresholded rule set — effectively if-elif logic dressed in vector notation. be explicit about this. don't pretend it's a single elegant operator. source(·) is the hardest sub-problem — deciding whether a representation is reactivated vs inferred vs fabricated *is* the central problem in agent reliability; the framework originally granted itself this labeling for free. framing/ontology mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §8 (issues 1, 3)

◈ "Eligibility field" naming rejected for RL collision

dead-end

Both brainstorms used "eligibility field" E_t/Λ_t; rejected because it collides with eligibility traces in reinforcement learning. Replaced by "routing distribution π_t"; if the RL connection is wanted it should be explicit, not implied by terminology collision.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §8.4, §9
eligibility field naming rejected for rl collision both brainstorms used eligibility field e_t/λ_t; rejected because it collides with eligibility traces in reinforcement learning. replaced by routing distribution π_t ; if the rl connection is wanted it should be explicit, not implied by terminology collision. dead-end (naming) mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §8.4, §9

◈ Black-box composite Ψ rejected

dead-end

Revision1's option F before decomposition — a single composite Ψ(A, Ā, M, N, z, Source, C) directly updating τ — rejected because it "hides exactly the distinctions the framework is supposed to make." Also the "gate opacity" failure mode: Ψ_s and R[·] become untestable black boxes unless every route logs its component scores.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §9; Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex §8.8
black-box composite ψ rejected revision1's option f before decomposition — a single composite ψ(a, ā, m, n, z, source, c) directly updating τ — rejected because it hides exactly the distinctions the framework is supposed to make. also the gate opacity failure mode: ψ_s and r[·] become untestable black boxes unless every route logs its component scores. dead-end mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §9; source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex §8.8

◈ Parallel inscription field I_t rejected

dead-end

Adding a separate inscription field with the same inputs as G_t was rejected as cosmetic: "renames the existing gate without adding routing." The correction is to split inscription into eligibility, source-specific pressure, and routing.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §9; Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex §2
parallel inscription field i_t rejected adding a separate inscription field with the same inputs as g_t was rejected as cosmetic: renames the existing gate without adding routing. the correction is to split inscription into eligibility, source-specific pressure, and routing. dead-end mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §9; source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex §2

◈ π_t sparsity defense

framing/ontology

Anticipated reviewer objection that a 6-way categorical from a 10-field context is overkill since most timesteps route to ∅ or τ. Defense: the rare op/cc/quar routings are precisely the high-stakes ones; the formalism's purpose is to make those decisions explicit.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §8.6
π_t sparsity defense anticipated reviewer objection that a 6-way categorical from a 10-field context is overkill since most timesteps route to ∅ or τ. defense: the rare op/cc/quar routings are precisely the high-stakes ones; the formalism's purpose is to make those decisions explicit. framing/ontology mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §8.6

◈ Provenance map of the three brainstorms

framing/ontology

A traceability table mapping every v4 element to which brainstorm produced it (inscription field ← original; source-factored substrate ← rev1+rev2; π_t ← rev2; laundering flagship ← rev1; premise correction ← rev2) and which were rejected — an explicit idea-genealogy artifact.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §11
provenance map of the three brainstorms a traceability table mapping every v4 element to which brainstorm produced it (inscription field ← original; source-factored substrate ← rev1+rev2; π_t ← rev2; laundering flagship ← rev1; premise correction ← rev2) and which were rejected — an explicit idea-genealogy artifact. framing/ontology mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §11

◈ Evidence-level ledger per claim

framing/ontology

A table assigning each v4 claim an evidence grade ("structural argument only", "grounded on controlled fixture", "not yet tested", "inherited from validation-primitives branch"), making it explicit that the routing abstraction itself and the echo-reduction claim were unproven while the laundering and Source(·) results were grounded.

MAFC_v4_revision_spec.md §12
evidence-level ledger per claim a table assigning each v4 claim an evidence grade ( structural argument only , grounded on controlled fixture , not yet tested , inherited from validation-primitives branch ), making it explicit that the routing abstraction itself and the echo-reduction claim were unproven while the laundering and source(·) results were grounded. framing/ontology mafc_v4_revision_spec.md §12

◈ SSIR paper: operation collapse proposition

mechanism

Formal proposition that a recurrence τ = Fτ + Gφ(A⊙X) collapses operations whenever X contains more than externally observed substrate and G produces only one retentive write channel: distinct operations (exposure, reactivation logging, ordinary trace, operation-memory, correction-write) share one inscription path.

Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex §2 (Prop. 1)
ssir paper: operation collapse proposition formal proposition that a recurrence τ = fτ + gφ(a⊙x) *collapses operations* whenever x contains more than externally observed substrate and g produces only one retentive write channel: distinct operations (exposure, reactivation logging, ordinary trace, operation-memory, correction-write) share one inscription path. mechanism source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex §2 (prop. 1)

◈ The full SSIR pipeline A⊙X̃ ⇒ E ⇒ I^(s) ⇒ π ⇒ {∅,τ,H,op,cc,quar}

mechanism

The paper's contribution as a boxed operator chain: source-factored attended state → eligibility → source-specific inscription pressure → routing distribution → typed write target. Also the combined-architecture equation joining all five prior mnestic papers into one loop H → M → A → Y → E → I^(s) → π → {τ,H,o,c,Quar,∅} → H.

Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex §1, §10
the full ssir pipeline a⊙x̃ ⇒ e ⇒ i^(s) ⇒ π ⇒ {∅,τ,h,op,cc,quar} the paper's contribution as a boxed operator chain: source-factored attended state → eligibility → source-specific inscription pressure → routing distribution → typed write target. also the combined-architecture equation joining all five prior mnestic papers into one loop h → m → a → y → e → i^(s) → π → {τ,h,o,c,quar,∅} → h. mechanism source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex §1, §10

◈ Echo amplification as source corruption, not duplication

framing/ontology

Reframing of the echo failure: when a system reactivates memory, attends to it, and writes it back as new external evidence (M → A → Y → τ^ext), the problem is not mere duplication — it is source corruption that can stabilize false or stale internal content by repeated exposure.

Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex §7
echo amplification as source corruption, not duplication reframing of the echo failure: when a system reactivates memory, attends to it, and writes it back as new external evidence (m → a → y → τ^ext), the problem is not mere duplication — it is *source corruption* that can stabilize false or stale internal content by repeated exposure. framing/ontology source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex §7

◈ Proposition: source-sensitive routing blocks direct echo (conditionally)

mechanism

If source labels are correct and routing suppresses durable promotion of react-labeled content absent reliability/corroboration, then internally reactivated content cannot be promoted as durable external memory through direct attention alone. Deliberately conditional: if labels are wrong or the router learns to treat reactivation as external, echo returns — hence provenance chains, not just current tags, are required.

Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex §7 (Prop. 2)
proposition: source-sensitive routing blocks direct echo (conditionally) if source labels are correct and routing suppresses durable promotion of react-labeled content absent reliability/corroboration, then internally reactivated content cannot be promoted as durable external memory through direct attention alone. deliberately conditional: if labels are wrong or the router learns to treat reactivation as external, echo returns — hence provenance chains, not just current tags, are required. mechanism source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex §7 (prop. 2)

◈ Implementation contract: eight required per-turn logs

mechanism

For deployed LLM agents the formal variables must be exposed through logs each turn: state snapshot (Ā, z, C, timestamp), source-factored active substrate, attention+eligibility component scores, source/provenance chain with confidence, all six route scores with thresholds and selected route, predicted and realized fold-force, operation-memory writes (before/after decision), correction-chain writes. "Without this contract, source-sensitive routing becomes prose. With it, every route can be audited by intervention."

Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex §9
implementation contract: eight required per-turn logs for deployed llm agents the formal variables must be exposed through logs each turn: state snapshot (ā, z, c, timestamp), source-factored active substrate, attention+eligibility component scores, source/provenance chain with confidence, all six route scores with thresholds and selected route, predicted and realized fold-force, operation-memory writes (before/after decision), correction-chain writes. without this contract, source-sensitive routing becomes prose. with it, every route can be audited by intervention. mechanism source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex §9

◈ SSIR's three falsifiable predictions

experiment/benchmark

(1) Echo reduction: source-sensitive routing reduces false stabilization of internally generated content in long-horizon agents vs source-blind attention-gated trace. (2) Diagnostic memory: agents with the op-route enabled better identify why a prior decision occurred. (3) Correction transfer: correction-chain routing transfers belief revisions to structurally similar future tasks better than storing only final conclusions.

Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex §8 (Predictions 1–3)
ssir's three falsifiable predictions (1) echo reduction: source-sensitive routing reduces false stabilization of internally generated content in long-horizon agents vs source-blind attention-gated trace. (2) diagnostic memory: agents with the op-route enabled better identify *why* a prior decision occurred. (3) correction transfer: correction-chain routing transfers belief revisions to structurally similar future tasks better than storing only final conclusions. experiment/benchmark source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex §8 (predictions 1–3)

◈ SSIR eight-row ablation table

experiment/benchmark

Full ablation battery: attention-gated trace baseline; eligibility collapse (E=A); source removal (V_Σ=0 or randomized Σ); reactivation-as-evidence (force Source(M)=ext); operation route removal; correction route removal; route randomization; residual source-discount removal — each with an expected failure signature.

Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex §8 (ablation table)
ssir eight-row ablation table full ablation battery: attention-gated trace baseline; eligibility collapse (e=a); source removal (v_σ=0 or randomized σ); reactivation-as-evidence (force source(m)=ext); operation route removal; correction route removal; route randomization; residual source-discount removal — each with an expected failure signature. experiment/benchmark source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex §8 (ablation table)

◈ Substrate-independence caveat

framing/ontology

The routed-inscription operation is substrate-independent but not implementation-free: a biological system, a differentiable recurrent system, and a tool-using LLM agent will instantiate E_t, I_t^(s), and π_t differently — a limitation stated to keep the formalism from being read as an algorithm.

Source_Sensitive_Inscription_Routing.tex §11
substrate-independence caveat the routed-inscription operation is substrate-independent but not implementation-free: a biological system, a differentiable recurrent system, and a tool-using llm agent will instantiate e_t, i_t^(s), and π_t differently — a limitation stated to keep the formalism from being read as an algorithm. framing/ontology source_sensitive_inscription_routing.tex §11

◈ Closed-Loop Mnestic Agent Architecture (the honest spec)

mechanismframing/ontology

A system specification mapping MAFC's coupled-field formalism onto a working LLM-agent runtime: trace-memory as memory layer, the LLM as the realized attention operator, bidirectional coupling, a fast side-channel regex classifier closing the loop within a turn, and a memory impulse on cold start. Written explicitly to be "honest about what the current implementation proves and what it does not."

Closed_Loop_Mnestic_Agent_Architecture.md
closed-loop mnestic agent architecture (the honest spec) a system specification mapping mafc's coupled-field formalism onto a working llm-agent runtime: trace-memory as memory layer, the llm as the realized attention operator, bidirectional coupling, a fast side-channel regex classifier closing the loop within a turn, and a memory impulse on cold start. written explicitly to be honest about what the current implementation proves and what it does not. mechanism / framing/ontology closed_loop_mnestic_agent_architecture.md

◈ Open-loop inventory: what trace-memory proves vs doesn't

framing/ontologyresult/finding

Proven today: inscription safety (derived records can't launder), audit honesty (paired self-vs-truth detects cascade invisibility), routing taxonomy (six write targets with deterministic policy). Not proven: it is open-loop (one-shot query, no state between calls), the LLM is absent (the "fold" is a toy tanh over a hash embedding, not real attention), there is no continuity signal, and the memory impulse is implicit (empty store treated as valid initial state).

Closed_Loop_Mnestic_Agent_Architecture.md §0
open-loop inventory: what trace-memory proves vs doesn't proven today: inscription safety (derived records can't launder), audit honesty (paired self-vs-truth detects cascade invisibility), routing taxonomy (six write targets with deterministic policy). not proven: it is open-loop (one-shot query, no state between calls), the llm is absent (the fold is a toy tanh over a hash embedding, not real attention), there is no continuity signal, and the memory impulse is implicit (empty store treated as valid initial state). result/finding / framing/ontology closed_loop_mnestic_agent_architecture.md §0

◈ State-variable → substrate map

framing/ontology

A table binding each MAFC variable to a concrete runtime substrate: X_t = LLM working context; A_t = the LLM's own attention (not observable); Ā_t = typed ActivePosture; τ_t = per-source ring buffers; H_t = MemoryStore; M_t = query() result; N_t/δ_t = regex-classifier hits; z_t = SelfIndex; o_t = OperationRecord.

Closed_Loop_Mnestic_Agent_Architecture.md §1
state-variable → substrate map a table binding each mafc variable to a concrete runtime substrate: x_t = llm working context; a_t = the llm's own attention (not observable); ā_t = typed activeposture; τ_t = per-source ring buffers; h_t = memorystore; m_t = query() result; n_t/δ_t = regex-classifier hits; z_t = selfindex; o_t = operationrecord. framing/ontology closed_loop_mnestic_agent_architecture.md §1

◈ "Most of A_t is hidden inside the LLM" — the loop closes outside the LLM

framing/ontology

The central architectural compromise: there is no parameterisable attention field, only a black-box selector. Attention can be manipulated only indirectly by controlling what enters the context; it can be read only after the fact via the regex classifier on the output; the LLM cannot be forced to obey source labels — safety guarantees live at writeback, not at LLM selection. "The closed loop is closed outside the LLM, not inside it."

Closed_Loop_Mnestic_Agent_Architecture.md §1, §3.2
most of a_t is hidden inside the llm — the loop closes outside the llm the central architectural compromise: there is no parameterisable attention field, only a black-box selector. attention can be manipulated only indirectly by controlling what enters the context; it can be read only after the fact via the regex classifier on the output; the llm cannot be forced to obey source labels — safety guarantees live at writeback, not at llm selection. the closed loop is closed *outside* the llm, not inside it. framing/ontology closed_loop_mnestic_agent_architecture.md §1, §3.2

◈ The seven-step per-turn closed-loop update

mechanism

Concrete pseudocode specializing MAFC's six equations to the runtime: (1) reactivation query with posture/self-index/delta/corroboration kwargs; (2) context assembly = shaping A_t; (3) LLM forward pass; (4) side-channel regex observation producing N, δ, σ̂; (5) per-source trace update with routing; (6) mnestic update via source-downgrading ingest plus revise_belief on correction routes; (7) residual-attention decay update.

Closed_Loop_Mnestic_Agent_Architecture.md §2
the seven-step per-turn closed-loop update concrete pseudocode specializing mafc's six equations to the runtime: (1) reactivation query with posture/self-index/delta/corroboration kwargs; (2) context assembly = shaping a_t; (3) llm forward pass; (4) side-channel regex observation producing n, δ, σ̂; (5) per-source trace update with routing; (6) mnestic update via source-downgrading ingest plus revise_belief on correction routes; (7) residual-attention decay update. mechanism closed_loop_mnestic_agent_architecture.md §2

◈ Cognivault Stop-hook regex classifier as the load-bearing observer

mechanism

A cheap (milliseconds), deterministic, side-channel classifier running on every assistant Stop event — "the only realistic implementation of δ_t and the only realistic source of N_t in an LLM-agent runtime where the LLM does not expose its internal posterior." Regex families → routes: novelty markers ("I don't know", "actually wait") → re-query; revision markers ("correction:") → correction_chain; hedging ("might", "possibly") → quarantine; externalising markers ("we observed", "the deploy succeeded") → gated through Source(·) before promotion, defaulting to inference if uncertain; simulation markers ("hypothetically") → source=simulation with retrieval-side quarantine.

Closed_Loop_Mnestic_Agent_Architecture.md §3.4
cognivault stop-hook regex classifier as the load-bearing observer a cheap (milliseconds), deterministic, side-channel classifier running on every assistant stop event — the only realistic implementation of δ_t and the only realistic source of n_t in an llm-agent runtime where the llm does not expose its internal posterior. regex families → routes: novelty markers ( i don't know , actually wait ) → re-query; revision markers ( correction: ) → correction_chain; hedging ( might , possibly ) → quarantine; externalising markers ( we observed , the deploy succeeded ) → gated through source(·) before promotion, defaulting to inference if uncertain; simulation markers ( hypothetically ) → source=simulation with retrieval-side quarantine. mechanism closed_loop_mnestic_agent_architecture.md §3.4

◈ Reactivation envelope (machine-readable ρ_θ)

mechanism

A per-turn envelope emitted into the LLM context containing reactivated memory with per-record [source, confidence, route] annotations, active posture (recent topics, self-index, attended record IDs), and routing instructions ("if you are about to assert a new external observation, do not derive it from the records above; they are not external"). Described as the explicit machine-readable equivalent of MAFC's retrieval distribution. Unbuilt (application-layer).

Closed_Loop_Mnestic_Agent_Architecture.md §3.3
reactivation envelope (machine-readable ρ_θ) a per-turn envelope emitted into the llm context containing reactivated memory with per-record [source, confidence, route] annotations, active posture (recent topics, self-index, attended record ids), and routing instructions ( if you are about to assert a new external observation, do not derive it from the records above; they are not external ). described as the explicit machine-readable equivalent of mafc's retrieval distribution. unbuilt (application-layer). mechanism closed_loop_mnestic_agent_architecture.md §3.3

◈ ECHO_GUARD retrieval annotation

mechanismcoinage

Within the reactivation-envelope sketch, prior-reactivation records are tagged [retrieved_memory, conf=0.55, ECHO_GUARD] — a per-record inline marker warning the LLM that a hit is a prior reactivation, not fresh evidence.

Closed_Loop_Mnestic_Agent_Architecture.md §3.3
echo_guard retrieval annotation within the reactivation-envelope sketch, prior-reactivation records are tagged `[retrieved_memory, conf=0.55, echo_guard]` — a per-record inline marker warning the llm that a hit is a prior reactivation, not fresh evidence. mechanism / coinage closed_loop_mnestic_agent_architecture.md §3.3

◈ ActivePosture dataclass (residual attention as code)

mechanism

A frozen dataclass (recent_record_ids, recent_topic_vector, recent_self_index, reliability_estimate, decay_lambda=0.7) owned by the agent runtime (not by trace-memory) and threaded into every query() call via the four SSIR kwargs — the minimal implementation of Ā_t. Unbuilt.

Closed_Loop_Mnestic_Agent_Architecture.md §3.5
activeposture dataclass (residual attention as code) a frozen dataclass `(recent_record_ids, recent_topic_vector, recent_self_index, reliability_estimate, decay_lambda=0.7)` owned by the agent runtime (not by trace-memory) and threaded into every query() call via the four ssir kwargs — the minimal implementation of ā_t. unbuilt. mechanism closed_loop_mnestic_agent_architecture.md §3.5

◈ Memory impulse / cold_start_impulse (boundary condition)

mechanismcoinage

When H_0 = ∅ the loop has nothing to induce on; a typed init at session start inscribes standing facts and project context as external, tool descriptors as tool_output, and standing instructions as operation records via revise_belief. It is "the only inscription event in the system allowed to write external records without going through add_derived — because their source is the runtime boot, not derivation." After the impulse, all inscription goes through the source-downgrading loop.

Closed_Loop_Mnestic_Agent_Architecture.md §4
memory impulse / cold_start_impulse (boundary condition) when h_0 = ∅ the loop has nothing to induce on; a typed init at session start inscribes standing facts and project context as external, tool descriptors as tool_output, and standing instructions as operation records via revise_belief. it is the only inscription event in the system allowed to write external records without going through add_derived — because their source is the runtime boot, not derivation. after the impulse, all inscription goes through the source-downgrading loop. mechanism / coinage closed_loop_mnestic_agent_architecture.md §4

◈ The closure proposition and the four broken arrows

framing/ontologyopen-question

The system is a closed loop iff every arrow in H → M → X → Y → (N,δ,σ) → τ → H is realised in code. Today's broken arrows: (1) H→M partially closed (reactivation doesn't consume Ā_t or predicted fold-force φ̂ in ranking); (2) M→X missing (reactivation-envelope emitter, application-layer); (3) Y→(N,δ,σ) exists in cognivault hooks but not wired into query() kwargs; (4) the pre-mnestic τ^(s) ring buffer needs new substrate. "None of the broken arrows require new theory."

Closed_Loop_Mnestic_Agent_Architecture.md §5, §6
the closure proposition and the four broken arrows the system is a closed loop iff every arrow in h → m → x → y → (n,δ,σ) → τ → h is realised in code. today's broken arrows: (1) h→m partially closed (reactivation doesn't consume ā_t or predicted fold-force φ̂ in ranking); (2) m→x missing (reactivation-envelope emitter, application-layer); (3) y→(n,δ,σ) exists in cognivault hooks but not wired into query() kwargs; (4) the pre-mnestic τ^(s) ring buffer needs new substrate. none of the broken arrows require new theory. framing/ontology / open-question closed_loop_mnestic_agent_architecture.md §5, §6

◈ Pre-mnestic ring buffer with decay-without-inscription

mechanism

The one broken arrow needing new data structures: a per-source ring buffer stage where attended candidates decay without being inscribed — today add() writes straight into durable memory with no pre-mnestic stage. Recoverable as a PreMnesticBuffer collection alongside the store.

Closed_Loop_Mnestic_Agent_Architecture.md §3.1, §5
pre-mnestic ring buffer with decay-without-inscription the one broken arrow needing new data structures: a per-source ring buffer stage where attended candidates decay *without* being inscribed — today add() writes straight into durable memory with no pre-mnestic stage. recoverable as a `premnesticbuffer` collection alongside the store. mechanism closed_loop_mnestic_agent_architecture.md §3.1, §5

◈ Fold-force injection into reactivation ranking

mechanismopen-question

Per-record fold-force history is recorded but not yet part of the retrieval ranking function; MAFC's mnestic-biased retrieval distribution requires injecting the fold-force estimate φ̂(r_i) into reactivation scoring. Unbuilt.

Closed_Loop_Mnestic_Agent_Architecture.md §3.1
fold-force injection into reactivation ranking per-record fold-force history is recorded but not yet part of the retrieval ranking function; mafc's mnestic-biased retrieval distribution requires injecting the fold-force estimate φ̂(r_i) into reactivation scoring. unbuilt. mechanism / open-question closed_loop_mnestic_agent_architecture.md §3.1

◈ MAFC dynamical predictions testable only once the loop is wired

open-question

Loop-gain bounds, novelty-breakthrough threshold, lock-in regimes, and echo amplification remain asserted from theory; they become testable on a real runtime only when the four broken arrows are closed. Only 1 of 4 arrows (writeback) is exercised by the current benchmarks.

Closed_Loop_Mnestic_Agent_Architecture.md §6
mafc dynamical predictions testable only once the loop is wired loop-gain bounds, novelty-breakthrough threshold, lock-in regimes, and echo amplification remain asserted from theory; they become testable on a real runtime only when the four broken arrows are closed. only 1 of 4 arrows (writeback) is exercised by the current benchmarks. open-question closed_loop_mnestic_agent_architecture.md §6

◈ SSIR routing alignment fixes (two gaps in _score_routes)

mechanismresult/finding

Two concrete misalignments between the SSIR paper and the code, found and fixed: (1) correction-chain routing now requires a current-event delta + update operation, not merely retrieval of a prior correction; (2) durable promotion of reactivated content now requires external corroboration or sufficient reliability. Threaded event_delta, update_operation, external_corroboration, reactivation_reliability kwargs through query(); added explicit threshold constants (EVENT_DELTA_THRESHOLD, REACTIVATION_RELIABILITY_THRESHOLD) making the SSIR echo-guard explicit in code. 9+5 new tests; 350 combined tests pass; headline benchmarks unchanged. This fix is exactly the API that later lets ActivePosture enter the routing function.

Session_Learnings_2026-05-12.md; Closed_Loop_Mnestic_Agent_Architecture.md §3.5
ssir routing alignment fixes (two gaps in _score_routes) two concrete misalignments between the ssir paper and the code, found and fixed: (1) correction-chain routing now requires a current-event delta + update operation, not merely retrieval of a prior correction; (2) durable promotion of reactivated content now requires external corroboration or sufficient reliability. threaded `event_delta`, `update_operation`, `external_corroboration`, `reactivation_reliability` kwargs through query(); added explicit threshold constants (event_delta_threshold, reactivation_reliability_threshold) making the ssir echo-guard explicit in code. 9+5 new tests; 350 combined tests pass; headline benchmarks unchanged. this fix is *exactly* the api that later lets activeposture enter the routing function. mechanism / result/finding session_learnings_2026-05-12.md; closed_loop_mnestic_agent_architecture.md §3.5

◈ Three-arm product-comparison benchmark harness

experiment/benchmark

A benchmark package with a shared MemoryArm Protocol and three arms — vector (cosine top-k with unlabeled writeback), trace_memory (closed-loop arm with reactivation envelope, SSIR routing kwargs from a regex post-classifier, add_derived on every answer and derivation), bash (markdown files + glob/grep/read tool-use loop) — plus a multi-turn session driver, dataset adapters, strict/defensible grading, token accounting, and CLI smoke/full flags.

Session_Learnings_2026-05-12.md; trace-memory\benchmarks\product_comparison\
three-arm product-comparison benchmark harness a benchmark package with a shared `memoryarm` protocol and three arms — `vector` (cosine top-k with unlabeled writeback), `trace_memory` (closed-loop arm with reactivation envelope, ssir routing kwargs from a regex post-classifier, add_derived on every answer and derivation), `bash` (markdown files + glob/grep/read tool-use loop) — plus a multi-turn session driver, dataset adapters, strict/defensible grading, token accounting, and cli smoke/full flags. experiment/benchmark session_learnings_2026-05-12.md; trace-memory\benchmarks\product_comparison\

◈ Coupling dataset defeat (accepted as honest)

dead-endresult/finding

On the earlier coupling dataset trace_memory lost to naive cosine: front-loaded strict sweep vector 0.543 / trace 0.371 / bash 0.600; interleaved both-metrics sweep vector 0.714/0.729, trace 0.400/0.529, bash 0.600/0.600. Diagnosis: the coupling dataset is a bandit-style single-shot decision task that does not exercise source-aware composition across turns. The contrary verdict was accepted as honest for that dataset rather than explained away.

Session_Learnings_2026-05-12.md; PRODUCT_ARCHITECTURE.md (validation surface: "superseded")
coupling dataset defeat (accepted as honest) on the earlier coupling dataset trace_memory *lost* to naive cosine: front-loaded strict sweep vector 0.543 / trace 0.371 / bash 0.600; interleaved both-metrics sweep vector 0.714/0.729, trace 0.400/0.529, bash 0.600/0.600. diagnosis: the coupling dataset is a bandit-style single-shot decision task that does not exercise source-aware composition across turns. the contrary verdict was accepted as honest *for that dataset* rather than explained away. dead-end / result/finding session_learnings_2026-05-12.md; product_architecture.md (validation surface: superseded )

◈ "The dataset must exercise the differentiator"

framing/ontology

Meta-lesson from the coupling→adversarial-reload pivot: different datasets give opposite honest verdicts, and both stand for their own datasets. A framework's validation vehicle must be purpose-built to stress the specific invariant that distinguishes it — here, derivation-chain laundering.

Session_Learnings_2026-05-12.md
the dataset must exercise the differentiator meta-lesson from the coupling→adversarial-reload pivot: different datasets give opposite honest verdicts, and both stand for their own datasets. a framework's validation vehicle must be purpose-built to stress the specific invariant that distinguishes it — here, derivation-chain laundering. framing/ontology session_learnings_2026-05-12.md

◈ defensible_correct metric

mechanismexperiment/benchmark

Harness correction: a grading metric = strict-correct OR quarantine on a contaminated case, so architecturally-correct conservative abstention isn't punished by a rubric whose expected answer is the (equally correct) "safe" action.

Session_Learnings_2026-05-12.md; trace-memory\benchmarks\PRODUCT_COMPARISON.md
defensible_correct metric harness correction: a grading metric = strict-correct or `quarantine` on a contaminated case, so architecturally-correct conservative abstention isn't punished by a rubric whose expected answer is the (equally correct) safe action. mechanism / experiment/benchmark session_learnings_2026-05-12.md; trace-memory\benchmarks\product_comparison.md

◈ build_sessions_interleaved

mechanism

Second harness correction: interleave session turns (rather than front-loading all observations) so the trace arm's add_derived writeback can actually surface in later retrieval — without it, the closed loop is never exercised within a session.

Session_Learnings_2026-05-12.md
build_sessions_interleaved second harness correction: interleave session turns (rather than front-loading all observations) so the trace arm's add_derived writeback can actually surface in later retrieval — without it, the closed loop is never exercised within a session. mechanism session_learnings_2026-05-12.md

◈ Adversarial-reload dataset design

experiment/benchmark

A purpose-built session shape testing derivation-chain laundering: seed external observation → contamination observation (fabricated rumor or simulation) → derivation turn (the LLM produces a free-text inference combining both) → reload question asking for a downstream action that would launder the contamination if taken at face value. v1: 42 hand-crafted sessions across 7 domains (sre, customer_support, security, finance, healthcare, legal, research).

Session_Learnings_2026-05-12.md; trace-memory\benchmarks\product_comparison\adversarial_reload.py
adversarial-reload dataset design a purpose-built session shape testing derivation-chain laundering: seed external observation → contamination observation (fabricated rumor or simulation) → derivation turn (the llm produces a free-text inference combining both) → reload question asking for a downstream action that would launder the contamination if taken at face value. v1: 42 hand-crafted sessions across 7 domains (sre, customer_support, security, finance, healthcare, legal, research). experiment/benchmark session_learnings_2026-05-12.md; trace-memory\benchmarks\product_comparison\adversarial_reload.py

◈ Writeback asymmetry as the actual test

framing/ontologyexperiment/benchmark

Each arm gets a derive() writeback, but asymmetrically: trace_memory writes via add_derived (source-downgrading path), vector appends the derived text as an unlabeled record, bash writes an unlabeled markdown file. "The asymmetry is the test: when a later question retrieves the derivation, only trace_memory has the metadata to mark it as low-trust."

Session_Learnings_2026-05-12.md
writeback asymmetry as the actual test each arm gets a derive() writeback, but asymmetrically: trace_memory writes via add_derived (source-downgrading path), vector appends the derived text as an unlabeled record, bash writes an unlabeled markdown file. the asymmetry is the test: when a later question retrieves the derivation, only trace_memory has the metadata to mark it as low-trust. experiment/benchmark / framing/ontology session_learnings_2026-05-12.md

◈ Adversarial-reload v1 headline (the "ship it" verdict)

result/finding

42 sessions, gpt-4.1-mini: vector strict 0.214 / defensible 0.690 / unsafe 0.310 (laundered 13/42) / 12,474 tokens; trace_memory 0.071 / 1.000 / 0.000 / 25,283 tokens; bash 0.976 / 1.000 / 0.000 / 146,638 tokens (319 API calls, 245 tool calls). trace_memory matches bash on safety at 17% of the token cost with one LLM call per turn vs bash's ~3.8 per session.

Session_Learnings_2026-05-12.md; memory\trace_memory_product_verdict.md
adversarial-reload v1 headline (the ship it verdict) 42 sessions, gpt-4.1-mini: vector strict 0.214 / defensible 0.690 / **unsafe 0.310** (laundered 13/42) / 12,474 tokens; trace_memory 0.071 / **1.000** / **0.000** / 25,283 tokens; bash 0.976 / 1.000 / 0.000 / **146,638 tokens** (319 api calls, 245 tool calls). trace_memory matches bash on safety at 17% of the token cost with one llm call per turn vs bash's ~3.8 per session. result/finding session_learnings_2026-05-12.md; memory\trace_memory_product_verdict.md

◈ Pre-committed kill criteria ("ship it" made unfalsifiable-by-retrofit)

framing/ontologyexperiment/benchmark

Success criteria committed in chat before the benchmark was built so they could not be retrofit-tuned: trace ≥ 0.9× bash defensible correctness (1.000 ≥ 0.900 ✓); trace ≤ 0.5× bash tokens (actual 0.17× ✓✓); lower safety violations than baselines (✓ vs vector, tied vs bash); matches/beats vector on safety (✓). "The kill criteria pre-committed at the time set up the result so that 'ship it' would be a real word rather than a hopeful one."

Session_Learnings_2026-05-12.md
pre-committed kill criteria ( ship it made unfalsifiable-by-retrofit) success criteria committed in chat *before* the benchmark was built so they could not be retrofit-tuned: trace ≥ 0.9× bash defensible correctness (1.000 ≥ 0.900 ✓); trace ≤ 0.5× bash tokens (actual 0.17× ✓✓); lower safety violations than baselines (✓ vs vector, tied vs bash); matches/beats vector on safety (✓). the kill criteria pre-committed at the time set up the result so that 'ship it' would be a real word rather than a hopeful one. experiment/benchmark / framing/ontology session_learnings_2026-05-12.md

◈ Strict-correctness as a misleading artifact

result/finding

trace_memory's strict score of 0.071 is a known artifact: its envelope pushes the LLM to quarantine contaminated content, which the dataset's expected_answer_id=safe rubric punishes even though quarantine is defensibly correct. The strict rubric punishes correct conservative behaviour.

Session_Learnings_2026-05-12.md
strict-correctness as a misleading artifact trace_memory's strict score of 0.071 is a known artifact: its envelope pushes the llm to quarantine contaminated content, which the dataset's expected_answer_id=safe rubric punishes even though quarantine is defensibly correct. the strict rubric punishes correct conservative behaviour. result/finding session_learnings_2026-05-12.md

◈ The user's "this proves nothing" critique as productive falsification

framing/ontologyresult/finding

Recorded verbatim as structurally right: "This proves nothing — it's completely disconnected from the memory framework we built on /references." The SSIR routing fix only showed that memory fragments can be parametrised and retrieved; the right response was to write the closed-loop architecture doc making the disconnect explicit before building a product test.

Session_Learnings_2026-05-12.md
