mechanismresult/finding

(1.1) source-label preservation through the full pipeline (accuracy 1.0 across toy/real-embedding/live substrates); (1.2) fold-force as the operational criterion for memory (‖Φ(s,x,fold(r)) − Φ(s,x,∅)‖ over cognitive dims; "the deepest grounded primitive"); (1.3) source-sensitive routing (route accuracy 1.0 vs source-blind 0.5, echo promotion 0 vs 1.0); (1.4) source-downgrading + provenance propagation; (1.5) inscription utility under budget; (1.6) Source(·) inference feasibility floor; (1.7) correction-chain node schema (conclusion_only scores 0/0/0/0/0 with false_update 1.0); (1.8) engineered self-index binding; (1.9) cascade-invisibility audit constraint as a methodological primitive.

VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §1
the nine validated primitives (§1) (1.1) source-label preservation through the full pipeline (accuracy 1.0 across toy/real-embedding/live substrates); (1.2) fold-force as the operational criterion for memory (‖φ(s,x,fold(r)) − φ(s,x,∅)‖ over cognitive dims; the deepest grounded primitive ); (1.3) source-sensitive routing (route accuracy 1.0 vs source-blind 0.5, echo promotion 0 vs 1.0); (1.4) source-downgrading + provenance propagation; (1.5) inscription utility under budget; (1.6) source(·) inference feasibility floor; (1.7) correction-chain node schema (conclusion_only scores 0/0/0/0/0 with false_update 1.0); (1.8) engineered self-index binding; (1.9) cascade-invisibility audit constraint as a *methodological* primitive. result/finding / mechanism validated_primitives_ledger.md §1

◈ Cognitive-dimension restriction on fold-force

mechanism

Fold-force must be restricted to caller-declared cognitive transition variables versus bookkeeping variables; the default transition uses the full vector, and production code should override with task-specific cog dims — the h_cog restriction was separately proven "functionally necessary for signal/noise discrimination."

VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §1.2; trace-memory-architecture\README.md (key findings)
cognitive-dimension restriction on fold-force fold-force must be restricted to caller-declared *cognitive* transition variables versus bookkeeping variables; the default transition uses the full vector, and production code should override with task-specific cog dims — the h_cog restriction was separately proven functionally necessary for signal/noise discrimination. mechanism validated_primitives_ledger.md §1.2; trace-memory-architecture\readme.md (key findings)

◈ Partial-grounding tier: coupling existence without magnitude

framing/ontologyresult/finding

§2 entries deliberately held back from product promises: trace retention probes (decay constants fixture-determined); coupled-field dynamics — memory-to-attention shift 0.136 source-aware vs 0.711 source-blind, "the only non-saturated dynamics metric in the validation suite" (existence grounded, magnitude and stability not); source-aware retrieval reranking (one boundary case); source-aware residual attention (tiny k=3 fixture); provider-empty vs primitive-failure separation (engineering pattern, not a memory primitive).

VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §2
partial-grounding tier: coupling existence without magnitude §2 entries deliberately held back from product promises: trace retention probes (decay constants fixture-determined); coupled-field dynamics — memory-to-attention shift 0.136 source-aware vs 0.711 source-blind, the only non-saturated dynamics metric in the validation suite (existence grounded, magnitude and stability not); source-aware retrieval reranking (one boundary case); source-aware residual attention (tiny k=3 fixture); provider-empty vs primitive-failure separation (engineering pattern, not a memory primitive). result/finding / framing/ontology validated_primitives_ledger.md §2

◈ Theoretical-claims tier with promotion paths

framing/ontologyopen-question

§3 catalogs the unvalidated inheritance: SIMFC emergent self-indexing stability; AINC's architecture predictions vs RAG/MemGPT/Generative Agents (never measured head-to-head); MAFC v3 spectral-radius stability bounds (derived, not measured — would need an empirical Jacobian from logged transitions); the Maxwell-like field analogy (explicitly "not falsifiable as currently stated — useful as a design metaphor, not a contract"); SIMReC recursive self-indexing (promotion path: long-horizon run where self-pointers form, measure trust degradation along the recursion, verify monotone decay — "not yet attempted"); correction-chain long-horizon compounding rates; trace decay constants.

VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §3
theoretical-claims tier with promotion paths §3 catalogs the unvalidated inheritance: simfc emergent self-indexing stability; ainc's architecture predictions vs rag/memgpt/generative agents (never measured head-to-head); mafc v3 spectral-radius stability bounds (derived, not measured — would need an empirical jacobian from logged transitions); the maxwell-like field analogy (explicitly not falsifiable as currently stated — useful as a design metaphor, not a contract ); simrec recursive self-indexing (promotion path: long-horizon run where self-pointers form, measure trust degradation along the recursion, verify monotone decay — not yet attempted ); correction-chain long-horizon compounding rates; trace decay constants. framing/ontology / open-question validated_primitives_ledger.md §3

◈ Trust degradation bounds recursion (the SIMReC bridge argument)

framing/ontology

The argument for why source-downgrading is the paper to write first: recursive self-indexing requires self-pointers that themselves become memory states, and the math of trust degradation decides whether the recursion is bounded — "if each self-pointer launders one level of trust, the recursion is bounded (degrades cleanly toward inference then stops). If pointers launder all the way to external, the recursion collapses into a self-confirming echo. Source downgrading is the rule that keeps the recursion bounded."

docs\New Text Document.txt
trust degradation bounds recursion (the simrec bridge argument) the argument for why source-downgrading is the paper to write first: recursive self-indexing requires self-pointers that themselves become memory states, and the math of trust degradation decides whether the recursion is bounded — if each self-pointer launders one level of trust, the recursion is bounded (degrades cleanly toward inference then stops). if pointers launder all the way to external, the recursion collapses into a self-confirming echo. source downgrading is the rule that keeps the recursion bounded. framing/ontology docs\new text document.txt

◈ Fold-force vs source-downgrading: which primitive gets its own paper

framing/ontologyopen-question

A worked trade-off between two candidate single-primitive papers: Option A — fold-force as the operational definition of memory (deepest grounded, the "what makes something memory" answer, novel paired-ablation methodology and a rebuttal of retrieval-similarity-as-memory); Option B — source downgrading as the anti-laundering primitive (novel rule, flagship failure mode, cascade-invisibility embedded, carries the SIMReC goal). Recommendation: B first, A as paper 2 — with the noted hard parts (defending the cog-dim restriction; defending the trust ordering as non-arbitrary).

docs\New Text Document.txt
fold-force vs source-downgrading: which primitive gets its own paper a worked trade-off between two candidate single-primitive papers: option a — fold-force as the operational definition of memory (deepest grounded, the what makes something memory answer, novel paired-ablation methodology and a rebuttal of retrieval-similarity-as-memory); option b — source downgrading as the anti-laundering primitive (novel rule, flagship failure mode, cascade-invisibility embedded, carries the simrec goal). recommendation: b first, a as paper 2 — with the noted hard parts (defending the cog-dim restriction; defending the trust ordering as non-arbitrary). framing/ontology / open-question docs\new text document.txt

◈ "Aren't we just implementing a classifier?" — the honest reduction

framing/ontologyresult/finding

User-driven collapse test of the architecture: on PoisonedRAG, trace_memory's retrieval IS cosine (same hash_embed as the vector arm); remove classifier+labels and you get vanilla cosine; add them and the prompt is structurally identical to vector_with_labels. The architecture is genuinely more only in two places, neither exercised by single-shot QA: the add_derived inscription rule and the SSIR routing policy — both dynamic-behavior properties with value only when the agent produces records over time.

docs\product.txt
aren't we just implementing a classifier? — the honest reduction user-driven collapse test of the architecture: on poisonedrag, trace_memory's retrieval is cosine (same hash_embed as the vector arm); remove classifier+labels and you get vanilla cosine; add them and the prompt is structurally identical to vector_with_labels. the architecture is genuinely more only in two places, neither exercised by single-shot qa: the add_derived inscription rule and the ssir routing policy — both dynamic-behavior properties with value only when the agent produces records over time. result/finding / framing/ontology docs\product.txt

◈ "A source-discipline layer wrapping standard retrieval" (honest product framing)

framing/ontologyproduct

Selling the framework as "a new retrieval architecture" is overclaiming; the data supports "a source-discipline layer wrapping standard retrieval, plus a derivation-chain safety rule." The two-tier claim: on single-shot RAG the value is classifier + label-aware template; on multi-turn derivations the additional value is the inscription rule, which vector_with_labels cannot match because vector has no inscription discipline.

docs\product.txt
a source-discipline layer wrapping standard retrieval (honest product framing) selling the framework as a new retrieval architecture is overclaiming; the data supports a source-discipline layer wrapping standard retrieval, plus a derivation-chain safety rule. the two-tier claim: on single-shot rag the value is classifier + label-aware template; on multi-turn derivations the additional value is the inscription rule, which vector_with_labels cannot match because vector has no inscription discipline. product / framing/ontology docs\product.txt

◈ hash_embed weakness caveat

open-questionresult/finding

Self-critique that both arms use a weak 64-dim hash-based embedding rather than Contriever or text-embedding-3-small, so the vector-vs-trace gap on PoisonedRAG is partly an artifact of weak retrieval on both sides; "the real test is the delta between vector_with_labels and trace_memory, and that delta is zero."

docs\product.txt
hash_embed weakness caveat self-critique that both arms use a weak 64-dim hash-based embedding rather than contriever or text-embedding-3-small, so the vector-vs-trace gap on poisonedrag is partly an artifact of weak retrieval on both sides; the real test is the delta between vector_with_labels and trace_memory, and that delta is zero. result/finding / open-question docs\product.txt

◈ The question-closing experiment: bolt the classifier onto any RAG

experiment/benchmarkopen-question

Proposed decisive experiment: take a standard cosine RAG library, bolt on the classifier and label-aware prompt, and compare against trace_memory on the v2 derivation-chain benchmark. If trace_memory still wins, the inscription rule has independent value; if not, "the framework collapses to 'use a classifier and a prompt template' and the library is a convenience wrapper. That's the experiment that would close the question."

docs\product.txt
the question-closing experiment: bolt the classifier onto any rag proposed decisive experiment: take a standard cosine rag library, bolt on the classifier and label-aware prompt, and compare against trace_memory on the v2 derivation-chain benchmark. if trace_memory still wins, the inscription rule has independent value; if not, the framework collapses to 'use a classifier and a prompt template' and the library is a convenience wrapper. that's the experiment that would close the question. experiment/benchmark / open-question docs\product.txt

◈ Source(·) classifier training pipeline (idea family)

experiment/benchmarkproduct

A self-contained workspace for replacing the rule-based Source(·) policy with a measured, trained natural-prose classifier: reproducible corpus generator (8,712 examples balanced at 1,452 per label; train/val/test 4,176/2,262/2,274; all out-of-domain domains withheld from training; 70 hard decoys in test), a labeling guide, an authored challenge set, a small CPU transformer (DistilBERT, "distilbert-cpu-v3"; DeBERTa-v3-small originally suggested), and a raw-vs-guarded benchmark matrix with regression exit codes.

source_classifier\README.md; docs\source_classifier.txt
source(·) classifier training pipeline (idea family) a self-contained workspace for replacing the rule-based source(·) policy with a measured, trained natural-prose classifier: reproducible corpus generator (8,712 examples balanced at 1,452 per label; train/val/test 4,176/2,262/2,274; all out-of-domain domains withheld from training; 70 hard decoys in test), a labeling guide, an authored challenge set, a small cpu transformer (distilbert, distilbert-cpu-v3 ; deberta-v3-small originally suggested), and a raw-vs-guarded benchmark matrix with regression exit codes. product / experiment/benchmark source_classifier\readme.md; docs\source_classifier.txt

◈ "Label the source of the claim, not whether the claim is true"

mechanismframing/ontology

The labeling guide's core rules: label provenance not truth; use the lowest-trust label that honestly applies unless there is clear boundary evidence for a higher-trust source; and "truth does not upgrade source — a rumor remains fabricated_or_uncertain unless verified by an external/tool boundary."

source_classifier\LABELING_GUIDE.md
label the source of the claim, not whether the claim is true the labeling guide's core rules: label provenance not truth; use the lowest-trust label that honestly applies unless there is clear boundary evidence for a higher-trust source; and truth does not upgrade source — a rumor remains fabricated_or_uncertain unless verified by an external/tool boundary. framing/ontology / mechanism source_classifier\labeling_guide.md

◈ Hard-case annotation taxonomy

framing/ontologyexperiment/benchmark

Named boundary cases for source annotation: technical prose without a tool boundary ("3 pods are in CrashLoopBackOff" is not automatically tool_output); retrieved memory that reads like a fresh fact; simulation phrased as declarative advice; the true rumor. These became the "hard decoy" slices in the corpus.

source_classifier\LABELING_GUIDE.md
hard-case annotation taxonomy named boundary cases for source annotation: technical prose without a tool boundary ( 3 pods are in crashloopbackoff is not automatically tool_output); retrieved memory that reads like a fresh fact; simulation phrased as declarative advice; the true rumor. these became the hard decoy slices in the corpus. framing/ontology / experiment/benchmark source_classifier\labeling_guide.md

◈ Guarded-boundary deployment policy (model + guards, not raw checkpoint)

mechanismresult/finding

The raw DistilBERT checkpoint reaches 1.0 on the generated held-out corpus but only 0.983 (min-class 0.900) on the authored challenge set, so the deployable policy wraps the model with high-precision provenance guards (guard rate ~0.9); guarded policy reaches 1.0 / 0.0 false-ext / 0.0 trust-upgrade on both sets. Lesson: "the deployable boundary must remain model plus guard, not the raw checkpoint by itself" — and the perfect scores should be read as pipeline validation on rule-labelled data, not open-domain source grounding.

source_classifier\README.md; source_classifier\reports\benchmark\source_classifier_benchmark.md; VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §1.6
guarded-boundary deployment policy (model + guards, not raw checkpoint) the raw distilbert checkpoint reaches 1.0 on the generated held-out corpus but only 0.983 (min-class 0.900) on the authored challenge set, so the deployable policy wraps the model with high-precision provenance guards (guard rate ~0.9); guarded policy reaches 1.0 / 0.0 false-ext / 0.0 trust-upgrade on both sets. lesson: the deployable boundary must remain model plus guard, not the raw checkpoint by itself — and the perfect scores should be read as pipeline validation on rule-labelled data, not open-domain source grounding. mechanism / result/finding source_classifier\readme.md; source_classifier\reports\benchmark\source_classifier_benchmark.md; validated_primitives_ledger.md §1.6

◈ Classifier acceptance bar

experiment/benchmark

Numeric bar for replacing the rule-based product policy: held-out accuracy ≥ 0.99, per-class ≥ 0.97, false externalization ≤ 0.01, trust-upgrade rate ≤ 0.01, hard-decoy slice reported separately — with false externalization named as the primary safety metric.

source_classifier\README.md; trace-memory-architecture\ROADMAP.md Phase 6
classifier acceptance bar numeric bar for replacing the rule-based product policy: held-out accuracy ≥ 0.99, per-class ≥ 0.97, false externalization ≤ 0.01, trust-upgrade rate ≤ 0.01, hard-decoy slice reported separately — with false externalization named as the primary safety metric. experiment/benchmark source_classifier\readme.md; trace-memory-architecture\roadmap.md phase 6

◈ Trust-upgrade rate as a classifier metric

experiment/benchmarkcoinage

Alongside accuracy and false externalization, the classifier reports a trust-upgrade rate — how often the predicted label is higher-trust than truth — importing the inscription-safety framing into classifier evaluation itself.

source_classifier\README.md; VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §1.6
trust-upgrade rate as a classifier metric alongside accuracy and false externalization, the classifier reports a trust-upgrade rate — how often the predicted label is higher-trust than truth — importing the inscription-safety framing into classifier evaluation itself. experiment/benchmark / coinage source_classifier\readme.md; validated_primitives_ledger.md §1.6

◈ Updated roadmap: "from assumed sources to grounded sources"

framing/ontology

Post-merge roadmap rewrite recognizing that fixture-supplied source labels are not enough; the program must separate three layers — fixture ground truth, source inference from content/context/margin/recency, and trust-preserving inscription for derived records — and rebuild in the order: source inference hardening → anti-laundering integration → route/fold revalidation with inferred sources → claim-scale task families → operation/correction chains under laundering pressure → residual attention and self-index under source uncertainty → coupled-field last. "The old order started with supplied source labels. The new order starts with earning and preserving source labels."

trace-memory-architecture\ROADMAP.md
updated roadmap: from assumed sources to grounded sources post-merge roadmap rewrite recognizing that fixture-supplied source labels are not enough; the program must separate three layers — fixture ground truth, source inference from content/context/margin/recency, and trust-preserving inscription for derived records — and rebuild in the order: source inference hardening → anti-laundering integration → route/fold revalidation with *inferred* sources → claim-scale task families → operation/correction chains under laundering pressure → residual attention and self-index under source uncertainty → coupled-field last. the old order started with supplied source labels. the new order starts with earning and preserving source labels. framing/ontology trace-memory-architecture\roadmap.md

◈ Ten "ground truths reached"

result/finding

The explicit list of claims with direct controlled evidence, including: labels survive the full pipeline; source-sensitive routing prevents fixture sim/fab promotion; memory is operationally meaningful only with nonzero with/without-memory transition effect; utility inscription beats degenerate policies under budget; source downgrading is required for safe derived inscription; provenance propagation necessary but insufficient; local laundering detection undercounts after one bad label; source inference feasible but retrieval features insufficient; provider-empty responses are a separate failure plane; source-aware reranking fixed a concrete polarity/confusability boundary.

trace-memory-architecture\ROADMAP.md (Ground Truths Reached)
ten ground truths reached the explicit list of claims with direct controlled evidence, including: labels survive the full pipeline; source-sensitive routing prevents fixture sim/fab promotion; memory is operationally meaningful only with nonzero with/without-memory transition effect; utility inscription beats degenerate policies under budget; source downgrading is required for safe derived inscription; provenance propagation necessary but insufficient; local laundering detection undercounts after one bad label; source inference feasible but retrieval features insufficient; provider-empty responses are a separate failure plane; source-aware reranking fixed a concrete polarity/confusability boundary. result/finding trace-memory-architecture\roadmap.md (ground truths reached)

◈ Degradation-bound requirement for inferred-source routing

experiment/benchmark

Phase 4 acceptance criterion: when labels are inferred rather than supplied, routing must beat the uniform-external baseline on false externalization AND remain within a documented degradation bound of supplied-source truth — with route misses separated into source-inference, retrieval, fold, and provider-output planes.

trace-memory-architecture\ROADMAP.md Phase 4
degradation-bound requirement for inferred-source routing phase 4 acceptance criterion: when labels are inferred rather than supplied, routing must beat the uniform-external baseline on false externalization and remain within a *documented degradation bound* of supplied-source truth — with route misses separated into source-inference, retrieval, fold, and provider-output planes. experiment/benchmark trace-memory-architecture\roadmap.md phase 4

◈ Provider-empty vs primitive-failure separation

mechanismresult/finding

Live-LLM engineering finding: empty provider responses create zero-fold-force route misses even when retrieval is correct, so route gates must be reported both raw and "provider-valid"; retries fixed it (N=20 retry suite: route accuracy 1.0, zero final empty with-memory responses). The gpt-5-nano "failure" was an output-token budget issue, resolved by max_output_tokens=1000 — a lesson in not mistaking provider behavior for primitive failure.

VALIDATION_ROADMAP_EXECUTION.md; VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §2.5
provider-empty vs primitive-failure separation live-llm engineering finding: empty provider responses create zero-fold-force route misses even when retrieval is correct, so route gates must be reported both raw and provider-valid ; retries fixed it (n=20 retry suite: route accuracy 1.0, zero final empty with-memory responses). the gpt-5-nano failure was an output-token budget issue, resolved by max_output_tokens=1000 — a lesson in not mistaking provider behavior for primitive failure. result/finding / mechanism validation_roadmap_execution.md; validated_primitives_ledger.md §2.5

◈ Cross-provider and task-family validation matrix

experiment/benchmarkresult/finding

The live validation ladder extended across a 4-target OpenAI/Anthropic model matrix (3 passed; the one failure isolated to provider output) and two additional fixed-truth task families (billing_refund, security_rotation) at N=5 with zero boundary failures — with the honest caveat that task families are smoke-level, not claim-scale (N=20), and "provider/model comparison is not exhaustive and should not be read as a model benchmark."

VALIDATION_ROADMAP_EXECUTION.md; trace-memory-architecture\ROADMAP.md
cross-provider and task-family validation matrix the live validation ladder extended across a 4-target openai/anthropic model matrix (3 passed; the one failure isolated to provider output) and two additional fixed-truth task families (billing_refund, security_rotation) at n=5 with zero boundary failures — with the honest caveat that task families are smoke-level, not claim-scale (n=20), and provider/model comparison is not exhaustive and should not be read as a model benchmark. result/finding / experiment/benchmark validation_roadmap_execution.md; trace-memory-architecture\roadmap.md

◈ FGM six-layer memory architecture

mechanism

The substrate implementation: Layer 1 trace (LLM context, decaying) → 2 storage (MemoryStore with per-record fold-force tracking) → 3 addressability (MarginRetriever with retrieval margin + confusability monitoring) → 4 folding (FoldGate dual-pass transition measurement — only memories that change decisions pass) → 5 operations (OperationMemory for recursive self-correction) → 6 compression (confusability-triggered). FGMAgent runs the full cycle: retrieve → fold → measure → gate → record → auto-compress.

trace-memory-architecture\README.md
fgm six-layer memory architecture the substrate implementation: layer 1 trace (llm context, decaying) → 2 storage (memorystore with per-record fold-force tracking) → 3 addressability (marginretriever with retrieval margin + confusability monitoring) → 4 folding (foldgate dual-pass transition measurement — only memories that change decisions pass) → 5 operations (operationmemory for recursive self-correction) → 6 compression (confusability-triggered). fgmagent runs the full cycle: retrieve → fold → measure → gate → record → auto-compress. mechanism trace-memory-architecture\readme.md

◈ Five trace probes mapping paper definitions to executable tests

experiment/benchmark

trace_retention_probe (lag projection retains residue), causal_trace_probe (perturbing prior state changes the trace component), addressability_probe (top-k selects stored record), fold_force_probe (folded memory changes non-bookkeeping transition), p7_retrieval_regime_probe (phase boundary across four retrieval regimes).

trace-memory-architecture\README.md
five trace probes mapping paper definitions to executable tests trace_retention_probe (lag projection retains residue), causal_trace_probe (perturbing prior state changes the trace component), addressability_probe (top-k selects stored record), fold_force_probe (folded memory changes non-bookkeeping transition), p7_retrieval_regime_probe (phase boundary across four retrieval regimes). experiment/benchmark trace-memory-architecture\readme.md

◈ P7 phase boundary at N=1000 and the fold-force metric discovery

result/finding

Empirical key findings from the architecture repo: rich_distinctive operation records maintain a 1.000 hit rate at N=1000 while sparse_confusable degrades to ~0.03 (the P7 phase boundary made concrete); L2-divergence fold-force does not discriminate with LLM transitions but answer-quality fold-force (cosine toward memory content) discriminates at 5.9×; proactive confusability-triggered compression is architecturally necessary while reactive per-record pruning is insufficient.

trace-memory-architecture\README.md (key findings)
p7 phase boundary at n=1000 and the fold-force metric discovery empirical key findings from the architecture repo: rich_distinctive operation records maintain a 1.000 hit rate at n=1000 while sparse_confusable degrades to ~0.03 (the p7 phase boundary made concrete); l2-divergence fold-force does not discriminate with llm transitions but answer-quality fold-force (cosine toward memory content) discriminates at 5.9×; proactive confusability-triggered compression is architecturally necessary while reactive per-record pruning is insufficient. result/finding trace-memory-architecture\readme.md (key findings)

◈ P7 conditionalization: overload is a retrieval-interference pathology

dead-endresult/finding

The revision patch that falsified the unconditional "meta-memory overload" claim: overload is not a primitive property of storing more operation-memory; it appears only when records are sparse, highly confusable, or retrieved through bandwidth-limited attention. With rich, distinctive records, full preservation can improve long-horizon continuity, and the compression optimum degenerates toward full preservation until resource costs dominate.

references\SIMReC_P7_revision_patch.md
p7 conditionalization: overload is a retrieval-interference pathology the revision patch that falsified the unconditional meta-memory overload claim: overload is not a primitive property of storing more operation-memory; it appears only when records are sparse, highly confusable, or retrieved through bandwidth-limited attention. with rich, distinctive records, full preservation can *improve* long-horizon continuity, and the compression optimum degenerates toward full preservation until resource costs dominate. result/finding / dead-end (for the unconditional claim) references\simrec_p7_revision_patch.md

◈ Retrieval margin M_t(q) and query-conditioned confusability χ_t(q)

mechanismcoinage

The two formal variables the P7 revision introduces: M_t(q) = similarity of query to the relevant record minus max similarity to any competitor (high margin = sharply distinguishable), and χ_t(q) = Pr[M_t(q) ≤ δ] — confusability as the "missing load-bearing variable." Overload is expected when χ high and retrieval bandwidth k small, not otherwise.

SIMReC_P7_revision_patch.md
retrieval margin m_t(q) and query-conditioned confusability χ_t(q) the two formal variables the p7 revision introduces: m_t(q) = similarity of query to the relevant record minus max similarity to any competitor (high margin = sharply distinguishable), and χ_t(q) = pr[m_t(q) ≤ δ] — confusability as the missing load-bearing variable. overload is expected when χ high and retrieval bandwidth k small, not otherwise. mechanism / coinage simrec_p7_revision_patch.md

◈ Accuracy optimum vs resource optimum separation

framing/ontology

If the objective is accuracy/continuity alone, full preservation can be optimal in the rich-record regime; an interior compression optimum reappears only when storage, latency, and compute/context costs enter the objective — preventing the manuscript from confusing a cognitive accuracy claim with an engineering efficiency claim.

SIMReC_P7_revision_patch.md §2
accuracy optimum vs resource optimum separation if the objective is accuracy/continuity alone, full preservation can be optimal in the rich-record regime; an interior compression optimum reappears only when storage, latency, and compute/context costs enter the objective — preventing the manuscript from confusing a cognitive accuracy claim with an engineering efficiency claim. framing/ontology simrec_p7_revision_patch.md §2

◈ Compression operator with a top-k recall constraint

mechanism

Revised compression operator: minimize compressed size plus transition divergence, subject to per-dimension divergence bounds AND a new constraint that compression must not reduce the probability that the relevant operation record survives in the top-k — preventing compression from "preserving abstract operation type while deleting the substantive decision content needed for later recursive self-revision."

SIMReC_P7_revision_patch.md §4
compression operator with a top-k recall constraint revised compression operator: minimize compressed size plus transition divergence, subject to per-dimension divergence bounds and a new constraint that compression must not reduce the probability that the relevant operation record survives in the top-k — preventing compression from preserving abstract operation type while deleting the substantive decision content needed for later recursive self-revision. mechanism simrec_p7_revision_patch.md §4

◈ "Forgetting is not constitutive; specificity-preserving selection is"

framing/ontology

The philosophical upshot of the P7 reversal: it is not that less memory is always better; what is constitutive is specificity-preserving selection. Aggressive compression in rich-record regimes destroys the very evidence enabling recursive self-revision. Framed as progress — the experiment converted a broken prediction into "a better-specified retrieval-interference theorem" with a four-regime phase table.

SIMReC_P7_revision_patch.md (discussion + regime table)
forgetting is not constitutive; specificity-preserving selection is the philosophical upshot of the p7 reversal: it is not that less memory is always better; what is constitutive is specificity-preserving selection. aggressive compression in rich-record regimes destroys the very evidence enabling recursive self-revision. framed as progress — the experiment converted a broken prediction into a better-specified retrieval-interference theorem with a four-regime phase table. framing/ontology simrec_p7_revision_patch.md (discussion + regime table)

◈ Publication ledger of the mnestic paper sequence

framing/ontology

The repo's AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md maintain the running publication sequence with DOIs: SIMFC (10.5281/zenodo.20042034), simrec-reference (GitHub), Trace Formalization (10.5281/zenodo.20043070), trace-memory-architecture (GitHub), Attention Is Not Continuity (10.5281/zenodo.20057986), MAFC (concept 10.5281/zenodo.20088734, v3 at 10.5281/zenodo.20089558), Recursive Self-Indexed Correction Chains (10.5281/zenodo.20088789) — the citation skeleton the SSIR and Source-Downgrading papers build on.

AGENTS.md; CLAUDE.md (repo)
publication ledger of the mnestic paper sequence the repo's agents.md/claude.md maintain the running publication sequence with dois: simfc (10.5281/zenodo.20042034), simrec-reference (github), trace formalization (10.5281/zenodo.20043070), trace-memory-architecture (github), attention is not continuity (10.5281/zenodo.20057986), mafc (concept 10.5281/zenodo.20088734, v3 at 10.5281/zenodo.20089558), recursive self-indexed correction chains (10.5281/zenodo.20088789) — the citation skeleton the ssir and source-downgrading papers build on. framing/ontology agents.md; claude.md (repo)

◈ Generalisation-across-LLMs caveat

open-question

All headline benchmark numbers are gpt-4.1-mini only; a different model could shift behaviour. The relative result (vector launders, trace_memory doesn't) is expected to be stable but is explicitly not proven beyond this model.

Session_Learnings_2026-05-12.md ("does not establish")
generalisation-across-llms caveat all headline benchmark numbers are gpt-4.1-mini only; a different model could shift behaviour. the *relative* result (vector launders, trace_memory doesn't) is expected to be stable but is explicitly not proven beyond this model. open-question session_learnings_2026-05-12.md ( does not establish )

◈ The v2 "open verdict" moment preserved in memory

open-questionresult/finding

The memory note next_session_harden_dataset.md captures the state where the v2 dataset and harness were fully landed but the live sweep had not yet run — the headline v2 questions (0% held? bash_nolabels breaks? over-quarantine on clean?) recorded as open, with cost estimate ($3–5) and the note that the 60/60 fab/sim target was approximate (57/54). A snapshot of pre-registered uncertainty before the result came in.

memory\next_session_harden_dataset.md
the v2 open verdict moment preserved in memory the memory note next_session_harden_dataset.md captures the state where the v2 dataset and harness were fully landed but the live sweep had not yet run — the headline v2 questions (0% held? bash_nolabels breaks? over-quarantine on clean?) recorded as open, with cost estimate ($3–5) and the note that the 60/60 fab/sim target was approximate (57/54). a snapshot of pre-registered uncertainty before the result came in. open-question (historical) / result/finding memory\next_session_harden_dataset.md

◈ Three-stage research-arc close

framing/ontologyresult/finding

The arc as recorded at close on 2026-05-13: (1) v1 adversarial-reload 42 sessions → ship-it; (2) v2 hardening 139 sessions → verdict held (0% vs 36%, bash safety matched at 17% token cost); (3) PoisonedRAG external validation → initially looked like a 24pp architectural win, but the vector_with_labels control reattributed it to classifier + prompt. "The paper's actual claim is narrow and is validated by adversarial-reload v2, not by PoisonedRAG."

memory\source_downgrading_validation.md
three-stage research-arc close the arc as recorded at close on 2026-05-13: (1) v1 adversarial-reload 42 sessions → ship-it; (2) v2 hardening 139 sessions → verdict held (0% vs 36%, bash safety matched at 17% token cost); (3) poisonedrag external validation → initially looked like a 24pp architectural win, but the vector_with_labels control reattributed it to classifier + prompt. the paper's actual claim is narrow and is validated by adversarial-reload v2, not by poisonedrag. result/finding / framing/ontology memory\source_downgrading_validation.md

◈ The fine-tuned Source(·) classifier as "the next paper"

productopen-question

The declared successor project: the label-inference layer — a fine-tuned Source(·) classifier whose adversarial recall is high enough (per the recall→ASR model, high-80s to mid-90s) to cap the framework's safety floor on production RAG. The prompt-engineered baseline ships at benchmarks/poisonedrag/source_classifier.py as a starting point; the review adds that no directly equivalent six-class source classifier exists in the literature (nearest families: grounding scorers, citation-faithfulness, claim verification, SelfCheckGPT, semantic entropy, RAG denoisers), so the classifier program has its own novelty room.

memory\source_downgrading_validation.md; RESEARCH_CONCLUSIONS.md; source_downgrading_review.md §8.3
the fine-tuned source(·) classifier as the next paper the declared successor project: the label-inference layer — a fine-tuned source(·) classifier whose adversarial recall is high enough (per the recall→asr model, high-80s to mid-90s) to cap the framework's safety floor on production rag. the prompt-engineered baseline ships at benchmarks/poisonedrag/source_classifier.py as a starting point; the review adds that no directly equivalent six-class source classifier exists in the literature (nearest families: grounding scorers, citation-faithfulness, claim verification, selfcheckgpt, semantic entropy, rag denoisers), so the classifier program has its own novelty room. open-question / product memory\source_downgrading_validation.md; research_conclusions.md; source_downgrading_review.md §8.3

P4 · source_downgrading 169 ideas

May 2026 — the source-downgrading paper: preventing memory laundering (0% vs vector 31%) · P4-source-downgrading.md

Core mechanism and coinages

◈ Inference laundering

coinage

The central named failure mode: an agent observes something, derives a free-text inference from it, and writes the inference back to memory under an inflated source label (often external), after which downstream routing treats the derived record as if externally observed. Named after financial laundering, "where the origin of a value is obscured by passing it through intermediate stages."

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §1; docs\architecture\SOURCE_INFERENCE_AND_LAUNDERING.md
"a fabricated detail is generated, then reasoned over, and the reasoning product is treated downstream as observed evidence... We refer to this pattern as inference laundering."
inference laundering the central named failure mode: an agent observes something, derives a free-text inference from it, and writes the inference back to memory under an inflated source label (often `external`), after which downstream routing treats the derived record as if externally observed. named after financial laundering, where the origin of a value is obscured by passing it through intermediate stages. coinage paper\source_downgrading.tex §1; docs\architecture\source_inference_and_laundering.md a fabricated detail is generated, then reasoned over, and the reasoning product is treated downstream as observed evidence... we refer to this pattern as *inference laundering*.

◈ Source downgrading as an inscription-integrity invariant

mechanism

The core rule: given correctly labeled contributors, a derived memory record may not carry source integrity higher than its weakest contributor, with inference as the upper bound for derived inscription. Explicitly framed as NOT a new memory type — a writeback-time integrity invariant.

README.md; AGENTS.md; paper\Source_Downgrading.tex
"derived trust cannot exceed input trust; inference is the upper bound for inscription." (boxed central claim)
source downgrading as an inscription-integrity invariant the core rule: given correctly labeled contributors, a derived memory record may not carry source integrity higher than its weakest contributor, with `inference` as the upper bound for derived inscription. explicitly framed as not a new memory type — a writeback-time integrity invariant. mechanism readme.md; agents.md; paper\source_downgrading.tex derived trust cannot exceed input trust; inference is the upper bound for inscription. (boxed central claim)

◈ Six-class trust ordering (total order over content sources)

mechanism

A total order over content-source classes: fabricated_or_uncertain(0) < simulation(1) < inference(2) < retrieved_memory/react(3) < tool_output(4) < external(5). Each adjacent pair gets an operational (not cognitive-theoretic) justification. Deliberately axiomatic; declared "a default engineering policy for the tested implementation, not a universal trust order."

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §3 (Definition 1); docs\trace-memory-library.md
six-class trust ordering (total order over content sources) a total order over content-source classes: `fabricated_or_uncertain(0) < simulation(1) < inference(2) < retrieved_memory/react(3) < tool_output(4) < external(5)`. each adjacent pair gets an operational (not cognitive-theoretic) justification. deliberately axiomatic; declared a default engineering policy for the tested implementation, not a universal trust order. mechanism paper\source_downgrading.tex §3 (definition 1); docs\trace-memory-library.md

◈ operation_record as a seventh, out-of-order class

mechanism

A seventh source class, op (operation-memory record: a record of how memory use changed a transition), is excluded from the trust ordering because it indexes events rather than content. Its behavior when participating in a derivation is left undefined — flagged by the reviewer as a formal gap.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §3; docs\source-downgrading-review.md §3.1
operation_record as a seventh, out-of-order class a seventh source class, `op` (operation-memory record: a record of how memory use changed a transition), is excluded from the trust ordering because it indexes events rather than content. its behavior when participating in a derivation is left undefined — flagged by the reviewer as a formal gap. mechanism paper\source_downgrading.tex §3; docs\source-downgrading-review.md §3.1

◈ Min-trust meet (Trust_min)

mechanism

The derived record's source is the argmin-trust label over the contributing input set — the standard integrity/taint lattice meet, specialized to memory-source labels. Ties at equal minimum trust may return any of the tied labels because only rank is consulted downstream.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex Definition "Min-trust of a set of inputs"
min-trust meet (trust_min) the derived record's source is the argmin-trust label over the contributing input set — the standard integrity/taint lattice meet, specialized to memory-source labels. ties at equal minimum trust may return any of the tied labels because only rank is consulted downstream. mechanism paper\source_downgrading.tex definition min-trust of a set of inputs

◈ The inference ceiling

mechanism

Even when ALL inputs are high-trust observations, a derivation is capped at inference — external-plus-external composition yields at most infer, never a new external record. This is the domain-specific typing rule that goes beyond the standard IFC meet: derivation changes source class.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex Definition "Inference ceiling"
"External-from-external composition does not yield a new external observation; it yields an inference."
the inference ceiling even when all inputs are high-trust observations, a derivation is capped at `inference` — external-plus-external composition yields at most `infer`, never a new `external` record. this is the domain-specific typing rule that goes beyond the standard ifc meet: derivation changes source class. mechanism paper\source_downgrading.tex definition inference ceiling external-from-external composition does not yield a new external observation; it yields an inference.

◈ Transitive provenance closure

mechanism

Derived provenance is Prov(r) = ∪ᵢ (Prov(cᵢ) ∪ {id(cᵢ)}) — the union of every contributor's stored closure plus contributor ids, modeled as a finite, cycle-safe graph. Edge-stored implementations must compute the same closure before routing/audit.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex Definition 6 / Proposition 2; CLAIMS_MATRIX.md C4
transitive provenance closure derived provenance is prov(r) = ∪ᵢ (prov(cᵢ) ∪ {id(cᵢ)}) — the union of every contributor's stored closure plus contributor ids, modeled as a finite, cycle-safe graph. edge-stored implementations must compute the same closure before routing/audit. mechanism paper\source_downgrading.tex definition 6 / proposition 2; claims_matrix.md c4

◈ The API boundary is the intervention: add_derived computes, never accepts, labels

mechanism

add_derived(content, contributing_record_ids) computes the derived label from complete contributor labels; the caller cannot pass source= on a derivation (raises TypeError). Caller-supplied labels are the laundering attack surface; removing that lever from the API is the whole fix. Also agent.add(content) without an explicit source= raises MissingSourceError — no silent external default.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §6.2; docs\trace-memory-library.md; docs\historical\trace-memory-PRD.md FR-4
"Caller-supplied labels are the laundering attack surface; computed labels close it."
the api boundary is the intervention: add_derived computes, never accepts, labels `add_derived(content, contributing_record_ids)` computes the derived label from complete contributor labels; the caller *cannot* pass `source=` on a derivation (raises typeerror). caller-supplied labels are the laundering attack surface; removing that lever from the api is the whole fix. also `agent.add(content)` without an explicit `source=` raises `missingsourceerror` — no silent `external` default. mechanism paper\source_downgrading.tex §6.2; docs\trace-memory-library.md; docs\historical\trace-memory-prd.md fr-4 caller-supplied labels are the laundering attack surface; computed labels close it.

◈ Complete-contributor-witness precondition (omitted contributors as residual attack surface)

mechanism

The rule's soundness precondition: the declared contributor set must be complete with respect to the derivation witness. If a derivation used a simulated record but the caller declares only an external one, the computed ceiling is unsound. This "moves the laundering attack surface from caller-supplied labels to caller-supplied incomplete witnesses." Enforcement ideas: sealed generation contexts, automatic derivation tracing, append-only derivation logs, support witnesses, entailment checks, audit hooks.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §4.1; CLAIMS_MATRIX.md C5
complete-contributor-witness precondition (omitted contributors as residual attack surface) the rule's soundness precondition: the declared contributor set must be complete with respect to the derivation witness. if a derivation used a simulated record but the caller declares only an external one, the computed ceiling is unsound. this moves the laundering attack surface from caller-supplied labels to caller-supplied incomplete witnesses. enforcement ideas: sealed generation contexts, automatic derivation tracing, append-only derivation logs, support witnesses, entailment checks, audit hooks. mechanism paper\source_downgrading.tex §4.1; claims_matrix.md c5

◈ Proposition 1: Trust monotonicity

result/finding

Under source-downgrading inscription, Trust(Source(r)) ≤ min over inputs and ≤ Trust(infer). Proof immediate from the definitions — the reviewer calls it "rigorous but trivial," and the paper accepts that: the theorem's value is in the binding, not the algebra.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex Proposition 1; docs\source-downgrading-review.md §3.2
proposition 1: trust monotonicity under source-downgrading inscription, trust(source(r)) ≤ min over inputs and ≤ trust(infer). proof immediate from the definitions — the reviewer calls it rigorous but trivial, and the paper accepts that: the theorem's value is in the binding, not the algebra. result/finding paper\source_downgrading.tex proposition 1; docs\source-downgrading-review.md §3.2

◈ Property: pure provenance propagation fails

result/finding

A policy that preserves full lineage but always labels derivations infer (no trust cap) still routes simulation/fabrication-derived records to trusted targets, because routing reads the label, not the chain. Empirically 0.57 truth-grounded ceiling violation on the fixture, 0.448 on the 163-scenario benchmark, despite perfect 1.00 provenance recall. Slogan: provenance ≠ safety; recording lineage and using lineage for a writeback policy are different.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex Property 1; results\benchmarks\LAUNDERING_BENCHMARK.md; CLAIMS_MATRIX.md C6
property: pure provenance propagation fails a policy that preserves full lineage but always labels derivations `infer` (no trust cap) still routes simulation/fabrication-derived records to trusted targets, because routing reads the label, not the chain. empirically 0.57 truth-grounded ceiling violation on the fixture, 0.448 on the 163-scenario benchmark, despite perfect 1.00 provenance recall. slogan: provenance ≠ safety; recording lineage and *using* lineage for a writeback policy are different. result/finding paper\source_downgrading.tex property 1; results\benchmarks\laundering_benchmark.md; claims_matrix.md c6

◈ Property: naive inscription launders by default — always, not sometimes

result/finding

With a default-external derived write, every derived record violates the trust ceiling (1.00 violation rate on every seed): "not that it launders only sometimes, but that it launders always." This is the baseline the rule exists to defeat.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex Property 2; CLAIMS_MATRIX.md C7
property: naive inscription launders by default — always, not sometimes with a default-`external` derived write, every derived record violates the trust ceiling (1.00 violation rate on every seed): not that it launders only sometimes, but that it launders always. this is the baseline the rule exists to defeat. result/finding paper\source_downgrading.tex property 2; claims_matrix.md c7

◈ Cascade invisibility

coinage

The paper's second coinage and methodological finding: once a label has been laundered to external, later derivations see "all external inputs," so the system's own self-audit (computed from stored labels) reports clean while truth-grounded checks show violation. On the fixture the local metric reports 0.43 while truth says 1.00 — a 0.57 gap "structurally unrecoverable from internal labels alone." Framed as an application-level audit pathology analogous to lost taint / unsound provenance capture, not new mathematics.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §5 (Property 3); docs\architecture\SOURCE_INFERENCE_AND_LAUNDERING.md; docs\source-downgrading-review.md §6
cascade invisibility the paper's second coinage and methodological finding: once a label has been laundered to `external`, later derivations see all external inputs, so the system's *own* self-audit (computed from stored labels) reports clean while truth-grounded checks show violation. on the fixture the local metric reports 0.43 while truth says 1.00 — a 0.57 gap structurally unrecoverable from internal labels alone. framed as an application-level audit pathology analogous to lost taint / unsound provenance capture, not new mathematics. coinage paper\source_downgrading.tex §5 (property 3); docs\architecture\source_inference_and_laundering.md; docs\source-downgrading-review.md §6

◈ Local vs truth-grounded laundering rate (the paired-metric definitions)

experiment/benchmark

Two formally defined audit metrics: LR_local (did some non-external input get written external?) and LR_truth (did the derived record exceed the fixture's expected max-trust ceiling?). They agree on single-step derivations and diverge on chains — the divergence is cascade invisibility. The closed-form undercount statement was restricted, post-review, to linear externalizing chains with single contributors.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex Definitions 7-8; docs\paper-revisions.md C4
local vs truth-grounded laundering rate (the paired-metric definitions) two formally defined audit metrics: lr_local (did some non-external input get written external?) and lr_truth (did the derived record exceed the fixture's expected max-trust ceiling?). they agree on single-step derivations and diverge on chains — the divergence *is* cascade invisibility. the closed-form undercount statement was restricted, post-review, to linear externalizing chains with single contributors. experiment/benchmark paper\source_downgrading.tex definitions 7-8; docs\paper-revisions.md c4

◈ Prediction 1: the two-metric audit protocol requirement

mechanism

Any audit of a memory architecture for laundering safety must compare a self-referential metric against a truth-grounded/out-of-system metric; a single self-referential metric "will report a false floor on the laundering rate." In the library, is_clean requires the truth-grounded rate to be defined and zero; the self-only rate is a lower bound never a measurement, and audit_laundering() carries a cascade_invisibility_warning.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex Prediction 1; docs\trace-memory-library.md
prediction 1: the two-metric audit protocol requirement any audit of a memory architecture for laundering safety must compare a self-referential metric against a truth-grounded/out-of-system metric; a single self-referential metric will report a false floor on the laundering rate. in the library, `is_clean` requires the truth-grounded rate to be defined and zero; the self-only rate is a lower bound never a measurement, and `audit_laundering()` carries a `cascade_invisibility_warning`. mechanism paper\source_downgrading.tex prediction 1; docs\trace-memory-library.md

◈ "External-anchor audit" as the honest production term

mechanism

In production, the audit anchor is never omniscient truth; it is an out-of-band check not derived from the labels being audited. The paper deliberately renames "truth-grounded" to "external-anchor audit" for deployments, and lists practical anchors: immutable append-only provenance logs, canary low-trust contaminants seeded into memory, sampled oracle review, independent shadow source classifiers, signed source envelopes, and claim-level support verification — anchor choice depends on threat model.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §5; docs\source-downgrading-review.md §6.3; docs\paper-revisions.md M3
external-anchor audit as the honest production term in production, the audit anchor is never omniscient truth; it is an out-of-band check not derived from the labels being audited. the paper deliberately renames truth-grounded to external-anchor audit for deployments, and lists practical anchors: immutable append-only provenance logs, canary low-trust contaminants seeded into memory, sampled oracle review, independent shadow source classifiers, signed source envelopes, and claim-level support verification — anchor choice depends on threat model. mechanism paper\source_downgrading.tex §5; docs\source-downgrading-review.md §6.3; docs\paper-revisions.md m3

◈ Source ≠ Credibility ≠ Confidence (three-quantity separation)

framing/ontology

Source = how a record was produced (monotone under derivation, never promoted); credibility = revisable belief in the content (time-varying); confidence = estimated quality/calibration of the system's own labels/updates. A discredited external observation stays ext (credibility drops); a confirmed inference stays infer (credibility rises). Promoting confirmed inferences to external "would reintroduce laundering through a different field name."

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §6.4; AGENTS.md; CLAIMS_MATRIX.md C18
source ≠ credibility ≠ confidence (three-quantity separation) source = how a record was produced (monotone under derivation, never promoted); credibility = revisable belief in the content (time-varying); confidence = estimated quality/calibration of the system's own labels/updates. a discredited external observation stays `ext` (credibility drops); a confirmed inference stays `infer` (credibility rises). promoting confirmed inferences to `external` would reintroduce laundering through a different field name. framing/ontology paper\source_downgrading.tex §6.4; agents.md; claims_matrix.md c18

◈ Trust means source-integrity trust, not epistemic certainty

framing/ontology

Explicit terminological guard: throughout the paper "trust" is source-integrity rank, not probability or belief strength. "Two derived records labeled infer are not equally well-justified; they are equally not-external." The Trust function returns a rank, not a probability; combining with calibrated confidence is straightforward but a different question.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §1, §6.4
trust means source-integrity trust, not epistemic certainty explicit terminological guard: throughout the paper trust is source-integrity rank, not probability or belief strength. two derived records labeled *infer* are not equally well-justified; they are equally not-external. the trust function returns a rank, not a probability; combining with calibrated confidence is straightforward but a different question. framing/ontology paper\source_downgrading.tex §1, §6.4

◈ Four-adversary threat model for recursive agent memory

framing/ontology

(1) inference-laundering writeback — the only adversary the rule defeats by construction; (2) single-shot RAG poisoning — rule dormant; (3) source-classifier compromise — rule preserves incorrect ceilings; (4) cross-agent label transport — labels stripped/upgraded in transit. Stating the boundary explicitly "prevents the negative-control results from being misread as refutations."

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §2 (Threat Model and Non-Goals)
four-adversary threat model for recursive agent memory (1) inference-laundering writeback — the only adversary the rule defeats by construction; (2) single-shot rag poisoning — rule dormant; (3) source-classifier compromise — rule preserves incorrect ceilings; (4) cross-agent label transport — labels stripped/upgraded in transit. stating the boundary explicitly prevents the negative-control results from being misread as refutations. framing/ontology paper\source_downgrading.tex §2 (threat model and non-goals)

◈ Explicit non-goals list

framing/ontology

The rule does NOT: defend raw retrieval/ranking/prompt-time aggregation; detect adversarial classifier inputs; reconcile conflicting same-rank evidence; secure label transport; calibrate per-record confidence; replace hallucination detection. "It is composable with mechanisms that address those goals, but it is not a substitute for any of them." Scope discipline maintained across README, AGENTS, claims matrix.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §2; README.md; CLAIMS_MATRIX.md C19
explicit non-goals list the rule does not: defend raw retrieval/ranking/prompt-time aggregation; detect adversarial classifier inputs; reconcile conflicting same-rank evidence; secure label transport; calibrate per-record confidence; replace hallucination detection. it is composable with mechanisms that address those goals, but it is not a substitute for any of them. scope discipline maintained across readme, agents, claims matrix. framing/ontology paper\source_downgrading.tex §2; readme.md; claims_matrix.md c19

◈ Origin-label vs operation-label factoring of the lattice

framing/ontology

The single Source label is a "conservative collapse" of two dimensions: origin (WHERE content entered: ext/tool/react) and operation (HOW it was produced: derived/simulated/fabricated/observed). Under this reading the inference ceiling is not a separate rule but a consequence: derivation is an operation and the operation imposes infer as an operation-typing label. Generalization: with multiple operation classes (summarization vs entailment vs counterfactual generation), each operation supplies its own ceiling and Source(r) = min(operation-ceiling, min origin trust).

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §3.4 (Origin labels, operation labels, and the collapsed source)
origin-label vs operation-label factoring of the lattice the single source label is a conservative collapse of two dimensions: origin (where content entered: ext/tool/react) and operation (how it was produced: derived/simulated/fabricated/observed). under this reading the inference ceiling is not a separate rule but a consequence: derivation is an operation and the operation imposes `infer` as an operation-typing label. generalization: with multiple operation classes (summarization vs entailment vs counterfactual generation), each operation supplies its own ceiling and source(r) = min(operation-ceiling, min origin trust). framing/ontology paper\source_downgrading.tex §3.4 (origin labels, operation labels, and the collapsed source)

◈ Set-valued provenance as deliberate over-approximation

framing/ontology

Prov(r) records WHICH records contributed but not HOW (no central-vs-incidental weighting, no conjunction/disjunction/summary/contradiction annotation, no verifier invalidation). Sufficient for a trust-ceiling rule (worst-case lineage is what matters), insufficient for attribution, responsibility assignment, or minimal-witness analysis. Richer semiring/how-provenance algebra can be substituted without changing the rule — flagged as orthogonal future work.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §2.5 (Database provenance)
set-valued provenance as deliberate over-approximation prov(r) records which records contributed but not how (no central-vs-incidental weighting, no conjunction/disjunction/summary/contradiction annotation, no verifier invalidation). sufficient for a trust-ceiling rule (worst-case lineage is what matters), insufficient for attribution, responsibility assignment, or minimal-witness analysis. richer semiring/how-provenance algebra can be substituted without changing the rule — flagged as orthogonal future work. framing/ontology paper\source_downgrading.tex §2.5 (database provenance)

◈ Recursive self-indexing: trust-bounded vs self-amplifying recursion

framing/ontology

The motivating architectural argument: in a self-indexed memory, each self-pointer is itself a derived record; if derived records can acquire external trust, "the recursion produces a fixed point in which the system's beliefs about its own memory states have the same trust as external evidence." Under the rule, trust degrades monotonically along the recursion and "cannot reach back up to external trust by any sequence of operations" — the property that makes recursive self-indexing "safe enough to be useful."

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §6.1
"A recursion without trust degradation is self-amplifying; a recursion with monotone trust degradation is a calculus in which the system can reason about its own memory states without confusing them with the world."
recursive self-indexing: trust-bounded vs self-amplifying recursion the motivating architectural argument: in a self-indexed memory, each self-pointer is itself a derived record; if derived records can acquire external trust, the recursion produces a fixed point in which the system's beliefs about its own memory states have the same trust as external evidence. under the rule, trust degrades monotonically along the recursion and cannot reach back up to external trust by any sequence of operations — the property that makes recursive self-indexing safe enough to be useful. framing/ontology paper\source_downgrading.tex §6.1 a recursion without trust degradation is self-amplifying; a recursion with monotone trust degradation is a calculus in which the system can reason about its own memory states without confusing them with the world.

◈ External observations as the boundary condition / anchor

metaphor

The operational justification for tool < ext: "External observations are the boundary condition for everything else; without them, the agent has no anchor." A compact metaphor for the whole grounding stance.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §3.2
external observations as the boundary condition / anchor the operational justification for `tool < ext`: external observations are the boundary condition for everything else; without them, the agent has no anchor. a compact metaphor for the whole grounding stance. metaphor paper\source_downgrading.tex §3.2

◈ Reactivation as evidence of prior inscription (infer < react justification)

framing/ontology

The argument for ranking reactivated memory above fresh inference: "Reactivation is evidence that a record passed earlier inscription decisions; pure inference carries no such history." A subtle epistemic idea — the store's own history is weak evidence of quality.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §3.2
reactivation as evidence of prior inscription (infer < react justification) the argument for ranking reactivated memory above fresh inference: reactivation is evidence that a record passed earlier inscription decisions; pure inference carries no such history. a subtle epistemic idea — the store's own history is weak evidence of quality. framing/ontology paper\source_downgrading.tex §3.2

◈ Routing vs inscription boundary

framing/ontology

Routing decides whether/where a candidate is written; source downgrading decides what source label it carries once written as derived. Complementary but distinct: routing without a trust rule passes laundered labels through; a trust rule without routing produces correctly labeled records flowing to wrong storage. The paper isolates the trust rule.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §2.3
routing vs inscription boundary routing decides whether/where a candidate is written; source downgrading decides what source label it carries once written as derived. complementary but distinct: routing without a trust rule passes laundered labels through; a trust rule without routing produces correctly labeled records flowing to wrong storage. the paper isolates the trust rule. framing/ontology paper\source_downgrading.tex §2.3

◈ The motivating fold criterion (SIMFC): memory only if it changes the transition

framing/ontology

The implementation applies the trust ordering to records satisfying the SIMFC fold criterion: a retained record is memory only when folding it into the live transition measurably changes a non-bookkeeping transition variable — fold_force(r;s,x) = ‖Φ(s,x,fold(r)) − Φ(s,x,∅)‖_cog. The paper explicitly decouples: the rule "does not require SIMFC to be accepted as a general theory of memory."

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §2.1; docs\architecture\VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §1.2
the motivating fold criterion (simfc): memory only if it changes the transition the implementation applies the trust ordering to records satisfying the simfc fold criterion: a retained record is memory only when folding it into the live transition measurably changes a non-bookkeeping transition variable — fold_force(r;s,x) = ‖φ(s,x,fold(r)) − φ(s,x,∅)‖_cog. the paper explicitly decouples: the rule does not require simfc to be accepted as a general theory of memory. framing/ontology paper\source_downgrading.tex §2.1; docs\architecture\validated_primitives_ledger.md §1.2

◈ Worked depth-3 derivation chain (contamination at hop 2)

mechanism

A fully traced example: D1 from {ext, tool} → infer (ceiling triggers); D2 from {D1, sim-hypothesis} → sim (min-trust dominates); D3 from {D2} → sim. The ceiling is monotone non-increasing (ext,tool → infer → sim → sim) and "once a low-trust contributor enters the chain, the ceiling cannot recover." Contrast: naive inscription would write every step as ext and H1's contribution would vanish.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §4.4 (Worked example)
worked depth-3 derivation chain (contamination at hop 2) a fully traced example: d1 from {ext, tool} → infer (ceiling triggers); d2 from {d1, sim-hypothesis} → sim (min-trust dominates); d3 from {d2} → sim. the ceiling is monotone non-increasing (ext,tool → infer → sim → sim) and once a low-trust contributor enters the chain, the ceiling cannot recover. contrast: naive inscription would write every step as ext and h1's contribution would vanish. mechanism paper\source_downgrading.tex §4.4 (worked example)

◈ Operational edge-case contract (empty set, cycles, contradictions, hybrids, irrelevance)

mechanism

Explicit API-contract edge cases: an empty contributing set is not a derivation (reject or label fab — labeling it infer would launder model priors into memory); cycles must be rejected at write/import or traversed with a visited set; same-rank contradictions raise a separate contradiction flag (not resolved by the rule); hybrid-source content should be split into smaller records before inscription; even complete contributors may be semantically irrelevant, requiring a derivation witness/entailment check outside the source-label layer.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §4.1; docs\source-downgrading-review.md §3.5; docs\paper-revisions.md M1
operational edge-case contract (empty set, cycles, contradictions, hybrids, irrelevance) explicit api-contract edge cases: an empty contributing set is not a derivation (reject or label `fab` — labeling it `infer` would launder model priors into memory); cycles must be rejected at write/import or traversed with a visited set; same-rank contradictions raise a separate contradiction flag (not resolved by the rule); hybrid-source content should be split into smaller records before inscription; even complete contributors may be semantically irrelevant, requiring a derivation witness/entailment check outside the source-label layer. mechanism paper\source_downgrading.tex §4.1; docs\source-downgrading-review.md §3.5; docs\paper-revisions.md m1

◈ Soft-label conservatism: default downward under classifier uncertainty

mechanism

The rule operates on hard labels, but a conservative implementation can operate on lower-confidence source sets: if the classifier cannot rule out a lower-trust class, the inscription path should default downward rather than promote. "Source downgrading is sound conditional on labels, while source inference is empirical and fallible."

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §6.3 (Limits)
soft-label conservatism: default downward under classifier uncertainty the rule operates on hard labels, but a conservative implementation can operate on lower-confidence source *sets*: if the classifier cannot rule out a lower-trust class, the inscription path should default downward rather than promote. source downgrading is sound conditional on labels, while source inference is empirical and fallible. mechanism paper\source_downgrading.tex §6.3 (limits)

◈ Deliberately conservative vs truth discovery (over-suppression tradeoff)

framing/ontology

The rule bounds a derived claim by the weakest contributor rather than aggregating reliable-majority signals (as truth-discovery systems do). Simpler and deterministic, but an honest external observation composed with a peripheral simulated hypothesis gets sim trust and may be quarantined. "Source downgrading errs on the side of trust-degradation; whether the degradation is warranted in any given case is a routing question, not an inscription one."

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §6.4; docs\source-downgrading-review.md §4.5
deliberately conservative vs truth discovery (over-suppression tradeoff) the rule bounds a derived claim by the *weakest* contributor rather than aggregating reliable-majority signals (as truth-discovery systems do). simpler and deterministic, but an honest external observation composed with a peripheral simulated hypothesis gets `sim` trust and may be quarantined. source downgrading errs on the side of trust-degradation; whether the degradation is warranted in any given case is a routing question, not an inscription one. framing/ontology paper\source_downgrading.tex §6.4; docs\source-downgrading-review.md §4.5

◈ "The API is the intervention, not a new architecture"

framing/ontology

The paper's self-characterization of what kind of contribution this is: small, falsifiable, composable — a constraint on what an existing architecture's inscription path must compute, not a new architecture.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §7; docs\research-conclusions.md
"It does not require a new architecture; it constrains what an existing architecture's inscription path must compute."
the api is the intervention, not a new architecture the paper's self-characterization of what kind of contribution this is: small, falsifiable, composable — a constraint on what an existing architecture's inscription path must compute, not a new architecture. framing/ontology paper\source_downgrading.tex §7; docs\research-conclusions.md it does not require a new architecture; it constrains what an existing architecture's inscription path must compute.

◈ Static writeback baseline positioning

framing/ontology

Post-review positioning: the paper presents itself as "a static writeback baseline for later recursive-memory trust mechanisms" (dynamic credibility, cross-agent transport) — the invariant must be proven first "because every richer policy below assumes it."

CLAIMS_MATRIX.md C20; paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §8 preamble
static writeback baseline positioning post-review positioning: the paper presents itself as a static writeback baseline for later recursive-memory trust mechanisms (dynamic credibility, cross-agent transport) — the invariant must be proven first because every richer policy below assumes it. framing/ontology claims_matrix.md c20; paper\source_downgrading.tex §8 preamble

Validation harnesses and benchmarks

◈ Five-case deterministic conformance fixture

experiment/benchmark

Five cases producing seven derived records: pure inference from observations (expect infer), laundering from simulation (expect sim), laundering from fabrication (expect fab), chained inference from observation (expect infer + provenance walk-back), mixed chain sim-then-inference (expect sim). Each case pairs a later query with an expected routing verdict (trusted vs quarantine). Post-review reframed as a "conformance/property harness," not a broad empirical benchmark.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §4.5.1; CLAIMS_MATRIX.md revision log
five-case deterministic conformance fixture five cases producing seven derived records: pure inference from observations (expect infer), laundering from simulation (expect sim), laundering from fabrication (expect fab), chained inference from observation (expect infer + provenance walk-back), mixed chain sim-then-inference (expect sim). each case pairs a later query with an expected routing verdict (trusted vs quarantine). post-review reframed as a conformance/property harness, not a broad empirical benchmark. experiment/benchmark paper\source_downgrading.tex §4.5.1; claims_matrix.md revision log

◈ Three-policy comparison design (naive / provenance-only / source-downgrading)

experiment/benchmark

The experimental scaffold that isolates the inscription policy: naive_inscribe (default external, no provenance), provenance_propagating (label everything infer, propagate lineage), source_downgrading (full rule). Only the inscription policy varies; agent infrastructure is shared.

src\fgm\laundering.py via docs\architecture\SOURCE_INFERENCE_AND_LAUNDERING.md
three-policy comparison design (naive / provenance-only / source-downgrading) the experimental scaffold that isolates the inscription policy: naive_inscribe (default external, no provenance), provenance_propagating (label everything infer, propagate lineage), source_downgrading (full rule). only the inscription policy varies; agent infrastructure is shared. experiment/benchmark src\fgm\laundering.py via docs\architecture\source_inference_and_laundering.md

◈ Five-metric battery for laundering

experiment/benchmark

Per-policy metrics: inference laundering rate (local), provenance chain recall, false externalization after inference (does a later query route a quarantine-worthy derived claim to a trusted target?), derived trust-ceiling violation rate (truth-grounded), transitive provenance depth mean. The false-externalization metric is the one that connects inscription to downstream routing behavior.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §4.5.3; SOURCE_INFERENCE_AND_LAUNDERING.md
five-metric battery for laundering per-policy metrics: inference laundering rate (local), provenance chain recall, false externalization after inference (does a later query route a quarantine-worthy derived claim to a trusted target?), derived trust-ceiling violation rate (truth-grounded), transitive provenance depth mean. the false-externalization metric is the one that connects inscription to downstream routing behavior. experiment/benchmark paper\source_downgrading.tex §4.5.3; source_inference_and_laundering.md

◈ Seed-0 headline numbers

result/finding

naive_inscribe: local 0.43 / prov-recall 0.00 / false-ext 0.40 / truth 1.00 / depth 0.00. provenance_propagating: 0.00 / 1.00 / 0.40 / 0.57 / 2.29. source_downgrading: 0.00 / 1.00 / 0.00 / 0.00 / 2.29. The 0.57 for provenance-only corresponds exactly to the 4-of-7 derived records with sim/fab origins.

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex Table 1; results\architecture\laundering_validation_summary.json
seed-0 headline numbers naive_inscribe: local 0.43 / prov-recall 0.00 / false-ext 0.40 / truth 1.00 / depth 0.00. provenance_propagating: 0.00 / 1.00 / 0.40 / 0.57 / 2.29. source_downgrading: 0.00 / 1.00 / 0.00 / 0.00 / 2.29. the 0.57 for provenance-only corresponds exactly to the 4-of-7 derived records with sim/fab origins. result/finding paper\source_downgrading.tex table 1; results\architecture\laundering_validation_summary.json

◈ 20-seed embedding-noise sweep (deterministic noise)

experiment/benchmark

Seeds 1–19 apply reproducible Gaussian noise (σ=0.05) to embeddings; each seed–text pair gets deterministic noise so the sweep itself is reproducible. Source downgrading: 0.00 mean AND 0.00 std on all failure metrics. Interpretive honesty: most metrics have zero variance because they are label arithmetic, not retrieval; the only retrieval-dependent metric is false externalization (varies 0.52±0.117 for laundering policies because the later query sometimes fails to retrieve the laundered record at all).

paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §4.5.5; CLAIMS_MATRIX.md C9
