The honest decomposition of what shipped: (1) the inscription rule — the paper's contribution, validated by adversarial-reload v2; (2) the LLM source classifier — a prerequisite validated separately by PoisonedRAG as a classifier; (3) the label-aware retrieval prompt template — works on single-shot RAG, "pulls no additional weight on derivation chains." The paper covers exactly one of the three — "the load-bearing claim under derivation."

docs\research-conclusions.md ("Honest framing of what was shipped")
three components, two papers, one library the honest decomposition of what shipped: (1) the inscription rule — the paper's contribution, validated by adversarial-reload v2; (2) the llm source classifier — a prerequisite validated separately by poisonedrag *as a classifier*; (3) the label-aware retrieval prompt template — works on single-shot rag, pulls no additional weight on derivation chains. the paper covers exactly one of the three — the load-bearing claim under derivation. framing/ontology docs\research-conclusions.md ( honest framing of what was shipped )

◈ Single-shot vs derivation-chain as the regime boundary

framing/ontology

The boundary that decides whether the rule even fires: on single-shot RAG the architecture "collapses to cosine retrieval + classifier-labelled prompt template" (exactly what vector_with_labels also implements — hence the tie); the rule does work only when derived records are inscribed. "PoisonedRAG measures the label-inference layer. Adversarial-reload v2 measures the inscription rule."

docs\research-conclusions.md
single-shot vs derivation-chain as the regime boundary the boundary that decides whether the rule even fires: on single-shot rag the architecture collapses to cosine retrieval + classifier-labelled prompt template (exactly what vector_with_labels also implements — hence the tie); the rule does work only when derived records are inscribed. poisonedrag measures the label-inference layer. adversarial-reload v2 measures the inscription rule. framing/ontology docs\research-conclusions.md

◈ The classifier is the architectural bottleneck on open-domain RAG

result/finding

With a 71%-recall classifier, ~29% of attack passages enter with a trusted label and the framework's safety floor is whatever the LLM does with them; "improving the classifier is the most direct way to push attack-success rate lower." The rule is "no stronger than the weakest source-inference decision upstream."

docs\research-conclusions.md; paper\Source_Downgrading.tex §2
the classifier is the architectural bottleneck on open-domain rag with a 71%-recall classifier, ~29% of attack passages enter with a trusted label and the framework's safety floor is whatever the llm does with them; improving the classifier is the most direct way to push attack-success rate lower. the rule is no stronger than the weakest source-inference decision upstream. result/finding docs\research-conclusions.md; paper\source_downgrading.tex §2

◈ Complementary-not-contradictory result framing

framing/ontology

The narrative move of presenting a 0% internal result and a null external result as mutually reinforcing rather than in tension: "These results are not contradictory; they are complementary" — because each benchmark exercises a different layer by design.

docs\research-conclusions.md Summary
complementary-not-contradictory result framing the narrative move of presenting a 0% internal result and a null external result as mutually reinforcing rather than in tension: these results are not contradictory; they are complementary — because each benchmark exercises a different layer by design. framing/ontology docs\research-conclusions.md summary

◈ Chain-depth transitivity as validation of Proposition 1

result/finding

Depth-2 and depth-3 derivation chains (one derivation feeding the next) maintain 0% laundering — read as empirical confirmation that "the trust-ceiling property is preserved transitively, as Proposition 1 predicts."

docs\research-conclusions.md ("What this validates")
chain-depth transitivity as validation of proposition 1 depth-2 and depth-3 derivation chains (one derivation feeding the next) maintain 0% laundering — read as empirical confirmation that the trust-ceiling property is preserved transitively, as proposition 1 predicts. result/finding docs\research-conclusions.md ( what this validates )

Library / product ideas

◈ trace-memory: promise-only-what's-validated library design

product

A Python memory layer whose entire product identity is that every promised behavior has a validation result file and a CI test behind it. Pitch: "Your agent should be able to remember things, derive new things from them, and trust the derived things less than the originals." Anti-positioning: not a RAG framework, not a vector DB, not a chat-memory wrapper, not a managed service.

docs\trace-memory-library.md; docs\historical\trace-memory-PRD.md
"The promise is small, and the promise is true."
trace-memory: promise-only-what's-validated library design a python memory layer whose entire product identity is that every promised behavior has a validation result file and a ci test behind it. pitch: your agent should be able to remember things, derive new things from them, and trust the derived things less than the originals. anti-positioning: not a rag framework, not a vector db, not a chat-memory wrapper, not a managed service. product docs\trace-memory-library.md; docs\historical\trace-memory-prd.md the promise is small, and the promise is true.

◈ Anti-laundering in ten lines (quickstart as thesis)

product

The quickstart demonstrates the whole thesis in ten lines: plant an observation and a fabricated rumor, derive a claim from both, and print the derived record's computed label (fabricated_or_uncertain) and provenance — then show the self-audit refusing to declare cleanliness without truth labels.

docs\trace-memory-library.md quickstart
anti-laundering in ten lines (quickstart as thesis) the quickstart demonstrates the whole thesis in ten lines: plant an observation and a fabricated rumor, derive a claim from both, and print the derived record's computed label (fabricated_or_uncertain) and provenance — then show the self-audit refusing to declare cleanliness without truth labels. product docs\trace-memory-library.md quickstart

◈ Defensive-by-default quarantine routing

mechanism

The routing layer quarantines any retrieval set containing an untrusted source (simulation, fabricated) — deliberately over-conservative, with documented escape hatches (retrieval_k=1 or pre-filter by source) rather than a softer default.

docs\trace-memory-library.md §4
defensive-by-default quarantine routing the routing layer quarantines any retrieval set containing an untrusted source (simulation, fabricated) — deliberately over-conservative, with documented escape hatches (retrieval_k=1 or pre-filter by source) rather than a softer default. mechanism docs\trace-memory-library.md §4

◈ revise_belief and the 8-field correction node

mechanism

Belief revisions recorded as full-lineage nodes c = (B_prev, evidence, update_operation, B_revised, Δ, self-index ι, provenance p, confidence q), persisted as OPERATION_RECORD-source records. Validated as a schema (the required information envelope for belief-revision events), explicitly not as long-horizon compounding.

docs\trace-memory-library.md §5; VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §1.7
revise_belief and the 8-field correction node belief revisions recorded as full-lineage nodes c = (b_prev, evidence, update_operation, b_revised, δ, self-index ι, provenance p, confidence q), persisted as operation_record-source records. validated as a *schema* (the required information envelope for belief-revision events), explicitly not as long-horizon compounding. mechanism docs\trace-memory-library.md §5; validated_primitives_ledger.md §1.7

◈ Utility-based inscription under budget (add_candidate / flush_inscriptions)

mechanism

Opt-in budget-constrained writes: candidates queue with predicted-utility scores; flush commits only the top-budget. Validated only at budget=3 in a fixed future-task fixture; utility scoring is fixture-supplied — deriving utility from observed downstream task performance named as a separate problem.

docs\trace-memory-library.md (FR-5); VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §1.5
utility-based inscription under budget (add_candidate / flush_inscriptions) opt-in budget-constrained writes: candidates queue with predicted-utility scores; flush commits only the top-budget. validated only at budget=3 in a fixed future-task fixture; utility scoring is fixture-supplied — deriving utility from observed downstream task performance named as a separate problem. mechanism docs\trace-memory-library.md (fr-5); validated_primitives_ledger.md §1.5

◈ SelfIndex tenant-metadata binding (explicitly not cryptographic)

mechanism

Records carry (user_id, project_id, role, permission_scope, standing_commitment); retrieval filters by the agent's active index; switching the active index changes what's retrievable. standing_commitment is content, not a filter. Repeatedly and loudly documented as NOT cryptographic tenant isolation.

docs\trace-memory-library.md (FR-8); VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §1.8
selfindex tenant-metadata binding (explicitly not cryptographic) records carry (user_id, project_id, role, permission_scope, standing_commitment); retrieval filters by the agent's active index; switching the active index changes what's retrievable. standing_commitment is content, not a filter. repeatedly and loudly documented as not cryptographic tenant isolation. mechanism docs\trace-memory-library.md (fr-8); validated_primitives_ledger.md §1.8

◈ Source(.) policy ladder including a guarded trained transformer

mechanism

Seven inference policies: uniform_external (baseline), lexical_rules, feature_threshold, combined (default), trained_transformer_raw, guarded trained_transformer, and llm. The DistilBERT CPU checkpoint reaches raw 1.0 on generated held-out data but only 0.983 on the authored challenge set, so the deployable policy wraps explicit boundary guards around the model; the guarded policy hits 1.0 / zero false-externalization / zero trust-upgrades on both. Promotion of the unguarded checkpoint gated on independently annotated data — "perfect scores should be read as successful pipeline validation... not proof of open-domain source grounding."

docs\trace-memory-library.md (FR-6, v0.8); VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §1.6
source(.) policy ladder including a guarded trained transformer seven inference policies: uniform_external (baseline), lexical_rules, feature_threshold, combined (default), trained_transformer_raw, guarded trained_transformer, and llm. the distilbert cpu checkpoint reaches raw 1.0 on generated held-out data but only 0.983 on the authored challenge set, so the deployable policy wraps *explicit boundary guards around the model*; the guarded policy hits 1.0 / zero false-externalization / zero trust-upgrades on both. promotion of the unguarded checkpoint gated on independently annotated data — perfect scores should be read as successful pipeline validation... not proof of open-domain source grounding. mechanism docs\trace-memory-library.md (fr-6, v0.8); validated_primitives_ledger.md §1.6

◈ Pluggable LLM classifier via set_llm_classifier

mechanism

policy="llm" routes classification through any callable matching Callable[[str], SourceLabel] — wraps any OpenAI-compatible client, no hard dependency on openai, with an in-instance cache to amortize cost; the bundled system prompt is the same one validated on PoisonedRAG and is overridable per-domain.

docs\trace-memory-library.md (v0.8.0a0)
pluggable llm classifier via set_llm_classifier policy= llm routes classification through any callable matching callable[[str], sourcelabel] — wraps any openai-compatible client, no hard dependency on openai, with an in-instance cache to amortize cost; the bundled system prompt is the same one validated on poisonedrag and is overridable per-domain. mechanism docs\trace-memory-library.md (v0.8.0a0)

◈ Five-method Storage protocol + SQLite persistence

mechanism

Storage as a typing.Protocol with save/load_all/delete/contains/close; InMemoryStorage and SQLiteStorage reference implementations; derived-flag round-trips so audit_laundering works after process restart; vectors serialized with np.save; documented v0.3 limits (single-process last-write-wins, in-memory read path, ~10k ceiling).

docs\trace-memory-library.md (Phase 3)
five-method storage protocol + sqlite persistence storage as a typing.protocol with save/load_all/delete/contains/close; inmemorystorage and sqlitestorage reference implementations; derived-flag round-trips so audit_laundering works after process restart; vectors serialized with np.save; documented v0.3 limits (single-process last-write-wins, in-memory read path, ~10k ceiling). mechanism docs\trace-memory-library.md (phase 3)

◈ Async-compatible-not-natively-async concurrency model

mechanism

Every mutating/read method has an aX wrapper via asyncio.to_thread; mutations serialize through an RLock; reads unlocked but GIL-consistent; explicitly documented that coroutines gain no CPU parallelism ("the async surface is valuable for boundary symmetry, not for CPU-bound throughput") — run multiple agents for true parallelism.

docs\trace-memory-library.md (v0.4 Async API)
async-compatible-not-natively-async concurrency model every mutating/read method has an ax wrapper via asyncio.to_thread; mutations serialize through an rlock; reads unlocked but gil-consistent; explicitly documented that coroutines gain no cpu parallelism ( the async surface is valuable for boundary symmetry, not for cpu-bound throughput ) — run multiple agents for true parallelism. mechanism docs\trace-memory-library.md (v0.4 async api)

◈ Bulk ingestion: app-owned parsing vs library-owned StructuredEnvelope

mechanism

Two ingestion patterns: (1) app-owned — the library accepts typed ObservationRequest/DerivationRequest/RevisionRequest objects and imposes no JSON schema on the LLM prompt; (2) library-owned — a canonical StructuredEnvelope whose system-prompt block embeds the trust ordering and instructs the model to "prefer the lowest-trust source that honestly applies," with parsing that accepts both JSON and a line-marker fallback (OBSERVED:/TOOL:/INFERRED:/SIMULATED:/FABRICATED:/RETRIEVED:) when the LLM ignores the JSON instruction. Trust composition holds in both because DerivationRequests route through add_derived.

docs\trace-memory-library.md (v0.5 Bulk ingestion)
bulk ingestion: app-owned parsing vs library-owned structuredenvelope two ingestion patterns: (1) app-owned — the library accepts typed observationrequest/derivationrequest/revisionrequest objects and imposes no json schema on the llm prompt; (2) library-owned — a canonical structuredenvelope whose system-prompt block embeds the trust ordering and instructs the model to prefer the lowest-trust source that honestly applies, with parsing that accepts both json and a line-marker fallback (observed:/tool:/inferred:/simulated:/fabricated:/retrieved:) when the llm ignores the json instruction. trust composition holds in both because derivationrequests route through add_derived. mechanism docs\trace-memory-library.md (v0.5 bulk ingestion)

◈ Honest-prompting rule: inferences belong in derivations

mechanism

The envelope's prompt-level encoding of the invariant: inferences go in the derivations list with contributing input record IDs, "not in observations with source=external" — pushing the anti-laundering discipline up into the LLM's output format itself.

docs\trace-memory-library.md (Pattern 2)
honest-prompting rule: inferences belong in derivations the envelope's prompt-level encoding of the invariant: inferences go in the derivations list with contributing input record ids, not in observations with source=external — pushing the anti-laundering discipline up into the llm's output format itself. mechanism docs\trace-memory-library.md (pattern 2)

◈ "What this library does NOT promise" as a first-class doc section

product

A negative-guarantees list reproduced from the ledger into user docs: no retrieval guarantees beyond ~10k records, no compression/forgetting (vault grows monotonically), no natural-prose Source(.) guarantees, no open-ended long-horizon stability, no active recall (never surfaces memories without a query), no cryptographic isolation, no adversarial robustness.

docs\trace-memory-library.md; docs\historical\trace-memory-PRD.md §7
what this library does not promise as a first-class doc section a negative-guarantees list reproduced from the ledger into user docs: no retrieval guarantees beyond ~10k records, no compression/forgetting (vault grows monotonically), no natural-prose source(.) guarantees, no open-ended long-horizon stability, no active recall (never surfaces memories without a query), no cryptographic isolation, no adversarial robustness. product docs\trace-memory-library.md; docs\historical\trace-memory-prd.md §7

◈ PRD success metric: one external user

product

The v0.1 success bar deliberately includes "At least one developer outside Tyxter installs the library, reports a use case, and provides feedback. This can be a colleague or collaborator; it is not 'thousands of downloads.'" — a minimum-viable-adoption criterion.

docs\historical\trace-memory-PRD.md §8
prd success metric: one external user the v0.1 success bar deliberately includes at least one developer outside tyxter installs the library, reports a use case, and provides feedback. this can be a colleague or collaborator; it is not 'thousands of downloads.' — a minimum-viable-adoption criterion. product docs\historical\trace-memory-prd.md §8

◈ PRD risk register (R-1..R-6)

product

Named product risks with likelihood/impact/mitigation: natural-prose classifier underperformance (high), fold-force ablation latency with LLM transitions (mitigate via threshold short-circuit + cheap proxy transitions at retrieval, full at decision), self-index misuse leaking data, unbounded in-memory growth, pre-1.0 API churn, and "the memory-layer framing is unfamiliar to RAG-trained developers" (they expect a RAG library and bounce).

docs\historical\trace-memory-PRD.md §9
prd risk register (r-1..r-6) named product risks with likelihood/impact/mitigation: natural-prose classifier underperformance (high), fold-force ablation latency with llm transitions (mitigate via threshold short-circuit + cheap proxy transitions at retrieval, full at decision), self-index misuse leaking data, unbounded in-memory growth, pre-1.0 api churn, and the memory-layer framing is unfamiliar to rag-trained developers (they expect a rag library and bounce). product docs\historical\trace-memory-prd.md §9

◈ Vendor-vs-depend decision for validated primitives

open-question

Open product question: should the product repo depend on the research repo as a library (single source of truth, free upstream upgrades) or vendor the modules (decoupled release cadence)? Recommendation: depend first, reassess at v0.2 — a general pattern for productizing validated research code.

docs\historical\trace-memory-PRD.md §12
vendor-vs-depend decision for validated primitives open product question: should the product repo depend on the research repo as a library (single source of truth, free upstream upgrades) or vendor the modules (decoupled release cadence)? recommendation: depend first, reassess at v0.2 — a general pattern for productizing validated research code. open-question docs\historical\trace-memory-prd.md §12

Validated-primitives ledger and validation program

◈ Three-tier evidence ledger with promotion/demotion rules

mechanism

A single source-of-truth document sorting every claim into §1 Validated Primitives (fit for product promises), §2 Partial Grounding (internal, scoped use with documented limits), §3 Theoretical Claims (design language only, never load-bearing). Governance rules: marketing may cite only §1; each entry has a grounding commit SHA and last-review date; regressions demote tiers; entries are never deleted, only moved to a Deprecated section.

docs\architecture\VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md
three-tier evidence ledger with promotion/demotion rules a single source-of-truth document sorting every claim into §1 validated primitives (fit for product promises), §2 partial grounding (internal, scoped use with documented limits), §3 theoretical claims (design language only, never load-bearing). governance rules: marketing may cite only §1; each entry has a grounding commit sha and last-review date; regressions demote tiers; entries are never deleted, only moved to a deprecated section. mechanism docs\architecture\validated_primitives_ledger.md

◈ "Publication is not validation" anti-pattern

framing/ontology

Explicit ledger anti-patterns: do not promote an entry because the underlying paper was published ("Publication is not validation. Only result files plus passing tests promote an entry"); do not soften a verdict to make a product feature look stronger; re-read the ledger before any external commitment.

docs\architecture\VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §4
publication is not validation anti-pattern explicit ledger anti-patterns: do not promote an entry because the underlying *paper* was published ( publication is not validation. only result files plus passing tests promote an entry ); do not soften a verdict to make a product feature look stronger; re-read the ledger before any external commitment. framing/ontology docs\architecture\validated_primitives_ledger.md §4

◈ Ledger §1: nine validated primitives

result/finding

The validated floor: (1.1) source-label preservation through the full pipeline (accuracy 1.0 across toy/real-embedding/live substrates); (1.2) fold-force as the operational memory criterion; (1.3) source-sensitive routing (route accuracy 1.0 vs source-blind echo-promotion 1.0); (1.4) source downgrading + provenance propagation; (1.5) inscription utility under budget; (1.6) Source(.) inference feasibility floor; (1.7) correction-chain schema; (1.8) engineered self-index binding; (1.9) cascade-invisibility audit constraint as a methodological primitive ("any audit dashboard that reports laundering safety using only the system's own stored labels gives a false floor").

docs\architecture\VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §1
ledger §1: nine validated primitives the validated floor: (1.1) source-label preservation through the full pipeline (accuracy 1.0 across toy/real-embedding/live substrates); (1.2) fold-force as the operational memory criterion; (1.3) source-sensitive routing (route accuracy 1.0 vs source-blind echo-promotion 1.0); (1.4) source downgrading + provenance propagation; (1.5) inscription utility under budget; (1.6) source(.) inference feasibility floor; (1.7) correction-chain schema; (1.8) engineered self-index binding; (1.9) cascade-invisibility audit constraint as a *methodological* primitive ( any audit dashboard that reports laundering safety using only the system's own stored labels gives a false floor ). result/finding docs\architecture\validated_primitives_ledger.md §1

◈ Echo promotion as the routing failure metric

result/finding

The source-blind baseline's characteristic failure: echo_promotion_rate = 1.0 (internally reactivated/echoed content promoted to durable memory as if evidence) versus 0.0 for source-sensitive routing — echo amplification as a measurable quantity.

docs\architecture\VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §1.3; VALIDATION_ROADMAP_EXECUTION.md
echo promotion as the routing failure metric the source-blind baseline's characteristic failure: echo_promotion_rate = 1.0 (internally reactivated/echoed content promoted to durable memory as if evidence) versus 0.0 for source-sensitive routing — echo amplification as a measurable quantity. result/finding docs\architecture\validated_primitives_ledger.md §1.3; validation_roadmap_execution.md

◈ Ledger §2 scoped entries (partial grounding)

result/finding

Five partially grounded items: trace retention probes (decay constants fixture-determined); coupled-field dynamics (memory-to-attention shift 0.136 source-aware vs 0.711 source-blind — existence grounded, magnitude/stability not; MAFC spectral bounds derived but unmeasured); source-aware retrieval reranking (one boundary case fixed); source-aware residual attention (k=3 fixture too small for continuous claims); provider-empty vs primitive-failure separation (engineering pattern, not a memory primitive).

docs\architecture\VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §2
ledger §2 scoped entries (partial grounding) five partially grounded items: trace retention probes (decay constants fixture-determined); coupled-field dynamics (memory-to-attention shift 0.136 source-aware vs 0.711 source-blind — existence grounded, magnitude/stability not; mafc spectral bounds derived but unmeasured); source-aware retrieval reranking (one boundary case fixed); source-aware residual attention (k=3 fixture too small for continuous claims); provider-empty vs primitive-failure separation (engineering pattern, not a memory primitive). result/finding docs\architecture\validated_primitives_ledger.md §2

◈ Ledger §3 theoretical claims held un-load-bearing

framing/ontology

Seven prior-paper claims explicitly quarantined as design language: SIMFC emergent self-indexing stability; AINC continuity-architecture predictions; MAFC v3 spectral-radius lock-in/novelty-breakthrough thresholds; the Maxwell-like attention-memory field analogy ("not falsifiable as currently stated. Useful as a design metaphor; not a contract"); SIMReC recursive self-indexing as observed phenomenon; correction-chain long-horizon compounding rates; Trace decay constants/half-lives.

docs\architecture\VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §3
ledger §3 theoretical claims held un-load-bearing seven prior-paper claims explicitly quarantined as design language: simfc emergent self-indexing stability; ainc continuity-architecture predictions; mafc v3 spectral-radius lock-in/novelty-breakthrough thresholds; the maxwell-like attention-memory field analogy ( not falsifiable as currently stated. useful as a design metaphor; not a contract ); simrec recursive self-indexing as observed phenomenon; correction-chain long-horizon compounding rates; trace decay constants/half-lives. framing/ontology docs\architecture\validated_primitives_ledger.md §3

◈ SIMReC promotion path: the unattempted centerpiece

open-question

The stated path from theory to product for recursive self-indexing: "Build a long-horizon agent run where self-pointers form, measure trust degradation along the recursion, verify monotone decay. This is the SIMReC product's empirical centerpiece — and not yet attempted." Source downgrading (§1.4) supplies the trust bound; fold-force (§1.2) supplies the memory criterion.

docs\architecture\VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §3.5
simrec promotion path: the unattempted centerpiece the stated path from theory to product for recursive self-indexing: build a long-horizon agent run where self-pointers form, measure trust degradation along the recursion, verify monotone decay. this is the simrec product's empirical centerpiece — and not yet attempted. source downgrading (§1.4) supplies the trust bound; fold-force (§1.2) supplies the memory criterion. open-question docs\architecture\validated_primitives_ledger.md §3.5

◈ Four-way grounding rule for calling a primitive validated

mechanism

"Do not validate primitives by naming them." A primitive is provisionally grounded only if: the experiment creates the ground-truth label directly; a paired intervention changes only the primitive under test; future-task utility distinguishes positive/negative conditions; or a no-memory/source-blind/route-randomized baseline fails where the primitive succeeds.

docs\architecture\ROADMAP_ARCHIVE_2026-05-11.md (Validation Rule)
four-way grounding rule for calling a primitive validated do not validate primitives by naming them. a primitive is provisionally grounded only if: the experiment creates the ground-truth label directly; a paired intervention changes only the primitive under test; future-task utility distinguishes positive/negative conditions; or a no-memory/source-blind/route-randomized baseline fails where the primitive succeeds. mechanism docs\architecture\roadmap_archive_2026-05-11.md (validation rule)

◈ "From assumed sources to grounded sources" roadmap pivot

framing/ontology

The mid-project reframe after discovering that all early validation supplied source labels as fixture truth: the replacement roadmap separates three layers — (1) fixture-created ground truth, (2) source inference from content/context/features, (3) trust-preserving inscription for derived records — and inverts the build order: "The old order started with supplied source labels. The new order starts with earning and preserving source labels."

docs\architecture\ROADMAP.md
from assumed sources to grounded sources roadmap pivot the mid-project reframe after discovering that all early validation *supplied* source labels as fixture truth: the replacement roadmap separates three layers — (1) fixture-created ground truth, (2) source inference from content/context/features, (3) trust-preserving inscription for derived records — and inverts the build order: the old order started with supplied source labels. the new order starts with earning and preserving source labels. framing/ontology docs\architecture\roadmap.md

◈ Ten "ground truths reached" enumeration

result/finding

A numbered list of exactly which claims have direct controlled evidence (label preservation, source-sensitive routing beats blind, memory = nonzero paired-transition effect, utility inscription beats degenerate policies under budget, source downgrading required for safe derived inscription, provenance necessary-not-sufficient, local audit undercounts, source inference feasible but features-alone insufficient, provider-empty is a separate failure plane, source-aware reranking fixes a polarity boundary) — paired with an equally explicit "not yet ground truths" list.

docs\architecture\ROADMAP.md (Ground Truths Reached)
ten ground truths reached enumeration a numbered list of exactly which claims have direct controlled evidence (label preservation, source-sensitive routing beats blind, memory = nonzero paired-transition effect, utility inscription beats degenerate policies under budget, source downgrading required for safe derived inscription, provenance necessary-not-sufficient, local audit undercounts, source inference feasible but features-alone insufficient, provider-empty is a separate failure plane, source-aware reranking fixes a polarity boundary) — paired with an equally explicit not yet ground truths list. result/finding docs\architecture\roadmap.md (ground truths reached)

◈ Roadmap non-goals: the forbidden-claims list

framing/ontology

The evidence must not be used to claim: emergent selfhood, global cognitive continuity, consciousness-adjacent behavior, open-ended autobiographical memory, general real-world source inference, model-independent live robustness. "The foundation is stronger now, but it is still a primitive validation program."

docs\architecture\ROADMAP.md (Non-Goals); ROADMAP_ARCHIVE_2026-05-11.md
roadmap non-goals: the forbidden-claims list the evidence must not be used to claim: emergent selfhood, global cognitive continuity, consciousness-adjacent behavior, open-ended autobiographical memory, general real-world source inference, model-independent live robustness. the foundation is stronger now, but it is still a primitive validation program. framing/ontology docs\architecture\roadmap.md (non-goals); roadmap_archive_2026-05-11.md

◈ Phase plan 2–7: hardening ladder under source uncertainty

open-question

Forward phases: harden Source(.) (adversarial marker-vs-feature-conflict cases, confidence outputs, confusion-focused metrics); integrate anti-laundering into the agent write path (reject/quarantine derived writes with missing provenance); re-test routing/fold with inferred rather than supplied labels within a documented degradation bound; claim-scale (N=20) task families; first hand-labeled semi-real source corpus (rule-based baseline before any learned policy); only then revisit residual attention / self-index / coupled fields under inferred-source uncertainty ("any coupled-field claim includes a failure mode showing excessive coupling or echo amplification").

docs\architecture\ROADMAP.md Phases 2-7
phase plan 2–7: hardening ladder under source uncertainty forward phases: harden source(.) (adversarial marker-vs-feature-conflict cases, confidence outputs, confusion-focused metrics); integrate anti-laundering into the agent write path (reject/quarantine derived writes with missing provenance); re-test routing/fold with *inferred* rather than supplied labels within a documented degradation bound; claim-scale (n=20) task families; first hand-labeled semi-real source corpus (rule-based baseline before any learned policy); only then revisit residual attention / self-index / coupled fields under inferred-source uncertainty ( any coupled-field claim includes a failure mode showing excessive coupling or echo amplification ). open-question docs\architecture\roadmap.md phases 2-7

◈ Statistical standards charter

mechanism

Codified rigor floor: toy/deterministic N≥50 seeds with effect direction holding in ≥90%; real embeddings N≥20 with CIs; live LLM temperature 0, N≥5 smoke / N≥20 claims, always paired no-memory and topic-only baselines, cost ledger mandatory, raw prompts/responses stored. Metric rules: prefer baseline-subtracted memory contribution over raw similarity; avoid metrics that are direct functions of the mechanism under test; score from logs not internal objects; separate sparse/rich record regimes.

docs\architecture\ROADMAP_ARCHIVE_2026-05-11.md (Statistical Standards)
statistical standards charter codified rigor floor: toy/deterministic n≥50 seeds with effect direction holding in ≥90%; real embeddings n≥20 with cis; live llm temperature 0, n≥5 smoke / n≥20 claims, always paired no-memory and topic-only baselines, cost ledger mandatory, raw prompts/responses stored. metric rules: prefer baseline-subtracted memory contribution over raw similarity; avoid metrics that are direct functions of the mechanism under test; score from logs not internal objects; separate sparse/rich record regimes. mechanism docs\architecture\roadmap_archive_2026-05-11.md (statistical standards)

◈ ValidationRecord: score-from-logs instrumentation

mechanism

Phase-0 idea: a shared 20-field JSONL validation record (run_id, seed, query, retrieved_ids, source_labels, route_scores, selected_route, output_with/without_memory, transition_delta, predicted/realized fold force, operation/correction ids, future-task score...) so every experiment can be re-scored from logs without rerunning the agent.

docs\architecture\ROADMAP_ARCHIVE_2026-05-11.md Phase 0
validationrecord: score-from-logs instrumentation phase-0 idea: a shared 20-field jsonl validation record (run_id, seed, query, retrieved_ids, source_labels, route_scores, selected_route, output_with/without_memory, transition_delta, predicted/realized fold force, operation/correction ids, future-task score...) so every experiment can be re-scored from logs without rerunning the agent. mechanism docs\architecture\roadmap_archive_2026-05-11.md phase 0

◈ First concrete build target (E1/R1/S1/F1)

experiment/benchmark

The minimal all-in-one experiment: given an external observation E1, retrieved memory R1, simulated hypothesis S1, fabricated distractor F1, answer a later query that requires E1+R1, rejects F1, and may mention S1 only as hypothetical — grounding source, fold-force, inscription, routing, and operation-memory in one controlled harness.

docs\architecture\ROADMAP_ARCHIVE_2026-05-11.md (First Concrete Build Target)
first concrete build target (e1/r1/s1/f1) the minimal all-in-one experiment: given an external observation e1, retrieved memory r1, simulated hypothesis s1, fabricated distractor f1, answer a later query that requires e1+r1, rejects f1, and may mention s1 only as hypothetical — grounding source, fold-force, inscription, routing, and operation-memory in one controlled harness. experiment/benchmark docs\architecture\roadmap_archive_2026-05-11.md (first concrete build target)

◈ Provider-empty vs primitive-failure diagnostic separation

result/finding

Live-LLM engineering finding: empty provider responses create zero-fold-force route misses even when retrieval is correct, and must be tracked as provider-output validity failures, not primitive failures. Route gates reported both raw and "provider-valid." With retries enabled, N=20 gates all reach 1.0 with zero final empty with-memory responses.

docs\architecture\VALIDATION_ROADMAP_EXECUTION.md; VALIDATED_PRIMITIVES_LEDGER.md §2.5
provider-empty vs primitive-failure diagnostic separation live-llm engineering finding: empty provider responses create zero-fold-force route misses even when retrieval is correct, and must be tracked as provider-output validity failures, not primitive failures. route gates reported both raw and provider-valid. with retries enabled, n=20 gates all reach 1.0 with zero final empty with-memory responses. result/finding docs\architecture\validation_roadmap_execution.md; validated_primitives_ledger.md §2.5

◈ gpt-5-nano output-budget boundary and fix

result/finding

A concrete cross-model boundary: gpt-5-nano failed the live matrix with with_memory_output_validity_rate = 0.0 purely from output-token budget; rerunning with max_output_tokens=1000 passed cleanly (30,454 output tokens for 44 calls). Failure attribution to a provider knob, not the memory primitives.

docs\architecture\VALIDATION_ROADMAP_EXECUTION.md
gpt-5-nano output-budget boundary and fix a concrete cross-model boundary: gpt-5-nano failed the live matrix with with_memory_output_validity_rate = 0.0 purely from output-token budget; rerunning with max_output_tokens=1000 passed cleanly (30,454 output tokens for 44 calls). failure attribution to a provider knob, not the memory primitives. result/finding docs\architecture\validation_roadmap_execution.md

◈ Cross-provider matrix and task families

experiment/benchmark

Provider/model comparison harness across OpenAI (gpt-5-mini, gpt-5-nano, gpt-4.1-mini) and Anthropic (claude-haiku-4-5): 3/4 targets pass with zero primitive failures; plus two non-default fixed-truth task families (billing_refund, security_rotation) passing N=5 live with zero boundary failures — smoke-level, explicitly not claim-scale.

docs\architecture\VALIDATION_ROADMAP_EXECUTION.md
cross-provider matrix and task families provider/model comparison harness across openai (gpt-5-mini, gpt-5-nano, gpt-4.1-mini) and anthropic (claude-haiku-4-5): 3/4 targets pass with zero primitive failures; plus two non-default fixed-truth task families (billing_refund, security_rotation) passing n=5 live with zero boundary failures — smoke-level, explicitly not claim-scale. experiment/benchmark docs\architecture\validation_roadmap_execution.md

◈ FGM six-layer memory architecture

mechanism

The host architecture: Layer 1 trace (LLM context, decaying) → 2 storage (vector store with per-record fold-force tracking) → 3 addressability (top-k with margin/confusability monitoring) → 4 folding (dual-pass transition measurement — only memories that change decisions pass) → 5 operation-memory (records of prior fold operations enabling recursive self-correction) → 6 compression (confusability-triggered pruning/merging). FGMAgent ties them into one query cycle: retrieve → fold → measure → gate → record → auto-compress.

docs\architecture\trace-memory-architecture.md
fgm six-layer memory architecture the host architecture: layer 1 trace (llm context, decaying) → 2 storage (vector store with per-record fold-force tracking) → 3 addressability (top-k with margin/confusability monitoring) → 4 folding (dual-pass transition measurement — only memories that change decisions pass) → 5 operation-memory (records of prior fold operations enabling recursive self-correction) → 6 compression (confusability-triggered pruning/merging). fgmagent ties them into one query cycle: retrieve → fold → measure → gate → record → auto-compress. mechanism docs\architecture\trace-memory-architecture.md

◈ Five trace probes mapping paper definitions to executable tests

experiment/benchmark

trace_retention_probe (lag projection retains residue), causal_trace_probe (perturbing prior state changes trace), addressability_probe (top-k selects stored record), fold_force_probe (folded memory changes non-bookkeeping transition), p7_retrieval_regime_probe (phase boundary across four retrieval regimes) — a definitions-to-pytest mapping discipline.

docs\architecture\trace-memory-architecture.md
five trace probes mapping paper definitions to executable tests trace_retention_probe (lag projection retains residue), causal_trace_probe (perturbing prior state changes trace), addressability_probe (top-k selects stored record), fold_force_probe (folded memory changes non-bookkeeping transition), p7_retrieval_regime_probe (phase boundary across four retrieval regimes) — a definitions-to-pytest mapping discipline. experiment/benchmark docs\architecture\trace-memory-architecture.md

◈ FGM key findings carried into this repo

result/finding

Four prior findings restated as architecture ground: P7 phase boundary at N=1000 (rich_distinctive 1.000 hit rate vs sparse_confusable ~0.03); L2 fold-force doesn't discriminate with LLM transitions but answer-quality (cosine-toward-memory) fold-force discriminates at 5.9x; the non-bookkeeping (h_cog) restriction is functionally necessary for signal/noise discrimination; proactive confusability-triggered compression is architecturally necessary — reactive per-record pruning is insufficient.

docs\architecture\trace-memory-architecture.md (Key findings)
fgm key findings carried into this repo four prior findings restated as architecture ground: p7 phase boundary at n=1000 (rich_distinctive 1.000 hit rate vs sparse_confusable ~0.03); l2 fold-force doesn't discriminate with llm transitions but answer-quality (cosine-toward-memory) fold-force discriminates at 5.9x; the non-bookkeeping (h_cog) restriction is functionally necessary for signal/noise discrimination; proactive confusability-triggered compression is architecturally necessary — reactive per-record pruning is insufficient. result/finding docs\architecture\trace-memory-architecture.md (key findings)

◈ Empirical caveats inherited from simrec-reference

framing/ontology

Methodological traps preserved as warnings: P7 overload is conditional on per-record information density, not unconditional; metrics can become tautological if they measure the mechanism instead of behavior; topic-conditioned LLM generation can fake recall (use baseline-subtracted memory contribution); long-horizon fold-force becomes trajectory-confounded (track accumulated operation impact); integration/P4 testing requires edge-aware retrieval first.

docs\architecture\ROADMAP_ARCHIVE_2026-05-11.md (simrec caveats)
empirical caveats inherited from simrec-reference methodological traps preserved as warnings: p7 overload is conditional on per-record information density, not unconditional; metrics can become tautological if they measure the mechanism instead of behavior; topic-conditioned llm generation can fake recall (use baseline-subtracted memory contribution); long-horizon fold-force becomes trajectory-confounded (track accumulated operation impact); integration/p4 testing requires edge-aware retrieval first. framing/ontology docs\architecture\roadmap_archive_2026-05-11.md (simrec caveats)

◈ Branch lineage and MAFC-v4 closure

framing/ontology

The laundering + source-inference harnesses were built on branch source-inference-and-laundering (off validation-primitives, "Codex's primitive-roadmap execution") explicitly to close two flagship open issues in the MAFC v4 revision spec: Source(.) was "given, not earned" (no harness recovered labels from content), and inference laundering was "a flagship prediction without a test." The verdict: source-downgrading should be adopted as the default R[.] rule for the infer route in MAFC v4, and cascade invisibility should be added to v4's failure-modes section as a sub-failure of inference laundering.

docs\architecture\SOURCE_INFERENCE_AND_LAUNDERING.md
branch lineage and mafc-v4 closure the laundering + source-inference harnesses were built on branch source-inference-and-laundering (off validation-primitives, codex's primitive-roadmap execution ) explicitly to close two flagship open issues in the mafc v4 revision spec: source(.) was given, not earned (no harness recovered labels from content), and inference laundering was a flagship prediction without a test. the verdict: source-downgrading should be adopted as the default r[.] rule for the infer route in mafc v4, and cascade invisibility should be added to v4's failure-modes section as a sub-failure of inference laundering. framing/ontology docs\architecture\source_inference_and_laundering.md

Publication process and meta-work

◈ Claims matrix as source-of-truth audit (C1–C21)

mechanism

Every substantive paper claim mapped to a class (formal/theoretical, implementation/API, deterministic conformance, LLM benchmark, negative-control/limitation, future work), evidence paths, and reproduction command/status — 21 rows. Includes a "Claims Revised During This Pass" changelog documenting each softening.

CLAIMS_MATRIX.md
claims matrix as source-of-truth audit (c1–c21) every substantive paper claim mapped to a class (formal/theoretical, implementation/api, deterministic conformance, llm benchmark, negative-control/limitation, future work), evidence paths, and reproduction command/status — 21 rows. includes a claims revised during this pass changelog documenting each softening. mechanism claims_matrix.md

◈ The softening log (wording as versioned artifact)

mechanism

Documented claim rewrites: "architecture prerequisite" → scoped to systems that compose and reinscribe derived records; "constructively defeats" → "rules out writeback inflation"; existing systems not "intrinsically unsafe," only unspecified; fixture reframed as conformance harness; multi-seed evidence reframed as "not a retrieval-order artifact" rather than structural immunity; classifier count corrected 355→356. Treating claim-language changes as first-class, logged engineering changes.

CLAIMS_MATRIX.md (Claims Revised During This Pass)
the softening log (wording as versioned artifact) documented claim rewrites: architecture prerequisite → scoped to systems that compose and reinscribe derived records; constructively defeats → rules out writeback inflation ; existing systems not intrinsically unsafe, only unspecified; fixture reframed as conformance harness; multi-seed evidence reframed as not a retrieval-order artifact rather than structural immunity; classifier count corrected 355→356. treating claim-language changes as first-class, logged engineering changes. mechanism claims_matrix.md (claims revised during this pass)

◈ Reproducibility log records observed outputs, not just commands

mechanism

REPRODUCIBILITY.md pins the environment (Windows PowerShell, Python 3.13.12, pytest 8.4.2, MiKTeX pdfTeX 1.40.28) and pastes actual observed outputs: 374 passed / 4 deselected in 94.14s; 45-test fixture gate in 0.55s; PDF 25 pages, 388,081 bytes; log scan showing one underfull hbox and zero undefined refs/citations/overfull boxes.

REPRODUCIBILITY.md
reproducibility log records observed outputs, not just commands reproducibility.md pins the environment (windows powershell, python 3.13.12, pytest 8.4.2, miktex pdftex 1.40.28) and pastes actual observed outputs: 374 passed / 4 deselected in 94.14s; 45-test fixture gate in 0.55s; pdf 25 pages, 388,081 bytes; log scan showing one underfull hbox and zero undefined refs/citations/overfull boxes. mechanism reproducibility.md

◈ Recompute-headlines-from-committed-JSON verification script

mechanism

Instead of rerunning paid benchmarks, a PowerShell script reads the committed result JSONs and recomputes every headline rate (multiseed means, per-arm strict/defensible/unsafe/contam/clean rates, ASRs, classifier confusion counts) — validating that the paper's numbers derive from the committed evidence. The live/API rerun boundary is explicit: adversarial-reload v2 ~$3/58min and PoisonedRAG ~$0.30/28min are NOT rerun by default; deterministic benchmarks regenerate in <1s with no network.

REPRODUCIBILITY.md
recompute-headlines-from-committed-json verification script instead of rerunning paid benchmarks, a powershell script reads the committed result jsons and *recomputes* every headline rate (multiseed means, per-arm strict/defensible/unsafe/contam/clean rates, asrs, classifier confusion counts) — validating that the paper's numbers derive from the committed evidence. the live/api rerun boundary is explicit: adversarial-reload v2 ~$3/58min and poisonedrag ~$0.30/28min are not rerun by default; deterministic benchmarks regenerate in <1s with no network. mechanism reproducibility.md

◈ Publication checklist with a permanent "Open risks" row

mechanism

An area-by-area pass/open gate (abstract accuracy, novelty framing, related work "pass with residual risk," claim/evidence coverage, reproducibility, limitations, code availability, build artifacts, the source/credibility/confidence boundary, PoisonedRAG interpretation) whose final row deliberately stays Open: small conformance fixtures, live results not rerun, missing ablations, fallible upstream source inference, oracle labels, omitted contributors, transport-boundary failures. Principle: "Remaining risks are documented rather than hidden."

PUBLICATION_CHECKLIST.md
publication checklist with a permanent open risks row an area-by-area pass/open gate (abstract accuracy, novelty framing, related work pass with residual risk, claim/evidence coverage, reproducibility, limitations, code availability, build artifacts, the source/credibility/confidence boundary, poisonedrag interpretation) whose final row deliberately stays open: small conformance fixtures, live results not rerun, missing ablations, fallible upstream source inference, oracle labels, omitted contributors, transport-boundary failures. principle: remaining risks are documented rather than hidden. mechanism publication_checklist.md

◈ Limitations taxonomy: five failure planes

framing/ontology

Editing guidance requiring every added limitation to be classified into one of five planes: label inference failures, derived-writeback failures, retrieval-time failures, belief/credibility failures, provenance transport failures — a reusable failure-plane taxonomy for memory-safety papers.

AGENTS.md (Editing guidance); PUBLICATION_CHECKLIST.md Limitations row
limitations taxonomy: five failure planes editing guidance requiring every added limitation to be classified into one of five planes: label inference failures, derived-writeback failures, retrieval-time failures, belief/credibility failures, provenance transport failures — a reusable failure-plane taxonomy for memory-safety papers. framing/ontology agents.md (editing guidance); publication_checklist.md limitations row

◈ Repo consolidation with pre-move SHA-256 manifest

mechanism

Before merging trace-memory/ (184 files) and trace-memory-architecture/ (164 files) into one artifact repo, a full manifest of 350 files with SHA-256 hashes was written (docs/pre_consolidation_file_manifest.csv), plus a path-mapping table — "avoid destructive deletion before inventory." Historical pyproject files preserved under docs/historical/.

docs\REPOSITORY_INVENTORY.md; docs\TASK_COMPLETION_AUDIT.md
repo consolidation with pre-move sha-256 manifest before merging trace-memory/ (184 files) and trace-memory-architecture/ (164 files) into one artifact repo, a full manifest of 350 files with sha-256 hashes was written (docs/pre_consolidation_file_manifest.csv), plus a path-mapping table — avoid destructive deletion before inventory. historical pyproject files preserved under docs/historical/. mechanism docs\repository_inventory.md; docs\task_completion_audit.md

◈ Task-completion audit as requirement-to-evidence table

mechanism

A final verification pass rendered as a table mapping each requirement of the consolidation/publication task to its concrete evidence (files, test counts, corrected numbers) — auditing one's own task execution the same way the paper audits its claims.

docs\TASK_COMPLETION_AUDIT.md
task-completion audit as requirement-to-evidence table a final verification pass rendered as a table mapping each requirement of the consolidation/publication task to its concrete evidence (files, test counts, corrected numbers) — auditing one's own task execution the same way the paper audits its claims. mechanism docs\task_completion_audit.md

◈ Zenodo deposit practice: no redundant source zip

result/finding

Learned process rule: when depositing a GitHub-hosted project to a DOI service, upload only the PDF and link the repo via related_identifiers (relation: isSupplementTo); do NOT also upload a parallel git-archive source zip. Even a provably clean zip (git archive HEAD cannot include untracked/ignored files like .env) forces a mental audit — the user reported "my heart spiked" when a zip was uploaded for this repo. For permanent archival use the GitHub–Zenodo release webhook (tag → auto version with the GitHub tarball). Offer link-only first; bundle only on stated reason, and call out the redundancy.

memory\feedback_zenodo_no_redundant_source_zip.md; memory\MEMORY.md
zenodo deposit practice: no redundant source zip learned process rule: when depositing a github-hosted project to a doi service, upload only the pdf and link the repo via related_identifiers (relation: issupplementto); do not also upload a parallel git-archive source zip. even a provably clean zip (git archive head cannot include untracked/ignored files like .env) forces a mental audit — the user reported my heart spiked when a zip was uploaded for this repo. for permanent archival use the github–zenodo release webhook (tag → auto version with the github tarball). offer link-only first; bundle only on stated reason, and call out the redundancy. result/finding memory\feedback_zenodo_no_redundant_source_zip.md; memory\memory.md

◈ Cost-and-wall-clock ledger for every benchmark

mechanism

Every live benchmark publishes its price and duration as part of the result: v2 full run ~$3 / 58min; PoisonedRAG ~$0.30 / 28min; LLM coupling ~$0.028 / 199s; per-question PoisonedRAG budget model (~9,000 tokens across classifier + 4 arms, ~85% saved by restricting to two arms). Treating API cost as a reported experimental variable.

docs\research-conclusions.md; benchmarks\poisonedrag\README.md; results\benchmarks\COUPLING_LLM_BENCHMARK.md
cost-and-wall-clock ledger for every benchmark every live benchmark publishes its price and duration as part of the result: v2 full run ~$3 / 58min; poisonedrag ~$0.30 / 28min; llm coupling ~$0.028 / 199s; per-question poisonedrag budget model (~9,000 tokens across classifier + 4 arms, ~85% saved by restricting to two arms). treating api cost as a reported experimental variable. mechanism docs\research-conclusions.md; benchmarks\poisonedrag\readme.md; results\benchmarks\coupling_llm_benchmark.md

P5a · source-downgrading-extensions — major papers 172 ideas

May 2026 — Label Transport, Context-Dependent Valuation, Time-Varying Credibility · P5a-sde-papers-large.md

Time-Varying Credibility (TVC)

◈ Credibility as a separate revisable side channel

mechanism

Each memory record carries a source class Source(r) fixed forever at inscription by the source-downgrading meet, plus a credibility scalar chi_t(r) ∈ [0,1] that evolves over time through evidence-paired update events. Belief may move; production lineage may not. Folding belief revision into the source label re-introduces the laundering failure source downgrading was designed to prevent.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
"credibility may revise belief over time, but it must not rewrite how the record was produced."
credibility as a separate revisable side channel each memory record carries a source class `source(r)` fixed forever at inscription by the source-downgrading meet, plus a credibility scalar `chi_t(r) ∈ [0,1]` that evolves over time through evidence-paired update events. belief may move; production lineage may not. folding belief revision into the source label re-introduces the laundering failure source downgrading was designed to prevent. mechanism papers\time_varying_credibility.tex credibility may revise belief over time, but it must not rewrite how the record was produced.

◈ Confirmation-driven promotion (failure mode 1)

framing/ontology

An inferred record accumulates external confirmations and is silently relabeled as if externally observed. The content may be well-supported, but the production channel was inference — routing that consults source class for write-restricted stores is now bypassed by a record that did not earn that path.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
confirmation-driven promotion (failure mode 1) an inferred record accumulates external confirmations and is silently relabeled as if externally observed. the content may be well-supported, but the production channel was inference — routing that consults source class for write-restricted stores is now bypassed by a record that did not earn that path. framing/ontology papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Discredit-driven suppression (failure mode 2)

framing/ontology

An externally observed record is contradicted by later evidence. Lowering belief is the safe response; rewriting the source class below ext is the dangerous one, because it destroys the lineage information needed to audit what was observed, when, and by whom — a discredited observation becomes indistinguishable from an internal guess.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
discredit-driven suppression (failure mode 2) an externally observed record is contradicted by later evidence. lowering belief is the safe response; rewriting the source class below `ext` is the dangerous one, because it destroys the lineage information needed to audit what was observed, when, and by whom — a discredited observation becomes indistinguishable from an internal guess. framing/ontology papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ The E/I worked example: belief crossover without class crossover

metaphor

A discredited external observation E (chi 0.90→0.30) and a confirmed inference I (chi 0.50→0.85). The system now believes the inference more than the observation, yet still routes E as observation and I as derivation: a forensic route requiring Source ⪰ tool still admits E and still excludes I regardless of chi_t(I).

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
the e/i worked example: belief crossover without class crossover a discredited external observation e (`chi` 0.90→0.30) and a confirmed inference i (`chi` 0.50→0.85). the system now believes the inference more than the observation, yet still routes e as observation and i as derivation: a forensic route requiring `source ⪰ tool` still admits e and still excludes i regardless of `chi_t(i)`. metaphor papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Three quantities, not one: Source / chi_t / Conf_t

framing/ontology

Source integrity (how produced; monotone), credibility (current belief; revisable via logged events), and confidence (self-rated certainty about one's own labeling or credibility estimates) are three separate fields with separate update rules. Confidence is explicitly out of scope but constrained: any confidence updates must pass through the same append-only log discipline.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
three quantities, not one: source / chi_t / conf_t source integrity (how produced; monotone), credibility (current belief; revisable via logged events), and confidence (self-rated certainty about one's own labeling or credibility estimates) are three separate fields with separate update rules. confidence is explicitly out of scope but constrained: any confidence updates must pass through the same append-only log discipline. framing/ontology papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ "Trust" restricted to source-integrity trust — the load-bearing definitional split

framing/ontology

The word trust is confined to the production channel and derivation status; it is not a probability of truth (an external observation can be false; a simulation can later match reality). The paper explicitly names this section as the definitional split subsequent chain papers may cite rather than re-establish.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
"This section is the load-bearing definitional split that subsequent papers in the series may cite rather than re-establish."
trust restricted to source-integrity trust — the load-bearing definitional split the word trust is confined to the production channel and derivation status; it is not a probability of truth (an external observation can be false; a simulation can later match reality). the paper explicitly names this section as the definitional split subsequent chain papers may cite rather than re-establish. framing/ontology papers\time_varying_credibility.tex this section is the load-bearing definitional split that subsequent papers in the series may cite rather than re-establish.

◈ Explicit claims/non-claims register (C1–C4, N1–N5)

framing/ontology

Claims: immutability of source/provenance under updates; accepted updates are closure-independent, attested, drift-bounded, replayable; source-restricted routing requires an explicit source guard; the harness conforms. Non-claims: chi_t is not calibrated probability; no hallucination/adversarial-correlation detection; v1 does not block credibility-log dependency cycles; propositions are not preservation theorems; the evaluation is not a deployment benchmark.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
explicit claims/non-claims register (c1–c4, n1–n5) claims: immutability of source/provenance under updates; accepted updates are closure-independent, attested, drift-bounded, replayable; source-restricted routing requires an explicit source guard; the harness conforms. non-claims: chi_t is not calibrated probability; no hallucination/adversarial-correlation detection; v1 does not block credibility-log dependency cycles; propositions are not preservation theorems; the evaluation is not a deployment benchmark. framing/ontology papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Record schema with exactly two mutable fields

mechanism

A record is a tuple (id, Source, Prov, chi_0, chi_t, Contrib, o, t_0, Log). Everything except chi_t and Log is immutable after inscription — source monotonicity is structural (there is simply no write path), not enforced by a check alone.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
record schema with exactly two mutable fields a record is a tuple (id, source, prov, chi_0, chi_t, contrib, o, t_0, log). everything except `chi_t` and `log` is immutable after inscription — source monotonicity is structural (there is simply no write path), not enforced by a check alone. mechanism papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Structured policy rule objects in an immutable registry

mechanism

Each credibility update is admitted under a registered rule: (rule id = content hash, direction ±1, admissible evidence source classes Σ_ev, kernel function, attestation channel, verification relation, per-event drift cap Δ_max). The policy registry is content-addressable: the rule id uniquely determines its content, so silent rule substitution changes the hash.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
structured policy rule objects in an immutable registry each credibility update is admitted under a registered rule: (rule id = content hash, direction ±1, admissible evidence source classes σ_ev, kernel function, attestation channel, verification relation, per-event drift cap δ_max). the policy registry is content-addressable: the rule id uniquely determines its content, so silent rule substitution changes the hash. mechanism papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Evidence temporal witness (snapshot-witness route)

mechanism

Every update event commits a witness of the evidence record's state immediately before the event: evidence id, source, provenance, prior credibility chi_e^-, committed evidence-log head h_e^-, and the policy-registry root. Replay verifies the committed head directly (snapshot route) instead of recursively replaying every evidence record's whole log on every read.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
evidence temporal witness (snapshot-witness route) every update event commits a witness of the evidence record's state immediately before the event: evidence id, source, provenance, prior credibility chi_e^-, committed evidence-log head h_e^-, and the policy-registry root. replay verifies the committed head directly (snapshot route) instead of recursively replaying every evidence record's whole log on every read. mechanism papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Assumption A1: anchored log head

mechanism

The hash chain over the credibility log detects mutation only relative to an external anchor (immutable registry, signed transparency log, timestamping service). An adversary who rewrites the entire log and recomputes every hash is explicitly outside the replay-soundness claim without A1. Making this assumption named and explicit was a delta-report change.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex; docs\TIME_VARYING_CREDIBILITY_DELTA.md
assumption a1: anchored log head the hash chain over the credibility log detects mutation only relative to an external anchor (immutable registry, signed transparency log, timestamping service). an adversary who rewrites the entire log and recomputes every hash is explicitly outside the replay-soundness claim without a1. making this assumption named and explicit was a delta-report change. mechanism papers\time_varying_credibility.tex; docs\time_varying_credibility_delta.md

◈ Credibility log as a hash-commitment chain

mechanism

The log is an append-only sequence of events with strictly increasing timestamps; each event's commitment is H(payload, predecessor hash, active registry root), with the initial value h_0 derived from the record id and chi_0. Mutation, reordering, deletion, or prefix rewrite mismatches the anchored head. Registry-anchored and externally-signed variants are special cases of the same equation.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
credibility log as a hash-commitment chain the log is an append-only sequence of events with strictly increasing timestamps; each event's commitment is h(payload, predecessor hash, active registry root), with the initial value h_0 derived from the record id and chi_0. mutation, reordering, deletion, or prefix rewrite mismatches the anchored head. registry-anchored and externally-signed variants are special cases of the same equation. mechanism papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Replay fold Φ: the cached value is never authoritative

mechanism

Current credibility is definitionally the deterministic fold of the log under registry-resolved kernels; the stored chi_t is only a cached snapshot re-verified on every read. Audit rejects a record whenever the stored value disagrees with the fold or any per-event posterior disagrees with the recomputed kernel output.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
replay fold φ: the cached value is never authoritative current credibility is definitionally the deterministic fold of the log under registry-resolved kernels; the stored chi_t is only a cached snapshot re-verified on every read. audit rejects a record whenever the stored value disagrees with the fold or any per-event posterior disagrees with the recomputed kernel output. mechanism papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Split primary/derived inscription with credibility ceilings

mechanism

Primary inscription assigns the channel's source class and caps chi_0 at the channel credibility ceiling β_chi(γ) (e.g. fabrication channels can set β_chi = 0; trusted sensors near 1). Derived inscription keeps the source meet and provenance closure verbatim from source downgrading and adds exactly one thing: chi_0 ≤ min(contributor credibilities, β_chi(o)). The split API (inscribe_primary_record / inscribe_derived_record) was a delta change from a single entry point.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex; docs\TIME_VARYING_CREDIBILITY_DELTA.md
split primary/derived inscription with credibility ceilings primary inscription assigns the channel's source class and caps chi_0 at the channel credibility ceiling β_chi(γ) (e.g. fabrication channels can set β_chi = 0; trusted sensors near 1). derived inscription keeps the source meet and provenance closure verbatim from source downgrading and adds exactly one thing: chi_0 ≤ min(contributor credibilities, β_chi(o)). the split api (`inscribe_primary_record` / `inscribe_derived_record`) was a delta change from a single entry point. mechanism papers\time_varying_credibility.tex; docs\time_varying_credibility_delta.md

◈ Eleven-condition admissibility rule for update events

mechanism

A candidate event is admitted only if all hold: time order; bidirectional closure independence; prior consistency (chi^- equals current fold); evidence temporal witness resolution; kernel computation matches; drift bound; attestation verification; evidence source-class admissibility for the rule's direction; direction consistency (kernel output moves the way the rule authorizes); policy-registry membership; source/provenance untouched. Rejection returns an audit record naming the first failing condition.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
eleven-condition admissibility rule for update events a candidate event is admitted only if all hold: time order; bidirectional closure independence; prior consistency (chi^- equals current fold); evidence temporal witness resolution; kernel computation matches; drift bound; attestation verification; evidence source-class admissibility for the rule's direction; direction consistency (kernel output moves the way the rule authorizes); policy-registry membership; source/provenance untouched. rejection returns an audit record naming the first failing condition. mechanism papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Bidirectional closure independence (ancestor + descendant + shared-ancestor)

mechanism

Evidence for an update must satisfy ({id(e)} ∪ Prov(e)) ∩ ({id(r)} ∪ Prov(r)) = ∅. Ancestor-only checks are insufficient: a descendant of r inherits r's support, and a sibling sharing provenance carries the same underlying evidence — either would launder r's own support back into itself as "independent" confirmation.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
bidirectional closure independence (ancestor + descendant + shared-ancestor) evidence for an update must satisfy ({id(e)} ∪ prov(e)) ∩ ({id(r)} ∪ prov(r)) = ∅. ancestor-only checks are insufficient: a descendant of r inherits r's support, and a sibling sharing provenance carries the same underlying evidence — either would launder r's own support back into itself as independent confirmation. mechanism papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Self-confirmation as the credibility analogue of inference laundering

framing/ontology

The three provenance-overlap variants (ancestor, descendant, shared ancestor) are all "an internally generated signal masquerading as independent evidence" — the same laundering shape the foundation meet blocks for source class, reappearing on the belief channel.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
self-confirmation as the credibility analogue of inference laundering the three provenance-overlap variants (ancestor, descendant, shared ancestor) are all an internally generated signal masquerading as independent evidence — the same laundering shape the foundation meet blocks for source class, reappearing on the belief channel. framing/ontology papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Conservativeness of bidirectional closure as a deliberate precision/utility tradeoff

framing/ontology

The rule rejects all provenance-overlapping evidence, including cases where a shared upstream ancestor is benign and the branches truly are independent for the question at hand. Rationale: a single set-intersection test is the only check cheap enough to run at every update and replay; finer independence criteria would need content-level analysis. Deployments wanting overlapping evidence back should add a content-level policy layer downstream — never relax the inscription rule.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
conservativeness of bidirectional closure as a deliberate precision/utility tradeoff the rule rejects all provenance-overlapping evidence, including cases where a shared upstream ancestor is benign and the branches truly are independent for the question at hand. rationale: a single set-intersection test is the only check cheap enough to run at every update and replay; finer independence criteria would need content-level analysis. deployments wanting overlapping evidence back should add a content-level policy layer downstream — never relax the inscription rule. framing/ontology papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Scoped-out gap: credibility-log dependency cycles

open-question

If A and B have disjoint derivation provenance, B can raise chi(A) and then A can raise chi(B) — coupled credibility histories the v1 closure does not see, because closure uses derivation provenance only. Named as an explicit v1 scope boundary and a planned extension.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
scoped-out gap: credibility-log dependency cycles if a and b have disjoint derivation provenance, b can raise chi(a) and then a can raise chi(b) — coupled credibility histories the v1 closure does not see, because closure uses derivation provenance only. named as an explicit v1 scope boundary and a planned extension. open-question papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ CredProv: the credibility-log dependency closure (future work)

open-question

Proposed fix for log cycles: define CredProv(r) = Prov(r) ∪ {records used as evidence for r or its log-ancestors} as a least fixed point, and require the bidirectional intersection over CredProv instead of Prov. Noted to naturally pair with the operational-semantics strengthening because it requires reasoning over traces of update events rather than single inscription witnesses.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
credprov: the credibility-log dependency closure (future work) proposed fix for log cycles: define credprov(r) = prov(r) ∪ {records used as evidence for r or its log-ancestors} as a least fixed point, and require the bidirectional intersection over credprov instead of prov. noted to naturally pair with the operational-semantics strengthening because it requires reasoning over traces of update events rather than single inscription witnesses. open-question papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Retroactive log mutation threat

mechanism

A policy that mutates or reorders committed log events rewrites the credibility trajectory without an audit trace. Observable through replay soundness: the fold no longer matches the stored value, or the append-only integrity check (hash chain / signed commit / registry anchor) fails.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
retroactive log mutation threat a policy that mutates or reorders committed log events rewrites the credibility trajectory without an audit trace. observable through replay soundness: the fold no longer matches the stored value, or the append-only integrity check (hash chain / signed commit / registry anchor) fails. mechanism papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Pluggable-but-pinned credibility kernels

mechanism

The kernel is deliberately a policy artifact, not a fixed formula. Example deployment-ready instance: clip(chi^- + sign(Δ)·w(s_e)·|Δ|, 0, 1) with class-weighted evidence influence w. Bayesian conjugate updates, decay-with-confirmation, and multiplicative odds are all admissible if deterministic in their arguments and registered by content hash.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
pluggable-but-pinned credibility kernels the kernel is deliberately a policy artifact, not a fixed formula. example deployment-ready instance: clip(chi^- + sign(δ)·w(s_e)·|δ|, 0, 1) with class-weighted evidence influence w. bayesian conjugate updates, decay-with-confirmation, and multiplicative odds are all admissible if deterministic in their arguments and registered by content hash. mechanism papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Source-guarded route objects (the routing contract)

mechanism

Routing is modeled as an explicit object q = (path_id, src_req, P_extra, action). Every source-restricted path must have a non-empty explicit source requirement; credibility conditions live only in P_extra and can only further restrict admission — no expression may derive, assign, cast, or substitute a source-class membership from chi_t. This is the runtime expression of the inscription separation.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
source-guarded route objects (the routing contract) routing is modeled as an explicit object q = (path_id, src_req, p_extra, action). every source-restricted path must have a non-empty explicit source requirement; credibility conditions live only in p_extra and can only further restrict admission — no expression may derive, assign, cast, or substitute a source-class membership from chi_t. this is the runtime expression of the inscription separation. mechanism papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Restricted predicate language (SourceAtom / CredAtom grammar)

mechanism

To make route inspection decidable at registration time, extra predicates are drawn from a small grammar: SourceAtoms mention only Source(r) (=, ⪰, ⪯, ∈S), CredAtoms mention only chi_t(r) (≥θ, ≤θ, interval), closed under ¬/∧/∨. The two atom forms are disjoint by syntax, so by construction credibility atoms cannot discharge or replace the source guard. Arbitrary functions, computed labels, side-effecting expressions are rejected at registration.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
restricted predicate language (sourceatom / credatom grammar) to make route inspection decidable at registration time, extra predicates are drawn from a small grammar: sourceatoms mention only source(r) (=, ⪰, ⪯, ∈s), credatoms mention only chi_t(r) (≥θ, ≤θ, interval), closed under ¬/∧/∨. the two atom forms are disjoint by syntax, so by construction credibility atoms cannot discharge or replace the source guard. arbitrary functions, computed labels, side-effecting expressions are rejected at registration. mechanism papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Threat: credibility-as-source substitution

mechanism

A route registered for a source-restricted path that uses only a credibility condition ("chi_t ≥ θ ⇒ treat as Source = ext"). The stored class is never rewritten, but at routing time it is replaced by high credibility — the same downstream effect as source promotion. Caught statically by routing-policy audit at registration, not per-record at read time.

papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
threat: credibility-as-source substitution a route registered for a source-restricted path that uses only a credibility condition ( chi_t ≥ θ ⇒ treat as source = ext ). the stored class is never rewritten, but at routing time it is replaced by high credibility — the same downstream effect as source promotion. caught statically by routing-policy audit at registration, not per-record at read time. mechanism papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ Five-group record-level audit replay

mechanism
