prior chain as a five-part contract on inscription inputs a self-contained restatement so readers can check lt's results without the prior papers: (1) total source order with meet+operation-ceiling; (2) per-class scalar interval policy; (3) append-only credibility log folded by a registered kernel; (4) authorized inscription context with policy hash; (5) the composite inscription witness w(r) that audit replay re-verifies. lt treats these as preconditions on the foreign witness, not theorems to re-prove. framing/ontology papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ The composite inscription witness W(r)

mechanism

W(r) = (Source, tau_kappa, kappa, h(kappa), chi_t, Log, o, Direct, Prov) — the artifact each chain layer contributes fields to; inside one agent it is what audit replay verifies, across a boundary it is what the envelope must carry.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
the composite inscription witness w(r) w(r) = (source, tau_kappa, kappa, h(kappa), chi_t, log, o, direct, prov) — the artifact each chain layer contributes fields to; inside one agent it is what audit replay verifies, across a boundary it is what the envelope must carry. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ LT non-claims register

framing/ontology

Not an authentication protocol for arbitrary messages; not a PKI (key distribution/rotation/revocation abstracted to a registry contract); not a global class-naming scheme (agents may disagree; translation handles it); not a bridge folding foreign credibility logs into local events; not unbounded mutability of imports; not a network-adversary evaluation (TLS assumed underneath); not a general information-flow-lattice result (finite total chains only — semilattice extension needs an explicit tie/selection policy).

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
lt non-claims register not an authentication protocol for arbitrary messages; not a pki (key distribution/rotation/revocation abstracted to a registry contract); not a global class-naming scheme (agents may disagree; translation handles it); not a bridge folding foreign credibility logs into local events; not unbounded mutability of imports; not a network-adversary evaluation (tls assumed underneath); not a general information-flow-lattice result (finite total chains only — semilattice extension needs an explicit tie/selection policy). framing/ontology papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ The four-component transport TCB

mechanism

KeyRegistry (append-only issuer→key map with validity intervals; rotation = appending a triple), TaxRegistry (immutable content-addressable store of taxonomies and translation policies — mutation makes a new hash, so retroactive substitution is detectable), IssuerLog (per-issuer class ceiling β_Source(I) and scalar ceiling β_tau(I), anchored like the credibility log), and the local valuation policies. If a deployment lacks one, the corresponding check degrades to best-effort and the policy must reject, quarantine, or take the explicit fallback.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
the four-component transport tcb keyregistry (append-only issuer→key map with validity intervals; rotation = appending a triple), taxregistry (immutable content-addressable store of taxonomies and translation policies — mutation makes a new hash, so retroactive substitution is detectable), issuerlog (per-issuer class ceiling β_source(i) and scalar ceiling β_tau(i), anchored like the credibility log), and the local valuation policies. if a deployment lacks one, the corresponding check degrades to best-effort and the policy must reject, quarantine, or take the explicit fallback. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Specified vs assumed layers table with failure effects

framing/ontology

A table separating what the paper fully specifies (canonicalization, translation certificates), specifies abstractly (verification relation), and assumes as TCB (key registry, attestation authority, issuer-log governance, policy registries) — with the failure that follows from each compromised assumption (issuer impersonation, silent amplification, over-trusting issuer, un-recomputable meets…). Reviewers are told to evaluate the contribution at that level.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
specified vs assumed layers table with failure effects a table separating what the paper fully specifies (canonicalization, translation certificates), specifies abstractly (verification relation), and assumes as tcb (key registry, attestation authority, issuer-log governance, policy registries) — with the failure that follows from each compromised assumption (issuer impersonation, silent amplification, over-trusting issuer, un-recomputable meets…). reviewers are told to evaluate the contribution at that level. framing/ontology papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Transport attestation objects and their verification relation

mechanism

An attestation α = (issuer, foreign registry root, attested object hash, kind, local verifier, transport rule, validity interval, authority, signature, hash) is a local TCB artifact recording that the importer accepts a specific foreign registry root / policy hash / log head / context hash for a specific issuer and rule. Verification requires authority trust, issuer/object match with the envelope, temporal validity + non-revocation + freshness under the local rollback policy, and local authorization of the rule. Failure means the foreign object is non-authoritative; only explicit fallback branches remain — except provenance, whose failure is a hard reject.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
transport attestation objects and their verification relation an attestation α = (issuer, foreign registry root, attested object hash, kind, local verifier, transport rule, validity interval, authority, signature, hash) is a local tcb artifact recording that the importer accepts a specific foreign registry root / policy hash / log head / context hash for a specific issuer and rule. verification requires authority trust, issuer/object match with the envelope, temporal validity + non-revocation + freshness under the local rollback policy, and local authorization of the rule. failure means the foreign object is non-authoritative; only explicit fallback branches remain — except provenance, whose failure is a hard reject. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Source taxonomy objects and nominal alignment

mechanism

A taxonomy Θ = (class set, total integrity order excluding op, operation ceilings, content hash of the canonical serialization). Two taxonomies are nominally aligned iff same class set and same hash; otherwise they are mutually foreign and translation is required — alignment is by hash, never by name.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
source taxonomy objects and nominal alignment a taxonomy θ = (class set, total integrity order excluding op, operation ceilings, content hash of the canonical serialization). two taxonomies are nominally aligned iff same class set and same hash; otherwise they are mutually foreign and translation is required — alignment is by hash, never by name. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ The local import context kappa_B^imp

mechanism

Before any import meet is computed, the importer resolves a local import context that fixes the operative order, intervals, and import-operation ceiling; a verified import is never computed under a context-free taxonomy order. If the foreign context is locally authorized it may serve; otherwise the fixed local audit/import context is used and the foreign context is recorded as non-authoritative annotation. (This is the paper's resolution of the chain review's "context-indexed import policy" open issue.)

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
the local import context kappa_b^imp before any import meet is computed, the importer resolves a local import context that fixes the operative order, intervals, and import-operation ceiling; a verified import is never computed under a context-free taxonomy order. if the foreign context is locally authorized it may serve; otherwise the fixed local audit/import context is used and the foreign context is recorded as non-authoritative annotation. (this is the paper's resolution of the chain review's context-indexed import policy open issue.) mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Content-bound vs metadata-bound envelopes

mechanism

The witness records how the record was produced; the content digest h_content = H(canon(id, payload, schema)) binds what the record is. Verified import recomputes the digest from the carried payload and rejects mismatches, so tamper coverage includes content mutation, not just label/scalar promotion. A malicious issuer freshly signing misleading content is outside content-binding and left to issuer trust + credibility revision.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
content-bound vs metadata-bound envelopes the witness records how the record was produced; the content digest h_content = h(canon(id, payload, schema)) binds what the record is. verified import recomputes the digest from the carried payload and rejects mismatches, so tamper coverage includes content mutation, not just label/scalar promotion. a malicious issuer freshly signing misleading content is outside content-binding and left to issuer trust + credibility revision. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Canonical envelope encoding contract

mechanism

The encoder must be deterministic and injective: fixed field set, explicit absent-field representation, duplicate-key rejection, lexicographic key sort, NFC UTF-8, single spellings for booleans/null, decimal integers without leading zeros, single round-trip float representation, NaN/infinity rejected, one declared UTC timestamp format, explicit type tags. Deployments should use deterministic CBOR (RFC 8949) or JCS canonical JSON (RFC 8785) and publish test vectors. Motivated as the same property database provenance calls semantic equality of provenance annotations.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
canonical envelope encoding contract the encoder must be deterministic and injective: fixed field set, explicit absent-field representation, duplicate-key rejection, lexicographic key sort, nfc utf-8, single spellings for booleans/null, decimal integers without leading zeros, single round-trip float representation, nan/infinity rejected, one declared utc timestamp format, explicit type tags. deployments should use deterministic cbor (rfc 8949) or jcs canonical json (rfc 8785) and publish test vectors. motivated as the same property database provenance calls semantic equality of provenance annotations. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Published canonical test vector

experiment/benchmark

The generated artifact includes a concrete vector for valid_import: payload hash 1c6e2312…, content hash 7c87f343…, fixture HMAC signature f8c08881… — worked bytes-level evidence that the canonicalization is pinned.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
published canonical test vector the generated artifact includes a concrete vector for `valid_import`: payload hash 1c6e2312…, content hash 7c87f343…, fixture hmac signature f8c08881… — worked bytes-level evidence that the canonicalization is pinned. experiment/benchmark papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Why the credibility-log head and registry anchor are mandatory envelope fields

mechanism

An envelope carrying only class/scalar/context/closure would silently drop the TVC audit-replay guarantee: the importer could not verify chi_t against the issuer's committed log and an adversary controlling credibility could forge any value in [0,1]. Carrying the head plus anchor makes credibility replay-verifiable across the boundary (recursive descent or snapshot-witness route).

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
why the credibility-log head and registry anchor are mandatory envelope fields an envelope carrying only class/scalar/context/closure would silently drop the tvc audit-replay guarantee: the importer could not verify chi_t against the issuer's committed log and an adversary controlling credibility could forge any value in [0,1]. carrying the head plus anchor makes credibility replay-verifiable across the boundary (recursive descent or snapshot-witness route). mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Export semantics: identity by (content, key, timestamp)

mechanism

Export is non-destructive; re-export of the same record under the same key and timestamp produces the same envelope identity hash (so envelope-equality is well-defined), while a new timestamp or key gives a new identity even with unchanged witness — intentional, because timestamp+key identify which signing event and hence which key-validity interval the importer must consult.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
export semantics: identity by (content, key, timestamp) export is non-destructive; re-export of the same record under the same key and timestamp produces the same envelope identity hash (so envelope-equality is well-defined), while a new timestamp or key gives a new identity even with unchanged witness — intentional, because timestamp+key identify which signing event and hence which key-validity interval the importer must consult. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Transparent vs issuer-reset re-export

mechanism

When re-exporting a previously imported record, transparent re-export carries the earlier import-audit event (prior envelope identity, first-hop caps, translation hash, prior issuer ceilings) inside the wrapped witness so downstream agents can audit the earlier hop; issuer-reset re-export strips it and presents the record as one's own export. Both are well-formed; only transparent re-export supports multi-hop composition claims. An executable predicate carries_recursive_import_witness decides which mode produced any envelope.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
transparent vs issuer-reset re-export when re-exporting a previously imported record, transparent re-export carries the earlier import-audit event (prior envelope identity, first-hop caps, translation hash, prior issuer ceilings) inside the wrapped witness so downstream agents can audit the earlier hop; issuer-reset re-export strips it and presents the record as one's own export. both are well-formed; only transparent re-export supports multi-hop composition claims. an executable predicate `carries_recursive_import_witness` decides which mode produced any envelope. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Translation policies from the affine-contraction family

mechanism

T_{A→B} = (class map μ, scalar reducer ν, global scalar ceiling ζ, locally-declared class upper-bound function μ̂, content hash covering all four). ν(x) = min(ρ·x, ζ) with ρ ∈ [0,1] — closed under composition and finitely checkable, so the non-amplification conditions reduce from a quantifier over all reals to checking two parameters plus a finite class enumeration.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
translation policies from the affine-contraction family t_{a→b} = (class map μ, scalar reducer ν, global scalar ceiling ζ, locally-declared class upper-bound function μ̂, content hash covering all four). ν(x) = min(ρ·x, ζ) with ρ ∈ [0,1] — closed under composition and finitely checkable, so the non-amplification conditions reduce from a quantifier over all reals to checking two parameters plus a finite class enumeration. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Policy-relative non-amplification with μ̂ inside the hash

mechanism

A translation is non-amplifying relative to the locally-declared upper-bound function μ̂ (the most permissive local class the importer will assign to each foreign class), with the reducer contractive and, for composition, the class map order-preserving and the reducer monotone. Registering μ̂ inside the hashed translation object means the certificate is not relative to a mutable external parameter — audit replays against the exact bound the registration was certified with. Honest caveat: a badly chosen μ̂ makes a bad translation locally admissible; the guarantee is only obedience to the registered local bound.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
policy-relative non-amplification with μ̂ inside the hash a translation is non-amplifying relative to the locally-declared upper-bound function μ̂ (the most permissive local class the importer will assign to each foreign class), with the reducer contractive and, for composition, the class map order-preserving and the reducer monotone. registering μ̂ inside the hashed translation object means the certificate is not relative to a mutable external parameter — audit replays against the exact bound the registration was certified with. honest caveat: a badly chosen μ̂ makes a bad translation locally admissible; the guarantee is only obedience to the registered local bound. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Translation composition law: direct ≤ composed

mechanism

Composition of two non-amplifying policies is again affine (factor ρ_1ρ_2, ceiling min(ρ_2ζ_1, ζ_2)); and any registry-authoritative direct policy A→C must be at-or-below the composition through B for every class and scalar — a direct shortcut may never be more permissive than the path it shortcuts.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
translation composition law: direct ≤ composed composition of two non-amplifying policies is again affine (factor ρ_1ρ_2, ceiling min(ρ_2ζ_1, ζ_2)); and any registry-authoritative direct policy a→c must be at-or-below the composition through b for every class and scalar — a direct shortcut may never be more permissive than the path it shortcuts. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Translation as the boundary's operation ceiling

framing/ontology

The framing that dissolves the need for a new meet rule: the foundation imposes β(o) on every derivation; the boundary imposes μ̂(foreign class) on every import. Both are policy artifacts bounding an inscription independently of contributors, and Property "translation is downgrade-only" already yields the meet-side bound.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
translation as the boundary's operation ceiling the framing that dissolves the need for a new meet rule: the foundation imposes β(o) on every derivation; the boundary imposes μ̂(foreign class) on every import. both are policy artifacts bounding an inscription independently of contributors, and property translation is downgrade-only already yields the meet-side bound. framing/ontology papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Hard preconditions vs auxiliary admissibility conditions

mechanism

Hard (failure = no verified import ever): signature integrity with key resolved at issue time, content binding, issuer admissibility, taxonomy resolution, translation availability, provenance resolution. Auxiliary (failure selects an explicit named fallback, never authoritative use): policy-hash attestation → local default audit context with foreign policy as annotation; credibility-log attestation → credibility-downgrade fallback; context authorization → local default context with foreign context annotation.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
hard preconditions vs auxiliary admissibility conditions hard (failure = no verified import ever): signature integrity with key resolved at issue time, content binding, issuer admissibility, taxonomy resolution, translation availability, provenance resolution. auxiliary (failure selects an explicit named fallback, never authoritative use): policy-hash attestation → local default audit context with foreign policy as annotation; credibility-log attestation → credibility-downgrade fallback; context authorization → local default context with foreign context annotation. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ The seven-step verified import operation

mechanism

(1) Resolve the local import context and load its order/intervals/ceiling before any meet; (2) translate label and scalar (identity translation for aligned taxonomies); (3) issuer-meet class bound = meet(translated class, issuer class ceiling, import-operation ceiling); (4) scalar cap = min(translated scalar, issuer scalar ceiling, interval top of the bound), then class-selection may lower the class for interval consistency but never raise it, with empty selection ⇒ reject/quarantine; (5) credibility carriage under a registered transport rule or the downgrade fallback; (6) provenance carriage: Direct = {envelope id}, Prov = {envelope id} ∪ resolved foreign closure; (7) append the import-audit event (envelope hash, taxonomy hash, translation hash, policy bundle, anchor, issuer, timestamp, import-policy hash) to the local log.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
the seven-step verified import operation (1) resolve the local import context and load its order/intervals/ceiling before any meet; (2) translate label and scalar (identity translation for aligned taxonomies); (3) issuer-meet class bound = meet(translated class, issuer class ceiling, import-operation ceiling); (4) scalar cap = min(translated scalar, issuer scalar ceiling, interval top of the bound), then class-selection may lower the class for interval consistency but never raise it, with empty selection ⇒ reject/quarantine; (5) credibility carriage under a registered transport rule or the downgrade fallback; (6) provenance carriage: direct = {envelope id}, prov = {envelope id} ∪ resolved foreign closure; (7) append the import-audit event (envelope hash, taxonomy hash, translation hash, policy bundle, anchor, issuer, timestamp, import-policy hash) to the local log. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ The envelope as the sole direct contributor

framing/ontology

An imported record's direct-contributor witness is the envelope identity itself — the import event is a derivation from an "envelope-shaped contributor," which is what lets the foundation meet apply at the boundary without pretending the foreign contributors are local records.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
the envelope as the sole direct contributor an imported record's direct-contributor witness is the envelope identity itself — the import event is a derivation from an envelope-shaped contributor, which is what lets the foundation meet apply at the boundary without pretending the foreign contributors are local records. framing/ontology papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Credibility-downgrade fallback

mechanism

If the cross-registry credibility attestation cannot be resolved: inscribe chi ≤ the issuer's registered fallback credibility ceiling chi_fb(I), attach an empty local log, and mark the record credibility-foreign for downstream consumers — imported belief without verifiable history is bounded and flagged, never trusted at face value.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
credibility-downgrade fallback if the cross-registry credibility attestation cannot be resolved: inscribe chi ≤ the issuer's registered fallback credibility ceiling chi_fb(i), attach an empty local log, and mark the record credibility-foreign for downstream consumers — imported belief without verifiable history is bounded and flagged, never trusted at face value. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ The import policy object P_imp

mechanism

(accept_unverified ∈ {reject, source_uncertain, quarantine}; fixed default audit/import context; import-operation class ceiling table; per-issuer credibility fallback table; quarantine target class; content hash of the whole policy). Defaults: reject, audit/import context, β(import)=infer, quarantine target fab. A retained failed-verification payload is written outside verified-import semantics as source-uncertain content with an empty imported witness — reusing the unverified envelope fields as labels is forbidden.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
the import policy object p_imp (accept_unverified ∈ {reject, source_uncertain, quarantine}; fixed default audit/import context; import-operation class ceiling table; per-issuer credibility fallback table; quarantine target class; content hash of the whole policy). defaults: reject, audit/import context, β(import)=infer, quarantine target fab. a retained failed-verification payload is written outside verified-import semantics as source-uncertain content with an empty imported witness — reusing the unverified envelope fields as labels is forbidden. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ β(import) = infer: the conservative default with a knob

mechanism

Verified import defaults to the same infer ceiling as ordinary derivation — the transport-side analogue of β(derived)=infer. Trusted issuers (e.g. signed sensor gateways with independently audited production chains) may earn a higher ceiling; non-amplification holds for any choice, the choice only sets how permissive the verified boundary is.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
β(import) = infer: the conservative default with a knob verified import defaults to the same infer ceiling as ordinary derivation — the transport-side analogue of β(derived)=infer. trusted issuers (e.g. signed sensor gateways with independently audited production chains) may earn a higher ceiling; non-amplification holds for any choice, the choice only sets how permissive the verified boundary is. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ source_uncertain: class or metadata, pick one

mechanism

Source-uncertain retained content defaults to the existing lowest class fab. A named source_uncertain class may be added only by inserting it into the taxonomy at/below infer and regenerating the interval policy and order hash; if kept only as metadata outside the taxonomy it is not a source label and cannot participate in the meet.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
source_uncertain: class or metadata, pick one source-uncertain retained content defaults to the existing lowest class fab. a named source_uncertain class may be added only by inserting it into the taxonomy at/below infer and regenerating the interval policy and order hash; if kept only as metadata outside the taxonomy it is not a source label and cannot participate in the meet. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Typed import results: five statuses instead of one verdict

mechanism

Rejected / SourceUncertainQuarantine / VerifiedFull / VerifiedWithCredibilityFallback / VerifiedWithContextDefault. All three Verified* statuses carry issuer-meet-bounded records and differ only in which auxiliary clause was attested versus replaced by fallback; double degradation names the most-degraded branch with per-branch truth in the audit record. Provenance failure is never an accepted status — no "verified with opaque provenance" constructor exists in v1.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
typed import results: five statuses instead of one verdict rejected / sourceuncertainquarantine / verifiedfull / verifiedwithcredibilityfallback / verifiedwithcontextdefault. all three verified* statuses carry issuer-meet-bounded records and differ only in which auxiliary clause was attested versus replaced by fallback; double degradation names the most-degraded branch with per-branch truth in the audit record. provenance failure is never an accepted status — no verified with opaque provenance constructor exists in v1. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ v1 hard-reject semantics for unresolved provenance

dead-end

Unresolved provenance closure is a hard verification failure: the payload may at most enter source-uncertain quarantine with an empty imported witness. Opaque provenance is deferred to future work behind a separate accepted status and an explicitly weakened recovery invariant — the resolution of the chain review's flagged contradiction between an opaque marker and hard reject.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
v1 hard-reject semantics for unresolved provenance unresolved provenance closure is a hard verification failure: the payload may at most enter source-uncertain quarantine with an empty imported witness. opaque provenance is deferred to future work behind a separate accepted status and an explicitly weakened recovery invariant — the resolution of the chain review's flagged contradiction between an opaque marker and hard reject. dead-end papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Six transport threat models, each paired with its observing check

mechanism

Label stripping (canonical serialization rejects incomplete witnesses); label promotion in transit (signature over canonical form fails); issuer impersonation (registered key at issue time fails to verify — reduces to a TCB key-distribution failure, not a labeling failure); taxonomy substitution in three variants — string-equality substitution, unregistered translation, amplifying translation (resolution + registration checks; amplifying maps rejected at registration); policy substitution (forged policy hashes / kernel roots / interval hashes — caught by the attestation clause; noted as formal-TCB rather than fixture-measured in v1); envelope replay (key-validity interval + envelope-identity cache in the audit log, with an optional permit-replay annotation).

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
six transport threat models, each paired with its observing check label stripping (canonical serialization rejects incomplete witnesses); label promotion in transit (signature over canonical form fails); issuer impersonation (registered key at issue time fails to verify — reduces to a tcb key-distribution failure, not a labeling failure); taxonomy substitution in three variants — string-equality substitution, unregistered translation, amplifying translation (resolution + registration checks; amplifying maps rejected at registration); policy substitution (forged policy hashes / kernel roots / interval hashes — caught by the attestation clause; noted as formal-tcb rather than fixture-measured in v1); envelope replay (key-validity interval + envelope-identity cache in the audit log, with an optional permit-replay annotation). mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Out-of-scope: perfectly signed, materially misleading content

framing/ontology

An envelope can verify perfectly and still carry false content — named as the transport-layer analogue of TVC's content-correlated adversarial-evidence boundary, handled by issuer trust, credibility revision, and downstream content checks, never by the transport rule.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
out-of-scope: perfectly signed, materially misleading content an envelope can verify perfectly and still carry false content — named as the transport-layer analogue of tvc's content-correlated adversarial-evidence boundary, handled by issuer trust, credibility revision, and downstream content checks, never by the transport rule. framing/ontology papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Nine-group envelope audit replay with threat mapping

mechanism

On read: signature+content binding; issuer admissibility; taxonomy hash resolution; translation hash resolution; policy-hash / cross-registry attestation; credibility-log anchor; import-context + issuer-meet recomputation; provenance witness exactness; replay uniqueness. The groups are separable by design and each threat model maps onto specific groups — group 7 is the boundary's analogue of the foundation's meet re-evaluation.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
nine-group envelope audit replay with threat mapping on read: signature+content binding; issuer admissibility; taxonomy hash resolution; translation hash resolution; policy-hash / cross-registry attestation; credibility-log anchor; import-context + issuer-meet recomputation; provenance witness exactness; replay uniqueness. the groups are separable by design and each threat model maps onto specific groups — group 7 is the boundary's analogue of the foundation's meet re-evaluation. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Transport non-amplification propositions (class and scalar)

result/finding

For a verified import: the stored local class is at-or-below the translated foreign class, the issuer class ceiling, and the import-operation ceiling under the resolved import context; the stored scalar is at-or-below the reduced foreign scalar (hence the original), the issuer scalar ceiling, and the selected class's interval top.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
transport non-amplification propositions (class and scalar) for a verified import: the stored local class is at-or-below the translated foreign class, the issuer class ceiling, and the import-operation ceiling under the resolved import context; the stored scalar is at-or-below the reduced foreign scalar (hence the original), the issuer scalar ceiling, and the selected class's interval top. result/finding papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Composition non-amplification under the recursive import witness hypothesis

result/finding

Two-hop A→B→C composition is non-amplifying if B re-exported transparently (or C resolves an attestation from B's import-audit log naming the first-hop fields): the final class is bounded by the composed translation of the original class and every translated issuer/import ceiling; the final scalar by min(composed reducers on the original, both issuer ceilings). Under issuer-reset re-export the hypothesis fails and the claim weakens to non-amplification relative to B's signed export only.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
composition non-amplification under the recursive import witness hypothesis two-hop a→b→c composition is non-amplifying if b re-exported transparently (or c resolves an attestation from b's import-audit log naming the first-hop fields): the final class is bounded by the composed translation of the original class and every translated issuer/import ceiling; the final scalar by min(composed reducers on the original, both issuer ceilings). under issuer-reset re-export the hypothesis fails and the claim weakens to non-amplification relative to b's signed export only. result/finding papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Multi-hop ceiling corollary

result/finding

Over any verified k-hop path with issuers I_1…I_k, the final scalar is bounded by min_j β_tau(I_j) and the final class by the meet of the composed translation and every issuer/import ceiling translated along the remaining path — trust through a chain is capped by its least-trusted issuer.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
multi-hop ceiling corollary over any verified k-hop path with issuers i_1…i_k, the final scalar is bounded by min_j β_tau(i_j) and the final class by the meet of the composed translation and every issuer/import ceiling translated along the remaining path — trust through a chain is capped by its least-trusted issuer. result/finding papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Conditional recovery of the four chain guarantees at import

result/finding

A record passing envelope audit replay satisfies: the foundation writeback invariant under the import context with the envelope as sole direct contributor; class-consistency of the scalar under the local interval policy; either a replayed credibility fold under a registered transport-rule kernel or the credibility-foreign bounded fallback; and a single authoritative recorded import context (foreign context authoritative only if locally authorized).

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
conditional recovery of the four chain guarantees at import a record passing envelope audit replay satisfies: the foundation writeback invariant under the import context with the envelope as sole direct contributor; class-consistency of the scalar under the local interval policy; either a replayed credibility fold under a registered transport-rule kernel or the credibility-foreign bounded fallback; and a single authoritative recorded import context (foreign context authoritative only if locally authorized). result/finding papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ The honest scope cut: local non-amplification of signed issuer claims

framing/ontology

A verified signature proves the issuer committed to the witness — not that the witness was produced by a correct run of the prior chain at the issuer. A registered high-ceiling issuer signing a fabricated closure still yields a locally-bounded import, but transitively recovering the foreign-side guarantees does not follow from the signature alone. The recovery invariant is therefore a statement about the local record r', not the foreign record r.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
"The honest guarantee is therefore \emph{local non-amplification of signed issuer claims}: the import cannot amplify whatever the issuer signed."
the honest scope cut: local non-amplification of signed issuer claims a verified signature proves the issuer committed to the witness — not that the witness was produced by a correct run of the prior chain at the issuer. a registered high-ceiling issuer signing a fabricated closure still yields a locally-bounded import, but transitively recovering the foreign-side guarantees does not follow from the signature alone. the recovery invariant is therefore a statement about the local record r', not the foreign record r. framing/ontology papers\label_transport_draft.tex the honest guarantee is therefore \emph{local non-amplification of signed issuer claims}: the import cannot amplify whatever the issuer signed.

◈ Foreign-witness audit certificates (AuditCert)

open-question

Future-work object AuditCert_I(W(r), π_I, t): an issuer-side attestation that the witness passed the issuer's own audit replay before export, carried as an envelope field and re-checked at import — the missing piece that would upgrade local non-amplification of signed claims to genuine transitive recovery of the prior chain's guarantees on the foreign record.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
foreign-witness audit certificates (auditcert) future-work object auditcert_i(w(r), π_i, t): an issuer-side attestation that the witness passed the issuer's own audit replay before export, carried as an envelope field and re-checked at import — the missing piece that would upgrade local non-amplification of signed claims to genuine transitive recovery of the prior chain's guarantees on the foreign record. open-question papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Collapse to local inscription

result/finding

With nominally aligned taxonomies, top issuer ceilings, identity translation, and a top import ceiling, verified import reduces exactly to local re-inscription of the foreign witness; with a stricter import ceiling it collapses to the local foundation meet shape with β(import) in the role of β(o). Explicitly the transport analogue of TVC's empty-log collapse and per-record's degenerate-interval collapse; deployments warned to use it only for independently audited intra-organization transports.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
collapse to local inscription with nominally aligned taxonomies, top issuer ceilings, identity translation, and a top import ceiling, verified import reduces exactly to local re-inscription of the foreign witness; with a stricter import ceiling it collapses to the local foundation meet shape with β(import) in the role of β(o). explicitly the transport analogue of tvc's empty-log collapse and per-record's degenerate-interval collapse; deployments warned to use it only for independently audited intra-organization transports. result/finding papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Three-agent worked transport: 0.22 → 0.154 → class fab

metaphor

Perception agent A exports sim record (tau 0.22, chi 0.55); intake B (issuer ceilings react/0.78) imports at sim/0.22 with credibility replayed via cross-attested kernel; forensic C (translation ν = 0.7x, ceilings react/0.65) computes cap 0.7·0.22 = 0.154, and because 0.154 falls below C's sim interval the class-selection branch lowers the stored class to fab_C — demonstrating scalar repair lowering class at a real boundary. C's credibility head doesn't resolve to a same-kernel anchor, so the credibility-foreign fallback caps chi at 0.50 with an empty local log.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
three-agent worked transport: 0.22 → 0.154 → class fab perception agent a exports sim record (tau 0.22, chi 0.55); intake b (issuer ceilings react/0.78) imports at sim/0.22 with credibility replayed via cross-attested kernel; forensic c (translation ν = 0.7x, ceilings react/0.65) computes cap 0.7·0.22 = 0.154, and because 0.154 falls below c's sim interval the class-selection branch lowers the stored class to fab_c — demonstrating scalar repair lowering class at a real boundary. c's credibility head doesn't resolve to a same-kernel anchor, so the credibility-foreign fallback caps chi at 0.50 with an empty local log. metaphor papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Cascading audit requires the predecessor witness

mechanism

In the walkthrough, C can audit B's export but can audit B's earlier import from A only if the witness carries B's import-audit event (transparent re-export) or C verifies an attestation from B's import-audit log — auditability across hops is an explicit data-carriage decision, never free.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
cascading audit requires the predecessor witness in the walkthrough, c can audit b's export but can audit b's earlier import from a only if the witness carries b's import-audit event (transparent re-export) or c verifies an attestation from b's import-audit log — auditability across hops is an explicit data-carriage decision, never free. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ HMAC fixture as a test double for the signed-payload contract

experiment/benchmark

The executable fixture uses dependency-free HMAC-SHA256 authenticators so conformance tests run without external crypto packages; deployment public-key signatures (Ed25519) instantiate the same signed-payload contract but are not measured by the fixture. The evidence-status framing is strict: implementation-versus-specification conformance, not a benchmark of key management or network security.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
hmac fixture as a test double for the signed-payload contract the executable fixture uses dependency-free hmac-sha256 authenticators so conformance tests run without external crypto packages; deployment public-key signatures (ed25519) instantiate the same signed-payload contract but are not measured by the fixture. the evidence-status framing is strict: implementation-versus-specification conformance, not a benchmark of key management or network security. experiment/benchmark papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Eleven deterministic import cases

experiment/benchmark

Honest import; three hard tamper cases (label promotion, content mutation, issuer impersonation); missing_translation and unsafe_translation (amplifying map) rejections; issuer_ceiling_cap and scalar_ceiling_cap (even ext-class foreign records import only at issuer-bounded class and scalar); scalar_lowering_class_repair (contractive reducer drops the scalar below the translated class interval and the stored class is lowered); credibility_fallback and context_fallback (accepted with non-authoritative foreign annotations).

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
eleven deterministic import cases honest import; three hard tamper cases (label promotion, content mutation, issuer impersonation); missing_translation and unsafe_translation (amplifying map) rejections; issuer_ceiling_cap and scalar_ceiling_cap (even ext-class foreign records import only at issuer-bounded class and scalar); scalar_lowering_class_repair (contractive reducer drops the scalar below the translated class interval and the stored class is lowered); credibility_fallback and context_fallback (accepted with non-authoritative foreign annotations). experiment/benchmark papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Six guard-blind negative controls with per-guard failure signatures

result/finding

signature_blind (FA 0.40, signature violations 0.25), issuer_blind (issuer violations 0.17), scalar_blind (scalar violations 0.20 plus FR 0.17), class_selection_blind (class violations 0.33), translation_blind (FA 0.20, translation violations 0.14), provenance_blind (provenance violations 1.00). The proposed rule is zero across all eleven columns.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
six guard-blind negative controls with per-guard failure signatures signature_blind (fa 0.40, signature violations 0.25), issuer_blind (issuer violations 0.17), scalar_blind (scalar violations 0.20 plus fr 0.17), class_selection_blind (class violations 0.33), translation_blind (fa 0.20, translation violations 0.14), provenance_blind (provenance violations 1.00). the proposed rule is zero across all eleven columns. result/finding papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ The scalar_blind diagnostic: a missing cap can also over-reject

result/finding

Disabling scalar capping while keeping class-interval consistency produces a false rejection: the uncapped external scalar cannot be stored under the class forced by the issuer ceiling, so the strict class-selection branch rejects a case the full rule accepts after scalar repair. An intentional demonstration that omitting a guard can cause unsafe acceptance or unnecessary rejection depending on which later guard survives.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
the scalar_blind diagnostic: a missing cap can also over-reject disabling scalar capping while keeping class-interval consistency produces a false rejection: the uncapped external scalar cannot be stored under the class forced by the issuer ceiling, so the strict class-selection branch rejects a case the full rule accepts after scalar repair. an intentional demonstration that omitting a guard can cause unsafe acceptance or unnecessary rejection depending on which later guard survives. result/finding papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ LT randomized + multiseed + mutation evidence

result/finding

20-seed jitter-0.01 sweep: maxima all 0.0 across eleven violation categories. Randomized harness: 500 valid imports all accepted, 500 tampered counterparts all rejected, zero accepted-record violations. Seeded mutation: seven guard-removal mutants (signature, issuer ceiling, scalar bound, class selection, translation certificate, provenance witness, replay uniqueness), 7/7 detected, rate 1.0.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
lt randomized + multiseed + mutation evidence 20-seed jitter-0.01 sweep: maxima all 0.0 across eleven violation categories. randomized harness: 500 valid imports all accepted, 500 tampered counterparts all rejected, zero accepted-record violations. seeded mutation: seven guard-removal mutants (signature, issuer ceiling, scalar bound, class selection, translation certificate, provenance witness, replay uniqueness), 7/7 detected, rate 1.0. result/finding papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Reproducibility manifests with dirty-tree flags

product

Artifact manifests record SHA-256 checksums, reproduction commands, git commit, dirty-tree flag, Python version, and dependency-manifest hash; release manifests must be regenerated from the tagged commit so dirty_tree reads false — a small idea about making "generated in this repository revision" checkable.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex; papers\Time_Varying_Credibility.tex
reproducibility manifests with dirty-tree flags artifact manifests record sha-256 checksums, reproduction commands, git commit, dirty-tree flag, python version, and dependency-manifest hash; release manifests must be regenerated from the tagged commit so dirty_tree reads false — a small idea about making generated in this repository revision checkable. product papers\label_transport_draft.tex; papers\time_varying_credibility.tex

◈ LT limitations as idea seeds

open-question

(1) Content not authenticated — envelopes bind claims, not truth. (2) Over-conservatism is real and unmanaged: the chain provides no automatic mechanism for deciding which issuers earn raised ceilings. (3) Key distribution deliberately unsolved (registry contract compatible with PKI or transparency logs). (4) Translation correctness not adjudicated: a certificate-satisfying but concept-misrepresenting translation cannot be detected by audit replay — over-conservative registration just loses information. (5) Transport-layer attacks (DoS, traffic analysis, cipher downgrade) assumed handled by TLS.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
lt limitations as idea seeds (1) content not authenticated — envelopes bind claims, not truth. (2) over-conservatism is real and unmanaged: the chain provides no automatic mechanism for deciding which issuers earn raised ceilings. (3) key distribution deliberately unsolved (registry contract compatible with pki or transparency logs). (4) translation correctness not adjudicated: a certificate-satisfying but concept-misrepresenting translation cannot be detected by audit replay — over-conservative registration just loses information. (5) transport-layer attacks (dos, traffic analysis, cipher downgrade) assumed handled by tls. open-question papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Import cost model with envelope-identity caching

mechanism

Import costs one signature verification + O(log|R|) registry lookups + O(|W|) witness traversal (dominated by the provenance closure); k-hop composition is O(k(log|R|+|W|)). Repeat imports of the same envelope (checkpoint reloads) can pay only a cache hit keyed by envelope identity in the import-event log.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
import cost model with envelope-identity caching import costs one signature verification + o(log|r|) registry lookups + o(|w|) witness traversal (dominated by the provenance closure); k-hop composition is o(k(log|r|+|w|)). repeat imports of the same envelope (checkpoint reloads) can pay only a cache hit keyed by envelope identity in the import-event log. mechanism papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Composition-without-modification claim

framing/ontology

Label transport composes the four prior layers without modifying any of them: issuer-meet ≙ foundation meet, translation policy ≙ registered analogue of the interval policy, cross-registry attestation ≙ transport-side kernel-registry root, import authorization entry ≙ transport-side bridge to the context authorization log. Framed as engineering specification, not a new theorem.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
composition-without-modification claim label transport composes the four prior layers without modifying any of them: issuer-meet ≙ foundation meet, translation policy ≙ registered analogue of the interval policy, cross-registry attestation ≙ transport-side kernel-registry root, import authorization entry ≙ transport-side bridge to the context authorization log. framed as engineering specification, not a new theorem. framing/ontology papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Construction-time versus replay-time invariants (named distinction)

framing/ontology

Construction-time well-formedness: any record produced by verified import is bounded at the moment of inscription. Replay-time detectability: every stored record either still satisfies the bounds when re-evaluated against current registries or is rejected. Related but distinct; the fixture exercises both; auditors transferring a guarantee to a deployment must keep them separate.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
construction-time versus replay-time invariants (named distinction) construction-time well-formedness: any record produced by verified import is bounded at the moment of inscription. replay-time detectability: every stored record either still satisfies the bounds when re-evaluated against current registries or is rejected. related but distinct; the fixture exercises both; auditors transferring a guarantee to a deployment must keep them separate. framing/ontology papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Social epistemology as the correct analogy stratum for transport

metaphor

The intra-brain source-monitoring analogy is weak for transport (a single nervous system has no cross-agent serialization boundary); the right analogue is one layer up — social epistemology, where a listener stores and re-transmits claims they did not observe and the production-channel tag is the speaker. The component map is claimed to be tighter than the intrapersonal one "because the cross-agent boundary genuinely exists between minds." (This scoping is exactly the discipline the repo's framing-feedback memory node records.)

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
social epistemology as the correct analogy stratum for transport the intra-brain source-monitoring analogy is weak for transport (a single nervous system has no cross-agent serialization boundary); the right analogue is one layer up — social epistemology, where a listener stores and re-transmits claims they did not observe and the production-channel tag is the speaker. the component map is claimed to be tighter than the intrapersonal one because the cross-agent boundary genuinely exists between minds. (this scoping is exactly the discipline the repo's framing-feedback memory node records.) metaphor papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ The eight-row biological/social mapping table

metaphor

Envelope = attributed memory ("who told me, when, how I came to know"); issuer identity/signature = recognizing the speaker; issuer trust ceiling = documented selective trust in testimony (children mistrust previously inaccurate speakers, Koenig–Harris); taxonomy translation = conceptual re-framing across domains (clinical jargon for lay listeners); β(import)=infer = hearsay entering memory tagged "I have heard," not "I observed"; composition non-amplification = telephone-game degradation (a rumor never regains source integrity, only loses it); label stripping = source-memory failure / the misinformation effect (post-event suggestions re-encoded as first-hand); envelope replay = repeated rehearsal of a told story until it acquires the phenomenal character of remembered observation.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
the eight-row biological/social mapping table envelope = attributed memory ( who told me, when, how i came to know ); issuer identity/signature = recognizing the speaker; issuer trust ceiling = documented selective trust in testimony (children mistrust previously inaccurate speakers, koenig–harris); taxonomy translation = conceptual re-framing across domains (clinical jargon for lay listeners); β(import)=infer = hearsay entering memory tagged i have heard, not i observed ; composition non-amplification = telephone-game degradation (a rumor never regains source integrity, only loses it); label stripping = source-memory failure / the misinformation effect (post-event suggestions re-encoded as first-hand); envelope replay = repeated rehearsal of a told story until it acquires the phenomenal character of remembered observation. metaphor papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ The Sarah/Tom layoffs chain

metaphor

A three-hop everyday testimony chain (Tom → Sarah → friend → partner) walked through the formalism: each retelling is an export/import with issuer-meet ceilings from both Sarah and Tom, so the partner's stored representation lands between rumor and inference, never observation. Dropping the "Tom said" indexical is label stripping at a biological boundary.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
the sarah/tom layoffs chain a three-hop everyday testimony chain (tom → sarah → friend → partner) walked through the formalism: each retelling is an export/import with issuer-meet ceilings from both sarah and tom, so the partner's stored representation lands between rumor and inference, never observation. dropping the tom said indexical is label stripping at a biological boundary. metaphor papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ "Software has no excuse": the normative kicker of the analogy

framing/ontology

Brains can lose source tags; an engineered memory system whose witness is explicit data has no comparable excuse. A signed-envelope discipline is valuable precisely because it turns a boundary human cognition handles unreliably into one the system handles by construction.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
"brains can lose source tags, but a memory system implemented in software has no comparable excuse when the witness is explicit data."
software has no excuse : the normative kicker of the analogy brains can lose source tags; an engineered memory system whose witness is explicit data has no comparable excuse. a signed-envelope discipline is valuable precisely because it turns a boundary human cognition handles unreliably into one the system handles by construction. framing/ontology papers\label_transport_draft.tex brains can lose source tags, but a memory system implemented in software has no comparable excuse when the witness is explicit data.

◈ Within-system memory failure = lossy transformation, not forgetting

framing/ontology

The engineered-vs-biological section argues that inside one system nothing erodes bound data on its own; the within-system analogue of memory failure is lossy transformation (summarization, compression, embedding, migration) that drops part of the witness, which must be treated as a new derivation bounded by the weakest verified component — "the meet discipline of this paper redirected inward rather than across a wire." An internal transformation ledger would make it auditable the way envelopes make the wire auditable.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
within-system memory failure = lossy transformation, not forgetting the engineered-vs-biological section argues that inside one system nothing erodes bound data on its own; the within-system analogue of memory failure is lossy transformation (summarization, compression, embedding, migration) that drops part of the witness, which must be treated as a new derivation bounded by the weakest verified component — the meet discipline of this paper redirected inward rather than across a wire. an internal transformation ledger would make it auditable the way envelopes make the wire auditable. framing/ontology papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Future work: bearer-attestation hardening (audience, nonce, validity window)

open-question

The v1 envelope is an intentional bearer attestation — bound to an issuer but not to a recipient, validity window, or single-use nonce; its only replay defense is key validity plus the identity cache. Deployments needing audience-restricted or single-use envelopes should add audience / not_before / not_after / nonce / transport_rule fields bound into the signed payload. Companion hardening: restrict scalar encoding to fixed-point decimals or bounded rationals for security-grade canonicalization.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
future work: bearer-attestation hardening (audience, nonce, validity window) the v1 envelope is an intentional bearer attestation — bound to an issuer but not to a recipient, validity window, or single-use nonce; its only replay defense is key validity plus the identity cache. deployments needing audience-restricted or single-use envelopes should add audience / not_before / not_after / nonce / transport_rule fields bound into the signed payload. companion hardening: restrict scalar encoding to fixed-point decimals or bounded rationals for security-grade canonicalization. open-question papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Future work: multi-agent operational semantics for transport

open-question

A transition system pairing each agent's store with the registries and an in-flight envelope buffer, with export / delivery / import / registry-update / adversary (forge, drop, replay, modify) transitions; the preservation target is composition non-amplification over reachable traces.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
future work: multi-agent operational semantics for transport a transition system pairing each agent's store with the registries and an in-flight envelope buffer, with export / delivery / import / registry-update / adversary (forge, drop, replay, modify) transitions; the preservation target is composition non-amplification over reachable traces. open-question papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Future work: translation-policy synthesis

open-question

Verification of a hand-written translation against a declared upper bound is the implemented finite check; automated synthesis of a translation policy from a taxonomy pair plus a declared μ̂ — reducing human error surface for many-issuer deployments — is the named open problem.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
future work: translation-policy synthesis verification of a hand-written translation against a declared upper bound is the implemented finite check; automated synthesis of a translation policy from a taxonomy pair plus a declared μ̂ — reducing human error surface for many-issuer deployments — is the named open problem. open-question papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Future work: cross-registry attestation wire protocols

open-question

The attestation object and verification relation are fixed, but not a wire protocol for issuing, transporting, and revoking attestations between agents that do not host each other's registries; transparency-log inclusion proofs and verifiable credentials named as starting points.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
future work: cross-registry attestation wire protocols the attestation object and verification relation are fixed, but not a wire protocol for issuing, transporting, and revoking attestations between agents that do not host each other's registries; transparency-log inclusion proofs and verifiable credentials named as starting points. open-question papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Future work: adversarial issuer reputation with a channel/content separation constraint

open-question

Reputation-driven updates could lower issuer ceilings after observed witness/cross-check inconsistencies — but constrained by the chain's core separation: an issuer's source-class ceiling should change only on learning about the issuer's production-channel integrity, never on learning about the content of a particular record.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
future work: adversarial issuer reputation with a channel/content separation constraint reputation-driven updates could lower issuer ceilings after observed witness/cross-check inconsistencies — but constrained by the chain's core separation: an issuer's source-class ceiling should change only on learning about the issuer's production-channel integrity, never on learning about the content of a particular record. open-question papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Future work: reactivation semantics for imported records

open-question

An imported record can become a reactivation candidate on a later local cycle; the interaction of react semantics with transport is undeveloped. Conservative defaults offered: a separate "transported-from-I" class, or inscribe under react with the cross-registry attestation as the reactivation witness.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
future work: reactivation semantics for imported records an imported record can become a reactivation candidate on a later local cycle; the interaction of react semantics with transport is undeveloped. conservative defaults offered: a separate transported-from-i class, or inscribe under react with the cross-registry attestation as the reactivation witness. open-question papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Future work: lossy memory transformations as a chain extension

open-question

Transport is the boundary where a foreign witness becomes local; a sibling boundary appears when a system compresses/summarizes/embeds/dedupes/migrates records. Witness-preserving transformations may retain fields; lossy ones should be modeled as derived inscriptions with class, scalar, credibility, and context recomputed or downgraded under a transformation ceiling — the future-work seed of the Lossy Transformation paper (witness-preserving compression, partial-witness corruption, downgrade rules for compaction).

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
future work: lossy memory transformations as a chain extension transport is the boundary where a foreign witness becomes local; a sibling boundary appears when a system compresses/summarizes/embeds/dedupes/migrates records. witness-preserving transformations may retain fields; lossy ones should be modeled as derived inscriptions with class, scalar, credibility, and context recomputed or downgraded under a transformation ceiling — the future-work seed of the lossy transformation paper (witness-preserving compression, partial-witness corruption, downgrade rules for compaction). open-question papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Related-work positioning: the DIFC exporter analogue

framing/ontology

The closest prior line is decentralized IFC across machines: dynamic security labels carried as runtime data (Zheng–Myers) and DStar's explicit exporter mapping one domain's labels into another's. Non-amplifying translation is read as the integrity-side analogue of such an exporter specialized to a total integrity chain; the claimed novelty is the composite envelope contract tying the translation morphism to a content-addressable registry and per-issuer ceilings so prior audit regimes can be re-verified at import.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
related-work positioning: the difc exporter analogue the closest prior line is decentralized ifc across machines: dynamic security labels carried as runtime data (zheng–myers) and dstar's explicit exporter mapping one domain's labels into another's. non-amplifying translation is read as the integrity-side analogue of such an exporter specialized to a total integrity chain; the claimed novelty is the composite envelope contract tying the translation morphism to a content-addressable registry and per-issuer ceilings so prior audit regimes can be re-verified at import. framing/ontology papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ Related-work positioning: beyond supply-chain attestation

framing/ontology

in-toto/DSSE, SLSA, TUF, and C2PA share the move of making provenance a signed verifiable artifact, and the envelope reuses their canonical-serialization and content-binding discipline. The drawn difference: supply-chain attestations certify that an artifact passed a step, whereas the envelope must carry a typed integrity label, scalar, credibility head, and recorded context, and the verifier must re-apply a meet that bounds those fields rather than only confirming a signature. Closest single frame: "a verifiable credential whose subject is an inscription witness and whose verifier is an integrity-meet rule."

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
related-work positioning: beyond supply-chain attestation in-toto/dsse, slsa, tuf, and c2pa share the move of making provenance a signed verifiable artifact, and the envelope reuses their canonical-serialization and content-binding discipline. the drawn difference: supply-chain attestations certify that an artifact passed a step, whereas the envelope must carry a typed integrity label, scalar, credibility head, and recorded context, and the verifier must re-apply a meet that bounds those fields rather than only confirming a signature. closest single frame: a verifiable credential whose subject is an inscription witness and whose verifier is an integrity-meet rule. framing/ontology papers\label_transport_draft.tex

◈ The chain's compact creed (conclusion formulation)

framing/ontology

The conclusion compresses the entire five-paper chain into one sentence of principles extended to the boundary by identifying the envelope as the inscription witness of the import event.

papers\Label_Transport_draft.tex
"production lineage is fixed, valuation is bounded by support, credibility is revisable but auditable, context binds at inscription, and the meet is the boundary at every layer"
the chain's compact creed (conclusion formulation) the conclusion compresses the entire five-paper chain into one sentence of principles extended to the boundary by identifying the envelope as the inscription witness of the import event. framing/ontology papers\label_transport_draft.tex production lineage is fixed, valuation is bounded by support, credibility is revisable but auditable, context binds at inscription, and the meet is the boundary at every layer

P5b · source-downgrading-extensions — papers, brainstorms, future work 215 ideas

May 2026 — Per-Record Trust, Source Decay, Lossy Transformation, Pre-Inscription, Witness Grounding, Organ Slot, raw brainstorms · P5b-sde-rest.md

Repo-level framing, method, and standing rulings

◈ The meet rule as the non-negotiable invariant

framing/ontology

The whole repo is built around one inherited rule from the base Source Downgrading paper: derived trust cannot exceed input trust, and the act of derivation itself imposes infer as the upper bound — formally src(Inscribe(derived)) <= min(src(r_1..r_n), infer), the integrity-meet of lattice IFC over the source-class chain fabricated < simulation < infer < retrieved < tool < ext. Every extension must preserve it; "if an extension cannot preserve it, the extension design is wrong, not the rule."

README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/ROADMAP.md
"The meet rule is non-negotiable. If an extension cannot preserve it, the extension design is wrong."
the meet rule as the non-negotiable invariant the whole repo is built around one inherited rule from the base source downgrading paper: derived trust cannot exceed input trust, and the act of derivation itself imposes `infer` as the upper bound — formally `src(inscribe(derived)) <= min(src(r_1..r_n), infer)`, the integrity-meet of lattice ifc over the source-class chain `fabricated < simulation < infer < retrieved < tool < ext`. every extension must preserve it; if an extension cannot preserve it, the extension design is wrong, not the rule. framing/ontology readme.md, agents.md, claude.md, docs/roadmap.md the meet rule is non-negotiable. if an extension cannot preserve it, the extension design is wrong.

◈ Extensions enrich the valuation, never the meet

framing/ontology

Design law for the extension chain: the base paper's one-rank-per-class lattice is a deliberate methodological choice to isolate the invariant; each extension is ordered by increasing departure from the static lattice, and each may only enrich the valuation the meet operates on (scalar, time, context, transport), never the meet itself.

docs/ROADMAP.md, README.md
extensions enrich the valuation, never the meet design law for the extension chain: the base paper's one-rank-per-class lattice is a deliberate methodological choice to isolate the invariant; each extension is ordered by increasing departure from the static lattice, and each may only enrich the valuation the meet operates on (scalar, time, context, transport), never the meet itself. framing/ontology docs/roadmap.md, readme.md

◈ Why This Work Exists: the chain as SIMReC's missing foundation

framing/ontology

The papers are explicitly not standalone research products but the foundation scaffolding for SIMReC (Self-Indexing Recursive Memory) — which "sits on foundations nobody has built." Two named missing mechanisms: memory selection (which records survive/route under integrity-bounded writeback) and memory decay (how labels/credibility degrade over time and witness loss without silently rewriting lineage).

CLAUDE.md
"a contribution that does not advance the scaffolding for self-indexing recursive memory is out of scope, even if it is otherwise interesting."
why this work exists: the chain as simrec's missing foundation the papers are explicitly not standalone research products but the foundation scaffolding for simrec (self-indexing recursive memory) — which sits on foundations nobody has built. two named missing mechanisms: memory selection (which records survive/route under integrity-bounded writeback) and memory decay (how labels/credibility degrade over time and witness loss without silently rewriting lineage). framing/ontology claude.md a contribution that does not advance the scaffolding for self-indexing recursive memory is out of scope, even if it is otherwise interesting.

◈ Chain order is load-bearing (and differs from build order)

framing/ontology

The citation chain order (Source Downgrading → Per-Record → Time-Varying → Context-Dependent → Label Transport) encodes which guarantees each paper is allowed to assume, and must never be silently reordered — even though the intra-repo build order was different (Time-Varying landed first). Label Transport is the closing piece because without it cross-agent transport silently launders every prior guarantee.

CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md
chain order is load-bearing (and differs from build order) the citation chain order (source downgrading → per-record → time-varying → context-dependent → label transport) encodes which guarantees each paper is allowed to assume, and must never be silently reordered — even though the intra-repo build order was different (time-varying landed first). label transport is the closing piece because without it cross-agent transport silently launders every prior guarantee. framing/ontology claude.md, agents.md

◈ _pre vs _draft suffix as epistemic status in filenames

mechanism

A naming convention where manuscript maturity is carried in the filename: *_pre.tex = preprint-ready, *_draft.tex = one final polishing pass still expected. Status is thus machine-readable and enforced in agent instructions.

CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md
`_pre` vs `_draft` suffix as epistemic status in filenames a naming convention where manuscript maturity is carried in the filename: `*_pre.tex` = preprint-ready, `*_draft.tex` = one final polishing pass still expected. status is thus machine-readable and enforced in agent instructions. mechanism claude.md, agents.md

◈ Keep four quantities separate

framing/ontology

Standing rule across all code and papers: source integrity (fixed at inscription), credibility (revisable side channel), probability of truth, and routing utility are four distinct quantities that must never be collapsed. Evidence may revise credibility; it must never promote a source label.

AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md
keep four quantities separate standing rule across all code and papers: source integrity (fixed at inscription), credibility (revisable side channel), probability of truth, and routing utility are four distinct quantities that must never be collapsed. evidence may revise credibility; it must never promote a source label. framing/ontology agents.md, claude.md

◈ Deterministic-first experimental methodology

experiment/benchmark

Repository-wide method ordering: deterministic fixtures with explicit ground truth first, multi-seed perturbation second, LLM-in-the-loop work only after invariant tests pass. Every extension lands with a synthetic fixture, a meet-preservation test, and a module docstring naming what it does AND does not do.

AGENTS.md, docs/ROADMAP.md
deterministic-first experimental methodology repository-wide method ordering: deterministic fixtures with explicit ground truth first, multi-seed perturbation second, llm-in-the-loop work only after invariant tests pass. every extension lands with a synthetic fixture, a meet-preservation test, and a module docstring naming what it does and does not do. experiment/benchmark agents.md, docs/roadmap.md

◈ Conformance-artifact manifest pattern

mechanism

Every extension ships an orchestrator benchmark that regenerates JSON summaries plus an artifact manifest recording git commit, dirty-tree flag, Python version, dependency-manifest hash, and per-file SHA-256 — so empirical claims are checksum-pinned and byte-reproducible. 315 tests green at publication.

README.md, papers/Source_Decay_pre.tex
conformance-artifact manifest pattern every extension ships an orchestrator benchmark that regenerates json summaries plus an artifact manifest recording git commit, dirty-tree flag, python version, dependency-manifest hash, and per-file sha-256 — so empirical claims are checksum-pinned and byte-reproducible. 315 tests green at publication. mechanism readme.md, papers/source_decay_pre.tex

◈ The biological-framing ruling (the core memory node)

framing/ontology

When a reviewer pushes a chain paper toward being a self-contained software-systems paper and away from "self-recursive memory primitives," do not just comply — the biological/social-epistemic framing is a deliberate research identity, not decoration. A reviewer reading one paper in isolation cannot see the chain, so it will structurally keep pushing toward a reduced systems paper.

memory/feedback_biological_framing.md (transcript memory)
"the biological/social-epistemic framing is a deliberate choice, not decoration."
the biological-framing ruling (the core memory node) when a reviewer pushes a chain paper toward being a self-contained software-systems paper and away from self-recursive memory primitives, do not just comply — the biological/social-epistemic framing is a deliberate research identity, not decoration. a reviewer reading one paper in isolation cannot see the chain, so it will structurally keep pushing toward a reduced systems paper. framing/ontology memory/feedback_biological_framing.md (transcript memory) the biological/social-epistemic framing is a deliberate choice, not decoration.

◈ The hedge-and-scope discipline for analogies

framing/ontology

How to keep biological framing defensible: (1) hedge every biological section as "design intuition, not evidence; the technical contribution does not depend on it"; (2) scope each paper's analogy to that paper's actual formal boundary (e.g. Label Transport uses social epistemology because its object is a cross-mind boundary; it does not carry the full SIMReC self-indexing thesis). A well-hedged, correctly-scoped biological section is valid; an over-reaching one is not.

memory/feedback_biological_framing.md
the hedge-and-scope discipline for analogies how to keep biological framing defensible: (1) hedge every biological section as design intuition, not evidence; the technical contribution does not depend on it ; (2) scope each paper's analogy to that paper's actual formal boundary (e.g. label transport uses social epistemology because its object is a cross-mind boundary; it does not carry the full simrec self-indexing thesis). a well-hedged, correctly-scoped biological section is valid; an over-reaching one is not. framing/ontology memory/feedback_biological_framing.md

◈ trace-memory: an API where laundering is a type error

product

The public trace_memory library wires the meet rule into the API surface itself: agent.add() without source= raises MissingSourceError, and agent.add_derived() accepts no source keyword at all — the label is computed from inputs, so the unsafe path is unexpressible. Promises: every record labeled, derived records can't launder, retrieval returns fold-force-meaningful memory, belief revisions preserve lineage as correction nodes.

docs/trace-memory-library.md
trace-memory: an api where laundering is a type error the public `trace_memory` library wires the meet rule into the api surface itself: `agent.add()` without `source=` raises `missingsourceerror`, and `agent.add_derived()` accepts no `source` keyword at all — the label is computed from inputs, so the unsafe path is unexpressible. promises: every record labeled, derived records can't launder, retrieval returns fold-force-meaningful memory, belief revisions preserve lineage as correction nodes. product docs/trace-memory-library.md

◈ Self-vs-truth laundering audit and cascade invisibility

mechanism

The trace-memory audit returns paired self-vs-truth metrics designed to detect "cascade-invisibility" — a self-only audit explicitly warns that truth labels are absent and "cannot declare clean," acknowledging that a system auditing itself with its own labels can be blind to cascaded laundering.

docs/trace-memory-library.md
---
