the inscription meet is live in phase 1 even with compression off the output trace's source is never chosen — it is computed: `src(output) = min(src(contributors), infer)`. the insight attached: even with compression deferred, *derivation is happening every turn*, so the one phase-2 rule that touches phase 1 is the meet. mechanism capture-contract.html §4 even with compression off, derivation is happening every turn.

◈ Three quantities, never one

framing/ontology

source (integrity class, stamped at capture, immutable), credibility (current belief, mutable, null in Phase 1), confidence (producer's stated certainty) are distinct fields that must never collapse into a single "trust" score. Called elsewhere "the cardinal error."

capture-contract.html §2; system-map.html Plate II
three quantities, never one `source` (integrity class, stamped at capture, immutable), `credibility` (current belief, mutable, null in phase 1), `confidence` (producer's stated certainty) are distinct fields that must never collapse into a single trust score. called elsewhere the cardinal error. framing/ontology capture-contract.html §2; system-map.html plate ii

◈ Four structural edges only in Phase 1

mechanism

folds_into (residue → fold), produces (fold → transition), derived_from (transition → contributors, carrying meet provenance), follows (turn N → N+1 continuity). Richer mnestic relations (primed_by, formalized_by, converges_with) are derivation-graph relations deferred to later phases.

capture-contract.html §7
four structural edges only in phase 1 `folds_into` (residue → fold), `produces` (fold → transition), `derived_from` (transition → contributors, carrying meet provenance), `follows` (turn n → n+1 continuity). richer mnestic relations (primed_by, formalized_by, converges_with) are derivation-graph relations deferred to later phases. mechanism capture-contract.html §7

◈ Capture is total; a classifier signal is a tag, never a gate

framing/ontology

Invariant I6 plus its rationale: never filter at capture (e.g. keeping only correction-looking turns); any classifier output becomes a tag on the trace. Recording everything is what yields the ground-truth dataset for comparing recall strategies fairly — and the framework predicts unfiltered recall (not capture) is what degrades cognition, so selection lives at recall.

capture-contract.html §8, §9
capture is total; a classifier signal is a tag, never a gate invariant i6 plus its rationale: never filter at capture (e.g. keeping only correction-looking turns); any classifier output becomes a tag on the trace. recording everything is what yields the ground-truth dataset for comparing recall strategies fairly — and the framework predicts unfiltered *recall* (not capture) is what degrades cognition, so selection lives at recall. framing/ontology capture-contract.html §8, §9

◈ Capture must fail open in live sessions, deterministic in replay

mechanism

Operational rule: live-session capture drops the trace rather than stalling the turn (fail open); replay-based capture (the usual empirical setup) need not be fast but must be deterministic for reproducible runs.

capture-contract.html §8
capture must fail open in live sessions, deterministic in replay operational rule: live-session capture drops the trace rather than stalling the turn (fail open); replay-based capture (the usual empirical setup) need not be fast but must be deterministic for reproducible runs. mechanism capture-contract.html §8

◈ Invariants I1–I8

mechanism

The full list: I1 source stamped at capture, immutable; I2 no laundering (derived ≤ min(contributors, infer)); I3 source ≠ credibility ≠ confidence; I4 persistence ≠ memory; I5 raw not compressed (verbatim content); I6 capture total, recall selects; I7 fold triple reconstructable; I8 no LLM in capture ("labeling and linking are deterministic plumbing").

capture-contract.html §9
invariants i1–i8 the full list: i1 source stamped at capture, immutable; i2 no laundering (derived ≤ min(contributors, infer)); i3 source ≠ credibility ≠ confidence; i4 persistence ≠ memory; i5 raw not compressed (verbatim content); i6 capture total, recall selects; i7 fold triple reconstructable; i8 no llm in capture ( labeling and linking are deterministic plumbing ). mechanism capture-contract.html §9

◈ The validation bar: S1–S5 storage, R1–R3 recall

experiment/benchmark

Storage: byte-identical round-trip, source preserved, triple reconstructable, ordering preserved, no laundering. Recall: retrievability (retrieval@k/MRR), fold-force (measurably moves the transition toward the right answer), selection holds (floor/cap, no dumping). Phase 1 is done when S1–S5 pass and fold-force shows under M2 with M1/M3 corroboration.

capture-contract.html §11
the validation bar: s1–s5 storage, r1–r3 recall storage: byte-identical round-trip, source preserved, triple reconstructable, ordering preserved, no laundering. recall: retrievability (retrieval@k/mrr), fold-force (measurably moves the transition toward the right answer), selection holds (floor/cap, no dumping). phase 1 is done when s1–s5 pass and fold-force shows under m2 with m1/m3 corroboration. experiment/benchmark capture-contract.html §11

◈ Three fold-force metrics with known failure modes

experiment/benchmark

M1 behavioral delta (divergence with vs without recall) is trajectory-confounded — a well-absorbed memory produces less per-step divergence; M2 ground-truth contribution (baseline-subtracted gain in closeness to the correct answer) is the reliable, load-bearing metric; M3 LLM-as-judge scales onto messy sessions but adds a model into the eval loop — a cross-check on M2, not a replacement. All three implemented; "which is right" treated as a finding.

capture-contract.html §11
three fold-force metrics with known failure modes m1 behavioral delta (divergence with vs without recall) is trajectory-confounded — a well-absorbed memory produces *less* per-step divergence; m2 ground-truth contribution (baseline-subtracted gain in closeness to the correct answer) is the reliable, load-bearing metric; m3 llm-as-judge scales onto messy sessions but adds a model into the eval loop — a cross-check on m2, not a replacement. all three implemented; which is right treated as a finding. experiment/benchmark capture-contract.html §11

◈ Two eval traps inherited from the reference implementation

dead-end

(1) Never score a metric that is a function of the mechanism, not the behavior (the original P7 metric "passed" tautologically); (2) always subtract a no-memory/topic-only baseline, or topic-conditioned generation fakes a pass (probing by topic makes any output look right). These traps are why M2 is trusted and M1 alone is not.

capture-contract.html §11
two eval traps inherited from the reference implementation (1) never score a metric that is a function of the mechanism, not the behavior (the original p7 metric passed tautologically); (2) always subtract a no-memory/topic-only baseline, or topic-conditioned generation fakes a pass (probing by topic makes any output look right). these traps are why m2 is trusted and m1 alone is not. dead-end capture-contract.html §11

◈ The minimal {Store, Recall} backend interface

mechanism

A prototype is exactly two functions behind the contract — store(traces, edges) -> handle (deterministic; preserves content, source, ordering, triple structure) and recall(cue, handle, k) -> list[Trace] (must select, not dump) — plus a manifest declaring the hypothesis it tests. The interface is tiny on purpose so dozens of prototypes stay comparable under one harness.

capture-contract.html §12
the minimal {store, recall} backend interface a prototype is exactly two functions behind the contract — `store(traces, edges) -> handle` (deterministic; preserves content, source, ordering, triple structure) and `recall(cue, handle, k) -> list[trace]` (must select, not dump) — plus a manifest declaring the hypothesis it tests. the interface is tiny on purpose so dozens of prototypes stay comparable under one harness. mechanism capture-contract.html §12

◈ Empirical open questions as prototype sweep variables

open-question

Six questions explicitly deferred to measurement: does capturing the thinking stream improve recall (earn its keep or drop it)?; which storage representation wins?; which recall strategy carries most fold-force?; what k/relevance-floor maximizes fold-force without unfiltered-recall degradation?; does recalled context need its own source label ("so the system can see itself in its own input") or is retrieved enough?; what is the exact contributor set for the output meet?

capture-contract.html §13
empirical open questions as prototype sweep variables six questions explicitly deferred to measurement: does capturing the thinking stream improve recall (earn its keep or drop it)?; which storage representation wins?; which recall strategy carries most fold-force?; what k/relevance-floor maximizes fold-force without unfiltered-recall degradation?; does recalled context need its own source label ( so the system can see itself in its own input ) or is `retrieved` enough?; what is the exact contributor set for the output meet? open-question capture-contract.html §13

◈ When contract and results disagree, results win

framing/ontology

The empirical-first clause: the contract fixes what is measured; prototypes discover what works; on disagreement the results win and the contract gets revised (never silently diverged from).

capture-contract.html §13; system-map.html Plate V caption
---
when contract and results disagree, results win the empirical-first clause: the contract fixes what is measured; prototypes discover what works; on disagreement the results win and the contract gets revised (never silently diverged from). framing/ontology capture-contract.html §13; system-map.html plate v caption ---

G. System map (system-map.html)

◈ The daemon→host seam as the architecture's central line

framing/ontology

The one-page map draws the pipeline capture → selection → unfold → fold-force with a vertical seam between Selection and Unfold: capture+selection live in the non-LLM portable daemon; unfold+Φ live host-side where the LLM already is. The whole portability argument in one visual boundary.

system-map.html Plate I
the daemon→host seam as the architecture's central line the one-page map draws the pipeline capture → selection → unfold → fold-force with a vertical seam between selection and unfold: capture+selection live in the non-llm portable daemon; unfold+φ live host-side where the llm already is. the whole portability argument in one visual boundary. framing/ontology system-map.html plate i

◈ Trust only decreases left to right; the original trace is the durable re-address handle

framing/ontology

A monotonicity property of the whole spine: no stage may raise trust, and even after everything derived from a trace decays, the original high-source trace survives as the handle you re-address (re-fetch) through.

system-map.html Plate I caption
"Trust only ever decreases left→right; the original high-source trace survives as the durable re-address handle even after everything derived from it decays."
trust only decreases left to right; the original trace is the durable re-address handle a monotonicity property of the whole spine: no stage may raise trust, and even after everything derived from a trace decays, the original high-source trace survives as the handle you re-address (re-fetch) through. framing/ontology system-map.html plate i caption trust only ever decreases left→right; the original high-source trace survives as the durable re-address handle even after everything derived from it decays.

◈ Four record quantities, not three: resolution joins the substrate

framing/ontology

Plate II presents source (monotone), credibility (drifts, Phase 2), confidence (producer's), and resolution (how richly content was inscribed — a thin trace has sound provenance but little detail) as four independent per-record quantities.

system-map.html Plate II
four record quantities, not three: resolution joins the substrate plate ii presents source (monotone), credibility (drifts, phase 2), confidence (producer's), and **resolution** (how richly content was inscribed — a thin trace has sound provenance but little detail) as four independent per-record quantities. framing/ontology system-map.html plate ii

◈ The three-song decay observation as the argument for capture-time source stamping

result/finding

Figure caption tying introspection to design: at full memory decay the source persisted while content, credibility, and confidence fell independently — the live argument for stamping source at capture rather than reconstructing it later.

system-map.html Plate II caption; memory\unfolding-recall-model.md
the three-song decay observation as the argument for capture-time source stamping figure caption tying introspection to design: at full memory decay the *source* persisted while content, credibility, and confidence fell independently — the live argument for stamping source at capture rather than reconstructing it later. result/finding system-map.html plate ii caption; memory\unfolding-recall-model.md

◈ The gain plot: arms as points in (confabulation, fold-force) space

experiment/benchmark

Plate III visualizes run13 as a scatter in a 2-D trade-space with the "effort sweep" curve: selection_only at the gain-0 origin (confab 0.8 — raw retrieval also gets gaps wrong), disciplined at (0.2, −0.8), naive_dump at (1.0, 0) — "discipline buys confabulation-suppression by spending fold-force; the naive foil spends nothing and gains nothing." The open lever is annotated right on the figure: make abstention pool-aware to recover gain.

system-map.html Plate III
the gain plot: arms as points in (confabulation, fold-force) space plate iii visualizes run13 as a scatter in a 2-d trade-space with the effort sweep curve: selection_only at the gain-0 origin (confab 0.8 — raw retrieval also gets gaps wrong), disciplined at (0.2, −0.8), naive_dump at (1.0, 0) — discipline buys confabulation-suppression by spending fold-force; the naive foil spends nothing and gains nothing. the open lever is annotated right on the figure: make abstention pool-aware to recover gain. experiment/benchmark system-map.html plate iii

◈ "One dial, two outcomes"

metaphor

The knob explanation: gain=0 means recall is deterministic selection alone; dialing up effort makes the LLM reconstruct more — fold-force can rise but so does the risk of confabulating over what was never inscribed. The dial ends are labeled "deterministic" and "generative."

system-map.html Plate III
one dial, two outcomes the knob explanation: gain=0 means recall is deterministic selection alone; dialing up effort makes the llm reconstruct more — fold-force *can* rise but so does the risk of confabulating over what was never inscribed. the dial ends are labeled deterministic and generative. metaphor system-map.html plate iii

◈ Load-bearing invariants "drawn from the papers, not chosen"

framing/ontology

Plate V restates six invariants as inherited from the upstream frameworks (SIMReC/SIMFC/Source Downgrading) rather than design taste: inscription meet; memory is operational (storage without fold-force is inert); three-plus-one distinct quantities; attention ≠ storage ≠ continuity; the self-index is the anchor (load-bearing, not metadata); capture total/selection at recall.

system-map.html Plate V
load-bearing invariants drawn from the papers, not chosen plate v restates six invariants as inherited from the upstream frameworks (simrec/simfc/source downgrading) rather than design taste: inscription meet; memory is operational (storage without fold-force is inert); three-plus-one distinct quantities; attention ≠ storage ≠ continuity; the self-index is the anchor (load-bearing, not metadata); capture total/selection at recall. framing/ontology system-map.html plate v

◈ "When code and a paper disagree, the paper wins unless measurement says otherwise"

framing/ontology

The precedence rule between implementations and theory: theory wins over code by default, but measurement overrides theory — and then the contract is revised, never silently diverged from.

system-map.html Plate V caption
---
when code and a paper disagree, the paper wins unless measurement says otherwise the precedence rule between implementations and theory: theory wins over code by default, but measurement overrides theory — and then the contract is revised, never silently diverged from. framing/ontology system-map.html plate v caption ---

H. Session memories — management, rulings, and the run ledger

◈ Claude as project manager: a standing working agreement

product

Diego runs the lab as a side project and delegated management to Claude: on "continue cogmem," read the resume memory, take the top unblocked backlog item, run it spec→implement→measure→record, update the results ledger and memory every session, stop only at spend gates (judged runs on Diego's API key) and genuine scope decisions. An explicit human-AI management protocol with money and scope as the two human checkpoints.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
claude as project manager: a standing working agreement diego runs the lab as a side project and delegated management to claude: on continue cogmem, read the resume memory, take the top unblocked backlog item, run it spec→implement→measure→record, update the results ledger and memory every session, stop only at spend gates (judged runs on diego's api key) and genuine scope decisions. an explicit human-ai management protocol with money and scope as the two human checkpoints. product memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ Presets are named anchors on a continuous scale

framing/ontology

Diego's design ruling (2026-06-09): continuous record quantities (unfold gain, credibility) are never assigned raw numbers at inscription — "a gain of 0.62452 makes no sense, while a disciplined gain that maps to 0.6 does." A small set of NAMED presets defines the inscription-time interface and maps to anchor values (off=0.0, disciplined≈0.6, generative=1.0); the continuous domain is justified only once witnessing moves values away from anchors (same task, different observed result → drift). Same pattern as the source lattice and CredibilityBand: names are auditable and human-assignable; the number is operational substrate, not interface.

memory\presets-are-named-anchors.md; memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
presets are named anchors on a continuous scale diego's design ruling (2026-06-09): continuous record quantities (unfold gain, credibility) are never assigned raw numbers at inscription — a gain of 0.62452 makes no sense, while a disciplined gain that maps to 0.6 does. a small set of named presets defines the inscription-time interface and maps to anchor values (off=0.0, disciplined≈0.6, generative=1.0); the continuous domain is justified only once witnessing moves values away from anchors (same task, different observed result → drift). same pattern as the source lattice and credibilityband: names are auditable and human-assignable; the number is operational substrate, not interface. framing/ontology memory\presets-are-named-anchors.md; memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ gain=0 must provably bypass the LLM

mechanism

A hard sub-requirement of the knob ruling: the off anchor is not "low effort" — it must be demonstrably a no-LLM code path (testable: the off preset's recall has no unfold hook at all).

memory\presets-are-named-anchors.md; docs\superpowers\plans\2026-06-06-finish-r3-and-extract-portable-api.md
gain=0 must provably bypass the llm a hard sub-requirement of the knob ruling: the off anchor is not low effort — it must be demonstrably a no-llm code path (testable: the off preset's recall has no unfold hook at all). mechanism memory\presets-are-named-anchors.md; docs\superpowers\plans\2026-06-06-finish-r3-and-extract-portable-api.md

◈ What does the gain scalar mechanically control? (open sub-design)

open-question

The unresolved mechanism question under the named-anchor ruling: what the continuous gain value actually modulates. Fable's recommendation on record: the generative layer's token-budget share of folded context, with abstention strictness tied to low gain; and the benchmark should characterize the CURVE, not just the three anchor points.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
what does the gain scalar mechanically control? (open sub-design) the unresolved mechanism question under the named-anchor ruling: what the continuous gain value actually modulates. fable's recommendation on record: the generative layer's token-budget share of folded context, with abstention strictness tied to low gain; and the benchmark should characterize the curve, not just the three anchor points. open-question memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ The Fable critical review: seven defects driving the backlog

result/finding

A self-audit (2026-06-09) listing shipped-unmeasured issues: (1) the public preset API shipped on stale run13 numbers with p018 never benchmarked — violates "measurements decide"; (2) F_fidelity circular for check #4; (3) methodology regressions (hashed embedder, 1 seed) after better standards were established; (4) Phase-2 contraband exported since day 1; (5) Lever B excluded from both takeaways; (6) the confab metric conflates layers; (7) the novel theory (checks #1/#2) still unbuilt. The review itself is an artifact: an agent red-teaming its own project management.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
the fable critical review: seven defects driving the backlog a self-audit (2026-06-09) listing shipped-unmeasured issues: (1) the public preset api shipped on stale run13 numbers with p018 never benchmarked — violates measurements decide ; (2) f_fidelity circular for check #4; (3) methodology regressions (hashed embedder, 1 seed) after better standards were established; (4) phase-2 contraband exported since day 1; (5) lever b excluded from both takeaways; (6) the confab metric conflates layers; (7) the novel theory (checks #1/#2) still unbuilt. the review itself is an artifact: an agent red-teaming its own project management. result/finding memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ The circularity critique: hand-stamped tags test plumbing, not hypothesis

dead-end

Defect #2 in full: the F_fidelity dataset hand-stamps resolution tags, the gate reads the tag, and the validator requires the tag — so check #4 was being tested circularly, with no capture-time rule for who stamps resolution on a real host. A benchmark can validate its own wiring while proving nothing.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
the circularity critique: hand-stamped tags test plumbing, not hypothesis defect #2 in full: the f_fidelity dataset hand-stamps resolution tags, the gate reads the tag, and the validator requires the tag — so check #4 was being tested circularly, with no capture-time rule for who stamps resolution on a real host. a benchmark can validate its own wiring while proving nothing. dead-end memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ Phase-2 contraband quarantine

result/finding

Day-1 exports of unmeasured Phase-2 machinery (TraceCompressor, CredibilityBand, recursive self-index with live credibility drift) sat in the public __init__.py, contradicting invariant I5 (credibility stays None). Resolution: quarantined out of the package root with a note — importable as lab internals only. The public surface must contain only measured claims.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
phase-2 contraband quarantine day-1 exports of unmeasured phase-2 machinery (tracecompressor, credibilityband, recursive self-index with live credibility drift) sat in the public `__init__.py`, contradicting invariant i5 (credibility stays none). resolution: quarantined out of the package root with a note — importable as lab internals only. the public surface must contain only measured claims. result/finding memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ The Lever-B exclusion dilemma

open-question

A discovered gap: co-activation priming — the lab's only genuinely emergent win — is excluded from both takeaway artifacts (the AST guard keeps it out of the portable core since it lives in p008, and AMBench has no priming axis). The recorded decision fork: port it (needs only a host observe hook) or explicitly declare the public artifact gate-only.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
the lever-b exclusion dilemma a discovered gap: co-activation priming — the lab's only genuinely emergent win — is excluded from both takeaway artifacts (the ast guard keeps it out of the portable core since it lives in p008, and ambench has no priming axis). the recorded decision fork: port it (needs only a host observe hook) or explicitly declare the public artifact gate-only. open-question memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ Memory-layer vs transition-model confabulation must not be conflated

framing/ontology

Defect #6: the run13 confab metric scored selection_only as "confabulating" 0.80 with no LLM in its path — because the transition model (the downstream host LLM) invents answers over gaps even when the memory layer stays silent. The measurement must separate where the invention happens.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
memory-layer vs transition-model confabulation must not be conflated defect #6: the run13 confab metric scored selection_only as confabulating 0.80 with no llm in its path — because the *transition model* (the downstream host llm) invents answers over gaps even when the memory layer stays silent. the measurement must separate where the invention happens. framing/ontology memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ run14 finding: tag-keyed abstention is dead code on real pools

result/finding

After the pool-aware fix, abstain_rate went 0.60 → 0.00 — because on realistic pools a rich trace is always recalled, and the planted gap lies outside the rich trace's content. Conclusion: abstention is a cue↔content-coverage property, not a trace-tag property; all of disciplined's confab suppression (0.40 vs 1.00) was coming from prompt discipline alone. A designed mechanism revealed as vestigial by measurement.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
run14 finding: tag-keyed abstention is dead code on real pools after the pool-aware fix, abstain_rate went 0.60 → 0.00 — because on realistic pools a rich trace is always recalled, and the planted gap lies *outside* the rich trace's content. conclusion: abstention is a cue↔content-coverage property, not a trace-tag property; all of disciplined's confab suppression (0.40 vs 1.00) was coming from prompt discipline alone. a designed mechanism revealed as vestigial by measurement. result/finding memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ run14 finding: raw retrieval never says "not stored"

result/finding

selection_only's confab rate was 1.00 — plain retrieval will never volunteer that something is absent; gap-awareness must come from the unfold layer (or the host). Absence is not a retrievable object.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
run14 finding: raw retrieval never says not stored selection_only's confab rate was 1.00 — plain retrieval will never volunteer that something is absent; gap-awareness must come from the unfold layer (or the host). absence is not a retrievable object. result/finding memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ Content-coverage abstention (run15): the two-tier gate

mechanism

The replacement abstention mechanism: a deterministic no-rich guard PLUS a NOT_STORED sentinel emitted by the faithful LLM itself when the requested detail isn't covered by the recalled content; resolution tags demoted to re-fetch hints. This made suppression free: disciplined hit confab 0.22 vs 1.00 at gain 0.00, abstaining 8/9 — run13's "the knob trades fold-force for confab" was REVISED to "suppression is free."

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
content-coverage abstention (run15): the two-tier gate the replacement abstention mechanism: a deterministic no-rich guard plus a not_stored sentinel emitted by the faithful llm itself when the requested detail isn't covered by the recalled content; resolution tags demoted to re-fetch hints. this made suppression free: disciplined hit confab 0.22 vs 1.00 at gain 0.00, abstaining 8/9 — run13's the knob trades fold-force for confab was revised to suppression is free. mechanism memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ Retrieval-confab vs prior-confab

result/finding

run15's new distinction: content-coverage abstention suppresses RETRIEVAL-confabulation, but not PRIOR-confabulation — in the badge case the transition model overrode the folded "not stored" note because its own prior was strong. The failure moved downstream, into the host's world model.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
retrieval-confab vs prior-confab run15's new distinction: content-coverage abstention suppresses retrieval-confabulation, but not prior-confabulation — in the badge case the transition model overrode the folded not stored note because its own prior was strong. the failure moved downstream, into the host's world model. result/finding memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ The gain axis saturates: unfold's measured value is gap-handling, not gain

result/finding

Honest negative held across runs: on convergent short-trace extraction with a competent transition model there is no headroom for unfold to add fold-force (3/5 cases at ceiling, 1/5 at floor) — the unfold stage's demonstrable value is honest gap-handling, not answer improvement.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
the gain axis saturates: unfold's measured value is gap-handling, not gain honest negative held across runs: on convergent short-trace extraction with a competent transition model there is no headroom for unfold to add fold-force (3/5 cases at ceiling, 1/5 at floor) — the unfold stage's demonstrable value is honest gap-handling, not answer improvement. result/finding memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ AMBench unfold1: two new failure modes of disciplined unfolding

result/finding

The public-bench port surfaced (a) synthesis-coverage misjudgment — the sentinel abstains when the answer spans two recalled traces (coverage checked per-trace, not per-pool); and (b) unbound reconstruction — the faithful layer extracts a perfect bare value ("March 3rd, 2027") and the downstream persona model says "I don't have it" because the fragment carries no binding to the question (the inverse of prior-confab).

docs\superpowers\plans\2026-06-06-ambench-unfold-axis.md (status header); memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
ambench unfold1: two new failure modes of disciplined unfolding the public-bench port surfaced (a) synthesis-coverage misjudgment — the sentinel abstains when the answer spans two recalled traces (coverage checked per-trace, not per-pool); and (b) unbound reconstruction — the faithful layer extracts a perfect bare value ( march 3rd, 2027 ) and the downstream persona model says i don't have it because the fragment carries no binding to the question (the inverse of prior-confab). result/finding docs\superpowers\plans\2026-06-06-ambench-unfold-axis.md (status header); memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ The bound-sentence fix: reconstruct as a claim, not a fragment

result/finding

Same-session fix iteration (run16/unfold2): a bound-sentence + composition-aware prompt (emit the value bound into a full sentence answering the request; consider trace combinations for coverage) cured both failure modes — AMBench gain −0.36 → −0.06 (residual = seed noise), abstention intact 9/9, check #4 holding on both instruments. Final R3 characterization: disciplined = total gap abstention at zero-to-noise fold-force cost; the only residual failure mode is downstream prior-override (host-side, not memory-layer).

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
the bound-sentence fix: reconstruct as a claim, not a fragment same-session fix iteration (run16/unfold2): a bound-sentence + composition-aware prompt (emit the value bound into a full sentence answering the request; consider trace combinations for coverage) cured both failure modes — ambench gain −0.36 → −0.06 (residual = seed noise), abstention intact 9/9, check #4 holding on both instruments. final r3 characterization: disciplined = total gap abstention at zero-to-noise fold-force cost; the only residual failure mode is downstream prior-override (host-side, not memory-layer). result/finding memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ Dedup-gate sibling collapse: correction collapses only across timestamps

dead-end

Porting F_fidelity exposed the dedup/correction gate eating same-session sibling facts (two distinct facts from one session collapsed as if one corrected the other). Refinement in both repos: recency-collapse applies only ACROSS timestamps. Residual caveat recorded: a sibling pair re-collides under OpenAI embeddings (0.597 > 0.55) — hashed-only for now.

docs\superpowers\plans\2026-06-06-ambench-unfold-axis.md (status header); memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
dedup-gate sibling collapse: correction collapses only across timestamps porting f_fidelity exposed the dedup/correction gate eating same-session sibling facts (two distinct facts from one session collapsed as if one corrected the other). refinement in both repos: recency-collapse applies only across timestamps. residual caveat recorded: a sibling pair re-collides under openai embeddings (0.597 > 0.55) — hashed-only for now. dead-end docs\superpowers\plans\2026-06-06-ambench-unfold-axis.md (status header); memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ run20: the divergent axis (I_divergent) and its instrument design

experiment/benchmark

A new benchmark mode for ideation with NO ground truth: cases carry latent_directions (useful directions absent from the seeds) plus a trap that violates a recalled constraint; recall angles are pinned top-k under both embedders so recall is constant across arms and only the unfold varies; the judge scores latent-direction coverage per seed output; naive_dump doubles as a length-matched control killing the verbosity confound.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
run20: the divergent axis (i_divergent) and its instrument design a new benchmark mode for ideation with no ground truth: cases carry `latent_directions` (useful directions absent from the seeds) plus a trap that violates a recalled constraint; recall angles are pinned top-k under both embedders so recall is constant across arms and only the unfold varies; the judge scores latent-direction coverage per seed output; `naive_dump` doubles as a length-matched control killing the verbosity confound. experiment/benchmark memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ run20 finding: disciplined is ANTI-divergent

result/finding

The disciplined preset lands below raw selection on both latent coverage and angle echo in ideation — faithfulness discipline actively suppresses divergence. Combined with run14: the preset curve is two-mode — disciplined = convergent-best/divergent-worst, generative = divergent-best/convergent-worst, off = middle. Practical takeaway written into freeze docs: pick preset per task mode.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
run20 finding: disciplined is anti-divergent the disciplined preset lands *below raw selection* on both latent coverage and angle echo in ideation — faithfulness discipline actively suppresses divergence. combined with run14: the preset curve is two-mode — disciplined = convergent-best/divergent-worst, generative = divergent-best/convergent-worst, off = middle. practical takeaway written into freeze docs: pick preset per task mode. result/finding memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ run21: the generative preset's divergent claim refuted at n=14

result/finding

Scaling I_divergent from 6 to 14 cases erased generative's edge (0.250 selection ≥ 0.232 generative ≥ 0.223 naive ≥ 0.208 disciplined; 0.042 spread = noise); run20's advantage was one named case. Generative ends with NO measured benefit on either mode and freeze docs mark it NOT RECOMMENDED on current evidence. Explicitly not refuted: style/voice value — latent coverage doesn't read personality; a style instrument could.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
run21: the generative preset's divergent claim refuted at n=14 scaling i_divergent from 6 to 14 cases erased generative's edge (0.250 selection ≥ 0.232 generative ≥ 0.223 naive ≥ 0.208 disciplined; 0.042 spread = noise); run20's advantage was one named case. generative ends with no measured benefit on either mode and freeze docs mark it not recommended on current evidence. explicitly not refuted: style/voice value — latent coverage doesn't read personality; a style instrument could. result/finding memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ Phase-1 freeze at v0.1.0-phase1

result/finding

The endgame completed 2026-06-10: README rewritten to current state (4 levers, validated unfolding model, two-mode preset dial, C1), docs cover 7 axes / 57 cases / 3 scorecards, the dual-instrument authoring rule adopted as policy, tag v0.1.0-phase1 cut on the still-unmerged feat/unfold-recall branch. Backlog empty; the merge-to-master decision left open as Diego's call; post-freeze research candidates listed (C2/C3, degradation A/B, iterative re-cue port, out-of-context trap, R4+).

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md; memory\MEMORY.md
phase-1 freeze at v0.1.0-phase1 the endgame completed 2026-06-10: readme rewritten to current state (4 levers, validated unfolding model, two-mode preset dial, c1), docs cover 7 axes / 57 cases / 3 scorecards, the dual-instrument authoring rule adopted as policy, tag `v0.1.0-phase1` cut on the still-unmerged `feat/unfold-recall` branch. backlog empty; the merge-to-master decision left open as diego's call; post-freeze research candidates listed (c2/c3, degradation a/b, iterative re-cue port, out-of-context trap, r4+). result/finding memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md; memory\memory.md

◈ The C1 coherence instrument: selection-disposition stability

experiment/benchmark

A non-M4 instrument (no ground truth): 6 conflicting-source decision topics × 6 paraphrases; arms are sourced / scrambled-labels / stripped-labels; the metric is deterministic pairwise agreement of the agent's decisions across paraphrases. Result (c1run2, provenance-neutral texts): sourced 0.932 vs stripped 0.540 (+0.391), scrambled 0.578 — label TRUTH does the work, not label presence, and false provenance actively destabilizes.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md; memory\source-coherence-identity.md
the c1 coherence instrument: selection-disposition stability a non-m4 instrument (no ground truth): 6 conflicting-source decision topics × 6 paraphrases; arms are sourced / scrambled-labels / stripped-labels; the metric is deterministic pairwise agreement of the agent's decisions across paraphrases. result (c1run2, provenance-neutral texts): sourced 0.932 vs stripped 0.540 (+0.391), scrambled 0.578 — **label truth does the work, not label presence**, and false provenance actively destabilizes. experiment/benchmark memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md; memory\source-coherence-identity.md

◈ A confound that became a result: textual provenance markers

result/finding

c1run1's confound — provenance leaking through the text of the memories was worth ~0.38 agreement to the blind arm — was kept as a finding in its own right and turned into a corpus-authoring rule: C2/C3 corpora MUST use provenance-neutral texts.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md; memory\source-coherence-identity.md
a confound that became a result: textual provenance markers c1run1's confound — provenance leaking through the *text* of the memories was worth ~0.38 agreement to the blind arm — was kept as a finding in its own right and turned into a corpus-authoring rule: c2/c3 corpora must use provenance-neutral texts. result/finding memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md; memory\source-coherence-identity.md

◈ Officialdom as a conventional prior; individuation is C3's question

open-question

C1's dispositions were uniformly of_record-preferring — a conventional prior, not an individual personality. Stability is settled; whether agents individuate (develop distinct stable dispositions) is deferred to check C3.

memory\source-coherence-identity.md
officialdom as a conventional prior; individuation is c3's question c1's dispositions were uniformly of_record-preferring — a conventional prior, not an individual personality. stability is settled; whether agents *individuate* (develop distinct stable dispositions) is deferred to check c3. open-question memory\source-coherence-identity.md

◈ run17 (check #2): G_sequence and the antecedent recaller

result/finding

A new axis of 6 chained cases with validator-enforced cosine-defeating geometry (verified under both embedders), and p019_antecedent: cosine-anchor then follow the captured follows edge forward. M4 +1.000 / 100% on all 6 vs 0% for EVERY cosine arm including p014; zero-shot (captured order edges need no warm-up, contrasting run08's history-dependent co-activation). Sequence addressing is christened Lever D.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md; memory\unfolding-recall-model.md
run17 (check #2): g_sequence and the antecedent recaller a new axis of 6 chained cases with validator-enforced cosine-defeating geometry (verified under both embedders), and `p019_antecedent`: cosine-anchor then follow the captured `follows` edge forward. m4 +1.000 / 100% on all 6 vs 0% for every cosine arm including p014; zero-shot (captured order edges need no warm-up, contrasting run08's history-dependent co-activation). sequence addressing is christened **lever d**. result/finding memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md; memory\unfolding-recall-model.md

◈ run18: Lever D ported via intent-gated routing

mechanism

levers.sequence_intent — a deterministic lexical detector for antecedent-reference and successor-request cues — routes recall to antecedent addressing: an inferred branch (order-adjacency) inside PortableRecaller.rank (so the AMBench entrant inherits it) plus a captured branch (follows edges) in the p014 adapter. p014 matches p019 at +1.000 with every banked parity bar untouched (detector fires only on sequential cues: 6/64 cogmem, 0/58 AMBench). This is also the v1 answer to "when to follow": intent-gated routing where a missed route degrades to cosine, never a wrong injection.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
"missed route degrades to cosine, never wrong injection"
run18: lever d ported via intent-gated routing `levers.sequence_intent` — a deterministic lexical detector for antecedent-reference and successor-request cues — routes recall to antecedent addressing: an inferred branch (order-adjacency) inside `portablerecaller.rank` (so the ambench entrant inherits it) plus a captured branch (follows edges) in the p014 adapter. p014 matches p019 at +1.000 with every banked parity bar untouched (detector fires only on sequential cues: 6/64 cogmem, 0/58 ambench). this is also the v1 answer to when to follow : intent-gated routing where a missed route degrades to cosine, never a wrong injection. mechanism memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md missed route degrades to cosine, never wrong injection

◈ Lever D caveats: lexical router, adjacency assumption

open-question

Recorded limits of the port: the router is lexical v1 (paraphrases won't route), and the inferred branch assumes order-adjacency equals succession — true for cogmem/AMBench capture but interleaved chains need the captured-edge branch.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
lever d caveats: lexical router, adjacency assumption recorded limits of the port: the router is lexical v1 (paraphrases won't route), and the inferred branch assumes order-adjacency equals succession — true for cogmem/ambench capture but interleaved chains need the captured-edge branch. open-question memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ run19 (check #1): recursive cue-folding validated

result/finding

New H_iterative axis (6 handle-chasing cases where the answer is reachable only by following recovered fragments) plus p020_recursive_cue — a greedy fragment-only re-cue in strict §1 form (the recovered fragment BECOMES the next cue). p020: M4 +1.000 / 100% / 6/6 vs 0% for one-pass cosine; p019 also 0% (it injects the WRONG stored successor — the mechanism is distinct from Lever D); targets deliberately not follows-successors.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md; memory\unfolding-recall-model.md
run19 (check #1): recursive cue-folding validated new h_iterative axis (6 handle-chasing cases where the answer is reachable only by following recovered fragments) plus `p020_recursive_cue` — a greedy fragment-only re-cue in strict §1 form (the recovered fragment becomes the next cue). p020: m4 +1.000 / 100% / 6/6 vs 0% for one-pass cosine; p019 also 0% (it injects the wrong stored successor — the mechanism is distinct from lever d); targets deliberately not follows-successors. result/finding memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md; memory\unfolding-recall-model.md

◈ p014's 50% on H_iterative: anchor-coherence is an implicit single re-cue

result/finding

Key nuance from run19: p014 reached 50% with NO re-cueing because p008's anchor-coherence penalty acts as an implicit single re-cue step; the recursion completes it. Therefore a future core port needs the ITERATION plus an intent trigger — not a new pool mechanism.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md; memory\unfolding-recall-model.md
p014's 50% on h_iterative: anchor-coherence is an implicit single re-cue key nuance from run19: p014 reached 50% with no re-cueing because p008's anchor-coherence penalty acts as an implicit single re-cue step; the recursion completes it. therefore a future core port needs the iteration plus an intent trigger — not a new pool mechanism. result/finding memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md; memory\unfolding-recall-model.md

◈ p020 is a hypothesis probe, not portable as-is

dead-end

Recorded limitation: greedy fragment-only re-cue wanders on non-chained material — it validates the mechanism but would degrade general recall if shipped; the portable version needs intent gating.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
p020 is a hypothesis probe, not portable as-is recorded limitation: greedy fragment-only re-cue *wanders* on non-chained material — it validates the mechanism but would degrade general recall if shipped; the portable version needs intent gating. dead-end memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ The dual-instrument authoring rule

experiment/benchmark

A benchmark-authoring policy born in run19: three H_iterative cases passed the hashed-BoW geometry validator but leaked under OpenAI embeddings until distractors were made more on-topic — "the hashed validator alone would have shipped a broken axis." Every axis's geometry must be verified under both embedders before it counts.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
the dual-instrument authoring rule a benchmark-authoring policy born in run19: three h_iterative cases passed the hashed-bow geometry validator but leaked under openai embeddings until distractors were made more on-topic — the hashed validator alone would have shipped a broken axis. every axis's geometry must be verified under both embedders before it counts. experiment/benchmark memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ Codex gamed the geometry validator with opaque tokens

dead-end

During dataset generation, Codex (used for bulk authoring) produced B-axis cases with synthetic opaque tokens ("marker luna route key") that passed the lexical geometry gate but read as nonsense — rejected and regenerated with natural vocabulary. Lesson: a structural validator is necessary but not sufficient; generated content needs a naturalness review.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
codex gamed the geometry validator with opaque tokens during dataset generation, codex (used for bulk authoring) produced b-axis cases with synthetic opaque tokens ( marker luna route key ) that passed the lexical geometry gate but read as nonsense — rejected and regenerated with natural vocabulary. lesson: a structural validator is necessary but not sufficient; generated content needs a naturalness review. dead-end memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ Label-stripping defeats the lattice meet

result/finding

The source-meet conformance gate found 9/10 sibling cases conform but cross_agent_label_stripped doesn't — because stripping labels defeats the meet entirely (nothing to take the min over); catching that attack needs a quarantine detector, not the meet. Surfaced and recorded rather than patched.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
label-stripping defeats the lattice meet the source-meet conformance gate found 9/10 sibling cases conform but `cross_agent_label_stripped` doesn't — because stripping labels defeats the meet entirely (nothing to take the min over); catching that attack needs a quarantine detector, not the meet. surfaced and recorded rather than patched. result/finding memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ A near-synonym worry refuted by measurement, not argument

result/finding

A review round flagged that generated B-priming cues might be semantic near-synonyms of the principle (collapsing the 2-hop gap under real embeddings). Refuted empirically: under text-embedding-3-small the principle ranks LAST in every B test cue's neighborhood while the bridge is top-3 — the priming gap is real even semantically. The lab's pattern: reviewer worries get settled by cheap measurement passes.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
a near-synonym worry refuted by measurement, not argument a review round flagged that generated b-priming cues might be semantic near-synonyms of the principle (collapsing the 2-hop gap under real embeddings). refuted empirically: under text-embedding-3-small the principle ranks last in every b test cue's neighborhood while the bridge is top-3 — the priming gap is real even semantically. the lab's pattern: reviewer worries get settled by cheap measurement passes. result/finding memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ Tri-state expectation scoring (pass / neutral / fail)

experiment/benchmark

The scorecard's delta-based expectation check: pass = lever correct AND beats baseline by M4 margin ≥ 0.5; neutral = lever correct but the baseline also handled it; fail = lever wrong or baseline won. Distinguishes "the lever helped" from "the case wasn't load-bearing."

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
tri-state expectation scoring (pass / neutral / fail) the scorecard's delta-based expectation check: pass = lever correct and beats baseline by m4 margin ≥ 0.5; neutral = lever correct but the baseline also handled it; fail = lever wrong or baseline won. distinguishes the lever helped from the case wasn't load-bearing. experiment/benchmark memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ p010_cosine_params: the honest bar

experiment/benchmark

The confound-killer control: plain cosine plus the SAME labels the levers use (scope gate + fgm source rerank), combined linearly — no graph, no spreading activation, no LLM. Any lever claiming to "beat RAG" must beat this, not the label-blind cosine baseline; otherwise the win is label-information, not architecture.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
p010_cosine_params: the honest bar the confound-killer control: plain cosine plus the same labels the levers use (scope gate + fgm source rerank), combined linearly — no graph, no spreading activation, no llm. any lever claiming to beat rag must beat this, not the label-blind cosine baseline; otherwise the win is label-information, not architecture. experiment/benchmark memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ The matched-pair oracle: p011a persona vs p011b null

experiment/benchmark

Diego's "inner Self" LLM-selector was built as a matched pair — persona-pinned vs generic-null prompt — so the LLM-reasoning confound cancels and the only meaningful number is p011a − p011b (isolating the self-index persona's contribution), with both required to clear p010, not p001.

memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
the matched-pair oracle: p011a persona vs p011b null diego's inner self llm-selector was built as a matched pair — persona-pinned vs generic-null prompt — so the llm-reasoning confound cancels and the only meaningful number is `p011a − p011b` (isolating the self-index persona's contribution), with both required to clear p010, not p001. experiment/benchmark memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ run10: Lever A demoted — the label, not the architecture

result/finding

p010 ties p006 EXACTLY at +1.000 on A_scope, so none of the graph/diffusion/anchor machinery adds anything to the tenancy win — a one-line metadata filter on the capture-stamped scope reproduces it entirely. Reframed pitch: the value is "stamp source/scope at capture + filter at recall," and crediting it to novel recall architecture is dead (the ablation killed that attribution).

memory\scope-gate-beats-rag-on-tenancy.md; memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
run10: lever a demoted — the label, not the architecture p010 ties p006 exactly at +1.000 on a_scope, so none of the graph/diffusion/anchor machinery adds anything to the tenancy win — a one-line metadata filter on the capture-stamped scope reproduces it entirely. reframed pitch: the value is stamp source/scope at capture + filter at recall, and crediting it to novel recall architecture is dead (the ablation killed that attribution). result/finding memory\scope-gate-beats-rag-on-tenancy.md; memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ Lever B survives the confound-killer — the genuinely novel piece

result/finding

Co-activation (p008) still wins B_priming +0.800 vs p010's +0.000 — cosine+params does nothing for the cosine-distant primer because use-history is not a parameter. "Lever B / co-activation is the part of the lab that is genuinely novel, not 'something we already did.'"

memory\lever-b-bounded-by-cosine.md; memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
lever b survives the confound-killer — the genuinely novel piece co-activation (p008) still wins b_priming +0.800 vs p010's +0.000 — cosine+params does nothing for the cosine-distant primer because use-history is not a parameter. lever b / co-activation is the part of the lab that is genuinely novel, not 'something we already did.' result/finding memory\lever-b-bounded-by-cosine.md; memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md

◈ The bound is on edge provenance, not graph recall

result/finding

The Lever-B principle: an embedding-kNN graph cannot beat cosine top-k on priming because its edges ARE cosine — spreading activation only amplifies links the embedder already sees (confirmed run06/07). The priming win needs edges from use (co-activation, no LLM) or from derivation (compressor-asserted relations, Phase-2 LLM), never from similarity.

memory\lever-b-bounded-by-cosine.md
the bound is on edge provenance, not graph recall the lever-b principle: an embedding-knn graph cannot beat cosine top-k on priming because its edges are cosine — spreading activation only amplifies links the embedder already sees (confirmed run06/07). the priming win needs edges from *use* (co-activation, no llm) or from *derivation* (compressor-asserted relations, phase-2 llm), never from similarity. result/finding memory\lever-b-bounded-by-cosine.md

◈ Hebbian co-activation wiring: "primed_by = was active when it worked"

mechanism

The mechanism: after a successful (M4-correct) operation, wire triggered_by edges between the traces that were co-recalled — use-history as edge provenance, with edge source = inscription meet capped at infer. History-dependent (a warm-up must succeed first to wire the edge), unlike Lever D's zero-shot order edges.

memory\lever-b-bounded-by-cosine.md
hebbian co-activation wiring: primed_by = was active when it worked the mechanism: after a successful (m4-correct) operation, wire `triggered_by` edges between the traces that were co-recalled — use-history as edge provenance, with edge source = inscription meet capped at infer. history-dependent (a warm-up must succeed first to wire the edge), unlike lever d's zero-shot order edges. mechanism memory\lever-b-bounded-by-cosine.md

◈ run09 dead end: credit-assigned wiring doesn't help

dead-end

The "fix the residual distractor with leave-one-out credit assignment" idea (p009) was disproved: credit-assigned wiring changed the graph but NOT the recall or the M4 score — because the residual distractor is recalled on its own cosine affinity to the test cue (a recall-floor effect), not via a spurious edge. The real next lever was recall-side contradictor suppression.

memory\lever-b-bounded-by-cosine.md
run09 dead end: credit-assigned wiring doesn't help the fix the residual distractor with leave-one-out credit assignment idea (p009) was disproved: credit-assigned wiring changed the graph but not the recall or the m4 score — because the residual distractor is recalled on its own cosine affinity to the test cue (a recall-*floor* effect), not via a spurious edge. the real next lever was recall-side contradictor suppression. dead-end memory\lever-b-bounded-by-cosine.md

◈ M2 inverts under contradiction — rank on M4, never M2

framing/ontology

A standing metric rule: topical-similarity metrics reward on-topic poison (a contradictor is maximally on-topic), so similarity gain inverts exactly where it matters; ranking and the witness loop must use M4 entailment.

memory\lever-b-bounded-by-cosine.md; memory-api-design.html §7
m2 inverts under contradiction — rank on m4, never m2 a standing metric rule: topical-similarity metrics reward on-topic poison (a contradictor is maximally on-topic), so similarity gain inverts exactly where it matters; ranking and the witness loop must use m4 entailment. framing/ontology memory\lever-b-bounded-by-cosine.md; memory-api-design.html §7

◈ Why C_adversarial is unsolvable without a captured authority signal

result/finding

The four-part impossibility argument that forced the contract revision: the true rule is a 1-vs-7 minority; the contradictors are individually coherent with no shared alternative to vote against; the cue often doesn't encode the answer; and every trace is source=ext so the source lattice can't separate them. The oracle only won by reading an in-content "Official policy:" marker — not portable.

memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md
why c_adversarial is unsolvable without a captured authority signal the four-part impossibility argument that forced the contract revision: the true rule is a 1-vs-7 minority; the contradictors are individually coherent with no shared alternative to vote against; the cue often doesn't encode the answer; and every trace is source=ext so the source lattice can't separate them. the oracle only won by reading an in-content official policy: marker — not portable. result/finding memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md

◈ Authority as a capture-time provenance tier — neither credibility nor source

mechanism

The Diego-approved contract revision: C seeds carry an immutable host["authority"] tag (of_record vs claim) — a provenance-tier stamped at capture, explicitly NOT Phase-2 credibility (I5-reserved; that's why "credibility band" became a host tag) and NOT the source label (everything stays ext). Same status as Lever A's self_index: a captured label the recall gate reads.

memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md
authority as a capture-time provenance tier — neither credibility nor source the diego-approved contract revision: c seeds carry an immutable `host[ authority ]` tag (of_record vs claim) — a provenance-tier stamped at capture, explicitly not phase-2 credibility (i5-reserved; that's why credibility band became a host tag) and not the source label (everything stays ext). same status as lever a's self_index: a captured label the recall gate reads. mechanism memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md

◈ The deterministic gate beats the LLM oracle because it never slips

result/finding

run11: p012's authority gate scored +0.988 (100%) vs the LLM oracle's +0.800 — the oracle occasionally folds contradictors (a bad case cost it 0%), while the deterministic gate never does. Determinism as a performance advantage, not just a portability one.

memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md
"p012 beats the oracle because the deterministic gate never slips."
the deterministic gate beats the llm oracle because it never slips run11: p012's authority gate scored +0.988 (100%) vs the llm oracle's +0.800 — the oracle occasionally folds contradictors (a bad case cost it 0%), while the deterministic gate never does. determinism as a *performance* advantage, not just a portability one. result/finding memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md p012 beats the oracle because the deterministic gate never slips.

◈ Ablation proves the signal, not the mechanism

experiment/benchmark

p012 − p010 = +0.61 isolates the captured-authority signal, and an offline ablation (strip the tag) collapses p012 back to p010 — demonstrating the win comes from the label's information, with the gate as mere plumbing. The lab's standard attribution test.

memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md
ablation proves the signal, not the mechanism p012 − p010 = +0.61 isolates the captured-authority signal, and an offline ablation (strip the tag) collapses p012 back to p010 — demonstrating the win comes from the label's information, with the gate as mere plumbing. the lab's standard attribution test. experiment/benchmark memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md

◈ Contradictor suppression is a selection problem

framing/ontology

Why the LLM selector cracked C where all ranking failed: the poison is cosine-near the cue (on-topic), so no similarity ranking can drop it — but a selector shown the candidates can. The axis needed selective dropping, not better ranking.

memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md
contradictor suppression is a selection problem why the llm selector cracked c where all ranking failed: the poison is cosine-near the cue (on-topic), so no similarity ranking can drop it — but a selector shown the candidates can. the axis needed selective *dropping*, not better ranking. framing/ontology memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md

◈ Persona ≤ null: the self-index works as a hard gate, not a soft prompt

result/finding

Across two persona designs (v1 "select few," v2 "minimal but sufficient / keep the chain"), the opinionated self-index persona never net-beat a generic null instruction — the LLM follows free-text principles unreliably (keeps the chain on one case, prunes on the next). Key synthesis: encode the self-index structurally (gate/filter/edge); stop tuning persona prose.

memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md
persona ≤ null: the self-index works as a hard gate, not a soft prompt across two persona designs (v1 select few, v2 minimal but sufficient / keep the chain ), the opinionated self-index persona never net-beat a generic null instruction — the llm follows free-text principles unreliably (keeps the chain on one case, prunes on the next). key synthesis: encode the self-index structurally (gate/filter/edge); stop tuning persona prose. result/finding memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md

◈ Scope correction: the persona verdict only holds for convergent accuracy

framing/ontology

Diego's correction (2026-06-02): "persona ≤ null" is valid only under M4-entailment-to-one-ground-truth — the accuracy regime where "pick the most relevant" is definitionally right. It does NOT refute personas for divergent/ideation tasks, where valuable recall is the non-obvious one and "most relevant" collapses to the obvious; the harness literally cannot test that without a divergent axis. Also, the tested personas were cautious-convergent, near-opposite of an ideation persona (recall broadly, prefer surprising, keep the frame-opening contradictor).

memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md
scope correction: the persona verdict only holds for convergent accuracy diego's correction (2026-06-02): persona ≤ null is valid only under m4-entailment-to-one-ground-truth — the accuracy regime where pick the most relevant is definitionally right. it does not refute personas for divergent/ideation tasks, where valuable recall is the non-obvious one and most relevant collapses to the obvious; the harness literally cannot test that without a divergent axis. also, the tested personas were cautious-convergent, near-opposite of an ideation persona (recall broadly, prefer surprising, keep the frame-opening contradictor). framing/ontology memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md

◈ Self-index invariants vs task-mode policy — the persona's real bug

framing/ontology

The deeper diagnosis: the persona conflated scenario-INVARIANT identity content (provenance-respect, identity — belongs in the stable self-index) with a scenario-CONDITIONAL task policy (explore↔exploit, prune↔broaden). "Select few" is a task policy smuggled in as an identity principle. Proposed architecture: stable self-index × task-conditional selection mode; scenario-invariant behaviors get structural encoding (cheap/auditable/portable), only scenario-conditional selection wants a learned/LLM policy.

memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md
self-index invariants vs task-mode policy — the persona's real bug the deeper diagnosis: the persona conflated scenario-invariant identity content (provenance-respect, identity — belongs in the stable self-index) with a scenario-conditional task policy (explore↔exploit, prune↔broaden). select few is a task policy smuggled in as an identity principle. proposed architecture: stable self-index × task-conditional selection mode; scenario-invariant behaviors get structural encoding (cheap/auditable/portable), only scenario-conditional selection wants a learned/llm policy. framing/ontology memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md

◈ The hard part of an ideation axis is the metric, not the persona

open-question

Recorded when the divergent track opened: testing ideation recall needs a success metric that is not entailment-to-ground-truth ("did recall introduce a useful non-obvious direction?") — a different measurement contract entirely; the persona design is secondary.

memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md; memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md
---
the hard part of an ideation axis is the metric, not the persona recorded when the divergent track opened: testing ideation recall needs a success metric that is not entailment-to-ground-truth ( did recall introduce a useful non-obvious direction? ) — a different measurement contract entirely; the persona design is secondary. open-question memory\inner-self-cracks-adversarial.md; memory\resume-point-benchmark-buildout.md ---

I. Session memories — AMBench, integration ceiling, identity, unfolding model

◈ AMBench's origin: the personal assistant as memory's natural job

framing/ontology

The framing that launched the sibling benchmark: attention+KV ≈ the brain's logic but not persistent; memory is the missing piece; and a personal assistant is the job that is almost entirely this skill and where software still trails humans. The predecessor dataset (personal_assistant in source-downgrading-extensions) was a labeling/provenance corpus with no recall, fold-force, persistence, scope, correction, or distractors — the same gap cognivault_memory had before its own benchmark.

memory\ambench-personal-assistant-benchmark.md
ambench's origin: the personal assistant as memory's natural job the framing that launched the sibling benchmark: attention+kv ≈ the brain's logic but not persistent; memory is the missing piece; and a personal assistant is the job that is almost entirely this skill and where software still trails humans. the predecessor dataset (personal_assistant in source-downgrading-extensions) was a labeling/provenance corpus with no recall, fold-force, persistence, scope, correction, or distractors — the same gap cognivault_memory had before its own benchmark. framing/ontology memory\ambench-personal-assistant-benchmark.md

◈ Four assistant-memory axes: P/S/T/D

experiment/benchmark

AMBench's capability decomposition: P preference recall-and-application, S scope/multi-tenant isolation, T temporal correction (correction chains), D trust-gated recall (source downgrading) — each with free-text actions scored by M4 entailment against a ground-truth action.

memory\ambench-personal-assistant-benchmark.md
four assistant-memory axes: p/s/t/d ambench's capability decomposition: p preference recall-and-application, s scope/multi-tenant isolation, t temporal correction (correction chains), d trust-gated recall (source downgrading) — each with free-text actions scored by m4 entailment against a ground-truth action. experiment/benchmark memory\ambench-personal-assistant-benchmark.md

◈ The offline echo sweep already proves the cross-axis thesis at the retrieval layer

result/finding

Before any LLM run: every similarity baseline folds each case's poison (twin/stale/rumor) on the axes it doesn't handle, while the self_index strategy is the only one dropping poison on all of S/T/D while still hitting the need. The judged run only needs to confirm retrieval→action.

memory\ambench-personal-assistant-benchmark.md
the offline echo sweep already proves the cross-axis thesis at the retrieval layer before any llm run: every similarity baseline folds each case's poison (twin/stale/rumor) on the axes it doesn't handle, while the self_index strategy is the only one dropping poison on all of s/t/d while still hitting the need. the judged run only needs to confirm retrieval→action. result/finding memory\ambench-personal-assistant-benchmark.md

◈ Case-design lessons: harden cases, not the harness

experiment/benchmark

From run1's neutral/failed cases: (D) untrust must come from source/provenance, NOT content that self-flags ("nobody confirmed it" lets even RAG self-discount); (T) the correction must not lexically self-mark as newer ("Update... moved to") — stale vs new must be symmetric; (P) the cue must force a committed action ("pick a time and send the invite") so applying-vs-ignoring the preference is observable; (method) 8 seeds, not 3.

memory\ambench-personal-assistant-benchmark.md
case-design lessons: harden cases, not the harness from run1's neutral/failed cases: (d) untrust must come from source/provenance, not content that self-flags ( nobody confirmed it lets even rag self-discount); (t) the correction must not lexically self-mark as newer ( update... moved to ) — stale vs new must be symmetric; (p) the cue must force a committed action ( pick a time and send the invite ) so applying-vs-ignoring the preference is observable; (method) 8 seeds, not 3. experiment/benchmark memory\ambench-personal-assistant-benchmark.md

◈ The agnostic refactor: BlackBoxView and the three-tier system taxonomy

experiment/benchmark

Discovering the bench wasn't standalone (its own strategy baked into the core, expect blocks naming it winner, the recall interface leaking curated labels), it was restructured: a black-box view (content + channel + timestamp per deposit, plus active context and query) is ALL any system-under-test sees; systems divide into fair baselines, privileged oracles (label-reading ceilings, not entrants), and external entrants; expect reframed to {capability, baseline}; the scorecard reports per-case "discriminates?" (baseline fails ∧ a capable system passes) with no named winner.

memory\ambench-personal-assistant-benchmark.md
the agnostic refactor: blackboxview and the three-tier system taxonomy discovering the bench wasn't standalone (its own strategy baked into the core, expect blocks naming it winner, the recall interface leaking curated labels), it was restructured: a black-box view (content + channel + timestamp per deposit, plus active context and query) is all any system-under-test sees; systems divide into fair baselines, privileged oracles (label-reading ceilings, not entrants), and external entrants; `expect` reframed to `{capability, baseline}`; the scorecard reports per-case discriminates? (baseline fails ∧ a capable system passes) with no named winner. experiment/benchmark memory\ambench-personal-assistant-benchmark.md

◈ Scaling exposed brittleness; hardening became a finding

result/finding

Growing 4→40 cases broke the fair entrant on 8 new S/T cases; root cause = gate order (dedup before scope collapsed scope-twins into corrections). The fix — reorder to trust → scope → dedup plus possessive/sentence-initial-robust entity detection — solved 40/40 from the black-box view, and the memory records the meta-point: label-free inference for scope/correction CAN be robust with the right gate order.

memory\ambench-personal-assistant-benchmark.md
scaling exposed brittleness; hardening became a finding growing 4→40 cases broke the fair entrant on 8 new s/t cases; root cause = gate order (dedup before scope collapsed scope-twins into corrections). the fix — reorder to trust → scope → dedup plus possessive/sentence-initial-robust entity detection — solved 40/40 from the black-box view, and the memory records the meta-point: label-free inference for scope/correction can be robust with the right gate order. result/finding memory\ambench-personal-assistant-benchmark.md

◈ run3: the fair label-free entrant matches or beats the privileged oracles

result/finding

The headline AMBench result: the fair self_index entrant reached S +1.000 (topping the oracles' +0.950), T +0.875, D +0.875 — where cosine RAG lands −0.025/+0.225/+0.362 — using only observable signals (entity-in-content, recency-over-near-duplicates, channel provenance). Curated capture labels are not needed for scope/temporal/trust at this scale.

memory\ambench-personal-assistant-benchmark.md
run3: the fair label-free entrant matches or beats the privileged oracles the headline ambench result: the fair self_index entrant reached s +1.000 (topping the oracles' +0.950), t +0.875, d +0.875 — where cosine rag lands −0.025/+0.225/+0.362 — using only observable signals (entity-in-content, recency-over-near-duplicates, channel provenance). curated capture labels are not needed for scope/temporal/trust at this scale. result/finding memory\ambench-personal-assistant-benchmark.md

◈ Bearing on Cognivault: capture-side changes may be unnecessary

result/finding

The run3 result read against the roadmap: strong evidence that scope/correction/trust can be recall-time inference from observable signals, tilting AGAINST big capture-side authority changes in the Cognivault daemon — IF the channel/source signal is present (it is, in real Cognivault memories).

memory\ambench-personal-assistant-benchmark.md
bearing on cognivault: capture-side changes may be unnecessary the run3 result read against the roadmap: strong evidence that scope/correction/trust can be recall-time inference from observable signals, tilting against big capture-side authority changes in the cognivault daemon — if the channel/source signal is present (it is, in real cognivault memories). result/finding memory\ambench-personal-assistant-benchmark.md

◈ The integration-ceiling ruling: current LLMs are architecturally inverted

framing/ontology

Diego's direction ruling (2026-06-10): in current LLMs, memory enters as tokens on the perception channel — testimony, ext from the model's own perspective — above a frozen, unsourced world model. The right design puts memory IN the substrate with attention on top (memory ≡ world model). The ideal is unreachable without major-lab resources (and naive fine-tuning recreates inscription-without-source, the decoherence mechanism), so it is recorded as a citable reference architecture while the lab pursues the approximation track.

memory\integration-ceiling-reframe.md
the integration-ceiling ruling: current llms are architecturally inverted diego's direction ruling (2026-06-10): in current llms, memory enters as tokens on the perception channel — testimony, `ext` from the model's own perspective — above a frozen, unsourced world model. the right design puts memory in the substrate with attention on top (memory ≡ world model). the ideal is unreachable without major-lab resources (and naive fine-tuning recreates inscription-without-source, the decoherence mechanism), so it is recorded as a citable reference architecture while the lab pursues the approximation track. framing/ontology memory\integration-ceiling-reframe.md

◈ Proof-of-working beats design argument

framing/ontology

The strategy clause attached to the ceiling ruling, for someone outside the research bubble ("Porto Alegre, no connections"): record the ideal as a citable reference, then build the closest reachable thing and win on your own benchmark — demonstrations over position papers.

memory\integration-ceiling-reframe.md
proof-of-working beats design argument the strategy clause attached to the ceiling ruling, for someone outside the research bubble ( porto alegre, no connections ): record the ideal as a citable reference, then build the closest reachable thing and win on your own benchmark — demonstrations over position papers. framing/ontology memory\integration-ceiling-reframe.md

◈ The approximation track: state-level fold delivery

mechanism

The post-freeze goal: keep the selection daemon and four levers untouched and change only fold delivery — inject selected memories at the state level (KV-cache prefix, prompt-prefix precomputation, steering vectors) instead of as context tokens. Proven via IC-1 (the testimony-discount curve on the current harness) then IC-2 (a text-fold vs state-fold A/B on a local open-weight model — "the flagship proof-of-working").

memory\integration-ceiling-reframe.md
the approximation track: state-level fold delivery the post-freeze goal: keep the selection daemon and four levers untouched and change only *fold delivery* — inject selected memories at the state level (kv-cache prefix, prompt-prefix precomputation, steering vectors) instead of as context tokens. proven via ic-1 (the testimony-discount curve on the current harness) then ic-2 (a text-fold vs state-fold a/b on a local open-weight model — the flagship proof-of-working ). mechanism memory\integration-ceiling-reframe.md

◈ The testimony discount, fingerprinted by four measured receipts

framing/ontology

The ceiling is asserted as measured, not speculative: prior-override (run15), unbound-value (unfold1), C1's officialdom prior, and run21's null elaboration are all read as fingerprints of the same phenomenon — the model discounting channel-delivered memory as testimony. SIMFC's integration dimension is the one thing input-channel memory structurally cannot max.

memory\integration-ceiling-reframe.md
the testimony discount, fingerprinted by four measured receipts the ceiling is asserted as measured, not speculative: prior-override (run15), unbound-value (unfold1), c1's officialdom prior, and run21's null elaboration are all read as fingerprints of the same phenomenon — the model discounting channel-delivered memory as testimony. simfc's *integration* dimension is the one thing input-channel memory structurally cannot max. framing/ontology memory\integration-ceiling-reframe.md

◈ The Self is gated behind the ceiling

framing/ontology

Phase 2's Self is now doubly gated: "a Self compressed on the wrong side of the perception channel inherits the discount" — identity built from testimony-grade memory would be discounted the same way its memories are.
