boundary paradox: the bookkeeping-self problem a database `user`/`agent` label is not a self-index unless shuffling it changes transition behavior ($\sigma_t > \theta_i$). blocks overgeneration of selfhood. framing/ontology simfc_manuscript.tex

◈ Prediction: storage without fold-force fails recursive continuity tasks

experiment/benchmark

Equal storage + retrieval accuracy; inert-context system vs fold-enabled system. The latter should win on long-horizon adaptation, contradiction repair, policy revision, self-correction.

SIMFC_manuscript.tex
prediction: storage without fold-force fails recursive continuity tasks equal storage + retrieval accuracy; inert-context system vs fold-enabled system. the latter should win on long-horizon adaptation, contradiction repair, policy revision, self-correction. experiment/benchmark simfc_manuscript.tex

◈ Prediction: self-indexing emerges without an explicit self symbol

experiment/benchmark

No programmed self token; observable signs include stable reference to prior commitments, distinguishing own prior state from external info, consistent correction using prior self-errors, clustering around one continuing process.

SIMFC_manuscript.tex
prediction: self-indexing emerges without an explicit self symbol no programmed self token; observable signs include stable reference to prior commitments, distinguishing own prior state from external info, consistent correction using prior self-errors, clustering around one continuing process. experiment/benchmark simfc_manuscript.tex

◈ Prediction: self-index shuffling selectively damages ownership (with clinical antecedents)

experiment/benchmark

Randomizing $\iota_t$ should impair ownership/autobiographical-continuity/goal-persistence more than factual recall. Antecedents cited: patient R.B. (accurate recall not felt as "his own"), schizophrenia ipseity disturbance, Conway & Pleydell-Pearce's "working self" dissociations.

SIMFC_manuscript.tex
prediction: self-index shuffling selectively damages ownership (with clinical antecedents) randomizing $\iota_t$ should impair ownership/autobiographical-continuity/goal-persistence more than factual recall. antecedents cited: patient r.b. (accurate recall not felt as his own ), schizophrenia ipseity disturbance, conway & pleydell-pearce's working self dissociations. experiment/benchmark simfc_manuscript.tex

◈ Prediction: integration ablation fragments agency more than factual recall

experiment/benchmark

Randomizing cross-channel binding edges → local competence with global inconsistency (contradictory plans across contexts, failure to bind events into one frame, weak cross-modal ownership).

SIMFC_manuscript.tex
prediction: integration ablation fragments agency more than factual recall randomizing cross-channel binding edges → local competence with global inconsistency (contradictory plans across contexts, failure to bind events into one frame, weak cross-modal ownership). experiment/benchmark simfc_manuscript.tex

◈ Prediction: recursion without index stability bifurcates identity

experiment/benchmark

High recursive depth + high drift produces competing local self-indices, context-dependent commitments, incompatible autobiographies, cue-dependent agent versions. $\rho\uparrow$ does not imply selfhood$\uparrow$ unless $d_Z<\zeta$.

SIMFC_manuscript.tex
prediction: recursion without index stability bifurcates identity high recursive depth + high drift produces competing local self-indices, context-dependent commitments, incompatible autobiographies, cue-dependent agent versions. $\rho\uparrow$ does not imply selfhood$\uparrow$ unless $d_z<\zeta$. experiment/benchmark simfc_manuscript.tex

◈ Prediction: selective fold weighting beats unrestricted recall

experiment/benchmark

Max persistence/addressability but poor fold selection underperforms filtered context-sensitive re-entry; the critical variable is precision of $\omega_t$, not raw memory volume. Directly challenges naive memory-maximization.

SIMFC_manuscript.tex
prediction: selective fold weighting beats unrestricted recall max persistence/addressability but poor fold selection underperforms filtered context-sensitive re-entry; the critical variable is precision of $\omega_t$, not raw memory volume. directly challenges naive memory-maximization. experiment/benchmark simfc_manuscript.tex

◈ Prediction: operation-memory compression has a conditional optimum

experiment/benchmark

No operation-memory → self-revision fails; sparse/confusable operation-memory → meta-memory overload as pool grows; compression preserving operation types + decision content wins in confusable regime; rich distinctive records with adequate bandwidth degenerate the optimum toward full preservation. Critical variables: confusability $\chi_t$, retrieval margin $M_t$, top-k bandwidth.

SIMFC_manuscript.tex
prediction: operation-memory compression has a conditional optimum no operation-memory → self-revision fails; sparse/confusable operation-memory → meta-memory overload as pool grows; compression preserving operation types + decision content wins in confusable regime; rich distinctive records with adequate bandwidth degenerate the optimum toward full preservation. critical variables: confusability $\chi_t$, retrieval margin $m_t$, top-k bandwidth. experiment/benchmark simfc_manuscript.tex

◈ No-primitive-self engineering assay

experiment/benchmark

The strongest artificial-agent test: give persistence/addressability/recursion/integration but no explicit self token or fixed identity slot; if a stable action-coupled index emerges because it improves prediction/control/compression, SIMFC gains support; if not despite clear $\mathcal{J}_t$ pressure, the emergence functional needs revision.

SIMFC_manuscript.tex
no-primitive-self engineering assay the strongest artificial-agent test: give persistence/addressability/recursion/integration but no explicit self token or fixed identity slot; if a stable action-coupled index emerges because it improves prediction/control/compression, simfc gains support; if not despite clear $\mathcal{j}_t$ pressure, the emergence functional needs revision. experiment/benchmark simfc_manuscript.tex

◈ Related-work: engram / hippocampal-indexing lineage

other

SIMFC lifts the hippocampal index/content split (Teyler-Rudy, Goode et al.) and the optogenetic engram $do$-criterion (Liu et al.) to an operator calculus; Najenson's information-theoretic causal-specificity reformulation is the closest neighbor of $\Delta_t^{mem}$. The self-index is the functional analogue of a hippocampal index at the agent level.

SIMFC_manuscript.tex
related-work: engram / hippocampal-indexing lineage simfc lifts the hippocampal index/content split (teyler-rudy, goode et al.) and the optogenetic engram $do$-criterion (liu et al.) to an operator calculus; najenson's information-theoretic causal-specificity reformulation is the closest neighbor of $\delta_t^{mem}$. the self-index is the functional analogue of a hippocampal index at the agent level. related-work distinction simfc_manuscript.tex

◈ Related-work: self-as-compression precedents (Schmidhuber; self-modeling robotics)

other

Schmidhuber (2009): a self-symbol emerges in a recurrent world-model because encoding the always-present agent improves compression. Bongard-Zykov-Lipson (2006): a four-legged robot inferred its own body structure from actuation-sensation correlations and used the self-variable to plan and recover from damage — physical-hardware support for self-index emergence.

SIMFC_manuscript.tex
related-work: self-as-compression precedents (schmidhuber; self-modeling robotics) schmidhuber (2009): a self-symbol emerges in a recurrent world-model because encoding the always-present agent improves compression. bongard-zykov-lipson (2006): a four-legged robot inferred its own body structure from actuation-sensation correlations and used the self-variable to plan and recover from damage — physical-hardware support for self-index emergence. related-work distinction simfc_manuscript.tex

◈ Limitation: thresholds are not universal constants

other

$\theta_P,\theta_A,\theta_E,\theta_R,\theta_G,\theta_I,\Theta,\zeta$ depend on substrate/task/timescale/measurement; SIMFC gives a structure for defining/testing thresholds, not fixed numbers. Also: divergence-measure and transition-variable choices matter; poor choices miss or overstate fold effects.

SIMFC_manuscript.tex
limitation: thresholds are not universal constants $\theta_p,\theta_a,\theta_e,\theta_r,\theta_g,\theta_i,\theta,\zeta$ depend on substrate/task/timescale/measurement; simfc gives a structure for defining/testing thresholds, not fixed numbers. also: divergence-measure and transition-variable choices matter; poor choices miss or overstate fold effects. limitation simfc_manuscript.tex

◈ Limitation: self-index ≠ moral/legal/social personhood

other

SIMFC formalizes a functional structure; normative classification (personhood, responsibility, moral status) requires additional criteria.

SIMFC_manuscript.tex
---
limitation: self-index ≠ moral/legal/social personhood simfc formalizes a functional structure; normative classification (personhood, responsibility, moral status) requires additional criteria. limitation simfc_manuscript.tex ---

Mnestic–Attentional Field Coupling (MAFC / SIMFC coupling)

◈ Attention and memory are orthogonal but coupled fields

framing/ontology

The fourth formal move beyond prior papers: attention is a present-oriented gain field, memory a retentive induction field; they are functionally non-reducible yet dynamically coupled (both cross-derivatives nonzero). "Orthogonal" means functional non-reducibility, explicitly NOT vector orthogonality or dynamical independence.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
"attention and memory are orthogonal but coupled fields."
attention and memory are orthogonal but coupled fields the fourth formal move beyond prior papers: attention is a present-oriented gain field, memory a retentive induction field; they are functionally non-reducible yet dynamically coupled (both cross-derivatives nonzero). orthogonal means functional non-reducibility, explicitly not vector orthogonality or dynamical independence. framing/ontology mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex attention and memory are orthogonal but coupled fields.

◈ Endogenous attention (the missing feedback)

mechanism

Prior trace work treated attention $A_t$ as an input to trace formation; MAFC makes attention partly endogenous: $A_{t+1}=f(X_{t+1},C_{t+1},\mathcal{H}_t,\bar A_t)$. Memory shapes future attention while attention shapes future memory.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
"attention gates trace formation; memory induces future attention."
endogenous attention (the missing feedback) prior trace work treated attention $a_t$ as an input to trace formation; mafc makes attention partly endogenous: $a_{t+1}=f(x_{t+1},c_{t+1},\mathcal{h}_t,\bar a_t)$. memory shapes future attention while attention shapes future memory. mechanism mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex attention gates trace formation; memory induces future attention.

◈ Residual attention as a distinct state variable

coinage

$\bar A_t$: a decaying attentional posture/aftereffect ($\bar A_{t+1}=\Pi_\Delta(\lambda_A \bar A_t + (1-\lambda_A)A_t)$) that biases later selection/retrieval without being stored, retrieved, or self-indexed. Sub-mnestic — between attention and memory. Coined as "residual attention."

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
residual attention as a distinct state variable $\bar a_t$: a decaying attentional posture/aftereffect ($\bar a_{t+1}=\pi_\delta(\lambda_a \bar a_t + (1-\lambda_a)a_t)$) that biases later selection/retrieval without being stored, retrieved, or self-indexed. sub-mnestic — between attention and memory. coined as residual attention. coinage mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Five theoretical commitments

framing/ontology

(1) attention is not memory; (2) memory is not storage; (3) continuity is not attention; (4) residual attention is a legitimate state variable; (5) the electromagnetic analogy is structural + biophysical, not literal.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
five theoretical commitments (1) attention is not memory; (2) memory is not storage; (3) continuity is not attention; (4) residual attention is a legitimate state variable; (5) the electromagnetic analogy is structural + biophysical, not literal. framing/ontology mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ The six-equation coupled loop

mechanism

$\mathcal{H}_t \to M_t \to A_{t+1} \to Y_{t+1} \to \tau_{t+1} \to \mathcal{H}_{t+1}$: mnestic state reactivates traces; reactivated traces bias attention; attention gates substrate-state; attended state becomes trace; trace updates memory; updated memory biases future attention. A closed feedback loop, not a one-way encoding pipeline. Includes inscription gate $G_t$ and persistence gate $F_t$.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
the six-equation coupled loop $\mathcal{h}_t \to m_t \to a_{t+1} \to y_{t+1} \to \tau_{t+1} \to \mathcal{h}_{t+1}$: mnestic state reactivates traces; reactivated traces bias attention; attention gates substrate-state; attended state becomes trace; trace updates memory; updated memory biases future attention. a closed feedback loop, not a one-way encoding pipeline. includes inscription gate $g_t$ and persistence gate $f_t$. mechanism mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Mnestic-biased retrieval distribution

mechanism

Retrieval augments semantic similarity with four extra pressures: residual-attention match $\beta_A\langle k_i,\bar A_t\rangle$, self-index binding $\beta_Z \iota(r_i,z_t)$, estimated fold-force $\beta_F \hat\phi(r_i)$, and novelty/contradiction $\beta_N \nu(r_i)$. Generalizes top-k RAG.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
mnestic-biased retrieval distribution retrieval augments semantic similarity with four extra pressures: residual-attention match $\beta_a\langle k_i,\bar a_t\rangle$, self-index binding $\beta_z \iota(r_i,z_t)$, estimated fold-force $\beta_f \hat\phi(r_i)$, and novelty/contradiction $\beta_n \nu(r_i)$. generalizes top-k rag. mechanism mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ The electromagnetic (Maxwell-like) analogy

metaphor

Attention is electric-like (active gain field orienting present processing); memory is magnetic-like (retentive induction field generated by trajectories of prior attention). Changing attention inscribes trace; reactivated memory biases attention — mutual induction. Continuous-time field equations with induction operators $C_{M\to A}, C_{A\to M}$, decay $\Gamma$, drive $J$, noise $\eta$.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
"attention is the electric-like selection field; memory is the magnetic-like retentive induction field."
the electromagnetic (maxwell-like) analogy attention is electric-like (active gain field orienting present processing); memory is magnetic-like (retentive induction field generated by trajectories of prior attention). changing attention inscribes trace; reactivated memory biases attention — mutual induction. continuous-time field equations with induction operators $c_{m\to a}, c_{a\to m}$, decay $\gamma$, drive $j$, noise $\eta$. metaphor mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex attention is the electric-like selection field; memory is the magnetic-like retentive induction field.

◈ The analogy as load-bearing hypothesis (not decoration, not identity)

framing/ontology

The structural claim is a committed working hypothesis: mutual induction is the right organizing shape for attention-memory feedback; sequential pipelines match the data only at low loop gain but fail to predict lock-in, novelty breakthrough, echo amplification, bounded stability. Three scope clarifications: Ω need not be physical 3D space; the mapping is one-way (analogy→cognition); no claim cognition obeys Maxwell's equations.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
the analogy as load-bearing hypothesis (not decoration, not identity) the structural claim is a committed working hypothesis: mutual induction is the right organizing shape for attention-memory feedback; sequential pipelines match the data only at low loop gain but fail to predict lock-in, novelty breakthrough, echo amplification, bounded stability. three scope clarifications: ω need not be physical 3d space; the mapping is one-way (analogy→cognition); no claim cognition obeys maxwell's equations. framing/ontology mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Biological correlates as a bridge hypothesis

framing/ontology

Extracellular fields/LFPs, MEG magnetic fields, theta-gamma coupling, LTP/LTD plasticity offered as measurable correlates that could ground the formal variables — explicitly correlates, not identities. Proposed mapping: macro-level neural gain ↔ $A_t$; micro-level synaptic plasticity/trace stabilization ↔ $\mathcal{H}_t$.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
biological correlates as a bridge hypothesis extracellular fields/lfps, meg magnetic fields, theta-gamma coupling, ltp/ltd plasticity offered as measurable correlates that could ground the formal variables — explicitly correlates, not identities. proposed mapping: macro-level neural gain ↔ $a_t$; micro-level synaptic plasticity/trace stabilization ↔ $\mathcal{h}_t$. framing/ontology mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Local stability via spectral radius of the coupled Jacobian

result/finding

Under smooth fixed-topology relaxation (Assumption 1), the system is locally asymptotically stable iff $\rho(J)<1$. Scope explicitly excludes discrete record creation/deletion, retrieval-graph rewires, source-label transitions, and self-index forking (hybrid/piecewise-smooth dynamics).

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
local stability via spectral radius of the coupled jacobian under smooth fixed-topology relaxation (assumption 1), the system is locally asymptotically stable iff $\rho(j)<1$. scope explicitly excludes discrete record creation/deletion, retrieval-graph rewires, source-label transitions, and self-index forking (hybrid/piecewise-smooth dynamics). result/finding mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Scalar loop-gain inequality (coupled-field loop gain)

mechanism

Sufficient stability: $|r_A|<1, |r_H|<1, |g_{h\to a}g_{a\to h}| < (1-|r_A|)(1-|r_H|)$. The product $g_{h\to a}g_{a\to h}$ is the coupled-field loop gain. Healthy cognition requires nonzero loop gain (else memory can't orient attention) but bounded (else lock-in).

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
scalar loop-gain inequality (coupled-field loop gain) sufficient stability: $|r_a|<1, |r_h|<1, |g_{h\to a}g_{a\to h}| < (1-|r_a|)(1-|r_h|)$. the product $g_{h\to a}g_{a\to h}$ is the coupled-field loop gain. healthy cognition requires nonzero loop gain (else memory can't orient attention) but bounded (else lock-in). mechanism mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Attentional lock-in (high-gain pathology)

mechanism

Prior traces bias attention so strongly that novel stimuli cannot break through. Because trace inscription is attention-gated, low attention to novelty → weak trace for novelty → the system fails to encode the novelty strongly enough to influence future attention. Self-reinforcing loop: old trace → biased attention → selective encoding → stronger old trace. "The cognitive analogue of a resonant attractor that converges to a maladaptive state."

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
attentional lock-in (high-gain pathology) prior traces bias attention so strongly that novel stimuli cannot break through. because trace inscription is attention-gated, low attention to novelty → weak trace for novelty → the system fails to *encode* the novelty strongly enough to influence future attention. self-reinforcing loop: old trace → biased attention → selective encoding → stronger old trace. the cognitive analogue of a resonant attractor that converges to a maladaptive state. mechanism mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Novelty breakthrough threshold (softmax-algebraic)

mechanism

For softmax attention (inverse-temp $\beta$, dimension $d$), a novel feature reaches probability $p$ iff its logit margin $\ge \frac{1}{\beta}\log\frac{(d-1)p}{1-p}$. With $\varepsilon$-exploration mixture the required margin is $\Delta_p^\varepsilon$. Gives a constructive assay: increase novelty magnitude in steps and read off the smallest value at which attention crosses $p$ — estimating lock-in strength.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
novelty breakthrough threshold (softmax-algebraic) for softmax attention (inverse-temp $\beta$, dimension $d$), a novel feature reaches probability $p$ iff its logit margin $\ge \frac{1}{\beta}\log\frac{(d-1)p}{1-p}$. with $\varepsilon$-exploration mixture the required margin is $\delta_p^\varepsilon$. gives a constructive assay: increase novelty magnitude in steps and read off the smallest value at which attention crosses $p$ — estimating lock-in strength. mechanism mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Stable-but-permeable dual requirement

framing/ontology

An adaptive system needs both $\rho(J)<1$ (stable under noise) and evidence-permeability (sufficiently strong novelty raises attention above $p$). A maladaptive attractor can be stable; stability alone is not adaptiveness.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
"the system should be stable under noise but permeable to evidence."
stable-but-permeable dual requirement an adaptive system needs both $\rho(j)<1$ (stable under noise) and evidence-permeability (sufficiently strong novelty raises attention above $p$). a maladaptive attractor can be stable; stability alone is not adaptiveness. framing/ontology mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex the system should be stable under noise but permeable to evidence.

◈ Failure mode: mnestic echo amplification

mechanism

Internally reactivated traces re-inscribed as if new external evidence when $\partial G_t/\partial M_t \gg \partial G_t/\partial X_t$ with poor source separation — "the system increasingly remembers its own reactivations rather than the world." Correction: source-sensitive inscription gate with explicit $U_S\cdot \text{Source}_{ext}(X_t,M_t)$ term.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
failure mode: mnestic echo amplification internally reactivated traces re-inscribed as if new external evidence when $\partial g_t/\partial m_t \gg \partial g_t/\partial x_t$ with poor source separation — the system increasingly remembers its own reactivations rather than the world. correction: source-sensitive inscription gate with explicit $u_s\cdot \text{source}_{ext}(x_t,m_t)$ term. mechanism mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Failure mode: novelty thrashing

mechanism

The opposite of lock-in — attention jumps to every new stimulus, no stable trajectory consolidates ($\lambda_A\approx 0$, $r_H\approx 0$, $|W_N|\gg|W_M|$). Interruptible but not continuous.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
failure mode: novelty thrashing the opposite of lock-in — attention jumps to every new stimulus, no stable trajectory consolidates ($\lambda_a\approx 0$, $r_h\approx 0$, $|w_n|\gg|w_m|$). interruptible but not continuous. mechanism mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Failure mode: confirmation attractor

mechanism

A hypothesis becomes an attractor because retrieval + attention repeatedly favor compatible records ($P(r_i\mid q_t,\bar A_t)\to 0$ for records outside the dominant cluster). The failure is in addressability/attentional admission, not storage — contradictory records may exist.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
failure mode: confirmation attractor a hypothesis becomes an attractor because retrieval + attention repeatedly favor compatible records ($p(r_i\mid q_t,\bar a_t)\to 0$ for records outside the dominant cluster). the failure is in addressability/attentional admission, not storage — contradictory records may exist. mechanism mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Failure mode: self-index rigidity

mechanism

Self-index-bound records dominate retrieval ($\beta_Z \gg \beta_N,\beta_C,\beta_F$); the system preserves identity/role consistency at the expense of evidence — continuity without revision.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
failure mode: self-index rigidity self-index-bound records dominate retrieval ($\beta_z \gg \beta_n,\beta_c,\beta_f$); the system preserves identity/role consistency at the expense of evidence — continuity without revision. mechanism mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Failure mode: associative undercoupling

mechanism

Memory-to-attention coupling too weak ($W_M\approx 0, W_{\bar A}\approx 0$): relevant but non-topical memories fail to prime ideation. Matters for creative cognition, where memory is load-bearing not because it matches the query but because it changes posture.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
failure mode: associative undercoupling memory-to-attention coupling too weak ($w_m\approx 0, w_{\bar a}\approx 0$): relevant but non-topical memories fail to prime ideation. matters for creative cognition, where memory is load-bearing not because it matches the query but because it changes posture. mechanism mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Healthy operating regime: bounded mnestic induction

framing/ontology

$0 < |g_{h\to a}g_{a\to h}| < (1-|r_A|)(1-|r_H|)$ with $0\le\lambda_A<1$, $\varepsilon>0$, large-enough $|W_N|$, and $\rho(J)<1$. Enough coupling for memory to shape attention, not enough to imprison it.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
"prior traces should prime attention, not imprison it."
healthy operating regime: bounded mnestic induction $0 < |g_{h\to a}g_{a\to h}| < (1-|r_a|)(1-|r_h|)$ with $0\le\lambda_a<1$, $\varepsilon>0$, large-enough $|w_n|$, and $\rho(j)<1$. enough coupling for memory to shape attention, not enough to imprison it. framing/ontology mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex prior traces should prime attention, not imprison it.

◈ Prediction: residual attention as field-dependent bias (LFP)

experiment/benchmark

Prior attentional orientation should leave measurable LFP/oscillatory signatures that bias subsequent stimulus selection even when the original stimulus is absent. Falsifier: residual-feature LFP states don't predict later feature-class attention above semantic baseline.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
prediction: residual attention as field-dependent bias (lfp) prior attentional orientation should leave measurable lfp/oscillatory signatures that bias subsequent stimulus selection even when the original stimulus is absent. falsifier: residual-feature lfp states don't predict later feature-class attention above semantic baseline. experiment/benchmark mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Prediction: memory-guided attention via theta-gamma coupling

experiment/benchmark

Memory-guided attention strongest under coordinated hippocampal-cortical oscillation. Falsifier: performance independent of theta-gamma coupling indices.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
prediction: memory-guided attention via theta-gamma coupling memory-guided attention strongest under coordinated hippocampal-cortical oscillation. falsifier: performance independent of theta-gamma coupling indices. experiment/benchmark mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Prediction: lock-in as low entropy + reactivation dominance

experiment/benchmark

Lock-in should show $H(A_t)\downarrow$ plus high recurrence of the same mnestic cluster. Falsifier: lock-in without measurable entropy reduction or reactivation-recurrence elevation.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
prediction: lock-in as low entropy + reactivation dominance lock-in should show $h(a_t)\downarrow$ plus high recurrence of the same mnestic cluster. falsifier: lock-in without measurable entropy reduction or reactivation-recurrence elevation. experiment/benchmark mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Prediction: LTP/LTD balance and novelty permeability

experiment/benchmark

Excess potentiation without depression → rigidity; excess depression → weak continuity. Falsifier: plasticity manipulation has no effect on novelty breakthrough at fixed semantic difficulty.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
prediction: ltp/ltd balance and novelty permeability excess potentiation without depression → rigidity; excess depression → weak continuity. falsifier: plasticity manipulation has no effect on novelty breakthrough at fixed semantic difficulty. experiment/benchmark mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Prediction: sleep replay alters future attentional priors

experiment/benchmark

Consolidation should not only improve recall but shift future attentional bias. Falsifier: sleep improves recall without corresponding attentional-bias shift.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
prediction: sleep replay alters future attentional priors consolidation should not only improve recall but shift future attentional bias. falsifier: sleep improves recall without corresponding attentional-bias shift. experiment/benchmark mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Prediction: residual-attention state improves stance-sensitive continuity (agent)

experiment/benchmark

Agents with explicit $\bar A_t$ beat semantic-retrieval-only on stance-sensitive continuity. Ablation: set $\bar A_t=0$.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
prediction: residual-attention state improves stance-sensitive continuity (agent) agents with explicit $\bar a_t$ beat semantic-retrieval-only on stance-sensitive continuity. ablation: set $\bar a_t=0$. experiment/benchmark mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Prediction: mnestic-to-attention ablation reduces long-horizon coherence

experiment/benchmark

$W_M=0$ leaves memories stored/retrievable but no longer biasing attention; long-horizon coherence on memory-guided salience tasks weakens.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
prediction: mnestic-to-attention ablation reduces long-horizon coherence $w_m=0$ leaves memories stored/retrievable but no longer biasing attention; long-horizon coherence on memory-guided salience tasks weakens. experiment/benchmark mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Prediction: attention-to-memory ablation reduces storage selectivity

experiment/benchmark

Removing $U_A,U_{\bar A}$ from $G_t$ makes storage indiscriminate, breaking the link between attentional relevance and trace formation.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
prediction: attention-to-memory ablation reduces storage selectivity removing $u_a,u_{\bar a}$ from $g_t$ makes storage indiscriminate, breaking the link between attentional relevance and trace formation. experiment/benchmark mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Prediction: source-sensitive inscription prevents echo amplification

experiment/benchmark

Vary $\text{Source}_{ext}(X_t,M_t)\to 0$ vs baseline; without source separation agents form self-confirming loops. Falsifier: removing source separation doesn't increase self-confirming inscription or memory-vs-world divergence under adversarial inputs.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
prediction: source-sensitive inscription prevents echo amplification vary $\text{source}_{ext}(x_t,m_t)\to 0$ vs baseline; without source separation agents form self-confirming loops. falsifier: removing source separation doesn't increase self-confirming inscription or memory-vs-world divergence under adversarial inputs. experiment/benchmark mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Prediction: novelty escape improves adaptation under distribution shift

experiment/benchmark

Agents with $\varepsilon>0$ and calibrated $W_N$ recover faster from outdated memories than high-coupling/no-escape agents.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
prediction: novelty escape improves adaptation under distribution shift agents with $\varepsilon>0$ and calibrated $w_n$ recover faster from outdated memories than high-coupling/no-escape agents. experiment/benchmark mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Prediction: excessive loop gain produces lock-in

experiment/benchmark

Increasing $|g_{h\to a}g_{a\to h}|$ toward the bound should reduce attention entropy and novelty breakthrough.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
prediction: excessive loop gain produces lock-in increasing $|g_{h\to a}g_{a\to h}|$ toward the bound should reduce attention entropy and novelty breakthrough. experiment/benchmark mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Matched-interventions table

experiment/benchmark

Seven diagnostic operations (residual-attention erasure, mnestic-to-attention ablation, attention-to-memory ablation, novelty suppression, source-separation ablation, self-index gain manipulation, novelty-breakthrough assay) separating the layers: attention ≠ residual attention ≠ trace ≠ memory ≠ operation-memory ≠ self-indexed memory.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
matched-interventions table seven diagnostic operations (residual-attention erasure, mnestic-to-attention ablation, attention-to-memory ablation, novelty suppression, source-separation ablation, self-index gain manipulation, novelty-breakthrough assay) separating the layers: attention ≠ residual attention ≠ trace ≠ memory ≠ operation-memory ≠ self-indexed memory. experiment/benchmark mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Self-index as engineered metadata (LLM implementation gap)

open-question

In black-box LLM agents $z_t$ is not the emergent SIMFC compression-attractor but engineered metadata (user ID, project ID, role, permission scope, active-commitment slot). Whether engineered $z_t$ converges to an action-coupled emergent index is open. $\iota(r_i,z_t)$ = compatibility score against metadata.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
self-index as engineered metadata (llm implementation gap) in black-box llm agents $z_t$ is not the emergent simfc compression-attractor but engineered metadata (user id, project id, role, permission scope, active-commitment slot). whether engineered $z_t$ converges to an action-coupled emergent index is open. $\iota(r_i,z_t)$ = compatibility score against metadata. open-question mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Residual attention is posture, not recency

mechanismopen-question

$\bar A_t$ must be implemented externally (black-box APIs expose no persistent attention vector) as a finite-dim decayed centroid over salient entities/goals/tools/constraints/failures/discourse-stance; a naive implementation collapses to a recency feature — the architectural commitment is that $\bar A_t$ is posture, bound to task-state variables, not retrieval timestamps.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
residual attention is posture, not recency $\bar a_t$ must be implemented externally (black-box apis expose no persistent attention vector) as a finite-dim decayed centroid over salient entities/goals/tools/constraints/failures/discourse-stance; a naive implementation collapses to a recency feature — the architectural commitment is that $\bar a_t$ is posture, bound to task-state variables, not retrieval timestamps. mechanism / open-question mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Fold-force at retrieval is counterfactual (must be predicted, not measured)

mechanismopen-question

True fold-force is defined by an intervention on a not-yet-occurred transition; at retrieval time $\hat\phi(r_i)$ must be a learned predictor trained on sampled ablation outcomes, with calibration uncertainty propagating into retrieval uncertainty. Two-stage $\beta$ calibration proposed.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
fold-force at retrieval is counterfactual (must be predicted, not measured) true fold-force is defined by an intervention on a not-yet-occurred transition; at retrieval time $\hat\phi(r_i)$ must be a learned predictor trained on sampled ablation outcomes, with calibration uncertainty propagating into retrieval uncertainty. two-stage $\beta$ calibration proposed. open-question / mechanism mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Minimal per-turn logging contract

experiment/benchmarkproduct

Eight structured fields per turn (state snapshot; query + candidates with per-component scores; selection + rejection reasons; fold-force estimate + uncertainty; source label with echo-amplification flag; decision/action + affected variable; sampled post-decision ablation; correction-chain-aligned operation-memory write). This is "the falsifier substrate" — without it ablations can't be scored.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
minimal per-turn logging contract eight structured fields per turn (state snapshot; query + candidates with per-component scores; selection + rejection reasons; fold-force estimate + uncertainty; source label with echo-amplification flag; decision/action + affected variable; sampled post-decision ablation; correction-chain-aligned operation-memory write). this is the falsifier substrate — without it ablations can't be scored. product / experiment/benchmark mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Record-level surprise ν(r_i) ≠ stimulus-level novelty W_N·N_t

mechanism

Distinction: $\nu(r_i)$ measures how much a candidate record disagrees with current state; $W_N N_t$ measures how much the incoming stimulus disagrees with prediction. Both can be active independently.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
record-level surprise ν(r_i) ≠ stimulus-level novelty w_n·n_t distinction: $\nu(r_i)$ measures how much a candidate record disagrees with current state; $w_n n_t$ measures how much the incoming stimulus disagrees with prediction. both can be active independently. mechanism mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Related-work: Adaptive Resonance Theory as closest architectural ancestor

other

ART's stability-plasticity dilemma, vigilance, top-down attention, novelty-triggered category creation is the closest precedent; MAFC differs by being substrate-independent, deriving (not choosing) the novelty threshold, and adding residual-attention + self-index layers. "The conjecture that ART instantiates a special case of the coupled-field regime is open empirical work, not a theorem."

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
related-work: adaptive resonance theory as closest architectural ancestor art's stability-plasticity dilemma, vigilance, top-down attention, novelty-triggered category creation is the closest precedent; mafc differs by being substrate-independent, deriving (not choosing) the novelty threshold, and adding residual-attention + self-index layers. the conjecture that art instantiates a special case of the coupled-field regime is open empirical work, not a theorem. related-work distinction mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Related-work: temporal-context models, communication-through-coherence, predictive coding

other

Howard-Kahana temporal context is the closest precedent for residual attention as a drifting/reinstated state variable; communication-through-coherence overlaps the biological reading; predictive coding says why states should be amplified while MAFC adds how amplified states become future-biasing fields.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
related-work: temporal-context models, communication-through-coherence, predictive coding howard-kahana temporal context is the closest precedent for residual attention as a drifting/reinstated state variable; communication-through-coherence overlaps the biological reading; predictive coding says *why* states should be amplified while mafc adds *how amplified states become future-biasing fields*. related-work distinction mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex

◈ Final theses (boxed)

framing/ontology

"cognitive continuity arises from bounded induction between attention and memory" and "stable enough to remember, plastic enough to be surprised" — coupling too weak loses continuity, too strong causes lock-in, intelligence requires the middle regime.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
"stable enough to remember, plastic enough to be surprised."
final theses (boxed) cognitive continuity arises from bounded induction between attention and memory and stable enough to remember, plastic enough to be surprised — coupling too weak loses continuity, too strong causes lock-in, intelligence requires the middle regime. framing/ontology mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex stable enough to remember, plastic enough to be surprised.

◈ Limitation: local (not global) convergence; substrate-relative thresholds

other

Prop 1 gives only local stability; global convergence needs stronger nonlinear constraints. Thresholds $p, \theta_E, \varepsilon$ and the loop-gain bound are substrate/task-relative. Novelty escape creates a thrashing/lock-in tradeoff a mature architecture must regulate adaptively. A stabilized memory can still be false/stale/poisoned/over-weighted.

Mnestic_Attentional_Field_Coupling.tex
---
limitation: local (not global) convergence; substrate-relative thresholds prop 1 gives only local stability; global convergence needs stronger nonlinear constraints. thresholds $p, \theta_e, \varepsilon$ and the loop-gain bound are substrate/task-relative. novelty escape creates a thrashing/lock-in tradeoff a mature architecture must regulate adaptively. a stabilized memory can still be false/stale/poisoned/over-weighted. limitation mnestic_attentional_field_coupling.tex ---

Trace Before Memory (revised)

◈ Trace as the pre-mnestic primitive of temporal continuity

coinage

Trace = a retained causal residue of a prior substrate-state within the present substrate, without requiring addressability, reportability, retrieval, or self-indexed use. It gives cognition temporal thickness before constituting memory. Disambiguates a term left formally ambiguous across memory theory, neuroscience, RL, and agent design.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
"A trace is a retained causal residue of prior substrate-state; memory is trace made addressable and transition-effective through folding."
trace as the pre-mnestic primitive of temporal continuity trace = a retained causal residue of a prior substrate-state within the present substrate, without requiring addressability, reportability, retrieval, or self-indexed use. it gives cognition temporal thickness before constituting memory. disambiguates a term left formally ambiguous across memory theory, neuroscience, rl, and agent design. coinage trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex a trace is a retained causal residue of prior substrate-state; memory is trace made addressable and transition-effective through folding.

◈ Causal trace: informational + intervention conditions

mechanism

A lag-specific component $\tau_{t\leftarrow t-k}=P_k\tau_t$ is a trace of $Y_{t-k}$ iff it carries detectable mutual information about $Y_{t-k}$ (evidential) AND an admissible intervention on $Y_{t-k}$ (holding the retentive mechanism fixed) changes the component (constitutive). MI alone shows retained dependence but not causal contribution.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
causal trace: informational + intervention conditions a lag-specific component $\tau_{t\leftarrow t-k}=p_k\tau_t$ is a trace of $y_{t-k}$ iff it carries detectable mutual information about $y_{t-k}$ (evidential) and an admissible intervention on $y_{t-k}$ (holding the retentive mechanism fixed) changes the component (constitutive). mi alone shows retained dependence but not causal contribution. mechanism trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Lag-projection operator P_k

mechanism

Because a present trace field can contain residues from several lags, the full field $\tau_t$ is written once and lag-specific components extracted via $P_k$ — avoiding the error of treating the same operator output as independently generated for each lag.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
lag-projection operator p_k because a present trace field can contain residues from several lags, the full field $\tau_t$ is written once and lag-specific components extracted via $p_k$ — avoiding the error of treating the same operator output as independently generated for each lag. mechanism trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Trace-filled substrate vs. zero-trace instantaneous mapping

framing/ontology

A substrate is trace-filled over $[t-n,t]$ if it holds nonzero causal traces of prior attended states across that interval. A zero-trace substrate is an instantaneous input→output map; it may compute but lacks temporal thickness.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
trace-filled substrate vs. zero-trace instantaneous mapping a substrate is trace-filled over $[t-n,t]$ if it holds nonzero causal traces of prior attended states across that interval. a zero-trace substrate is an instantaneous input→output map; it may compute but lacks temporal thickness. framing/ontology trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Six constraints on the trace operator

mechanism

Causality, causal sensitivity (intervention changes a lag component), retention (detectable MI), decay/bounded persistence, content dependence ($\partial\tau_t/\partial Y_{t-k}\neq 0$), and sub-mnestic status (no addressability/fold-force required).

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
six constraints on the trace operator causality, causal sensitivity (intervention changes a lag component), retention (detectable mi), decay/bounded persistence, content dependence ($\partial\tau_t/\partial y_{t-k}\neq 0$), and sub-mnestic status (no addressability/fold-force required). mechanism trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Trace as multidimensional causal convolution

mechanism

General causal trace operator: $\tau(t,\xi)=\int_0^\infty\int_\Omega K_\theta(s,\xi,\xi';C_t)\phi(Y(t-s,\xi'))d\xi' ds$. If the kernel is separable, it decomposes into temporal retention $K_T$ and representational spread $K_\Omega$ (across space/feature/modality/graph/semantics). The temporal dimension is load-bearing.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
trace as multidimensional causal convolution general causal trace operator: $\tau(t,\xi)=\int_0^\infty\int_\omega k_\theta(s,\xi,\xi';c_t)\phi(y(t-s,\xi'))d\xi' ds$. if the kernel is separable, it decomposes into temporal retention $k_t$ and representational spread $k_\omega$ (across space/feature/modality/graph/semantics). the temporal dimension is load-bearing. mechanism trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Convolution-recurrence equivalence (proposition)

result/finding

For an LTI trace operator with exponential kernel $K_k=(1-\lambda)\lambda^k$, the causal convolution obeys the recurrence $\tau_t=\lambda\tau_{t-1}+(1-\lambda)\phi(Y_t)$ — convolutional and recurrent EMA traces are equivalent representations of the same retentive operation (with proof).

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
convolution-recurrence equivalence (proposition) for an lti trace operator with exponential kernel $k_k=(1-\lambda)\lambda^k$, the causal convolution obeys the recurrence $\tau_t=\lambda\tau_{t-1}+(1-\lambda)\phi(y_t)$ — convolutional and recurrent ema traces are equivalent representations of the same retentive operation (with proof). result/finding trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Gated retentive form (context-sensitive, learned)

mechanism

$\tau_t=F_t\odot\tau_{t-1}+G_t\odot\phi(Y_t)$ with forgetting gate $F_t$ and inscription gate $G_t$ conditioned on state/attention/context/error-novelty-salience $e_t$. Close to eligibility-trace and state-dependent RL/neuroscience accounts. Some events decay fast; others persist because surprising, task-relevant, self-relevant, repeated, or tied to reward/error.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
gated retentive form (context-sensitive, learned) $\tau_t=f_t\odot\tau_{t-1}+g_t\odot\phi(y_t)$ with forgetting gate $f_t$ and inscription gate $g_t$ conditioned on state/attention/context/error-novelty-salience $e_t$. close to eligibility-trace and state-dependent rl/neuroscience accounts. some events decay fast; others persist because surprising, task-relevant, self-relevant, repeated, or tied to reward/error. mechanism trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Folding as the transition-making operation

mechanism

$m_t=\text{Fold}(r_t,q_t,C_t,z_t)$ — a fold may select, bind, compress, compare, reconstruct, or transform traces before they enter the next-state update. Distinguished from mere retrieval.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
folding as the transition-making operation $m_t=\text{fold}(r_t,q_t,c_t,z_t)$ — a fold may select, bind, compress, compare, reconstruct, or transform traces before they enter the next-state update. distinguished from mere retrieval. mechanism trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Mnestic act with intervention-route index ι_t

coinage

$\mu_t=(q_t,r_t,m_t,C_t,z_t,\iota_t)$ where $\iota_t$ records the intervention-relevant route by which $r_t$ was selected and folded. The mnestic act — not storage — is the primitive unit of cognitive memory-use.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
mnestic act with intervention-route index ι_t $\mu_t=(q_t,r_t,m_t,c_t,z_t,\iota_t)$ where $\iota_t$ records the intervention-relevant route by which $r_t$ was selected and folded. the mnestic act — not storage — is the primitive unit of cognitive memory-use. coinage trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Non-bookkeeping transition scope

mechanism

A fold counts as cognitive memory only if it changes a non-bookkeeping projection $h\in\mathcal{H}_{cog}$ (action choice, policy, planning, error model, belief, future retrieval weights, contradiction repair, task utility), not merely a log entry "record r was retrieved." A log variable qualifies only if it later affects a non-bookkeeping variable.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
non-bookkeeping transition scope a fold counts as cognitive memory only if it changes a non-bookkeeping projection $h\in\mathcal{h}_{cog}$ (action choice, policy, planning, error model, belief, future retrieval weights, contradiction repair, task utility), not merely a log entry record r was retrieved. a log variable qualifies only if it later affects a non-bookkeeping variable. mechanism trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Bookkeeping-exclusion proposition and storage-insufficiency corollary

result/finding

If a fold changes only a bookkeeping log while all admissible $h$ are unchanged, fold-force is 0 → not cognitive memory. Corollary: two systems with identical stored records and retrieval probabilities differ in memory status if only one lets the folded record alter an admissible transition variable.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
bookkeeping-exclusion proposition and storage-insufficiency corollary if a fold changes only a bookkeeping log while all admissible $h$ are unchanged, fold-force is 0 → not cognitive memory. corollary: two systems with identical stored records and retrieval probabilities differ in memory status if only one lets the folded record alter an admissible transition variable. result/finding trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Six-level retentive hierarchy with isolated-failure signatures

framing/ontology

Trace / storage / addressable trace / folded-trace-memory / operation-memory / self-indexed recursive memory, each with the failure characteristic of that level in isolation (temporal residue without memory; archive without use; retrieval candidate without transition effect; memory-use without recursion; self-correction without stable identity; continuity+ownership when stable, fragmentation when drift high).

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
six-level retentive hierarchy with isolated-failure signatures trace / storage / addressable trace / folded-trace-memory / operation-memory / self-indexed recursive memory, each with the failure characteristic of that level in isolation (temporal residue without memory; archive without use; retrieval candidate without transition effect; memory-use without recursion; self-correction without stable identity; continuity+ownership when stable, fragmentation when drift high). framing/ontology trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Six system-level dimensions + trace-level sub-quantities

mechanism

Persistence, addressability, fold-force, recursive depth, integration span, index-binding. Trace-level quantities feeding into them: intensity ($\|\tau_t\|$), retention timescale ($\lambda$ / kernel $e^{-s/\ell}$), specificity (source-sharpness vs diffusion), diffusion (spread across Ω), fold-readiness (probability of future selection), confusability.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
six system-level dimensions + trace-level sub-quantities persistence, addressability, fold-force, recursive depth, integration span, index-binding. trace-level quantities feeding into them: intensity ($\|\tau_t\|$), retention timescale ($\lambda$ / kernel $e^{-s/\ell}$), specificity (source-sharpness vs diffusion), diffusion (spread across ω), fold-readiness (probability of future selection), confusability. mechanism trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Dimension independence witnesses

result/finding

Minimal constructions proving each dimension can vary while others are held fixed: high-persistence/low-fold-force archive; high-addressability/low-persistence buffer; high-fold-force/low-addressability rare cue; high-recursion/low-integration modules; high-integration/low-self-indexing cross-modal control; high-index-binding/low-factual-breadth narrow autobiographical index.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
dimension independence witnesses minimal constructions proving each dimension can vary while others are held fixed: high-persistence/low-fold-force archive; high-addressability/low-persistence buffer; high-fold-force/low-addressability rare cue; high-recursion/low-integration modules; high-integration/low-self-indexing cross-modal control; high-index-binding/low-factual-breadth narrow autobiographical index. result/finding trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Self-index as equivalence class (not raw token)

framing/ontology

The self-index is defined over equivalence classes $[z]_t=\{z':z'\sim z\}$ of candidate bindings; functional equivalence holds when candidates differ only by relabeling/graph-isomorphism preserving downstream distributions and shuffle-intervention results up to tolerance. Blocks treating an arbitrary raw symbol as the self.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
self-index as equivalence class (not raw token) the self-index is defined over equivalence classes $[z]_t=\{z':z'\sim z\}$ of candidate bindings; functional equivalence holds when candidates differ only by relabeling/graph-isomorphism preserving downstream distributions and shuffle-intervention results up to tolerance. blocks treating an arbitrary raw symbol as the self. framing/ontology trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Drift metric, MDL term, and index utility over classes

mechanism

Drift $d_Z([z_t],[z_{t-1}])=\inf d_{bind}$; description-length $K([z]\mid G_t)$ relative to explicit coding language $\mathcal{L}_Z$ (preventing an ungrounded appeal to complexity); additive index utility $\mathcal{J}_t([z])=\beta_C\text{Comp}+\beta_P\text{Pred}+\beta_O\text{Own}+\beta_R\text{Pol}-\beta_D d_Z -\beta_K K$. The additive form avoids assuming heterogeneous quantities multiply or that one zero nullifies all.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
drift metric, mdl term, and index utility over classes drift $d_z([z_t],[z_{t-1}])=\inf d_{bind}$; description-length $k([z]\mid g_t)$ relative to explicit coding language $\mathcal{l}_z$ (preventing an ungrounded appeal to complexity); additive index utility $\mathcal{j}_t([z])=\beta_c\text{comp}+\beta_p\text{pred}+\beta_o\text{own}+\beta_r\text{pol}-\beta_d d_z -\beta_k k$. the additive form avoids assuming heterogeneous quantities multiply or that one zero nullifies all. mechanism trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Index-shuffle sensitivity and self-indexed-recursive-memory definition

mechanism

$\psi_t([z])=D(P(W_{t+1:t+L}\mid do([z])) \| P(\cdot\mid do(\text{Shuffle}([z]))))$. Self-indexed recursive memory holds when a stable class maximizes $\mathcal{J}_t$, has bounded drift $\le\theta_D$, has shuffle-sensitivity $\ge\theta_Z$, and binds addressable transition-effective operation records. Does not prove a self-index MUST emerge in every recursive system.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
index-shuffle sensitivity and self-indexed-recursive-memory definition $\psi_t([z])=d(p(w_{t+1:t+l}\mid do([z])) \| p(\cdot\mid do(\text{shuffle}([z]))))$. self-indexed recursive memory holds when a stable class maximizes $\mathcal{j}_t$, has bounded drift $\le\theta_d$, has shuffle-sensitivity $\ge\theta_z$, and binds addressable transition-effective operation records. does not prove a self-index must emerge in every recursive system. mechanism trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Conditional top-k interference (binomial proposition)

result/finding

If each additional distractor independently outranks the target with probability $p(q)>0$ and retrieval returns top-k, then $H_q(k,N)=\sum_{j=0}^{k-1}\binom{N-1}{j}p^j(1-p)^{N-1-j}\to 0$ as $N\to\infty$ for fixed $k$; if $p(q)=0$ then $H_q=1$ for all $N$. Overload is a phase boundary set by distinctive retrieval margin, not richness alone.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
conditional top-k interference (binomial proposition) if each additional distractor independently outranks the target with probability $p(q)>0$ and retrieval returns top-k, then $h_q(k,n)=\sum_{j=0}^{k-1}\binom{n-1}{j}p^j(1-p)^{n-1-j}\to 0$ as $n\to\infty$ for fixed $k$; if $p(q)=0$ then $h_q=1$ for all $n$. overload is a phase boundary set by distinctive retrieval margin, not richness alone. result/finding trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Overload defined by finite differences, not derivatives

mechanism

Because $N$ is discrete and changing $N$ changes the distractor distribution, overload is $\Delta_N H(k,N)<-\epsilon_H$ or $\Delta_N E[U]<-\epsilon_U$, not an unqualified $\partial H/\partial N$.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
overload defined by finite differences, not derivatives because $n$ is discrete and changing $n$ changes the distractor distribution, overload is $\delta_n h(k,n)<-\epsilon_h$ or $\delta_n e[u]<-\epsilon_u$, not an unqualified $\partial h/\partial n$. mechanism trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Revised operation-memory principle (specificity-preserving selection)

result/finding

"Unorganized operation-memory degrades under bounded retrieval when records are confusable. Specificity-preserving selection is constitutive under bounded resources. With rich, distinctive records and sufficient bandwidth, full preservation can improve continuity until external storage/latency/context/compute costs dominate." Corrects the naive "forgetting is always constitutive" claim.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
"The load-bearing variable is not richness alone but distinctive retrieval margin."
revised operation-memory principle (specificity-preserving selection) unorganized operation-memory degrades under bounded retrieval when records are confusable. specificity-preserving selection is constitutive under bounded resources. with rich, distinctive records and sufficient bandwidth, full preservation can improve continuity until external storage/latency/context/compute costs dominate. corrects the naive forgetting is always constitutive claim. result/finding trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex the load-bearing variable is not richness alone but distinctive retrieval margin.

◈ Compression objective with admissibility constraints

mechanism

Accuracy-only objective favors $K_{full}$ in rich regimes; resource-sensitive objective reintroduces interior optimum ($-\lambda_S S -\lambda_L L -\lambda_C C$). A compression operator is admissible only if it preserves operation type + decision content: constraint on transition behavior AND on top-k recall degradation.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
compression objective with admissibility constraints accuracy-only objective favors $k_{full}$ in rich regimes; resource-sensitive objective reintroduces interior optimum ($-\lambda_s s -\lambda_l l -\lambda_c c$). a compression operator is admissible only if it preserves operation type + decision content: constraint on transition behavior and on top-k recall degradation. mechanism trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ SIMReC vs SIMFC integration and the seven-line integrated hierarchy

framing/ontology

SIMReC = the broad recursive-memory thesis (systems gain recursive self-revision when prior memory-use operations re-enter later computation); SIMFC = the formal calculus (residues, graph, fold operators, self-indexing). Trace is the missing base layer for both. Integrated pipeline: $Y_t=A_t\odot X_t \to \tau_t \to r_t \to m_t \to W_{t+1} \to o_t \to [z]_{t+1}$.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
simrec vs simfc integration and the seven-line integrated hierarchy simrec = the broad recursive-memory thesis (systems gain recursive self-revision when prior memory-use operations re-enter later computation); simfc = the formal calculus (residues, graph, fold operators, self-indexing). trace is the missing base layer for both. integrated pipeline: $y_t=a_t\odot x_t \to \tau_t \to r_t \to m_t \to w_{t+1} \to o_t \to [z]_{t+1}$. framing/ontology trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Recursion does not require logging every act (narrow regress claim)

framing/ontology

A system need not record every act of recording to be recursive; it suffices that prior memory-use operations relevant to future self-revision are stored and foldable. Uncompressed logging becomes a resource/retrieval-interference problem, not an infinite regress that follows from recursion alone.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
recursion does not require logging every act (narrow regress claim) a system need not record every act of recording to be recursive; it suffices that prior memory-use operations relevant to future self-revision are stored and foldable. uncompressed logging becomes a resource/retrieval-interference problem, not an infinite regress that follows from recursion alone. framing/ontology trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Matched-intervention semantics (seven-row table)

experiment/benchmark

Trace erasure, storage ablation, addressability blocking, fold ablation, operation-memory ablation, integration randomization, index shuffling — each with the operation and the diagnostic contrast. Also identifies rejected countermodels (a database label is not a self-index; a retrieval log is not memory unless it affects a non-bookkeeping projection).

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
matched-intervention semantics (seven-row table) trace erasure, storage ablation, addressability blocking, fold ablation, operation-memory ablation, integration randomization, index shuffling — each with the operation and the diagnostic contrast. also identifies rejected countermodels (a database label is not a self-index; a retrieval log is not memory unless it affects a non-bookkeeping projection). experiment/benchmark trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Prediction: zero-trace limitation

experiment/benchmark

Matched agents with identical feedforward computation but no internal trace should fail continuity-across-delay tasks unless prior state is externally re-presented.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
prediction: zero-trace limitation matched agents with identical feedforward computation but no internal trace should fail continuity-across-delay tasks unless prior state is externally re-presented. experiment/benchmark trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Prediction: trace-memory dissociation

experiment/benchmark

Short-lived retentive dynamics without addressability → immediate context sensitivity but failed later retrieval/report, distinguishing trace from stored memory.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
prediction: trace-memory dissociation short-lived retentive dynamics without addressability → immediate context sensitivity but failed later retrieval/report, distinguishing trace from stored memory. experiment/benchmark trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Prediction: folding predicts adaptive memory-use

experiment/benchmark

With stored records + retrieval held constant, agents that fold selected records into policy/planning/error-correction beat agents that merely display/concatenate/log without transition influence.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
prediction: folding predicts adaptive memory-use with stored records + retrieval held constant, agents that fold selected records into policy/planning/error-correction beat agents that merely display/concatenate/log without transition influence. experiment/benchmark trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Prediction: integration and index dissociation

experiment/benchmark

Integration ablation impairs cross-context coherence more than local recall; index shuffling impairs ownership/continuity/policy/self-revision more than non-index factual retrieval.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
prediction: integration and index dissociation integration ablation impairs cross-context coherence more than local recall; index shuffling impairs ownership/continuity/policy/self-revision more than non-index factual retrieval. experiment/benchmark trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Prediction: conditional operation-memory overload

experiment/benchmark

Under bounded top-k, sparse/confusable operation records show falling target rank, lower margin, lower hit rate as $N$ grows; rich distinctive records maintain margins and avoid overload over the same range unless resource costs dominate.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
prediction: conditional operation-memory overload under bounded top-k, sparse/confusable operation records show falling target rank, lower margin, lower hit rate as $n$ grows; rich distinctive records maintain margins and avoid overload over the same range unless resource costs dominate. experiment/benchmark trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Prediction: qualified self-index emergence

experiment/benchmark

An agent with persistence/addressability/operation-memory/integration but no explicit self slot develops a stable action-coupled index class when such a class improves compression/prediction/control; if the environment is modular and cross-context binding provides no benefit, local recursive memory can exist without a global self-index.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
prediction: qualified self-index emergence an agent with persistence/addressability/operation-memory/integration but no explicit self slot develops a stable action-coupled index class when such a class improves compression/prediction/control; if the environment is modular and cross-context binding provides no benefit, local recursive memory can exist without a global self-index. experiment/benchmark trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Five-condition operation-memory assay (P7 conditional test)

experiment/benchmark

Compare matched agents: (1) no operation-memory; (2) sparse/confusable; (3) compressed preserving operation type + decision content; (4) full rich/distinctive; (5) aggressive lossy compression removing decision content. Tasks: delayed response, context-dependent planning, contradiction repair, policy revision, long-horizon self-correction, continuity under distractors. Report record schema, embedding method, top-k, target rank, similarity margin, distractor distribution.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
five-condition operation-memory assay (p7 conditional test) compare matched agents: (1) no operation-memory; (2) sparse/confusable; (3) compressed preserving operation type + decision content; (4) full rich/distinctive; (5) aggressive lossy compression removing decision content. tasks: delayed response, context-dependent planning, contradiction repair, policy revision, long-horizon self-correction, continuity under distractors. report record schema, embedding method, top-k, target rank, similarity margin, distractor distribution. experiment/benchmark trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Five-architecture minimal trace-hierarchy assay

experiment/benchmark

no-trace feedforward; trace-only recurrent; storage-without-fold; fold-enabled memory; recursive+self-index agent — with matched record volume, query distribution, top-k bandwidth, non-memory capacity (else improvement may be scale, not the trace/fold distinction).

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
five-architecture minimal trace-hierarchy assay no-trace feedforward; trace-only recurrent; storage-without-fold; fold-enabled memory; recursive+self-index agent — with matched record volume, query distribution, top-k bandwidth, non-memory capacity (else improvement may be scale, not the trace/fold distinction). experiment/benchmark trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Biological assay as approximate dissociation

experiment/benchmark

Short-delay tasks (passive retention), interference (decay/specificity), retrieval-cue manipulation (addressability), recall-induced divergence (fold-force), ownership/autobiographical-continuity (index-binding). Frame biological claims as approximate assays, not surgical tests; direct variable→observable mapping is an empirical bridge, not a completed derivation.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
biological assay as approximate dissociation short-delay tasks (passive retention), interference (decay/specificity), retrieval-cue manipulation (addressability), recall-induced divergence (fold-force), ownership/autobiographical-continuity (index-binding). frame biological claims as approximate assays, not surgical tests; direct variable→observable mapping is an empirical bridge, not a completed derivation. experiment/benchmark trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Related-work distinctions (trace level)

other

Distinct from classical memory-systems theory (asks what must exist before any such system), levels-of-processing (a lower retentive layer beneath controlled encoding), working-memory theory (passive trace vs controlled maintenance), neural state-dependent accounts (abstracts from mechanism), GWT/IIT (no identification with consciousness), predictive processing (a retentive layer conditioning future inference), causal modeling (explicitly interventionist), narrative theories of self (self-index is a functional binding class, not a story).

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
related-work distinctions (trace level) distinct from classical memory-systems theory (asks what must exist *before* any such system), levels-of-processing (a lower retentive layer beneath controlled encoding), working-memory theory (passive trace vs controlled maintenance), neural state-dependent accounts (abstracts from mechanism), gwt/iit (no identification with consciousness), predictive processing (a retentive layer conditioning future inference), causal modeling (explicitly interventionist), narrative theories of self (self-index is a functional binding class, not a story). related-work distinction trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex

◈ Limitations (trace)

other

Substrate/task-relative thresholds; MI only evidential (overcounts in deterministic high-dim systems without intervention/coarse-graining/task-relevance); trace necessary but not sufficient for memory/selfhood/consciousness; convolutional model a first approximation; self-index existence conditional; compression claim bounded-resource-relative; no normative personhood claims.

Trace_Formalization_SIMReC_SIMFC_revised.tex
---
limitations (trace) substrate/task-relative thresholds; mi only evidential (overcounts in deterministic high-dim systems without intervention/coarse-graining/task-relevance); trace necessary but not sufficient for memory/selfhood/consciousness; convolutional model a first approximation; self-index existence conditional; compression claim bounded-resource-relative; no normative personhood claims. limitation trace_formalization_simrec_simfc_revised.tex ---

Recursive Self-Indexed Correction Chains (SIRAC / correction chains)

◈ World models compound through recursively self-indexed correction chains

framing/ontology

The central claim: a world model does not compound from isolated memories but from correction chains that record what the agent previously believed, what disrupted the belief, how it was revised, whether the revision improved behavior, and how the revision stays bound to the same continuing agent.

Recursive_Self_Indexed_Correction_Chains.tex
"A world model does not compound from isolated memories; it compounds from recursively self-indexed correction chains."
world models compound through recursively self-indexed correction chains the central claim: a world model does not compound from isolated memories but from correction chains that record what the agent previously believed, what disrupted the belief, how it was revised, whether the revision improved behavior, and how the revision stays bound to the same continuing agent. framing/ontology recursive_self_indexed_correction_chains.tex a world model does not compound from isolated memories; it compounds from recursively self-indexed correction chains.

◈ Three conflated functions: attention / storage / continuity

framing/ontology

Attention selects among available representations; storage preserves records; continuity determines which prior traces remain causally available. Current agents (longer context, appended history, summaries, RAG, tiered buffers) conflate these; the paper adds a fourth construct on top.

Recursive_Self_Indexed_Correction_Chains.tex
three conflated functions: attention / storage / continuity attention selects among available representations; storage preserves records; continuity determines which prior traces remain causally available. current agents (longer context, appended history, summaries, rag, tiered buffers) conflate these; the paper adds a fourth construct on top. framing/ontology recursive_self_indexed_correction_chains.tex

◈ Five-level hierarchy: trace → memory → recursive memory → self-indexed memory → correction chain

framing/ontology

A restated hierarchy culminating in the recursive self-indexed correction chain as the compounding mechanism that distinguishes a passive store from an evolving world model.

Recursive_Self_Indexed_Correction_Chains.tex
five-level hierarchy: trace → memory → recursive memory → self-indexed memory → correction chain a restated hierarchy culminating in the recursive self-indexed correction chain as the compounding mechanism that distinguishes a passive store from an evolving world model. framing/ontology recursive_self_indexed_correction_chains.tex

◈ Correction-chain node schema (8 fields)

mechanism

Each node $v_i=(B_{i-1}, e_i, u_i, B_i, \Delta_i, \iota_i, p_i, q_i)$ = prior belief, evidence/prediction-error, update operation, revised belief, model delta, self-index binding, provenance, confidence. Edges link by dependency, contradiction, abstraction, reinforcement, revision. World model evolves $W_{i+1}=U(W_i,\Delta_i,\mathcal{C}_i)$.

Recursive_Self_Indexed_Correction_Chains.tex
correction-chain node schema (8 fields) each node $v_i=(b_{i-1}, e_i, u_i, b_i, \delta_i, \iota_i, p_i, q_i)$ = prior belief, evidence/prediction-error, update operation, revised belief, model delta, self-index binding, provenance, confidence. edges link by dependency, contradiction, abstraction, reinforcement, revision. world model evolves $w_{i+1}=u(w_i,\delta_i,\mathcal{c}_i)$. mechanism recursive_self_indexed_correction_chains.tex

◈ Preserving the lineage of corrections as first-class structure

framing/ontology

In predictive-coding terms $e_i$ is prediction error and $u_i$ the update, but the chain preserves the history of updates as a first-class object rather than collapsing them into the current posterior. "$W$ improves not merely because the agent stores more data, but because it preserves the lineage of its own corrections."

Recursive_Self_Indexed_Correction_Chains.tex
preserving the lineage of corrections as first-class structure in predictive-coding terms $e_i$ is prediction error and $u_i$ the update, but the chain preserves the *history* of updates as a first-class object rather than collapsing them into the current posterior. $w$ improves not merely because the agent stores more data, but because it preserves the lineage of its own corrections. framing/ontology recursive_self_indexed_correction_chains.tex

◈ Recursive memory = "I believed A, encountered B, applied update C, now believe D"

framing/ontology

Recursive memory records the update path (belief → evidence → update-operation → revised belief), not merely the result — memory of memory-forming operations.

Recursive_Self_Indexed_Correction_Chains.tex
"I believed $A$. I encountered evidence $B$. I applied update operation $C$. I now believe $D$."
recursive memory = i believed a, encountered b, applied update c, now believe d recursive memory records the *update path* (belief → evidence → update-operation → revised belief), not merely the result — memory of memory-forming operations. framing/ontology recursive_self_indexed_correction_chains.tex i believed $a$. i encountered evidence $b$. i applied update operation $c$. i now believe $d$.

◈ Self-indexed memory binds a correction to a continuing responsibility structure

framing/ontology

"This correction belongs to this agent, in this role, with this action history, under this responsibility structure." The self-index need not imply human-like consciousness; it is a stable binding function over agent states, actions, obligations, policies, memory updates.

Recursive_Self_Indexed_Correction_Chains.tex
self-indexed memory binds a correction to a continuing responsibility structure this correction belongs to this agent, in this role, with this action history, under this responsibility structure. the self-index need not imply human-like consciousness; it is a stable binding function over agent states, actions, obligations, policies, memory updates. framing/ontology recursive_self_indexed_correction_chains.tex

◈ Situated world model requires belief/error/correction history

framing/ontology

Pretraining gives world priors, but an agent's world model must be situated: what happened, what the agent did, what changed because of its action, what it previously believed, what corrected it, what to do differently. Requires correction chains, not isolated memories.

Recursive_Self_Indexed_Correction_Chains.tex
situated world model requires belief/error/correction history pretraining gives world priors, but an agent's world model must be *situated*: what happened, what the agent did, what changed because of its action, what it previously believed, what corrected it, what to do differently. requires correction chains, not isolated memories. framing/ontology recursive_self_indexed_correction_chains.tex

◈ Mnestic-reactivation architecture (12-stage pipeline)

product

Experience stream → attention/salience gate → trace buffer → fold operator → memory graph → operation-memory → self-indexed correction chains → context builder → LLM attention workspace → reasoning/planning/action → new traces. The context builder is the bridge, asking which traces are transition-effective now, which chains constrain the decision, which commitments are active, which prior errors to avoid, which memories need revalidation.
