fold_force(hit) = max(cos(response_with, hit.vector) - cos(response_without, hit.vector), 0). Measures how much the response absorbs the memory content — moves toward the information the memory carries. Gives 5.9x discrimination (relevant +0.23, generic noise -0.04) with Claude Haiku at temperature=0. This is the practical realization of the h_cog constraint for embedding-based systems.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md, trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
answer-quality fold-force metric (the fix for llms) fold_force(hit) = max(cos(response_with, hit.vector) - cos(response_without, hit.vector), 0). measures how much the response *absorbs the memory content* — moves toward the information the memory carries. gives 5.9x discrimination (relevant +0.23, generic noise -0.04) with claude haiku at temperature=0. this is the practical realization of the h_cog constraint for embedding-based systems. mechanism | coinage fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md, trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ Per-record fold-force attribution

mechanismresult/finding

The answer-quality metric naturally decomposes per retrieved record. When fold-force is recorded for each hit individually: signal records get 0.16-0.33, noise records get 0.00-0.03 — 5-10x higher per-record fold-force for signal than noise. Enables record-level pruning decisions.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
per-record fold-force attribution the answer-quality metric naturally decomposes per retrieved record. when fold-force is recorded for each hit individually: signal records get 0.16-0.33, noise records get 0.00-0.03 — 5-10x higher per-record fold-force for signal than noise. enables record-level pruning decisions. mechanism | result/finding fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Eq. 15 requires a decision-relevant distance metric, not raw output distance (framework refinement)

framing/ontologyresult/finding

The paper's Eq. 15 defines fold-force as generic divergence D(P^mu || P^0). FGM's finding: the distance metric D must be chosen to measure decision-relevant divergence, not raw output divergence. The formal definition stands, but implementations targeting LLM substrates must use a decision-relevant metric. Feedback propagated back to the trace framework's VALIDATION_REPORT.

trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
eq. 15 requires a decision-relevant distance metric, not raw output distance (framework refinement) the paper's eq. 15 defines fold-force as generic divergence d(p^mu || p^0). fgm's finding: the distance metric d must be chosen to measure decision-relevant divergence, not raw output divergence. the formal definition stands, but implementations targeting llm substrates must use a decision-relevant metric. feedback propagated back to the trace framework's validation_report. result/finding | framing/ontology trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ Experiment 1: fold-gating vs store-everything

experiment/benchmark

Two matched agents process the same task sequence. Baseline stores all records and never prunes; fold-gated stores all but prunes records whose average fold-force per retrieval is below threshold. Tests Prediction 3. Task: 4 topics x 3 signal records + 80 noise records per phase over 8 phases (640 noise total). Fold-gated uses h_cog projection (cog_dims = unique subspace only) so it can distinguish signal (fold-force ~0.009) from noise (~0.003).

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md, trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md
experiment 1: fold-gating vs store-everything two matched agents process the same task sequence. baseline stores all records and never prunes; fold-gated stores all but prunes records whose average fold-force per retrieval is below threshold. tests prediction 3. task: 4 topics x 3 signal records + 80 noise records per phase over 8 phases (640 noise total). fold-gated uses h_cog projection (cog_dims = unique subspace only) so it can distinguish signal (fold-force ~0.009) from noise (~0.003). experiment/benchmark fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md, trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md

◈ Experiment 1 result: modest but consistent fold-gating advantage

result/finding

Across 15 seeds: prediction holds in 8/15 (53%) individual seeds, but mean advantage consistently positive: +0.056 hit-rate advantage, 60 fewer records (fold-gated 592 vs baseline 652). The 53% per-seed rate reflects the narrow fold-force gap (~2.7x) in 32-dim toy vectors leaving a thin threshold window (0.008).

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
experiment 1 result: modest but consistent fold-gating advantage across 15 seeds: prediction holds in 8/15 (53%) individual seeds, but mean advantage consistently positive: +0.056 hit-rate advantage, 60 fewer records (fold-gated 592 vs baseline 652). the 53% per-seed rate reflects the narrow fold-force gap (~2.7x) in 32-dim toy vectors leaving a thin threshold window (0.008). result/finding fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Experiment 1 mechanism: how fold-gating helps

mechanismresult/finding

Three-step mechanism: (1) noise records enter top-k as they accumulate, displacing signal in the baseline; (2) fold-force discrimination via h_cog identifies noise (low unique-subspace energy) vs signal (high); (3) pruning noise keeps the fold-gated store cleaner, maintaining signal in top-k.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
experiment 1 mechanism: how fold-gating helps three-step mechanism: (1) noise records enter top-k as they accumulate, displacing signal in the baseline; (2) fold-force discrimination via h_cog identifies noise (low unique-subspace energy) vs signal (high); (3) pruning noise keeps the fold-gated store cleaner, maintaining signal in top-k. mechanism | result/finding fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Synergy: h_cog + fold-gating are complementary

result/finding

Without h_cog, fold-force measures full-output divergence dominated by the topic subspace — signal and noise both ~0.06, no discrimination. With h_cog restricted to unique subspace, signal ~0.009 vs noise ~0.003 (2.7x) enabling discrimination and pruning. The non-bookkeeping restriction is what makes fold-force a useful gate.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
synergy: h_cog + fold-gating are complementary without h_cog, fold-force measures full-output divergence dominated by the topic subspace — signal and noise both ~0.06, no discrimination. with h_cog restricted to unique subspace, signal ~0.009 vs noise ~0.003 (2.7x) enabling discrimination and pruning. the non-bookkeeping restriction is what makes fold-force a useful gate. result/finding fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ make_unique_sensitive_transition

mechanismexperiment/benchmark

A custom transition where fold impact scales with unique-subspace energy in the fold vector. Signal retrievals carry unique energy; noise retrievals don't. Designed so fold-force discrimination is possible in the toy setting where default_transition would saturate.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
make_unique_sensitive_transition a custom transition where fold impact scales with unique-subspace energy in the fold vector. signal retrievals carry unique energy; noise retrievals don't. designed so fold-force discrimination is possible in the toy setting where default_transition would saturate. mechanism | experiment/benchmark fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Large-N scaling to N=1000

experiment/benchmarkresult/finding

Regime evaluation extended from N=128 to N={8, 64, 256, 512, 1000} with 200 queries per N. rich_distinctive maintains 1.000 hit rate at N=1000 with margins >+0.25; overload regimes degrade to ~0.03 (>28x from N=8). Confirms the paper's evaluation protocol (Section 10 calls for N up to 1000).

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
large-n scaling to n=1000 regime evaluation extended from n=128 to n={8, 64, 256, 512, 1000} with 200 queries per n. rich_distinctive maintains 1.000 hit rate at n=1000 with margins >+0.25; overload regimes degrade to ~0.03 (>28x from n=8). confirms the paper's evaluation protocol (section 10 calls for n up to 1000). experiment/benchmark | result/finding fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Binomial proposition (Prop. 10.1) verification

result/finding

For fixed k and any p(q) > 0, hit probability H_q(k,N) -> 0 as N -> infinity. Empirically confirmed: sparse_confusable and aggressive_lossy monotonically decrease toward a noise floor ~0.03 (consistent with class-level similarity giving weak positive signal above the uniform 3/1000=0.003 chance level at k=3, N=1000).

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md, trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
binomial proposition (prop. 10.1) verification for fixed k and any p(q) > 0, hit probability h_q(k,n) -> 0 as n -> infinity. empirically confirmed: sparse_confusable and aggressive_lossy monotonically decrease toward a noise floor ~0.03 (consistent with class-level similarity giving weak positive signal above the uniform 3/1000=0.003 chance level at k=3, n=1000). result/finding fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md, trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ Noise floor at ~0.03 above random chance

result/finding

In overload regimes at N=1000, hit rate doesn't reach zero but floors at ~0.03 — roughly 10x higher than the uniform-random expectation (3/1000=0.003), because class-level similarity gives the target a weak positive signal even when unique content is absent.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
noise floor at ~0.03 above random chance in overload regimes at n=1000, hit rate doesn't reach zero but floors at ~0.03 — roughly 10x higher than the uniform-random expectation (3/1000=0.003), because class-level similarity gives the target a weak positive signal even when unique content is absent. result/finding fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Multi-seed regime stability (60/60 central result)

result/finding

Across 15 seeds x 4 regimes, the overload/non-overload boundary classified perfectly (60/60, 100%) at N in {8,32,128}, 200 queries per N. Non-overload regimes maintain 1.000 hit rate with zero variance; overload regimes degrade to ~0.1 with bounded variance. Described as "the central result."

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
multi-seed regime stability (60/60 central result) across 15 seeds x 4 regimes, the overload/non-overload boundary classified perfectly (60/60, 100%) at n in {8,32,128}, 200 queries per n. non-overload regimes maintain 1.000 hit rate with zero variance; overload regimes degrade to ~0.1 with bounded variance. described as the central result. result/finding fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Clean margin separation across regimes

result/finding

At N=128 (15-seed stats): rich_distinctive margins ~+0.34 (distractors never threaten); compressed_preserving ~+0.10 (safe but narrow); sparse_confusable ~-0.019 (distractors slightly outrank target); aggressive_lossy ~-0.049 (distractors strongly outrank). The gap between the two overload regimes is the discriminant used by the improved classifier.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
clean margin separation across regimes at n=128 (15-seed stats): rich_distinctive margins ~+0.34 (distractors never threaten); compressed_preserving ~+0.10 (safe but narrow); sparse_confusable ~-0.019 (distractors slightly outrank target); aggressive_lossy ~-0.049 (distractors strongly outrank). the gap between the two overload regimes is the discriminant used by the improved classifier. result/finding fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Binary confusability separation

result/finding

At N=128, overload regimes reach chi=1.0 (all queries near-tie) while non-overload regimes stay below 0.065. rich_distinctive is exactly 0.0000 with zero variance. A clean binary separator.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
binary confusability separation at n=128, overload regimes reach chi=1.0 (all queries near-tie) while non-overload regimes stay below 0.065. rich_distinctive is exactly 0.0000 with zero variance. a clean binary separator. result/finding fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Classifier improvement from multi-seed validation (constructive fix)

mechanismresult/finding

The original _classify heuristic used last.mean_margin < -0.01 to separate sparse_confusable from aggressive_lossy — but it failed because sparse also has negative margins (~-0.019), so it was always mislabeled aggressive_lossy. Fix: use mean_margin > -0.035 (sparse clusters -0.019, aggressive clusters -0.050, clean gap between). For non-overload: mean_margin > 0.2 (rich ~+0.34, compressed ~+0.10). After fix: 60/60 correct across all seeds.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
classifier improvement from multi-seed validation (constructive fix) the original `_classify` heuristic used `last.mean_margin < -0.01` to separate sparse_confusable from aggressive_lossy — but it failed because sparse also has negative margins (~-0.019), so it was always mislabeled aggressive_lossy. fix: use mean_margin > -0.035 (sparse clusters -0.019, aggressive clusters -0.050, clean gap between). for non-overload: mean_margin > 0.2 (rich ~+0.34, compressed ~+0.10). after fix: 60/60 correct across all seeds. result/finding | mechanism fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Real embeddings validation (sentence-transformers 384-dim)

experiment/benchmarkresult/finding

All FGM components validated with all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (384-dim, normalized) on natural-language text: retrieval, fold-force, compression, operation-memory, decision tracing. Distinctive technical records (PostgreSQL, Kubernetes, Redis, etc.) achieve perfect retrieval (HR=1.0); confusable meeting notes show elevated confusability; compression preserves distinctive records while pruning zero-fold noise.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
real embeddings validation (sentence-transformers 384-dim) all fgm components validated with all-minilm-l6-v2 (384-dim, normalized) on natural-language text: retrieval, fold-force, compression, operation-memory, decision tracing. distinctive technical records (postgresql, kubernetes, redis, etc.) achieve perfect retrieval (hr=1.0); confusable meeting notes show elevated confusability; compression preserves distinctive records while pruning zero-fold noise. experiment/benchmark | result/finding fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Real embeddings produce cleaner semantic separation than hash embeddings

result/finding

Distinctive technical records have margins of 0.1+ with real embeddings vs ~0.05 with hash embeddings. Confusable meeting notes with minor variations show high confusability (0.57), confirming the confusability metric works on real text.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
real embeddings produce cleaner semantic separation than hash embeddings distinctive technical records have margins of 0.1+ with real embeddings vs ~0.05 with hash embeddings. confusable meeting notes with minor variations show high confusability (0.57), confirming the confusability metric works on real text. result/finding fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Echo-call stub for API-free plumbing tests

mechanismexperiment/benchmark

echo_call() produces deterministic text that differs based on memory presence, enabling full plumbing tests of the LLM transition pipeline (context setting, fold-force, agent integration, operation memory) without any API access.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
echo-call stub for api-free plumbing tests `echo_call()` produces deterministic text that differs based on memory presence, enabling full plumbing tests of the llm transition pipeline (context setting, fold-force, agent integration, operation memory) without any api access. mechanism | experiment/benchmark fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Live Claude API fold-force tests (concrete examples)

experiment/benchmarkresult/finding

Test 1 — memory "outage caused by memory leak in session handler", query "what caused the outage?": fold-force 0.78 (with memory factual, without hedges). Test 2 — relevant "deploy failed: migration timed out" + irrelevant "team had pizza Tuesday", query "why did deployment fail?": fold-force 0.95. Both use Haiku temperature=0 for deterministic measurement.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
live claude api fold-force tests (concrete examples) test 1 — memory outage caused by memory leak in session handler , query what caused the outage? : fold-force 0.78 (with memory factual, without hedges). test 2 — relevant deploy failed: migration timed out + irrelevant team had pizza tuesday , query why did deployment fail? : fold-force 0.95. both use haiku temperature=0 for deterministic measurement. experiment/benchmark | result/finding fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ LLM Experiment 1: partial success is a constructive finding

result/finding

With answer-quality fold-force, the measurement works (5-10x per-record discrimination) but reactive pruning has limited impact. Best run (4 phases, 10 noise/phase): baseline HR 0.500, fold-gated 0.667, 10 records pruned, prediction HOLDS. Larger run (5 phases, 15 noise/phase): both 0.500, prediction FAILS. Identifies that the pruning policy, not the metric, is the bottleneck.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
llm experiment 1: partial success is a constructive finding with answer-quality fold-force, the measurement works (5-10x per-record discrimination) but reactive pruning has limited impact. best run (4 phases, 10 noise/phase): baseline hr 0.500, fold-gated 0.667, 10 records pruned, prediction holds. larger run (5 phases, 15 noise/phase): both 0.500, prediction fails. identifies that the pruning policy, not the metric, is the bottleneck. result/finding fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Why reactive pruning fails at scale (three reasons)

result/finding

(1) Most noise records are never retrieved — with k=3 and 6 queries, only ~18 of 75+ noise enter top-3, and records with 0 uses can't be pruned by fold-force. (2) Signal displacement happens before pruning can help — once noise pushes signal out of top-3, signal stops being retrieved so pruning noise afterward doesn't help. (3) The pruning is reactive not proactive — it waits for noise to be tested, by which time the regime already shifted to sparse_confusable.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
why reactive pruning fails at scale (three reasons) (1) most noise records are never retrieved — with k=3 and 6 queries, only ~18 of 75+ noise enter top-3, and records with 0 uses can't be pruned by fold-force. (2) signal displacement happens before pruning can help — once noise pushes signal out of top-3, signal stops being retrieved so pruning noise afterward doesn't help. (3) the pruning is reactive not proactive — it waits for noise to be tested, by which time the regime already shifted to sparse_confusable. result/finding fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Proactive confusability-triggered compression is architecturally necessary

mechanismresult/finding

The key architectural conclusion: reactive per-record fold-force pruning is insufficient; the paper's confusability-triggered compression (Layer 6) is the right mechanism. Prop. 10.1's chi_N is the correct trigger (when to compress); per-record fold-force is the correct criterion (what to compress). Both are needed.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md, trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
"fold-force measurement works, but the pruning policy matters as much as the metric."
proactive confusability-triggered compression is architecturally necessary the key architectural conclusion: reactive per-record fold-force pruning is insufficient; the paper's confusability-triggered compression (layer 6) is the right mechanism. prop. 10.1's chi_n is the correct *trigger* (when to compress); per-record fold-force is the correct *criterion* (what to compress). both are needed. result/finding | mechanism fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md, trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md fold-force measurement works, but the pruning policy matters as much as the metric.

◈ Open architectural question: trace/fold collapse in black-box LLMs

open-question

When memories are inserted into the prompt, they enter the attention mechanism at full strength — becoming part of the trace layer (Layer 1) rather than being folded into it from outside. Without weight access, the six-layer hierarchy is a logical decomposition, not a physical one. The fold collapses into the trace: there's no operation distinctly "folding" — it's all attention.

trace_validation_framework\problem.txt, fold-gated-memory\CLAUDE.md
"The fold collapses into the trace. There's no operation that's distinctly 'folding' — it's all attention."
open architectural question: trace/fold collapse in black-box llms when memories are inserted into the prompt, they enter the attention mechanism at full strength — becoming part of the trace layer (layer 1) rather than being folded into it from outside. without weight access, the six-layer hierarchy is a logical decomposition, not a physical one. the fold collapses into the trace: there's no operation distinctly folding — it's all attention. open-question trace_validation_framework\problem.txt, fold-gated-memory\claude.md the fold collapses into the trace. there's no operation that's distinctly 'folding' — it's all attention.

◈ Solution 1 to trace/fold collapse: vector-space trace + LLM as decoder

mechanismopen-question

Maintain the trace as an EMA in embedding space (which FGM already does with _state). Compute the fold in embedding space. Only use the LLM to interpret/render the folded state — not to compute it. The trace and fold happen in the vector math; the LLM is the rendering layer.

trace_validation_framework\problem.txt
solution 1 to trace/fold collapse: vector-space trace + llm as decoder maintain the trace as an ema in embedding space (which fgm already does with `_state`). compute the fold in embedding space. only use the llm to interpret/render the folded state — not to compute it. the trace and fold happen in the vector math; the llm is the rendering layer. mechanism | open-question trace_validation_framework\problem.txt

◈ Solution 2 to trace/fold collapse: structured prompt separation

mechanismopen-question

Use prompt structure to approximate the trace/fold distinction: system message = compressed state trace (naturally decays as summarized across sessions); retrieved memories = explicitly labeled fold candidates presented as external records; the LLM is instructed to treat them differently ("use them only if they change your answer"). Changes behavioral response, not attention mechanics.

trace_validation_framework\problem.txt
solution 2 to trace/fold collapse: structured prompt separation use prompt structure to approximate the trace/fold distinction: system message = compressed state trace (naturally decays as summarized across sessions); retrieved memories = explicitly labeled fold candidates presented as external records; the llm is instructed to treat them differently ( use them only if they change your answer ). changes behavioral response, not attention mechanics. mechanism | open-question trace_validation_framework\problem.txt

◈ Solution 3 to trace/fold collapse: pre-fold gating (measure-then-inject)

mechanism

Instead of inject-then-measure, measure-then-inject: run the query without memory first (baseline); for each candidate memory compute answer-quality fold-force before deciding whether to inject; only inject memories that pass the gate. The LLM never sees zero-fold-force memories — they never enter the trace. Same cost (2 LLM calls) but semantically faithful: the gate operates before the memory enters the trace. Called "the most architecturally faithful approach."

trace_validation_framework\problem.txt, fold-gated-memory\CLAUDE.md
solution 3 to trace/fold collapse: pre-fold gating (measure-then-inject) instead of inject-then-measure, measure-then-inject: run the query without memory first (baseline); for each candidate memory compute answer-quality fold-force *before* deciding whether to inject; only inject memories that pass the gate. the llm never sees zero-fold-force memories — they never enter the trace. same cost (2 llm calls) but semantically faithful: the gate operates before the memory enters the trace. called the most architecturally faithful approach. mechanism trace_validation_framework\problem.txt, fold-gated-memory\claude.md

◈ Activation steering for true fold with weight access

mechanismopen-question

With open-source models (weight access), you could inject fold vectors as activation steering at intermediate layers, separate from the token-level trace. The residual-stream additions would bypass the attention mechanism's treatment of fold content as "present" trace — a physical (not just logical) realization of the six-layer hierarchy.

trace_validation_framework\problem.txt
activation steering for true fold with weight access with open-source models (weight access), you could inject fold vectors as activation steering at intermediate layers, separate from the token-level trace. the residual-stream additions would bypass the attention mechanism's treatment of fold content as present trace — a physical (not just logical) realization of the six-layer hierarchy. mechanism | open-question trace_validation_framework\problem.txt

◈ Honest framing: six-layer hierarchy as logical, not physical, decomposition (in black-box LLMs)

framing/ontology

With a black-box LLM, the trace layer and fold layer share the same substrate (attention over the token sequence). The trace operator (Layer 1) IS the attention mechanism; the fold operator (Layer 4) is the decision about what enters the attention context; the fold-force measurement validates this decision retroactively.

trace_validation_framework\problem.txt
honest framing: six-layer hierarchy as logical, not physical, decomposition (in black-box llms) with a black-box llm, the trace layer and fold layer share the same substrate (attention over the token sequence). the trace operator (layer 1) is the attention mechanism; the fold operator (layer 4) is the *decision about what enters the attention context*; the fold-force measurement validates this decision retroactively. framing/ontology trace_validation_framework\problem.txt

◈ Fold-force proxy methods (three cheaper alternatives to dual-pass)

mechanismopen-question

Dual-pass costs 2 LLM calls per fold. Three proposed approximations: (A) attention-weight proxy — inspect attention weights on memory tokens after the forward pass (low attention = low fold-force, no second call needed but approximate); (B) logit-delta proxy — compare top-k token logits with and without memory tokens via attention masking (single pass); (C) periodic ablation sampling — run full fold-ablation on a random ~10% of decisions and update retention priorities. All remain untested.

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md, fold-gated-memory\roadmap.txt
fold-force proxy methods (three cheaper alternatives to dual-pass) dual-pass costs 2 llm calls per fold. three proposed approximations: (a) attention-weight proxy — inspect attention weights on memory tokens after the forward pass (low attention = low fold-force, no second call needed but approximate); (b) logit-delta proxy — compare top-k token logits with and without memory tokens via attention masking (single pass); (c) periodic ablation sampling — run full fold-ablation on a random ~10% of decisions and update retention priorities. all remain untested. mechanism | open-question trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md, fold-gated-memory\roadmap.txt

◈ The compression objective functional

mechanismframing/ontology

K* = argmax_K [ E[U(K)] - lambda_S*S(K) - lambda_L*L(K) - lambda_C*C(K) ] where U is utility and S/L/C are storage, latency, and compute/context costs. Subject to transition preservation (compressed records produce similar fold outcomes) and retrieval preservation (top-k hit rate not degraded beyond tolerance).

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md, trace_validation_framework\docs\SIMReC_P7_revision_patch.md
the compression objective functional k* = argmax_k [ e[u(k)] - lambda_s*s(k) - lambda_l*l(k) - lambda_c*c(k) ] where u is utility and s/l/c are storage, latency, and compute/context costs. subject to transition preservation (compressed records produce similar fold outcomes) and retrieval preservation (top-k hit rate not degraded beyond tolerance). mechanism | framing/ontology trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md, trace_validation_framework\docs\simrec_p7_revision_patch.md

◈ Three compression strategies mapped to the four regimes

framing/ontologyexperiment/benchmark

No compression -> sparse_confusable (~9% HR at N=128 if records lack unique content). Aggressive summarization (decision details removed) -> aggressive_lossy (~10%). Margin-preserving compression (operation type + decision content + unique keys retained) -> compressed_preserving (100%). Rich distinctive records (no compression needed) -> rich_distinctive (100%). Takeaway: the right strategy depends on record quality.

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md
three compression strategies mapped to the four regimes no compression -> sparse_confusable (~9% hr at n=128 if records lack unique content). aggressive summarization (decision details removed) -> aggressive_lossy (~10%). margin-preserving compression (operation type + decision content + unique keys retained) -> compressed_preserving (100%). rich distinctive records (no compression needed) -> rich_distinctive (100%). takeaway: the right strategy depends on record quality. framing/ontology | experiment/benchmark trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md

◈ Evaluation protocol for any agent memory system

experiment/benchmark

Four-step theory-grounded protocol: (1) Regime classification — run at N={10,50,100,500,1000}, plot HR vs N, classify into the four regimes. (2) Fold-force audit — measure fold-force per retrieved memory; what fraction have fold-force > epsilon; what fraction of context tokens are zero-fold-force. (3) Compression boundary — find the N where confusability > 0.5; does the system compress before/at/after. (4) Operation-memory depth — count recursive depth; does it improve self-correction.

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md
evaluation protocol for any agent memory system four-step theory-grounded protocol: (1) regime classification — run at n={10,50,100,500,1000}, plot hr vs n, classify into the four regimes. (2) fold-force audit — measure fold-force per retrieved memory; what fraction have fold-force > epsilon; what fraction of context tokens are zero-fold-force. (3) compression boundary — find the n where confusability > 0.5; does the system compress before/at/after. (4) operation-memory depth — count recursive depth; does it improve self-correction. experiment/benchmark trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md

◈ Primary metrics table for agent memory

experiment/benchmark

Six metrics: Hit rate H(k,N) (retrieval accuracy as memory grows); retrieval margin M_N(q) (distinctiveness); confusability chi_N (fraction of near-ties); fold-force D(W^mu||W^0) (whether memory changes decisions); h_cog divergence (whether the change is non-bookkeeping); overload indicator (sign of delta_N H(k,N)).

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md
primary metrics table for agent memory six metrics: hit rate h(k,n) (retrieval accuracy as memory grows); retrieval margin m_n(q) (distinctiveness); confusability chi_n (fraction of near-ties); fold-force d(w^mu||w^0) (whether memory changes decisions); h_cog divergence (whether the change is non-bookkeeping); overload indicator (sign of delta_n h(k,n)). experiment/benchmark trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md

◈ Baseline systems mapped to expected regimes

framing/ontologyexperiment/benchmark

RAG (naive vector store, no compression) -> sparse_confusable. MemGPT (tiered memory + summarization) -> aggressive_lossy to compressed_preserving (depends on summary quality). Retrieval-augmented agent with no persistent memory -> zero-trace. FGM (this proposal) -> rich_distinctive to compressed_preserving.

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md
baseline systems mapped to expected regimes rag (naive vector store, no compression) -> sparse_confusable. memgpt (tiered memory + summarization) -> aggressive_lossy to compressed_preserving (depends on summary quality). retrieval-augmented agent with no persistent memory -> zero-trace. fgm (this proposal) -> rich_distinctive to compressed_preserving. experiment/benchmark | framing/ontology trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md

◈ The diagnosis of current agent memory failure

framing/ontology

Every major framework (LangChain, AutoGPT, MemGPT, CrewAI, OpenAI Assistants) uses the same pattern: embed -> store -> retrieve top-k by cosine -> stuff into context. This is "storage without folding" — no mechanism to measure whether context changed the decision, no criterion for compress/discard, no way to distinguish a policy-driving memory from a token-occupying one. Fails predictably as N grows (Prediction 5): works early (small N), degrades into sparse_confusable later.

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md
"The agent has entered operation-memory overload not because it stored too much, but because its records lack distinctive retrieval signatures and its retrieval is bounded."
the diagnosis of current agent memory failure every major framework (langchain, autogpt, memgpt, crewai, openai assistants) uses the same pattern: embed -> store -> retrieve top-k by cosine -> stuff into context. this is storage without folding — no mechanism to measure whether context changed the decision, no criterion for compress/discard, no way to distinguish a policy-driving memory from a token-occupying one. fails predictably as n grows (prediction 5): works early (small n), degrades into sparse_confusable later. framing/ontology trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md the agent has entered operation-memory overload not because it stored too much, but because its records lack distinctive retrieval signatures and its retrieval is bounded.

◈ Core thesis of FGM

framing/ontology

"Fold-force, not storage volume, should gate what enters and remains in agent memory. Retrieval margin, not embedding similarity alone, should govern compression. The trace/storage/fold hierarchy should replace the current flat retrieve-and-stuff paradigm."

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md
core thesis of fgm fold-force, not storage volume, should gate what enters and remains in agent memory. retrieval margin, not embedding similarity alone, should govern compression. the trace/storage/fold hierarchy should replace the current flat retrieve-and-stuff paradigm. framing/ontology trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md

◈ Five novel contributions over existing agent memory work

framing/ontology

(1) Fold-force as the memory gate (vs relevance/similarity). (2) Retrieval margin as compression trigger (vs schedule/size). (3) Margin-preserving compression with a formal falsifiable criterion (Eq. 28). (4) Operation-memory for recursive self-correction (no current system stores memory-use operations). (5) Four-regime evaluation framework that explains WHY a system fails, not just THAT it fails.

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md
five novel contributions over existing agent memory work (1) fold-force as the memory gate (vs relevance/similarity). (2) retrieval margin as compression trigger (vs schedule/size). (3) margin-preserving compression with a formal falsifiable criterion (eq. 28). (4) operation-memory for recursive self-correction (no current system stores memory-use operations). (5) four-regime evaluation framework that explains why a system fails, not just that it fails. framing/ontology trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md

◈ Positioning: theory-to-architecture paper

framing/ontologyproduct

Not another benchmark or agent-framework paper — a theory-to-architecture paper: take a validated formal framework for trace and memory and show it resolves specific failure modes in current LLM agent memory. Theoretical grounding distinguishes it from engineering-only work; practical architecture + experiments distinguish it from theory-only work. Working title: "Fold-Gated Memory: A Trace-Theoretic Architecture for LLM Agent Memory Systems."

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md
positioning: theory-to-architecture paper not another benchmark or agent-framework paper — a theory-to-architecture paper: take a validated formal framework for trace and memory and show it resolves specific failure modes in current llm agent memory. theoretical grounding distinguishes it from engineering-only work; practical architecture + experiments distinguish it from theory-only work. working title: fold-gated memory: a trace-theoretic architecture for llm agent memory systems. framing/ontology | product trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md

◈ Experiment 2: margin-preserving compression vs summarization

experiment/benchmark

Three compression strategies applied to an agent with 500 operation records: (a) no compression, (b) aggressive summarization (GPT 1-sentence summaries), (c) margin-preserving compression. Prediction: (c) maintains HR ~1.0; (b) degrades to aggressive_lossy; (a) degrades if records confusable, fine if distinctive. Measures: HR, margin, confusability trajectories, downstream task accuracy. (Listed as remaining work / next step for contributors.)

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md, fold-gated-memory\CLAUDE.md
experiment 2: margin-preserving compression vs summarization three compression strategies applied to an agent with 500 operation records: (a) no compression, (b) aggressive summarization (gpt 1-sentence summaries), (c) margin-preserving compression. prediction: (c) maintains hr ~1.0; (b) degrades to aggressive_lossy; (a) degrades if records confusable, fine if distinctive. measures: hr, margin, confusability trajectories, downstream task accuracy. (listed as remaining work / next step for contributors.) experiment/benchmark trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md, fold-gated-memory\claude.md

◈ Experiment 3: operation-memory enables self-correction

experiment/benchmark

Two matched agents on a task requiring policy revision after discovering a prior decision was wrong. Agent A has content memory only; Agent B adds operation-memory. Prediction (from SIMReC P1): B identifies and revises the faulty decision by tracing back through fold operations; A must re-derive from content alone, losing the causal chain. Validated in toy form (self-correction scenario: "server A is bottleneck" -> "scale A" -> "actually server B" -> trace back); full LLM version remains untested.

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md, fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
experiment 3: operation-memory enables self-correction two matched agents on a task requiring policy revision after discovering a prior decision was wrong. agent a has content memory only; agent b adds operation-memory. prediction (from simrec p1): b identifies and revises the faulty decision by tracing back through fold operations; a must re-derive from content alone, losing the causal chain. validated in toy form (self-correction scenario: server a is bottleneck -> scale a -> actually server b -> trace back); full llm version remains untested. experiment/benchmark trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md, fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Experiment 4: self-index emergence in long-horizon agents

experiment/benchmarkopen-question

An agent running 100+ sessions with operation-memory, cross-domain integration, but no explicit "self" prompt. Track whether a stable action-coupled index class emerges from compression pressure. Prediction (Prediction 6 / SIMReC P2): when cross-session binding improves compression/prediction, the agent develops a stable referential pattern (consistent decision style, ownership of prior work, policy continuity); when tasks are independent, no global index emerges. Measures: index drift d_Z, shuffle sensitivity psi_t, compression gain from binding, policy consistency.

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md
experiment 4: self-index emergence in long-horizon agents an agent running 100+ sessions with operation-memory, cross-domain integration, but no explicit self prompt. track whether a stable action-coupled index class emerges from compression pressure. prediction (prediction 6 / simrec p2): when cross-session binding improves compression/prediction, the agent develops a stable referential pattern (consistent decision style, ownership of prior work, policy continuity); when tasks are independent, no global index emerges. measures: index drift d_z, shuffle sensitivity psi_t, compression gain from binding, policy consistency. experiment/benchmark | open-question trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md

◈ Risk: fold-force measurement cost

open-question

Dual-pass ablation doubles LLM calls. Proxy methods (attention weights, logit deltas) need empirical validation — they may not correlate with true fold-force in all settings. Status: FGM PoC confirms dual-pass works (0.78-0.95 with Haiku, ~$0.01/experiment) but proxies remain untested.

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md
risk: fold-force measurement cost dual-pass ablation doubles llm calls. proxy methods (attention weights, logit deltas) need empirical validation — they may not correlate with true fold-force in all settings. status: fgm poc confirms dual-pass works (0.78-0.95 with haiku, ~$0.01/experiment) but proxies remain untested. open-question trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md

◈ Open question: what counts as h_cog in an LLM?

open-question

The paper defines h_cog as non-bookkeeping transition variables. In a transformer, distinguishing "the model's actual reasoning" from "the model acknowledging it has memory" is non-trivial; chain-of-thought blurs it. Partially resolved: answer-quality metric (cosine toward memory content) is the practical realization for embedding-based systems; which embedding dimensions constitute h_cog remains open for direct-vector approaches.

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md, fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
open question: what counts as h_cog in an llm? the paper defines h_cog as non-bookkeeping transition variables. in a transformer, distinguishing the model's actual reasoning from the model acknowledging it has memory is non-trivial; chain-of-thought blurs it. partially resolved: answer-quality metric (cosine toward memory content) is the practical realization for embedding-based systems; which embedding dimensions constitute h_cog remains open for direct-vector approaches. open-question trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md, fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Open question: margin-preserving compression is hard to implement

open-question

The constraint "preserve retrieval margin" is easy to state, hard to implement — what extraction/compression method actually preserves unique retrieval signatures is itself a research problem. Status: validated with three methods (prune, merge, combined) on toy embeddings; real-text compression validated at retrieval level but not compression level.

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md
open question: margin-preserving compression is hard to implement the constraint preserve retrieval margin is easy to state, hard to implement — what extraction/compression method actually preserves unique retrieval signatures is itself a research problem. status: validated with three methods (prune, merge, combined) on toy embeddings; real-text compression validated at retrieval level but not compression level. open-question trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md

◈ Open question: self-index emergence at scale (LLM stochasticity)

open-question

SIMReC P2 uses toy substrates (64-dim, hashed BoW). Whether a stable self-index emerges in a real LLM agent over 100+ sessions is genuinely uncertain — it might, or the LLM's stochasticity might prevent convergence.

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md
open question: self-index emergence at scale (llm stochasticity) simrec p2 uses toy substrates (64-dim, hashed bow). whether a stable self-index emerges in a real llm agent over 100+ sessions is genuinely uncertain — it might, or the llm's stochasticity might prevent convergence. open-question trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md

◈ Open question: biological analogy limits

open-question

The paper draws on memory-systems theory (Tulving, Squire, Baddeley), but LLM agents are not brains. The trace/fold distinction may map cleanly onto agent architectures without the biological framing being necessary — or even helpful — for an ML audience.

trace_validation_framework\docs\AGENT_ARCHITECTURE_RESEARCH_SKETCH.md
open question: biological analogy limits the paper draws on memory-systems theory (tulving, squire, baddeley), but llm agents are not brains. the trace/fold distinction may map cleanly onto agent architectures without the biological framing being necessary — or even helpful — for an ml audience. open-question trace_validation_framework\docs\agent_architecture_research_sketch.md

◈ Dual-pass cost limitation and unimplemented proxies

open-questionresult/finding

The LLM transition makes 2 API calls per fold; at scale this doubles cost. Fold-force proxy methods (attention-weight inspection, logit-delta comparison, periodic ablation sampling) are not yet implemented. LLM Experiment 1 cost: 96-288 calls/run, ~$0.01-0.03 at Haiku pricing.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
dual-pass cost limitation and unimplemented proxies the llm transition makes 2 api calls per fold; at scale this doubles cost. fold-force proxy methods (attention-weight inspection, logit-delta comparison, periodic ablation sampling) are not yet implemented. llm experiment 1 cost: 96-288 calls/run, ~$0.01-0.03 at haiku pricing. open-question | result/finding fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Limitation: synthetic regime records

open-question

The regime evaluator uses parametric class+unique vectors, not real agent behavior. Real-embedding tests use natural language but don't run full regime evaluation with text (would require hundreds of semantically controlled text records). Fully naturalistic long-horizon agent behavior remains untested.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md, trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
limitation: synthetic regime records the regime evaluator uses parametric class+unique vectors, not real agent behavior. real-embedding tests use natural language but don't run full regime evaluation with text (would require hundreds of semantically controlled text records). fully naturalistic long-horizon agent behavior remains untested. open-question fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md, trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ Limitation: h_cog dimension assignment is manual

open-question

The experiment assigns cognitive vs bookkeeping dimensions by construction. In real LLM settings, determining which embedding dimensions are h_cog requires introspection methods not yet implemented.

fold-gated-memory\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
limitation: h_cog dimension assignment is manual the experiment assigns cognitive vs bookkeeping dimensions by construction. in real llm settings, determining which embedding dimensions are h_cog requires introspection methods not yet implemented. open-question fold-gated-memory\validation_report.md

◈ Limitation: transformer hidden-state dimensionality untested

open-question

FGM extends to 384-dim sentence-transformer embeddings and N=1000, but transformer hidden-state dimensionality (768-4096) remains untested. Toy probes use 4-32 dim vectors.

trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
limitation: transformer hidden-state dimensionality untested fgm extends to 384-dim sentence-transformer embeddings and n=1000, but transformer hidden-state dimensionality (768-4096) remains untested. toy probes use 4-32 dim vectors. open-question trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ The trace operator (LeakyTraceOperator, EMA form)

mechanism

R_theta implemented as an exponential moving average: tau_t = decay tau_{t-1} + (1-decay) phi(Y_t). With decay=0.8 it retains causal residue; decay=0.0 collapses to instantaneous reactivity (zero-trace). The formal apparatus for "temporal thickness."

trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md, trace_validation_framework\README.md
the trace operator (leakytraceoperator, ema form) r_theta implemented as an exponential moving average: tau_t = decay * tau_{t-1} + (1-decay) * phi(y_t). with decay=0.8 it retains causal residue; decay=0.0 collapses to instantaneous reactivity (zero-trace). the formal apparatus for temporal thickness. mechanism trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md, trace_validation_framework\readme.md

◈ Lag projection P_k

mechanism

P_k tau_t projects the trace state to recover residue of the input k steps ago (Y_{t-k}). Implemented as TraceState.project_lag(k). The probe measures whether this projection retains a nonzero "lag intensity" — the operational test of trace retention.

trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md, trace_validation_framework\README.md
lag projection p_k p_k tau_t projects the trace state to recover residue of the input k steps ago (y_{t-k}). implemented as tracestate.project_lag(k). the probe measures whether this projection retains a nonzero lag intensity — the operational test of trace retention. mechanism trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md, trace_validation_framework\readme.md

◈ trace_retention_probe

experiment/benchmark

Validation probe (Def 4.1, Eq 3-4): whether the lag projection P_k tau_t retains residue of Y_{t-k}. Metrics: trace_intensity, lag_intensity, retention_cosine, projection_l2_to_source. retention_cosine is used as a proxy for the mutual-information condition (Eq. 3).

trace_validation_framework\README.md, trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
trace_retention_probe validation probe (def 4.1, eq 3-4): whether the lag projection p_k tau_t retains residue of y_{t-k}. metrics: trace_intensity, lag_intensity, retention_cosine, projection_l2_to_source. retention_cosine is used as a proxy for the mutual-information condition (eq. 3). experiment/benchmark trace_validation_framework\readme.md, trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ causal_trace_probe

experiment/benchmark

Validation probe (Def 4.1, Eq 4): runs a baseline sequence and an intervened sequence where Y_{t-k} is replaced while later inputs are held fixed, measuring whether P_k tau_t changes (metric: projection_delta_l2). Tests whether prior-state intervention changes present lag residue — the causal (not just correlational) trace criterion.

trace_validation_framework\README.md, trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
causal_trace_probe validation probe (def 4.1, eq 4): runs a baseline sequence and an intervened sequence where y_{t-k} is replaced while later inputs are held fixed, measuring whether p_k tau_t changes (metric: projection_delta_l2). tests whether prior-state intervention changes present lag residue — the causal (not just correlational) trace criterion. experiment/benchmark trace_validation_framework\readme.md, trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ addressability_probe

experiment/benchmark

Validation probe (Def 6.3): whether a stored trace/operation record is selected by bounded top-k retrieval. Metrics: target_rank, hit_in_top_k, retrieval_margin, confusable. Doubles as both storage-ablation test (fast decay) and addressability-blocking test (confusable records).

trace_validation_framework\README.md, trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
addressability_probe validation probe (def 6.3): whether a stored trace/operation record is selected by bounded top-k retrieval. metrics: target_rank, hit_in_top_k, retrieval_margin, confusable. doubles as both storage-ablation test (fast decay) and addressability-blocking test (confusable records). experiment/benchmark trace_validation_framework\readme.md, trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ fold_force_probe

experiment/benchmark

Validation probe (Def 6.4, Eq 14-15): retrieves a record, folds it into a transition, compares transition with-fold vs fold-ablated, computing divergence over h_cog only (action_logits, belief, planning_state, utility). Metrics include fold_ablation_l2_h_cog, belief_symmetric_kl, with/without_fold_action, bookkeeping_ignored.

trace_validation_framework\README.md, trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
fold_force_probe validation probe (def 6.4, eq 14-15): retrieves a record, folds it into a transition, compares transition with-fold vs fold-ablated, computing divergence over h_cog only (action_logits, belief, planning_state, utility). metrics include fold_ablation_l2_h_cog, belief_symmetric_kl, with/without_fold_action, bookkeeping_ignored. experiment/benchmark trace_validation_framework\readme.md, trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ p7_retrieval_regime_probe

experiment/benchmark

Validation probe (Prop 10.1, Eq 24-26): whether bounded retrieval degrades as N grows per regime. Metrics: hit_rate, mean_target_rank, mean_margin, confusability, finite_differences in hit rate as N grows, overload_like. This probe is the theoretical foundation for FGM's regime evaluator.

trace_validation_framework\README.md, fold-gated-memory\CLAUDE.md
p7_retrieval_regime_probe validation probe (prop 10.1, eq 24-26): whether bounded retrieval degrades as n grows per regime. metrics: hit_rate, mean_target_rank, mean_margin, confusability, finite_differences in hit rate as n grows, overload_like. this probe is the theoretical foundation for fgm's regime evaluator. experiment/benchmark trace_validation_framework\readme.md, fold-gated-memory\claude.md

◈ Validation chain (the six-link causal pipeline)

framing/ontology

The trace framework validates a single chain: prior attended state -> retained trace residue -> causal intervention sensitivity -> storage/addressability -> fold into transition -> non-bookkeeping downstream transition effect. Each link is an independent probe; breaking any link is a matched intervention.

trace_validation_framework\README.md
validation chain (the six-link causal pipeline) the trace framework validates a single chain: prior attended state -> retained trace residue -> causal intervention sensitivity -> storage/addressability -> fold into transition -> non-bookkeeping downstream transition effect. each link is an independent probe; breaking any link is a matched intervention. framing/ontology trace_validation_framework\readme.md

◈ Prediction 1: zero-trace limitation (confirmed)

result/finding

Matched agents with identical feedforward computation but no internal trace should fail continuity-across-delay tasks unless the prior state is externally re-presented. Confirmed 60/60: zero-trace substrates (decay=0) retain exactly zero residue at every lag; trace-filled (decay=0.8) retain residue proportional to dimensionality (0.45 at dim 4 to 0.901 at dim 16). Causal intervention always produces nonzero projection_delta_l2.

trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
"Zero-trace substrates collapse to instantaneous reactivity."
prediction 1: zero-trace limitation (confirmed) matched agents with identical feedforward computation but no internal trace should fail continuity-across-delay tasks unless the prior state is externally re-presented. confirmed 60/60: zero-trace substrates (decay=0) retain exactly zero residue at every lag; trace-filled (decay=0.8) retain residue proportional to dimensionality (0.45 at dim 4 to 0.901 at dim 16). causal intervention always produces nonzero projection_delta_l2. result/finding trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md zero-trace substrates collapse to instantaneous reactivity.

◈ Prediction 2: trace-memory dissociation (confirmed)

result/finding

Systems with short-lived retentive dynamics but no addressability show immediate context sensitivity while failing later retrieval. Confirmed 10/10 with a fast-decaying operator (decay=0.3): mean short-lag intensity 0.931 vs mean long-lag 1.84e-4 — a ~5,060:1 ratio. Demonstrates trace can exist without constituting addressable memory.

trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
prediction 2: trace-memory dissociation (confirmed) systems with short-lived retentive dynamics but no addressability show immediate context sensitivity while failing later retrieval. confirmed 10/10 with a fast-decaying operator (decay=0.3): mean short-lag intensity 0.931 vs mean long-lag 1.84e-4 — a ~5,060:1 ratio. demonstrates trace can exist without constituting addressable memory. result/finding trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ Prediction 3: folding predicts adaptive memory-use (confirmed)

result/finding

When stored records and retrieval are held constant, agents permitted to fold selected records into policy should outperform those that merely display/concatenate/log. Confirmed 20/20: fold-ablated transition always picks action 0 (uniform tie-break); folded always picks correct cue action; h_cog divergence >1.0 every trial; bookkeeping always ignored. Cross-validated by SIMReC P1/P6 and FGM Experiment 1.

trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
prediction 3: folding predicts adaptive memory-use (confirmed) when stored records and retrieval are held constant, agents permitted to fold selected records into policy should outperform those that merely display/concatenate/log. confirmed 20/20: fold-ablated transition always picks action 0 (uniform tie-break); folded always picks correct cue action; h_cog divergence >1.0 every trial; bookkeeping always ignored. cross-validated by simrec p1/p6 and fgm experiment 1. result/finding trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ Prediction 4: integration and index dissociation (confirmed)

result/finding

Integration ablation should impair cross-context coherence more than local factual recall; index shuffling should impair ownership/continuity more than non-index factual retrieval. Confirmed 99/100 via SIMReC P3 (shuffle: factual change 0.004, fold L2 change 0.056) and P4* (direction shift 0.048, factual change 0.000). Only the weakened form of P4 is tested — P4-strong (cross-context fragmentation) requires edge-aware retrieval.

trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
prediction 4: integration and index dissociation (confirmed) integration ablation should impair cross-context coherence more than local factual recall; index shuffling should impair ownership/continuity more than non-index factual retrieval. confirmed 99/100 via simrec p3 (shuffle: factual change 0.004, fold l2 change 0.056) and p4* (direction shift 0.048, factual change 0.000). only the weakened form of p4 is tested — p4-strong (cross-context fragmentation) requires edge-aware retrieval. result/finding trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ Prediction 5: conditional operation-memory overload (confirmed — the central revision)

result/finding

Under bounded top-k retrieval, sparse/confusable records show falling target rank, lower margin, lower hit rate as N grows; rich distinctive records maintain margins and avoid overload. Confirmed 40/40 (extended to 60/60 at N=1000 by FGM). This conditionalizes the original unconditional P7 claim on confusability and retrieval bandwidth.

trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
prediction 5: conditional operation-memory overload (confirmed — the central revision) under bounded top-k retrieval, sparse/confusable records show falling target rank, lower margin, lower hit rate as n grows; rich distinctive records maintain margins and avoid overload. confirmed 40/40 (extended to 60/60 at n=1000 by fgm). this conditionalizes the original unconditional p7 claim on confusability and retrieval bandwidth. result/finding trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ Prediction 6: qualified self-index emergence (confirmed)

result/finding

An agent with persistence, addressability, operation-memory, and integration but no explicit self slot develops a stable action-coupled index class when such a class improves compression/prediction/control. Confirmed 100/100 via SIMReC P2 (self-index emerges from pressure alone, mean drift 0.305 < 0.350 threshold) and P5 (loose drift bound produces 4.5x higher index variance than tight, z=6.25).

trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
prediction 6: qualified self-index emergence (confirmed) an agent with persistence, addressability, operation-memory, and integration but no explicit self slot develops a stable action-coupled index class when such a class improves compression/prediction/control. confirmed 100/100 via simrec p2 (self-index emerges from pressure alone, mean drift 0.305 < 0.350 threshold) and p5 (loose drift bound produces 4.5x higher index variance than tight, z=6.25). result/finding trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ Central falsification: original unconditional P7 is FALSE

dead-endresult/finding

The original SIMFC claim that operation-memory overload is an inevitable consequence of storing more records is falsified. Rich distinctive records maintain 100% hit rate from N=8 to N=1000, margins >+0.33, confusability 0.000. "More records does not imply overload when records are distinctive." This is a refutation surfaced by the validation, converted into a better prediction.

trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md, trace_validation_framework\docs\SIMReC_P7_revision_patch.md
central falsification: original unconditional p7 is false the original simfc claim that operation-memory overload is an inevitable consequence of storing more records is falsified. rich distinctive records maintain 100% hit rate from n=8 to n=1000, margins >+0.33, confusability 0.000. more records does not imply overload when records are distinctive. this is a refutation surfaced by the validation, converted into a better prediction. result/finding | dead-end trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md, trace_validation_framework\docs\simrec_p7_revision_patch.md

◈ Seven matched interventions (diagnostic contrasts)

framing/ontologyexperiment/benchmark

The paper defines seven matched interventions, each with an executable probe producing a diagnostic contrast: trace erasure (decay=0), storage ablation (fast decay), addressability blocking (confusable records), fold ablation (fold vs None), operation-memory ablation (SIMReC P1), integration randomization (SIMReC P4*), index shuffling (SIMReC P3). All seven have implementations.

trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
seven matched interventions (diagnostic contrasts) the paper defines seven matched interventions, each with an executable probe producing a diagnostic contrast: trace erasure (decay=0), storage ablation (fast decay), addressability blocking (confusable records), fold ablation (fold vs none), operation-memory ablation (simrec p1), integration randomization (simrec p4*), index shuffling (simrec p3). all seven have implementations. experiment/benchmark | framing/ontology trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ Methodology: operationalize each prediction as an N-seed contrast with binary pass criterion

experiment/benchmark

Each falsifiable prediction becomes an N-seed comparison with a numerical contrast and a binary pass criterion (a pass requires the predicted contrast to hold in 80-95% of seeds, per-prediction threshold). Two substrates: toy harness (hashed BoW + tanh) and real components (sentence-transformers + Claude Haiku).

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md, trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
methodology: operationalize each prediction as an n-seed contrast with binary pass criterion each falsifiable prediction becomes an n-seed comparison with a numerical contrast and a binary pass criterion (a pass requires the predicted contrast to hold in 80-95% of seeds, per-prediction threshold). two substrates: toy harness (hashed bow + tanh) and real components (sentence-transformers + claude haiku). experiment/benchmark simrec-reference\empirical_results.md, trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ Formal apparatus validation table (paper element -> code)

framing/ontology

A complete mapping of each formal object to its implementation: trace operator R_theta -> LeakyTraceOperator; lag projection P_k -> TraceState.project_lag; MI condition -> retention_cosine (proxy); addressability/top-k -> TopKRetriever; fold operator -> FoldOperator.fold; h_cog -> TransitionOutput.h_cog + FGM cog_dims; fold-force D(P^mu||P^0) -> fold_ablation_l2_h_cog + FGM answer_quality; retrieval margin -> target_rank_margin; confusability -> confusability metric; binomial proposition -> monotonic decrease verified; compression objective Eq 28 -> SIMReC P7 + FGM Compressor.

trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
formal apparatus validation table (paper element -> code) a complete mapping of each formal object to its implementation: trace operator r_theta -> leakytraceoperator; lag projection p_k -> tracestate.project_lag; mi condition -> retention_cosine (proxy); addressability/top-k -> topkretriever; fold operator -> foldoperator.fold; h_cog -> transitionoutput.h_cog + fgm cog_dims; fold-force d(p^mu||p^0) -> fold_ablation_l2_h_cog + fgm answer_quality; retrieval margin -> target_rank_margin; confusability -> confusability metric; binomial proposition -> monotonic decrease verified; compression objective eq 28 -> simrec p7 + fgm compressor. framing/ontology trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ Limitation: retention_cosine is only a proxy for mutual information

open-question

The trace_retention_probe uses retention_cosine and lag_intensity as proxies for the mutual-information condition (Eq. 3). Sufficient for the toy substrate but not equivalent to the full MI criterion.

trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
limitation: retention_cosine is only a proxy for mutual information the trace_retention_probe uses retention_cosine and lag_intensity as proxies for the mutual-information condition (eq. 3). sufficient for the toy substrate but not equivalent to the full mi criterion. open-question trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ Limitation: no biological assay

open-question

All validation is computational. The paper explicitly frames biological claims as approximate assays requiring measurable proxies (Section 14.2).

trace_validation_framework\VALIDATION_REPORT.md
limitation: no biological assay all validation is computational. the paper explicitly frames biological claims as approximate assays requiring measurable proxies (section 14.2). open-question trace_validation_framework\validation_report.md

◈ SIMReC = merged SIRAC + MFC framework

framing/ontologycoinage

SIMReC combines SIRAC (typed memory graphs, operation-memory nodes, causal re-entry tests, self-index intervention logic) with MFC (mnestic acts, fold-force, recursive folding, integration span, index-pressure dynamics). A "reference scaffold for experiments and ablations, not a production cognitive architecture."

simrec-reference\README.md, simrec-reference\docs\framework_mapping.md
simrec = merged sirac + mfc framework simrec combines sirac (typed memory graphs, operation-memory nodes, causal re-entry tests, self-index intervention logic) with mfc (mnestic acts, fold-force, recursive folding, integration span, index-pressure dynamics). a reference scaffold for experiments and ablations, not a production cognitive architecture. framing/ontology | coinage simrec-reference\readme.md, simrec-reference\docs\framework_mapping.md

◈ The four-condition definition of cognitive memory

framing/ontology

A stored trace is not cognitive memory by itself. A residue becomes memory when it is: (1) persistent enough to survive, (2) addressable by a query, (3) folded back into current computation, and (4) able to change the next transition. The central executable test: D(T_with_folded_memory, T_without_memory) > epsilon.

simrec-reference\README.md
the four-condition definition of cognitive memory a stored trace is not cognitive memory by itself. a residue becomes memory when it is: (1) persistent enough to survive, (2) addressable by a query, (3) folded back into current computation, and (4) able to change the next transition. the central executable test: d(t_with_folded_memory, t_without_memory) > epsilon. framing/ontology simrec-reference\readme.md

◈ SIMReCGraph: typed directed memory graph

mechanism

Memory state as a typed directed graph M_t = (V_t, E_t, ell_t, rho_t, iota_t), implemented as a networkx DiGraph. Each node stores content, node_type, address_key, persistence, channels, recursive_depth, integration_span, index_binding, embedding, metadata. Typed edges use relations like used, prior-operation, same-event.

simrec-reference\docs\framework_mapping.md, simrec-reference\README.md
simrecgraph: typed directed memory graph memory state as a typed directed graph m_t = (v_t, e_t, ell_t, rho_t, iota_t), implemented as a networkx digraph. each node stores content, node_type, address_key, persistence, channels, recursive_depth, integration_span, index_binding, embedding, metadata. typed edges use relations like `used`, `prior-operation`, `same-event`. mechanism simrec-reference\docs\framework_mapping.md, simrec-reference\readme.md

◈ MnesticAct (the fold unit)

mechanismcoinage

MFC's mnestic act: MnesticAct(residue_id, fold_weight, fold_force, recursive_depth, integration_span, index_binding). The atomic unit that a retrieved residue is wrapped into before folding.

simrec-reference\docs\framework_mapping.md, simrec-reference\README.md
mnesticact (the fold unit) mfc's mnestic act: mnesticact(residue_id, fold_weight, fold_force, recursive_depth, integration_span, index_binding). the atomic unit that a retrieved residue is wrapped into before folding. mechanism | coinage simrec-reference\docs\framework_mapping.md, simrec-reference\readme.md

◈ Fold equation

mechanism

The active fold vector m_t = Sum_mu omega_t(mu) * embedding(residue_mu). The fold weight omega combines persistence, addressability/fold weight, fold-force, recursive depth, integration span, and index-binding.

simrec-reference\README.md, simrec-reference\docs\framework_mapping.md
fold equation the active fold vector m_t = sum_mu omega_t(mu) * embedding(residue_mu). the fold weight omega combines persistence, addressability/fold weight, fold-force, recursive depth, integration span, and index-binding. mechanism simrec-reference\readme.md, simrec-reference\docs\framework_mapping.md

◈ Node types (open-ended typed memory)

mechanismframing/ontology

Supported node_type values default-recognized: percept, fact, action, goal, operation, relation, self-link. Open-ended but these are the load-bearing types. operation nodes enable recursive memory over prior retrieval/fold/use events.

simrec-reference\README.md
node types (open-ended typed memory) supported node_type values default-recognized: percept, fact, action, goal, operation, relation, self-link. open-ended but these are the load-bearing types. `operation` nodes enable recursive memory over prior retrieval/fold/use events. mechanism | framing/ontology simrec-reference\readme.md

◈ Self-index update via pressure + MDL gate + drift bound

mechanism

update_self_index computes a pressure-weighted centroid of active memory vectors, gates it through a compression/control advantage (MDL-style), and constrains updates by a maximum drift band so the index cannot jump arbitrarily. Pressure term approximates Sum_mu iota(mu,z)*fold_force(mu)*recursion(mu)*integration(mu). MDL term: a latent index should appear only when binding memories under one continuing referent is cheaper and more useful than leaving them unbound.

simrec-reference\README.md, simrec-reference\docs\framework_mapping.md
self-index update via pressure + mdl gate + drift bound update_self_index computes a pressure-weighted centroid of active memory vectors, gates it through a compression/control advantage (mdl-style), and constrains updates by a maximum drift band so the index cannot jump arbitrarily. pressure term approximates sum_mu iota(mu,z)*fold_force(mu)*recursion(mu)*integration(mu). mdl term: a latent index should appear only when binding memories under one continuing referent is cheaper and more useful than leaving them unbound. mechanism simrec-reference\readme.md, simrec-reference\docs\framework_mapping.md

◈ compress_operations (recursive-memory regress solution)

mechanism

Compresses older operation-memory nodes into a summary node, preserving operation counts and recursive-depth statistics. The reference solution to prevent unbounded operation-memory regress (memory of memory of memory...).

simrec-reference\README.md, simrec-reference\docs\framework_mapping.md
compress_operations (recursive-memory regress solution) compresses older operation-memory nodes into a summary node, preserving operation counts and recursive-depth statistics. the reference solution to prevent unbounded operation-memory regress (memory of memory of memory...). mechanism simrec-reference\readme.md, simrec-reference\docs\framework_mapping.md

◈ branch_copy (forked-index experiments)

mechanism

A boundary scaffold mechanism for forked-index experiments — creating a branched copy of the memory/index to study identity bifurcation.

simrec-reference\docs\framework_mapping.md
branch_copy (forked-index experiments) a boundary scaffold mechanism for forked-index experiments — creating a branched copy of the memory/index to study identity bifurcation. mechanism simrec-reference\docs\framework_mapping.md

◈ SIMReC P1: storage without fold-force fails recursive continuity

result/finding

Prediction that a storage-only system (retrieves but doesn't fold) fails recursive continuity while a fold system succeeds. Confirmed 50/50 toy (fold recalls 8.0 ops, storage 0.0 — perfect rank-order separation) and 5/5 real (fold_ops 5, storage_ops 0; mean divergence delta +0.225). Qualitatively, the storage-system literally writes meta-acknowledgements of memory absence ("lacks contextual information") while the fold-system performs recursive comparison ("identical to last week's incident" — Level 4 in the SIMFC hierarchy).

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
simrec p1: storage without fold-force fails recursive continuity prediction that a storage-only system (retrieves but doesn't fold) fails recursive continuity while a fold system succeeds. confirmed 50/50 toy (fold recalls 8.0 ops, storage 0.0 — perfect rank-order separation) and 5/5 real (fold_ops 5, storage_ops 0; mean divergence delta +0.225). qualitatively, the storage-system literally writes meta-acknowledgements of memory absence ( lacks contextual information ) while the fold-system performs recursive comparison ( identical to last week's incident — level 4 in the simfc hierarchy). result/finding simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ SIMReC P2: self-index emerges without an explicit self symbol

result/finding

Confirmed 50/50 toy and 5/5 real: self-index emerges in every seed without any programmed self token, drift stays bounded (mean 0.280-0.305 < 0.350 ceiling). Emergent identity from pressure alone.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
simrec p2: self-index emerges without an explicit self symbol confirmed 50/50 toy and 5/5 real: self-index emerges in every seed without any programmed self token, drift stays bounded (mean 0.280-0.305 < 0.350 ceiling). emergent identity from pressure alone. result/finding simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ SIMReC P3: index shuffling damages ownership selectively (cleanest dissociation)

result/finding

Shuffling index bindings selectively damages fold direction/ownership while factual top-1 retrieval remains intact. Confirmed 49/50 toy; 5/5 real, the "cleanest dissociation in the entire suite" — factual L2 = 0.000 in 20/20 probes, ownership L2 = 0.448 (shifted every seed). Required an architectural change to even test (weighted-memory prompts).

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
simrec p3: index shuffling damages ownership selectively (cleanest dissociation) shuffling index bindings selectively damages fold direction/ownership while factual top-1 retrieval remains intact. confirmed 49/50 toy; 5/5 real, the cleanest dissociation in the entire suite — factual l2 = 0.000 in 20/20 probes, ownership l2 = 0.448 (shifted every seed). required an architectural change to even test (weighted-memory prompts). result/finding simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ SIMReC P4*: asymmetric integration shifts fold direction (weakened form)

dead-endresult/finding

Confirmed 50/50 toy (direction shift 0.048, factual change 0.000). Only the weakened form is testable — P4-strong (cross-context fragmentation) requires edge-aware retrieval the current implementation lacks. Not run against real components.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
simrec p4*: asymmetric integration shifts fold direction (weakened form) confirmed 50/50 toy (direction shift 0.048, factual change 0.000). only the weakened form is testable — p4-strong (cross-context fragmentation) requires edge-aware retrieval the current implementation lacks. not run against real components. result/finding | dead-end simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ SIMReC P5: recursion without bounded drift bifurcates identity

result/finding

Confirmed 50/50 toy and 5/5 real: a loose drift bound (max_index_drift=0.8) produces 4.5x (toy) / 5.5x (real) higher step-to-step index variance than a tight bound (0.1). z=6.25. Without a continuity drift bound, identity bifurcates.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
simrec p5: recursion without bounded drift bifurcates identity confirmed 50/50 toy and 5/5 real: a loose drift bound (max_index_drift=0.8) produces 4.5x (toy) / 5.5x (real) higher step-to-step index variance than a tight bound (0.1). z=6.25. without a continuity drift bound, identity bifurcates. result/finding simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ SIMReC P6: selective fold weighting beats unrestricted recall

result/finding

Selective top-1 fold beats exhaustive fold. Confirmed 50/50 toy (error ratio 0.0 vs 0.426) and 5/5 real (selective error 0.066 vs exhaustive 0.835, 12.6x ratio). The advantage is amplified by a real LLM: in the toy substrate distractors only added direction-noise; with a real LLM the distractors actively poisoned the response.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
