simrec p6: selective fold weighting beats unrestricted recall selective top-1 fold beats exhaustive fold. confirmed 50/50 toy (error ratio 0.0 vs 0.426) and 5/5 real (selective error 0.066 vs exhaustive 0.835, 12.6x ratio). the advantage is *amplified* by a real llm: in the toy substrate distractors only added direction-noise; with a real llm the distractors actively poisoned the response. result/finding simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ SIMReC P7: operation-memory compression has an optimal band

result/finding

Prediction of an optimal compression band (windowed beats both no-compression and aggressive). Required three metric iterations to test what it claims. Toy passes 50/50 (but tautologically). Real: PASS with sparse op records, FALSIFIED with rich op records — the most consequential finding of the entire run.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
simrec p7: operation-memory compression has an optimal band prediction of an optimal compression band (windowed beats both no-compression and aggressive). required three metric iterations to test what it claims. toy passes 50/50 (but tautologically). real: pass with sparse op records, falsified with rich op records — the most consequential finding of the entire run. result/finding simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ P7 v1 was tautological (methodological dead-end)

dead-endresult/finding

The original metric F1(recovery, efficiency) is determined entirely by the compression operator's mechanics (op-node count after T steps), independent of LLM behavior. Identical scores across all 5 seeds. 1800 LLM calls produced data the metric ignored. Lesson: beware metrics that are functions of mechanism, not behavior.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
"The 1800 LLM calls produced data the metric ignored."
p7 v1 was tautological (methodological dead-end) the original metric f1(recovery, efficiency) is determined entirely by the compression operator's mechanics (op-node count after t steps), independent of llm behavior. identical scores across all 5 seeds. 1800 llm calls produced data the metric ignored. lesson: beware metrics that are functions of mechanism, not behavior. dead-end | result/finding simrec-reference\empirical_results.md the 1800 llm calls produced data the metric ignored.

◈ P7 v2 was topic-confounded (methodological dead-end)

dead-endresult/finding

The rank-against-all-GTs metric was confounded because the recall probe contained the topic name, so even with no relevant memory the LLM generated topic-conditioned text that ranked highly from token overlap. Aggressive (2 op nodes) had the highest recall — opposite to prediction. The metric measured topic identification, not decision recall. Lesson: topic-conditioned LLM generation is a strong baseline that must be subtracted.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
p7 v2 was topic-confounded (methodological dead-end) the rank-against-all-gts metric was confounded because the recall probe contained the topic name, so even with no relevant memory the llm generated topic-conditioned text that ranked highly from token overlap. aggressive (2 op nodes) had the *highest* recall — opposite to prediction. the metric measured topic identification, not decision recall. lesson: topic-conditioned llm generation is a strong baseline that must be subtracted. dead-end | result/finding simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ P7 v3: baseline-subtracted memory-contribution metric (the fix)

mechanismexperiment/benchmark

score(probe) = sim(recall_with_memory, GT) - sim(recall_no_memory, GT). Isolates the contribution of memory by subtracting a no-memory (topic-only) baseline. If retrieved ops actually inform recall, score is positive; if memory adds nothing beyond topic-conditioned generation, score ~0. Finally a metric that depends on LLM behavior.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
p7 v3: baseline-subtracted memory-contribution metric (the fix) score(probe) = sim(recall_with_memory, gt) - sim(recall_no_memory, gt). isolates the contribution of memory by subtracting a no-memory (topic-only) baseline. if retrieved ops actually inform recall, score is positive; if memory adds nothing beyond topic-conditioned generation, score ~0. finally a metric that depends on llm behavior. mechanism | experiment/benchmark simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ P7 v3 result and its reversal (the falsification)

result/finding

With sparse op nodes ({query, retrieved_ids, fold_norm, output_norm}), windowed beats none by +0.062 in 10/10 seeds (overload confirmed). But after preserving the LLM's actual response text in op nodes (a strictly more realistic schema), the "no-compression overloads" half FALSIFIED: windowed > none went from 10/10 to 0/5 seeds; mean_none jumped from 0.119 to 0.545. More storage = more useful info per retrieval = better recall.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
p7 v3 result and its reversal (the falsification) with sparse op nodes ({query, retrieved_ids, fold_norm, output_norm}), windowed beats none by +0.062 in 10/10 seeds (overload confirmed). but after preserving the llm's actual response text in op nodes (a strictly more realistic schema), the no-compression overloads half falsified: windowed > none went from 10/10 to 0/5 seeds; mean_none jumped from 0.119 to 0.545. more storage = more useful info per retrieval = better recall. result/finding simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ The conditional-on-information-density finding

framing/ontologyresult/finding

P7's overload is conditional on per-record information density. Sparse records + many of them -> retrieval noise dominates -> overload (P7 holds). Rich records + many of them -> more information per retrieval -> more is better (P7 falsifies). "Real cognitive systems probably live closer to the rich-record regime." An implementation change (more realistic op schema) falsified a framework prediction — an empirical update to the theory, not just the code.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
"The 'overload' pathology is a property of the SIMReC reference implementation's original schema, not of memory in general."
the conditional-on-information-density finding p7's overload is conditional on per-record information density. sparse records + many of them -> retrieval noise dominates -> overload (p7 holds). rich records + many of them -> more information per retrieval -> more is better (p7 falsifies). real cognitive systems probably live closer to the rich-record regime. an implementation change (more realistic op schema) falsified a framework prediction — an empirical update to the theory, not just the code. result/finding | framing/ontology simrec-reference\empirical_results.md the 'overload' pathology is a property of the simrec reference implementation's original schema, not of memory in general.

◈ Trajectory-confounded intervention metric (long-horizon methodology finding)

result/finding

In the P1 real run, storage_final_divergence (0.991) was larger than fold_final_divergence (0.765) — counterintuitively. Interpretation: a well-functioning recursive-memory system shows less per-step divergence as memory becomes absorbed into its running interpretation, while the memoryless storage-system swings wildly when a fold is finally injected. So D(T_with||T_without) is trajectory-confounded at long horizons; the relevant signal is the accumulation Sum_t Delta_t^op, not per-step divergence. Invisible in the memoryless toy substrate.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
trajectory-confounded intervention metric (long-horizon methodology finding) in the p1 real run, storage_final_divergence (0.991) was *larger* than fold_final_divergence (0.765) — counterintuitively. interpretation: a well-functioning recursive-memory system shows *less* per-step divergence as memory becomes absorbed into its running interpretation, while the memoryless storage-system swings wildly when a fold is finally injected. so d(t_with||t_without) is trajectory-confounded at long horizons; the relevant signal is the accumulation sum_t delta_t^op, not per-step divergence. invisible in the memoryless toy substrate. result/finding simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ Weighted-memory prompt path (P3 enabling adapter)

mechanism

To make fold weights visible to an LLM-Phi, memories are rendered with explicit importance labels derived from graph.fold_weight: "- (high|medium|low importance) <text>", sorted by weight descending. Shuffling index_binding changes fold_weight changes the labels while contents are unchanged. This turned P3 from "untestable" into the cleanest dissociation in the suite.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
weighted-memory prompt path (p3 enabling adapter) to make fold weights visible to an llm-phi, memories are rendered with explicit importance labels derived from graph.fold_weight: - (high|medium|low importance) <text> , sorted by weight descending. shuffling index_binding changes fold_weight changes the labels while contents are unchanged. this turned p3 from untestable into the cleanest dissociation in the suite. mechanism simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ Caveat: weighted-memory prompts put a thumb on the scale

open-questionresult/finding

A sufficiently agreeable LLM will respect any importance label inserted into a prompt, so P3's result validates the framework "conditional on the LLM-Phi adapter being reasonable." A skeptic could argue the LLM is "just doing what the prompt told it." But the LLM had no way to know which memories were originally important before vs after shuffle, and the strong pattern (factual unchanged, ownership shifted) is exactly what the manuscript predicts.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
caveat: weighted-memory prompts put a thumb on the scale a sufficiently agreeable llm will respect any importance label inserted into a prompt, so p3's result validates the framework conditional on the llm-phi adapter being reasonable. a skeptic could argue the llm is just doing what the prompt told it. but the llm had no way to know which memories were originally important before vs after shuffle, and the strong pattern (factual unchanged, ownership shifted) is exactly what the manuscript predicts. open-question | result/finding simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ Preserving LLM responses in operation nodes (realism change that falsified P7)

mechanismresult/finding

transition_step reads last_response from the transition via getattr (loose coupling — toy default_transition unaffected) and write_operation accepts response_text stored in content + folded into address_key. Op nodes become retrievable by what was decided, not only what was asked. This realism increase is what falsified P7's overload half.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
preserving llm responses in operation nodes (realism change that falsified p7) transition_step reads last_response from the transition via getattr (loose coupling — toy default_transition unaffected) and write_operation accepts response_text stored in content + folded into address_key. op nodes become retrievable by what was *decided*, not only what was *asked*. this realism increase is what falsified p7's overload half. mechanism | result/finding simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ P4 architectural gap: retrieval ignores graph edges

open-questiondead-end

P4 (integration ablation) requires retrieve() to consult graph edges so cross-channel binding changes the retrieved set, but the implementation scores residues by embedding similarity, persistence, and self-index alone — edges contribute nothing. With uniform integration boost, fold_norm is identical (0.8212 vs 0.8212) because fold() normalizes by total_weight and uniform scaling cancels. Closing it needs personalized PageRank or graph-attention over the relation structure.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
p4 architectural gap: retrieval ignores graph edges p4 (integration ablation) requires retrieve() to consult graph edges so cross-channel binding changes the retrieved set, but the implementation scores residues by embedding similarity, persistence, and self-index alone — edges contribute nothing. with uniform integration boost, fold_norm is identical (0.8212 vs 0.8212) because fold() normalizes by total_weight and uniform scaling cancels. closing it needs personalized pagerank or graph-attention over the relation structure. dead-end | open-question simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ Real components amplify predicted effects rather than wash them out

result/finding

A general finding: semantic retrieval + real LLM Phi produced larger effect sizes than the toy substrate for P5 (5.5x vs 4.5x variance ratio) and P6 (12.6x vs 4.7x error ratio). The framework's dissociations strengthen, not weaken, under realistic substrate.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
real components amplify predicted effects rather than wash them out a general finding: semantic retrieval + real llm phi produced *larger* effect sizes than the toy substrate for p5 (5.5x vs 4.5x variance ratio) and p6 (12.6x vs 4.7x error ratio). the framework's dissociations strengthen, not weaken, under realistic substrate. result/finding simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ Internal consistency does not equal cognitive validity (honest scoping)

framing/ontologyopen-question

"Predictions hold" means internal dissociations behave as the manuscript claims, NOT that the framework outperforms baselines on real cognition. Nothing was validated against real downstream cognitive tasks (planning, multi-hop QA, contradiction repair, agentic eval). The parameter-replacement hypothesis (small-LLM + SIMReC vs larger pure-parametric LLM) is unanswered.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
internal consistency does not equal cognitive validity (honest scoping) predictions hold means internal dissociations behave as the manuscript claims, not that the framework outperforms baselines on real cognition. nothing was validated against real downstream cognitive tasks (planning, multi-hop qa, contradiction repair, agentic eval). the parameter-replacement hypothesis (small-llm + simrec vs larger pure-parametric llm) is unanswered. open-question | framing/ontology simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ Cost ledger discipline

experiment/benchmark

Every real-component run tracked LLM calls and Anthropic spend (~8,300 calls, ~$3.30 total, $5 budget). P7 alone consumed ~$2.91 across four metric iterations. A methodological practice: budget-aware experiment design (--budget-usd flag) for LLM-substrate validation.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
cost ledger discipline every real-component run tracked llm calls and anthropic spend (~8,300 calls, ~$3.30 total, $5 budget). p7 alone consumed ~$2.91 across four metric iterations. a methodological practice: budget-aware experiment design (--budget-usd flag) for llm-substrate validation. experiment/benchmark simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ Recommended next step: downstream task harness

experiment/benchmarkopen-question

Pick one task that exercises all six dimensions (long-horizon agentic dialog with consistency requirements, contradiction repair across sessions), rerun predictions on top of it, compare against vanilla LLM, RAG, and MemGPT baselines. "The experiment that turns 'internally coherent toy' into 'research result.'"

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
recommended next step: downstream task harness pick one task that exercises all six dimensions (long-horizon agentic dialog with consistency requirements, contradiction repair across sessions), rerun predictions on top of it, compare against vanilla llm, rag, and memgpt baselines. the experiment that turns 'internally coherent toy' into 'research result.' experiment/benchmark | open-question simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ Recommended next step: test the parameter-replacement hypothesis

experiment/benchmarkopen-question

Hybrid small-LLM + SIMReC vs a larger pure-parametric LLM on standard benchmarks (MMLU, AgentBench, RULER, LongBench). Could externalized memory reduce LLM parameter requirements? A major project, only worth doing if the downstream harness shows signal.

simrec-reference\EMPIRICAL_RESULTS.md
recommended next step: test the parameter-replacement hypothesis hybrid small-llm + simrec vs a larger pure-parametric llm on standard benchmarks (mmlu, agentbench, ruler, longbench). could externalized memory reduce llm parameter requirements? a major project, only worth doing if the downstream harness shows signal. open-question | experiment/benchmark simrec-reference\empirical_results.md

◈ Revised P7 as a phase-boundary theorem

framing/ontologyresult/finding

The revision reframes meta-memory overload from a primitive property of storage volume into a retrieval-interference pathology. Phase table: sparse/confusable (low specificity, small margin) -> overload likely, compression helps; rich/distinctive (high, large) -> overload unlikely, full preservation helps; rich but bandwidth/cost-limited -> accuracy improves but efficiency optimum needs compression; aggressively compressed (target absent) -> continuity fails.

trace_validation_framework\docs\SIMReC_P7_revision_patch.md
revised p7 as a phase-boundary theorem the revision reframes meta-memory overload from a primitive property of storage volume into a retrieval-interference pathology. phase table: sparse/confusable (low specificity, small margin) -> overload likely, compression helps; rich/distinctive (high, large) -> overload unlikely, full preservation helps; rich but bandwidth/cost-limited -> accuracy improves but efficiency optimum needs compression; aggressively compressed (target absent) -> continuity fails. framing/ontology | result/finding trace_validation_framework\docs\simrec_p7_revision_patch.md

◈ Confusability as the missing load-bearing variable

framing/ontologycoinage

The revision patch adds query-conditioned confusability chi_t(q) = Pr[M_t(q) <= delta] as the term that determines overload. Meta-memory overload is expected when chi_t is high and k_t (retrieval bandwidth) is small; not expected when chi_t is low and records are distinctive. This is the variable the original P7 omitted.

trace_validation_framework\docs\SIMReC_P7_revision_patch.md
confusability as the missing load-bearing variable the revision patch adds query-conditioned confusability chi_t(q) = pr[m_t(q) <= delta] as the term that determines overload. meta-memory overload is expected when chi_t is high and k_t (retrieval bandwidth) is small; not expected when chi_t is low and records are distinctive. this is the variable the original p7 omitted. coinage | framing/ontology trace_validation_framework\docs\simrec_p7_revision_patch.md

◈ Separate accuracy optimum from resource optimum

framing/ontology

If the dependent variable is accuracy/continuity alone, the rich-record regime makes full preservation optimal (argmax = K_full). If the objective includes cost (storage S, latency L, compute/context C), an interior compression optimum reappears. This prevents confusing a cognitive accuracy claim with an engineering efficiency claim.

trace_validation_framework\docs\SIMReC_P7_revision_patch.md
separate accuracy optimum from resource optimum if the dependent variable is accuracy/continuity alone, the rich-record regime makes full preservation optimal (argmax = k_full). if the objective includes cost (storage s, latency l, compute/context c), an interior compression optimum reappears. this prevents confusing a cognitive accuracy claim with an engineering efficiency claim. framing/ontology trace_validation_framework\docs\simrec_p7_revision_patch.md

◈ Updated compression operator with third constraint

mechanism

The revised operator K* = argmin_K [|K(V^op)| + beta*D(T^full||T^K)] subject to per-variable divergence bound AND a top-k retrieval-preservation constraint: Pr[r* in Top_k(K(V^op),q)] >= Pr[r* in Top_k(V^op,q)] - epsilon_H. The third constraint prevents compression from preserving abstract operation type while deleting the substantive decision content needed for later recursive self-revision.

trace_validation_framework\docs\SIMReC_P7_revision_patch.md
updated compression operator with third constraint the revised operator k* = argmin_k [|k(v^op)| + beta*d(t^full||t^k)] subject to per-variable divergence bound and a top-k retrieval-preservation constraint: pr[r* in top_k(k(v^op),q)] >= pr[r* in top_k(v^op,q)] - epsilon_h. the third constraint prevents compression from preserving abstract operation type while deleting the substantive decision content needed for later recursive self-revision. mechanism trace_validation_framework\docs\simrec_p7_revision_patch.md

◈ Editorial reframing: forgetting is not "less is better"

framing/ontology

The empirical reversal of P7 clarifies that forgetting is not constitutive in the simple sense that less memory is always better. What is constitutive is specificity-preserving selection. Compression is beneficial only when it reduces retrieval interference without deleting transition-relevant decision content.

trace_validation_framework\docs\SIMReC_P7_revision_patch.md
"What is constitutive is specificity-preserving selection."
editorial reframing: forgetting is not less is better the empirical reversal of p7 clarifies that forgetting is not constitutive in the simple sense that less memory is always better. what is constitutive is *specificity-preserving selection*. compression is beneficial only when it reduces retrieval interference without deleting transition-relevant decision content. framing/ontology trace_validation_framework\docs\simrec_p7_revision_patch.md what is constitutive is specificity-preserving selection.

◈ Reporting standard for memory experiments

experiment/benchmark

A methodological recommendation added to the manuscript's Limitations: future experiments should report record schema, embedding method, top-k bandwidth, target rank, similarity margin, and distractor similarity distribution — the variables that determine which regime a system is in.

trace_validation_framework\docs\SIMReC_P7_revision_patch.md
reporting standard for memory experiments a methodological recommendation added to the manuscript's limitations: future experiments should report record schema, embedding method, top-k bandwidth, target rank, similarity margin, and distractor similarity distribution — the variables that determine which regime a system is in. experiment/benchmark trace_validation_framework\docs\simrec_p7_revision_patch.md

◈ Related-work gap: hippocampal indexing theory is the literal prior "index"

framing/ontologyopen-question

The manuscript's central "self-index" construct has a 35-year prior in memory neuroscience — the hippocampal index (Teyler & DiScenna 1986; Teyler & Rudy 2007). The core move is identical: separate content from a pointer/binding structure that reactivates content during recall. SIMFC should either inherit and credit this lineage or explicitly distinguish self-indexing from memory-indexing. Grounds the index in an existing falsifiable neural theory.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work gap: hippocampal indexing theory is the literal prior index the manuscript's central self-index construct has a 35-year prior in memory neuroscience — the hippocampal index (teyler & discenna 1986; teyler & rudy 2007). the core move is identical: separate content from a pointer/binding structure that reactivates content during recall. simfc should either inherit and credit this lineage or explicitly distinguish self-indexing from memory-indexing. grounds the index in an existing falsifiable neural theory. framing/ontology | open-question reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: engram optogenetics as interventionist memory test

framing/ontology

Liu et al. 2012 (Nature) and Ramirez et al. 2013 (Science) provide gain-of-function/sufficiency optogenetic tests of the engram-as-index. SIMFC's do(mu<-0) criterion is the loss-of-function dual. A 2025 "Engrams and causal specificity" paper fuses Woodward interventionism with information theory — the closest prior art to SIMFC's information-theoretic do-criterion.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: engram optogenetics as interventionist memory test liu et al. 2012 (nature) and ramirez et al. 2013 (science) provide gain-of-function/sufficiency optogenetic tests of the engram-as-index. simfc's do(mu<-0) criterion is the loss-of-function dual. a 2025 engrams and causal specificity paper fuses woodward interventionism with information theory — the closest prior art to simfc's information-theoretic do-criterion. framing/ontology reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: metacognition/source-monitoring is the science of "memory of operations"

framing/ontologyopen-question

SIMFC's recursive-depth dimension (memory of prior operations) is structurally identical to constructs the metacognition/HOT literature studied for 35 years: Nelson & Narens' object-level/meta-level metamemory, Johnson's Source Monitoring Framework (whose "cognitive operations" dimension is exactly SIMFC's recursive-memory target), Fleming & Daw's second-order Bayesian metacognition, Wang et al.'s meta-RL. SIMFC's contribution there is a graph-operator formalism; the empirical phenomena already have measurement paradigms.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: metacognition/source-monitoring is the science of memory of operations simfc's recursive-depth dimension (memory of prior operations) is structurally identical to constructs the metacognition/hot literature studied for 35 years: nelson & narens' object-level/meta-level metamemory, johnson's source monitoring framework (whose cognitive operations dimension is exactly simfc's recursive-memory target), fleming & daw's second-order bayesian metacognition, wang et al.'s meta-rl. simfc's contribution there is a graph-operator formalism; the empirical phenomena already have measurement paradigms. framing/ontology | open-question reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: recursive remindings as closest empirical instance of foldable operation-nodes

framing/ontology

The recursive-remindings literature (Hintzman; Jacoby; Negley/Kelley/Jacoby) shows memory for a List-1 response embedded within memory for a List-2 response, with proactive facilitation only when the prior retrieval is recollected. Plus the testing effect (Karpicke & Roediger 2008): retrieval acts modify the trace. These are the closest empirical instantiations of SIMFC's operation-node-as-foldable-residue.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: recursive remindings as closest empirical instance of foldable operation-nodes the recursive-remindings literature (hintzman; jacoby; negley/kelley/jacoby) shows memory for a list-1 response embedded within memory for a list-2 response, with proactive facilitation *only when the prior retrieval is recollected*. plus the testing effect (karpicke & roediger 2008): retrieval acts modify the trace. these are the closest empirical instantiations of simfc's operation-node-as-foldable-residue. framing/ontology reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: Prediction 3 is already half-observed clinically

framing/ontologyresult/finding

The predicted dissociation of memory content from ownership is instantiated in at least four neuropsychiatric phenotypes: patient R.B. (Klein & Nichols 2012 — retained content but memories didn't feel "his"), depersonalization disorder ("factual aspect preserved but without the feeling the event really happened"), schizophrenia ipseity disturbance (Sass & Parnas), and persistent déjà vécu / confabulation. Converts Prediction 3 from "in-principle testable" to "tested in N existing pathologies."

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: prediction 3 is already half-observed clinically the predicted dissociation of memory content from ownership is instantiated in at least four neuropsychiatric phenotypes: patient r.b. (klein & nichols 2012 — retained content but memories didn't feel his ), depersonalization disorder ( factual aspect preserved but without the feeling the event really happened ), schizophrenia ipseity disturbance (sass & parnas), and persistent déjà vécu / confabulation. converts prediction 3 from in-principle testable to tested in n existing pathologies. framing/ontology | result/finding reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: Conway's Self-Memory System as closest cognitive model to the self-index

framing/ontology

Conway & Pleydell-Pearce's SMS (working self + autobiographical knowledge base with reciprocal coupling) is structurally the same bidirectional coupling SIMFC posits between z_t and M_t via the index-update operator J. Conway's adaptive-correspondence-vs-self-coherence tension recapitulates SIMFC's continuity-revision paradox.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: conway's self-memory system as closest cognitive model to the self-index conway & pleydell-pearce's sms (working self + autobiographical knowledge base with reciprocal coupling) is structurally the same bidirectional coupling simfc posits between z_t and m_t via the index-update operator j. conway's adaptive-correspondence-vs-self-coherence tension recapitulates simfc's continuity-revision paradox. framing/ontology reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: self-as-emergent-construct consensus

framing/ontologyopen-question

Every major modern account treats the self as an emergent functional construct, not a primitive: Metzinger's Phenomenal Self-Model, Hofstadter's strange loop, FEP/active-inference self-models (Hohwy, Limanowski & Blankenburg, Seth), Graziano's Attention Schema, Dennett's center of narrative gravity, Deane's deep self-models. SIMFC's Proposition 2 (self-index emergence without a primitive self token) sits squarely inside this consensus but cites none of it.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: self-as-emergent-construct consensus every major modern account treats the self as an emergent functional construct, not a primitive: metzinger's phenomenal self-model, hofstadter's strange loop, fep/active-inference self-models (hohwy, limanowski & blankenburg, seth), graziano's attention schema, dennett's center of narrative gravity, deane's deep self-models. simfc's proposition 2 (self-index emergence without a primitive self token) sits squarely inside this consensus but cites none of it. framing/ontology | open-question reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: Lipson self-modeling robots as constructive existence proof

framing/ontology

The Lipson-lab self-modeling robotics line (Bongard/Zykov/Lipson 2006; Kwiatkowski/Lipson 2019) is the only literature where an agent derives a self-variable purely from prediction/control objectives, in hardware, with no programmed self token. It is essentially a constructive existence proof that SIMFC's Proposition 2 is realizable.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: lipson self-modeling robots as constructive existence proof the lipson-lab self-modeling robotics line (bongard/zykov/lipson 2006; kwiatkowski/lipson 2019) is the only literature where an agent derives a self-variable purely from prediction/control objectives, in hardware, with no programmed self token. it is essentially a constructive existence proof that simfc's proposition 2 is realizable. framing/ontology reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: AI/LLM agent memory is the biggest engagement gap

framing/ontologyopen-question

The manuscript's keywords include "agent memory" and "cognitive architecture" yet the bibliography cites zero AI-memory-architecture references. SIMFC must differentiate from four concrete lines of prior art: differentiable external memory (NTM/DNC/Memory Networks), retrieval-augmented LLMs (RAG/Atlas/REALM), agent reflection systems (Reflexion/Self-Refine/CRITIC), and the formal blueprint CoALA.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: ai/llm agent memory is the biggest engagement gap the manuscript's keywords include agent memory and cognitive architecture yet the bibliography cites zero ai-memory-architecture references. simfc must differentiate from four concrete lines of prior art: differentiable external memory (ntm/dnc/memory networks), retrieval-augmented llms (rag/atlas/realm), agent reflection systems (reflexion/self-refine/critic), and the formal blueprint coala. open-question | framing/ontology reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: DNC temporal link matrix as ancestor of operation-nodes

framing/ontology

The Differentiable Neural Computer (Graves et al. 2016) extends NTMs with a temporal link matrix that records the order of writes — "the closest deep-learning ancestor to SIMFC's operation-node construction." Neural Episodic Control's differentiable neural dictionary is the RL episodic-memory analog.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: dnc temporal link matrix as ancestor of operation-nodes the differentiable neural computer (graves et al. 2016) extends ntms with a temporal link matrix that records the order of writes — the closest deep-learning ancestor to simfc's operation-node construction. neural episodic control's differentiable neural dictionary is the rl episodic-memory analog. framing/ontology reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: Reflexion is the clearest prior art for operation-memory

framing/ontology

Reflexion (Shinn et al. 2023) uses an episodic memory buffer of self-generated verbal reflections on prior failures, folded back into the next trial — "the clearest direct instance of SIMFC operation-memory." ReAct's thought-action-observation traces and Toolformer's tool-call records are also operation-trace precedents; Self-Refine and CRITIC are recursion operators on the agent's own output.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: reflexion is the clearest prior art for operation-memory reflexion (shinn et al. 2023) uses an episodic memory buffer of self-generated verbal reflections on prior failures, folded back into the next trial — the clearest direct instance of simfc operation-memory. react's thought-action-observation traces and toolformer's tool-call records are also operation-trace precedents; self-refine and critic are recursion operators on the agent's own output. framing/ontology reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: closest graph-memory neighbors SIMFC must beat

framing/ontologyopen-question

Four 2024-2025 systems sit closest and must be differentiated: A-MEM (Zettelkasten self-linking memory graph — strongest "graph-operator self-indexing" precedent), AriGraph (evolving semantic+episodic memory graph for TextWorld agents), Graph of Thoughts (graph-operator over transient thoughts, not persistent memory), Nemori (cognitive-science-grounded event segmentation + FEP). Plus MemGPT (OS-inspired tiered memory), Generative Agents (memory stream + importance-weighted retrieval + reflection + planning).

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: closest graph-memory neighbors simfc must beat four 2024-2025 systems sit closest and must be differentiated: a-mem (zettelkasten self-linking memory graph — strongest graph-operator self-indexing precedent), arigraph (evolving semantic+episodic memory graph for textworld agents), graph of thoughts (graph-operator over transient thoughts, not persistent memory), nemori (cognitive-science-grounded event segmentation + fep). plus memgpt (os-inspired tiered memory), generative agents (memory stream + importance-weighted retrieval + reflection + planning). framing/ontology | open-question reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: CoALA is the most important formal reference

framing/ontology

CoALA (Sumers/Yao/Narasimhan/Griffiths 2024, TMLR) is the dominant published blueprint organizing language agents along working/episodic/semantic/procedural memory + internal/external actions + planning loop. SIMFC and CoALA address the same abstraction level with different vocabularies (operator calculus vs cognitive-architecture taxonomy) and must be contrasted explicitly.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: coala is the most important formal reference coala (sumers/yao/narasimhan/griffiths 2024, tmlr) is the dominant published blueprint organizing language agents along working/episodic/semantic/procedural memory + internal/external actions + planning loop. simfc and coala address the same abstraction level with different vocabularies (operator calculus vs cognitive-architecture taxonomy) and must be contrasted explicitly. framing/ontology reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: the novelty claim

framing/ontologyresult/finding

Across all surveyed literature there is NO paper that explicitly defines an operator calculus over a graph-of-memory for agent self-indexing. This supports SIMFC's novelty. None of the neighbors offers a formal operator calculus, an interventionist memory criterion, OR an MDL-derived self-index — SIMFC's contribution is the unifying formal layer over an architectural space they already populate.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
"there is no paper that explicitly defines an operator calculus over a graph-of-memory for agent self-indexing."
related-work: the novelty claim across all surveyed literature there is no paper that explicitly defines an operator calculus over a graph-of-memory for agent self-indexing. this supports simfc's novelty. none of the neighbors offers a formal operator calculus, an interventionist memory criterion, or an mdl-derived self-index — simfc's contribution is the unifying formal layer over an architectural space they already populate. framing/ontology | result/finding reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md there is no paper that explicitly defines an operator calculus over a graph-of-memory for agent self-indexing.

◈ Related-work: interventionist causation grounds the do-criterion

framing/ontologyopen-question

SIMFC's do(mu<-0) criterion for cognitive memory has the same logical form as Woodward's interventionism ("X causes Y" iff an ideal intervention on X changes Y) and Craver's mutual-manipulability for constitutive relevance. The manuscript should flag the contested debate (Baumgartner, Couch, Leuridan) over whether do-interventions are legitimately constitutive vs merely epistemic tests of memory.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: interventionist causation grounds the do-criterion simfc's do(mu<-0) criterion for cognitive memory has the same logical form as woodward's interventionism ( x causes y iff an ideal intervention on x changes y) and craver's mutual-manipulability for constitutive relevance. the manuscript should flag the contested debate (baumgartner, couch, leuridan) over whether do-interventions are legitimately *constitutive* vs merely *epistemic* tests of memory. framing/ontology | open-question reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: MDL/compression grounds the self-index (J-functional)

framing/ontology

SIMFC's MDL/J-functional derivation of the self-index sits inside a large uncited literature: Hinton & van Camp's bits-back coding, Honkela & Valpola's variational-inference=MDL equivalence, Friston's FEP (the brain as compressor). The single closest prior proposal is Schmidhuber's compression-progress theory (2008): a self-symbol emerges in a world-model purely because encoding the always-present agent yields better compression of action-perception history. SIMFC's J_t formalizes Schmidhuber's prose argument.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: mdl/compression grounds the self-index (j-functional) simfc's mdl/j-functional derivation of the self-index sits inside a large uncited literature: hinton & van camp's bits-back coding, honkela & valpola's variational-inference=mdl equivalence, friston's fep (the brain as compressor). the single closest prior proposal is schmidhuber's compression-progress theory (2008): a self-symbol emerges in a world-model purely because encoding the always-present agent yields better compression of action-perception history. simfc's j_t formalizes schmidhuber's prose argument. framing/ontology reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: J-functional reformulable as Information Bottleneck

framing/ontologyopen-question

SIMFC's J-functional could be reformulated as an Information Bottleneck objective (Tishby & Zaslavsky: minimize I(X;T) subject to preserving I(T;Y)) — noted as not yet applied to a derived self-token — and tested empirically. Plus Premakumar et al. 2024 and Bhui/Lai/Gershman 2024 provide recent empirical support that self-modeling / dual-process structure emerges from minimizing description length.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: j-functional reformulable as information bottleneck simfc's j-functional could be reformulated as an information bottleneck objective (tishby & zaslavsky: minimize i(x;t) subject to preserving i(t;y)) — noted as not yet applied to a *derived self-token* — and tested empirically. plus premakumar et al. 2024 and bhui/lai/gershman 2024 provide recent empirical support that self-modeling / dual-process structure emerges from minimizing description length. open-question | framing/ontology reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: Kanerva SDM / attention lineage

framing/ontologyopen-question

Kanerva's Sparse Distributed Memory (1988) is an explicit address/pointer abstraction for distributed memory; Bricken & Pehlevan (2021) showed SDM approximates Transformer attention — drawing a direct line from the old "indexing" tradition to current LLM substrates. Von der Malsburg's "binding" imported CS variable-binding; SIMFC's repeated use of "binding" should be reconciled with or demarcated from this prior usage.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: kanerva sdm / attention lineage kanerva's sparse distributed memory (1988) is an explicit address/pointer abstraction for distributed memory; bricken & pehlevan (2021) showed sdm approximates transformer attention — drawing a direct line from the old indexing tradition to current llm substrates. von der malsburg's binding imported cs variable-binding; simfc's repeated use of binding should be reconciled with or demarcated from this prior usage. framing/ontology | open-question reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: agent identity evaluation benchmarks

framing/ontologyexperiment/benchmark

Emerging benchmarks directly relevant to operationalizing SIMFC's self-index predictions: Agent Identity Evals (Perrier & Bennett 2025 — identifiability, continuity, persistence, consistency), Generative Agent Simulations of 1,000 People, Spontaneous Emergence of Agent Individuality (Takata et al. 2024), and "What Do LLM Agents Do When Left Alone?" (2025 — reports recursive self-conceptualization in unsupervised agents with persistent memory). Natural test benches for the emergence claims.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: agent identity evaluation benchmarks emerging benchmarks directly relevant to operationalizing simfc's self-index predictions: agent identity evals (perrier & bennett 2025 — identifiability, continuity, persistence, consistency), generative agent simulations of 1,000 people, spontaneous emergence of agent individuality (takata et al. 2024), and what do llm agents do when left alone? (2025 — reports recursive self-conceptualization in unsupervised agents with persistent memory). natural test benches for the emergence claims. experiment/benchmark | framing/ontology reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: Constitutional AI as self-representation precedent

framing/ontology

Constitutional AI (Bai et al. 2022) trains an LLM to critique/revise its own outputs against an internalized constitution — the closest mainstream LLM precedent for explicit self-representation as a normative model maintained across generations.

reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md
related-work: constitutional ai as self-representation precedent constitutional ai (bai et al. 2022) trains an llm to critique/revise its own outputs against an internalized constitution — the closest mainstream llm precedent for explicit self-representation as a normative model maintained across generations. framing/ontology reports\self-indexing-recursive-memory-systems-related-work.md

◈ Related-work: eighteen highest-leverage citations

framing/ontology

The report distills 104 references down to 18 highest-leverage additions in priority order: Teyler & Rudy 2007, Goode et al. 2020, Conway & Pleydell-Pearce 2000, Klein & Nichols 2012, Sass & Parnas 2003, Limanowski & Blankenburg 2013, Bongard et al. 2006, Graves et al. 2016 (DNC), Reflexion, Generative Agents, CoALA, A-MEM, Woodward 2003, Craver 2007, Liu et al. 2012, Engrams and causal specificity 2025, Hinton & van Camp 1993, Schmidhuber 2008.
