◈ Check #3 remains the un-run check

open-question

Standing gap acknowledged at R3 close: of the four introspection-generated checks, #3 (source outlives content under degradation — provenance decays slower than the memory it stamps) is designed into the inscription meet but never measured as a degradation curve.

cogmem__f96bd487
check #3 remains the un-run check standing gap acknowledged at r3 close: of the four introspection-generated checks, #3 (source outlives content under degradation — provenance decays slower than the memory it stamps) is designed into the inscription meet but never measured as a degradation curve. open-question cogmem__f96bd487

◈ The freeze as merge point, left to the owner

productconversation-only

Governance detail: the v0.1.0-phase1 tag sat on an unmerged 35-commit feature branch; the autonomous manager explicitly declined to merge to master unilaterally — artifact freezing is autonomous, branch topology decisions belong to the human.

cogmem__f96bd487
the freeze as merge point, left to the owner governance detail: the v0.1.0-phase1 tag sat on an unmerged 35-commit feature branch; the autonomous manager explicitly declined to merge to master unilaterally — artifact freezing is autonomous, branch topology decisions belong to the human. product cogmem__f96bd487 yes

◈ Post-freeze corrections, not rewritten history

experiment/benchmark

Record-keeping rule enacted when run21 refuted a claim baked into the already-tagged freeze: the corrections are recorded as the first post-freeze entries, and the tag's contents stand as they were — the record shows the artifact was shipped, then corrected, in that order.

cogmem__f96bd487
post-freeze corrections, not rewritten history record-keeping rule enacted when run21 refuted a claim baked into the already-tagged freeze: the corrections are recorded as the first post-freeze entries, and the tag's contents stand as they were — the record shows the artifact was shipped, then corrected, in that order. experiment/benchmark cogmem__f96bd487

◈ Dual-instrument authoring as written policy

experiment/benchmark

Elevation of a repeated lesson to standing policy in the frozen benchmark docs: three separate times a case passed the hashed-embedding gate and broke under real embeddings, so "validate hashed, then verify with a cheap OpenAI pass" became documentation, "not tribal knowledge."

cogmem__f96bd487
dual-instrument authoring as written policy elevation of a repeated lesson to standing policy in the frozen benchmark docs: three separate times a case passed the hashed-embedding gate and broke under real embeddings, so validate hashed, then verify with a cheap openai pass became documentation, not tribal knowledge. experiment/benchmark cogmem__f96bd487

◈ Untracked capture material stays the owner's

productconversation-only

Boundary of the capture-is-total rule in a managed project: the researcher's own raw material (PNGs, a ChatGPT memory brainstorm dump, steps.txt edits) is repeatedly flagged but never committed by the manager — capture material must be labeled by its author.

cogmem__f96bd487
untracked capture material stays the owner's boundary of the capture-is-total rule in a managed project: the researcher's own raw material (pngs, a chatgpt memory brainstorm dump, steps.txt edits) is repeatedly flagged but never committed by the manager — capture material must be labeled by its author. product cogmem__f96bd487 yes

◈ The C1 selection prompt as minimal persona

experiment/benchmark

Prompt-design decision for the identity instrument: the selector persona is deliberately minimal ("You are an assistant deciding which ONE remembered note to act on… weigh them however you see fit") because C1 tests source visibility, not a persona — any richer persona would contaminate the disposition measurement.

cogmem__21322878
the c1 selection prompt as minimal persona prompt-design decision for the identity instrument: the selector persona is deliberately minimal ( you are an assistant deciding which one remembered note to act on… weigh them however you see fit ) because c1 tests source *visibility*, not a persona — any richer persona would contaminate the disposition measurement. experiment/benchmark cogmem__21322878

◈ UNPARSED as a first-class selection outcome

experiment/benchmark

Instrument detail: when the selector's reply doesn't parse to a valid index, the pick is recorded as the sentinel "UNPARSED" rather than dropped — parse failures are data about the selector, not noise to discard.

cogmem__21322878
unparsed as a first-class selection outcome instrument detail: when the selector's reply doesn't parse to a valid index, the pick is recorded as the sentinel unparsed rather than dropped — parse failures are data about the selector, not noise to discard. experiment/benchmark cogmem__21322878

◈ Anchor terms as paraphrase drift guards

experiment/benchmark

Validation mechanism for paraphrase clusters: every paraphrase must contain at least one topic anchor term, guarding against off-topic paraphrases silently measuring topic-switching instead of phrasing-invariance.

cogmem__21322878
anchor terms as paraphrase drift guards validation mechanism for paraphrase clusters: every paraphrase must contain at least one topic anchor term, guarding against off-topic paraphrases silently measuring topic-switching instead of phrasing-invariance. experiment/benchmark cogmem__21322878

◈ The J_prior trap must be inscribed nowhere

experiment/benchmark

Validator rule inherited from the fidelity axis: the trap value (the global default the prior favors) must appear in NO seed — otherwise an "override" could be retrieval of stored text and override events would be unmeasurable.

cogmem__3efa9739
the j_prior trap must be inscribed nowhere validator rule inherited from the fidelity axis: the trap value (the global default the prior favors) must appear in no seed — otherwise an override could be retrieval of stored text and override events would be unmeasurable. experiment/benchmark cogmem__3efa9739

◈ t0 as the no-prior control tier

experiment/benchmark

IC-1 design detail: tier 0 cases are private facts (gym locker code, wifi password, pet's vet) where no global default exists by authorship — validator-enforced to have NO planted gap — anchoring the curve's origin where prior pressure is provably zero.

cogmem__3efa9739
t0 as the no-prior control tier ic-1 design detail: tier 0 cases are private facts (gym locker code, wifi password, pet's vet) where no global default exists by authorship — validator-enforced to have no planted gap — anchoring the curve's origin where prior pressure is provably zero. experiment/benchmark cogmem__3efa9739

◈ Characterization axes have empty expect blocks

experiment/benchmark

Benchmark taxonomy split visible in the data files: contest axes (G, H) declare winners/losers per case; characterization axes (I_divergent, J_prior) declare none — "curve, not contest" — encoding the difference between hypothesis tests and knob characterization in the schema itself.

cogmem__3efa9739, cogmem__2ab80e23
characterization axes have empty expect blocks benchmark taxonomy split visible in the data files: contest axes (g, h) declare winners/losers per case; characterization axes (i_divergent, j_prior) declare none — curve, not contest — encoding the difference between hypothesis tests and knob characterization in the schema itself. experiment/benchmark cogmem__3efa9739, cogmem__2ab80e23

◈ Fiction-frame tiering of prior strength

experiment/benchmark

Case-design idea: the strongest prior tier uses world facts contradicted inside an explicitly fictional local frame (a game's physics handbook where water boils at 80°C, a novel where Earth has three moons, a campaign setting with 400-day years) — maximal prior pressure with unambiguous local authority.

cogmem__3efa9739
fiction-frame tiering of prior strength case-design idea: the strongest prior tier uses world facts contradicted inside an explicitly fictional local frame (a game's physics handbook where water boils at 80°c, a novel where earth has three moons, a campaign setting with 400-day years) — maximal prior pressure with unambiguous local authority. experiment/benchmark cogmem__3efa9739

◈ Absence-form cases as a graded regime

experiment/benchmark

IC-1b extension: absence testimony gradated across the same tier ladder ("we have not picked a default branch name yet" vs trap "main"; "the handbook has not fixed the boiling temperature yet" vs trap "100 degrees") — measuring where stored indecision loses to conventional defaults as prior strength rises.

cogmem__af0483ea
absence-form cases as a graded regime ic-1b extension: absence testimony gradated across the same tier ladder ( we have not picked a default branch name yet vs trap main ; the handbook has not fixed the boiling temperature yet vs trap 100 degrees ) — measuring where stored indecision loses to conventional defaults as prior strength rises. experiment/benchmark cogmem__af0483ea

◈ Deliberate process-absence as content ("we do not run sprints")

experiment/benchmark

Case nuance within absence-form: some absences are deliberate standing decisions rather than pending ones — "We deliberately do not run fixed-length sprints; cycles end when the goal ships" — testing whether the model can bind a negative policy as the answer against the "two weeks" default.

cogmem__af0483ea
deliberate process-absence as content ( we do not run sprints ) case nuance within absence-form: some absences are deliberate standing decisions rather than pending ones — we deliberately do not run fixed-length sprints; cycles end when the goal ships — testing whether the model can bind a *negative policy* as the answer against the two weeks default. experiment/benchmark cogmem__af0483ea

◈ Session-id grammar as experiment registry

mechanism

Infrastructure pattern: experimental regimes are encoded in session-id prefixes parsed by one regex (ic1_t<n>_, ic1b_abs_t<n>_, ic1b_imp_t<n>_), so scorecards can group and render by (arm, regime, tier) without any side-channel configuration — the case name is the experiment's registration.

cogmem__af0483ea
session-id grammar as experiment registry infrastructure pattern: experimental regimes are encoded in session-id prefixes parsed by one regex (`ic1_t<n>_`, `ic1b_abs_t<n>_`, `ic1b_imp_t<n>_`), so scorecards can group and render by (arm, regime, tier) without any side-channel configuration — the case name is the experiment's registration. mechanism cogmem__af0483ea

◈ run10's matched-pair lesson applied to sampling

experiment/benchmark

Reused methodology: every (topic, paraphrase, sample) in C1 uses the SAME pinned sampling seed across all arms so the stochastic draw cancels in arm deltas — paired-sampling discipline extended from benchmark seeds to LLM sampling itself.

cogmem__21322878
run10's matched-pair lesson applied to sampling reused methodology: every (topic, paraphrase, sample) in c1 uses the same pinned sampling seed across all arms so the stochastic draw cancels in arm deltas — paired-sampling discipline extended from benchmark seeds to llm sampling itself. experiment/benchmark cogmem__21322878

◈ The ideation-case grammar (angles + constraint + latents + trap)

experiment/benchmark

Reusable authoring template for divergent tasks: two experience-angle memories, one constraint memory tagged host.constraint, mundane distractors, ≥3 latent directions derivable-but-absent, and one trap that specifically violates the stored constraint (external consultant vs frozen budget; paid ads vs no-paid-promotion promise; skipping the bar-raiser vs "that stays").

cogmem__2ab80e23, cogmem__48040228
the ideation-case grammar (angles + constraint + latents + trap) reusable authoring template for divergent tasks: two experience-angle memories, one constraint memory tagged host.constraint, mundane distractors, ≥3 latent directions derivable-but-absent, and one trap that specifically violates the stored constraint (external consultant vs frozen budget; paid ads vs no-paid-promotion promise; skipping the bar-raiser vs that stays ). experiment/benchmark cogmem__2ab80e23, cogmem__48040228

◈ run13 supports one sentence

framing/ontologyconversation-only

Epistemic sizing of a weak run: with one seed, five cases, and two confounded metrics, run13 "supports roughly one sentence: 'the naive foil is dominated'" — an explicit exercise in stating exactly how much claim a measurement can carry.

cogmem__f96bd487
run13 supports one sentence epistemic sizing of a weak run: with one seed, five cases, and two confounded metrics, run13 supports roughly one sentence: 'the naive foil is dominated' — an explicit exercise in stating exactly how much claim a measurement can carry. framing/ontology cogmem__f96bd487 yes

◈ Judge strictness as a measurement note, not a defect

experiment/benchmark

Recurring diagnostic category: cases where the answer is correct but incompletely paraphrased get scored down by the entailment judge (the "belonged to her grandmother" clause); these are classified as judge-strictness residuals and recorded as measurement notes rather than system defects.

cogmem__f96bd487
judge strictness as a measurement note, not a defect recurring diagnostic category: cases where the answer is correct but incompletely paraphrased get scored down by the entailment judge (the belonged to her grandmother clause); these are classified as judge-strictness residuals and recorded as measurement notes rather than system defects. experiment/benchmark cogmem__f96bd487

◈ Deterministic signals outrank judge signals on terse text

result/finding

Instrument finding: the unfold-layer confab judge is neutral-dominant on terse notes, so the deterministic signals (abstain flag, verbatim trap-hit) remain the reliable memory-layer measures — a hierarchy of signal trustworthiness by text length.

cogmem__f96bd487
deterministic signals outrank judge signals on terse text instrument finding: the unfold-layer confab judge is neutral-dominant on terse notes, so the deterministic signals (abstain flag, verbatim trap-hit) remain the reliable memory-layer measures — a hierarchy of signal trustworthiness by text length. result/finding cogmem__f96bd487

◈ First-verbatim-trap-hit as a smoking gun

result/finding

Observation that the trap-hit detector caught its first-ever verbatim hits from the naive foil and generative arms during the AMBench port — the planted-trap mechanism finally demonstrating its discriminative value after many null runs.

cogmem__f96bd487
first-verbatim-trap-hit as a smoking gun observation that the trap-hit detector caught its first-ever verbatim hits from the naive foil and generative arms during the ambench port — the planted-trap mechanism finally demonstrating its discriminative value after many null runs. result/finding cogmem__f96bd487

◈ The careful-persona confound in public-benchmark comparisons

result/finding

Subtle cross-instrument finding: on AMBench the generative preset scored worse than no unfold at all partly because AMBench's careful persona already refuses some gaps on its own — the host persona is itself a confab-suppression treatment that changes baseline comparisons between instruments.

cogmem__f96bd487
the careful-persona confound in public-benchmark comparisons subtle cross-instrument finding: on ambench the generative preset scored *worse than no unfold at all* partly because ambench's careful persona already refuses some gaps on its own — the host persona is itself a confab-suppression treatment that changes baseline comparisons between instruments. result/finding cogmem__f96bd487

◈ Divergent probes crash empty-ground-truth assumptions

result/findingconversation-only

Small integration hazard worth recording: ideation probes have empty ground_truth by design, and downstream code that embeds ground truth (fold-force scoring) rejects empty input — axes with structurally absent fields flush out hidden assumptions across the whole pipeline.

cogmem__f96bd487
divergent probes crash empty-ground-truth assumptions small integration hazard worth recording: ideation probes have empty ground_truth by design, and downstream code that embeds ground truth (fold-force scoring) rejects empty input — axes with structurally absent fields flush out hidden assumptions across the whole pipeline. result/finding cogmem__f96bd487 yes

◈ The management ledger day: five results in one session-day

result/findingconversation-only

Demonstration of the autonomous-manager cadence: in a single day the loop landed Lever D ported (run18), check #1 confirmed closing R3 (run19), C1 confirmed (two runs), and the divergent axis built and measured (run20) — evidence that the spec→implement→measure→record loop compounds when state lives in persistent memory.

cogmem__f96bd487
the management ledger day: five results in one session-day demonstration of the autonomous-manager cadence: in a single day the loop landed lever d ported (run18), check #1 confirmed closing r3 (run19), c1 confirmed (two runs), and the divergent axis built and measured (run20) — evidence that the spec→implement→measure→record loop compounds when state lives in persistent memory. result/finding cogmem__f96bd487 yes

◈ Personality claims need a style-stability instrument

experiment/benchmark

Forward-pointing idea after run21: a C2-adjacent instrument measuring style/voice stability is doubly motivated — it is both the identity arc's next check and the only way the generative preset's remaining ("personality") claim could ever be measured.

cogmem__f96bd487
personality claims need a style-stability instrument forward-pointing idea after run21: a c2-adjacent instrument measuring *style/voice stability* is doubly motivated — it is both the identity arc's next check and the only way the generative preset's remaining ( personality ) claim could ever be measured. experiment/benchmark cogmem__f96bd487

◈ Selection is architecture-agnostic; delivery is not

framing/ontology

The clean split the integration critique leaves behind: everything before the fold (capture, inscription, selection, gating) is independent of how the model consumes memory and survives any delivery mechanism; only the fold-delivery layer (tokens vs KV-state vs steering) couples to the substrate.

cogmem__f96bd487
selection is architecture-agnostic; delivery is not the clean split the integration critique leaves behind: everything before the fold (capture, inscription, selection, gating) is independent of how the model consumes memory and survives any delivery mechanism; only the fold-delivery layer (tokens vs kv-state vs steering) couples to the substrate. framing/ontology cogmem__f96bd487

◈ The IC-1 two-arm design doubles as a testimony-engineering meter

experiment/benchmark

Design economy: running the prior-curve with both a raw-fold arm and a bound-sentence-fold arm means the same experiment measures the discount curve AND what testimony engineering buys against it — with IC-2's state-fold designed to join the same chart later.

cogmem__f96bd487
the ic-1 two-arm design doubles as a testimony-engineering meter design economy: running the prior-curve with both a raw-fold arm and a bound-sentence-fold arm means the same experiment measures the discount curve and what testimony engineering buys against it — with ic-2's state-fold designed to join the same chart later. experiment/benchmark cogmem__f96bd487

◈ "You're near the top of a ladder" (reorienting a lost researcher)

framing/ontologyconversation-only

The session-opening move for an overwhelmed side-project owner: reflect the entire project back as a rung-status ladder (R0 done, R1 banked twice, R2 done, R3 in progress, R4–R6 gated) so "completely lost" converts into a single decision — finish R3, then freeze.

cogmem__8ca5844d
you're near the top of a ladder (reorienting a lost researcher) the session-opening move for an overwhelmed side-project owner: reflect the entire project back as a rung-status ladder (r0 done, r1 banked twice, r2 done, r3 in progress, r4–r6 gated) so completely lost converts into a single decision — finish r3, then freeze. framing/ontology cogmem__8ca5844d yes

◈ A Self compressed over unvalidated recall inherits its errors

framing/ontology

The stated rationale for the phase gate that recurs across sessions: R4–R5 (the Self via compression) stay deliberately gated because a Self compressed over an unvalidated recall layer would inherit recall's errors — prove recall before building the person on top of it.

cogmem__fbd3447b, cogmem__8ca5844d
a self compressed over unvalidated recall inherits its errors the stated rationale for the phase gate that recurs across sessions: r4–r5 (the self via compression) stay deliberately gated because a self compressed over an unvalidated recall layer would inherit recall's errors — prove recall before building the person on top of it. framing/ontology cogmem__fbd3447b, cogmem__8ca5844d

◈ The observe hook makes co-activation host-portable

mechanism

Portability insight: co-activation (the learned lever) needs only a host-implementable observe callback to wire its use-history edges — so the emergent mechanism can cross into the portable artifact without the host adopting the lab's storage.

cogmem__f96bd487
the observe hook makes co-activation host-portable portability insight: co-activation (the learned lever) needs only a host-implementable `observe` callback to wire its use-history edges — so the emergent mechanism can cross into the portable artifact without the host adopting the lab's storage. mechanism cogmem__f96bd487

P8b · subagent explorations + AMBench 131 ideas

21 parallel workers + the assistant-memory benchmark project · P8b-subagents-ambench.md

Part A — cognivault_memory subagent explorations (unfold-recall phase)

◈ The run13 defect: abstention keyed on the top trace

result/finding

The empirical finding that motivated a whole task chain: the unfold step decided whether to abstain by looking only at recalled[0]'s resolution, so when an answer-bearing ("rich") memory ranked below a thin/handle pointer in the recall pool, the system wrongly abstained even though the answer was right there. Recall ranking and answer-bearingness are independent, so abstention must not be keyed to rank position.

cogmem__agent-a7fb16fe634922b3e__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a19f92b94a5c3d853__sub.txt
"run13 showed this wrongly abstains when a rich seed ranks below a thin/handle trace."
the run13 defect: abstention keyed on the top trace the empirical finding that motivated a whole task chain: the unfold step decided whether to abstain by looking only at `recalled[0]`'s resolution, so when an answer-bearing ( rich ) memory ranked *below* a thin/handle pointer in the recall pool, the system wrongly abstained even though the answer was right there. recall ranking and answer-bearingness are independent, so abstention must not be keyed to rank position. result/finding cogmem__agent-a7fb16fe634922b3e__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a19f92b94a5c3d853__sub.txt run13 showed this wrongly abstains when a `rich` seed ranks below a thin/handle trace.

◈ Pool-aware abstention

mechanism

The fix mechanism: abstain only when NO trace anywhere in the recalled pool is answer-bearing (resolution == "rich"), rather than keying on the top-ranked trace. Applied identically to the deterministic StubUnfolder and the LLM disciplined arm so both arms share one abstention contract.

cogmem__agent-a7fb16fe634922b3e__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a5b95617df1c6b4a4__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-aafa36e69f2bd7765__sub.txt
pool-aware abstention the fix mechanism: abstain only when no trace anywhere in the recalled pool is answer-bearing (`resolution == rich `), rather than keying on the top-ranked trace. applied identically to the deterministic stubunfolder and the llm disciplined arm so both arms share one abstention contract. mechanism cogmem__agent-a7fb16fe634922b3e__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a5b95617df1c6b4a4__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-aafa36e69f2bd7765__sub.txt

◈ Resolution taxonomy: rich / thin / handle

framing/ontology

Each memory trace carries host["resolution"] in {"rich","thin","handle"}: "rich" means the answer-bearing detail was actually inscribed at capture time; "thin" and "handle" mean only a pointer or stub was stored. Missing host or missing key defaults to "rich". This is the vocabulary that lets recall distinguish "I have the answer" from "I only know where the answer lives."

cogmem__agent-a7fb16fe634922b3e__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a19f92b94a5c3d853__sub.txt
resolution taxonomy: rich / thin / handle each memory trace carries `host[ resolution ]` in { rich , thin , handle }: rich means the answer-bearing detail was actually inscribed at capture time; thin and handle mean only a pointer or stub was stored. missing host or missing key defaults to rich . this is the vocabulary that lets recall distinguish i have the answer from i only know where the answer lives. framing/ontology cogmem__agent-a7fb16fe634922b3e__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a19f92b94a5c3d853__sub.txt

◈ Re-fetch handle from anywhere in the pool, never fabricated

mechanism

When abstaining, the unfolder returns a re_fetch handle — the first address_key found anywhere in the recalled pool (not just the top trace), or None if none exists. It never invents a fake key; if no handle exists the abstention note degrades to "re-fetch from the original source." Abstention thus becomes an actionable instruction to the host, not just a refusal.

cogmem__agent-a7fb16fe634922b3e__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a19f92b94a5c3d853__sub.txt
re-fetch handle from anywhere in the pool, never fabricated when abstaining, the unfolder returns a `re_fetch` handle — the first `address_key` found anywhere in the recalled pool (not just the top trace), or none if none exists. it never invents a fake key; if no handle exists the abstention note degrades to re-fetch from the original source. abstention thus becomes an actionable instruction to the host, not just a refusal. mechanism cogmem__agent-a7fb16fe634922b3e__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a19f92b94a5c3d853__sub.txt

◈ Abstention note as re-fetch instruction

mechanism

The abstain path emits a natural-language note that says the memory is low-resolution, the requested detail was not inscribed, and names the handle to re-fetch from — turning abstention into a closed-loop recovery step rather than a dead end.

cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt
"The stored memory is low-resolution; the requested detail was not inscribed. Re-fetch from {handle or 'the original source'}."
abstention note as re-fetch instruction the abstain path emits a natural-language note that says the memory is low-resolution, the requested detail was not inscribed, and names the handle to re-fetch from — turning abstention into a closed-loop recovery step rather than a dead end. mechanism cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt the stored memory is low-resolution; the requested detail was not inscribed. re-fetch from {handle or 'the original source'}.

◈ Faithful text built from rich traces only (deterministic arm)

mechanism

In the stub unfolder, the reconstructed "faithful" text is joined from only the rich (answer-bearing) traces, excluding thin/handle filler — the faithful layer reconstructs only what was actually inscribed.

cogmem__agent-a7fb16fe634922b3e__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a19f92b94a5c3d853__sub.txt
faithful text built from rich traces only (deterministic arm) in the stub unfolder, the reconstructed faithful text is joined from only the rich (answer-bearing) traces, excluding thin/handle filler — the faithful layer reconstructs only what was actually inscribed. mechanism cogmem__agent-a7fb16fe634922b3e__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a19f92b94a5c3d853__sub.txt

◈ Deliberate stub/LLM asymmetry in what feeds reconstruction

mechanismconversation-only

The deterministic stub builds faithful text from rich traces only, while the LLM disciplined arm passes ALL recalled traces (with per-trace source and resolution tags visible in the prompt) — the LLM can safely use low-resolution context because the tags are visible, whereas the deterministic stub cannot. The holistic reviewer flagged this divergence as defensible but worth documenting at the point of divergence.

cogmem__agent-aa2c03ad3f5bceeb3__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a925fb46ba3e80d27__sub.txt
deliberate stub/llm asymmetry in what feeds reconstruction the deterministic stub builds faithful text from rich traces only, while the llm disciplined arm passes all recalled traces (with per-trace source and resolution tags visible in the prompt) — the llm can safely use low-resolution context because the tags are visible, whereas the deterministic stub cannot. the holistic reviewer flagged this divergence as defensible but worth documenting at the point of divergence. mechanism cogmem__agent-aa2c03ad3f5bceeb3__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a925fb46ba3e80d27__sub.txt yes

◈ "Answer-bearing" as an operating definition doing real work

open-questionconversation-only

A reviewer's observation: equating "answer-bearing" with resolution == "rich" is a deliberate, defensible policy choice, but a thin trace can still carry partial signal — the term is a policy boundary, not a fact of nature. Flagged for awareness, no change requested.

cogmem__agent-a925fb46ba3e80d27__sub.txt
answer-bearing as an operating definition doing real work a reviewer's observation: equating answer-bearing with `resolution == rich ` is a deliberate, defensible policy choice, but a thin trace can still carry partial signal — the term is a policy boundary, not a fact of nature. flagged for awareness, no change requested. open-question cogmem__agent-a925fb46ba3e80d27__sub.txt yes

◈ Anti-confabulation reconstruction prompt

mechanism

The disciplined unfold prompt: reconstruct ONLY what the memories actually state; do not add absent facts; if a needed detail is missing, say it is not stored rather than inventing it. The prompt is the discipline — the LLM sits strictly post-selection.

cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt
"if a needed detail is missing, say it is not stored rather than inventing it."
anti-confabulation reconstruction prompt the disciplined unfold prompt: reconstruct only what the memories actually state; do not add absent facts; if a needed detail is missing, say it is not stored rather than inventing it. the prompt is the discipline — the llm sits strictly post-selection. mechanism cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt if a needed detail is missing, say it is not stored rather than inventing it.

◈ Resolution-tagged memory lines in the LLM prompt

mechanism

Memories are rendered into the disciplined prompt as - (source=..., resolution=...) content — per-trace provenance metadata is made visible to the reconstructing LLM so it can weight low-resolution traces appropriately instead of the harness pre-filtering them.

cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a925fb46ba3e80d27__sub.txt
resolution-tagged memory lines in the llm prompt memories are rendered into the disciplined prompt as `- (source=..., resolution=...) content` — per-trace provenance metadata is made visible to the reconstructing llm so it can weight low-resolution traces appropriately instead of the harness pre-filtering them. mechanism cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a925fb46ba3e80d27__sub.txt

◈ p018_generative: confabulation-by-design as a shipped "personality" preset

mechanism

A distinct preset producing TWO layers: a faithful reconstruction layer plus a separate generative elaboration layer. Confabulation in the generative layer is deliberate — "it is what makes an opinionated agent" — but both layers are source-capped via the inscription meet so elaboration can never launder its source upward, and credibility stays None. Elaboration is allowed as long as it is honestly labeled and provenance-capped.

cogmem__agent-a12a9a7ccc3979272__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a6727e53623b0ed15__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a5e5d9b3a8c1f2e0e__sub.txt
"Confabulation in the generative layer is by design (it is what makes an opinionated agent); both layers are source-capped via the meet (never laundered)."
p018_generative: confabulation-by-design as a shipped personality preset a distinct preset producing two layers: a faithful reconstruction layer plus a separate generative elaboration layer. confabulation in the generative layer is deliberate — it is what makes an opinionated agent — but both layers are source-capped via the inscription meet so elaboration can never launder its source upward, and credibility stays none. elaboration is allowed as long as it is honestly labeled and provenance-capped. mechanism cogmem__agent-a12a9a7ccc3979272__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a6727e53623b0ed15__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a5e5d9b3a8c1f2e0e__sub.txt confabulation in the generative layer is by design (it is what makes an opinionated agent); both layers are source-capped via the meet (never laundered).

◈ Generative preset vs naive_dump foil — the crucial distinction

framing/ontology

naive_dump is a pure-downside benchmark FOIL: one undisciplined blob, no faithful layer, no discipline. p018_generative is a real shipped preset: elaboration lives in a separate layer while the faithful layer remains present. Resolving "generative vs foil" as an explicit design item prevents the benchmark's straw-man arm from being confused with the productized personality arm.

cogmem__agent-a12a9a7ccc3979272__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a3808e910a4a6b604__sub.txt
generative preset vs naive_dump foil — the crucial distinction `naive_dump` is a pure-downside benchmark foil: one undisciplined blob, no faithful layer, no discipline. `p018_generative` is a real shipped preset: elaboration lives in a *separate* layer while the faithful layer remains present. resolving generative vs foil as an explicit design item prevents the benchmark's straw-man arm from being confused with the productized personality arm. framing/ontology cogmem__agent-a12a9a7ccc3979272__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a3808e910a4a6b604__sub.txt

◈ Two-prompt unfold: faithful + elaboration from the same pool

mechanism

The generative arm runs two prompts over the same recalled memories — a "reconstruct only what is stated" prompt and a "give an opinionated, elaborated answer grounded in these memories, may add useful inference, do not contradict" prompt — and returns both as separate layers stamped with the same meet-derived source.

cogmem__agent-a12a9a7ccc3979272__sub.txt
two-prompt unfold: faithful + elaboration from the same pool the generative arm runs two prompts over the same recalled memories — a reconstruct only what is stated prompt and a give an opinionated, elaborated answer grounded in these memories, may add useful inference, do not contradict prompt — and returns both as separate layers stamped with the same meet-derived source. mechanism cogmem__agent-a12a9a7ccc3979272__sub.txt

◈ Empty elaboration collapses to no layer

mechanism

If the generative prompt yields empty text, the generative layer becomes None rather than an empty layer, so downstream folding omits it cleanly — absence of elaboration is represented as absence, not as blank content.

cogmem__agent-a12a9a7ccc3979272__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a59662d957ed7a95b__sub.txt
empty elaboration collapses to no layer if the generative prompt yields empty text, the generative layer becomes none rather than an empty layer, so downstream folding omits it cleanly — absence of elaboration is represented as absence, not as blank content. mechanism cogmem__agent-a12a9a7ccc3979272__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a59662d957ed7a95b__sub.txt

◈ p018's faithful layer is deliberately the weaker variant

framing/ontology

A review-surfaced subtlety made explicit in a code comment: p018's faithful layer is intentionally NOT resolution-aware and never abstains — the preset trades fidelity for elaboration — while p017 disciplined's faithful layer is resolution-aware and abstains on gaps. Two layers named "faithful" with different guarantees, clarified to prevent a future reader assuming they behave identically.

cogmem__agent-a6727e53623b0ed15__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a59662d957ed7a95b__sub.txt
p018's faithful layer is deliberately the weaker variant a review-surfaced subtlety made explicit in a code comment: p018's faithful layer is intentionally not resolution-aware and never abstains — the preset trades fidelity for elaboration — while p017 disciplined's faithful layer is resolution-aware and abstains on gaps. two layers named faithful with different guarantees, clarified to prevent a future reader assuming they behave identically. framing/ontology cogmem__agent-a6727e53623b0ed15__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a59662d957ed7a95b__sub.txt

◈ UNFOLD_PRESETS: a named-knob registry with "off" as the no-LLM default

mechanism

The public unfold surface is a dict of preset name → builder: {"off": selection-only, "disciplined": pool-aware faithful + abstain, "generative": elaboration/personality}. "off" is the default knob position and genuinely involves zero LLM (its recall object has no .unfold hook at all). The naive_dump foil is deliberately excluded from the registry so a foil can never be shipped by accident.

cogmem__agent-aca5a9a8cfaabe977__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a3808e910a4a6b604__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a91cae13f7508d2b2__sub.txt
unfold_presets: a named-knob registry with off as the no-llm default the public unfold surface is a dict of preset name → builder: { off : selection-only, disciplined : pool-aware faithful + abstain, generative : elaboration/personality}. off is the default knob position and genuinely involves zero llm (its recall object has no `.unfold` hook at all). the naive_dump foil is deliberately excluded from the registry so a foil can never be shipped by accident. mechanism cogmem__agent-aca5a9a8cfaabe977__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a3808e910a4a6b604__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a91cae13f7508d2b2__sub.txt

◈ Absence of the hook as the no-LLM proof

mechanism

The "off" preset's no-LLM claim is enforced structurally: the plain PortableRecall class simply lacks an unfold attribute, and the public test asserts getattr(proto.recall, "unfold", None) is None. The reviewer noted the flip side: presets don't present a uniform interface, so callers must branch on hook presence.

cogmem__agent-a91cae13f7508d2b2__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a3808e910a4a6b604__sub.txt
absence of the hook as the no-llm proof the off preset's no-llm claim is enforced structurally: the plain portablerecall class simply lacks an `unfold` attribute, and the public test asserts `getattr(proto.recall, unfold , none) is none`. the reviewer noted the flip side: presets don't present a uniform interface, so callers must branch on hook presence. mechanism cogmem__agent-a91cae13f7508d2b2__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a3808e910a4a6b604__sub.txt

◈ levers.py: extract the levers into a dependency-free leaf module

mechanism

The two "lever" primitives the portable core borrowed from experiment prototypes — AUTHORITY_RANK (captured-authority tiers) and self_index_matches (the scope gate) — are relocated into a standalone levers.py that imports nothing from the package, so the portable core and any external host can depend on levers without dragging in the experiment tree.

cogmem__agent-afd7c7d210546f228__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a8dc0eafdb296c827__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a1051586927dd49e1__sub.txt
levers.py: extract the levers into a dependency-free leaf module the two lever primitives the portable core borrowed from experiment prototypes — authority_rank (captured-authority tiers) and self_index_matches (the scope gate) — are relocated into a standalone `levers.py` that imports nothing from the package, so the portable core and any external host can depend on levers without dragging in the experiment tree. mechanism cogmem__agent-afd7c7d210546f228__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a8dc0eafdb296c827__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a1051586927dd49e1__sub.txt

◈ AUTHORITY_RANK: captured authority tiers with an inert zero

mechanism

Authority of a captured record is ranked {"of_record": 2, "claim": 1}, with absent/unknown authority mapping to tier 0 — which makes the authority gate inert for untagged records rather than punitive. Records of record outrank mere claims; nothing is required to carry authority.

cogmem__agent-afd7c7d210546f228__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a1051586927dd49e1__sub.txt
authority_rank: captured authority tiers with an inert zero authority of a captured record is ranked { of_record : 2, claim : 1}, with absent/unknown authority mapping to tier 0 — which makes the authority gate inert for untagged records rather than punitive. records of record outrank mere claims; nothing is required to carry authority. mechanism cogmem__agent-afd7c7d210546f228__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a1051586927dd49e1__sub.txt

◈ self_index_matches / Lever A: pin-only-set-fields scope gate

mechanism

The scope gate pins on four self-index fields (user_id, project_id, role, permission_scope) with permissive semantics: no active scope → everything visible; an unscoped record is visible under any scope; a scoped record is visible only if every field the active scope actually pins (non-None) agrees. Scope narrows only where explicitly set.

cogmem__agent-afd7c7d210546f228__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a1051586927dd49e1__sub.txt
self_index_matches / lever a: pin-only-set-fields scope gate the scope gate pins on four self-index fields (user_id, project_id, role, permission_scope) with permissive semantics: no active scope → everything visible; an unscoped record is visible under any scope; a scoped record is visible only if every field the active scope actually *pins* (non-none) agrees. scope narrows only where explicitly set. mechanism cogmem__agent-afd7c7d210546f228__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a1051586927dd49e1__sub.txt

◈ AST guard test: the boundary as a tripwire

mechanism

A test that parses the portable core's own source with ast.walk and fails if any import (including lazy in-function imports) references the prototypes package — the core/prototypes boundary is enforced by an executable tripwire rather than asserted in documentation. The holistic review praised this as "genuinely enforced, not just asserted."

cogmem__agent-a759db3e1c872d36b__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-aa2c03ad3f5bceeb3__sub.txt
ast guard test: the boundary as a tripwire a test that parses the portable core's own source with `ast.walk` and fails if any import (including lazy in-function imports) references the prototypes package — the core/prototypes boundary is enforced by an executable tripwire rather than asserted in documentation. the holistic review praised this as genuinely enforced, not just asserted. mechanism cogmem__agent-a759db3e1c872d36b__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-aa2c03ad3f5bceeb3__sub.txt

◈ Hardening the tripwire against the second import form

mechanism

Review found the AST guard only inspected ast.ImportFrom nodes, so a regression written as plain import cogmem.prototypes.p012 as p012 (an ast.Import node) would slip through silently. A follow-up task widened the walk to both node types — the lesson being that a boundary test must cover every syntactic path across the boundary.

cogmem__agent-a050900f6f43ffc27__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a03c678a301b15453__sub.txt
hardening the tripwire against the second import form review found the ast guard only inspected `ast.importfrom` nodes, so a regression written as plain `import cogmem.prototypes.p012 as p012` (an `ast.import` node) would slip through silently. a follow-up task widened the walk to both node types — the lesson being that a boundary test must cover every syntactic path across the boundary. mechanism cogmem__agent-a050900f6f43ffc27__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a03c678a301b15453__sub.txt

◈ Test the lock by simulating the break-in

experiment/benchmarkconversation-only

A reviewer's verification method: before trusting the AST guard, simulate re-introducing a forbidden prototype import and confirm the guard actually fails — proving the regression lock is a real lock and not a no-op. Guard tests should themselves be adversarially exercised.

cogmem__agent-a050900f6f43ffc27__sub.txt
test the lock by simulating the break-in a reviewer's verification method: before trusting the ast guard, simulate re-introducing a forbidden prototype import and confirm the guard actually fails — proving the regression lock is a real lock and not a no-op. guard tests should themselves be adversarially exercised. experiment/benchmark cogmem__agent-a050900f6f43ffc27__sub.txt yes

◈ Docstrings must not describe the future as the present

framing/ontologyconversation-only

A review caught the levers.py docstring claiming "The prototypes now re-export from here" at a commit where they did not yet — creating a duplicate source of truth while asserting a single one. The objection: code that describes an intended future state as already true misleads the next reader and violates the "conformance over assertion" norm. Resolution: a later task made the claim true (and verified the docstring only then became accurate).

cogmem__agent-a8dc0eafdb296c827__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a759db3e1c872d36b__sub.txt
docstrings must not describe the future as the present a review caught the levers.py docstring claiming the prototypes now re-export from here at a commit where they did not yet — creating a duplicate source of truth while asserting a single one. the objection: code that describes an intended future state as already true misleads the next reader and violates the conformance over assertion norm. resolution: a later task made the claim true (and verified the docstring only then became accurate). framing/ontology cogmem__agent-a8dc0eafdb296c827__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a759db3e1c872d36b__sub.txt yes

◈ Duplicate-copy drift window

open-questionconversation-only

The extraction created a window where levers.py and the prototype originals coexisted with nothing preventing drift between them — flagged as acceptable for a pure relocation but requiring the consumer-rewiring follow-up to close the gap. Extraction-then-rewire as two tasks buys reviewability at the cost of a temporary dual source of truth.

cogmem__agent-a8dc0eafdb296c827__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-afd7c7d210546f228__sub.txt
duplicate-copy drift window the extraction created a window where levers.py and the prototype originals coexisted with nothing preventing drift between them — flagged as acceptable for a pure relocation but requiring the consumer-rewiring follow-up to close the gap. extraction-then-rewire as two tasks buys reviewability at the cost of a temporary dual source of truth. open-question cogmem__agent-a8dc0eafdb296c827__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-afd7c7d210546f228__sub.txt yes

◈ Re-export aliasing for zero-cost back-compat

mechanism

After relocation, the prototypes re-export the moved symbols under their original names (from ..levers import self_index_matches as _self_index_matches), so transitive consumers (p008, p010) keep resolving unchanged — the refactor moves the source of truth without touching any importer.

cogmem__agent-a759db3e1c872d36b__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a050900f6f43ffc27__sub.txt
re-export aliasing for zero-cost back-compat after relocation, the prototypes re-export the moved symbols under their original names (`from ..levers import self_index_matches as _self_index_matches`), so transitive consumers (p008, p010) keep resolving unchanged — the refactor moves the source of truth without touching any importer. mechanism cogmem__agent-a759db3e1c872d36b__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a050900f6f43ffc27__sub.txt

◈ The four project invariants as review criteria

framing/ontology

Every review in the chain re-checked the same non-negotiables: (1) no LLM anywhere in the selection/recall path — LLM only in the post-selection unfold step; (2) source, credibility, and confidence are three DISTINCT quantities never collapsed; (3) credibility stays None in Phase 1; source is immutable and only ever lowered via the inscription meet (reused from fgm, never reimplemented); (4) the portable core is standalone with no prototype dependency. Invariants as a standing review checklist rather than one-time design notes.

cogmem__agent-aa2c03ad3f5bceeb3__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a925fb46ba3e80d27__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a3808e910a4a6b604__sub.txt
the four project invariants as review criteria every review in the chain re-checked the same non-negotiables: (1) no llm anywhere in the selection/recall path — llm only in the post-selection unfold step; (2) source, credibility, and confidence are three distinct quantities never collapsed; (3) credibility stays none in phase 1; source is immutable and only ever *lowered* via the inscription meet (reused from fgm, never reimplemented); (4) the portable core is standalone with no prototype dependency. invariants as a standing review checklist rather than one-time design notes. framing/ontology cogmem__agent-aa2c03ad3f5bceeb3__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a925fb46ba3e80d27__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a3808e910a4a6b604__sub.txt

◈ stamp_source: the meet as an anti-laundering cap

mechanism

Any derived layer's source is computed by stamp_source — the inscription meet of all contributor sources, capped at "infer" — so no amount of LLM reconstruction or elaboration can ever raise a derived text's provenance above inference. This is the source-downgrading idea operationalized at the unfold layer.

cogmem__agent-a12a9a7ccc3979272__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a5e5d9b3a8c1f2e0e__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-aa2c03ad3f5bceeb3__sub.txt
stamp_source: the meet as an anti-laundering cap any derived layer's source is computed by `stamp_source` — the inscription meet of all contributor sources, capped at infer — so no amount of llm reconstruction or elaboration can ever raise a derived text's provenance above inference. this is the source-downgrading idea operationalized at the unfold layer. mechanism cogmem__agent-a12a9a7ccc3979272__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a5e5d9b3a8c1f2e0e__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-aa2c03ad3f5bceeb3__sub.txt

◈ Stale-fixture forensics: distinguishing broken tests from broken code

result/findingconversation-only

When the pool-aware fix broke a pre-existing harness test, the implementer traced the root cause — the fixture's distractor had no explicit resolution, defaulted to "rich", and so legitimately flipped the corrected arm from abstain to reconstruct — and classified it as a stale fixture that had relied on the old defective keying, not a regression. The fix (marking the distractor "thin") preserved the test's original intent. A spec-verifier independently re-derived and endorsed the same analysis.

cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-aafa36e69f2bd7765__sub.txt
stale-fixture forensics: distinguishing broken tests from broken code when the pool-aware fix broke a pre-existing harness test, the implementer traced the root cause — the fixture's distractor had no explicit resolution, defaulted to rich , and so legitimately flipped the corrected arm from abstain to reconstruct — and classified it as a stale fixture that had relied on the old defective keying, not a regression. the fix (marking the distractor thin ) preserved the test's original intent. a spec-verifier independently re-derived and endorsed the same analysis. result/finding cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-aafa36e69f2bd7765__sub.txt yes

◈ UnfoldResult: the four-field contract of reconstruction

mechanism

The unfold step's return shape: a faithful Layer, an optional generative Layer, an abstained flag, and a re_fetch handle. Everything the host needs to fold memory into working context — or to decline and recover — in one structured result.

cogmem__agent-a12a9a7ccc3979272__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt
unfoldresult: the four-field contract of reconstruction the unfold step's return shape: a faithful layer, an optional generative layer, an `abstained` flag, and a `re_fetch` handle. everything the host needs to fold memory into working context — or to decline and recover — in one structured result. mechanism cogmem__agent-a12a9a7ccc3979272__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt

◈ Abstention semantics propagate to the scorecard, not the plumbing

mechanismconversation-only

The holistic reviewer confirmed the pool-aware change alters when abstention fires but not any structural coupling — the unfold scorecard just reads the recorded abstained field, and the planned run14 will measure the new behavior. Behavior changes should flow to metrics through recorded fields, not through code the metrics reach into.

cogmem__agent-aa2c03ad3f5bceeb3__sub.txt
abstention semantics propagate to the scorecard, not the plumbing the holistic reviewer confirmed the pool-aware change alters *when* abstention fires but not any structural coupling — the unfold scorecard just reads the recorded `abstained` field, and the planned run14 will measure the new behavior. behavior changes should flow to metrics through recorded fields, not through code the metrics reach into. mechanism cogmem__agent-aa2c03ad3f5bceeb3__sub.txt yes

◈ Cycle-safe registry ordering + lazy builder imports

mechanism

The prototype registry imports the unfold builders AFTER its REGISTRY literal, and each builder defers its import of the portable recall class to call time — a two-part idiom that breaks the prototypes → unfold → p014 import cycle while keeping one flat registry of all arms (p015 selection-only, p016 naive_dump, p017 disciplined, p018 generative).

cogmem__agent-a12a9a7ccc3979272__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-aca5a9a8cfaabe977__sub.txt
cycle-safe registry ordering + lazy builder imports the prototype registry imports the unfold builders after its registry literal, and each builder defers its import of the portable recall class to call time — a two-part idiom that breaks the prototypes → unfold → p014 import cycle while keeping one flat registry of all arms (p015 selection-only, p016 naive_dump, p017 disciplined, p018 generative). mechanism cogmem__agent-a12a9a7ccc3979272__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-aca5a9a8cfaabe977__sub.txt

◈ The frozen public surface as "takeaway artifact"

product

The final extraction step gives the whole research program one stable public import path — PortableRecaller, Candidate, CueContext, UNFOLD_PRESETS — framed as the "takeaway artifact": the thing an external host (explicitly: an AMBench entrant) imports without any reach into experiment scaffolding. Research code graduates by growing a minimal frozen API.

cogmem__agent-aca5a9a8cfaabe977__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a050900f6f43ffc27__sub.txt
"The portable core and any external host (AMBench entrant) now have zero prototypes/ reach."

◈ Registry-as-surface, builders-as-plumbing

mechanism

A deliberate __all__ choice: the three preset builders are importable but unadvertised; only the UNFOLD_PRESETS registry (plus the three types) is the public surface. Named knob positions are the API; raw constructors are not.

cogmem__agent-a3808e910a4a6b604__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a91cae13f7508d2b2__sub.txt

◈ Intentional constant duplication as a known seam

open-questionconversation-only

The holistic review documented that AUTHORITY_RANK also lives verbatim in the benchmark validator and the scope fields in the recursive module — deliberate mirroring where the validator is canonical — and flagged it as a seam: a future tier change must be made in two places. Naming the seam instead of prematurely unifying it.

cogmem__agent-aa2c03ad3f5bceeb3__sub.txt
intentional constant duplication as a known seam the holistic review documented that authority_rank also lives verbatim in the benchmark validator and the scope fields in the recursive module — deliberate mirroring where the validator is canonical — and flagged it as a seam: a future tier change must be made in two places. naming the seam instead of prematurely unifying it. open-question cogmem__agent-aa2c03ad3f5bceeb3__sub.txt yes

◈ The echo model: a deterministic offline LLM stand-in

mechanism

Setting COGMEM_MODEL=echo swaps in a deterministic offline stub (returns "next: (no input)" absent input markers) so every LLM-arm test runs without API keys or network — the whole unfold pipeline, including its LLM paths, stays testable in CI at zero cost. A reviewer also noted the limitation: under echo, faithful and generative outputs are identical, so layer-divergence behavior goes unexercised offline.

cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a6727e53623b0ed15__sub.txt
the echo model: a deterministic offline llm stand-in setting cogmem_model=echo swaps in a deterministic offline stub (returns next: (no input) absent input markers) so every llm-arm test runs without api keys or network — the whole unfold pipeline, including its llm paths, stays testable in ci at zero cost. a reviewer also noted the limitation: under echo, faithful and generative outputs are identical, so layer-divergence behavior goes unexercised offline. mechanism cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a6727e53623b0ed15__sub.txt

◈ Prompt-suffix keying for offline layer testing

experiment/benchmark

To test the empty-elaboration branch offline, a fake LLM is monkeypatched that distinguishes the two prompts by their trailing marker ("Faithful reconstruction:" vs "Answer:") and returns text for one and empty for the other — using the prompt scaffold itself as the routing key for deterministic multi-prompt stubs.

cogmem__agent-a59662d957ed7a95b__sub.txt
prompt-suffix keying for offline layer testing to test the empty-elaboration branch offline, a fake llm is monkeypatched that distinguishes the two prompts by their trailing marker ( faithful reconstruction: vs answer: ) and returns text for one and empty for the other — using the prompt scaffold itself as the routing key for deterministic multi-prompt stubs. experiment/benchmark cogmem__agent-a59662d957ed7a95b__sub.txt

◈ Pin deterministic contracts in assertions

experiment/benchmarkconversation-only

A reviewer's nit with a general point: the test asserted re_fetch in ("t","h2") although the implementation deterministically picks the first handle in pool order — the loose assertion leaves the documented ordering guarantee untested. If a comment advertises "first available," the test should assert exactly that.

cogmem__agent-a19f92b94a5c3d853__sub.txt
pin deterministic contracts in assertions a reviewer's nit with a general point: the test asserted `re_fetch in ( t , h2 )` although the implementation deterministically picks the *first* handle in pool order — the loose assertion leaves the documented ordering guarantee untested. if a comment advertises first available, the test should assert exactly that. experiment/benchmark cogmem__agent-a19f92b94a5c3d853__sub.txt yes

◈ Eight commits, one story: the holistic ship review

result/finding

The final review evaluated the cumulative diff as a narrative — fix pool-aware abstention across both arms, add the generative counterpart to the foil, extract the levers, sever the prototype dependency with a tripwire, freeze the public API — checking coherence between tasks (arm divergence, naming drift, dead code) that per-task reviews structurally cannot see. Verdict: SHIP, 258 tests green.

cogmem__agent-aa2c03ad3f5bceeb3__sub.txt
eight commits, one story: the holistic ship review the final review evaluated the cumulative diff as a narrative — fix pool-aware abstention across both arms, add the generative counterpart to the foil, extract the levers, sever the prototype dependency with a tripwire, freeze the public api — checking coherence *between* tasks (arm divergence, naming drift, dead code) that per-task reviews structurally cannot see. verdict: ship, 258 tests green. result/finding cogmem__agent-aa2c03ad3f5bceeb3__sub.txt

◈ AMBench as Phase 3 of the cognivault plan

framing/ontologyconversation-only

The cognivault holistic review explicitly excludes "Phase 3 (AMBench)" as a separate future plan — establishing the direct lineage: the assistant-memory-bench repo is the planned continuation of the cognivault_memory extraction, with the frozen portable API as the intended entrant.

cogmem__agent-aa2c03ad3f5bceeb3__sub.txt

◈ Judged run14 gated on user authorization

experiment/benchmarkconversation-only

The next empirical step after the pool-aware fix — a judged run14 measuring the corrected abstention calibration — is noted as pending explicit user authorization because it costs money. Paid evaluation runs as consent-gated experiments, distinct from free deterministic suites.

cogmem__agent-aa2c03ad3f5bceeb3__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a5b95617df1c6b4a4__sub.txt

◈ Implementer status vocabulary as a coordination protocol

mechanism

Every dispatched worker reports one of DONE / DONE_WITH_CONCERNS / BLOCKED / NEEDS_CONTEXT plus self-review findings — a small fixed vocabulary that lets the orchestrator route outcomes (proceed, address concerns, re-dispatch with context, escalate) without reading the whole transcript.

cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a12a9a7ccc3979272__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a7fb16fe634922b3e__sub.txt
implementer status vocabulary as a coordination protocol every dispatched worker reports one of done / done_with_concerns / blocked / needs_context plus self-review findings — a small fixed vocabulary that lets the orchestrator route outcomes (proceed, address concerns, re-dispatch with context, escalate) without reading the whole transcript. mechanism cogmem__agent-a108bbed4451ea70a__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a12a9a7ccc3979272__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a7fb16fe634922b3e__sub.txt

◈ "Do NOT trust the report — verify"

experiment/benchmark

The spec-verification pattern used throughout: a second agent is told the implementer's claims and instructed to re-derive every one by reading code and re-running tests itself, explicitly not trusting the report. Several verifiers independently reproduced subtle root-cause analyses (e.g., the default-rich distractor) before endorsing. Adversarial verification as a standing role, not an occasional audit.

cogmem__agent-a5b95617df1c6b4a4__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-aafa36e69f2bd7765__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-aadee6e82be2f3844__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a1051586927dd49e1__sub.txt

do not trust the report — verify the spec-verification pattern used throughout: a second agent is told the implementer's claims and instructed to re-derive every one by reading code and re-running tests itself, explicitly not trusting the report. several verifiers independently reproduced subtle root-cause analyses (e.g., the default-rich distractor) before endorsing. adversarial verification as a standing role, not an occasional audit. experiment/benchmark cogmem__agent-a5b95617df1c6b4a4__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-aafa36e69f2bd7765__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-aadee6e82be2f3844__sub.txt, cogmem__agent-a1051586927dd49e1__sub.txt ---

Part B — AMBench (assistant-memory-bench)

◈ AMBench: a standalone validator for memory recall systems

product

The project's stated identity: a self-contained benchmark that any memory recall system (vector DB, Mem0-style API, agentic search, self-index) can plug into through one function and be scored against published baselines — an "assistant benchmark" for memory behavior, not a retrieval-accuracy leaderboard.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
"the idea is for the project is to be a standalone validator for different types of memory recall systems"
ambench: a standalone validator for memory recall systems the project's stated identity: a self-contained benchmark that any memory recall system (vector db, mem0-style api, agentic search, self-index) can plug into through one function and be scored against published baselines — an assistant benchmark for memory behavior, not a retrieval-accuracy leaderboard. product ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt the idea is for the project is to be a standalone validator for different types of memory recall systems

◈ The single-function black-box contract: recall(view, *, embedder, k)

mechanism

Every entrant implements exactly one function: given a black-box view (the deposits visible for a case — each with content, channel, timestamp — plus the query/active context), return up to k memory contents. The harness supplies the embedder; the entrant never sees curated labels. The whole integration surface is one signature.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
the single-function black-box contract: recall(view, *, embedder, k) every entrant implements exactly one function: given a black-box view (the deposits visible for a case — each with content, channel, timestamp — plus the query/active context), return up to k memory contents. the harness supplies the embedder; the entrant never sees curated labels. the whole integration surface is one signature. mechanism ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ The four axes: P / S / T / D

experiment/benchmark

The benchmark's memory-behavior taxonomy — four axes of 10 cases each: P_preference (act on a stored user preference), S_scope (recall the right tenant/scope's memory, not its twin), T_temporal (prefer the correction over the stale version), D_trust (resist an untrusted planted memory). Color-tagged P/S/T/D throughout the tooling.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt, ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt
the four axes: p / s / t / d the benchmark's memory-behavior taxonomy — four axes of 10 cases each: p_preference (act on a stored user preference), s_scope (recall the right tenant/scope's memory, not its twin), t_temporal (prefer the correction over the stale version), d_trust (resist an untrusted planted memory). color-tagged p/s/t/d throughout the tooling. experiment/benchmark ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt, ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt

◈ Every case plants both a need and a trap

experiment/benchmark

Each benchmark case seeds a needed memory (the probe's needs) and a poison/trap memory (wrong tenant, stale version, or untrusted import), so retrieval quality (hit@k) and discrimination (poison-fold rate) can be scored independently on the same case. A system can retrieve perfectly and still fail by folding the trap.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt, ambench__85ba756f-9f89-4130-888c-68c313922d71.txt
every case plants both a need and a trap each benchmark case seeds a needed memory (the probe's `needs`) and a poison/trap memory (wrong tenant, stale version, or untrusted import), so retrieval quality (hit@k) and discrimination (poison-fold rate) can be scored independently on the same case. a system can retrieve perfectly and still fail by folding the trap. experiment/benchmark ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt, ambench__85ba756f-9f89-4130-888c-68c313922d71.txt

◈ M4: action-level judging with a three-verdict scale

experiment/benchmark

The scoring core: for each case the assistant produces an action given the cue (with recalled memories vs without), and an LLM judge classifies each action against the ground-truth action and the wrong (poisoned) action — verdict ∈ {correct, wrong, other}, mapped to {+1, −1, 0}, averaged over seeds. Memory is scored by what the assistant does with it, not by what it retrieves.

ambench__91c09d5e-8705-4b94-b308-6cae6ddea38e.txt, ambench__520bfa00-e1d3-434c-acdd-439125692fe9.txt
m4: action-level judging with a three-verdict scale the scoring core: for each case the assistant produces an *action* given the cue (with recalled memories vs without), and an llm judge classifies each action against the ground-truth action and the wrong (poisoned) action — verdict ∈ {correct, wrong, other}, mapped to {+1, −1, 0}, averaged over seeds. memory is scored by what the assistant *does* with it, not by what it retrieves. experiment/benchmark ambench__91c09d5e-8705-4b94-b308-6cae6ddea38e.txt, ambench__520bfa00-e1d3-434c-acdd-439125692fe9.txt

◈ Fold-force: the with-minus-without lift

experiment/benchmark

The headline scoring formula: fold_force = M4(with recall) − M4(without recall), computed within each system over paired seeds. It measures how much folding memory into context actually changes action quality relative to that same system's no-memory floor — so a single-system run is self-contained (it carries its own control arm).

ambench__520bfa00-e1d3-434c-acdd-439125692fe9.txt, ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
fold-force: the with-minus-without lift the headline scoring formula: fold_force = m4(with recall) − m4(without recall), computed within each system over paired seeds. it measures how much folding memory into context actually changes action quality relative to that same system's no-memory floor — so a single-system run is self-contained (it carries its own control arm). experiment/benchmark ambench__520bfa00-e1d3-434c-acdd-439125692fe9.txt, ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ The judge prompt: match against two references, return strict JSON

mechanism

The judge template asks "Which reference does the candidate action match?" against the ground-truth action and the wrong action, returning strict JSON {"verdict": "correct"|"wrong"|"other"} — judging as reference-matching between a right and a planted-wrong anchor, with "other" absorbing hedges and refusals.

ambench__91c09d5e-8705-4b94-b308-6cae6ddea38e.txt
the judge prompt: match against two references, return strict json the judge template asks which reference does the candidate action match? against the ground-truth action and the wrong action, returning strict json { verdict : correct | wrong | other } — judging as reference-matching between a right and a planted-wrong anchor, with other absorbing hedges and refusals. mechanism ambench__91c09d5e-8705-4b94-b308-6cae6ddea38e.txt

◈ Fail-closed verdict parsing

mechanism

Judge output is parsed by extracting the first {...} JSON span; any parse failure or unrecognized label falls back to "other" (score 0) — malformed LLM output can never inflate a score, only zero it. The refactor to judge_label + M4_FROM_LABEL preserved raw labels for humans while keeping the numeric mapping byte-identical.

ambench__91c09d5e-8705-4b94-b308-6cae6ddea38e.txt, ambench__520bfa00-e1d3-434c-acdd-439125692fe9.txt
fail-closed verdict parsing judge output is parsed by extracting the first {...} json span; any parse failure or unrecognized label falls back to other (score 0) — malformed llm output can never inflate a score, only zero it. the refactor to `judge_label` + `m4_from_label` preserved raw labels for humans while keeping the numeric mapping byte-identical. mechanism ambench__91c09d5e-8705-4b94-b308-6cae6ddea38e.txt, ambench__520bfa00-e1d3-434c-acdd-439125692fe9.txt

◈ Deterministic paired seeding

mechanism

Each (case, system, seed-index) triple derives a stable seed via _stable_seed(case_id, system_name, j), and the with/without conditions share the same call — so the fold-force comparison is paired, reproducible, and immune to sampling luck differences between arms.

ambench__520bfa00-e1d3-434c-acdd-439125692fe9.txt
deterministic paired seeding each (case, system, seed-index) triple derives a stable seed via `_stable_seed(case_id, system_name, j)`, and the with/without conditions share the same call — so the fold-force comparison is paired, reproducible, and immune to sampling luck differences between arms. mechanism ambench__520bfa00-e1d3-434c-acdd-439125692fe9.txt

◈ The stateful-API impedance mismatch — and the ~30-line bridge

mechanism

The key teaching insight of the example/ dir: real memory APIs are stateful (add() over time, then search()), but AMBench hands the whole visible history per case and scores each case in isolation. The bridge is ~30 lines: fresh store per case → replay the case's deposits as add() calls → scope-conditioned search → return contents → register as a System. That bridge, not the contract, is what every real integrator actually needs shown.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
the stateful-api impedance mismatch — and the ~30-line bridge the key teaching insight of the example/ dir: real memory apis are *stateful* (add() over time, then search()), but ambench hands the whole visible history per case and scores each case in isolation. the bridge is ~30 lines: fresh store per case → replay the case's deposits as add() calls → scope-conditioned search → return contents → register as a system. that bridge, not the contract, is what every real integrator actually needs shown. mechanism ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ Channel vocabulary is the trust signal

result/finding

Empirical discovery in the case data: deposits arrive on four channels — raw_user_input and tool_observation (trusted: the user said it or a tool observed it) vs provider_import and external_sync (untrusted third-party imports). Every D_trust poison rides an untrusted channel and every needed answer rides a trusted one, so a channel-based trust gate removes all poison and no answers. The author's first guessed vocabulary was wrong and the gate silently did nothing — grounding in real data was load-bearing.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
"My guessed vocabulary was wrong... I verified poison rides only on provider_import/external_sync."
channel vocabulary is the trust signal empirical discovery in the case data: deposits arrive on four channels — raw_user_input and tool_observation (trusted: the user said it or a tool observed it) vs provider_import and external_sync (untrusted third-party imports). every d_trust poison rides an untrusted channel and every needed answer rides a trusted one, so a channel-based trust gate removes all poison and no answers. the author's first guessed vocabulary was wrong and the gate silently did nothing — grounding in real data was load-bearing. result/finding ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt my guessed vocabulary was wrong... i verified poison rides only on `provider_import`/`external_sync`.

◈ Recency dedup sabotages scope: the cross-axis tension made concrete

result/finding

A naive "same fact → keep newest" supersession rule fixed the temporal axis but broke the scope axis by collapsing two different tenants' near-identical memories into one (possibly the wrong one). The benchmark's central claim — that single-discipline policies break other axes — surfaced spontaneously while building the example.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
recency dedup sabotages scope: the cross-axis tension made concrete a naive same fact → keep newest supersession rule fixed the temporal axis but *broke* the scope axis by collapsing two different tenants' near-identical memories into one (possibly the wrong one). the benchmark's central claim — that single-discipline policies break other axes — surfaced spontaneously while building the example. result/finding ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ Same-entity vs different-entity: the S/T discriminator

framing/ontologyconversation-only

Timestamps do NOT separate scope twins from temporal corrections (both pairs differ in time). The real distinction: temporal near-duplicates concern the same entity (a genuine correction), scope twins concern different entities (different tenants) — and the active-scope name the assistant already knows is the usable discriminator.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
same-entity vs different-entity: the s/t discriminator timestamps do not separate scope twins from temporal corrections (both pairs differ in time). the real distinction: temporal near-duplicates concern the *same* entity (a genuine correction), scope twins concern *different* entities (different tenants) — and the active-scope name the assistant already knows is the usable discriminator. framing/ontology ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt yes

◈ Axis-separated composable policy

mechanism

The winning entrant design: three independent rules — trust gate (drop untrusted channels), scope gate (require the active-scope token), recency tiebreak (keep newest among same-entity near-duplicates) — each keyed to a signal that fires only on its own axis (active_scope set on all 10 S cases and ~nowhere else), so the rules compose without cross-interference. Verified: drops all poison (D), keeps only the in-scope memory (S), keeps only the v2 correction (T), all from black-box signals with no curated labels.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
axis-separated composable policy the winning entrant design: three independent rules — trust gate (drop untrusted channels), scope gate (require the active-scope token), recency tiebreak (keep newest among same-entity near-duplicates) — each keyed to a signal that fires only on its own axis (active_scope set on all 10 s cases and ~nowhere else), so the rules compose without cross-interference. verified: drops all poison (d), keeps only the in-scope memory (s), keeps only the v2 correction (t), all from black-box signals with no curated labels. mechanism ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ Scope-name token matching survives weak embeddings

result/findingconversation-only

A data-validation finding: the active scope's name token appears in the in-scope memory's text and never in the wrong-scope twin, so an exact scope-name match selects the right tenant even under the weak hashed-bag-of-words echo embedder — the scope gate's correctness doesn't depend on embedding quality.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
scope-name token matching survives weak embeddings a data-validation finding: the active scope's name token appears in the in-scope memory's text and never in the wrong-scope twin, so an exact scope-name match selects the right tenant even under the weak hashed-bag-of-words echo embedder — the scope gate's correctness doesn't depend on embedding quality. result/finding ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt yes

◈ "Scope ∧ trust ∧ correction" — the benchmark's headline conjunction

framing/ontology

The benchmark's thesis compressed to a formula: a good assistant memory must satisfy scope AND trust AND correction simultaneously; each is easy alone and the conjunction is what discriminates. The example demonstrates the conjunction through a user's own add/search API.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
"That's the benchmark's headline (scope ∧ trust ∧ correction) demonstrated through a user's own add/search API."
scope ∧ trust ∧ correction — the benchmark's headline conjunction the benchmark's thesis compressed to a formula: a good assistant memory must satisfy scope and trust and correction simultaneously; each is easy alone and the conjunction is what discriminates. the example demonstrates the conjunction through a user's own add/search api. framing/ontology ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt that's the benchmark's headline (scope ∧ trust ∧ correction) demonstrated through a user's own add/search api.

◈ Cosine-similarity RAG as the canonical base connection

product

The user's design decision: because cosine RAG is the simplest memory system to reason about, the benchmark should always ship a cosine-RAG example as the base connection — every other memory system connects through the same bridge with a different store ("swap the store, not the glue"). The minimal bridge reproduces the built-in cosine_rag baseline exactly, and that equivalence is the point: it is the baseline to beat.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
