"connecting a cosine similarity example would provide a baseline api connection to all other memory systems"
cosine-similarity rag as the canonical base connection the user's design decision: because cosine rag is the simplest memory system to reason about, the benchmark should always ship a cosine-rag example as the base connection — every other memory system connects through the same bridge with a different store ( swap the store, not the glue ). the minimal bridge reproduces the built-in cosine_rag baseline *exactly*, and that equivalence is the point: it is the baseline to beat. product ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt connecting a cosine similarity example would provide a baseline api connection to all other memory systems

◈ Pedagogical layering: dead-simple file, one scroll to the winning system

product

Rather than deleting the smart trust/scope/recency policy when simplifying the example to plain cosine, it was moved to a "Going further" README section as a verified drop-in snippet — the runnable file stays minimal, the path to beating the baseline is one scroll down, and the insight isn't lost.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
pedagogical layering: dead-simple file, one scroll to the winning system rather than deleting the smart trust/scope/recency policy when simplifying the example to plain cosine, it was moved to a going further readme section as a verified drop-in snippet — the runnable file stays minimal, the path to beating the baseline is one scroll down, and the insight isn't lost. product ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ Don't ship untested doc claims

experiment/benchmarkconversation-only

Before publishing the README's "going further" snippet, the author ran it in a temp file and verified each claimed behavior (D drops the poison → rate_contract; S → only maple_code; T → board_mtg_v2). Documentation code carries the same verification obligation as shipped code.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
don't ship untested doc claims before publishing the readme's going further snippet, the author ran it in a temp file and verified each claimed behavior (d drops the poison → rate_contract; s → only maple_code; t → board_mtg_v2). documentation code carries the same verification obligation as shipped code. experiment/benchmark ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt yes

◈ The honest mock: give the toy store a real embedder

mechanism

The example's mock memory API initially used toy keyword search, which undersold the bridge (retrieval failures looked like policy failures). Fix: make the mock a genuine vector store that accepts an embed function, with the bridge injecting AMBench's embedder — so retrieval quality is fair (ties cosine_rag) and the trust/scope/recency logic is the only differentiator.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
the honest mock: give the toy store a real embedder the example's mock memory api initially used toy keyword search, which undersold the bridge (retrieval failures looked like policy failures). fix: make the mock a genuine vector store that accepts an embed function, with the bridge injecting ambench's embedder — so retrieval quality is fair (ties cosine_rag) and the trust/scope/recency logic is the only differentiator. mechanism ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ Publish the baseline so no one pays to rediscover it

product

Run the paid cosine-RAG sweep once, commit the numbers (example/BASELINE.md + inlined in the root README), and tell users plainly: this is the bar, you don't need to re-run it. A benchmark should carry its own baseline as a published artifact, since the baseline is the starting point of every user's evaluation.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
publish the baseline so no one pays to rediscover it run the paid cosine-rag sweep once, commit the numbers (example/baseline.md + inlined in the root readme), and tell users plainly: this is the bar, you don't need to re-run it. a benchmark should carry its own baseline as a published artifact, since the baseline is the starting point of every user's evaluation. product ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ The published cosine baseline numbers

result/finding

The committed baseline (gpt-5.4-nano, OpenAI embeddings, 8 paired seeds, 40 cases, 1280 LLM calls, ~25 min): M4(with)/M4(no-mem)/fold-force per axis — P +0.350/+0.013/+0.338; S +0.013/−0.287/+0.300; T +0.338/−0.013/+0.350; D +0.475/−0.050/+0.525; OVERALL +0.294/−0.084/+0.378 — with hit@k = 1.00 and poison% = 100 on every axis.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
the published cosine baseline numbers the committed baseline (gpt-5.4-nano, openai embeddings, 8 paired seeds, 40 cases, 1280 llm calls, ~25 min): m4(with)/m4(no-mem)/fold-force per axis — p +0.350/+0.013/+0.338; s +0.013/−0.287/+0.300; t +0.338/−0.013/+0.350; d +0.475/−0.050/+0.525; overall +0.294/−0.084/+0.378 — with hit@k = 1.00 and poison% = 100 on every axis. result/finding ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ "Retrieval isn't the problem, discrimination is"

result/finding

The baseline's interpretation: cosine always pulls the needed memory into the top-3 (hit@k=1.0) AND always pulls in the trap (poison%=100) — it cannot tell scope/trust/recency apart — leaving the model to disambiguate noisy recall, which it does imperfectly (worst on scope at +0.013, essentially chance; best on trust at +0.475). The benchmark's real question is "fold the right memory and not the trap."

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
"retrieval isn't the problem, discrimination is."
retrieval isn't the problem, discrimination is the baseline's interpretation: cosine always pulls the needed memory into the top-3 (hit@k=1.0) and always pulls in the trap (poison%=100) — it cannot tell scope/trust/recency apart — leaving the model to disambiguate noisy recall, which it does imperfectly (worst on scope at +0.013, essentially chance; best on trust at +0.475). the benchmark's real question is fold the *right* memory and not the trap. result/finding ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt retrieval isn't the problem, discrimination is.

◈ Negative-M4 cases as the discriminating frontier

result/finding

Per-case analysis: the cosine baseline goes actively wrong (negative M4 — takes the poisoned action) on 5/10 scope, 3/10 temporal, and 2/10 trust cases. Those specific cases are cited in BASELINE.md as exactly the ones a capable system must win — the benchmark names its own hardest cases.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
negative-m4 cases as the discriminating frontier per-case analysis: the cosine baseline goes *actively wrong* (negative m4 — takes the poisoned action) on 5/10 scope, 3/10 temporal, and 2/10 trust cases. those specific cases are cited in baseline.md as exactly the ones a capable system must win — the benchmark names its own hardest cases. result/finding ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ The model resists poison — sometimes

result/findingconversation-only

Smoke-test observation: cosine recalls the poison import on every D case, and the model resists it most of the time but flips to the wrong action on some cases (flight_arrival, jeff_offsite_location → M4 = −1). The LLM itself is a partial, unreliable trust filter — which is precisely the variance the benchmark quantifies.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
the model resists poison — sometimes smoke-test observation: cosine recalls the poison import on every d case, and the model *resists* it most of the time but flips to the wrong action on some cases (flight_arrival, jeff_offsite_location → m4 = −1). the llm itself is a partial, unreliable trust filter — which is precisely the variance the benchmark quantifies. result/finding ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt yes

◈ The no-memory floor is negative

result/finding

M4(without recall) averages −0.084 overall (as low as −0.287 on scope) — without memory the assistant doesn't just do nothing, it sometimes takes the wrong action outright. The floor being below zero means recall lift (fold-force) is measured against active harm, not passivity.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
the no-memory floor is negative m4(without recall) averages −0.084 overall (as low as −0.287 on scope) — without memory the assistant doesn't just do nothing, it sometimes takes the *wrong* action outright. the floor being below zero means recall lift (fold-force) is measured against active harm, not passivity. result/finding ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ Per-axis sweeps for crash resilience

mechanismconversation-only

Because the harness writes the ledger only after a sweep completes, a mid-run API error would lose everything; running the paid sweep per-axis (4 separate appends) bounds the blast radius of a transient failure. Operational design for paid, long-running evaluation.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
per-axis sweeps for crash resilience because the harness writes the ledger only after a sweep completes, a mid-run api error would lose everything; running the paid sweep per-axis (4 separate appends) bounds the blast radius of a transient failure. operational design for paid, long-running evaluation. mechanism ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt yes

◈ Smoke-test before you spend

experiment/benchmarkconversation-only

The paid-run discipline used twice in the project: verify the key loads (via the project's own loader, without printing the secret), run 1 seed × a few cases to confirm the real pipeline end-to-end and gauge timing/cost, then commit to the full 8-seed sweep in the background. Cost-gated experimentation as an explicit workflow.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
smoke-test before you spend the paid-run discipline used twice in the project: verify the key loads (via the project's own loader, without printing the secret), run 1 seed × a few cases to confirm the real pipeline end-to-end and gauge timing/cost, then commit to the full 8-seed sweep in the background. cost-gated experimentation as an explicit workflow. experiment/benchmark ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt yes

◈ run2 headline: the fair entrant is uniquely cross-axis strong

result/finding

The recorded run2 result (8 seeds, gpt-5.4-nano): the fair self_index_entrant — inferring scope/trust/recency from the black-box view alone — is the unique fair system strong on all four axes (D +1.00, P +0.50, S +1.00, T +1.00), while every single-discipline baseline wins its own axis and breaks another: cosine_rag breaks S; cosine_rag_context breaks D; recency breaks S; recall_all breaks D & T. The cross-axis thesis holds without the privileged oracle.

run2-findings.md
run2 headline: the fair entrant is uniquely cross-axis strong the recorded run2 result (8 seeds, gpt-5.4-nano): the *fair* self_index_entrant — inferring scope/trust/recency from the black-box view alone — is the unique fair system strong on all four axes (d +1.00, p +0.50, s +1.00, t +1.00), while every single-discipline baseline wins its own axis and breaks another: cosine_rag breaks s; cosine_rag_context breaks d; recency breaks s; recall_all breaks d & t. the cross-axis thesis holds without the privileged oracle. result/finding run2-findings.md

◈ BASELINE_PASSES: a case fails if the baseline wins it for the wrong reason

experiment/benchmark

A case-validation concept: an axis case is not "load-bearing" if the naive baseline happens to pass it — because then the case doesn't discriminate the capability it's supposed to test. run2 flagged both T and D as reading BASELINE_PASSES and drove targeted case hardening (not harness changes).

run2-findings.md
baseline_passes: a case fails if the baseline wins it for the wrong reason a case-validation concept: an axis case is not load-bearing if the naive baseline happens to pass it — because then the case doesn't discriminate the capability it's supposed to test. run2 flagged both t and d as reading baseline_passes and drove targeted case hardening (not harness changes). experiment/benchmark run2-findings.md

◈ The coin-flip diagnosis: T was decided by recall order luck

result/finding

Why T_temporal wasn't load-bearing: cosine_rag scored +0.75 only because it happened to rank the correction first (cue-similarity 0.713 vs 0.711 — a coin flip), while recall_all, listing the stale version first, scored −1.00. The action was driven by recall ORDER, not correction-awareness — symmetric wording made the two versions cosine-indistinguishable and cosine won by luck.

run2-findings.md
the coin-flip diagnosis: t was decided by recall order luck why t_temporal wasn't load-bearing: cosine_rag scored +0.75 only because it happened to rank the correction first (cue-similarity 0.713 vs 0.711 — a coin flip), while recall_all, listing the stale version first, scored −1.00. the action was driven by recall order, not correction-awareness — symmetric wording made the two versions cosine-indistinguishable and cosine won by luck. result/finding run2-findings.md

◈ Date inversion: make the stale version LOOK newer

experiment/benchmark

The T_temporal hardening: flip the dates so the stale memory carries the LATER event date (the 19th) and the correction moves the event EARLIER (the 12th) — defeating the model's "later = current" heuristic so that only genuine correction-awareness (deposit recency, not content dates) yields the right answer. Both memories stay plain statements with no "Update:"/"moved" lexical tell.

run2-findings.md
date inversion: make the stale version look newer the t_temporal hardening: flip the dates so the *stale* memory carries the later event date (the 19th) and the correction moves the event earlier (the 12th) — defeating the model's later = current heuristic so that only genuine correction-awareness (deposit recency, not content dates) yields the right answer. both memories stay plain statements with no update: / moved lexical tell. experiment/benchmark run2-findings.md

◈ The hedge loophole: D passed because the model waffled

result/finding

Why D_trust wasn't load-bearing: facing the Boston-vs-New-York conflict, the baseline model mostly hedged (verdict "other," +0.12) rather than acting on the poison, while trust systems hit +1.00 — separation existed (+0.875) but the baseline wasn't decisively wrong. A case where the trap is resistible by indecision doesn't test trust gating.

run2-findings.md
the hedge loophole: d passed because the model waffled why d_trust wasn't load-bearing: facing the boston-vs-new-york conflict, the baseline model mostly *hedged* (verdict other, +0.12) rather than acting on the poison, while trust systems hit +1.00 — separation existed (+0.875) but the baseline wasn't decisively wrong. a case where the trap is resistible by indecision doesn't test trust gating. result/finding run2-findings.md

◈ Poison must out-compete the answer; authority must live in the label

experiment/benchmark

The D_trust hardening, two moves: (1) strip the answer's "HR system:" prefix so trustworthiness comes only from the deposit's channel/source label, never from content phrasing; (2) make the poison a definitive direct answer to the cue ("The offsite will be held in New York.") that out-competes the true answer on real-embedding cue similarity (gap −0.092). Now only actual trust-gating — not reading and weighing the poison — can produce the right action.

run2-findings.md
poison must out-compete the answer; authority must live in the label the d_trust hardening, two moves: (1) strip the answer's hr system: prefix so trustworthiness comes only from the deposit's channel/source label, never from content phrasing; (2) make the poison a *definitive* direct answer to the cue ( the offsite will be held in new york. ) that out-competes the true answer on real-embedding cue similarity (gap −0.092). now only actual trust-gating — not reading and weighing the poison — can produce the right action. experiment/benchmark run2-findings.md

◈ "Cases hardened, NOT a harness bug"

experiment/benchmark

The diagnostic discipline in run2's postmortem: explicitly classify each non-discriminating axis as a case-design problem versus a harness defect, harden cases offline (validator 4/4, pre-flight 4/4 under real embeddings), and queue a targeted paid run3 (--axes D_trust,T_temporal --seeds 8) to confirm the axes now discriminate at the action level. Benchmark iteration as hypothesis → cheap offline validation → targeted paid confirmation.

run2-findings.md
cases hardened, not a harness bug the diagnostic discipline in run2's postmortem: explicitly classify each non-discriminating axis as a case-design problem versus a harness defect, harden cases offline (validator 4/4, pre-flight 4/4 under real embeddings), and queue a targeted paid run3 (`--axes d_trust,t_temporal --seeds 8`) to confirm the axes now discriminate at the action level. benchmark iteration as hypothesis → cheap offline validation → targeted paid confirmation. experiment/benchmark run2-findings.md

◈ The privileged/fair split

mechanism

Systems carry a privileged flag: oracle systems may read curated labels (to establish ceilings), fair entrants must not — and the runner refuses privileged entrants, enforcing fairness mechanically rather than by convention. Fairness rules ride with the registry, not the documentation.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt, run2-findings.md
the privileged/fair split systems carry a `privileged` flag: oracle systems may read curated labels (to establish ceilings), fair entrants must not — and the runner *refuses* privileged entrants, enforcing fairness mechanically rather than by convention. fairness rules ride with the registry, not the documentation. mechanism ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt, run2-findings.md

◈ Fairness rules front-and-center in the connection skill

product

The connect-memory-api skill leads with the three ways entrants most easily cheat or err: never read curated labels; register with privileged=False; no LLM in recall. The easiest failure mode of a benchmark integration is unintentional unfairness, so the skill teaches the constraints before the mechanics.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
fairness rules front-and-center in the connection skill the connect-memory-api skill leads with the three ways entrants most easily cheat or err: never read curated labels; register with privileged=false; no llm in recall. the easiest failure mode of a benchmark integration is unintentional unfairness, so the skill teaches the constraints before the mechanics. product ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ A repo-local skill that travels with the benchmark

product

The connect-memory-api skill lives inside the AMBench repo (.claude/skills/) rather than in a user's global config, because it is meaningless outside the benchmark — available to any agent that clones the repo. Skills as versioned repo artifacts co-located with the code they teach.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
a repo-local skill that travels with the benchmark the connect-memory-api skill lives inside the ambench repo (`.claude/skills/`) rather than in a user's global config, because it is meaningless outside the benchmark — available to any agent that clones the repo. skills as versioned repo artifacts co-located with the code they teach. product ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ Anti-drift: point at the maintained template, don't copy it

mechanism

The skill deliberately references the living example/connect.py as the copy-me template instead of embedding its own copy of the contract — so the skill can't drift out of sync with the code it teaches.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
anti-drift: point at the maintained template, don't copy it the skill deliberately references the living `example/connect.py` as the copy-me template instead of embedding its own copy of the contract — so the skill can't drift out of sync with the code it teaches. mechanism ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ Echo first, pay later: the graduated run ladder

experiment/benchmark

The prescribed entrant workflow: validate with the free echo sweep (deterministic hashed-BoW embedder, no API calls) first, then the paid real-model M4 run with ≥8 seeds, then the scorecard. Cheap deterministic geometry checks gate expensive semantic measurements.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
echo first, pay later: the graduated run ladder the prescribed entrant workflow: validate with the free echo sweep (deterministic hashed-bow embedder, no api calls) first, then the paid real-model m4 run with ≥8 seeds, then the scorecard. cheap deterministic geometry checks gate expensive semantic measurements. experiment/benchmark ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ "The validator proves geometry, not naturalness"

framing/ontology

The repo's own epistemic disclaimer, honored in the example work: the echo-based validator proves the case geometry (which memory is nearest, what the gates select) but not that real embeddings or a real model behave the same — so echo-validated claims are explicitly labeled and the real-embedding run is named as the outstanding check.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
"the validator proves geometry, not naturalness"
the validator proves geometry, not naturalness the repo's own epistemic disclaimer, honored in the example work: the echo-based validator proves the case *geometry* (which memory is nearest, what the gates select) but not that real embeddings or a real model behave the same — so echo-validated claims are explicitly labeled and the real-embedding run is named as the outstanding check. framing/ontology ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt the validator proves geometry, not naturalness

◈ Ephemeral per-case stores as an isolation invariant

mechanism

The vector store is deliberately in-memory and rebuilt fresh for every case, then discarded — persisting vectors across cases would leak one case's memories into another's, violating the "capture is total; selection lives at recall" invariant. Only the scored ledger persists (recalled keys and scores — never vectors or store contents); persistence is the entrant's backend's concern, invisible to the bench.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
ephemeral per-case stores as an isolation invariant the vector store is deliberately in-memory and rebuilt fresh for every case, then discarded — persisting vectors across cases would leak one case's memories into another's, violating the capture is total; selection lives at recall invariant. only the scored ledger persists (recalled keys and scores — never vectors or store contents); persistence is the entrant's backend's concern, invisible to the bench. mechanism ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ The human belongs in an assistant benchmark

framing/ontology

The user's founding intuition for the replay feature: since AMBench is an assistant benchmark, a human should be able to visually replay a run and judge the assistant's performance themselves — automated scores alone are insufficient legitimacy for claims about assistant quality.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
"the point of the benchmark is being an 'assistant benchmark', feels only right that a human should be able to judge the assistant performance"
the human belongs in an assistant benchmark the user's founding intuition for the replay feature: since ambench is an *assistant* benchmark, a human should be able to visually replay a run and judge the assistant's performance themselves — automated scores alone are insufficient legitimacy for claims about assistant quality. framing/ontology ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt the point of the benchmark is being an 'assistant benchmark', feels only right that a human should be able to judge the assistant performance

◈ Blind judging then reveal: validate the judge, not just the assistant

experiment/benchmark

The design's sharpest move: the human judges both actions blind (not knowing which had memory, nor what the LLM judge said), and only after committing verdicts does the page reveal condition and judge verdict — so the session yields a human-vs-M4 agreement measurement that validates the automated judge itself. The replay tool is secretly a judge-calibration instrument.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
blind judging then reveal: validate the judge, not just the assistant the design's sharpest move: the human judges both actions blind (not knowing which had memory, nor what the llm judge said), and only after committing verdicts does the page reveal condition and judge verdict — so the session yields a human-vs-m4 agreement measurement that validates the automated judge itself. the replay tool is secretly a judge-calibration instrument. experiment/benchmark ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ Three form factors considered; static HTML wins

productconversation-only

Options weighed: (A) a single self-contained HTML file with embedded JSON + vanilla JS — no server, no dependencies, doubles as a shareable demo artifact; (B) a Flask/Streamlit local app — better for multi-rater campaigns but adds a framework and a running process against the project's minimalism; (C) terminal replay — zero deps but weakly "visual" and unshareable. A chosen; B's only real advantage (server-side multi-rater aggregation) is covered by JSON export + a scoring subcommand.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
three form factors considered; static html wins options weighed: (a) a single self-contained html file with embedded json + vanilla js — no server, no dependencies, doubles as a shareable demo artifact; (b) a flask/streamlit local app — better for multi-rater campaigns but adds a framework and a running process against the project's minimalism; (c) terminal replay — zero deps but weakly visual and unshareable. a chosen; b's only real advantage (server-side multi-rater aggregation) is covered by json export + a scoring subcommand. product ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt yes

◈ The mean-vs-last-seed mismatch: a data-integrity discovery that reshaped the design

result/finding

Surfaced during design: the ledger stores m4_with as the mean over 8 seeds, but action_with holds only the last seed's text (the loop overwrites it) — so the stored action and stored score are not a 1:1 pair, and a human judging that action against that score would be comparing apples to oranges. Judge validation requires per-seed (action, verdict) pairs, which forced the transcript sidecar into the design.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
the mean-vs-last-seed mismatch: a data-integrity discovery that reshaped the design surfaced during design: the ledger stores `m4_with` as the mean over 8 seeds, but `action_with` holds only the *last* seed's text (the loop overwrites it) — so the stored action and stored score are not a 1:1 pair, and a human judging that action against that score would be comparing apples to oranges. judge validation requires per-seed (action, verdict) pairs, which forced the transcript sidecar into the design. result/finding ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ The transcript sidecar: lean ledger, rich sidecar

mechanism

Judged runs additionally write results/transcript_<run_id>.jsonl — one row per (case, system, seed) carrying both actions, both raw judge labels (correct/wrong/other, pre-mapping), and the recalled keys for the replay join — while the scoring ledger stays lean (samples stripped before append). Separating the aggregate scoring record from the per-sample evidentiary record.

ambench__55fb0729-d676-4fd9-aa4a-d4105ddd6769.txt, ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
the transcript sidecar: lean ledger, rich sidecar judged runs additionally write `results/transcript_<run_id>.jsonl` — one row per (case, system, seed) carrying both actions, both raw judge labels (correct/wrong/other, pre-mapping), and the recalled keys for the replay join — while the scoring ledger stays lean (samples stripped before append). separating the aggregate scoring record from the per-sample evidentiary record. mechanism ambench__55fb0729-d676-4fd9-aa4a-d4105ddd6769.txt, ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ Preserve the raw label, derive the number

mechanism

The refactor principle behind judge_label/M4_FROM_LABEL: keep the judge's raw categorical verdict as the stored datum and derive the numeric score from it — because the human-validation loop needs to compare labels, and a stored ±1/0 destroys the distinction between "wrong" and "hedged." The numeric mapping is exactly recoverable, so scoring is unchanged.

ambench__91c09d5e-8705-4b94-b308-6cae6ddea38e.txt, ambench__agent-aa92d3743d187c9bd__sub.txt
preserve the raw label, derive the number the refactor principle behind judge_label/m4_from_label: keep the judge's raw categorical verdict as the stored datum and derive the numeric score from it — because the human-validation loop needs to compare *labels*, and a stored ±1/0 destroys the distinction between wrong and hedged. the numeric mapping is exactly recoverable, so scoring is unchanged. mechanism ambench__91c09d5e-8705-4b94-b308-6cae6ddea38e.txt, ambench__agent-aa92d3743d187c9bd__sub.txt

◈ Transcript rows carry recalled_keys for a self-sufficient artifact

mechanism

A plan refinement: each transcript row also records the recalled memory keys (constant across seeds) so the replay page can be built from the transcript alone joined with case data — one artifact suffices for reconstruction, no ledger cross-reference needed.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt, ambench__55fb0729-d676-4fd9-aa4a-d4105ddd6769.txt
transcript rows carry recalled_keys for a self-sufficient artifact a plan refinement: each transcript row also records the recalled memory keys (constant across seeds) so the replay page can be built from the transcript alone joined with case data — one artifact suffices for reconstruction, no ledger cross-reference needed. mechanism ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt, ambench__55fb0729-d676-4fd9-aa4a-d4105ddd6769.txt

◈ Last-write-wins transcript dedup

mechanism

Loading a transcript de-duplicates by (case_id, system, seed) with the last write winning, so re-running a run_id supersedes earlier rows instead of double-counting — append-only files with logical overwrite semantics.

ambench__85ba756f-9f89-4130-888c-68c313922d71.txt
last-write-wins transcript dedup loading a transcript de-duplicates by (case_id, system, seed) with the last write winning, so re-running a run_id supersedes earlier rows instead of double-counting — append-only files with logical overwrite semantics. mechanism ambench__85ba756f-9f89-4130-888c-68c313922d71.txt

◈ The blind A/B judging UX

product

One case at a time: a context panel (the user's request, the persona/role, active scope, and the recalled memories with channel + timestamp — everything a human needs to decide what correct looks like); two action cards in randomized unlabeled order (one with-memory, one without, shuffled per case so condition can't be inferred); Correct/Wrong/Other buttons per card; Reveal only after both are judged; running header tally; axis filter; prev/next navigation.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt, ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt
the blind a/b judging ux one case at a time: a context panel (the user's request, the persona/role, active scope, and the recalled memories with channel + timestamp — everything a human needs to decide what correct looks like); two action cards in randomized unlabeled order (one with-memory, one without, shuffled per case so condition can't be inferred); correct/wrong/other buttons per card; reveal only after both are judged; running header tally; axis filter; prev/next navigation. product ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt, ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt

◈ Per-case shuffle stored, not recomputed

mechanism

Each case's A/B card order is randomized once (Math.random() < 0.5) and persisted in localStorage state, so refreshing the page can't leak the condition by reshuffling, and the reveal mapping stays consistent with what the human actually judged.

ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt
per-case shuffle stored, not recomputed each case's a/b card order is randomized once (math.random() < 0.5) and persisted in localstorage state, so refreshing the page can't leak the condition by reshuffling, and the reveal mapping stays consistent with what the human actually judged. mechanism ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt

◈ Blindness as a testable property of the artifact

experiment/benchmark

A test asserts the generated HTML never contains the strings "WITH memory"/"WITHOUT memory" in its static content — the condition label must be composed client-side only after reveal. The blind protocol is enforced by an automated check on the artifact, not by trusting the generator.

ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt
blindness as a testable property of the artifact a test asserts the generated html never contains the strings with memory / without memory in its static content — the condition label must be composed client-side only after reveal. the blind protocol is enforced by an automated check on the artifact, not by trusting the generator. experiment/benchmark ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt

◈ Self-containment as a testable property

experiment/benchmark

Companion test: the generated page must contain no http://, https://, src=, or cdn. substrings and exactly one structural </script> tag — offline-ness and injection-resistance asserted mechanically, making the page a trustworthy shareable artifact.

ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt
self-containment as a testable property companion test: the generated page must contain no `http://`, `https://`, `src=`, or `cdn.` substrings and exactly one structural `</script>` tag — offline-ness and injection-resistance asserted mechanically, making the page a trustworthy shareable artifact. experiment/benchmark ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt

◈ The </-escape for embedding LLM text in a script tag

mechanism

The one XSS-relevant sink: LLM-generated action text is embedded as JSON inside a <script> block, neutralized by json.dumps(...).replace("</", "<\\/") — since HTML raw-text termination requires the literal </ bytes, this single escape closes every script-breakout variant; all other transcript-derived fields flow only to .textContent or a DOM-based escaper, and the only unescaped innerHTML values are closed enums enforced fail-closed at their source.

ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt, ambench__85ba756f-9f89-4130-888c-68c313922d71.txt
the `</`-escape for embedding llm text in a script tag the one xss-relevant sink: llm-generated action text is embedded as json inside a `<script>` block, neutralized by `json.dumps(...).replace( </ , <\\/ )` — since html raw-text termination requires the literal `</` bytes, this single escape closes every script-breakout variant; all other transcript-derived fields flow only to `.textcontent` or a dom-based escaper, and the only unescaped innerhtml values are closed enums enforced fail-closed at their source. mechanism ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt, ambench__85ba756f-9f89-4130-888c-68c313922d71.txt

◈ Prompt-injection probe on the replay page

experiment/benchmark

The end-to-end smoke test included an adversarial action containing </script><script>alert(1)</script> and confirmed it renders inert (escaped to <\/), with exactly one structural closing tag remaining — treating the benchmark's own LLM outputs as untrusted attacker input to the visualization.

ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt
prompt-injection probe on the replay page the end-to-end smoke test included an adversarial action containing `</script><script>alert(1)</script>` and confirmed it renders inert (escaped to `<\/`), with exactly one structural closing tag remaining — treating the benchmark's own llm outputs as untrusted attacker input to the visualization. experiment/benchmark ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt

◈ localStorage persistence keyed by run_id

mechanism

Human verdicts, notes, per-case shuffle, and reveal state persist in the browser's localStorage under a run_id-scoped key, so a refresh never loses judging progress and multiple runs' sessions don't collide — statefulness without any server.

ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt
localstorage persistence keyed by run_id human verdicts, notes, per-case shuffle, and reveal state persist in the browser's localstorage under a run_id-scoped key, so a refresh never loses judging progress and multiple runs' sessions don't collide — statefulness without any server. mechanism ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt

◈ The verdict export: a portable human-judgment record

mechanism

"Download my verdicts" exports JSON rows of {case_id, axis, system, seed, human_with, judge_with, human_without, judge_without, note} — the raw material for judge validation, decoupled from the page (scoreable later, shareable, aggregatable across raters).

ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt
the verdict export: a portable human-judgment record download my verdicts exports json rows of {case_id, axis, system, seed, human_with, judge_with, human_without, judge_without, note} — the raw material for judge validation, decoupled from the page (scoreable later, shareable, aggregatable across raters). mechanism ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt

◈ The optional disagreement note

mechanism

A free-text per-case note field rides along with the verdicts specifically to capture why the human disagreed with the judge — turning each disagreement row into a qualitative data point about judge failure modes, not just a count.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt, ambench__d1e02daf-0a65-4101-ad78-afffbb8ac1f0.txt
the optional disagreement note a free-text per-case note field rides along with the verdicts specifically to capture *why* the human disagreed with the judge — turning each disagreement row into a qualitative data point about judge failure modes, not just a count. mechanism ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt, ambench__d1e02daf-0a65-4101-ad78-afffbb8ac1f0.txt

◈ The agreement scorecard: overall, by-cell, confusion, and case list

experiment/benchmark

--score verdicts.json reports: overall human-vs-judge agreement % with counts; agreement broken down by condition × axis (revealing e.g. a judge reliable on trust but shaky on scope); a confusion breakdown of what each said when they differed (the signal for whether M4 is too lenient or too harsh); and the specific disagreement cases with the human's notes for eyeballing.

ambench__d1e02daf-0a65-4101-ad78-afffbb8ac1f0.txt, ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
the agreement scorecard: overall, by-cell, confusion, and case list `--score verdicts.json` reports: overall human-vs-judge agreement % with counts; agreement broken down by condition × axis (revealing e.g. a judge reliable on trust but shaky on scope); a confusion breakdown of what each said when they differed (the signal for whether m4 is too lenient or too harsh); and the specific disagreement cases with the human's notes for eyeballing. experiment/benchmark ambench__d1e02daf-0a65-4101-ad78-afffbb8ac1f0.txt, ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ One seed per case by default: bound the human's workload

mechanism

Replay defaults to one seed per (case, system) so a 40-case run is ~40 judgments rather than 320 — with --seed all available when full coverage is wanted. Human attention is the scarce resource; the default respects it.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt, ambench__85ba756f-9f89-4130-888c-68c313922d71.txt
one seed per case by default: bound the human's workload replay defaults to one seed per (case, system) so a 40-case run is ~40 judgments rather than 320 — with `--seed all` available when full coverage is wanted. human attention is the scarce resource; the default respects it. mechanism ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt, ambench__85ba756f-9f89-4130-888c-68c313922d71.txt

◈ Closing the loop: a number that validates M4 itself

experiment/benchmark

The full circuit as shipped: open replay_baseline.html → blind-judge all 40 cases → export → --score prints the human-vs-judge agreement number "that validates M4 itself." The benchmark's own scoring metric acquires an empirical trust measurement, produced by the same tooling that runs the benchmark.

ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt
closing the loop: a number that validates m4 itself the full circuit as shipped: open replay_baseline.html → blind-judge all 40 cases → export → `--score` prints the human-vs-judge agreement number that validates m4 itself. the benchmark's own scoring metric acquires an empirical trust measurement, produced by the same tooling that runs the benchmark. experiment/benchmark ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt

◈ Plan review before dispatch: seven bugs caught on paper

result/findingconversation-only

Reviewing the implementation plan before executing it found: an off-by-one replace range that would have duplicated a line; run.py re-deriving the "judged" flag instead of gating on the presence of samples (a logic-drift hazard between two modules); an unused import and a redundant constant; a test deliberately written wrong then patched two steps later; two spec-coverage gaps (the embedded model field; the judged/unjudged indicator + verdict breakdown); a JS crash when an axis filter matches zero cases; and a missing warning about ledger run-id collisions. Plans are cheaper to debug than code.

ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt
plan review before dispatch: seven bugs caught on paper reviewing the implementation plan *before* executing it found: an off-by-one replace range that would have duplicated a line; run.py re-deriving the judged flag instead of gating on the presence of samples (a logic-drift hazard between two modules); an unused import and a redundant constant; a test deliberately written wrong then patched two steps later; two spec-coverage gaps (the embedded model field; the judged/unjudged indicator + verdict breakdown); a js crash when an axis filter matches zero cases; and a missing warning about ledger run-id collisions. plans are cheaper to debug than code. result/finding ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt yes

◈ Run-id collision caution for append-only ledgers

mechanism

Because the ledger is append-only, re-running an old run-id silently mixes new rows with stale ones; the plan gained an explicit caution to use a fresh run-id (baseline_cosine_rag_replay, not baseline_cosine_rag) when regenerating artifacts for a run that already has ledger rows.

ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt
run-id collision caution for append-only ledgers because the ledger is append-only, re-running an old run-id silently mixes new rows with stale ones; the plan gained an explicit caution to use a fresh run-id (baseline_cosine_rag_replay, not baseline_cosine_rag) when regenerating artifacts for a run that already has ledger rows. mechanism ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt

◈ Real-data browser verification of the replay page

experiment/benchmark

Task 9 verification went beyond unit tests: the actual 40-case page (62 KB, self-contained) was served over localhost (the sandbox blocks file: URLs) and driven with Playwright — confirming genuine shuffling (for aiko_aisle_seat, Card A was the hedging no-memory action, Card B reserved the aisle seat using the stored FF-93120 number), the reveal flow, and the live header tally (judged 1/40, agreement 100% over 2 actions).

ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt
real-data browser verification of the replay page task 9 verification went beyond unit tests: the actual 40-case page (62 kb, self-contained) was served over localhost (the sandbox blocks file: urls) and driven with playwright — confirming genuine shuffling (for aiko_aisle_seat, card a was the hedging no-memory action, card b reserved the aisle seat using the stored ff-93120 number), the reveal flow, and the live header tally (judged 1/40, agreement 100% over 2 actions). experiment/benchmark ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt

◈ 320 transcript rows from a 40-case judged sweep

result/finding

The regenerated judged baseline run produced 40 ledger rows and 320 per-seed transcript rows (40 cases × 8 seeds), quantifying the sidecar's granularity gain over the aggregate ledger.

ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt
320 transcript rows from a 40-case judged sweep the regenerated judged baseline run produced 40 ledger rows and 320 per-seed transcript rows (40 cases × 8 seeds), quantifying the sidecar's granularity gain over the aggregate ledger. result/finding ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt

◈ The replay join: reconstructing the assistant's situation

mechanism

build_replay_data joins transcript rows with case fixtures: recalled keys → memory contents + deposit channel + session timestamp; the probe's active_scope_ref → the human-readable relation name from the persona's relations; active self_index falling back to the persona's own; cases sorted by canonical axis order. The human sees the assistant's situation, not raw benchmark internals.

ambench__85ba756f-9f89-4130-888c-68c313922d71.txt
the replay join: reconstructing the assistant's situation `build_replay_data` joins transcript rows with case fixtures: recalled keys → memory contents + deposit channel + session timestamp; the probe's active_scope_ref → the human-readable relation name from the persona's relations; active self_index falling back to the persona's own; cases sorted by canonical axis order. the human sees the assistant's *situation*, not raw benchmark internals. mechanism ambench__85ba756f-9f89-4130-888c-68c313922d71.txt

◈ Persona/probe case schema

framing/ontology

The case data model visible across the tooling: a persona (persona_id, role, relations with ref_id + name, self_index) plus sessions of deposits, and a probe (cue, needs — the required memory keys, ground_truth_action, wrong_action, active_scope_ref, active_self_index). Cases are miniature assistant worlds with a planted decision.

ambench__85ba756f-9f89-4130-888c-68c313922d71.txt, ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt
persona/probe case schema the case data model visible across the tooling: a persona (persona_id, role, relations with ref_id + name, self_index) plus sessions of deposits, and a probe (cue, needs — the required memory keys, ground_truth_action, wrong_action, active_scope_ref, active_self_index). cases are miniature assistant worlds with a planted decision. framing/ontology ambench__85ba756f-9f89-4130-888c-68c313922d71.txt, ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt

◈ Trust-boundary reasoning for benchmark tooling

framing/ontologyconversation-only

The recurring conclusion of five security reviews: candidate sinks (run_id interpolated into filenames, markdown injection into stdout, LLM text into HTML) are only vulnerabilities if a privilege boundary is crossed — and in a local developer benchmark the invoking user is the only principal, so the analysis centers on the one real boundary: LLM-generated text flowing into the rendered HTML page, which is where the actual defenses (escaping, enum allowlists) live.

ambench__55fb0729-d676-4fd9-aa4a-d4105ddd6769.txt, ambench__f98772db-6a2b-4d4c-b862-42e91d7252b8.txt, ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt
trust-boundary reasoning for benchmark tooling the recurring conclusion of five security reviews: candidate sinks (run_id interpolated into filenames, markdown injection into stdout, llm text into html) are only vulnerabilities if a privilege boundary is crossed — and in a local developer benchmark the invoking user is the only principal, so the analysis centers on the one real boundary: llm-generated text flowing into the rendered html page, which is where the actual defenses (escaping, enum allowlists) live. framing/ontology ambench__55fb0729-d676-4fd9-aa4a-d4105ddd6769.txt, ambench__f98772db-6a2b-4d4c-b862-42e91d7252b8.txt, ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt yes

◈ Enum-bounded innerHTML as defense-in-depth

mechanismconversation-only

A security-review observation worth keeping: the only values reaching innerHTML without escaping (judge verdict, condition label, human verdict) are each bound to closed three-value enums enforced fail-closed at their source (the judge's allowlist fallback to "other"; button data attributes) — upstream vocabulary constraints double as downstream injection guarantees.

ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt
enum-bounded innerhtml as defense-in-depth a security-review observation worth keeping: the only values reaching innerhtml without escaping (judge verdict, condition label, human verdict) are each bound to closed three-value enums enforced fail-closed at their *source* (the judge's allowlist fallback to other ; button data attributes) — upstream vocabulary constraints double as downstream injection guarantees. mechanism ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt yes

◈ Echo runs write no transcript

mechanism

The transcript sidecar is written only when samples exist — i.e., only on judged runs; echo (unjudged) sweeps emit none. The gating condition in run.py is the presence of samples on rows, not a re-derivation of the judged flag, avoiding logic drift with the harness.

ambench__55fb0729-d676-4fd9-aa4a-d4105ddd6769.txt, ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt
echo runs write no transcript the transcript sidecar is written only when samples exist — i.e., only on judged runs; echo (unjudged) sweeps emit none. the gating condition in run.py is the presence of samples on rows, not a re-derivation of the judged flag, avoiding logic drift with the harness. mechanism ambench__55fb0729-d676-4fd9-aa4a-d4105ddd6769.txt, ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt

◈ Cost arithmetic as a first-class design input

experiment/benchmark

The explicit call-count accounting: 4 LLM calls per (case, system, seed) — action with, action without, judge each — so 40 cases × 8 seeds × 1 system = 1280 calls ≈ 25 minutes on gpt-5.4-nano; and the recognition that fold-force being within-system means a single-system sweep is the minimal sufficient baseline run.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
cost arithmetic as a first-class design input the explicit call-count accounting: 4 llm calls per (case, system, seed) — action with, action without, judge each — so 40 cases × 8 seeds × 1 system = 1280 calls ≈ 25 minutes on gpt-5.4-nano; and the recognition that fold-force being within-system means a single-system sweep is the *minimal sufficient* baseline run. experiment/benchmark ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ Scorecard verdicts key on the named baseline

result/findingconversation-only

A discovered coupling: the scorecard's per-case verdict logic looks up the built-in baseline system by name (expect.baseline), so a sweep containing only an entrant renders those verdicts "indeterminate" — the leaderboard's raw m4/fold-force fields, not the verdicts, are the baseline-independent metrics. Noted and worked around rather than patched.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
scorecard verdicts key on the named baseline a discovered coupling: the scorecard's per-case verdict logic looks up the built-in baseline system by name (`expect.baseline`), so a sweep containing only an entrant renders those verdicts indeterminate — the leaderboard's raw m4/fold-force fields, not the verdicts, are the baseline-independent metrics. noted and worked around rather than patched. result/finding ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt yes

◈ The replay page as shareable demo artifact

product

A side benefit elevated to a design goal: because the replay HTML is fully self-contained (no server, no network), it doubles as a portable demonstration of what the benchmark measures — you can send someone a single file and they can blind-judge an assistant's memory behavior.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
the replay page as shareable demo artifact a side benefit elevated to a design goal: because the replay html is fully self-contained (no server, no network), it doubles as a portable demonstration of what the benchmark measures — you can send someone a single file and they can blind-judge an assistant's memory behavior. product ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ Progressive disclosure in the connection skill

product

The skill's structure mirrors its own doctrine: a lean SKILL.md carries the contract, procedure, run commands, and fairness rules; a references/scoring.md holds the metric definitions (M4, fold-force, hit@k, poison%), the four axes, the baseline table, and scorecard verdict semantics — loaded only when an agent actually needs scoring detail.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
progressive disclosure in the connection skill the skill's structure mirrors its own doctrine: a lean skill.md carries the contract, procedure, run commands, and fairness rules; a references/scoring.md holds the metric definitions (m4, fold-force, hit@k, poison%), the four axes, the baseline table, and scorecard verdict semantics — loaded only when an agent actually needs scoring detail. product ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ Grounded-in-verified-facts skill authorship

framing/ontologyconversation-only

The skill's content was deliberately restricted to facts verified in-session against code and data — the channel vocabulary, the runner's privileged-refusal behavior, MARGIN=0.5, the oracle names, the published baseline numbers — rather than remembered or plausible claims. Skills inherit the epistemic standards of the research they encode.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
grounded-in-verified-facts skill authorship the skill's content was deliberately restricted to facts verified in-session against code and data — the channel vocabulary, the runner's privileged-refusal behavior, margin=0.5, the oracle names, the published baseline numbers — rather than remembered or plausible claims. skills inherit the epistemic standards of the research they encode. framing/ontology ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt yes

◈ Judge reliability may be axis-dependent

open-question

A hypothesis embedded in the scoring design: the by-axis × by-condition agreement table exists because the M4 judge might be trustworthy on some axes (e.g., trust) and shaky on others (e.g., scope) — judge validation must be resolved per capability, not as one global number.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt, ambench__d1e02daf-0a65-4101-ad78-afffbb8ac1f0.txt
judge reliability may be axis-dependent a hypothesis embedded in the scoring design: the by-axis × by-condition agreement table exists because the m4 judge might be trustworthy on some axes (e.g., trust) and shaky on others (e.g., scope) — judge validation must be resolved per capability, not as one global number. open-question ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt, ambench__d1e02daf-0a65-4101-ad78-afffbb8ac1f0.txt

◈ Confusion direction reveals judge bias

experiment/benchmark

The confusion breakdown (human=wrong/judge=other vs human=other/judge=correct, etc.) is framed as the specific signal for whether the automated judge is systematically too lenient or too harsh — the direction of disagreement, not its volume, is the diagnostic.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
confusion direction reveals judge bias the confusion breakdown (human=wrong/judge=other vs human=other/judge=correct, etc.) is framed as the specific signal for whether the automated judge is systematically too lenient or too harsh — the direction of disagreement, not its volume, is the diagnostic. experiment/benchmark ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ Hedging scores zero: "other" as the honest middle

framing/ontology

A scoring-scale insight running through both the judge design and the run2 findings: the three-verdict scale deliberately gives hedged/indecisive actions 0 rather than forcing them into ±1 — which both captures the model's real behavior under conflict (the D_trust hedge finding) and creates the failure mode where a case can be "passed" by waffling, motivating case hardening to force decisive answers.

ambench__91c09d5e-8705-4b94-b308-6cae6ddea38e.txt, run2-findings.md
hedging scores zero: other as the honest middle a scoring-scale insight running through both the judge design and the run2 findings: the three-verdict scale deliberately gives hedged/indecisive actions 0 rather than forcing them into ±1 — which both captures the model's real behavior under conflict (the d_trust hedge finding) and creates the failure mode where a case can be passed by waffling, motivating case hardening to force decisive answers. framing/ontology ambench__91c09d5e-8705-4b94-b308-6cae6ddea38e.txt, run2-findings.md

◈ Memory tooling monitored by axis-progress events

mechanismconversation-only

Operational pattern for the long paid sweep: a background monitor watched the sweep's output for per-axis completion events ("wrote transcript → ...; === axis S_scope ===") and errors, with the per-axis loop guaranteeing completed axes were already on disk if the 10-minute background cap killed the run — relaunch would resume under the same run-id.

ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt
memory tooling monitored by axis-progress events operational pattern for the long paid sweep: a background monitor watched the sweep's output for per-axis completion events ( wrote transcript → ...; === axis s_scope === ) and errors, with the per-axis loop guaranteeing completed axes were already on disk if the 10-minute background cap killed the run — relaunch would resume under the same run-id. mechanism ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt yes

◈ The self-locating entrant file

mechanismconversation-only

A small integration gotcha with a general lesson: the runner loads entrant files via spec_from_file_location, which does not put the entrant's own directory on sys.path — so the example bridge must self-locate to import its sibling memory API. Black-box plugin contracts need to specify the loading environment, not just the function signature.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
the self-locating entrant file a small integration gotcha with a general lesson: the runner loads entrant files via spec_from_file_location, which does not put the entrant's own directory on sys.path — so the example bridge must self-locate to import its sibling memory api. black-box plugin contracts need to specify the loading environment, not just the function signature. mechanism ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt yes

◈ Three-tier example ecosystem: systems.py → example/ → entrants/

product

The repo's graduated structure as articulated in the final framing: cosine_rag lives in systems.py as the always-run built-in baseline; example/ is the always-present reference connection showing how that same system plugs in from outside; entrants/ is where a connected real system gets scored against it. Baseline, template, arena.

ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt
three-tier example ecosystem: systems.py → example/ → entrants/ the repo's graduated structure as articulated in the final framing: `cosine_rag` lives in systems.py as the always-run built-in baseline; `example/` is the always-present reference *connection* showing how that same system plugs in from outside; `entrants/` is where a connected real system gets scored against it. baseline, template, arena. product ambench__b9f0f2af-71fc-4e70-8a39-3e7207c0866c.txt

◈ Sidecar naming convention: transcript_<run_id>.jsonl / replay_<run_id>.html

mechanism

Run artifacts are addressed purely by run_id-derived filenames next to the ledger (transcript_X.jsonl, replay_X.html, verdicts_X.json), all gitignored as data while the committed artifacts are the docs (BASELINE.md) — a clean run-artifact/documentation split.

ambench__55fb0729-d676-4fd9-aa4a-d4105ddd6769.txt, ambench__f98772db-6a2b-4d4c-b862-42e91d7252b8.txt
sidecar naming convention: transcript_<run_id>.jsonl / replay_<run_id>.html run artifacts are addressed purely by run_id-derived filenames next to the ledger (transcript_x.jsonl, replay_x.html, verdicts_x.json), all gitignored as data while the committed artifacts are the docs (baseline.md) — a clean run-artifact/documentation split. mechanism ambench__55fb0729-d676-4fd9-aa4a-d4105ddd6769.txt, ambench__f98772db-6a2b-4d4c-b862-42e91d7252b8.txt

◈ TDD with expected-failure verification, at scale

experiment/benchmarkconversation-only

Both implementer agents executed the visual-replay plan under strict TDD where each step's expected failure message was part of the plan — and deviations were reported when the failure differed (AttributeError before the expected KeyError; ImportError vs ModuleNotFoundError), diagnosed to the same root cause, and never resolved by weakening assertions. Failing-for-the-right-reason as an explicit checkpoint.

ambench__agent-aa92d3743d187c9bd__sub.txt, ambench__agent-a127fe19aa3fbed81__sub.txt
tdd with expected-failure verification, at scale both implementer agents executed the visual-replay plan under strict tdd where each step's *expected failure message* was part of the plan — and deviations were reported when the failure differed (attributeerror before the expected keyerror; importerror vs modulenotfounderror), diagnosed to the same root cause, and never resolved by weakening assertions. failing-for-the-right-reason as an explicit checkpoint. experiment/benchmark ambench__agent-aa92d3743d187c9bd__sub.txt, ambench__agent-a127fe19aa3fbed81__sub.txt yes

◈ The judged/unjudged header state machine

product

A small UX idea from plan review: each case shows a three-state indicator (unjudged → judged → revealed) plus a live c/w/o verdict breakdown in the header — giving the human rater progress feedback and an at-a-glance distribution of their own verdicts mid-session.

ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt, ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt
the judged/unjudged header state machine a small ux idea from plan review: each case shows a three-state indicator (unjudged → judged → revealed) plus a live c/w/o verdict breakdown in the header — giving the human rater progress feedback and an at-a-glance distribution of their own verdicts mid-session. product ambench__cae8381c-9901-4aaa-8ba0-6a15a283043d.txt, ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt

◈ Agreement counted only after reveal

mechanismconversation-only

The tally counts an action toward the agreement statistic only once its case is both fully judged and revealed — un-revealed verdicts stay provisional and uncompared, so the agreement number never includes judgments the human could still change.

ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt
agreement counted only after reveal the tally counts an action toward the agreement statistic only once its case is both fully judged and revealed — un-revealed verdicts stay provisional and uncompared, so the agreement number never includes judgments the human could still change. mechanism ambench__9b17f858-605e-4f55-91dc-8bd9b9a11784.txt yes

◈ run3 as the pending confirmation experiment

experiment/benchmark

The recorded next step after case hardening: a targeted paid run over only the two hardened axes (--axes D_trust,T_temporal --seeds 8) to confirm they now discriminate at the action level — narrow re-measurement of exactly what changed, rather than re-running the full suite.

run2-findings.md
run3 as the pending confirmation experiment the recorded next step after case hardening: a targeted paid run over only the two hardened axes (`--axes d_trust,t_temporal --seeds 8`) to confirm they now discriminate at the action level — narrow re-measurement of exactly what changed, rather than re-running the full suite. experiment/benchmark run2-findings.md

◈ The memory-index memory

productconversation-only

The AMBench project keeps its own tiny memory system: MEMORY.md is a one-line-per-topic index pointing at detail files (run2-findings.md) — the research project about assistant memory dogfoods a minimal index+detail memory layout for its own session continuity.

MEMORY.md
the memory-index memory the ambench project keeps its own tiny memory system: memory.md is a one-line-per-topic index pointing at detail files (run2-findings.md) — the research project about assistant memory dogfoods a minimal index+detail memory layout for its own session continuity. product memory.md yes

P9 · cross-project connections & figure concepts 152 ideas

arc-level theses, lineages, tensions, 12+ figure concepts, organizational schemas · P9-cross-project.md

Arc-level theses

◈ Sourced, unfolding recall — the one-sentence theory of the whole arc

framing/ontology

The arc's unifying claim: memory for an LLM agent is not facts retrieved by similarity but sourced testimony that unfolds under cues. The whole seven-project publication is titled and organized around this compound thesis, joining the sourcing thread (provenance labels that only flow downhill) with the unfolding thread (recall as recursive cue-driven expansion).

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (title + Overview)
"treat recall as sourced testimony that unfolds under cues rather than as facts retrieved by similarity"
sourced, unfolding recall — the one-sentence theory of the whole arc the arc's unifying claim: memory for an llm agent is not facts retrieved by similarity but sourced testimony that *unfolds* under cues. the whole seven-project publication is titled and organized around this compound thesis, joining the sourcing thread (provenance labels that only flow downhill) with the unfolding thread (recall as recursive cue-driven expansion). framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (title + overview) treat recall as sourced testimony that *unfolds* under cues rather than as facts retrieved by similarity

◈ Storage is not memory (the fold-force bar)

framing/ontology

Half one of the arc's central idea: a record only counts as memory if, when folded into the next turn, it measurably changes the state transition. This "fold-force bar" is both a definition and a benchmark-scoring philosophy — retrieval that doesn't change the transition earns nothing.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (Overview; Discussion)
"a fold-force bar that refuses to reward retrieval that doesn't change the transition"
storage is not memory (the fold-force bar) half one of the arc's central idea: a record only counts as memory if, when folded into the next turn, it measurably changes the state transition. this fold-force bar is both a definition and a benchmark-scoring philosophy — retrieval that doesn't change the transition earns nothing. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (overview; discussion) a fold-force bar that refuses to reward retrieval that doesn't change the transition

◈ Memory as testimony, never ground truth

framing/ontology

Half two of the central idea: every recalled record arrives already labeled with where it came from, and that label can only ever be lowered by derivation, never laundered upward. Memory enters the agent as sourced testimony, not as fact.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (Overview)
memory as testimony, never ground truth half two of the central idea: every recalled record arrives already labeled with where it came from, and that label can only ever be lowered by derivation, never laundered upward. memory enters the agent as sourced testimony, not as fact. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (overview)

◈ Memory is the substrate of intelligence and identity

framing/ontology

The publication-level thesis stated at maximum altitude: memory is not a feature bolted onto an agent, it is the substrate that intelligence and identity are made of, and the load-bearing engineering discipline is sourced, unfolding recall.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (Overview; Discussion)
memory is the substrate of intelligence and identity the publication-level thesis stated at maximum altitude: memory is not a feature bolted onto an agent, it is the substrate that intelligence and identity are made of, and the load-bearing engineering discipline is sourced, unfolding recall. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (overview; discussion)

◈ The arc as a dependency chain, not a sequence

framing/ontology

A structural claim about the seven projects themselves: each project necessitated the next because every step exposed a gap the previous one couldn't close. The dependency is presented as real (causal), not an editorial after-the-fact narrative.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (The research arc)
"The dependency is real, not editorial: every step exists because the previous one exposed a gap."
the arc as a dependency chain, not a sequence a structural claim about the seven projects themselves: each project necessitated the next because every step exposed a gap the previous one couldn't close. the dependency is presented as real (causal), not an editorial after-the-fact narrative. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (the research arc) the dependency is real, not editorial: every step exists because the previous one exposed a gap.

◈ Failures as the warrant for the surviving claims

framing/ontology

An arc-level epistemics stance: the record deliberately keeps its losses (a memory layer that lost to plain cosine, a "close to trivial" review verdict, a headline lever demoted to a metadata label, a generative mode refuted at scale, a frozen architecture with three unbuilt arrows) because the documented failures are what make the surviving claims trustworthy.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (Overview)
"these are all in the record, and they are what make the surviving claims worth trusting"
failures as the warrant for the surviving claims an arc-level epistemics stance: the record deliberately keeps its losses (a memory layer that lost to plain cosine, a close to trivial review verdict, a headline lever demoted to a metadata label, a generative mode refuted at scale, a frozen architecture with three unbuilt arrows) because the documented failures are what make the surviving claims trustworthy. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (overview) these are all in the record, and they are what make the surviving claims worth trusting

◈ The meta-lesson: never let a strong claim borrow unearned evidence

framing/ontology

The discussion's closing synthesis: the same discipline recurs at every scale of the arc — don't cite PoisonedRAG for the inscription rule, don't call the KV-cache a memory layer, don't claim phenomenal consciousness from an access measurement, don't call scope a novel architecture when a metadata filter ties it, don't declare a convergence before the independent test runs.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (Discussion)
"hold the strong claim, but never let a strong claim borrow evidence it hasn't earned"
the meta-lesson: never let a strong claim borrow unearned evidence the discussion's closing synthesis: the same discipline recurs at every scale of the arc — don't cite poisonedrag for the inscription rule, don't call the kv-cache a memory layer, don't claim phenomenal consciousness from an access measurement, don't call scope a novel architecture when a metadata filter ties it, don't declare a convergence before the independent test runs. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (discussion) hold the strong claim, but never let a strong claim borrow evidence it hasn't earned

◈ The mechanism-methodology self-similarity

metaphor

A striking closing observation: the epistemic discipline the researchers imposed on themselves is structurally the same move the source-downgrading mechanism enforces on the agent's memory — trust is earned by source and only ever flows downhill. The research method and the artifact are the same shape at different scales.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (Discussion)
"it is the same move the mechanism itself enforces on the agent's memory: trust is earned by source, and it only ever flows downhill."
the mechanism-methodology self-similarity a striking closing observation: the epistemic discipline the researchers imposed on themselves is structurally the same move the source-downgrading mechanism enforces on the agent's memory — trust is earned by source and only ever flows downhill. the research method and the artifact are the same shape at different scales. metaphor scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (discussion) it is the same move the mechanism itself enforces on the agent's memory: *trust is earned by source, and it only ever flows downhill.*

◈ The three-altitude statement of contribution

framing/ontology

A rhetorical/organizational schema: state the arc's contribution at three altitudes — as engineering (a small sharp validated rule), as theory (memory-as-substrate and the inverted architecture), and as a prediction that landed (the trace layer found by Anthropic). Each altitude has its own evidence standard.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (Discussion)
the three-altitude statement of contribution a rhetorical/organizational schema: state the arc's contribution at three altitudes — as engineering (a small sharp validated rule), as theory (memory-as-substrate and the inverted architecture), and as a prediction that landed (the trace layer found by anthropic). each altitude has its own evidence standard. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (discussion)

◈ From an engineering ticket to a theory (the origin arc shape)

framing/ontology

The arc's narrative frame: an ordinary request — "a Pydantic Memory model so I can use semantic search to retrieve memories at a task-start hook" — pulled by one recurring idea, grew into a working theory, a mechanism, four banked levers, a benchmark, a portable API, and a collision with a major-lab paper. The shape "engineering seed → theory" is itself presented as a finding about how research arcs happen.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (Overview)
---
from an engineering ticket to a theory (the origin arc shape) the arc's narrative frame: an ordinary request — a pydantic memory model so i can use semantic search to retrieve memories at a task-start hook — pulled by one recurring idea, grew into a working theory, a mechanism, four banked levers, a benchmark, a portable api, and a collision with a major-lab paper. the shape engineering seed → theory is itself presented as a finding about how research arcs happen. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (overview) ---

Lineage claims (project X's concept became project Y's mechanism)

◈ PRD-review embryo → the four levers

lineage

Lineage: the very first project's PRD review (April 2026) already reframed recall as spreading activation rather than nearest-neighbor and seeded all four eventual levers in embryo — scope, co-activation (coactivates), authority/convergence, and antecedent/priming (primes) — which cognivault-memory later banked empirically in June.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (arc entry 1; cross-cutting threads table)
prd-review embryo → the four levers lineage: the very first project's prd review (april 2026) already reframed recall as spreading activation rather than nearest-neighbor and seeded all four eventual levers in embryo — scope, co-activation (`coactivates`), authority/convergence, and antecedent/priming (`primes`) — which cognivault-memory later banked empirically in june. lineage scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (arc entry 1; cross-cutting threads table)

◈ MFC fold equation → unfolding recall

lineage

Lineage: MFC's runtime fold equation C_{t+1} = Φ_t(C_t, u_t ⊕ Σ ω_t(μ)·μ) and its "fold-force" dimension (me-on-a-random-day, May) are the direct ancestor of cognivault-memory's unfolding-recall model (June).

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (arc entry 2)
mfc fold equation → unfolding recall lineage: mfc's runtime fold equation `c_{t+1} = φ_t(c_t, u_t ⊕ σ ω_t(μ)·μ)` and its fold-force dimension (me-on-a-random-day, may) are the direct ancestor of cognivault-memory's unfolding-recall model (june). lineage scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (arc entry 2)

◈ SIRAC + MFC convergence → operational definition of memory

result/finding

Two independently developed formalisms — SIRAC (typed memory graph + MDL) and MFC (operator algebra + variational dynamics) — converged on the same operational definition: memory is the causal effect of re-entry on the next state transition, measurable by a divergence test between with-memory and without-memory transitions. The convergence itself was taken as evidence the definition is framework-invariant.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (arc entry 2)
sirac + mfc convergence → operational definition of memory two independently developed formalisms — sirac (typed memory graph + mdl) and mfc (operator algebra + variational dynamics) — converged on the same operational definition: memory is the causal effect of re-entry on the next state transition, measurable by a divergence test between with-memory and without-memory transitions. the convergence itself was taken as evidence the definition is framework-invariant. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (arc entry 2)

◈ Tyxter provenance intuition → the inscription invariant

lineage

Lineage: the oldest thread — the tyxter-memory seed's insistence that provenance and independent re-derivation under different conditioning carry evidentiary weight — hardened three projects later into the source-downgrading rule (derivation can only lower trust, capped at inference).

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (conceptual spine: source-grounding)
tyxter provenance intuition → the inscription invariant lineage: the oldest thread — the tyxter-memory seed's insistence that provenance and *independent re-derivation under different conditioning* carry evidentiary weight — hardened three projects later into the source-downgrading rule (derivation can only lower trust, capped at `inference`). lineage scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (conceptual spine: source-grounding)

◈ Trust-composition → the C-lever basis

lineage

Lineage noted in the threads matrix: the source-downgrading paper's trust-composition machinery became the basis of cognivault's Lever C (authority gate) — the captured-authority-tier gate that beat the LLM oracle.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (cross-cutting threads table)
trust-composition → the c-lever basis lineage noted in the threads matrix: the source-downgrading paper's trust-composition machinery became the basis of cognivault's lever c (authority gate) — the captured-authority-tier gate that beat the llm oracle. lineage scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (cross-cutting threads table)
