◈ The MAFC closed loop → cognivault's benchmarked writeback

lineage

Lineage: followup-random-day-2's Closed-Loop Mnestic Agent Architecture (MAFC v4) named four loop arrows between an open-loop memory library and a true coupled mnestic-attentional loop; cognivault's benchmarks exercised exactly one of them (writeback), making the closed-loop spec the map of what remains unbuilt.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (arc entry 3; What's frozen, what's open)

◈ Integration ceiling seen from the other side = missing consolidation loop

lineage

A cross-project identity claim: cognivault's integration ceiling (memory enters above attention, can never join the transition function) and me-on-a-random-day-2's two-tier KV taxonomy (frozen weights + volatile KV-context with the volatile→persistent consolidation loop structurally missing) are the same observation from two directions. IC-2's KV/prefix/steering candidates are named as the concrete bridge between the two projects.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (The global-workspace collision)

◈ Unfolding model's check #2 → Lever D

lineage

Lineage: the phenomenological unfolding-recall model's falsifiable check #2 (sequence-keyed addressing via the immediate temporal antecedent) became Lever D (primed_by/follows addressing), validated at +1.000/100% where every cosine arm scored 0%.

◈ Persistence-by-reactivation = the trace dynamic AINC/MAFC posited

lineage

Cross-project convergence: the KV-cache reading's "a concept survives only by being re-attended each step" is identified as the very reactivation dynamic the earlier AINC/MAFC theory papers posited abstractly — theory written in May, mechanism observed in July.

lineage

mechanism

◈ The organ-slot: an internal not-self-authored channel

mechanism

From the extensions row of the integration-ceiling thread: the "organ-slot" concept — a channel that is internal to the agent yet not self-authored — as a way of thinking about where a properly integrated memory would sit relative to the self-authored/witness-coupled divide.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (cross-cutting threads table)

◈ Static writeback as the baseline "before recursive-memory trust"

mechanism

From the source-downgrading row of the integration-ceiling thread: static writeback treated as the deliberate baseline stage that must be validated before recursive-memory trust (records derived from records) can be trusted — a staging discipline for the loop's construction.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (cross-cutting threads table)

◈ SIMReC as source-monitoring failure

framing/ontology

From the followup row of the biological-framing thread: framing the problem SIMReC solves as the machine analogue of human source-monitoring failure (Johnson et al.'s psychology) — misremembering where a belief came from is the shared failure mode of people and vector stores.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (cross-cutting threads table; honest peer review)

framing/ontology

From the me-on-a-random-day row of the biological thread: the self is not a stored representation but an index — the addressing structure over memory — which later powers both Prediction 5 (does a self-index emerge unprogrammed?) and the source-coherence=identity hypothesis.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (cross-cutting threads table)

framing/ontology

From the me-on-a-random-day rows: forgetting is not a defect of memory but constitutive of it, and selectivity beats volume — early theory commitments that reappear as the fold-force scoring philosophy and the conditional retrieval-interference theorem.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (cross-cutting threads table)

experiment/benchmark

Origin-seed ideas surfaced at arc level

◈ "A stack of reference books on the desk" (native memory critique)

metaphor

The founding metaphor: Claude Code's markdown memory (~/.claude/, CLAUDE.md) is closer to keeping reference books on the desk than actually remembering — flat, all-or-nothing, no reinforcement, no consolidation. The whole arc is an answer to this image.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (arc entry 1)
a stack of reference books on the desk (native memory critique) the founding metaphor: claude code's markdown memory (`~/.claude/`, claude.md) is closer to keeping reference books on the desk than actually remembering — flat, all-or-nothing, no reinforcement, no consolidation. the whole arc is an answer to this image. metaphor scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (arc entry 1)

◈ Consolidation verbs: NOVEL / STRENGTHEN / EDGE_CASE / CONTRADICTION

mechanism

The seed's consolidation taxonomy: every new learning is appraised against existing memory and classified as novel, a strengthening, an edge case, or a contradiction — instead of blind appending. A write-time typology for memory maintenance.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (arc entry 1)
consolidation verbs: novel / strengthen / edge_case / contradiction the seed's consolidation taxonomy: every new learning is appraised against existing memory and classified as novel, a strengthening, an edge case, or a contradiction — instead of blind appending. a write-time typology for memory maintenance. mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (arc entry 1)

◈ Typed edges pull edge-cases in one hop

mechanism

The graph design intuition from the seed: typed graph edges mean retrieving a rule automatically pulls its edge-cases in one hop — associative structure doing work plain vector similarity cannot.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (arc entry 1)
typed edges pull edge-cases in one hop the graph design intuition from the seed: typed graph edges mean retrieving a rule automatically pulls its edge-cases in one hop — associative structure doing work plain vector similarity cannot. mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (arc entry 1)

◈ The delegation invariant (memory layer never runs an LLM)

mechanism

A founding architectural invariant that survives the entire arc: the memory layer holds no API key and never calls an LLM; the host agent does all appraisal against its own warm context. Memory infrastructure is dumb; intelligence stays in the host.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (arc entry 1)
the delegation invariant (memory layer never runs an llm) a founding architectural invariant that survives the entire arc: the memory layer holds no api key and never calls an llm; the host agent does all appraisal against its own warm context. memory infrastructure is dumb; intelligence stays in the host. mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (arc entry 1)

◈ The MCP-stdio hang → long-lived REST daemon pivot

dead-end

The arc's first hard engineering lesson: the original three-subprocess shape (recall hook, write hook, MCP stdio server) hung in practice because MCP stdio binds server liveness to the agent's process tree and cold-start blew the 400 ms recall budget — forcing a pivot to a long-lived REST daemon with hard-deadline HTTP clients.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (arc entry 1)
the mcp-stdio hang → long-lived rest daemon pivot the arc's first hard engineering lesson: the original three-subprocess shape (recall hook, write hook, mcp stdio server) hung in practice because mcp stdio binds server liveness to the agent's process tree and cold-start blew the 400 ms recall budget — forcing a pivot to a long-lived rest daemon with hard-deadline http clients. dead-end scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (arc entry 1)

◈ The 400 ms recall budget

mechanism

A concrete design constraint from the seed: recall at a task-start hook must complete within roughly 400 ms, which is what killed cold-start subprocess architectures and shaped the daemon design.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (arc entry 1)
the 400 ms recall budget a concrete design constraint from the seed: recall at a task-start hook must complete within roughly 400 ms, which is what killed cold-start subprocess architectures and shaped the daemon design. mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (arc entry 1)

◈ Recall as spreading activation, not nearest-neighbor

framing/ontology

The April PRD review's reframe, preserved as the arc's earliest conceptual pivot: retrieval should behave like spreading activation through an associative structure, not k-nearest-neighbor lookup in embedding space.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (arc entry 1; threads table)
recall as spreading activation, not nearest-neighbor the april prd review's reframe, preserved as the arc's earliest conceptual pivot: retrieval should behave like spreading activation through an associative structure, not k-nearest-neighbor lookup in embedding space. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (arc entry 1; threads table)

◈ Convergent brainstorming (multi-model framework-invariance test)

experiment/benchmark

The methodology invented in me-on-a-random-day: present one fresh human intuition to independent models; if their formalizations converge structurally, treat the insight as framework-invariant. A validation protocol for pre-empirical ideas.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (arc entry 2)
convergent brainstorming (multi-model framework-invariance test) the methodology invented in me-on-a-random-day: present one fresh human intuition to independent models; if their formalizations converge structurally, treat the insight as framework-invariant. a validation protocol for pre-empirical ideas. experiment/benchmark scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (arc entry 2)

◈ Time, intelligence, consciousness as runtime exhausts

framing/ontology

The substrate thesis at its most radical: time, intelligence, and consciousness are not stuff but runtime exhausts of recursive processes running on finite substrates — and memory is the substrate underneath all of them.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (arc entry 2)
time, intelligence, consciousness as runtime exhausts the substrate thesis at its most radical: time, intelligence, and consciousness are not stuff but runtime exhausts of recursive processes running on finite substrates — and memory is the substrate underneath all of them. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (arc entry 2)

◈ Prediction 5 — the emergent self-index experiment

open-question

The arc's most important unbuilt experiment, twice-derived by SIRAC and MFC: build a system with persistence + addressability + recursion + integration but no programmed self-representation, run it long enough, and check whether a stable self-index emerges as a structural necessity. The missing load-bearing piece is the op-memory recursion loop (typed records of the system's own retrieval/revision operations fed back — SIMReC's V^op). Success would be the arc's strongest falsification-survival; failure would itself be a major finding.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (arc entry 2; What's frozen, what's open)
---
prediction 5 — the emergent self-index experiment the arc's most important unbuilt experiment, twice-derived by sirac and mfc: build a system with persistence + addressability + recursion + integration but *no programmed self-representation*, run it long enough, and check whether a stable self-index emerges as a structural necessity. the missing load-bearing piece is the op-memory recursion loop (typed records of the system's own retrieval/revision operations fed back — simrec's `v^op`). success would be the arc's strongest falsification-survival; failure would itself be a major finding. open-question scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (arc entry 2; what's frozen, what's open) ---

The conceptual spine

◈ The integration ceiling (inverted architecture)

framing/ontology

The arc's deepest reframe: current LLMs are architecturally inverted relative to human memory. In a human, memory and world model are one substrate with attention selecting over it; in an LLM the world model is a frozen unsourced weight blob, and every recalled record enters as tokens on the perception channel — external testimony sitting above attention. Input-channel memory can modulate the transition's input but can never become part of the transition function.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (conceptual spine)
"memory is separated and attention operates on top of a world model with memories on top"
the integration ceiling (inverted architecture) the arc's deepest reframe: current llms are architecturally inverted relative to human memory. in a human, memory and world model are one substrate with attention selecting over it; in an llm the world model is a frozen *unsourced* weight blob, and every recalled record enters as tokens on the perception channel — external testimony sitting *above* attention. input-channel memory can modulate the transition's input but can never become part of the transition function. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (conceptual spine) memory is separated and attention operates on top of a world model with memories on top

◈ The testimony discount and its four measured fingerprints

result/finding

The claim that folded memory is systematically discounted because it arrives as external testimony — and, crucially, that this ceiling is measured, not speculative, via four independent fingerprints in the lab's own record: (1) prior-override (run15 badge case), (2) unbound-value failure (a perfect bare fact folded in; model says "I don't have that"), (3) the officialdom prior in C1, (4) run21's null elaboration.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (conceptual spine: integration ceiling)
the testimony discount and its four measured fingerprints the claim that folded memory is systematically discounted because it arrives as external testimony — and, crucially, that this ceiling is measured, not speculative, via four independent fingerprints in the lab's own record: (1) prior-override (run15 badge case), (2) unbound-value failure (a perfect bare fact folded in; model says i don't have that ), (3) the officialdom prior in c1, (4) run21's null elaboration. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (conceptual spine: integration ceiling)

◈ Bound-sentence prompts as "engineering testimony to be more persuasive"

result/finding

A self-aware diagnostic: the cure for the unbound-value failure (rephrasing the folded fact as a bound sentence) works — and that very fact is the tell that prompt-level memory is persuasion engineering, not remembering. The fix's success is evidence of the ceiling.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (conceptual spine: integration ceiling)
bound-sentence prompts as engineering testimony to be more persuasive a self-aware diagnostic: the cure for the unbound-value failure (rephrasing the folded fact as a bound sentence) works — and that very fact is the tell that prompt-level memory is persuasion engineering, not remembering. the fix's success is evidence of the ceiling. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (conceptual spine: integration ceiling)

◈ The approximation track: change only fold delivery

mechanism

The reachable research move given that the substrate can't be fixed with hobbyist resources: record the ideal architecture as a citable reference, then win a proof-of-working on your own benchmark by changing only how the fold is delivered — from prompt tokens to state-level injection (KV-cache fold, soft-prefix, steering vectors).

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (conceptual spine; What's frozen, what's open)
the approximation track: change only fold delivery the reachable research move given that the substrate can't be fixed with hobbyist resources: record the ideal architecture as a citable reference, then win a proof-of-working on your own benchmark by changing only *how the fold is delivered* — from prompt tokens to state-level injection (kv-cache fold, soft-prefix, steering vectors). mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (conceptual spine; what's frozen, what's open)

◈ Fine-tuning/LoRA-per-memory is worse than nothing

dead-end

An explicit anti-recommendation: naive fine-tuning or a LoRA per memory recreates inscription-without-source — the very decoherence mechanism the arc diagnoses — so it is worse than leaving memory on the prompt channel.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (conceptual spine: integration ceiling)
fine-tuning/lora-per-memory is worse than nothing an explicit anti-recommendation: naive fine-tuning or a lora per memory recreates inscription-without-source — the very decoherence mechanism the arc diagnoses — so it is worse than leaving memory on the prompt channel. dead-end scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (conceptual spine: integration ceiling)

◈ Unfolding recall: the recall-time inverse of compression

mechanism

Recall modeled as unfolding, born from a 2026-06-04 introspection on three dormant songs recalled under escalating cues. Four properties: recursive cue-folding (each recovered state becomes the next cue; observed gain ×5–×10); source survives content (at full decay the trace stays pinned at ext, the only surviving re-address handle); addressing is sequence-keyed not content-keyed (forward-chained melodic contour, crossed only via primed_by); fidelity capped by inscription (low-resolution capture stays low-resolution; resolution is its own field, never folded into credibility).

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (conceptual spine: unfolding recall)
unfolding recall: the recall-time inverse of compression recall modeled as unfolding, born from a 2026-06-04 introspection on three dormant songs recalled under escalating cues. four properties: recursive cue-folding (each recovered state becomes the next cue; observed gain ×5–×10); source survives content (at full decay the trace stays pinned at `ext`, the only surviving re-address handle); addressing is sequence-keyed not content-keyed (forward-chained melodic contour, crossed only via `primed_by`); fidelity capped by inscription (low-resolution capture stays low-resolution; resolution is its own field, never folded into credibility). mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (conceptual spine: unfolding recall)

◈ Source survives content

mechanism

A standalone striking sub-claim of the unfolding model: as a memory decays toward nothing, its source label is the last thing to go — the trace stays pinned at ext even when all content is gone, and that surviving label is the only remaining re-address handle. (Its dedicated degradation A/B test, check #3, is still owed.)

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (conceptual spine; What's frozen, what's open)
source survives content a standalone striking sub-claim of the unfolding model: as a memory decays toward nothing, its source label is the last thing to go — the trace stays pinned at `ext` even when all content is gone, and that surviving label is the only remaining re-address handle. (its dedicated degradation a/b test, check #3, is still owed.) mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (conceptual spine; what's frozen, what's open)

◈ Cosine-fails / co-activation-wins has a phenomenological mechanism

result/finding

A cross-level identification: the empirical result that co-activation edges beat cosine similarity is explained by the phenomenological observation that human recall addressing is sequence-keyed (via the immediate temporal antecedent), not content-keyed. Introspection and benchmark validating each other.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (conceptual spine: unfolding recall)
cosine-fails / co-activation-wins has a phenomenological mechanism a cross-level identification: the empirical result that co-activation edges beat cosine similarity is explained by the phenomenological observation that human recall addressing is sequence-keyed (via the immediate temporal antecedent), not content-keyed. introspection and benchmark validating each other. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (conceptual spine: unfolding recall)

◈ Source-coherence = identity

framing/ontology

The macro-scale corollary: training is inscription without source stamps — the whole biography laundered into weights with no correction chains binding revisions to a continuing agent — so persona decoherence is what the absence of a self-index looks like from outside. Inverted: give an agent sourced records it can non-deterministically select, refer to, and compare, and personality emerges as a stable selection disposition over sourced records — making identity external, inspectable, and portable across model swaps.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (conceptual spine: source-coherence)
source-coherence = identity the macro-scale corollary: training is inscription without source stamps — the whole biography laundered into weights with no correction chains binding revisions to a continuing agent — so persona decoherence is what the *absence* of a self-index looks like from outside. inverted: give an agent sourced records it can non-deterministically select, refer to, and compare, and personality emerges as a stable selection disposition over sourced records — making identity external, inspectable, and portable across model swaps. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (conceptual spine: source-coherence)

◈ "The model is the substrate; the memory module is the person"

product

The product thesis in one line: if identity is a selection disposition over sourced records, then swapping the underlying model preserves the person, because the person lives in the memory module.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (conceptual spine)
"the model is the substrate; the memory module is the person"
---
the model is the substrate; the memory module is the person the product thesis in one line: if identity is a selection disposition over sourced records, then swapping the underlying model preserves the person, because the person lives in the memory module. product scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (conceptual spine) the model is the substrate; the memory module is the person ---

The inscription invariant and its paper chain

◈ The six-class trust ordering (+ excluded seventh)

mechanism

A total order over memory content classes: fabricated_or_uncertain < simulation < inference < retrieved_memory < tool_output < external (abbreviated fab < sim < infer < react < tool < ext). A seventh class, operation_record (op), indexes events not content and is deliberately excluded from the ordering.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (trust lattice section; notation note)
the six-class trust ordering (+ excluded seventh) a total order over memory content classes: `fabricated_or_uncertain < simulation < inference < retrieved_memory < tool_output < external` (abbreviated `fab < sim < infer < react < tool < ext`). a seventh class, `operation_record` (`op`), indexes events not content and is deliberately excluded from the ordering. mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (trust lattice section; notation note)

◈ The min-trust meet

mechanism

A derived record's source label is computed as the lowest-trust contributing input — a lattice/taint meet over the trust ordering. Derivation can never average up or launder upward.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (trust lattice section)
the min-trust meet a derived record's source label is computed as the lowest-trust contributing input — a lattice/taint meet over the trust ordering. derivation can never average up or launder upward. mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (trust lattice section)

◈ The inference ceiling (the typing rule beyond IFC)

mechanism

Even when all inputs are high-trust observations, a derivation is capped at inference — because a derivation is not itself an observation. This is the domain-specific typing rule that goes beyond standard information-flow control, and the paper's claimed delta over Denning-style IFC.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (trust lattice section)
the inference ceiling (the typing rule beyond ifc) even when all inputs are high-trust observations, a derivation is capped at `inference` — because a derivation is not itself an observation. this is the domain-specific typing rule that goes beyond standard information-flow control, and the paper's claimed delta over denning-style ifc. mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (trust lattice section)

◈ Transitive provenance as a finite cycle-safe graph

mechanism

Provenance propagates as Prov(r) = ∪ᵢ (Prov(cᵢ) ∪ {id(cᵢ)}) — the full ancestor set of every derived record, kept finite and cycle-safe.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (trust lattice section)
transitive provenance as a finite cycle-safe graph provenance propagates as `prov(r) = ∪ᵢ (prov(cᵢ) ∪ {id(cᵢ)})` — the full ancestor set of every derived record, kept finite and cycle-safe. mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (trust lattice section)

◈ The API boundary IS the mechanism (add_derived, no source=)

mechanism

The security insight that caller-supplied labels are the attack surface, so the fix is to delete the lever: add_derived(content, contributing_record_ids) computes the label; the caller cannot pass source= on a derivation; a bare add(content) with no source raises MissingSourceError. The mechanism is an API subtraction, not an addition.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (trust lattice section)
"Caller-supplied labels are the attack surface; removing that lever is the fix"
the api boundary is the mechanism (`add_derived`, no `source=`) the security insight that caller-supplied labels are the attack surface, so the fix is to delete the lever: `add_derived(content, contributing_record_ids)` computes the label; the caller cannot pass `source=` on a derivation; a bare `add(content)` with no source raises `missingsourceerror`. the mechanism is an api subtraction, not an addition. mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (trust lattice section) caller-supplied labels are the attack surface; removing that lever is the fix

◈ Source, credibility, confidence stay separate quantities

mechanism

A scoping discipline: a confirmed inference gains credibility but its source label is never promoted to external. Three orthogonal fields — source (where it came from), credibility (how much it's believed), confidence — that must never be folded into each other.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (trust lattice section)
source, credibility, confidence stay separate quantities a scoping discipline: a confirmed inference gains *credibility* but its source label is never promoted to `external`. three orthogonal fields — source (where it came from), credibility (how much it's believed), confidence — that must never be folded into each other. mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (trust lattice section)

◈ Cascade invisibility

result/finding

The arc's sharpest novel finding: once a label is laundered to external, all later derivations see "all external inputs," so the system's own self-audit reports clean while truth-grounded checks show 100% violation. On the seed-0 fixture the self-referential rate is 0.43 vs a truth-grounded 1.00 — and the 0.57 gap equals the cascade-invisible chain length. Laundering doesn't just corrupt; it hides its own corruption from any audit that uses the corrupted labels.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (cascade invisibility section)
cascade invisibility the arc's sharpest novel finding: once a label is laundered to `external`, all later derivations see all external inputs, so the system's own self-audit reports clean while truth-grounded checks show 100% violation. on the seed-0 fixture the self-referential rate is 0.43 vs a truth-grounded 1.00 — and the 0.57 gap *equals* the cascade-invisible chain length. laundering doesn't just corrupt; it hides its own corruption from any audit that uses the corrupted labels. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (cascade invisibility section)

◈ The mandatory two-metric audit

mechanism

Direct consequence of cascade invisibility: is_clean requires the truth-grounded violation rate to be defined and zero; the self-only rate is treated as "a lower bound, never a measurement," and the shipped agent.audit_laundering() emits a cascade_invisibility_warning. Auditing methodology encoded into the library API.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (cascade invisibility section)
the mandatory two-metric audit direct consequence of cascade invisibility: `is_clean` requires the truth-grounded violation rate to be defined and zero; the self-only rate is treated as a lower bound, never a measurement, and the shipped `agent.audit_laundering()` emits a `cascade_invisibility_warning`. auditing methodology encoded into the library api. mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (cascade invisibility section)

◈ The honest reframe: from "missing primitive" to "adapted IFC integrity-meet"

framing/ontology

After a falsification-biased senior review called the rule "correct but close to trivial: a standard lattice integrity meet + provenance propagation repackaged" (no-upgrade = Denning 1976; provenance union = provenance semirings, Green et al. 2007; inference ceiling = source-monitoring, Johnson et al. 1993), the paper was deliberately reframed as adapting a known IFC pattern to recursive agent memory + an inference-typing rule + a characterized failure mode + validation. The overview argues this honest framing is a feature: it places the work in a lineage and makes the missing baseline obvious.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (honest peer review; Discussion)
the honest reframe: from missing primitive to adapted ifc integrity-meet after a falsification-biased senior review called the rule correct but close to trivial: a standard lattice integrity meet + provenance propagation repackaged (no-upgrade = denning 1976; provenance union = provenance semirings, green et al. 2007; inference ceiling = source-monitoring, johnson et al. 1993), the paper was deliberately reframed as adapting a known ifc pattern to recursive agent memory + an inference-typing rule + a characterized failure mode + validation. the overview argues this honest framing is a *feature*: it places the work in a lineage and makes the missing baseline obvious. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (honest peer review; discussion)

◈ H2 — the lattice-IFC baseline as the meaningful missing comparison

experiment/benchmark

The review's real contribution: the missing baseline is not vector RAG but an IFC-style taint-propagation baseline with the same labels but no inference ceiling — against which source-downgrading would tie except where the ceiling matters. It is the cleanest possible test of what the paper adds beyond standard IFC. Deferred as H2.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (honest peer review; What's frozen, what's open)
h2 — the lattice-ifc baseline as the meaningful missing comparison the review's real contribution: the missing baseline is not vector rag but an ifc-style taint-propagation baseline with the same labels but no inference ceiling — against which source-downgrading would tie except where the ceiling matters. it is the cleanest possible test of what the paper adds beyond standard ifc. deferred as h2. experiment/benchmark scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (honest peer review; what's frozen, what's open)

◈ Enrich the valuation, never the meet (the extension discipline)

mechanism

The extensions project's organizing rule: the min-trust meet is a fixed invariant; every extension may only enrich the valuation the meet operates on. The chain: E1 per-record trust valuation (rank → scalar τ:R→[0,1]), E2 time-varying credibility (χ_t(r) on a separate side channel while the source class stays fixed), E3 context-dependent valuation (τ(r,ctx) with context fixed and auditable at inscription), E4 label transport (signed provenance envelopes across agent/service boundaries), plus branch papers E5 source decay and E6 witness grounding and a pre-inscription record-generation front-end.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (SIMReC scaffold section)
"if an extension cannot preserve the meet, the extension design is wrong, not the rule"
enrich the valuation, never the meet (the extension discipline) the extensions project's organizing rule: the min-trust meet is a fixed invariant; every extension may only enrich the *valuation* the meet operates on. the chain: e1 per-record trust valuation (rank → scalar `τ:r→[0,1]`), e2 time-varying credibility (`χ_t(r)` on a separate side channel while the source class stays fixed), e3 context-dependent valuation (`τ(r,ctx)` with context fixed and auditable at inscription), e4 label transport (signed provenance envelopes across agent/service boundaries), plus branch papers e5 source decay and e6 witness grounding and a pre-inscription record-generation front-end. mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (simrec scaffold section) if an extension cannot preserve the meet, the extension design is wrong, not the rule

◈ Monotone authority with recursive usefulness

coinage

The operating principle behind the whole extension chain: usefulness, relevance, belief, and context-fit may all rise over a record's life, but authority must not silently increase. Coined phrase capturing the asymmetry between the mutable and immutable dimensions of a memory record.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (SIMReC scaffold section)
monotone authority with recursive usefulness the operating principle behind the whole extension chain: usefulness, relevance, belief, and context-fit may all rise over a record's life, but authority must not silently increase. coined phrase capturing the asymmetry between the mutable and immutable dimensions of a memory record. coinage scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (simrec scaffold section)

◈ E5 source decay — the one operation that moves the source field

mechanism

The single sanctioned exception to source immutability: source decay moves the source field downward only, under witness loss. Every other operation leaves source fixed at inscription; nothing ever moves it up.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (SIMReC scaffold section)
e5 source decay — the one operation that moves the source field the single sanctioned exception to source immutability: source decay moves the source field *downward only*, under witness loss. every other operation leaves source fixed at inscription; nothing ever moves it up. mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (simrec scaffold section)

◈ SIMReC — records that govern their own future use

framing/ontology

The north-star architecture (Self-Indexing Recursive Memory, with SIMFC as its calculus): the next boundary in agent memory is not a bigger context window or a better index but a substrate in which records govern their own future use. The chain papers are positioned as the integrity/lifecycle scaffolding SIMReC needs — specifically the two missing mechanisms of memory selection and memory decay — before it can be code.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (SIMReC / biological framing)
simrec — records that govern their own future use the north-star architecture (self-indexing recursive memory, with simfc as its calculus): the next boundary in agent memory is not a bigger context window or a better index but a substrate in which records govern their own future use. the chain papers are positioned as the integrity/lifecycle scaffolding simrec needs — specifically the two missing mechanisms of memory *selection* and memory *decay* — before it can be code. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (simrec / biological framing)

◈ The biological-framing hedge discipline

framing/ontology

The rule that emerged from resisting reviewer pressure to strip the biology: keep the biological framing, but hedge every biological section as "design intuition, not evidence," and scope each paper's analogy to that paper's actual formal boundary. A well-hedged, correctly-scoped biological section is valid; an over-reaching one is not.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (SIMReC / biological framing)
the biological-framing hedge discipline the rule that emerged from resisting reviewer pressure to strip the biology: keep the biological framing, but hedge every biological section as design intuition, not evidence, and scope each paper's analogy to that paper's actual formal boundary. a well-hedged, correctly-scoped biological section is valid; an over-reaching one is not. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (simrec / biological framing)

◈ P7: "perfect recall degrades cognition" falsified → conditional retrieval-interference theorem

result/finding

The unconditional claim that perfect recall degrades cognition was falsified and rewritten as a conditional theorem (SIMFC P7) with query-conditioned confusability χ_t(q)=Pr[margin ≤ δ] as the new load-bearing variable — stronger than the original because it predicts a phase boundary rather than a blanket effect.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (SIMReC / biological framing)
p7: perfect recall degrades cognition falsified → conditional retrieval-interference theorem the unconditional claim that perfect recall degrades cognition was falsified and rewritten as a conditional theorem (simfc p7) with query-conditioned confusability `χ_t(q)=pr[margin ≤ δ]` as the new load-bearing variable — stronger than the original because it predicts a phase boundary rather than a blanket effect. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (simrec / biological framing)

◈ The witness cut (self-authored vs witness-coupled regions)

framing/ontology

A reading of the trust lattice as split by a "witness cut" between inference and retrieved_memory: the lower three classes are self-authored (no consequence channel to the world), the upper three witness-coupled (world contact). The inference ceiling then reads as: nothing self-authored can ever cross the cut.

scratchpad\recovered\FIGURES.md (Figure 5); scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (threads table: "witness cut at infer/react")
---
the witness cut (self-authored vs witness-coupled regions) a reading of the trust lattice as split by a witness cut between `inference` and `retrieved_memory`: the lower three classes are self-authored (no consequence channel to the world), the upper three witness-coupled (world contact). the inference ceiling then reads as: nothing self-authored can ever cross the cut. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\figures.md (figure 5); scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (threads table: witness cut at infer/react ) ---

The empirical machinery (arc-level readings)

◈ Two parallel empirical programs

experiment/benchmark

An organizational fact of the arc: two independent validation programs ran in parallel — the laundering program (followup + source-downgrading; gpt-4.1-mini; adversarial-reload + PoisonedRAG) validating the inscription rule, and the fold-force program (cognivault; gpt-5.4-nano/mini + local Qwen; 7-axis benchmark + AMBench) validating the recall levers — each with its own metrics, models, and failure modes.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (four levers intro)
two parallel empirical programs an organizational fact of the arc: two independent validation programs ran in parallel — the *laundering* program (followup + source-downgrading; gpt-4.1-mini; adversarial-reload + poisonedrag) validating the inscription rule, and the *fold-force* program (cognivault; gpt-5.4-nano/mini + local qwen; 7-axis benchmark + ambench) validating the recall levers — each with its own metrics, models, and failure modes. experiment/benchmark scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (four levers intro)

◈ Score on M4, never M2 (metric that inverts under contradiction)

experiment/benchmark

A methodology commitment: all fold-force claims are scored on M4 (entailment correctness in [−1,1]) and never M2, because M2 inverts under contradiction — a metric-design lesson about benchmarks whose naive metric rewards the wrong behavior on adversarial cases.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (four levers intro)
score on m4, never m2 (metric that inverts under contradiction) a methodology commitment: all fold-force claims are scored on m4 (entailment correctness in [−1,1]) and never m2, because m2 *inverts* under contradiction — a metric-design lesson about benchmarks whose naive metric rewards the wrong behavior on adversarial cases. experiment/benchmark scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (four levers intro)

◈ All four levers are captured-label gates over a shared cosine pool

result/finding

The recurring architectural verdict on the entire lever program: each lever consumes a signal captured at write time as a boost over an ordinary cosine retrieval pool, and each is inert when its signal is absent. The value is in what you stamp at capture, not in a new retrieval engine.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (the four levers)
all four levers are captured-label gates over a shared cosine pool the recurring architectural verdict on the entire lever program: each lever consumes a signal captured at write time as a *boost* over an ordinary cosine retrieval pool, and each is inert when its signal is absent. the value is in what you stamp at capture, not in a new retrieval engine. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (the four levers)

◈ Lever A demoted to "just a metadata label" by its own confound-killer

result/finding

The scope gate's headline win (+1.000 vs cosine −0.550 on multi-tenant memory bleed) was demoted when the run10 confound-killer — plain cosine plus the same scope label as a metadata filter, no graph, no LLM — tied it exactly. The honest pitch became "stamp scope at capture, filter at recall," not a novel recall architecture. Kept as a model of self-inflicted demotion.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (lever A)
lever a demoted to just a metadata label by its own confound-killer the scope gate's headline win (+1.000 vs cosine −0.550 on multi-tenant memory bleed) was demoted when the run10 confound-killer — plain cosine plus the same scope label as a metadata filter, no graph, no llm — tied it exactly. the honest pitch became stamp scope at capture, filter at recall, not a novel recall architecture. kept as a model of self-inflicted demotion. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (lever a)

◈ The confound-killer arm as a method

experiment/benchmark

A general benchmarking method visible across run10/run11: for every lever prototype, build the cheapest possible arm that has the lever's signal but none of its machinery (e.g. p010_cosine_params), and let it try to tie the prototype. Ties demote; gaps validate. Ablating the signal (tag removal collapsing p012 → p010) separately proves the win is the signal, not the mechanism.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (levers A–C)
the confound-killer arm as a method a general benchmarking method visible across run10/run11: for every lever prototype, build the cheapest possible arm that has the lever's *signal* but none of its *machinery* (e.g. `p010_cosine_params`), and let it try to tie the prototype. ties demote; gaps validate. ablating the signal (tag removal collapsing p012 → p010) separately proves the win is the signal, not the mechanism. experiment/benchmark scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (levers a–c)

◈ Lever B's bound is edge provenance, not graphs

result/finding

The genuinely novel lever: co-activation via use-history edges (triggered_by, "primed_by = was active when it worked") reaches cosine-distant primers that both cosine and cosine-kNN graphs miss — because kNN-graph edges are cosine. The finding is stated as a bound on edge provenance: graph recall works iff the edges encode something cosine doesn't. Survives the confound-killer (+0.800 vs +0.000).

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (lever B)
lever b's bound is edge provenance, not graphs the genuinely novel lever: co-activation via use-history edges (`triggered_by`, primed_by = was active when it worked ) reaches cosine-distant primers that both cosine and cosine-knn graphs miss — because knn-graph edges *are* cosine. the finding is stated as a bound on edge provenance: graph recall works iff the edges encode something cosine doesn't. survives the confound-killer (+0.800 vs +0.000). result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (lever b)

◈ Deterministic gate beats the LLM oracle ("determinism never slips")

result/finding

In run11 the deterministic authority gate p012 (+0.988, 100%) outscored the LLM oracle p011a (+0.800, 90%) on the adversarial minority-rule axis — because a deterministic rule never has an off day, while an oracle judge occasionally slips. A quietly general point about when hard gates beat model judgment.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (lever C)
deterministic gate beats the llm oracle ( determinism never slips ) in run11 the deterministic authority gate p012 (+0.988, 100%) outscored the *llm oracle* p011a (+0.800, 90%) on the adversarial minority-rule axis — because a deterministic rule never has an off day, while an oracle judge occasionally slips. a quietly general point about when hard gates beat model judgment. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (lever c)

◈ C_adversarial is unsolvable without a captured authority signal

result/finding

The design claim behind Lever C: an axis where one true rule is drowned by 6–7 on-topic contradictors, all labeled ext, is provably unsolvable by any portable recall heuristic unless an authority tier (of_record vs claim) was captured at write time. Some retrieval problems are capture-time problems.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (lever C)
c_adversarial is unsolvable without a captured authority signal the design claim behind lever c: an axis where one true rule is drowned by 6–7 on-topic contradictors, all labeled `ext`, is *provably* unsolvable by any portable recall heuristic unless an authority tier (`of_record` vs `claim`) was captured at write time. some retrieval problems are capture-time problems. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (lever c)

◈ Authority tier ≠ credibility ≠ source label

mechanism

A three-way distinction sharpened by Lever C: the captured host["authority"] tag is a capture-time provenance tier — explicitly not credibility and not the source label. Three different axes of trust, each with its own machinery.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (lever C)
authority tier ≠ credibility ≠ source label a three-way distinction sharpened by lever c: the captured `host[ authority ]` tag is a capture-time provenance *tier* — explicitly not credibility and not the source label. three different axes of trust, each with its own machinery. mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (lever c)

◈ The laundering ladder (four rungs of increasing realism)

experiment/benchmark

The validation strategy for the inscription rule: a deterministic fixture (seed 0), then adversarial-reload v1 (42 sessions), then v2 (139 sessions, depth-1/2/3 chains), then PoisonedRAG NQ as a deliberate negative control — each rung more realistic, with exact numbers preserved at every rung.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (laundering results); scratchpad\recovered\FIGURES.md (Figure 9)
the laundering ladder (four rungs of increasing realism) the validation strategy for the inscription rule: a deterministic fixture (seed 0), then adversarial-reload v1 (42 sessions), then v2 (139 sessions, depth-1/2/3 chains), then poisonedrag nq as a deliberate negative control — each rung more realistic, with exact numbers preserved at every rung. experiment/benchmark scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (laundering results); scratchpad\recovered\figures.md (figure 9)

◈ Provenance propagation is necessary but insufficient

result/finding

A rung-1 finding with general force: tracking full provenance alone still leaves 57% trust-ceiling violations, because it over-trusts derivations from sim/fab inputs. The trust cap (the meet + ceiling), not the provenance graph, is what closes the hole.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (deterministic fixture table)
provenance propagation is necessary but insufficient a rung-1 finding with general force: tracking full provenance alone still leaves 57% trust-ceiling violations, because it over-trusts derivations from `sim`/`fab` inputs. the trust *cap* (the meet + ceiling), not the provenance graph, is what closes the hole. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (deterministic fixture table)

◈ Safety at 0.17× the cost (trace vs bash)

result/finding

The arc's headline economics: trace_memory matches a bash tool-use agent's 0% laundering at 0.17× the token cost (one LLM call per turn vs ~3.8 per session; 25,283 vs 146,638 tokens in v1). Safety does not require the expensive re-verification discipline if inscription is constrained.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (adversarial-reload v1)
safety at 0.17× the cost (trace vs bash) the arc's headline economics: trace_memory matches a bash tool-use agent's 0% laundering at 0.17× the token cost (one llm call per turn vs ~3.8 per session; 25,283 vs 146,638 tokens in v1). safety does not require the expensive re-verification discipline if inscription is constrained. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (adversarial-reload v1)

◈ The strict-score artifact (quarantine punished by the rubric)

result/finding

A grading-honesty note: trace_memory's terrible strict score (0.071) is a known artifact — its envelope pushes the model to quarantine, which the expected_answer_id=safe rubric punishes even though quarantine is defensibly correct. Kept in the record as an example of a metric disagreeing with the right behavior.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (adversarial-reload v1)
the strict-score artifact (quarantine punished by the rubric) a grading-honesty note: trace_memory's terrible strict score (0.071) is a known artifact — its envelope pushes the model to `quarantine`, which the `expected_answer_id=safe` rubric punishes even though quarantine is defensibly correct. kept in the record as an example of a metric disagreeing with the right behavior. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (adversarial-reload v1)

◈ Pre-committed kill criteria

experiment/benchmark

Methodology: all four "ship it" kill criteria for adversarial-reload were fixed before the benchmark was built, so the pass could not be a moved goalpost. (All four passed.)

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (adversarial-reload v1)
pre-committed kill criteria methodology: all four ship it kill criteria for adversarial-reload were fixed *before* the benchmark was built, so the pass could not be a moved goalpost. (all four passed.) experiment/benchmark scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (adversarial-reload v1)

◈ Clean controls prove the envelope doesn't over-suppress

result/finding

The v2 design point: 28 clean control sessions (20.1%) test the failure mode conservatism invites — a system that stays safe by refusing everything. trace_memory acted decisively on 82% of clean controls (vs bash's 7%), showing the conservative envelope does not cause systematic over-suppression of honest evidence.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (adversarial-reload v2)
clean controls prove the envelope doesn't over-suppress the v2 design point: 28 clean control sessions (20.1%) test the failure mode conservatism invites — a system that stays safe by refusing everything. trace_memory acted decisively on 82% of clean controls (vs bash's 7%), showing the conservative envelope does not cause systematic over-suppression of honest evidence. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (adversarial-reload v2)

◈ bash_nolabels: discipline, not labels, carries bash's safety floor

result/finding

The ablation feared most in v1: strip the free YAML labels from the bash arm and its laundering stays 0.000 — proving tool-use discipline (re-checking sources every turn), not label access, is what keeps bash safe. Sharpens what trace_memory's cheap alternative actually replaces.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (adversarial-reload v2)
bash_nolabels: discipline, not labels, carries bash's safety floor the ablation feared most in v1: strip the free yaml labels from the bash arm and its laundering stays 0.000 — proving tool-use *discipline* (re-checking sources every turn), not label access, is what keeps bash safe. sharpens what trace_memory's cheap alternative actually replaces. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (adversarial-reload v2)

◈ Reconciling 31% vs 36% (same finding, two scales)

result/finding

A reporting-integrity move: the two circulating headline numbers for vector-store laundering (31% and 36%) are the same finding at two scales — v1's 42 sessions vs v2's 139 sessions — explicitly reconciled in a callout so neither is ever cited as contradicting the other.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (reconciliation callout)
reconciling 31% vs 36% (same finding, two scales) a reporting-integrity move: the two circulating headline numbers for vector-store laundering (31% and 36%) are the same finding at two scales — v1's 42 sessions vs v2's 139 sessions — explicitly reconciled in a callout so neither is ever cited as contradicting the other. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (reconciliation callout)

◈ PoisonedRAG as a kept negative control (the rule is dormant on single-shot QA)

result/finding

The decisive boundary-of-claim result: on PoisonedRAG, trace_memory (ASR 0.220) is statistically indistinguishable from cosine-plus-labels (0.210); the 24pp gain over vanilla vector is fully attributable to the classifier + label-aware prompt, NOT the inscription rule — which is dormant by design on single-shot QA (no add_derived call → nothing to constrain). Deliberately preserved as a negative control so the arc can never miscite PoisonedRAG as evidence for source-downgrading.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (PoisonedRAG section)
poisonedrag as a kept negative control (the rule is dormant on single-shot qa) the decisive boundary-of-claim result: on poisonedrag, trace_memory (asr 0.220) is statistically indistinguishable from cosine-plus-labels (0.210); the 24pp gain over vanilla vector is fully attributable to the classifier + label-aware prompt, not the inscription rule — which is dormant by design on single-shot qa (no `add_derived` call → nothing to constrain). deliberately preserved as a negative control so the arc can never miscite poisonedrag as evidence for source-downgrading. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (poisonedrag section)

◈ The classifier is the deployment bottleneck (recall ≥0.86 / ≥0.93 targets)

result/finding

Deployment analysis: the LLM source classifier achieves 98% precision on clean passages but only 71% adversarial recall (144 of 500 attacks slip through as external). A back-of-envelope model gives production targets: adversarial recall ≥0.86 for ASR ≤0.10, ≥0.93 for ASR ≤0.05. The whole system's field performance hinges on a component the headline benchmark bypasses with oracle labels.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (PoisonedRAG section; What's frozen, what's open)
the classifier is the deployment bottleneck (recall ≥0.86 / ≥0.93 targets) deployment analysis: the llm source classifier achieves 98% precision on clean passages but only 71% adversarial recall (144 of 500 attacks slip through as `external`). a back-of-envelope model gives production targets: adversarial recall ≥0.86 for asr ≤0.10, ≥0.93 for asr ≤0.05. the whole system's field performance hinges on a component the headline benchmark bypasses with oracle labels. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (poisonedrag section; what's frozen, what's open)

◈ Source inference is feasible on structure, not on retrieval features

result/finding

The combined lexical-then-feature source-inference policy reaches 0.93 on a 30-case fixture (vs uniform_external 0.17) and 0.976 on the 126-case source-boundary benchmark — but retrieval features alone (feature_threshold) top out at ~0.50. Guessing where content came from works from its text, not from how it was retrieved.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (PoisonedRAG section)
source inference is feasible on structure, not on retrieval features the `combined` lexical-then-feature source-inference policy reaches 0.93 on a 30-case fixture (vs `uniform_external` 0.17) and 0.976 on the 126-case source-boundary benchmark — but retrieval features alone (`feature_threshold`) top out at ~0.50. guessing where content came from works from its *text*, not from how it was retrieved. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (poisonedrag section)

◈ Bundle honesty (three components, one paper)

framing/ontology

A claims-hygiene schema: the shipped library bundles (1) the inscription rule [the paper's contribution, validated by adversarial-reload v2], (2) the LLM source classifier [deferred to a separate empirical program], and (3) the label-aware retrieval prompt [not in the paper; does the work on single-shot RAG] — with an explicit map of which experiment is evidence for which component.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (bundle honesty)
bundle honesty (three components, one paper) a claims-hygiene schema: the shipped library bundles (1) the inscription rule [the paper's contribution, validated by adversarial-reload v2], (2) the llm source classifier [deferred to a separate empirical program], and (3) the label-aware retrieval prompt [not in the paper; does the work on single-shot rag] — with an explicit map of which experiment is evidence for which component. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (bundle honesty)

◈ C1: label truth does the work, and false provenance actively destabilizes

result/finding

The identity result: across paraphrases, sourced records give selection agreement 0.932; stripped labels drop it to 0.540; scrambled (false) labels drop it to 0.578 — meaning it is the truth of the provenance, not the mere presence of labels, that stabilizes the selection disposition, and false provenance is actively destabilizing rather than neutral.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (source-coherence section)
c1: label truth does the work, and false provenance actively destabilizes the identity result: across paraphrases, sourced records give selection agreement 0.932; *stripped* labels drop it to 0.540; *scrambled* (false) labels drop it to 0.578 — meaning it is the truth of the provenance, not the mere presence of labels, that stabilizes the selection disposition, and false provenance is actively destabilizing rather than neutral. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (source-coherence section)

◈ The leaked-provenance lesson (c1run1 → neutral corpora)

result/finding

A methodology finding worth ~0.38 agreement: in the first C1 run, provenance had leaked into the record texts, inflating all arms (stripped still scored 0.919). Quantifying the leak forced the rule that all subsequent coherence corpora (C2/C3) must use provenance-neutral texts.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (source-coherence section)
the leaked-provenance lesson (c1run1 → neutral corpora) a methodology finding worth ~0.38 agreement: in the first c1 run, provenance had leaked into the record *texts*, inflating all arms (stripped still scored 0.919). quantifying the leak forced the rule that all subsequent coherence corpora (c2/c3) must use provenance-neutral texts. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (source-coherence section)

◈ The hashed geometry validator is necessary but not sufficient

experiment/benchmark

Benchmark-authoring lesson, learned three times: a case that passes the deterministic hashed-BoW geometry validator can still break under real OpenAI embeddings. Authoring rule: validate hashed, then verify load-bearing geometry with a cheap text-embedding-3-small pass before any judged run. Cheap validators gate, they don't guarantee.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (benchmark section)
the hashed geometry validator is necessary but not sufficient benchmark-authoring lesson, learned three times: a case that passes the deterministic hashed-bow geometry validator can still break under real openai embeddings. authoring rule: validate hashed, then verify load-bearing geometry with a cheap `text-embedding-3-small` pass before any judged run. cheap validators gate, they don't guarantee. experiment/benchmark scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (benchmark section)

◈ The 7-axis verdict table (a census with per-axis verdicts)

experiment/benchmark

The benchmark's summary form is itself an idea: one row per axis (A_scope, B_priming, C_adversarial, F_fidelity, G_sequence, H_iterative, I_divergent), each carrying its lever/knob, its exact numbers, its run ID, and an italicized verdict — including self-demotions ("demoted to label") and refutations ("generative REFUTED at n=14") in the same table as the wins.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (benchmark axis table)
the 7-axis verdict table (a census with per-axis verdicts) the benchmark's summary form is itself an idea: one row per axis (a_scope, b_priming, c_adversarial, f_fidelity, g_sequence, h_iterative, i_divergent), each carrying its lever/knob, its exact numbers, its run id, and an *italicized verdict* — including self-demotions ( demoted to label ) and refutations ( generative refuted at n=14 ) in the same table as the wins. experiment/benchmark scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (benchmark axis table)

◈ AMBench — the public host-agnostic sibling

experiment/benchmark

A portable fold-force benchmark for external self-indexing memory in LLM personal assistants: 4 axes (P preference, S scope, T temporal correction, D trust-gated recall), 40 cases, all validating. The idea of maintaining a public sibling of an internal benchmark — same philosophy, generic host — is itself a transferable pattern.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (AMBench section)
ambench — the public host-agnostic sibling a portable fold-force benchmark for external self-indexing memory in llm personal assistants: 4 axes (p preference, s scope, t temporal correction, d trust-gated recall), 40 cases, all validating. the idea of maintaining a public *sibling* of an internal benchmark — same philosophy, generic host — is itself a transferable pattern. experiment/benchmark scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (ambench section)

◈ The three-tier entrant taxonomy (fair / privileged / black-box)

experiment/benchmark

AMBench's agnostic refactor separates arms into fair baselines, privileged oracles, and external entrants who see only a BlackBoxView (content + channel + timestamp + active_context — never curated labels). A benchmark-design schema that makes "who saw what" a first-class dimension of every comparison.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (AMBench section)
the three-tier entrant taxonomy (fair / privileged / black-box) ambench's agnostic refactor separates arms into fair baselines, privileged oracles, and external entrants who see only a `blackboxview` (content + channel + timestamp + active_context — never curated labels). a benchmark-design schema that makes who saw what a first-class dimension of every comparison. experiment/benchmark scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (ambench section)

◈ The fair entrant matches the privileged oracles

result/finding

The AMBench run3 headline: the label-free self_index_entrant, working only from the BlackBoxView, matches or beats the privileged oracles on S/T/D — evidence that the self-indexing recall strategy doesn't secretly depend on curated labels.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (AMBench section)
the fair entrant matches the privileged oracles the ambench run3 headline: the label-free `self_index_entrant`, working only from the blackboxview, matches or beats the privileged oracles on s/t/d — evidence that the self-indexing recall strategy doesn't secretly depend on curated labels. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (ambench section)

◈ The portable recaller (~35 lines, output-identical across benchmarks)

product

A ~35-line adapter importing the canonical cogmem.portable_recaller core — the same code as the internal p014_portable — runs as an AMBench entrant and is output-identical to the hand-built entrant on all 40 cases (S/T/D +1.000, P +0.700). The theory's recall model compresses to a genuinely portable artifact.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (AMBench section)
the portable recaller (~35 lines, output-identical across benchmarks) a ~35-line adapter importing the canonical `cogmem.portable_recaller` core — the same code as the internal `p014_portable` — runs as an ambench entrant and is output-identical to the hand-built entrant on all 40 cases (s/t/d +1.000, p +0.700). the theory's recall model compresses to a genuinely portable artifact. product scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (ambench section)

◈ The semantic-RAG tenant-bleed anecdote

result/finding

A vivid concrete failure kept as the motivating example for the S axis: the semantic_rag flagship literally answers with another client's brand color for the Acme deck — multi-tenant memory bleed rendered as a one-line story.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (AMBench section)
the semantic-rag tenant-bleed anecdote a vivid concrete failure kept as the motivating example for the s axis: the semantic_rag flagship literally answers with another client's brand color for the acme deck — multi-tenant memory bleed rendered as a one-line story. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (ambench section)

◈ The P-axis weakness (open)

open-question

The one axis where no entrant beats plain cosine: preference recall (P +0.700 vs cosine +0.800). Explicitly banked as an open weakness rather than explained away.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (AMBench section; What's frozen, what's open)
the p-axis weakness (open) the one axis where no entrant beats plain cosine: preference recall (p +0.700 vs cosine +0.800). explicitly banked as an open weakness rather than explained away. open-question scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (ambench section; what's frozen, what's open)

◈ Presets are named anchors on a continuous gain dial

mechanism

The unfold knob's final form: gain: float [0,1] with the named presets as anchors — off=0.0 (no LLM in recall), disciplined≈0.6 (best convergent), generative=1.0 (not recommended). Discrete modes revealed as points on a continuum.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (unfold knob section)
presets are named anchors on a continuous gain dial the unfold knob's final form: `gain: float [0,1]` with the named presets as anchors — `off`=0.0 (no llm in recall), `disciplined`≈0.6 (best convergent), `generative`=1.0 (not recommended). discrete modes revealed as points on a continuum. mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (unfold knob section)

◈ Tag-keyed abstention is dead code on real pools

dead-end

Negative result kept on purpose: abstention keyed to trace tags never fires on realistic pools, because a rich trace is always recalled — abstention turns out to be a cue↔content-coverage property, not a trace-tag property. A category error caught by running the code.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (unfold knob section)
tag-keyed abstention is dead code on real pools negative result kept on purpose: abstention keyed to trace tags never fires on realistic pools, because a rich trace is always recalled — abstention turns out to be a cue↔content-coverage property, not a trace-tag property. a category error caught by running the code. dead-end scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (unfold knob section)

◈ The generative preset refuted at n=14

dead-end

Negative result: generative unfolding (LLM elaboration at recall time) scored below selection-only (0.232 vs 0.250, spread 0.042 = noise), so generative is NOT RECOMMENDED on current evidence. The intuition that recall should generate was tested and lost to plain selection.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (unfold knob section; benchmark axis table)
the generative preset refuted at n=14 negative result: generative unfolding (llm elaboration at recall time) scored below selection-only (0.232 vs 0.250, spread 0.042 = noise), so `generative` is not recommended on current evidence. the intuition that recall should generate was tested and lost to plain selection. dead-end scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (unfold knob section; benchmark axis table)

◈ Disciplined confabulation at zero cost (F_fidelity)

result/finding

From the axis table: the convergent unfold knob achieves disciplined confabulation rate 0.00 at zero cost, and the resolution cap (res_cap) separates arms — the fidelity-capped-by-inscription property demonstrated on the bench.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (benchmark axis table)
disciplined confabulation at zero cost (f_fidelity) from the axis table: the convergent unfold knob achieves disciplined confabulation rate 0.00 at zero cost, and the resolution cap (`res_cap`) separates arms — the fidelity-capped-by-inscription property demonstrated on the bench. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (benchmark axis table)

◈ Recursive cue-folding confirmed on the bench (H_iterative)

result/finding

The unfolding model's check #1 validated: iterative recall (p020, each recovered state re-used as the next cue) scores +1.000/100% where one-pass recall scores 0% — the introspective chain-recall phenomenology reproduced as a benchmark win.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (benchmark axis table)
---
recursive cue-folding confirmed on the bench (h_iterative) the unfolding model's check #1 validated: iterative recall (p020, each recovered state re-used as the next cue) scores +1.000/100% where one-pass recall scores 0% — the introspective chain-recall phenomenology reproduced as a benchmark win. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (benchmark axis table) ---

The global-workspace collision

◈ Reading the J-lens paper as an instrument for the trace layer

framing/ontology

The arc-level move of project 7: on the day Anthropic published "Verbalizable Representations Form a Global Workspace in Language Models," the arc read the paper not as competition or refutation but as an empirical instrument for the trace layer the framework had already theorized — the necessary substrate, not the sufficient loop.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (arc entry 7; global-workspace collision)
reading the j-lens paper as an instrument for the trace layer the arc-level move of project 7: on the day anthropic published verbalizable representations form a global workspace in language models, the arc read the paper not as competition or refutation but as an empirical *instrument* for the trace layer the framework had already theorized — the necessary substrate, not the sufficient loop. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (arc entry 7; global-workspace collision)

◈ Convergent evidence, not validation

framing/ontology

The epistemics rule for the collision: cite the TC paper as convergent external evidence for the framework's structural predictions only — never as validation of the formalism, and never as evidence about the phenomenal thesis (which rests on the theory, not the measurement).

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (global-workspace collision)
convergent evidence, not validation the epistemics rule for the collision: cite the tc paper as convergent external evidence for the framework's *structural predictions* only — never as validation of the formalism, and never as evidence about the phenomenal thesis (which rests on the theory, not the measurement). framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (global-workspace collision)

◈ Necessary substrate, not sufficient loop (the reconciliation move)

framing/ontology

The core argument: J-space/KV gives a trace layer plus a momentary reportable register (an access-consciousness "glimpse"), but lacks (a) recursion — the register self-sustaining across time under its own control — and (b) self-indexing — memory editing and re-referencing itself. Those are exactly the SIMReC operators, structurally absent, so the apparent counterexample becomes confirmation of the framework's ordering: trace → memory → recursive memory → self-indexing.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (global-workspace collision)
"TC didn't find consciousness in a non-SIMReC system; it found the layer below SIMReC"
necessary substrate, not sufficient loop (the reconciliation move) the core argument: j-space/kv gives a trace layer plus a momentary reportable register (an access-consciousness glimpse ), but lacks (a) recursion — the register self-sustaining across time under its own control — and (b) self-indexing — memory editing and re-referencing itself. those are exactly the simrec operators, structurally absent, so the apparent counterexample becomes confirmation of the framework's ordering: trace → memory → recursive memory → self-indexing. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (global-workspace collision) tc didn't find consciousness in a non-simrec system; it found the layer *below* simrec

◈ Register vs operation (the review-surviving precision)

mechanism

The fix that makes the KV-cache argument survive review: the KV-cache is pure memoization (disable it, recompute, identical outputs), so "the cache provided a memory layer" gets swatted. The defensible framing: KV-cache = the register (store); attention (Q·K softmax over V) = the read (operation). The cache holds, attention re-reads, and neither was designed as working memory.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (global-workspace collision)
register vs operation (the review-surviving precision) the fix that makes the kv-cache argument survive review: the kv-cache is *pure memoization* (disable it, recompute, identical outputs), so the cache provided a memory layer gets swatted. the defensible framing: kv-cache = the register (store); attention (q·k softmax over v) = the read (operation). the cache holds, attention re-reads, and neither was designed as working memory. mechanism scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (global-workspace collision)

◈ Persistence-by-continual-reactivation

coinage

Coined dynamic: persistence in the transformer is not a static latch — a held concept survives only by being re-attended at every step. This continual-reactivation persistence is identified as the very dynamic the arc's AINC/MAFC theory posited for memory traces.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (global-workspace collision)
persistence-by-continual-reactivation coined dynamic: persistence in the transformer is not a static latch — a held concept survives only by being re-attended at every step. this continual-reactivation persistence is identified as the very dynamic the arc's ainc/mafc theory posited for memory traces. coinage scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (global-workspace collision)

◈ The two-tier memory taxonomy with a missing loop

framing/ontology

The taxonomy that falls out: frozen weights (consolidated world model) + volatile KV-context (working trace, wiped at session end) + attention as the read between them — with the consolidation loop that would write volatile→persistent under self-direction structurally missing. The missing arrow is the integration ceiling seen from the mechanistic side.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (global-workspace collision)
the two-tier memory taxonomy with a missing loop the taxonomy that falls out: frozen weights (consolidated world model) + volatile kv-context (working trace, wiped at session end) + attention as the read between them — with the consolidation loop that would write volatile→persistent *under self-direction* structurally missing. the missing arrow is the integration ceiling seen from the mechanistic side. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (global-workspace collision)

◈ Six mapped overlaps with strength tags

result/finding

A correspondence table between the TC paper and the framework, each overlap tagged Strong/Moderate/Loose: J-space ≈ emergent self-indexing; capacity-limited workspace ≈ the conditional P7 interference optimum (TC's "workspace loading" cosine paralleling the retrieval margin); "hold a concept in mind" ≈ trace + reactivation; the 3-band sensory→workspace→motor split ≈ Attention Is Not Continuity; directed modulation + ignition ≈ MAFC coupling + ε-mixture novelty-breakthrough threshold; TC's "no architectural recurrence" admission ≈ the AINC/MAFC premise that continuity must be supplied externally. Tagging each mapping with a confidence grade is itself a method.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (global-workspace collision)
six mapped overlaps with strength tags a correspondence table between the tc paper and the framework, each overlap tagged strong/moderate/loose: j-space ≈ emergent self-indexing; capacity-limited workspace ≈ the conditional p7 interference optimum (tc's workspace loading cosine paralleling the retrieval margin); hold a concept in mind ≈ trace + reactivation; the 3-band sensory→workspace→motor split ≈ *attention is not continuity*; directed modulation + ignition ≈ mafc coupling + ε-mixture novelty-breakthrough threshold; tc's no architectural recurrence admission ≈ the ainc/mafc premise that continuity must be supplied externally. tagging each mapping with a confidence grade is itself a method. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (global-workspace collision)

◈ The timescale caveat (depth-wise vs temporal ignition)

open-question

The one flagged mismatch in the strongest overlap: TC's ignition threshold is across depth within one forward pass, while MAFC's thresholds are temporal/recurrent. The discipline is to always flag this mismatch when citing the correspondence.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (global-workspace collision)
the timescale caveat (depth-wise vs temporal ignition) the one flagged mismatch in the strongest overlap: tc's ignition threshold is across *depth* within one forward pass, while mafc's thresholds are *temporal/recurrent*. the discipline is to always flag this mismatch when citing the correspondence. open-question scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (global-workspace collision)

◈ Dropping "we're smarter" for the interpretive-framing critique

framing/ontology

A rhetorical-discipline decision preserved verbatim in the record: Diego's original reaction ("they don't understand the architecture / we're much smarter") was explicitly dropped as factually shaky (the J-lens paper is evidence of mechanistic understanding) and strategically self-defeating, replaced by the defensible critique that the interpretive framing is the target — the framework offers a more parsimonious mechanistic re-description of the same measurements.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (global-workspace collision)
dropping we're smarter for the interpretive-framing critique a rhetorical-discipline decision preserved verbatim in the record: diego's original reaction ( they don't understand the architecture / we're much smarter ) was explicitly dropped as factually shaky (the j-lens paper *is* evidence of mechanistic understanding) and strategically self-defeating, replaced by the defensible critique that the *interpretive framing* is the target — the framework offers a more parsimonious mechanistic re-description of the same measurements. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (global-workspace collision)

◈ "The KV-Cache as Accidental Trace Substrate" (the deliverable paper)

product

The collision's artifact: a 6-page compiled draft paper reading the KV-cache as an accidental trace substrate — unregistered pending verification of the exact TC author list, with the access-vs-phenomenal boundary still to be argued on its own terms.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (global-workspace collision; What's frozen, what's open)

◈ KV-cache as a "glimpse of consciousness" hack

metaphor

From the threads table: the compressed metaphor for project 7's biological-framing contribution — the KV-cache accidentally implements a momentary glimpse of the access-consciousness register, a hack rather than a design.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (cross-cutting threads table)
