◈ Three falsifiers for the KV-cache reading

experiment/benchmark

From the threads table's benchmarking row: the KV-cache-as-trace reading was equipped with three falsifiers — the collision project extended the arc's falsification discipline even to its most interpretive claim.

The frozen/open ledger (status ideas)

◈ The R-ladder (R0–R6) with a principled deferral

framing/ontology

A capability ladder used as the freeze's bookkeeping: R0 total source-stamped capture (Done), R1 fold-force recall (Banked ×2), R2 selective unfolding without an LLM in recall (Done), R3 unfolding-model recall (Done), then R4 Self-via-compression, R5 identity continuity via correction chains, R6 runtime integration — all three deliberately deferred with a stated reason: a Self compressed on the wrong side of the perception channel would inherit the testimony discount.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (What's frozen, what's open)
the r-ladder (r0–r6) with a principled deferral a capability ladder used as the freeze's bookkeeping: r0 total source-stamped capture (done), r1 fold-force recall (banked ×2), r2 selective unfolding without an llm in recall (done), r3 unfolding-model recall (done), then r4 self-via-compression, r5 identity continuity via correction chains, r6 runtime integration — all three *deliberately* deferred with a stated reason: a self compressed on the wrong side of the perception channel would inherit the testimony discount. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (what's frozen, what's open)

◈ Freeze-at-tag with an empty backlog

framing/ontology

A project-hygiene idea: Phase 1 frozen at a named tag (v0.1.0-phase1) with the backlog explicitly empty — "everything below is a research candidate, not a blocker." Separating owed work from optional futures at the moment of freezing.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (What's frozen, what's open)
freeze-at-tag with an empty backlog a project-hygiene idea: phase 1 frozen at a named tag (`v0.1.0-phase1`) with the backlog explicitly empty — everything below is a research candidate, not a blocker. separating owed work from optional futures at the moment of freezing. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (what's frozen, what's open)

◈ The one open engineering decision (merge or not)

open-question

The record deliberately isolates a single human decision left open: the frozen tag sits on branch feat/unfold-recall, ~35 commits ahead of master, and merging it is Diego's call (he chose "keep as-is" mid-R3). Naming the single open decision is itself an organizational move.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (What's frozen, what's open)
the one open engineering decision (merge or not) the record deliberately isolates a single human decision left open: the frozen tag sits on branch `feat/unfold-recall`, ~35 commits ahead of master, and merging it is diego's call (he chose keep as-is mid-r3). naming the single open decision is itself an organizational move. open-question scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (what's frozen, what's open)

◈ IC-1: the ceiling is flat at 3-4B... and BENDS at 7B

result/finding

The measured shape of the testimony discount: run22 found the override curve flat (with-memory M4 0.97–1.00, 0/256 overrides — prior pressure alone does not discount a clean declarative); run25 found the curve bends at 7B (Qwen2.5-7B, q4_K_M on an RTX 3070, 416 local generations) — the first nonzero overrides on clean declaratives (0.031–0.094), including a memory revision toward the prior. The discount is model-scale-dependent and empirically locatable.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (What's frozen, what's open)
ic-1: the ceiling is flat at 3-4b... and bends at 7b the measured shape of the testimony discount: run22 found the override curve flat (with-memory m4 0.97–1.00, 0/256 overrides — prior pressure alone does not discount a clean declarative); run25 found the curve bends at 7b (qwen2.5-7b, q4_k_m on an rtx 3070, 416 local generations) — the first nonzero overrides on clean declaratives (0.031–0.094), including a memory *revision toward the prior*. the discount is model-scale-dependent and empirically locatable. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (what's frozen, what's open)

◈ "A stored absence is content, not a gap"

result/finding

The run23/24 fix: the testimony discount had been isolated to meta-testimony form — records asserting an absence ("there is no X") were being read as gaps in memory rather than as content. Reformulating a stored absence as content took abs-t3 from M4 0.000 to 0.938.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (What's frozen, what's open)
"a stored absence is content, not a gap"
a stored absence is content, not a gap the run23/24 fix: the testimony discount had been isolated to *meta-testimony form* — records asserting an absence ( there is no x ) were being read as gaps in memory rather than as content. reformulating a stored absence as content took abs-t3 from m4 0.000 to 0.938. result/finding scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (what's frozen, what's open) a stored absence is content, not a gap

◈ IC-2: the state-level fold A/B with pre-registered targets

experiment/benchmark

The flagship post-freeze build: precompute selected memory records as a cached KV-prefix (llama-cpp-python / HF with manual KV control; Ollama can't inject KV state) and A/B against text-fold — with the beat-targets fixed in advance from IC-1's measurements (M4 0.94 / override 0.094 on declaratives; M4 0.19–0.50 on absences). Publishing the bar before running the experiment.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (What's frozen, what's open)
ic-2: the state-level fold a/b with pre-registered targets the flagship post-freeze build: precompute selected memory records as a cached kv-prefix (llama-cpp-python / hf with manual kv control; ollama can't inject kv state) and a/b against text-fold — with the beat-targets fixed in advance from ic-1's measurements (m4 0.94 / override 0.094 on declaratives; m4 0.19–0.50 on absences). publishing the bar before running the experiment. experiment/benchmark scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (what's frozen, what's open)

◈ The honest open-loop caveat (predictions asserted from theory only)

open-question

The self-audit that only writeback of the four MAFC loop arrows is exercised by any benchmark; the other three (H→M reactivation ranking needing an ActivePosture variable, M→X per-turn reactivation envelope, Y→routing side-channel via the Stop-hook regex classifier) are unbuilt — so the MAFC dynamical predictions (loop-gain bounds, novelty-breakthrough threshold, lock-in, echo amplification) remain theory-only. "None of the broken arrows require new theory."

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (What's frozen, what's open)
the honest open-loop caveat (predictions asserted from theory only) the self-audit that only writeback of the four mafc loop arrows is exercised by any benchmark; the other three (h→m reactivation ranking needing an `activeposture` variable, m→x per-turn reactivation envelope, y→routing side-channel via the stop-hook regex classifier) are unbuilt — so the mafc dynamical predictions (loop-gain bounds, novelty-breakthrough threshold, lock-in, echo amplification) remain theory-only. none of the broken arrows require new theory. open-question scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (what's frozen, what's open)

◈ H1 — five concrete adversarial tests for the meet

experiment/benchmark

The owed adversarial battery for source-downgrading: reconvergent DAGs, cyclic provenance, contradictory same-rank inputs, cross-agent trust transfer, and classifier prompt-injection — five named ways the meet might break that no current fixture exercises.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (What's frozen, what's open)
h1 — five concrete adversarial tests for the meet the owed adversarial battery for source-downgrading: reconvergent dags, cyclic provenance, contradictory same-rank inputs, cross-agent trust transfer, and classifier prompt-injection — five named ways the meet might break that no current fixture exercises. experiment/benchmark scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (what's frozen, what's open)

◈ H4 — the branching-DAG benchmark

experiment/benchmark

Owed realism upgrade: adversarial-reload v2's provenance chains are mostly linear and depth-1-heavy (118/14/7 for depths 1/2/3); a benchmark with genuinely branching derivation DAGs is needed before the depth claims generalize.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (What's frozen, what's open)
h4 — the branching-dag benchmark owed realism upgrade: adversarial-reload v2's provenance chains are mostly linear and depth-1-heavy (118/14/7 for depths 1/2/3); a benchmark with genuinely branching derivation dags is needed before the depth claims generalize. experiment/benchmark scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (what's frozen, what's open)

◈ The headline 0% is conditional on oracle labels

open-question

An explicit scope limit on the arc's flagship number: the 0%-laundering result bypasses the deployment-critical classifier layer via authored oracle labels, and is unproven beyond gpt-4.1-mini. The strongest claim carries its own asterisk in the record.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (What's frozen, what's open)
the headline 0% is conditional on oracle labels an explicit scope limit on the arc's flagship number: the 0%-laundering result bypasses the deployment-critical classifier layer via authored oracle labels, and is unproven beyond gpt-4.1-mini. the strongest claim carries its own asterisk in the record. open-question scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (what's frozen, what's open)

◈ The related-work gap: the 2024–25 IFC-for-LLM-agents wave

open-question

Identified but unintegrated: the base paper misses the contemporaneous wave of IFC-for-LLM-agent work — CaMeL, FIDES, AgentPoison, MINJA, MemoryGraft — a named reading list for situating the inscription rule.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (What's frozen, what's open)
the related-work gap: the 2024–25 ifc-for-llm-agents wave identified but unintegrated: the base paper misses the contemporaneous wave of ifc-for-llm-agent work — camel, fides, agentpoison, minja, memorygraft — a named reading list for situating the inscription rule. open-question scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (what's frozen, what's open)

◈ The trust ordering is only half-validated (and react > tool breaks it)

open-question

A tension the record keeps live: fixtures exercise only the lower half of the lattice (fab/sim/infer/ext); the react/tool/ext adjacencies are untested, and a realistic counterexample — a verified reactivated postmortem should outrank an attacker-controlled tool scrape, i.e. react > tool — shows the single total order is "a default engineering policy, not a universal trust order." The E1/E3 extensions exist precisely to absorb this.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (What's frozen, what's open)
the trust ordering is only half-validated (and `react > tool` breaks it) a tension the record keeps live: fixtures exercise only the lower half of the lattice (`fab/sim/infer/ext`); the `react/tool/ext` adjacencies are untested, and a realistic counterexample — a verified reactivated postmortem should outrank an attacker-controlled tool scrape, i.e. `react > tool` — shows the single total order is a default engineering policy, not a universal trust order. the e1/e3 extensions exist precisely to absorb this. open-question scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (what's frozen, what's open)

◈ Label Transport's two unresolved design choices

open-question

The E4 draft's open decisions: opaque-provenance semantics (what a receiving agent may infer from provenance it cannot inspect) and context-indexed import policy (how a receiver's context should gate what it accepts). Two named forks in cross-boundary trust design.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (What's frozen, what's open)
---

Figure concepts (visualization designs as creative artifacts)

◈ Figure 1 — the arc timeline with a thickening spine

figure-concept

Visualization concept: the seven projects on a horizontal April–July timeline, connected by a spine that visibly thickens toward cognivault-memory (the densest node, drawn largest), with a per-project theme glyph above each node (graph / folding motif / control loop / shield-lattice / branching paper stack / four levers + benchmark bar / spotlight-lens). Line weight as a metaphor for intellectual mass.

scratchpad\recovered\FIGURES.md (Figure 1)
figure 1 — the arc timeline with a thickening spine visualization concept: the seven projects on a horizontal april–july timeline, connected by a spine that visibly *thickens* toward cognivault-memory (the densest node, drawn largest), with a per-project theme glyph above each node (graph / folding motif / control loop / shield-lattice / branching paper stack / four levers + benchmark bar / spotlight-lens). line weight as a metaphor for intellectual mass. figure-concept scratchpad\recovered\figures.md (figure 1)

◈ Figure 2 — the four levers as a 2×2 gate-panel

figure-concept

Visualization concept: each lever as a distinct mechanical device in a quadrant — Scope as a funnel with a "wrong scope" duplicate falling away; Co-activation as a solid primed_by edge visually opposed to a faint dashed cosine edge; Authority as a valve stamped host.authority passing of_record and blocking claim cards; Antecedent as an arrow running backward along a time axis to a follows record then forward to the answer.

scratchpad\recovered\FIGURES.md (Figure 2)
figure 2 — the four levers as a 2×2 gate-panel visualization concept: each lever as a distinct mechanical device in a quadrant — scope as a funnel with a wrong scope duplicate falling away; co-activation as a solid `primed_by` edge visually opposed to a faint dashed cosine edge; authority as a valve stamped `host.authority` passing `of_record` and blocking `claim` cards; antecedent as an arrow running backward along a time axis to a `follows` record then forward to the answer. figure-concept scratchpad\recovered\figures.md (figure 2)

◈ Figure 3 — the unfolding chain with a halt-at-boundary branch

figure-concept

Visualization concept: recursive cue-folding drawn as a left-to-right chain (cue-0 → fold → state-1 → becomes cue-1 → ...) with each box growing to suggest ×5–×10 expansion, three annotated invariants along the bottom, and — the sharp detail — one branch hitting a wall labeled "halt at boundary (stored but not addressable)": memory that exists but cannot be reached from this cue chain.

scratchpad\recovered\FIGURES.md (Figure 3)
figure 3 — the unfolding chain with a halt-at-boundary branch visualization concept: recursive cue-folding drawn as a left-to-right chain (cue-0 → fold → state-1 → becomes cue-1 → ...) with each box growing to suggest ×5–×10 expansion, three annotated invariants along the bottom, and — the sharp detail — one branch hitting a wall labeled halt at boundary (stored but not addressable) : memory that exists but cannot be reached from this cue chain. figure-concept scratchpad\recovered\figures.md (figure 3)

◈ Figure 4 — the inversion as mirror-image panels

figure-concept

Visualization concept: the integration ceiling drawn as two mirror-imaged panels — ideal (one sourced memory=world-model slab, attention resting on top, the Self forming inside the slab) vs current LLMs (frozen unsourced weights at bottom, attention band, recalled-memory token cards above attention, their downward arrow stopped at a dashed red ceiling line). The mirror symmetry is the argument.

scratchpad\recovered\FIGURES.md (Figure 4)
figure 4 — the inversion as mirror-image panels visualization concept: the integration ceiling drawn as two mirror-imaged panels — ideal (one sourced memory=world-model slab, attention resting on top, the self forming *inside* the slab) vs current llms (frozen unsourced weights at bottom, attention band, recalled-memory token cards *above* attention, their downward arrow stopped at a dashed red ceiling line). the mirror symmetry is the argument. figure-concept scratchpad\recovered\figures.md (figure 4)

◈ Figure 5 — the trust lattice as a ladder with a witness cut

figure-concept

Visualization concept: the six trust classes as ladder rungs, a bold dashed "witness cut" line between inference and retrieved_memory, brackets labeling the lower region "self-authored (no consequence channel)" and the upper "witness-coupled (world contact)," a ceiling icon on inference, and operation_record drawn detached to the side with a dotted line.

scratchpad\recovered\FIGURES.md (Figure 5)

◈ Figure 6 — laundering vs the meet, with a forbidden arrow

figure-concept

Visualization concept: panel A shows naive inscription laundering (an external card through a derive box emerging mislabeled external (laundered), plus an inset of the 0.43-self-audit vs 1.00-truth cascade-invisibility gap); panel B shows add_derived as a literal lattice meet operator (∧) labeled "min-trust, capped at inference" — with a blocked, greyed arrow labeled "source= (forbidden)" making the API subtraction visible.

scratchpad\recovered\FIGURES.md (Figure 6)

◈ Figure 7 — ladder plus hub-and-satellites

figure-concept

Visualization concept, two views of SIMReC in one figure: (left) the five-rung state-preservation ladder trace → storage → memory → recursive memory → self-indexed recursive memory, annotated "a trace becomes memory only when its fold changes the next transition"; (right) the meet as a fixed central hub with six extension satellites (per-record valuation, time-varying credibility, context valuation, label transport, source decay, witness grounding) all pointing inward, under the banner "enrich the valuation, never the meet."

◈ The five-rung state-preservation ladder

framing/ontology

The ladder itself, independent of the figure: trace → storage → memory → recursive memory → self-indexed recursive memory, with the promotion criterion between rungs being fold-force (a trace becomes memory only when folding it changes the next transition). The whole arc is described as climbing this ladder.

figure-concept

◈ Figure 11 — register-vs-operation and the missing arrow

figure-concept

Visualization concept: panel A draws the KV-cache as a register box and attention as a separate read operator joined by a looping "persistence by continual reactivation" arrow (caption: "disable the cache → identical output; neither was designed as working memory"); panel B draws the two-tier taxonomy with a greyed, dashed, clearly-absent arrow from the volatile band down to the weights labeled "MISSING: consolidation loop." Drawing the absence is the figure's point.

◈ Figure 12 — the control-loop diagram with one LIVE arrow

figure-concept

Visualization concept: MAFC v4 as a classical control-theory block diagram — LLM = plant, source classifier = sensor, inscription rule = control law, storage = state x(t), retrieval+envelope = controller, untrusted external content = disturbance — with exactly one loop arrow (writeback) drawn solid and highlighted "LIVE" and the other three dashed/greyed "unbuilt." Build-status rendered as line style inside an architecture diagram.

◈ The control-theory mapping of the mnestic agent

metaphor

Independent of the drawing: the systematic identification of memory-agent components with control-theory roles (plant/sensor/control-law/state/controller/disturbance), with the punchline that "the closed loop is closed OUTSIDE the LLM, not inside it, because most of live attention is hidden inside the model."

scratchpad\recovered\FIGURES.md (Figure 12 caption)
"the loop is closed OUTSIDE the LLM — most of live attention is hidden inside the model"
the control-theory mapping of the mnestic agent independent of the drawing: the systematic identification of memory-agent components with control-theory roles (plant/sensor/control-law/state/controller/disturbance), with the punchline that the closed loop is closed outside the llm, not inside it, because most of live attention is hidden inside the model. metaphor scratchpad\recovered\figures.md (figure 12 caption) the loop is closed outside the llm — most of live attention is hidden inside the model

◈ The Imagen/DATA-chart split rule

figure-concept

A publication-engineering rule: conceptual/schematic figures may be generated by an image model, but any figure encoding exact experimental numbers must be built as a real SVG/plot at build time and never fed to the image model — "Imagen will invent inaccurate values." A provenance discipline for figures that mirrors the arc's data-integrity theme.

scratchpad\recovered\FIGURES.md (header)
the imagen/data-chart split rule a publication-engineering rule: conceptual/schematic figures may be generated by an image model, but any figure encoding exact experimental numbers must be built as a real svg/plot at build time and never fed to the image model — imagen will invent inaccurate values. a provenance discipline for figures that mirrors the arc's data-integrity theme. figure-concept scratchpad\recovered\figures.md (header)

◈ The house-style prompt formula

figure-concept

A reusable visual-identity string appended to every diagram prompt: "Editorial technical illustration in the Anthropic / Distill.pub style — off-white background, muted terracotta + slate palette, thin clean strokes, labeled boxes, flat vector, no photorealism, text limited to the labels named here." A design system compressed into a prompt suffix, including the anti-hallucination clause limiting text to named labels.

scratchpad\recovered\FIGURES.md (all Imagen prompts)
the house-style prompt formula a reusable visual-identity string appended to every diagram prompt: editorial technical illustration in the anthropic / distill.pub style — off-white background, muted terracotta + slate palette, thin clean strokes, labeled boxes, flat vector, no photorealism, text limited to the labels named here. a design system compressed into a prompt suffix, including the anti-hallucination clause limiting text to named labels. figure-concept scratchpad\recovered\figures.md (all imagen prompts)

◈ The figure-spec schema

figure-concept

An organizational schema for figure production: every figure carries id, filename, aspect ratio, kind (Imagen vs DATA), the OVERVIEW sections that reference it, a full standalone caption, and either an Imagen prompt or an exact-numbers render spec — making each figure independently buildable and auditable against the text.

scratchpad\recovered\FIGURES.md (structure)
---
the figure-spec schema an organizational schema for figure production: every figure carries id, filename, aspect ratio, kind (imagen vs data), the overview sections that reference it, a full standalone caption, and either an imagen prompt or an exact-numbers render spec — making each figure independently buildable and auditable against the text. figure-concept scratchpad\recovered\figures.md (structure) ---

Meta-ideas: how the compilation organizes a creativity substrate

◈ The arc census table (one line per project)

framing/ontology

The compilation's top-level index: a chronological table with number, project, date, a one-line essence, a transcript-volume column (flagging where the raw material is dense — "18 files, 12 MB" for the core), and the repo mapping. A census that makes the whole arc scannable in seven rows and marks its center of gravity.

C:\Users\DiegoPC\Documents\GitHub\memory-compilation\CLAUDE.md
the arc census table (one line per project) the compilation's top-level index: a chronological table with number, project, date, a one-line essence, a transcript-volume column (flagging where the raw material is dense — 18 files, 12 mb for the core), and the repo mapping. a census that makes the whole arc scannable in seven rows and marks its center of gravity. framing/ontology c:\users\diegopc\documents\github\memory-compilation\claude.md

◈ Inline-citation auditability (every claim anchored to a file)

framing/ontology

The overview's contract with itself: every substantive claim is cited inline to project/memory/<file>.md, repo docs, or a .jsonl transcript, "so the narrative stays auditable back to the transcripts," with exact numbers, run IDs, tags, and DOIs preserved verbatim. The synthesis applies the source-grounding rule to its own prose.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (header note)
inline-citation auditability (every claim anchored to a file) the overview's contract with itself: every substantive claim is cited inline to `project/memory/<file>.md`, repo docs, or a `.jsonl` transcript, so the narrative stays auditable back to the transcripts, with exact numbers, run ids, tags, and dois preserved verbatim. the synthesis applies the source-grounding rule to its own prose. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (header note)

◈ Status vocabulary: Banked / Done / Deferred / Frozen / CONFIRMED / owed / REFUTED / demoted / unbuilt

framing/ontology

The compilation's consistent status lexicon for ideas and results — results are "banked" on a benchmark, checks are "CONFIRMED" or "owed," presets are "REFUTED" or "NOT RECOMMENDED," levers are "demoted," ladder rungs are "Done/Deferred," arrows are "LIVE/unbuilt," phases are "frozen at tag." A graded status ontology that never collapses into pass/fail.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (throughout); scratchpad\recovered\FIGURES.md (Figure 12)
status vocabulary: banked / done / deferred / frozen / confirmed / owed / refuted / demoted / unbuilt the compilation's consistent status lexicon for ideas and results — results are banked on a benchmark, checks are confirmed or owed, presets are refuted or not recommended, levers are demoted, ladder rungs are done/deferred, arrows are live/unbuilt, phases are frozen at tag. a graded status ontology that never collapses into pass/fail. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (throughout); scratchpad\recovered\figures.md (figure 12)

◈ The cross-cutting threads matrix (6 themes × 7 projects)

framing/ontology

An organizational schema: a matrix with one row per cross-cutting theme (source-grounding, unfolding recall, four levers, integration ceiling, biological/SIMReC framing, benchmarking) and one column per project, each cell a one-phrase statement of that project's contribution to that thread. It converts a chronological narrative into a theme-by-time grid where lineages become readable as rows.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (cross-cutting threads)
the cross-cutting threads matrix (6 themes × 7 projects) an organizational schema: a matrix with one row per cross-cutting theme (source-grounding, unfolding recall, four levers, integration ceiling, biological/simrec framing, benchmarking) and one column per project, each cell a one-phrase statement of that project's contribution to that thread. it converts a chronological narrative into a theme-by-time grid where lineages become readable as rows. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (cross-cutting threads)

◈ The publication architecture (arc → spine → mechanism → numbers → collision → threads → ledger → altitudes)

framing/ontology

The overview's section order as a reusable template for synthesizing a research arc: chronological arc first, then the conceptual spine (the few ideas everything hangs on), then the mechanism and its paper chain, then levers and exact numbers, then the external collision, then the cross-cutting threads matrix, then an explicit frozen/open ledger, then a discussion at three altitudes, then an appendix source map.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (overall structure)
the publication architecture (arc → spine → mechanism → numbers → collision → threads → ledger → altitudes) the overview's section order as a reusable template for synthesizing a research arc: chronological arc first, then the conceptual spine (the few ideas everything hangs on), then the mechanism and its paper chain, then levers and exact numbers, then the external collision, then the cross-cutting threads matrix, then an explicit frozen/open ledger, then a discussion at three altitudes, then an appendix source map. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (overall structure)

◈ The frozen/open ledger as a first-class section

framing/ontology

The idea of ending a synthesis not with conclusions but with an explicit ledger: what is frozen (with tags), the one open engineering decision, the flagship next build with pre-registered targets, the honest caveats, the owed experiments per project, and the half-validated assumptions. Debts are catalogued with the same rigor as assets.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (What's frozen, what's open)
the frozen/open ledger as a first-class section the idea of ending a synthesis not with conclusions but with an explicit ledger: what is frozen (with tags), the one open engineering decision, the flagship next build with pre-registered targets, the honest caveats, the owed experiments per project, and the half-validated assumptions. debts are catalogued with the same rigor as assets. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (what's frozen, what's open)

◈ The notation-reconciliation note

framing/ontology

A small but load-bearing compilation practice: when two notations for the same object circulate in the sources (fabricated_or_uncertain < ... < external vs fab < ... < ext), the synthesis carries an explicit note declaring them interchangeable and mapping the terms — preventing a future reader from seeing two lattices where there is one.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (Appendix, notation note)
the notation-reconciliation note a small but load-bearing compilation practice: when two notations for the same object circulate in the sources (`fabricated_or_uncertain < ... < external` vs `fab < ... < ext`), the synthesis carries an explicit note declaring them interchangeable and mapping the terms — preventing a future reader from seeing two lattices where there is one. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (appendix, notation note)

◈ The appendix source map + key-artifact registry

framing/ontology

The compilation closes with a registry of durable artifacts: per-project compiled sources each carrying its own source map, plus the paper (with page count), the library (with version, merge hash, and pre-validation baseline tag), the frozen core tag, the benchmark package name, and the full Zenodo DOI sequence for the theory papers. Everything citable is pinned to an immutable identifier.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (Appendix)
the appendix source map + key-artifact registry the compilation closes with a registry of durable artifacts: per-project compiled sources each carrying its own source map, plus the paper (with page count), the library (with version, merge hash, and pre-validation baseline tag), the frozen core tag, the benchmark package name, and the full zenodo doi sequence for the theory papers. everything citable is pinned to an immutable identifier. framing/ontology scratchpad\recovered\overview.md (appendix)

◈ Verbatim quotes as anchor evidence in a synthesis

framing/ontology

The practice of preserving the founding actor's exact words at pivotal moments (the idea.txt request, the integration-ceiling ruling) inside an otherwise analytic synthesis — the human's phrasing treated as primary-source data with the same status as run numbers.

scratchpad\recovered\OVERVIEW.md (Overview; conceptual spine)

Generated 2026-07-06 by the memory-compilation extraction fleet (14 agents over 7 repos, 8 transcript sets, and 9 recovered prior compilations). Raw per-part lists live beside this file in compiled/ideas/. Everything is precious.
